Table transaction.instrument_issuance {
  instrument_issuance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the instrument issuance event.']
  delivery_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the address where a physical instrument (card, letter) is to be sent.']
  issuing_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (bank, fintech, or partner) that issued the instrument.']
  original_issuance_instrument_issuance_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original issuance record when this is a re‑issue.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch run that processed this issuance (e.g., nightly settlement batch).']
  activation_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the instrument must be activated before it expires or is de‑provisioned.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening for the issuance.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The delivery channel through which the issuance request originated.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance review result for the issuance (PCI, regulatory).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the instrument is no longer valid for transactions.']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the physical or digital instrument to the holder.']
  instrument_issuance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the issuance record.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Categorises the kind of payment instrument being issued.']
  is_reissued boolean [note: 'True if this issuance replaces a previously issued instrument.']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the instrument is stored as a token rather than a clear PAN.']
  issuance_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary fee charged to the customer for issuing the instrument.']
  issuance_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the issuance fee is expressed.']
  issuance_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible sequential number assigned to each issuance for tracking and reconciliation.']
  issuance_status_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑form text providing additional context about the current status (e.g., reason for rejection).']
  issuance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment the issuance event occurred in the real world.']
  issuing_bank_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the financial institution that issued the instrument.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Result of the Know‑Your‑Customer verification for the instrument holder.']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount credited to the holder after deducting issuance fees.']
  net_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the net amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for operational comments or exceptions.']
  pan varchar [note: 'The 16‑digit card number assigned to the issued card. Stored in encrypted form per PCI DSS.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the specific product variant (e.g., Visa Platinum, Debit‑Lite).']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the issuance record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the issuance record.']
  token_service_provider_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the TSP that generated the token.']
  token_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Non‑sensitive token that replaces the PAN for tokenized instruments.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the issuance event of a payment instrument — card printing and dispatch, virtual card generation, ACH account enrollment, or token provisioning. Stores issuance date, issuance channel (branch, online, mobile, API), issuing entity, fulfillment method (instant issue, mail, digital), delivery address reference, activation deadline, and issuance status. Provides the authoritative issuance audit trail per PCI DSS and regulatory requirements.'
}

Table transaction.payment_txn {
  payment_txn_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each payment transaction record.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key of the cardholder (or payer) involved in the transaction.']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_assessment. Business justification: Compliance audit mandates storing the detailed risk assessment record generated for each transaction, linking to risk.risk_assessment.']
  compliance_aml_screening_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_aml_screening_result. Business justification: Regulatory AML screening per transaction stores result in compliance_aml_screening_result; linking enables audit trails and SAR generation.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Enables treasury to aggregate exposure and risk metrics by currency pair for each payment transaction.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to present the payment credential; may be a DPAN or device serial number.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Wallet‑originated payments must reference the originating digital wallet for reconciliation and AML reporting.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key of the acquiring financial institution that processed the transaction.']
  gateway_api_credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.api_credential. Business justification: Billing and security audits track which API credential (client) processed each payment transaction.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key of the merchant that originated the transaction.']
  merchant_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_location. Business justification: Required for location‑level transaction reporting, risk scoring and settlement attribution; merchants need to know which physical location processed each payment.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.payment_instrument. Business justification: Required for settlement and instrument‑level reporting linking each payment transaction to the specific card or token used.']
  pos_entry_mode_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.pos_entry_mode. Business justification: Standardize entry mode by linking payment_txn to the pos_entry_mode reference table; replace the free‑text entry_mode column with a foreign key.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that captured the transaction.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Required for audit and regulatory reporting to identify the exact FX rate applied to each cross‑border payment transaction.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each transaction must be tagged with the applicable regulatory obligation (e.g., PSD2, AML) for reporting and compliance monitoring.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Audit & compliance require linking each payment transaction to the originating API request for traceability and dispute investigation.']
  response_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_response. Business justification: Reconciliation and settlement reports need the exact gateway response that settled the payment transaction.']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Regulatory AML/fraud monitoring requires each payment transaction to be linked to the associated risk profile used for scoring.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Regulatory interchange reporting requires each payment transaction to be linked to its card scheme for fee calculation and compliance.']
  sub_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.sub_merchant. Business justification: Needed for PayFac models to attribute each payment to the originating sub‑merchant for settlement, revenue share, and compliance reporting.']
  transaction_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Linking transaction currency to the currency master enables consistent currency handling and conversion logic. Add transaction_currency_id (FK to reference.currency.currency_id) and retire the free‑fo']
  transaction_mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: Transaction records need a formal link to the MCC master data for reporting and fee calculation. Adding transaction_mcc_id (FK to reference.mcc.mcc_id) replaces the loosely‑typed mcc_code.']
  acquirer_code varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key of the acquiring financial institution that processed the transaction.']
  auth_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code generated by the issuer confirming approval of the transaction.']
  avs_result varchar [note: 'Result code from AVS indicating match level between billing address and card issuer records.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch in which the transaction was processed for settlement.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Delivery channel through which the transaction was captured (POS, mPOS, eCommerce, QR, NFC, HCE, mobile_app, API).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction passed all AML/KYC and regulatory screening checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  cvv_result varchar [note: 'Result code indicating whether the CVV supplied matched the issuer\'s record.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reason provided when a transaction is declined (e.g., insufficient funds, suspected fraud).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Conversion rate applied when the transaction involves a foreign currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of fees (interchange, network, processing) applied to the transaction.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was flagged for potential fraud and requires review.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the transaction involved currency conversion or parties in different countries.']
  is_stp boolean [note: 'True when the transaction was processed end‑to‑end without manual intervention.']
  iso_message_type varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code identifying the message class (e.g., 0100 for authorization request).']
  iso_message_version varchar [note: 'Year of the ISO 8583 version used for the message (e.g., 2003).']
  mcc varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s primary business activity.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final amount settled to the merchant after fees, expressed in transaction currency.']
  network varchar [note: 'Card scheme or payment network that processed the transaction (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, UnionPay, Other).']
  original_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Transaction amount in the original currency prior to conversion.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency code of the amount before conversion (if different from transaction_currency).']
  payment_txn_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the transaction (e.g., authorized, captured, settled, declined, reversed, chargeback).']
  processing_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit ISO 8583 processing code indicating transaction type and account handling.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating generated by the fraud engine; higher values indicate greater suspicion.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was settled to the merchant\'s account.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.instruction. Business justification: Audit trail requires linking each payment transaction to its settlement instruction for regulatory reporting and dispute reconciliation.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the settlement process (pending, settled, failed).']
  tokenization_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction used a tokenized payment credential instead of a clear PAN.']
  transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the transaction before fees or adjustments, expressed in transaction currency.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the acquiring network (e.g., Acquirer Reference Number) that uniquely identifies the transaction across the payment ecosystem.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the transaction was initiated or authorized in the payment network.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the transaction (purchase, refund, reversal, cash_advance, preauth, capture).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this transaction record (e.g., status change, settlement update).']

  Note: 'Core SSOT for every payment transaction processed through the platform — card-present, card-not-present, and digital payment channels. Captures the full ISO 8583 / ISO 20022 message lifecycle from authorization request through capture, including transaction amount, currency, MCC, channel type (POS, mPOS, eCommerce, QR, NFC, HCE), entry mode (EMV, swipe, contactless, manual), transaction timestamp, acquirer reference number (ARN), network transaction ID, processing code, response code, and STP flag. Single authoritative record per payment event.'
}

Table transaction.authorization {
  authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the authorization record.']
  authorization_response_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.authorization_response_code. Business justification: Compliance and fraud analysis need the exact authorization response code entity to interpret response meanings.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Card scheme lookup (Visa, MC, etc.) drives fee rules and risk scoring; linking to scheme master is required for accurate processing.']
  decline_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.decline_code. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requires linking each authorization decline to the official decline_code reference for audit and analytics.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the transaction.']
  original_auth_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the first authorization attempt when this record is a retry.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal or device used for the transaction.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Each authorization originates from a gateway API request; linking enables end‑to‑end audit of auth decisions.']
  amount_authorized decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount that was approved by the issuer.']
  amount_requested decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount requested for authorization.']
  auth_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the authorization attempt, used for reconciliation and reporting.']
  auth_retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of authorization attempts made for this transaction.']
  auth_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the authorization request – real‑time or batch processing.']
  auth_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the authorization attempt.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuer indicating approval.']
  avs_result varchar [note: 'Result of the AVS check. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Y|N|X|U|R|S|D|M|P|I|C|B|E|G|A|N — promote to reference product]']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the card.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction passed mandatory compliance checks (e.g., AML, sanctions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this authorization record was first persisted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction.']
  cvv_result varchar [note: 'Result of the CVV check. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: M|N|P|S|U|X — promote to reference product]']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Unique identifier derived from device characteristics for fraud detection.']
  device_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device that originated the authorization request.']
  fraud_flag varchar [note: 'Categorical risk level derived from the fraud score.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned by the fraud detection engine.']
  is_card_present boolean [note: 'True if the card was physically present at the point of sale.']
  is_contactless boolean [note: 'True if the transaction used contactless technology.']
  is_test_transaction boolean [note: 'True if the transaction is a test or sandbox transaction.']
  issuer_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds taken by the issuer to respond.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code representing the merchant\'s business type.']
  merchant_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the merchant location.']
  network_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Measured network latency between gateway and issuer.']
  pan_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the Primary Account Number (masked).']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was initiated.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used for the payment.']
  processing_time_ms varchar [note: 'Overall time taken to process the authorization request end‑to‑end.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the reporting requirement status for the transaction.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authorization request was sent to the issuer.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authorization response was received from the issuer.']
  sca_status varchar [note: 'Result of the SCA evaluation for the transaction.']
  three_ds_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the 3‑D Secure authentication flow.']
  token_used boolean [note: 'True if a tokenized PAN was used for the transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this authorization record.']

  Note: 'Real-time authorization decision record produced by the Payment Gateway and Authorization Engine for each payment transaction. Stores the authorization request and response pair including authorization code, response code, decline reason, AVS result, CVV result, 3DS authentication result, SCA outcome, issuer response time, network latency, and the final approve/decline/referral decision. One authorization record per authorization attempt; a single payment may have multiple authorization attempts (retries).'
}

Table transaction.capture {
  capture_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the capture record.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (consumer) for the captured transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (consumer) for the captured transaction.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the captured transaction.']
  original_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original authorization transaction that this capture fulfills.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the payment terminal used for the capture.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement. Business justification: Each capture is settled via a settlement record; linking supports audit of captured amounts against settlement.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement batch that includes this capture.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device (e.g., token or POS terminal) that originated the capture.']
  acquirer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference assigned by the acquiring bank for tracking the transaction.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Authorized amount captured before any adjustments, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  auth_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuing bank confirming the original authorization.']
  capture_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the capture was performed (immediate, delayed, incremental, or partial).']
  capture_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing identifier for the capture event, often used in reporting and reconciliation.']
  capture_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capture (e.g., pending, completed, failed, reversed, partial).']
  capture_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the capture was performed, per ISO 8583/20022 event time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capture record was first persisted in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the capture amount.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate applied when converting capture currency to settlement currency.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the capture was flagged for potential fraud.']
  interchange_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee paid between acquiring and issuing banks for this capture.']
  is_incremental boolean [note: 'True when the capture adds to a previously captured amount (multiple captures).']
  is_partial_capture boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the capture represents a partial amount of the original authorization.']
  is_settled boolean [note: 'True when the capture amount has been successfully settled.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s business type.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount after tip, surcharge, and tax adjustments.']
  network varchar [note: 'Payment network that processed the transaction (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  processing_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the payment processor for handling the capture.']
  reversal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount reversed from the capture, if a reversal occurred.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capture was reversed.']
  risk_score_at_capture decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned at the moment of capture.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal number of this capture within a series of incremental captures.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount settled to the merchant after fees and adjustments.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the settlement amount is paid to the merchant.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the captured amount was settled to the merchant.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the settlement process for this capture.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any surcharge (e.g., service fee) applied at capture time.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the captured amount, if applicable.']
  tip_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional tip amount added to the capture, if applicable.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the transaction captured (e.g., purchase, refund).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the capture record.']

  Note: 'Capture record finalizing an authorized transaction for downstream clearing and settlement. Tracks the capture amount (which may differ from authorized amount for tip-adjusted or partial captures), capture timestamp, capture method (immediate, delayed, incremental), batch ID reference, and capture status. Represents the business event that moves a transaction from authorized to billable state in the Transaction Processing Platform.'
}

Table transaction.clearing_submission {
  clearing_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the clearing submission record.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder who performed the transaction.']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder who performed the transaction.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that originated the transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the original captured transaction.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch file that contains this clearing record.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the original captured transaction.']
  acquiring_institution_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring financial institution.']
  aml_screened_flag boolean [note: 'True when the transaction passed AML screening.']
  amount_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all fees applied to the transaction.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction amount before fees and adjustments.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount after fees are deducted.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuer approving the transaction.']
  bin_number varchar [note: 'First six digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Interface through which the transaction was initiated.']
  chargeback_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction resulted in a chargeback.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the transaction cleared.']
  clearing_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the clearing submission.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of why a transaction was declined.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,10) [note: 'Exchange rate used when converting to settlement currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the fee specified by fee_type.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Category of fee applied to the transaction.']
  iin_number varchar [note: 'Six‑digit identifier of the card issuer.']
  interchange_category varchar [note: 'Classification used to determine interchange fees.']
  irf_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee amount calculated based on IRF rate category.']
  irf_rate_category varchar [note: 'Rate tier applied for IRF calculation.']
  issuing_institution_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issuing financial institution.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s business.']
  pci_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction data meets PCI compliance requirements.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the clearing submission record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Two‑digit response indicating approval or decline reason.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was reversed after initial capture.']
  sanction_check_flag boolean [note: 'True when the transaction passed sanctions screening.']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Payment network brand associated with the transaction.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount transferred to the acquiring institution after conversion.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the transaction is settled.']
  submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference assigned by the card scheme or ACH network for this clearing submission.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transaction was submitted to the scheme for settlement.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the original transaction was authorized.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of payment instrument used for the transaction.']

  Note: 'Clearing file record generated by the Transaction Processing Platform representing a captured transaction submitted to the card scheme or ACH network for interbank settlement. Contains clearing amount, clearing currency, interchange qualification category, MCC, BIN, IIN, scheme-assigned clearing reference, clearing file batch ID, clearing date, and scheme-specific data elements required for IRF calculation. SSOT for the clearing lifecycle stage of each transaction.'
}

Table transaction.txn_lifecycle_event {
  txn_lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each immutable transaction lifecycle event record.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder (customer) for the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder (customer) for the transaction.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant involved in the transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction to which this lifecycle event belongs.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS or terminal that captured the transaction.']
  tokenization_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the token representing the payment credential used.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch (if the event is part of a batch).']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction to which this lifecycle event belongs.']
  triggering_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Operational audit: manual overrides or adjustments in transaction lifecycle must capture the responsible employee.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount associated with the event, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  auth_code varchar [note: 'Issuer‑provided code confirming authorization.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Delivery channel through which the transaction was initiated.']
  chargeback_flag boolean [note: 'True if a chargeback has been filed for the transaction.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Compliance regime applicable to the event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Textual reason for a declined transaction, if applicable.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction is currently under dispute.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text providing additional context for the event.']
  event_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the event within the transaction lifecycle.']
  event_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the event record.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the lifecycle event occurred in the source system.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any fee applied at the time of the event (e.g., interchange, service fee).']
  iso_message_type_code varchar [note: 'ISO 8583 or ISO 20022 message type identifier associated with the event (e.g., 0100, 0200, 0300).']
  network_code varchar [note: 'Payment network identifier (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, ACH).']
  new_state varchar [note: 'State of the transaction after this event was applied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authorized|captured|cleared|settled|reversed|voided|refunded|chargeback|disputed|pending — promote to reference product]']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to fund the transaction.']
  prior_state varchar [note: 'State of the transaction before this event occurred. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authorized|captured|cleared|settled|reversed|voided|refunded|chargeback|disputed|pending — promote to reference product]']
  processing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds taken to process the event from receipt to completion.']
  refund_flag boolean [note: 'True if the event represents a refund.']
  response_code varchar [note: 'ISO response code indicating success, decline, or error.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'True if the event represents a reversal of a prior transaction.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score calculated at the moment of the event.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final settled amount after fees and adjustments.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the transaction is settled.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the transaction was settled.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the settlement process for the transaction.']
  source_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the system that originated the event.']
  stp_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was processed without manual intervention.']
  tps varchar [note: 'Observed TPS rate at the time of the event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this immutable record (normally equals created_timestamp).']
  void_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was voided before capture.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of every state transition in a payment transaction\'s lifecycle — authorization, capture, clearing submission, settlement, reversal, void, refund initiation, chargeback flag. Each row records the prior state, new state, event timestamp, triggering actor (system, user, network), and ISO message type code. Enables full end-to-end transaction traceability required for PCI DSS audit trails and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table transaction.reversal {
  reversal_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the reversal record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the party that initiated the reversal.']
  original_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original payment transaction that is being reversed.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original payment transaction that is being reversed.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement instruction generated for the reversal.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch processing run that includes this reversal.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount being reversed from the original transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reversal record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the reversal amount.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any fee associated with processing the reversal.']
  initiating_party_role varchar [note: 'Role of the entity that initiated the reversal.']
  is_successful boolean [note: 'True when the reversal completed successfully.']
  net_reversal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after deducting reversal fees.']
  partial_reversal_flag boolean [note: 'True if the reversal is for a portion of the original amount.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the reversal was performed (e.g., ISO 8583 reason code).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the reversal reason.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External reference code assigned to the reversal (e.g., RR202300001).']
  reversal_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the reversal.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the reversal was executed in the payment network.']
  reversal_type varchar [note: 'Category of reversal: void, partial, timeout, or duplicate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reversal record.']

  Note: 'Transaction reversal record capturing full and partial reversals, voids, and cancellations processed against an original payment transaction. Stores reversal type (void, partial reversal, timeout reversal), reversal amount, original transaction reference, reversal reason code, initiating party (merchant, acquirer, issuer, network), and reversal processing timestamp. Distinct from refunds (which are new debit transactions) and chargebacks (which are dispute-driven).'
}

Table transaction.refund {
  refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the refund record.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who made the original purchase.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who made the original purchase.']
  chargeback_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chargeback case associated with this refund, if any.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to initiate the refund (e.g., POS terminal ID).']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant that originated the original transaction.']
  original_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original sale transaction that is being refunded.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original sale transaction that is being refunded.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch that includes this refund.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch that includes this refund.']
  tokenization_request_id varchar [note: 'Token representing the original payment instrument used for the refund.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount credited back to the cardholder or alternative instrument.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch file in which the refund was processed.']
  cardholder_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the cardholder for communication and receipt delivery.']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the cardholder.']
  cardholder_pan_masked varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the cardholder\'s PAN (first 6 and last 4 digits visible) to comply with PCI DSS.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the refund request was submitted.']
  compliance_flag_aml boolean [note: 'True if the refund triggered an AML screening alert.']
  compliance_flag_fraud boolean [note: 'True if the refund was flagged by the fraud detection engine.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the refund record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the refund amount.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied when the refund currency differs from the original transaction currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Any fee charged by the processor for processing the refund.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the refund fee amount.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the merchant initiated the refund request.']
  is_chargeback_related boolean [note: 'True if the refund is linked to an ongoing chargeback case.']
  is_partial_refund boolean [note: 'True if the refund amount is less than the original transaction amount.']
  original_authorization_code varchar [note: 'Six‑character code returned by the issuer for the original authorization.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the original transaction before any conversion.']
  partial_refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount refunded when the refund is partial.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the refund was successfully posted to the cardholder\'s account.']
  processor_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier returned by the payment processor for the refund transaction.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the refund was issued.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the refund reason.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the payment scheme or processor for this refund.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method used to return funds: original payment instrument, alternative instrument, or store credit.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the refund.']
  risk_decision varchar [note: 'Decision outcome based on the risk score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score assigned to the refund by the fraud engine.']
  scheme_refund_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the card scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) for the refund.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement batch containing the refund was settled.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the refund record.']

  Note: 'Refund transaction record representing a merchant-initiated credit back to a cardholder\'s payment instrument following a completed sale. Captures refund amount, refund reason, original transaction reference, refund method (original payment instrument, alternative instrument), refund status, merchant-initiated timestamp, and scheme refund reference. Operationally distinct from reversals (pre-settlement) and chargebacks (dispute-driven). SSOT for merchant-initiated credit events.'
}

Table transaction.transaction_batch {
  transaction_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the transaction batch record.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant whose transactions are included in this batch.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.cycle. Business justification: Batch processing is grouped into settlement cycles; linking enables cycle‑level performance and compliance reporting.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch received approval from downstream settlement validation.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the batch purpose.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum (e.g., SHA‑256) of the settlement file for integrity verification.']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was closed to further transaction inclusion.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the batch amounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CHF|AUD|CAD|... — promote to reference product]']
  error_count varchar [note: 'Number of error records detected during batch validation.']
  exchange_rate_to_usd decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate used to convert batch amounts to USD for reporting.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the file generated for settlement transmission.']
  is_stp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch met straight‑through processing criteria.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was opened for transaction capture.']
  processing_rate_tps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of transactions processed per second during batch execution.']
  processing_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds to process the batch from open to close.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this batch record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch record.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External business reference number assigned to the batch for reconciliation with scheme and settlement files.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reason for batch rejection, if applicable.']
  scheme_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the payment scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) for this batch.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the batch was settled with the acquiring bank.']
  settlement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier returned by the settlement system.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current settlement outcome for the batch.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that produced the batch record.']
  stp_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transactions in the batch processed without manual intervention.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was submitted to the clearing/settlement system.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of the gross amounts of all transactions in the batch, before fees and adjustments.']
  total_amount_local decimal(18,2) [note: 'Batch total amount expressed in the batch\'s native currency.']
  total_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Batch total amount expressed in US dollars.']
  total_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate of all fees (interchange, MDR, IRF, etc.) applied to the batch.']
  total_fee_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate fee amount converted to USD.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after subtracting fees and adjustments from the total amount.']
  total_net_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net batch amount after fees, expressed in USD.']
  total_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual transactions captured in the batch.']
  transaction_batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the batch as defined by the processing workflow.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version of the batch record for audit and re‑processing purposes.']
  warning_count varchar [note: 'Number of warning records detected during batch validation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or service account that initiated the batch creation.']

  Note: 'Batch processing record representing a grouped set of captured transactions submitted together for clearing and settlement by the Transaction Processing Platform. Stores batch open timestamp, batch close timestamp, batch total transaction count, batch total amount, batch currency, batch status (open, closed, submitted, accepted, rejected), scheme batch reference, and STP rate for the batch. Enables reconciliation between individual transaction records and settlement file totals.'
}

Table transaction.batch_item {
  batch_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each line item within a settlement batch.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used for tokenized or mobile payments.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant associated with the transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original payment transaction captured in this batch line.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that captured the transaction.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch that this line belongs to.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original payment transaction captured in this batch line.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuing bank approving the transaction.']
  batch_item_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes associated with the line item.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network brand of the card used (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  chargeback_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the line item originated from a chargeback.']
  chargeback_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the reason for a chargeback. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FRAUD|DISPUTE|AUTH_ERROR|OTHER] ']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the transaction originated (derived from terminal location).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the transaction\'s originating country.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch item record was first created in the data lake.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any processing exception for the line item. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ERR01|ERR02|ERR03|ERR04|ERR05|ERR06|ERR07|ERR08] ']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the original amount to the settlement currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any fee applied to the line item (e.g., interchange or service fee).']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the fee amount.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address of the transaction request.']
  is_test_transaction boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was generated in a test or sandbox environment.']
  item_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount for the line item in the transaction currency.']
  item_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code of the item amount.']
  item_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the batch line item.']
  item_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the line item (e.g., purchase, refund).']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential position of the item within the batch.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s business type.']
  network_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the payment network or interchange used for routing.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount in the originating currency before conversion.']
  original_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the transaction was originally authorized.']
  original_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the original transaction identifier when this line is a reversal or adjustment.']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the line item was processed by the settlement engine.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units represented by the line item (typically 1 for payment transactions).']
  response_code varchar [note: 'ISO 8583 response code indicating the outcome of the authorization.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the line item represents a reversal of a prior transaction.']
  risk_decision varchar [note: 'Result of the risk assessment for the transaction.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned by the risk engine (0‑100).']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current settlement state of the line item.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the line item was settled to the acquiring bank.']
  transaction_category varchar [note: 'High‑level channel category of the transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch item record.']

  Note: 'Individual transaction line item within a settlement batch, linking each captured transaction to its parent batch record. Stores the sequence number within the batch, item amount, item currency, item status (included, excluded, error), and any item-level exception codes. Enables granular reconciliation and exception handling at the individual transaction level within a batch submission.'
}

Table transaction.transaction_reconciliation {
  transaction_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each reconciliation record.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant whose transactions are being reconciled.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch run that produced this reconciliation record.']
  acquiring_bank_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank involved in the transaction flow.']
  compliance_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of automated compliance checks performed on the reconciliation data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_flag boolean [note: 'True if the batch contains any cross‑border transactions, false otherwise.']
  currency_conversion_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied when converting foreign‑currency transaction amounts to the settlement currency.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation resulted in any exceptions (true) or not (false).']
  issuing_bank_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issuing bank that supplied the payment instrument.']
  matched_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of transactions that successfully matched between internal records and scheme files.']
  net_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after applying variance adjustments (total_matched_amount minus total_variance_amount).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for manual comments, observations, or actions taken during reconciliation.']
  processing_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Duration of the reconciliation process measured in seconds.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the reconciliation run, used for external reporting and audit.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reconciliation outcome: balanced (no differences), exception (discrepancies found), or pending (still processing).']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation process was executed.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation is performed intraday, at end of day, or as a T+1 settlement.']
  regulatory_report_indicator varchar [note: 'Flag indicating whether this reconciliation record is included in regulatory reporting submissions.']
  risk_score_average decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average fraud risk score of the transactions included in this reconciliation batch.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the settlement amount (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement for the reconciled batch is scheduled or completed.']
  source_file_internal varchar [note: 'File name or path of the internal transaction file used in the reconciliation.']
  source_file_scheme varchar [note: 'File name or path of the scheme‑provided clearing/settlement file used for comparison.']
  total_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all fees (interchange, processing, network) associated with the batch.']
  total_matched_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all matched transactions, expressed in settlement currency.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax component captured in the transaction batch, if applicable.']
  total_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all transactions (matched and unmatched) before any adjustments.']
  total_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of monetary differences between internal and scheme amounts for unmatched or partially matched transactions.']
  transaction_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the original transaction amounts before conversion.']
  transaction_volume varchar [note: 'Total count of individual transaction lines evaluated in this reconciliation.']
  unmatched_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of transactions that did not find a counterpart in the scheme file.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the primary cause of the variance (e.g., amount mismatch, missing transaction).']

  Note: 'Reconciliation record produced by the Transaction Processing Platform comparing internal transaction records against scheme-reported clearing and settlement files. Captures reconciliation date, reconciliation type (intraday, end-of-day, T+1 settlement), matched transaction count, unmatched transaction count, total matched amount, total variance amount, reconciliation status (balanced, exception, pending), and the source file references for both sides of the comparison. SSOT for transaction-level reconciliation outcomes.'
}

Table transaction.reconciliation_exception {
  reconciliation_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each reconciliation exception.']
  assigned_resolver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or team responsible for resolving the exception.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or team responsible for resolving the exception.']
  primary_reconciliation_internal_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal transaction record that participated in the reconciliation.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch settlement or clearing run associated with the exception.']
  amount_difference decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric difference between internal and scheme transaction amounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied when converting amounts between currencies for the exception.']
  exception_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with the exception (e.g., mismatched amount).']
  exception_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the exception amount.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the discrepancy that caused the exception.']
  exception_severity varchar [note: 'Risk severity rating assigned to the exception.']
  exception_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the reconciliation exception.']
  exception_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reconciliation exception was first identified.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category describing why the reconciliation exception occurred.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the internal investigation into the exception.']
  is_duplicate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception represents a duplicate transaction record.']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'True if the exception triggers mandatory regulatory reporting (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes documenting the resolution actions and outcomes.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was resolved or closed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that generated the exception (e.g., transaction_processing, gateway).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the exception record.']

  Note: 'Individual exception item identified during the reconciliation process where an internal transaction record does not match the scheme or bank-reported record. Stores exception type (missing in scheme file, missing in internal records, amount mismatch, duplicate), exception amount, exception currency, investigation status, assigned resolver, resolution timestamp, and resolution notes. Drives the exception management workflow in the Transaction Processing Platform.'
}

Table transaction.txn_routing_decision {
  txn_routing_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each routing decision record.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device (e.g., mobile device ID) used for the transaction.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank or processor selected.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_endpoint. Business justification: Routing decision records the specific network endpoint used for processing, needed for audit trails and latency monitoring.']
  gateway_routing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_routing_rule. Business justification: Regulatory and performance reporting require knowing which routing rule was applied to each transaction routing decision.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that originated the transaction.']
  network_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment network (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction for which the routing decision was made.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that captured the transaction.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment network (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  token_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the token used in place of the PAN.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction for which the routing decision was made.']
  candidate_networks varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of networks evaluated during routing decision.']
  card_present boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was card‑present (vs. card‑not‑present).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the transaction was initiated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing decision record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  cross_border_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional fee applied for cross‑border processing, if any.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction amount.']
  decision_reference varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference code for the routing decision (e.g., RD‑20231101‑0001).']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing decision was recorded.']
  durbin_compliance_applied boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether Durbin Amendment routing rules were enforced.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction involved different currency jurisdictions.']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'True if the payment instrument was tokenized for this transaction.']
  issuer_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issuing bank or card scheme.']
  lcr_applied boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether least‑cost routing logic was applied.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after fees (transaction_amount – transaction_fee).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes added by operators or auditors.']
  routing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds from receipt of the transaction to routing decision.']
  routing_path varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of the processing path taken (e.g., gateway → network → acquirer).']
  routing_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason why the selected network was chosen.']
  routing_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the routing rule set applied to this transaction.']
  selected_network varchar [note: 'Payment network that was selected to process the transaction.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the transaction before fees or discounts.']
  transaction_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees applied to the transaction (e.g., interchange, network, processing).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the transaction was initiated by the cardholder or payer.']
  txn_routing_decision_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the routing decision record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the routing decision record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System or user that initially created the routing decision record.']

  Note: 'Record of the real-time routing decision made by the Payment Gateway and Authorization Engine for each transaction, capturing which network rail, acquirer, and processing path was selected. Stores routing rule version applied, candidate networks evaluated, selected network, routing reason (least cost, preferred network, fallback, regulatory), routing latency in milliseconds, and whether least-cost routing (LCR) or Durbin Amendment routing rules were applied. Enables routing optimization analytics and regulatory compliance evidence.'
}

Table transaction.iso_message {
  iso_message_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each raw ISO message record.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_endpoint. Business justification: ISO message audit must reference the exact network endpoint that handled the message for compliance and troubleshooting.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant associated with the transaction.']
  network_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Code representing the card scheme or payment network.']
  original_message_iso_message_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior ISO message (e.g., original authorization for a reversal).']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that originated the message.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Code representing the card scheme or payment network.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Result of the authorization decision for the message.']
  bitmap varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal representation of the presence map for data elements in the ISO message.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ISO message record was first loaded into the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the transaction currency.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the message was inbound to the gateway or outbound to the network.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency conversion rate applied when the transaction involves multiple currencies.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Any fee charged as part of the transaction (e.g., interchange fee).']
  is_test_message boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the message was generated in a test environment.']
  message_payload varchar [note: 'Full raw message content as received (hex or base64).']
  message_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ISO message was sent or received (event time).']
  message_type_indicator varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code that identifies the high‑level function of the message (e.g., 0200 for authorization request).']
  pan_masked varchar [note: 'Card Primary Account Number with middle digits masked to protect cardholder data.']
  processing_code varchar [note: 'Six‑digit code that defines the transaction type and processing rules.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the ISO protocol used (e.g., ISO8583_1987, ISO20022).']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Two‑digit code indicating the outcome of the transaction (e.g., 00 = approved).']
  retrieval_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier used to retrieve the transaction in later processing stages.']
  session_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking multiple messages that belong to the same processing session.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction is scheduled to settle.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated or received the ISO message.']
  system_trace_audit_number varchar [note: 'Unique numeric identifier assigned by the originating system for traceability.']
  transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction in the transaction currency.']
  transmission_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the message was transmitted, as captured in DE7.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ISO message record.']

  Note: 'Raw ISO 8583 and ISO 20022 financial message record capturing the full message payload exchanged between the gateway, acquirer, and card scheme networks. Stores message type indicator (MTI), processing code, bitmap fields, message direction (inbound/outbound), protocol version, message timestamp, session ID, and parsed key data elements (DE2 PAN masked, DE4 amount, DE7 transmission datetime, DE11 STAN, DE37 retrieval reference number, DE39 response code). SSOT for network message traceability and ISO compliance.'
}

Table transaction.txn_fee {
  txn_fee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fee record associated with a payment transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent payment transaction to which this fee applies.']
  risk_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing rule or algorithm that generated the fee.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent payment transaction to which this fee applies.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the fee in the fee currency.']
  fee_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee amount converted to US Dollars for standardized reporting.']
  fee_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval state of the fee after compliance review.']
  fee_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee was approved or rejected.']
  fee_audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded audit log capturing changes to the fee record.']
  fee_calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the fee.']
  fee_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee record was created.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the fee amount.']
  fee_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the fee.']
  fee_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the fee becomes effective.']
  fee_exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate used to convert the fee amount to the reporting currency.']
  fee_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the fee ceases to be applicable (null if indefinite).']
  fee_flat_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed amount applied when the calculation basis is flat.']
  fee_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating the jurisdiction governing the fee.']
  fee_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units used in tiered or volume‑based fee calculations.']
  fee_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Percentage rate applied when the calculation basis is percentage.']
  fee_regulatory_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for regulatory reporting of the fee (e.g., PCI, PSD2).']
  fee_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for paying the fee.']
  fee_reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fee record represents a reversal.']
  fee_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version of the fee calculation rule applied.']
  fee_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the fee line within the transaction fee set.']
  fee_settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee was settled.']
  fee_settlement_status varchar [note: 'Settlement state of the fee with respect to the financial ledger.']
  fee_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the fee (e.g., gateway, settlement platform).']
  fee_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the fee.']
  fee_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount applied to the fee, if taxable.']
  fee_taxable_flag boolean [note: 'True if the fee is subject to tax.']
  fee_tier_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the tier applied for tiered fee calculations.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Category of the fee assessed on the transaction.']
  fee_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fee record.']

  Note: 'Fee record associated with a payment transaction, capturing all fee types assessed at the transaction level — interchange fees (IRF), scheme fees, processing fees, gateway fees, and cross-border surcharges. Stores fee type, fee amount, fee currency, fee calculation basis (percentage, flat, tiered), applicable rate, and the party responsible for the fee (merchant, acquirer, issuer). Enables MDR decomposition, fee revenue recognition, and interchange cost reporting.'
}

Table transaction.fx_conversion {
  fx_conversion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each foreign exchange conversion record.']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder/customer involved in the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder/customer involved in the transaction.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the original transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original payment transaction that triggered the FX conversion.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement instruction linked to this conversion.']
  source_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: FX conversion reports must reference the source currency entity to calculate rates and regulatory disclosures.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original payment transaction that triggered the FX conversion.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the conversion record.']
  conversion_fee_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Fee charged for performing the foreign exchange conversion, expressed in the target currency.']
  conversion_fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which the conversion fee is denominated.']
  conversion_method varchar [note: 'How the conversion rate was applied: automatically, manually, or overridden by an operator.']
  conversion_note varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for any additional remarks about the conversion.']
  conversion_reference varchar [note: 'Business reference code assigned to the conversion event for external tracking.']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the conversion record.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the foreign exchange conversion was executed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the conversion record was first created in the system.']
  dcc_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cardholder opted in to Dynamic Currency Conversion; otherwise False.']
  exchange_rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FX rate was sourced, which may differ from conversion_timestamp.']
  fx_rate decimal(18,10) [note: 'Exchange rate applied (target currency per one unit of source currency).']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the underlying transaction is cross‑border; otherwise False.']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the FX rate (payment scheme, internal FX engine, or external provider).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate used for conversion (e.g., mid‑market, DCC, scheme‑provided, internal, third‑party).']
  rate_validity_end date [note: 'Date after which the quoted FX rate is no longer valid (nullable).']
  rate_validity_start date [note: 'Date from which the quoted FX rate is considered valid.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if this conversion must be included in regulatory reports (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  source_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Transaction amount expressed in the source currency before conversion.']
  target_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Transaction amount expressed in the target currency after conversion.']
  target_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency after conversion.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the original payment transaction that triggered the conversion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the conversion record.']

  Note: 'Foreign exchange conversion record for cross-border and multi-currency transactions, capturing the source currency, target currency, FX rate applied, rate type (mid-market, DCC, scheme rate), conversion amount, conversion timestamp, rate source (scheme, internal FX engine, third-party provider), and DCC opt-in/opt-out flag. SSOT for currency conversion events at the transaction level, supporting cross-border payment reporting and DCC revenue tracking.'
}

Table transaction.txn_channel {
  txn_channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each payment acceptance channel.']
  authentication_3ds boolean [note: 'True if the channel supports 3‑D Secure (3DS) for online card‑not‑present transactions.']
  authentication_biometric boolean [note: 'True if the channel supports biometric methods such as fingerprint or facial recognition.']
  authentication_pin boolean [note: 'True if the channel requires a Personal Identification Number (PIN) for authentication.']
  authentication_signature boolean [note: 'True if the channel requires a handwritten signature for transaction approval.']
  card_present boolean [note: 'True if the channel supports card‑present transactions (physical card read).']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the channel based on its delivery mode.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the channel (e.g., POS, EC, NFC).']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment acceptance channel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel record was first created.']
  entry_mode_chip boolean [note: 'True if the channel can accept chip‑based card data.']
  entry_mode_contactless boolean [note: 'True if the channel supports contactless NFC transactions.']
  entry_mode_manual boolean [note: 'True if the channel allows manual entry of card details (e.g., key‑in).']
  entry_mode_swipe boolean [note: 'True if the channel can accept magnetic‑stripe swipe data.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'True if this channel is the default selection for new merchant onboarding.']
  sca_required boolean [note: 'True if Strong Customer Authentication is mandatory for this channel under PSD2.']
  supported_transaction_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of transaction types the channel supports (e.g., card_present, card_not_present, digital_wallet, qr, nfc, moto, atm, kiosk). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: card_present|card_not_present|digital_wallet|qr|nfc|moto|atm|kiosk — promote to reference product]']
  txn_channel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the payment acceptance channel, its typical use cases and any special handling.']
  txn_channel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the channel.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the channel record.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the payment acceptance channels supported by the platform — POS, mPOS, eCommerce, in-app, QR code, NFC contactless, HCE, MOTO (mail order/telephone order), ATM, and unattended kiosk. Each channel record stores channel code, channel name, card-present indicator, authentication requirements (PIN, signature, biometric, 3DS), supported entry modes, and applicable regulatory rules (SCA requirements under PSD2). Used to classify transactions by acceptance environment.'
}

Table transaction.txn_type {
  txn_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each transaction type record.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Enumerated list of regulatory or industry standards applicable to the transaction type.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the transaction type record was first inserted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Default currency in which amounts for this transaction type are expressed.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Specifies if the transaction is a debit (funds out) or credit (funds in).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Calendar date from which the transaction type is valid for processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Calendar date after which the transaction type is no longer valid; null indicates indefinite validity.']
  is_cross_border_allowed boolean [note: 'True when the transaction type may be processed for payments involving different currency jurisdictions.']
  is_fraud_sensitive boolean [note: 'True when transactions of this type trigger enhanced fraud monitoring.']
  is_recurring_allowed boolean [note: 'True when the transaction type can be used for scheduled or subscription payments.']
  iso_processing_code varchar [note: 'Standardized processing code defined by ISO 8583 / ISO 20022 that identifies the transaction function.']
  max_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper limit on the monetary value that can be processed under this transaction type.']
  min_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower bound on the monetary value for this transaction type.']
  network_rule_set varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule set that governs routing, fees, and interchange for the transaction type.']
  processing_fee_fixed decimal(15,2) [note: 'Flat fee amount applied to each transaction of this type.']
  processing_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variable component of the fee expressed as a percent of the transaction amount.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if transactions of this type must be reported to regulators (e.g., SAR, CTR).']
  requires_3ds boolean [note: 'True when 3‑D Secure authentication is required for this transaction type.']
  requires_aml_check boolean [note: 'True when anti‑money‑laundering screening must be performed for this transaction type.']
