  requires_authentication boolean [note: 'True if the transaction must pass an authentication step before authorization.']
  requires_kyc boolean [note: 'True when the transaction type mandates Know‑Your‑Customer checks.']
  risk_score_category varchar [note: 'Categorization used by risk models to apply appropriate scoring thresholds.']
  settlement_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'True when transactions of this type can be settled to a financial institution.']
  transaction_category varchar [note: 'Broad category indicating the channel or medium of the transaction.']
  txn_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the transaction type, including typical use‑cases.']
  txn_type_status varchar [note: 'Operational state indicating whether the type is in active use, retired, or awaiting approval.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Compact alphanumeric code representing the transaction type (e.g., PUR for Purchase).']
  type_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the transaction type as used in UI and reports.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the transaction type record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version identifier used when the definition of the transaction type is updated.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of payment transaction types supported across the platform — purchase, cash advance, balance inquiry, refund, reversal, pre-authorization, incremental authorization, account verification, P2P transfer, A2A transfer, BNPL installment, ACH debit, ACH credit, RTGS, RTP. Each type record stores the ISO processing code, transaction type code, debit/credit indicator, settlement eligibility, and applicable network rules. Provides standardized classification for transaction reporting and routing logic.'
}

Table transaction.transaction_response_code {
  transaction_response_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each response code record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the response code record was first created in the system.']
  decline_reason_classification varchar [note: 'Specific reason why a transaction was declined, aligned with internal analytics taxonomy.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the response code is considered valid for processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the response code should no longer be used (null if indefinite).']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the response code has been deprecated and should be phased out.']
  numeric varchar [note: 'Integer version of the response code for systems that store numeric values.']
  recommended_merchant_action varchar [note: 'Suggested next step for the merchant (e.g., "Retry", "Contact Cardholder", "Check CVV").']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing standard or regulation that defines the response code (e.g., PCI DSS, Visa Core Rules).']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code representing the outcome of an authorization request (e.g., "00", "05").']
  response_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the response code within the processing stack.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the response code based on fraud propensity.']
  scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network or card scheme associated with the response code.']
  transaction_response_code_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the response outcome.']
  transaction_response_code_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of what the response code means (e.g., "Approved", "Do Not Honor").']
  transaction_response_code_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the response code is currently in use.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the response code record.']

  Note: 'Reference master of ISO 8583 and scheme-specific authorization response codes used across the platform. Each record stores the response code value, response code description, response category (approved, declined, referral, error, timeout), decline reason classification (insufficient funds, do not honor, invalid card, expired card, fraud suspect, velocity limit), and recommended merchant action. Enables standardized decline reason reporting and authorization rate analysis.'
}

Table transaction.tps_metric {
  tps_metric_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each TPS metric record.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.transaction. Business justification: Metrics are captured per transaction; adding transaction_id links each TPS metric to its transaction.']
  average_tps decimal(15,2) [note: 'Average transactions per second across the measurement window.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the TPS metric record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the region where the measurement originated.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the TPS measurement was recorded.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for TPS values; always \'tps\' (transactions per second).']
  measurement_window varchar [note: 'Time interval over which the TPS metric is aggregated (e.g., 1 second, 5 seconds, 60 seconds).']
  metric_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the metric record indicating if the value is validated, estimated, or invalid.']
  observed_tps decimal(15,2) [note: 'Number of transactions processed per second observed during the measurement window.']
  payment_rail varchar [note: 'Payment channel or rail on which the transaction flow was processed.']
  peak_tps decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transactions per second recorded within the measurement window.']
  processing_node_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing node or server that generated the measurement.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if the observed TPS fell below the SLA target for the interval; otherwise False.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the TPS metric record.']

  Note: 'Operational throughput metric record capturing Transactions Per Second (TPS) measurements for the payment processing platform at defined intervals. Stores measurement timestamp, measurement window (1s, 5s, 60s), observed TPS, peak TPS, average TPS, processing node identifier, geographic region, payment rail, and SLA breach indicator. Enables capacity planning, SLA compliance monitoring, and peak load analysis for the Transaction Processing Platform.'
}

Table transaction.stp_record {
  stp_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each STP outcome record.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (consumer) involved in the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (consumer) involved in the transaction.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device (e.g., token, POS terminal) that originated the transaction.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that originated the transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment transaction to which this STP record pertains.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical or virtual terminal used for the transaction.']
  risk_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud/compliance rule that triggered manual intervention, if applicable.']
  scheme_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Acquirer Reference Number or other scheme‑assigned transaction identifier.']
  stp_initiated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory compliance: STP exception handling requires tracking which compliance employee initiated the STP event.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch that includes this transaction.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment transaction to which this STP record pertains.']
  acquirer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference assigned by the acquiring bank for reconciliation.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction required compliance review (e.g., AML, sanctions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the STP record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'System error code returned when STP failed.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the error that caused STP failure.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'True if an exception (technical or business) prevented automatic processing.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was flagged for fraud during processing.']
  intervention_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the manual intervention context.']
  intervention_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why manual intervention was required.']
  intervention_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time when the manual intervention was initiated.']
  intervention_type varchar [note: 'Category of manual action required when STP was not achieved.']
  network varchar [note: 'Payment scheme/network that processed the transaction.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Delivery channel used for the transaction (e.g., web, mobile app, POS).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to fund the transaction.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time when the manual intervention was resolved.']
  risk_score_at_stp decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud/risk score evaluated at the point of STP determination.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was settled to the merchant.']
  stp_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes added by operators regarding the STP outcome.']
  stp_kpi_contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of the transaction amount that contributes to the overall STP KPI.']
  stp_outcome_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the STP status was determined.']
  stp_processing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in milliseconds from transaction receipt to STP determination.']
  stp_rate_contribution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction counts toward the STP KPI rate.']
  stp_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the manual intervention resolution process.']
  stp_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the STP record.']
  stp_status varchar [note: 'Overall STP outcome for the transaction: achieved, required manual intervention, or exception.']
  stp_version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original transaction amount before any fees or adjustments.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code of the transaction amount.']
  transaction_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees (interchange, processing, etc.) applied to the transaction.']
  transaction_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after fees, representing the amount settled to the merchant.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the STP record.']

  Note: 'Straight-Through Processing (STP) outcome record for each transaction, capturing whether the transaction completed end-to-end without manual intervention. Stores STP status (STP achieved, manual intervention required, exception), intervention type if applicable (fraud hold, compliance review, reconciliation break, technical error), intervention timestamp, resolution timestamp, and STP rate contribution flag. Enables STP rate KPI tracking and operational efficiency reporting required by scheme SLAs.'
}

Table transaction.threeds_authentication {
  threeds_authentication_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the 3-D Secure authentication event.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder involved in the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder involved in the transaction.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used for the authentication (e.g., token, device fingerprint).']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment transaction.']
  primary_threeds_ds_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the Directory Server for the authentication transaction.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment transaction.']
  acquirer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the acquiring bank for reconciliation.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction being authenticated.']
  auth_code varchar [note: 'Issuer‑provided authorization code for the transaction.']
  authentication_flow varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the authentication was frictionless or required a challenge.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate the cardholder during the 3-D Secure flow.']
  authentication_result varchar [note: 'Final result of the authentication process.']
  cavv varchar [note: 'Cryptographic authentication value generated by the ACS.']
  challenge_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a challenge was required, requested, or preferred during authentication.']
  challenge_response varchar [note: 'Raw data returned from the challenge step (e.g., one‑time password), stored for audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authentication record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount.']
  eci varchar [note: 'Indicator of the level of authentication risk for the transaction.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the 3-D Secure authentication was performed.']
  exemption_type varchar [note: 'Type of SCA exemption applied, if any, for this authentication.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was flagged for potential fraud at authentication time.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the authentication request originated.']
  is_test_transaction boolean [note: 'True if the authentication pertains to a test or sandbox transaction.']
  liability_shift boolean [note: 'True when liability for fraud shifts to the issuer as a result of successful authentication.']
  network varchar [note: 'Card scheme or payment network used for the transaction.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk score assigned by the fraud engine at the time of authentication.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that generated the authentication event (e.g., payment_gateway).']
  threeds_authentication_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the authentication attempt.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the authentication record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version of the 3-D Secure protocol used for this authentication.']

  Note: '3-D Secure authentication record for card-not-present transactions, capturing the full SCA challenge and authentication outcome. Stores 3DS version (1.0, 2.1, 2.2), authentication status (authenticated, attempted, failed, not enrolled, decoupled), ECI (Electronic Commerce Indicator), authentication value (CAVV/AAV), DS transaction ID, ACS transaction ID, challenge indicator, exemption type applied (TRA, low-value, merchant-initiated, SCA exemption), and liability shift indicator. SSOT for 3DS authentication events.'
}

Table transaction.tokenized_txn {
  tokenized_txn_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the tokenized transaction record.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant acquiring the transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment transaction as recorded in the core transaction system.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal used for the transaction.']
  token_requestor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity (e.g., merchant) that requested the token.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment transaction as recorded in the core transaction system.']
  acquirer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the acquiring bank for reconciliation.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original transaction amount before fees, discounts, or adjustments.']
  auth_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuer approving the transaction.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Logical channel through which the payment was initiated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tokenized transaction record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the transaction currency.']
  device_binding_reference varchar [note: 'Reference linking the token to the specific device used for tokenization.']
  dpan varchar [note: 'Device‑specific PAN used for the transaction, masked per PCI requirements.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate applied when converting transaction amount to settlement currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees charged by the acquiring network and processor.']
  fpan_masked varchar [note: 'Masked version of the original PAN for audit and reporting.']
  is_fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was flagged as fraudulent.']
  is_test_transaction boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was generated in a test environment.']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'True indicates the transaction used a token rather than a clear PAN.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s business type.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount after fees, taxes, and adjustments have been applied.']
  network varchar [note: 'Card scheme or network that processed the transaction.']
  party_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder or account holder involved in the transaction.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to fund the transaction.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk assessment generated at authorization time.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount transferred to the merchant after fees and adjustments.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the settlement amount is paid.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was settled with the acquiring bank.']
  token varchar [note: 'Token representing the DPAN for the transaction, compliant with EMVCo tokenization.']
  token_assurance_level varchar [note: 'Assurance tier indicating the security strength of the token.']
  token_cryptogram_validation_result varchar [note: 'Result of cryptogram validation performed during token usage.']
  token_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the token is no longer valid.']
  token_service_provider_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the token service provider that issued the token.']
  tokenization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the token was generated for the DPAN.']
  tokenized_txn_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the tokenized transaction.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the real‑world payment event (authorization, capture, etc.).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tokenized transaction record.']

  Note: 'Record linking a payment transaction to its tokenization context when the transaction was initiated using a DPAN (Device Primary Account Number) or network token rather than the physical PAN. Stores the DPAN used, token requestor ID, token assurance level, FPAN reference (masked), TSP identifier, device binding reference, and token cryptogram validation result. Enables token-based transaction traceability and supports TSP reporting requirements under EMVCo tokenization standards.'
}

Table transaction.ach_entry {
  ach_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the ACH entry record.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch that contains this ACH entry.']
  ach_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ACH entry.']
  addenda_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of addenda records attached to this ACH entry.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the ACH entry in the transaction currency.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the ACH entry was originated.']
  compliance_flag_aml boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry passed AML screening.']
  compliance_flag_fraud boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry passed fraud screening.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ACH entry record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the transaction amount.']
  discretionary_data varchar [note: 'Optional data field for originator‑specific information.']
  effective_entry_date date [note: 'Date on which the ACH entry is scheduled to be processed.']
  entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description supplied by the originator for the ACH entry.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total fees assessed on the ACH entry.']
  individual_account_number varchar [note: 'Masked account number of the individual (PII, masked for security).']
  individual_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the account holder (PII).']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this entry is a duplicate of another entry.']
  is_returned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ACH entry has been returned.']
  is_test_transaction boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the entry is a test (non‑production) transaction.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount after fees are deducted (gross amount minus fee_amount).']
  originating_company_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the originating merchant.']
  originating_company_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the company that originated the ACH entry.']
  originating_dfi_routing varchar [note: 'Routing number of the financial institution that originated the ACH entry.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment method used for the entry; always ACH for this product.']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ACH entry was processed by the system.']
  receiving_company_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the receiving bank.']
  receiving_company_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the receiving financial institution.']
  receiving_dfi_routing varchar [note: 'Routing number of the financial institution that receives the ACH entry.']
  regulatory_report_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this entry must be included in regulatory reports (e.g., SAR, CTR).']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating why an ACH entry was returned, if applicable.']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the return reason.']
  return_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return was recorded.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned by fraud detection models at entry creation.']
  sec_code varchar [note: 'SEC code indicating the type of ACH entry (e.g., PPD, CCD).']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the ACH entry settles to the receiving account.']
  trace_number varchar [note: 'Unique NACHA-assigned trace number for the ACH transaction.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the ACH entry is a credit or debit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ACH entry record.']

  Note: 'ACH (Automated Clearing House) transaction entry record for account-to-account and direct payment transactions processed over the ACH network. Stores SEC code (PPD, CCD, WEB, TEL, CIE), NACHA trace number, originating DFI routing number, receiving DFI routing number, individual account number (masked), individual name, transaction amount, effective entry date, settlement date, return reason code if returned, and addenda record count. SSOT for ACH payment events distinct from card-based transactions.'
}

Table transaction.rtp_payment {
  rtp_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key uniquely identifying the RTP payment record.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to initiate the payment.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the payment, if any.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the RTP network for this payment.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal (if applicable).']
  primary_rtp_cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Tokenized identifier of the sender\'s account used for the payment.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement instruction linked to this payment.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch that includes this payment.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the RTP network for this payment.']
  aml_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment triggered AML screening alerts.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the payment in the transaction currency.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was initiated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RTP payment record was created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if currency conversion occurred.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees charged for processing the RTP payment.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment was flagged as potentially fraudulent.']
  funds_availability_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when funds became available to the receiver.']
  initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was initiated by the sender.']
  iso_message_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the ISO 20022 message (e.g., pacs.008) that carries this payment.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code representing the merchant\'s business category.']
  network_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the RTP network (e.g., RTP, FedNow, UPI, Faster Payments).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or remarks about the payment.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to fund the payment.']
  payment_purpose_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the purpose of the payment as defined by ISO 20022.']
  regulatory_report_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the payment must be reported to regulatory authorities (e.g., SAR, CTR).']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for rejection when RTP status is rejected.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score assigned by the fraud engine at initiation.']
  rtp_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the RTP payment.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment is scheduled to settle.']
  stp_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the payment was processed end‑to‑end without manual intervention.']
  tps varchar [note: 'Number of transactions processed per second at the time of this payment.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this RTP payment record.']

  Note: 'Real-Time Payment (RTP) record for instant payment transactions processed over RTP rails (The Clearing House RTP, FedNow, UPI, Faster Payments). Stores RTP network identifier, end-to-end transaction ID, payment initiation timestamp, funds availability timestamp, sender account reference, receiver account reference, payment purpose code, ISO 20022 message reference (pacs.008), RTP status (accepted, rejected, returned), and rejection reason code. SSOT for real-time and instant payment events.'
}

Table transaction.swift_payment {
  swift_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SWIFT payment record.']
  beneficiary_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer receiving the payment.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the settlement instruction generated for this payment.']
  beneficiary_iban varchar [note: 'IBAN of the beneficiary account receiving the funds.']
  beneficiary_institution_bic varchar [note: 'Bank Identifier Code of the beneficiary (receiving) financial institution.']
  beneficiary_institution_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the beneficiary institution.']
  beneficiary_institution_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the beneficiary financial institution.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment instruction was submitted.']
  charges_instruction varchar [note: 'Indicates who bears the transaction charges: Ordering bank (OUR), shared (SHA), or beneficiary (BEN).']
  charges_instruction_details varchar [note: 'Free-text details of charges instruction.']
  compliance_screening_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to AML/KYC screening result associated with the payment.']
  correspondent_bank_chain varchar [note: 'Semicolon-separated list of intermediate banks\' BICs used in the payment routing.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Rate used to convert payment amount to settlement currency if applicable.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees applied to the payment.']
  gpi_tracker_status varchar [note: 'Status of the payment in the SWIFT gpi tracking system.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the payment is cross-border.']
  is_fee_charged_to_beneficiary boolean [note: 'Indicates if fees are charged to the beneficiary (true for BEN).']
  is_test_payment boolean [note: 'Indicates if the payment is a test/simulation.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the SWIFT payment.']
  message_type varchar [note: 'Type of SWIFT message (MT103, MT202, or ISO 20022 pacs.008).']
  ordering_institution_bic varchar [note: 'Bank Identifier Code of the ordering (originating) financial institution.']
  ordering_institution_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the ordering institution.']
  ordering_institution_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the ordering financial institution.']
  originating_customer_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who originated the payment.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the payment in the transaction currency.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the payment amount.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the payment (e.g., credit transfer, wire).']
  payment_purpose varchar [note: 'Narrative purpose of the payment as provided by the originator.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first ingested into the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']
  regulatory_report_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this payment must be reported to regulators.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Aggregated risk score from fraud detection systems.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount after conversion in settlement currency.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the payment will be settled after conversion.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference provided by the originator for reconciliation.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment instruction was created in the originating system.']
  uetr varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier for the payment transaction across the SWIFT network.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment amount is to be settled.']

  Note: 'SWIFT cross-border payment record for international wire transfers processed through the SWIFT network. Stores SWIFT message type (MT103, MT202, pacs.008 ISO 20022), UETR (Unique End-to-End Transaction Reference), BIC of ordering institution, BIC of beneficiary institution, IBAN of beneficiary, correspondent bank chain, payment amount, payment currency, value date, charges instruction (OUR, SHA, BEN), gpi tracker status, and compliance screening reference. SSOT for SWIFT-routed cross-border payment events.'
}

Table transaction.txn_limit_rule {
  txn_limit_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the transaction limit rule.']
  txn_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.txn_type. Business justification: Limit rules apply to specific transaction types; linking provides rule lookup based on type.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the transaction is initiated.']
  contactless_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for contactless (tap‑and‑go) transactions under this rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount permitted for cross‑border transactions governed by this rule.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the rule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the rule expires or is de‑activated (null if indefinite).']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment instrument to which the rule applies.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule overrides other applicable rules when conditions overlap.']
  limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold value for the rule expressed in the limit currency.']
  limit_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule type indicating which limit dimension it governs.']
  limit_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the limit amount.']
  max_amount_per_day decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for the total value of transactions in a single day.']
  max_amount_per_transaction decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upper bound on the monetary value of an individual transaction.']
  max_transactions_per_day varchar [note: 'Velocity limit specifying the maximum number of transactions allowed in a calendar day.']
  max_transactions_per_hour varchar [note: 'Velocity limit specifying the maximum number of transactions allowed in any rolling hour.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts between multiple applicable rules (lower number = higher priority).']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory requirement (e.g., PSD2, PCI DSS) that drives the rule.']
  risk_basis varchar [note: 'Explanation of the risk model or fraud scenario that justifies the rule.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the rule for lookup and configuration management.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rule used for display and reporting.']
  tap_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount for NFC tap transactions (often lower than standard contactless limit).']
  txn_limit_rule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the business purpose and logic of the rule.']
  txn_limit_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last modified the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rule record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the rule definition for change management.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Business rule configuration defining transaction-level limits enforced during authorization — single transaction amount limits, daily cumulative limits, velocity limits (count per hour/day), card-present vs card-not-present limits, cross-border transaction limits, and contactless tap limits. Each rule stores the limit type, limit value, limit currency, applicable instrument type, applicable channel, effective date range, and the regulatory or risk basis for the limit (e.g., PSD2 contactless limit, scheme velocity rule). Owned by the transaction domain as these rules are enforced at authorization time.'
}

Table transaction.pos_entry_mode {
  pos_entry_mode_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each POS entry mode record. _canonical_skip_reason: Inferred role REFERENCE_LOOKUP, no minimum categories required.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate the payment credential.']
  card_present_indicator boolean [note: 'True when the card was physically present during the transaction.']
  chargeback_liability_rule varchar [note: 'Rule that defines which party bears chargeback liability for this entry mode.']
  contactless_flag boolean [note: 'True if the entry mode supports contactless (NFC) capture.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entry mode record was first created in the system.']
  credential_on_file_flag boolean [note: 'True when the payment credential is stored for future recurring or tokenized transactions.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the entry mode definition becomes effective for processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the entry mode definition is retired or superseded (null if still active).']
  entry_mode_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the entry mode (e.g., "EMV Chip Contact", "Magnetic Stripe").']
  entry_mode_type varchar [note: 'Categorized type of entry mode used at the point of interaction.']
  fallback_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry mode can fall back to a lower‑security method if the primary method fails.']
  interchange_qualification_code varchar [note: 'Code used by acquiring networks to qualify interchange rates for this entry mode.']
  iso_8583_de22_code varchar [note: 'ISO 8583 field code that identifies the point-of-service entry mode used for the transaction.']
  moto_flag boolean [note: 'True if the entry mode represents a MOTO transaction (card not present, entered manually).']
  pos_entry_mode_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the entry mode, including any special handling notes.']
  pos_entry_mode_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the entry mode definition.']
  qr_code_flag boolean [note: 'True if the entry mode is QR code scan.']
  risk_score_average decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical average fraud risk score associated with transactions using this entry mode.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the entry mode record.']

  Note: 'Reference master of POS entry modes defining how payment credential data was captured at the point of interaction — EMV chip contact, EMV chip contactless (NFC), magnetic stripe, manual key entry, QR code scan, HCE, credential-on-file, and MOTO. Each entry mode record stores the ISO 8583 DE22 code, entry mode name, card-present indicator, authentication method, fallback eligibility, and chargeback liability rules applicable to that entry mode. Used to classify transactions for interchange qualification and fraud risk assessment.'
}

Table transaction.transaction {
  transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for transaction']
  a2a_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.a2a_product. Business justification: Account‑to‑account transfers are governed by a2a product settings; linking enables settlement and compliance checks.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder (or payer) involved in the transaction.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Links transaction to its accounting period, required for period‑close, trial‑balance generation, and financial reporting.']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch that includes this transaction.']
  bnpl_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bnpl_plan. Business justification: BNPL purchases are tracked against a specific BNPL plan for installment schedules and regulatory reporting.']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.card_program. Business justification: Card program analytics and spend‑control rules need the program identifier for every transaction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Enables cost allocation per transaction for internal reporting and budgeting, used in expense tracking and cost‑center performance reports.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Currency master linkage is essential for settlement, FX conversion, and regulatory currency reporting.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank or payment processor.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Required for posting each transaction to the General Ledger revenue/expense account during journal entry creation, a core accounting process.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Associates each transaction with the owning legal entity for regulatory filing, consolidation, and entity‑level financial statements.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: Linking transaction MCC to reference.mcc enables merchant category analytics and compliance with industry reporting.']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant_account. Business justification: Enables linking each transaction to the merchant’s settlement account used for funds transfer, essential for account‑level reconciliation and audit.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant that originated the transaction.']
  original_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on transaction (original_transaction_id)']
  p2p_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.p2p_product. Business justification: Peer‑to‑peer payments require linking each transaction to the P2P product definition for fee and risk rules.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Required for product‑level reporting, fee calculation and compliance; each transaction must be tied to the specific payment product used.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS or ATM terminal used for the transaction.']
  transaction_response_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.transaction_response_code. Business justification: Response code strings are normalized to transaction_response_code reference for consistency and reporting.']
  txn_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.txn_channel. Business justification: Transaction records store channel as a string; linking to txn_channel provides a controlled list and eliminates redundancy.']
  txn_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.txn_type. Business justification: Transaction type is currently a free‑text field; linking to txn_type enables standardized processing and rule enforcement.']
  virtual_account_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.virtual_account_product. Business justification: Virtual account usage must be recorded per transaction for balance control and regulatory audit.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the end‑user device that originated the transaction.']
  aml_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction passed AML screening.']
  amount_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all fees (interchange, network, service) applied to the transaction.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount authorized before fees, taxes, or discounts.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount settled to the merchant after fees.']
  auth_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuer approving the transaction.']
  auth_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authorization request was processed.']
  capture_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authorized amount was captured.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction met all internal compliance checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the data lake.']
  cross_border_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction involved parties in different countries.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of why the transaction was declined.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency conversion rate applied when transaction involves multiple currencies.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was flagged as fraudulent.']
  is_card_present boolean [note: 'True if the card was physically present at the point of sale.']
  is_contactless boolean [note: 'True if the transaction used a contactless interface (NFC, tap).']
  issuing_institution_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issuing bank or card scheme.']
  pan_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the Primary Account Number (masked for PCI compliance).']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Technical delivery channel for the payment request.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to fund the transaction.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External reference number (e.g., ARN, ISO 8583 Retrieval Reference Number) used to locate the transaction in downstream systems.']
  regulatory_report_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the transaction must be reported to regulators (e.g., SAR, AML).']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when a reversal (void) was executed.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk assessment from the fraud detection platform.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was settled with the acquiring bank.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the settlement process.']
  stp_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was processed without manual intervention.']
  token_used boolean [note: 'True if a token (DPAN) was used instead of the PAN.']
  tps varchar [note: 'Observed TPS rate at the time of processing.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transaction.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction event occurred (e.g., authorization request time).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for transaction. '
}

Table merchant.merchant {
  merchant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant entity. Primary key. System-of-record identifier assigned during merchant onboarding and provisioning.']
  account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT: assigns a dedicated account‑manager employee to a merchant, required for relationship‑manager performance dashboards.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Internal cost allocation and profitability analysis map each merchant to a cost center, supporting budgeting and expense tracking reports.']
  default_acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.acquirer_endpoint. Business justification: Merchants select a primary acquirer endpoint for settlement; this FK drives settlement routing and regulatory reporting.']
  default_interchange_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.interchange_program. Business justification: Merchants may enroll in specific interchange programs (e.g., debit or incentive programs); linking enables program eligibility reporting and rate application.']
  default_irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: Pricing engine needs a default IRF rate category per merchant to calculate fees; merchants are enrolled in a rate category for fee simulation and reporting.']
  default_scheme_fee_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_fee_table. Business justification: Regulatory fee disclosure requires linking each merchant to the scheme fee table that defines applicable scheme fees for its transactions.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the ISO (Independent Sales Organization) partner responsible for merchant acquisition and relationship management. Null for direct merchants. Used for commission calculation and partner performance tracking.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Regulatory consolidation requires each merchant to be linked to its legal entity for tax, KYC, and financial statement reporting.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: Interchange risk and reporting require linking each merchant to the MCC reference for accurate fee and compliance calculations.']
  merchant_reserve_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.reserve_account. Business justification: Merchant may have a rolling reserve account; linking merchant to reserve_account centralizes reserve data and removes duplicated reserve fields from merchant.']
  participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.participant. Business justification: Required for participant onboarding and daily settlement reporting; each merchant must be linked to a settlement participant for AML/KYC and regulatory reporting.']
  payfac_parent_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent Payment Facilitator (PayFac) merchant if this merchant is a sub-merchant operating under a PayFac model. Null for direct merchants and top-level PayFacs. Used for hierarchical reporting and liability management.']
  primary_account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.account_holder. Business justification: Required for corporate settlement reporting: merchant must reference its primary account holder (owner) to allocate fees and generate compliance reports.']
  processing_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Settlement and FX processing depend on the merchant\'s processing currency, which must reference the master currency list for conversion rates and regulatory reporting.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_region. Business justification: Regulatory and data‑residency rules require each merchant to be associated with a processing region.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Merchant onboarding assigns regulatory obligations (e.g., AML, PSD2) to each merchant; linking supports obligation tracking, reporting, and ongoing compliance monitoring.']
  revenue_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue from merchant transactions must be posted to a specific GL account; linking enables automated journal entry generation for compliance and audit.']
  routing_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_endpoint. Business justification: Required for assigning a specific network endpoint for transaction routing during merchant onboarding, enabling routing decisions per merchant.']
  underwriting_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.underwriting_policy. Business justification: Every merchant is assigned an underwriting policy that defines risk limits; this link supports policy enforcement and compliance reporting.']
  activation_date date [note: 'The date when the merchant was activated for live transaction processing. May differ from onboarding_date if there is a testing or approval period. Marks the start of revenue-generating activity.']
  business_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the merchant\'s registered business address (street number and name). Used for KYB verification, regulatory reporting, and correspondence. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  business_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the merchant\'s registered business address (suite, floor, building name). Optional field. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  business_city varchar [note: 'The city or municipality of the merchant\'s registered business address. Used for geographic analysis, tax jurisdiction determination, and regulatory reporting. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  business_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the merchant\'s registered business address. Used for sanctions screening, cross-border rules, and regulatory reporting. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  business_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal code or ZIP code of the merchant\'s registered business address. Used for address verification, tax calculation, and geographic analysis. Format varies by country. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  business_registration_number varchar [note: 'The official business registration or company number issued by the government authority in the incorporation jurisdiction. Used for KYB (Know Your Business) verification and legal entity validation.']
  business_state_province varchar [note: 'The state, province, or administrative region of the merchant\'s registered business address. Used for tax jurisdiction, regulatory compliance, and geographic segmentation. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  business_type varchar [note: 'The legal structure and classification of the merchant business entity. Determines regulatory requirements, tax treatment, and liability structure.']
  chargeback_threshold_count varchar [note: 'The maximum number of chargebacks allowed per month before the merchant is flagged for review or placed in a monitoring program. Exceeding this threshold may result in increased reserves, fines, or termination.']
  chargeback_threshold_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'The maximum allowed chargeback ratio (chargebacks divided by total transactions) expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0100 for 1.00%). Exceeding this threshold triggers card scheme monitoring programs and potential penalties.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this merchant record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record retention compliance. Immutable after initial creation.']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'The trade name or DBA (Doing Business As) name under which the merchant operates and is publicly known. May differ from legal business name. Displayed on cardholder statements.']
  incorporation_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the merchant business is legally incorporated or registered. Used for regulatory compliance, sanctions screening, and cross-border payment rules.']
  kyc_verification_date date [note: 'The date when KYC/KYB verification was successfully completed for the merchant. Used for compliance audit trails and periodic re-verification scheduling.']
  kyc_verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the KYC/KYB (Know Your Business) verification process for the merchant. Indicates whether identity verification, business documentation, and beneficial ownership checks have been completed and approved.']
  legal_business_name varchar [note: 'The official registered legal name of the merchant business entity as recorded in incorporation or business registration documents. Used for regulatory reporting, compliance verification, and legal contracts.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the merchant in the payment platform lifecycle. Governs transaction processing eligibility, settlement, and platform access. Transitions managed through merchant lifecycle workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: application|pending_review|approved|active|suspended|terminated|offboarded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mdr_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage rate charged to the merchant for payment processing services, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0275 for 2.75%). Represents the merchant\'s cost of acceptance. Varies by card type, transaction type, and merchant volume tier.']
  mid varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique Merchant Identification Number (MID) assigned by the acquiring bank or payment processor. Used for transaction routing, authorization, settlement, and reporting. Distinct from internal merchant_id surrogate key.']
  monthly_processing_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum total payment volume (in processing currency) that the merchant is authorized to process per calendar month. Used for risk management and fraud prevention. Exceeding this limit may trigger holds or additional review.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'The date when the merchant was successfully onboarded to the payment platform and assigned a MID. Marks the start of the merchant relationship and eligibility for transaction processing.']
  operating_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country where the merchant conducts business operations. May differ from incorporation country for multinational merchants.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the primary business contact for the merchant account. Used for operational notifications, settlement reports, dispute alerts, and compliance communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the primary business contact person for the merchant account. Used for operational communication, support escalation, and account management.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number of the primary business contact for the merchant account. Used for urgent operational communication, fraud alerts, and account verification. Format varies by country.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'The risk classification assigned to the merchant based on MCC, transaction patterns, chargeback history, geographic location, and business model. Determines monitoring intensity, reserve requirements, and processing limits.']
  settlement_account_number varchar [note: 'The bank account number where merchant settlement funds are deposited. Encrypted at rest. Used for ACH, wire transfer, or RTGS settlement processing. Format varies by country and payment rail.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which settled funds are transferred to the merchant\'s bank account. Configured based on merchant agreement, risk profile, and processing volume.']
  settlement_routing_number varchar [note: 'The bank routing number, sort code, or SWIFT/BIC code for the merchant\'s settlement account. Used to route settlement funds to the correct financial institution. Format varies by jurisdiction (e.g., ABA routing number in USA, sort code in UK, SWIFT BIC internationally).']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'The government-issued tax identification number for the merchant business entity. Format varies by jurisdiction (e.g., EIN in USA, VAT number in EU, GST number in India). Used for tax reporting, regulatory compliance, and identity verification.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date when the merchant relationship was terminated and the merchant was offboarded from the payment platform. Nullable for active merchants. Used for retention analysis and regulatory record-keeping.']
  transaction_limit_single decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum amount (in processing currency) allowed for a single transaction. Transactions exceeding this limit are declined at authorization. Set based on merchant risk profile and business model.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this merchant record was last modified. Updated automatically on any field change. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'The primary website URL of the merchant business. Used for business verification, fraud screening, and customer service reference. Required for e-commerce merchants.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a merchant business entity onboarded to the payments platform. Owns the authoritative merchant identity record including legal business name, DBA name, MID (Merchant Identification Number), business registration details, tax identification, business type, incorporation country, operational status, and lifecycle stage from application through offboarding. SSOT for all merchant identity data.'
}

Table merchant.application {
  application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant application record. Primary key for the merchant application entity.']
  assigned_underwriter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underwriting analyst assigned to review this merchant application. Used for workload distribution and accountability tracking.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ISO partner who referred or manages this merchant relationship. Used for commission calculation and relationship management.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underwriting analyst assigned to review this merchant application. Used for workload distribution and accountability tracking.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent Payment Facilitator if this is a sub-merchant application. Establishes hierarchical relationship for settlement and liability.']
  parent_payfac_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent Payment Facilitator if this is a sub-merchant application. Establishes hierarchical relationship for settlement and liability.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique application reference number assigned at submission. Used for tracking and customer communication throughout the onboarding lifecycle.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the merchant application. Tracks progression from initial draft through underwriting review to final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|pending_documentation|approved|rejected|withdrawn|expired — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the application purpose: new merchant onboarding, change to existing merchant profile, reinstatement after termination, upgrade or downgrade of service tier, or reactivation of dormant account.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number for merchant settlement deposits. Used for ACH credit transfers of processed transaction funds. Subject to account verification before approval.']
  bank_account_type varchar [note: 'Type of bank account designated for settlement. Determines ACH transaction codes and processing rules.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit ABA routing number for the merchant\'s settlement bank. Used for ACH settlement processing and account verification.']
  business_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the merchant\'s principal place of business. Used for KYB verification and AVS validation.']
  business_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, unit, or building information. Optional field for complete business address.']
  business_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the merchant\'s principal place of business.']
  business_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the merchant\'s principal place of business. Used for cross-border risk assessment and sanctions screening.']
  business_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the merchant\'s business model, products sold, services offered, and target customer base. Used by underwriters to assess risk and validate MCC classification.']
  business_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the merchant\'s business address. Used for AVS verification and geographic risk assessment.']
  business_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the merchant\'s business address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination and regulatory compliance.']
  business_type varchar [note: 'Legal structure of the merchant entity. Determines underwriting requirements, liability assessment, and documentation checklist.']
  business_website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for the merchant\'s business. Used for business verification, fraud screening, and brand reputation assessment.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact. Used for application status notifications and ongoing merchant communication.']
  contact_first_name varchar [note: 'First name of the primary business contact or authorized representative for the merchant application.']
  contact_last_name varchar [note: 'Last name of the primary business contact or authorized representative for the merchant application.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the business contact. Used for verification calls and urgent communication during underwriting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the merchant application record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_check_performed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a credit bureau check was performed on the business owner. Required for most merchant applications per underwriting policy.']
  credit_score varchar [note: 'Personal credit score of the principal business owner obtained from credit bureau. Used for creditworthiness assessment and pricing determination.']
  decision_code varchar [note: 'Final underwriting decision outcome. Determines next steps in merchant onboarding workflow and triggers downstream provisioning or notification processes.']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the underwriting decision including risk factors considered, policy violations identified, or conditions imposed. Used for adverse action notices and audit trail.']
  documentation_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required documentation has been received and validated. Gates progression to final approval stage.']
  doing_business_as_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or DBA name under which the merchant operates. May differ from legal name and appears on customer-facing materials.']
  estimated_average_ticket decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected average transaction amount in base currency. Used for fraud risk modeling and chargeback exposure assessment.']
  estimated_monthly_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Merchant\'s projected monthly payment processing volume in base currency. Used for risk assessment, pricing tier determination, and reserve calculation.']
  kyb_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of KYB business entity verification including legal registration, business license, and beneficial ownership validation. Required for regulatory compliance.']
  kyc_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of KYC identity verification process for business owners and authorized signers. Must pass before final approval per AML regulations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the merchant application record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  legal_business_name varchar [note: 'Official registered legal name of the merchant entity as it appears on incorporation documents. Used for KYB (Know Your Business) verification and regulatory reporting.']
  ofac_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of OFAC sanctions list screening for business entity and principals. Must be clear before approval to comply with US sanctions regulations.']
  owner_date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the principal business owner. Used for identity verification and age validation during KYC process.']
  owner_first_name varchar [note: 'First name of the principal business owner or majority stakeholder. Required for KYC verification and beneficial ownership identification per FinCEN rules.']
  owner_last_name varchar [note: 'Last name of the principal business owner or majority stakeholder. Required for KYC verification and beneficial ownership identification per FinCEN rules.']
  owner_ssn varchar [note: 'Social Security Number of the principal business owner. Required for personal credit check, identity verification, and OFAC screening during underwriting.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of business ownership held by the principal owner. Used to determine beneficial ownership reporting requirements per FinCEN 25% threshold rule.']
  payfac_sub_merchant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this application is for a sub-merchant under a Payment Facilitator model. Determines underwriting requirements and settlement hierarchy.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Source of the merchant application referral (e.g., direct, partner, web, sales_rep). Used for marketing attribution and channel performance analysis.']
  requested_mcc varchar [note: 'Four-digit MCC code requested by the merchant that classifies their business type. Subject to underwriter validation and may be adjusted based on actual business model. Determines interchange rates and risk profile.']
  requested_processing_methods varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment processing methods requested by merchant (e.g., card_present, card_not_present, ecommerce, moto, recurring). Determines equipment needs and risk profile.']
  required_documentation_checklist varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of required documents for application completion (e.g., articles_of_incorporation, bank_statement, voided_check, government_id). Varies by business type and risk profile.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk assessment score assigned during underwriting. Combines credit risk, fraud risk, and business risk factors. Used for pricing tier assignment and reserve determination.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the merchant application was submitted. Used for channel performance analysis and fraud pattern detection.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the merchant application was formally submitted for underwriting review. Represents the business event timestamp for SLA tracking.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Federal tax identification number (EIN for businesses, SSN for sole proprietors). Used for IRS reporting, KYB verification, and OFAC screening.']
  underwriting_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when underwriting review was completed and final decision was rendered. Used for SLA compliance reporting and cycle time analysis.']
  underwriting_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the application entered underwriting review. Used for SLA tracking and process efficiency measurement.']

  Note: 'Captures the full merchant onboarding application lifecycle — from initial submission through underwriting review, KYC/KYB verification, and final approval or rejection. Tracks application type (new, change, reinstatement), submission channel, assigned underwriter, decision rationale, required documentation checklist, and timestamps for each stage. Source of record for the Merchant Management System onboarding workflow.'
}

Table merchant.merchant_location {
  merchant_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant location. Primary key.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Merchant location needs a proper link to the country master to support geo‑analytics and compliance checks. Add location_country_id (FK to reference.country.country_id) and retire the raw country_code']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent merchant entity that owns this location.']
  merchant_settlement_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement account where funds for transactions at this location are deposited.']
  processing_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Location‑specific processing may use a different currency; linking to the currency reference supports localized settlement rules.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when this location was activated and began processing live transactions.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the merchant location (street number, street name, suite/unit).']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details (building, floor, department).']
  chargeback_threshold_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of chargebacks allowed per monitoring period before triggering compliance review or location suspension.']
  chargeback_threshold_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Maximum chargeback-to-transaction ratio allowed (e.g., 0.0090 for 0.9%) before triggering compliance action per card scheme rules.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the merchant location is situated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this merchant location record was first created in the system.']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total transaction volume allowed per day for this location, used for risk management and fraud prevention.']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'The trade name or \'Doing Business As\' name under which this location operates, if different from the legal merchant name.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when this location was deactivated or closed, ceasing transaction processing.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for location-specific communications and notifications.']
  fraud_monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time fraud detection and prevention monitoring is enabled for transactions at this location.']
  kyc_verification_date date [note: 'Date when KYC/KYB verification was completed for this location.']
  kyc_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of Know Your Customer (KYC) and Know Your Business (KYB) verification for this location: verified, pending review, failed verification, or expired requiring re-verification.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent transaction processed at this location, used for dormancy monitoring and lifecycle management.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the merchant location in decimal degrees for mapping and proximity analytics.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Business name or identifier for this specific merchant location (e.g., \'Downtown Store\', \'Airport Terminal 3\').']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the merchant location in its lifecycle: active (processing transactions), inactive (temporarily not processing), suspended (compliance hold), pending (onboarding), or closed (permanently offboarded).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the merchant location type: physical storefront, kiosk, online, mobile Point of Sale (mPOS), virtual, or pop-up.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the merchant location in decimal degrees for mapping and proximity analytics.']
  mcc varchar [note: 'Four-digit Merchant Category Code (MCC) classification for this location, which may override the parent merchant MCC for location-specific business activities.']
  mid varchar [note: 'Unique Merchant Identification Number (MID) assigned to this location by the acquiring bank or payment processor for transaction routing and settlement.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when this merchant location was successfully onboarded and provisioned in the payment processing system.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the location (e.g., \'Mon-Fri 9AM-5PM, Sat 10AM-2PM\') used for customer service and transaction pattern analysis.']
  pci_attestation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent PCI DSS compliance attestation or Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) completion for this location.']
  pci_compliance_level varchar [note: 'PCI DSS compliance level assigned to this location based on annual transaction volume: Level 1 (>6M), Level 2 (1M-6M), Level 3 (20K-1M), Level 4 (<20K).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the merchant location.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the merchant location address.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current risk assessment score for this location (0-100 scale) based on transaction patterns, chargeback history, fraud indicators, and compliance factors.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification tier for this location: low, medium, high, or critical, determining monitoring intensity and processing controls.']
  single_transaction_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for a single transaction at this location, enforced during authorization.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the merchant location.']
  three_ds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3-D Secure (3DS) authentication is mandatory for card-not-present transactions at this location, per PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) or merchant policy.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the merchant location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London) used for transaction timestamp normalization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this merchant location record was last modified.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Website URL specific to this merchant location, if applicable (e.g., location-specific landing page).']

  Note: 'Represents a physical or virtual merchant outlet location associated with a merchant entity. Stores location name, address (street, city, state, postal code, country), location type (physical storefront, kiosk, online, mPOS), operating hours, geo-coordinates, timezone, and location-level MCC override. A single merchant may operate multiple locations each with independent settlement and terminal configurations.'
}

Table merchant.merchant_contact {
  merchant_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant contact record. Primary key.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant entity this contact is associated with. Links contact to the parent merchant record for relationship management.']
  can_receive_dispute_notifications boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact should receive chargeback and dispute notifications. Enables targeted communication for time-sensitive dispute response requirements.']
  can_receive_pci_data boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is authorized to receive communications containing Payment Card Industry (PCI) sensitive data. Ensures PCI DSS compliance in data transmission.']
  can_receive_settlement_reports boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact should receive settlement and funding reports. Used for financial reconciliation and treasury management communications.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the merchant contact record. Determines whether the contact should receive communications and appear in active contact lists.']
