  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact role within the merchant organization. Determines the purpose and scope of communication with this contact.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this contact record. Supports audit trail and accountability for data governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business unit or department within the merchant organization where the contact works. Used for routing communications to appropriate functional areas.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when this contact record became active and valid for the merchant relationship. Supports temporal tracking and audit requirements.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when this contact record ceased to be active for the merchant relationship. Null for currently active contacts. Supports historical tracking and compliance audit trails.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for electronic communication with the merchant contact. Used for transaction notifications, dispute correspondence, and operational alerts.']
  email_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has opted out of marketing or non-essential email communications. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Facsimile number for document transmission. Used for formal dispute documentation and regulatory correspondence requiring written records.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the merchant contact person. Used for personalized communication and identification.']
  is_authorized_signer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has legal authority to sign contracts and agreements on behalf of the merchant. Critical for KYC and contract execution workflows.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the merchant. Used to prioritize communication routing and escalation paths.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Professional title or position of the contact within the merchant organization. Indicates authority level and functional responsibility.']
  kyc_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current KYC verification expires and re-verification is required. Supports periodic review requirements under AML and CTF regulations.']
  kyc_verification_date date [note: 'Date when KYC verification was completed for this contact. Used to track verification currency and trigger re-verification workflows per regulatory requirements.']
  kyc_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of identity verification and KYC checks for this contact. Required for compliance with AML and CTF regulations for authorized signers and key contacts.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent communication or interaction with this contact. Used for relationship management and contact data quality maintenance.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this contact record. Supports audit trail and accountability for data changes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this contact record. Critical for change tracking, audit trails, and data synchronization.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the merchant contact person. Used for formal identification and communication.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the merchant contact person. Optional field for complete name representation.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile or cellular phone number for SMS notifications and mobile communication. Used for time-sensitive alerts and two-factor authentication.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information, special instructions, or relationship notes about the contact. Supports relationship management and customer service quality.']
  phone_extension varchar [note: 'Internal extension number for direct routing within the merchant organization phone system. Enables direct contact bypass of main switchboard.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for voice communication with the merchant contact. Used for urgent matters, dispute resolution, and relationship management.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'Contact\'s preferred method for receiving communications from the payment processor. Ensures compliance with communication preferences and improves response rates.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code indicating the contact\'s preferred language for communication. Supports multilingual merchant base and regulatory compliance in international markets.']
  secondary_email_address varchar [note: 'Alternate email address for backup communication. Used when primary email is unavailable or for redundant critical notifications.']
  sms_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has opted out of SMS or text message communications. Required for compliance with telecommunications regulations and privacy laws.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that originated this contact record. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system integration scenarios.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this contact in the source operational system. Enables cross-system reconciliation and bidirectional data synchronization.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for the current contact status. Provides audit trail for status changes and supports compliance documentation.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the contact\'s location. Used for scheduling communications during business hours and timestamping interactions appropriately.']

  Note: 'Stores named contacts associated with a merchant entity — including primary business contact, billing contact, technical integration contact, and dispute contact. Captures full name, title, role type, email, phone, preferred communication channel, and contact status. Supports multi-contact management per merchant for relationship and operational communications.'
}

Table merchant.kyb_verification {
  kyb_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the KYB verification record. Primary key for the KYB verification entity.']
  compliance_kyc_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_kyc_verification. Business justification: KYB verification results are derived from detailed compliance KYC verification records; linking provides traceability for regulatory audits and risk assessments.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance officer or user who performed manual review of the KYB verification results.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant entity undergoing KYB verification. Links this verification to the merchant master record.']
  reviewer_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance officer or user who performed manual review of the KYB verification results.']
  adverse_media_check_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of adverse media screening to identify negative news, criminal activity, fraud, or reputational risks associated with the business entity.']
  aml_screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of Anti-Money Laundering screening checks to identify potential money laundering risks or suspicious activity patterns.']
  business_identity_check_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the business entity identity verification check confirming legal existence, registration status, and business name match against official registries.']
  business_legal_name varchar [note: 'Verified legal name of the business entity as registered with government authorities and confirmed during KYB verification.']
  business_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official government-issued business registration or incorporation number verified during the KYB process (e.g., EIN, Company Registration Number).']
  business_type varchar [note: 'Legal structure type of the business entity verified during KYB (e.g., corporation, limited liability company, partnership, sole proprietorship).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the KYB verification record was first created in the system, marking the initiation of the verification process.']
  ctf_screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of Counter-Terrorism Financing screening checks to identify potential terrorism financing risks or connections to terrorist organizations.']
  data_sources_used varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of data sources and registries consulted during KYB verification (e.g., Companies House, Dun & Bradstreet, OFAC SDN, Dow Jones Watchlist).']
  document_types_verified varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of document types that were verified during the KYB process (e.g., articles of incorporation, business license, tax ID certificate, bank statement).']
  document_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of business document verification including articles of incorporation, business licenses, tax certificates, and other supporting documentation.']
  incorporation_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the business entity is legally incorporated or registered.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date when the business entity was officially incorporated or registered as verified during KYB checks.']
  manual_review_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why manual compliance review is required, detailing specific alerts, inconclusive checks, or policy triggers.']
  manual_review_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator that the KYB verification results require manual review by compliance officers due to inconclusive results, risk alerts, or policy exceptions.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic KYB re-verification or refresh based on risk tier and regulatory requirements for ongoing due diligence.']
  ofac_screening_result varchar [note: 'Specific outcome of screening against the US Office of Foreign Assets Control Specially Designated Nationals and Blocked Persons List.']
  overall_kyb_decision varchar [note: 'Final pass or fail decision for the KYB verification determining whether the merchant is approved for onboarding, rejected, conditionally approved, or requires manual review.']
  pep_screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of screening to identify if the business or its beneficial owners are politically exposed persons requiring enhanced due diligence.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the KYB verification was rejected or failed, including specific check failures and compliance concerns.']
  review_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when manual compliance review of the KYB verification was completed and final decision was rendered.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Internal compliance notes and comments recorded by the reviewer during manual review of the KYB verification, documenting decision rationale.']
  risk_factors varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific risk factors identified during KYB verification (e.g., high-risk MCC, high-risk jurisdiction, adverse media, PEP association).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score calculated by the verification provider based on all KYB checks, typically ranging from 0 to 100 with higher scores indicating greater risk.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification tier assigned to the merchant based on KYB verification results, determining the level of ongoing monitoring and due diligence required.']
  sanctions_screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of sanctions and watchlist screening against OFAC, UN, EU, and other global sanctions lists to identify prohibited business relationships.']
  ubo_count varchar [note: 'Number of Ultimate Beneficial Owners identified and verified during the KYB process who meet the ownership or control threshold.']
  ubo_screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the Ultimate Beneficial Owner screening check verifying individuals who own or control 25% or more of the business entity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the KYB verification record was last modified, tracking changes to verification status, results, or review outcomes.']
  verification_cost_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost charged by the verification provider for performing the KYB verification checks, used for cost tracking and merchant billing.']
  verification_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the verification cost amount.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the KYB verification was performed and results were received from the verification provider.']
  verification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current KYB verification expires and requires renewal to maintain merchant compliance status.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the KYB verification indicating whether checks were fully automated, manual, or a hybrid combination.']
  verification_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party KYB verification service provider that performed the business identity and compliance checks (e.g., Refinitiv, LexisNexis, Dow Jones).']
  verification_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the verification provider for tracking and audit purposes.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KYB verification process indicating whether checks are pending, in progress, completed successfully, failed, expired, or require manual review.']

  Note: 'Records the Know Your Business (KYB) verification outcome for a merchant entity. Captures verification provider, verification date, business identity check result, UBO (Ultimate Beneficial Owner) screening result, sanctions/OFAC screening result, adverse media check, document verification status, risk tier assigned, and overall KYB pass/fail decision. Supports AML and regulatory compliance requirements for merchant onboarding.'
}

Table merchant.beneficial_owner {
  beneficial_owner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the beneficial owner record. Primary key for the beneficial owner entity.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant entity for which this beneficial owner record applies. Links the UBO to the merchant undergoing KYC/KYB verification.']
  adverse_media_screening_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the most recent adverse media screening was performed on the beneficial owner.']
  adverse_media_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of adverse media screening to identify negative news or reputational risks associated with the beneficial owner. Part of enhanced due diligence process.']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall AML risk rating assigned to the beneficial owner based on risk factors including PEP status, sanctions screening, country risk, and ownership structure.']
  beneficial_owner_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the beneficial owner record. Indicates whether the record is active, under review, or has been rejected due to compliance issues.']
  control_role varchar [note: 'Role or position held by the beneficial owner that grants significant control over the merchant entity. Required for FinCEN CDD Rule compliance to identify individuals with control authority. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: director|officer|shareholder|trustee|beneficiary|authorized_signatory|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  control_role_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the beneficial owner\'s control role and responsibilities within the merchant entity. Provides context for the nature of control exercised.']
  country_of_residence varchar [note: 'Country where the beneficial owner currently resides using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Used for tax reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the beneficial owner record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the beneficial owner in yyyy-MM-dd format. Required for identity verification, age validation, and AML screening under FinCEN CDD Rule.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the beneficial owner relationship with the merchant entity became effective. Used for temporal tracking of ownership changes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when the beneficial owner relationship with the merchant entity ended. Null for currently active beneficial owners.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name or first name of the beneficial owner. Used for identity verification and screening purposes.']
  full_legal_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the beneficial owner as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Required for BSA/AML compliance and identity verification.']
  government_id_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the government-issued identification document. Used to ensure validity of identity verification.']
  government_id_issuing_country varchar [note: 'Country that issued the government identification document using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.']
  government_id_number varchar [note: 'Unique identification number from the government-issued document. Encrypted at rest and used for identity verification and AML screening.']
  government_id_type varchar [note: 'Type of government-issued identification document provided by the beneficial owner for identity verification. Required under FinCEN CDD Rule.']
  identity_verification_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the beneficial owner\'s identity was successfully verified. Required for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  identity_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the identity of the beneficial owner. Supports multiple verification channels to meet regulatory requirements.']
  identity_verification_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party service or internal system used to perform identity verification. Used for audit trail and vendor management.']
  identity_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the identity verification process for the beneficial owner. Indicates whether identity has been successfully verified, is pending review, or has failed verification checks.']
  is_pep boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the beneficial owner is a Politically Exposed Person. PEPs require enhanced due diligence under FATF recommendations and AML regulations.']
  kyc_refresh_due_date date [note: 'Date when the beneficial owner KYC information is due for periodic refresh and re-verification. Required for ongoing due diligence under AML regulations.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Surname or family name of the beneficial owner. Required for identity verification and AML screening.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the beneficial owner. Optional field for enhanced identity matching.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Country of citizenship of the beneficial owner using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Required for sanctions screening and cross-border compliance.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership stake held by the beneficial owner in the merchant entity. FinCEN CDD Rule requires disclosure for owners with 25% or more equity interest.']
  pep_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the PEP status indicating whether the individual is a domestic PEP, foreign PEP, member of international organization, family member, or close associate of a PEP.']
  pep_position_title varchar [note: 'Title or position held by the beneficial owner that qualifies them as a PEP. Examples include government official, senior executive of state-owned enterprise, or high-ranking military officer.']
  residential_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the beneficial owner\'s residential address. Required for identity verification and KYC compliance.']
  residential_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number of the beneficial owner\'s residential address.']
  residential_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the beneficial owner\'s residential address.']
  residential_country varchar [note: 'Country of the beneficial owner\'s residential address using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.']
  residential_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the beneficial owner\'s residential address.']
  residential_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the beneficial owner\'s residential address.']
  sanctions_match_details varchar [note: 'Details of any sanctions list matches including list name, match score, and reason for match. Used for investigation and resolution of potential sanctions violations.']
  sanctions_screening_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the most recent sanctions screening was performed on the beneficial owner. Required for audit trail and compliance monitoring.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening against OFAC, UN, EU, and other sanctions lists. Indicates whether the beneficial owner is clear, has a match, or requires further review.']
  source_of_funds varchar [note: 'Description of the origin of funds used by the beneficial owner in the merchant relationship. Required for enhanced due diligence and AML compliance.']
  source_of_wealth varchar [note: 'Description of how the beneficial owner accumulated their wealth. Required for enhanced due diligence on high-risk individuals and PEPs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the beneficial owner record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Captures the Ultimate Beneficial Owner (UBO) records for a merchant entity as required by BSA/AML regulations. Stores owner full name, date of birth, nationality, government ID type and number, ownership percentage, control role (director, officer, shareholder), PEP (Politically Exposed Person) flag, sanctions screening status, and identity verification outcome. Regulatory requirement for FinCEN CDD Rule compliance.'
}

Table merchant.merchant_account {
  merchant_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant processing account. Primary key.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent merchant entity that owns this processing account.']
  processing_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Each account may operate in a specific currency; linking to the currency reference enables correct fee schedules and cross‑border rules.']
  reserve_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to settlement.reserve_account.reserve_account_id — MUST-HAVE: After removing merchant.reserve_account (SSOT violation), merchant.merchant_account needs FK to settlement.reserve_account to maintain the merchant-reserve relationship. Enables merchant re']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique account number assigned to this processing account for identification in statements and reports.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the merchant processing account. Active accounts can process transactions; suspended/restricted accounts have limited processing; closed accounts are terminated.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the processing account model: standard merchant account, Payment Facilitator (PayFac) sub-merchant account, Independent Sales Organization (ISO) account, aggregator account, marketplace account, or direct acquiring account.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the merchant account was activated and became eligible to process transactions.']
  avs_check_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Address Verification System checks are enabled for card-not-present transactions to validate cardholder billing address.']
  batch_settlement_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account uses batch settlement processing where transactions are grouped and settled in scheduled batches rather than real-time settlement.']
  card_not_present_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is authorized to process card-not-present transactions such as e-commerce, mail order, or telephone order (MOTO) payments.']
  card_present_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is authorized to process card-present transactions via Point of Sale (POS) or mobile Point of Sale (mPOS) terminals.']
  chargeback_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable chargeback ratio (chargebacks divided by total transactions) expressed as a percentage. Exceeding this threshold may trigger account review or restrictions per card scheme monitoring programs.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the merchant account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this merchant account record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_processing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is authorized to process cross-border transactions where the cardholder and merchant are in different countries.']
  cvv_check_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Card Verification Value (CVV/CVV2/CVC) validation is enabled for card-not-present transactions to verify card possession.']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total transaction volume (in processing currency) that this account is permitted to process within a single calendar day. Used for risk management and velocity controls.']
  dynamic_currency_conversion_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is enabled for Dynamic Currency Conversion, allowing cardholders to see and pay in their home currency at the point of sale.']
  enhanced_monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is subject to enhanced transaction monitoring due to elevated risk profile, regulatory requirements, or past compliance issues.']
  fraud_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable fraud ratio (fraudulent transactions divided by total transactions) expressed as a percentage. Exceeding this threshold may trigger enhanced fraud controls or account restrictions.']
  installment_payment_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account supports installment payment plans where a single purchase is split into multiple scheduled payments.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this merchant account record was last updated or modified.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent transaction processed through this merchant account. Used for account activity monitoring and dormancy detection.']
  monthly_transaction_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total transaction volume (in processing currency) that this account is permitted to process within a single calendar month. Used for risk management and underwriting controls.']
  multi_currency_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is configured to accept and process transactions in multiple currencies beyond the primary processing currency.']
  pci_compliance_level varchar [note: 'PCI DSS compliance validation level assigned to this merchant account based on annual transaction volume: Level 1 (>6M transactions), Level 2 (1M-6M), Level 3 (20K-1M), Level 4 (<20K).']
  pci_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI DSS compliance status of the merchant account. Non-compliant accounts may face processing restrictions or higher fees.']
  pci_validation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent PCI DSS compliance validation or attestation for this merchant account.']
  real_time_settlement_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account supports real-time or near-real-time settlement where funds are transferred to the merchant immediately or within minutes of transaction authorization.']
  recurring_billing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is configured to support recurring or subscription-based billing transactions.']
  reserve_account_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this merchant account is required to maintain a reserve account (holdback) to cover potential chargebacks, refunds, or other liabilities.']
  reserve_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction volume withheld in reserve account to cover potential chargebacks and refunds. Null if no reserve is required.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification tier assigned to this merchant account based on underwriting assessment, industry category, transaction patterns, and fraud/chargeback history.']
  sca_exemption_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is permitted to apply Strong Customer Authentication exemptions for low-risk or low-value transactions under PSD2 regulations.']
  settlement_delay_days varchar [note: 'Number of business days between transaction capture and settlement disbursement to the merchant. Standard is T+1 or T+2; higher-risk accounts may have longer delays.']
  single_transaction_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount (in processing currency) permitted for a single transaction on this account. Transactions exceeding this limit will be declined.']
  supported_card_schemes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of card payment networks enabled for this account (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, JCB, UnionPay, Diners).']
  supported_payment_methods varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment methods enabled for this account (e.g., card_present, card_not_present, digital_wallet, ach, bank_transfer, qr_code, bnpl).']
  three_ds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3-D Secure authentication is mandatory for card-not-present transactions on this account to comply with Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) requirements.']
  three_ds_version varchar [note: 'Version of the 3-D Secure authentication protocol configured for this account (1.0, 2.0, 2.1, 2.2).']
  tokenization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payment tokenization is enabled for this account to replace sensitive Primary Account Number (PAN) data with non-sensitive tokens.']

  Note: 'Represents the processing account configuration for a merchant — the operational account that governs transaction processing parameters. Stores account status, processing currency, accepted card schemes (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover), enabled payment methods, 3DS configuration, SCA requirements, AVS/CVV policy settings, transaction velocity limits, and account-level risk controls. Distinct from the merchant settlement bank account.'
}

Table merchant.mcc_assignment {
  mcc_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MCC assignment record. Primary key for the MCC assignment entity.']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or manager who approved this MCC assignment, if a separate approval step is required. Nullable if no approval workflow exists or approval is pending.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system operator who created or approved this MCC assignment. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system operator who last modified this MCC assignment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant to whom this MCC is assigned. Links to the merchant master record.']
  merchant_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific merchant location or outlet for which this MCC is assigned. Nullable if MCC applies to the entire merchant entity rather than a specific location.']
  primary_mcc_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system operator who created or approved this MCC assignment. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  tertiary_mcc_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system operator who last modified this MCC assignment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the MCC assignment was approved by the designated approver. Nullable if approval is not required or has not yet occurred.']
  assigning_authority varchar [note: 'Entity that assigned or approved the MCC. Can be a card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) or the acquiring bank/payment facilitator. Determines the authoritative source for the MCC classification.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date on which the MCC was assigned to the merchant or merchant location. Represents the business event date of the assignment decision.']
  assignment_rationale varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or business justification for the MCC assignment. May include details about the merchant\'s primary business activity, product mix, or regulatory considerations that influenced the MCC selection.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MCC assignment. Active assignments are used for transaction processing; pending assignments await approval; suspended or revoked assignments are temporarily or permanently disabled.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for changing the MCC from a previous assignment. Applicable only when previous_mcc_code is populated. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: business_change|correction|scheme_mandate|fraud_mitigation|merchant_request|regulatory_requirement|audit_finding|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MCC assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the MCC assignment becomes active and is used for transaction processing, interchange qualification, and reporting. May differ from assignment_date if there is a planned future activation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the MCC assignment expires or is no longer valid. Nullable for open-ended assignments. Used to track MCC changes and historical assignments.']
  interchange_qualification_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates the impact of this MCC assignment on interchange rate qualification. Certain MCCs qualify for enhanced or premium interchange rates, while others may result in downgrades or restrictions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MCC assignment record was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit code that classifies the merchant\'s type of business. Determines interchange rates, spending category analytics, and fraud risk profiling. Assigned by card schemes (Visa, Mastercard) or acquirer.']
  mcc_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the MCC code, describing the business category (e.g., \'Grocery Stores and Supermarkets\', \'Airlines\', \'Hotels and Motels\').']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments related to this MCC assignment. May include operational details, special instructions, or historical context.']
  previous_mcc_code varchar [note: 'The MCC code that was previously assigned to this merchant or location before the current assignment. Nullable for initial assignments. Used to track MCC change history and analyze reclassification patterns.']
  regulatory_reporting_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance reporting category associated with this MCC. Used for AML/CTF reporting, tax reporting (e.g., 1099-K thresholds), and industry-specific regulatory requirements.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with this MCC for fraud detection and prevention purposes. High-risk MCCs (e.g., gambling, adult entertainment, travel) trigger enhanced monitoring and fraud rules.']
  scheme_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the card scheme approved this MCC assignment. Nullable if scheme approval is not required or has not yet been obtained.']
  scheme_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or case ID provided by the card scheme when approving this MCC assignment. Used for audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  scheme_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MCC assignment requires explicit approval from the card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) before it can be activated. True for high-risk or restricted MCCs.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the operational system from which this MCC assignment record originated (e.g., Merchant Management System, CRM, card scheme portal).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this MCC assignment record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Records the Merchant Category Code (MCC) assignment for a merchant or merchant location, including the assigned MCC value, assignment date, assigning authority (card scheme or acquirer), assignment rationale, previous MCC if changed, effective date, and expiry date. Tracks MCC history for interchange qualification, fraud risk profiling, and regulatory reporting. MCC drives interchange rate eligibility and spending category analytics.'
}

Table merchant.merchant_pricing_plan {
  merchant_pricing_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pricing plan. Primary key.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Pricing plans are defined per currency; referencing the currency master ensures consistent pricing and compliance with local regulations.']
  ach_transaction_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged per ACH (bank transfer) transaction processed for the merchant, expressed in the plan\'s base currency. Typically lower than card transaction fees.']
  amex_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Separate MDR percentage applied specifically to American Express card transactions, which often have different pricing than Visa/Mastercard, expressed as a decimal.']
  avs_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged per transaction when Address Verification System (AVS) checks are performed, expressed in the plan\'s base currency. Helps reduce fraud risk for card-not-present transactions.']
  batch_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged per settlement batch submitted by the merchant, expressed in the plan\'s base currency. Applies to merchants using batch settlement rather than real-time capture.']
  chargeback_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the merchant for each chargeback dispute, regardless of outcome, expressed in the plan\'s base currency. Covers administrative and processing costs.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'The minimum contract duration in months that the merchant must commit to when assigned this pricing plan. Null for month-to-month plans.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or administrator who created this pricing plan record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pricing plan record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  currency_conversion_markup_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Markup percentage applied to the foreign exchange (FX) rate when Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) is offered to cardholders, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 for 3.00% above spot rate).']
  debit_card_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'MDR percentage applied specifically to debit card transactions, typically lower than credit card rates due to Regulation II (Durbin Amendment) caps, expressed as a decimal.']
  early_termination_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Penalty fee charged if the merchant terminates the agreement before the contract term expires, expressed in the plan\'s base currency. Null if no early termination penalty applies.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this pricing plan becomes binding and applicable to assigned merchants. Transactions on or after this date are governed by this plan\'s terms.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this pricing plan ceases to be valid. Nullable for open-ended plans. Transactions after this date require a new plan assignment.']
  interchange_markup_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The markup percentage added on top of the actual interchange cost in interchange-plus pricing models (e.g., 0.0050 for 0.50% above interchange). Null for non-interchange-plus models.']
  interchange_passthrough_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether actual interchange fees (IRF) are passed through to the merchant at cost (true) or absorbed into a blended rate (false). True for interchange-plus and cost-plus models.']
  international_transaction_fee_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Additional percentage fee applied to cross-border transactions where the card issuer is in a different country than the merchant, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0100 for 1.00%).']
  mdr_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The base percentage rate charged to the merchant per transaction, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0275 for 2.75%). This is the primary revenue component for flat-rate and blended pricing models.']
  merchant_pricing_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing plan. Active plans are available for assignment; inactive plans are no longer offered but may still govern existing merchants; pending plans are under approval; suspended plans are temporarily unavailable; archived plans are historical records only.']
  mid_qualified_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The middle MDR percentage tier in tiered pricing models, applied to transactions that partially meet qualification criteria (e.g., keyed-in transactions, rewards cards), expressed as a decimal.']
  monthly_account_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly account maintenance or service fee charged to the merchant, independent of transaction volume, expressed in the plan\'s base currency.']
  monthly_minimum_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum monthly fee the merchant must pay regardless of transaction volume, expressed in the plan\'s base currency. If monthly processing fees fall below this threshold, the merchant is charged the difference.']
  non_qualified_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The highest MDR percentage tier in tiered pricing models, applied to transactions that fail qualification criteria (e.g., corporate cards, international cards, manually entered), expressed as a decimal.']
  payfac_sub_merchant_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing plan is available for assignment to sub-merchants under a Payment Facilitator (PayFac) model. True if eligible for PayFac sub-merchants; false if only for direct merchants.']
  pci_compliance_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual or monthly fee charged to merchants for PCI DSS compliance program administration, security scanning, and attestation support, expressed in the plan\'s base currency.']
  pci_non_compliance_penalty_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monthly penalty fee charged to merchants who fail to maintain PCI DSS compliance, expressed in the plan\'s base currency. Escalates with duration of non-compliance.']
  per_transaction_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed fee charged per transaction in addition to the percentage-based MDR, expressed in the plan\'s base currency (e.g., 0.10 for $0.10 per transaction).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the pricing plan, used in contracts and merchant communications.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the pricing plan, including key features, target merchant segments, and value proposition.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the pricing plan (e.g., \'Standard Retail\', \'Enterprise Plus\', \'Startup Flat Rate\').']
  pricing_model_type varchar [note: 'The fundamental pricing structure applied to merchant transactions. Flat-rate charges a single percentage; interchange-plus passes through interchange costs plus a markup; tiered groups transactions into qualified/mid-qualified/non-qualified buckets; blended averages all transaction types; cost-plus adds a fixed margin to actual costs; custom indicates bespoke negotiated terms.']
  qualified_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The lowest MDR percentage tier in tiered pricing models, applied to transactions that meet all qualification criteria (e.g., swiped consumer credit cards), expressed as a decimal.']
  refund_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the merchant for processing a refund or credit transaction, expressed in the plan\'s base currency. Some plans waive this fee; others charge per refund.']
  retrieval_request_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the merchant for each retrieval request (pre-chargeback inquiry) from the issuing bank, expressed in the plan\'s base currency.']
  setup_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'One-time fee charged when a merchant is onboarded and assigned to this pricing plan, covering account setup, KYC processing, and initial configuration, expressed in the plan\'s base currency.']
  statement_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged for generating and delivering monthly merchant statements (paper or electronic), expressed in the plan\'s base currency.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user or administrator who last modified this pricing plan record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this pricing plan record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  voice_authorization_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged when a merchant obtains authorization via phone call to the authorization center rather than electronic authorization, expressed in the plan\'s base currency.']
  volume_tier_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monthly transaction volume threshold (in base currency) that triggers preferential pricing or tier upgrades. Used in volume-based pricing models where rates decrease as volume increases.']

  Note: 'Defines the commercial pricing plan assigned to a merchant — the fee structure governing MDR (Merchant Discount Rate), interchange pass-through model, flat-rate or tiered pricing, monthly minimums, setup fees, PCI compliance fees, and chargeback fees. Stores plan name, pricing model type (flat-rate, interchange-plus, tiered, blended), effective date, expiry date, and negotiated rate overrides. SSOT for merchant commercial pricing configuration.'
}

Table merchant.merchant_fee_schedule {
  merchant_fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each merchant fee schedule record.']
  fee_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Fee schedules are charged in specific currencies; linking to the currency reference supports accurate fee calculation and reporting.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant to which this fee schedule applies.']
  merchant_pricing_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant pricing plan that owns this fee schedule.']
  merchant_settlement_account_id varchar [note: 'Settlement account used for fee revenue collection.']
  applicable_scheme varchar [note: 'Card network to which the fee applies.']
  applicable_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Transaction type that triggers the fee.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which a recurring fee is billed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee schedule record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount applied when fee_basis is flat_monthly or per_transaction.']
  fee_basis varchar [note: 'Determines how the fee amount is calculated.']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum total fee that can be charged within a billing period.']
  fee_cap_currency varchar [note: 'Currency for the fee cap amount.']
  fee_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the fee purpose.']
  fee_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'True if the merchant is exempt from this fee under special conditions.']
  fee_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining why the fee is exempted.']
  fee_override_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'True if the fee amount or rate can be manually overridden for a specific merchant.']
  fee_override_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any manual override of the fee.']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage applied to the transaction amount when fee_basis is percentage.']
  fee_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the fee definition.']
  fee_taxable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee is subject to tax.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Specific fee line item such as authorization fee, capture fee, refund fee, chargeback fee, monthly service fee, PCI non‑compliance fee, or early termination fee. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authorization|capture|refund|chargeback|monthly_service|pci_non_compliance|early_termination — promote to reference product]']
  fee_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee recurs on a regular billing cycle.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule was last reviewed for accuracy or compliance.']
  max_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Ceiling amount that the fee will not exceed.']
  merchant_fee_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the fee schedule purpose and any special conditions.']
  merchant_fee_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fee schedule.']
  min_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Floor amount that the fee will not go below.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when multiple schedules could apply; lower numbers have higher precedence.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the fee is mandated by regulatory requirements (e.g., interchange fees).']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to reference the fee schedule in merchant contracts and internal systems.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the structural type of the fee schedule.']
  tax_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate when fee_taxable_flag is true.']
  tier_end_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction amount for a tiered fee range (exclusive).']
  tier_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage applied within the defined tier range.']
  tier_start_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction amount for a tiered fee range (inclusive).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fee schedule record.']

  Note: 'Granular fee line-item schedule associated with a merchant pricing plan. Captures individual fee types (authorization fee, capture fee, refund fee, chargeback fee, monthly service fee, PCI non-compliance fee, early termination fee), fee amount or rate, fee basis (per-transaction, percentage, flat monthly), applicable card scheme, applicable transaction type, and effective date range. Enables detailed merchant billing and revenue recognition.'
}

Table merchant.merchant_settlement_account {
  merchant_settlement_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the settlement account record.']

  account_close_date date [note: 'Date when the bank account was closed or de‑activated (nullable).']

  account_number_token varchar [note: 'PCI‑P2PE token representing the underlying bank account number.']
  account_open_date date [note: 'Date when the bank account was originally opened.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the settlement account (checking, savings, or current).']
  bank_branch varchar [note: 'Branch or location identifier of the bank (e.g., city or branch code).']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution where the settlement account is held.']
  bic_swift varchar [note: 'SWIFT code identifying the bank for international transfers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement account record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the settlement account. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CHF|AUD|CAD|... — promote to reference product]']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the settlement account became active for the merchant.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the settlement account is scheduled to be deactivated or closed (nullable).']
  iban varchar [note: 'Standardized international bank account number used for cross‑border settlements.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the merchant\'s primary settlement account.']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'True if the account number is stored as a token rather than clear text.']
  last_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful verification of the account.']
  merchant_settlement_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the settlement account.']
  pci_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the settlement account handling meets PCI DSS requirements.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Domestic routing number used for ACH or wire transfers.']
  settlement_delay_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between transaction finalization and fund disbursement.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often settlement funds are transferred to the account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement account record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the settlement account.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the settlement account verification process.']

  Note: 'Stores the merchant\'s designated bank account(s) for settlement fund disbursement. Captures bank name, account holder name, account type (checking, savings), IBAN, account number (tokenized/masked), routing number, BIC/SWIFT code, currency, settlement frequency (daily, weekly, monthly), settlement delay days, and account verification status. Supports multi-currency and multi-bank settlement configurations per merchant.'
}

Table merchant.processing_limit {
  processing_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the processing limit record.']
  control_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific compliance rule governing this limit.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant to which this processing limit applies.']
  underwriting_decision_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underwriting decision record that approved the limit.']
  card_not_present_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for card‑not‑present (CNP) transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the processing limit record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value for cross‑border transactions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency of monetary limits.']
  daily_transaction_count_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of individual transactions permitted per day.']
  daily_volume_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary volume limit for transactions within a calendar day.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the limit becomes enforceable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the limit expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended limits.']
  is_compliant boolean [note: 'True when the limit configuration satisfies all applicable regulatory and internal compliance rules.']
  is_manual_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if transactions breaching the limit trigger a manual review workflow.']
  is_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether authorized personnel may temporarily override the limit.']
  limit_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description explaining why the limit was created or modified.']
  limit_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the type of limit configuration.']
  limit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for periodic review and possible adjustment of the limit.']
  limit_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the limit configuration – whether set by underwriting, risk model, or manual policy.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the limit, indicating which transaction dimension it governs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single_transaction|daily_volume|monthly_tpv|transaction_count|cnp|cross_border|refund — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  monthly_tpv_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative transaction value allowed in a month.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form notes or comments about the limit.']
  processing_limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the limit configuration.']
  refund_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total amount that can be refunded within the limit period.']
  risk_score_at_set decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk model score associated with the merchant at the time the limit was established.']
  single_transaction_max_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value allowed for any single transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the processing limit record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking revisions to the limit configuration.']

  Note: 'Defines the transaction processing limits and velocity controls configured for a merchant account. Stores single transaction maximum amount, daily transaction volume cap, monthly TPV (Total Payment Volume) limit, maximum transaction count per day, card-not-present limit, cross-border transaction limit, refund limit, and limit review date. Limits are set during underwriting and adjusted based on risk profile changes.'
}

Table merchant.merchant_hierarchy {
  merchant_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each merchant hierarchy relationship record.']
  child_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant that is the subordinate entity in the hierarchy.']
  parent_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant that acts as the parent in the relationship (e.g., PayFac, ISO, corporate parent).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the relationship meets applicable AML, KYC, and PCI‑DSS requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the hierarchy record was initially inserted into the lakehouse.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Calendar date on which the parent‑child relationship starts to apply.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Calendar date on which the relationship terminates; null for open‑ended relationships.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the child merchant relative to the root of the hierarchy (root = 0).']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the relationship (e.g., PayFac to sub‑merchant, ISO portfolio, corporate parent‑subsidiary, aggregator‑merchant).']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the child merchant may belong to only this parent; false if multi‑parenting is allowed.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'True when the child merchant is designated as the primary account within the parent grouping.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the hierarchy relationship was last examined for regulatory or risk compliance.']
  merchant_hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Operational state of the relationship (e.g., active, pending approval, suspended).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional textual comments or business rationale for the hierarchy entry.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑99.99) reflecting the aggregate risk of the parent‑child grouping.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that created or last updated the hierarchy entry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gateway|transaction_platform|merchant_mgmt|fraud_platform|digital_wallet|risk_compliance|crm|erp|analytics — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to any field in the hierarchy record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing integer used to manage concurrent updates.']

  Note: 'Models the organizational hierarchy relationships between merchant entities — including PayFac (Payment Facilitator) to sub-merchant relationships, ISO (Independent Sales Organization) portfolios, corporate parent-to-subsidiary chains, and aggregator-to-merchant structures. Captures parent merchant ID, child merchant ID, hierarchy type (PayFac, ISO, corporate, aggregator), effective date, and hierarchy level. Enables consolidated reporting and risk exposure aggregation across merchant groups.'
}

Table merchant.sub_merchant {
  sub_merchant_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sub-merchant entity.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PayFac (Payment Facilitator) that sponsors the sub‑merchant.']
  merchant_pricing_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing plan assigned to the sub‑merchant.']
  merchant_settlement_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bank account used for settlement payouts.']
  parent_aggregator_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the aggregator (if any) that the sub‑merchant is linked to.']
  parent_payfac_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PayFac (Payment Facilitator) that sponsors the sub‑merchant.']
  payment_facilitator_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the aggregator (if any) that the sub‑merchant is linked to.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date the sub‑merchant became active for processing transactions.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the sub‑merchant\'s physical address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the sub‑merchant\'s physical address (optional).']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of AML screening for the sub‑merchant.']
  average_monthly_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average monetary volume of transactions per month for the sub‑merchant.']
  average_ticket_size decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average monetary value per individual transaction.']
  business_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the sub-merchant\'s primary business activity.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the sub‑merchant\'s address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall regulatory compliance status of the sub‑merchant.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for sub‑merchant communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the sub‑merchant.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the sub‑merchant\'s location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sub‑merchant record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Default settlement currency for the sub‑merchant.']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'Trade name under which the sub-merchant operates.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code describing the sub-merchant\'s sector.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Result of the KYC verification process for the sub‑merchant.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or risk review for the sub‑merchant.']
  mcc varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code that classifies the sub-merchant\'s transaction type.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the sub‑merchant.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date the sub‑merchant completed the onboarding process.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the sub‑merchant\'s address.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the sub‑merchant is currently allowed to process transactions.']
  risk_classification varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned to the sub‑merchant based on underwriting and fraud scoring.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening for the sub‑merchant.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the sub‑merchant\'s address.']
  sub_merchant_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the sub-merchant as registered.']
  sub_merchant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sub‑merchant.']
  sub_mid varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the sub-merchant by the acquiring bank.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the sub‑merchant relationship was terminated or closed.']
  transaction_volume_tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification based on historical transaction volume.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sub‑merchant record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Represents a sub-merchant entity operating under a PayFac or aggregator umbrella. Stores sub-merchant name, sub-MID, parent PayFac or aggregator reference, business type, onboarding date, processing status, assigned MCC, transaction volume tier, and sub-merchant-level risk classification. Sub-merchants are distinct from direct merchants in that they are sponsored and managed by a PayFac rather than directly contracted with the acquirer.'
}

  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the document is considered expired and must be refreshed.']

  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or government body that issued the document.']

  retention_policy_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the document must be retained to satisfy regulatory requirements.']
