  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the merchant uploaded the document to the system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of automated or manual verification of the document\'s authenticity.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the document verification was performed.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the document when re‑uploaded or amended.']

  Note: 'Tracks documents submitted by or on behalf of a merchant during onboarding, periodic review, or compliance processes. Captures document type (business license, certificate of incorporation, bank statement, voided check, PCI attestation, tax certificate), document status (submitted, under review, approved, rejected, expired), submission date, expiry date, issuing authority, and document storage reference. Supports KYB, PCI DSS, and regulatory audit requirements.'
}

Table merchant.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each status transition record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific user, system, or process that performed the transition.']
  initiating_actor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific user, system, or process that performed the transition.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant whose status changed.']
  merchant_pricing_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing plan active for the merchant during the transition.']
  merchant_settlement_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement account linked to the merchant at the time of status change.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Status changes may be governed by jurisdictional regulations; linking to the regulatory jurisdiction table supports compliance reporting.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch job that processed the status change.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the status change, if applicable.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening at the moment of change.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent amendment to the audit record (e.g., correction).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transition was required by a compliance rule.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the new status is considered effective (may differ from transition_timestamp).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the status is scheduled to end or be superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk model score indicating fraud likelihood at the time of status change.']
  initiating_actor_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity that initiated the status change.']
  is_override_allowed boolean [note: 'True if the status change could be manually overridden by privileged users.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current Know‑Your‑Customer verification status at transition time.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Status of the merchant after the transition. Same enumeration as previous_status.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the operator or system during the transition.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when an override to the status change was performed.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Status of the merchant before the transition. Values include application_submitted, under_review, approved, active, suspended, terminated, offboarded.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the status transition was triggered (e.g., KYC_APPROVED, FRAUD_SUSPEND, COMPLIANCE_TERMINATE).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation of the reason code.']
  regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'True if the status transition must be included in regulatory reporting (e.g., SAR, AML).']
  risk_score_at_change decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk score assigned to the merchant at the moment of transition.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of sanctions list screening at transition time.']
  status_change_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the status change was recorded.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the status change occurred.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version for the audit row to support corrections.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of all merchant lifecycle status transitions — from application submitted, under review, approved, active, suspended, terminated, to offboarded. Captures previous status, new status, transition timestamp, reason code, initiating actor (system, user, compliance trigger), and supporting notes. Enables full merchant lifecycle traceability for regulatory audits and dispute investigations.'
}

Table merchant.acquiring_agreement {
  acquiring_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the acquiring agreement record.']
  amendment_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the stored document that contains the amendment text.']
  contract_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Contracts specify settlement currency; linking to the currency reference ensures legal clarity and correct settlement processing.']
  control_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific compliance rule set governing this agreement.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Business need: Acquiring agreements bind a merchant to an acquiring partner (bank). Required for settlement reporting, regulatory compliance, and partner performance metrics.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant party to which the agreement applies.']
  merchant_pricing_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pricing plan associated with this agreement.']
  merchant_settlement_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bank account where settlement funds are transferred.']
  acquiring_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the agreement.']
  agreement_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category of the agreement.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External contract number assigned by the acquirer or merchant for reference.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement based on its purpose and parties involved.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date on which a specific amendment to the agreement took effect.']
  amendment_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the changes introduced by the amendment.']
  amendment_history_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the historical log of all amendments made to the agreement.']
  amendment_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the specific amendment.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'True if the agreement automatically renews at expiry.']
  contract_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the agreement.']
  contract_closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement was formally closed or terminated.']
  contract_closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the agreement was closed or terminated.']
  contract_status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status of the agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  early_termination_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty payable if the agreement is terminated early.']
  early_termination_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the early termination fee.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement terminates or expires; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating the legal jurisdiction governing the contract.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s primary business activity.']
  mid varchar [note: 'Acquirer‑assigned merchant identification number.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition defined in the agreement.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the agreement meets all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiry that renewal notice must be provided.']
  signatory_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement was signed.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual who signed the agreement on behalf of the acquirer.']
  signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the signatory at the time of signing.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement becomes binding.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to notify the counter‑party before terminating the agreement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the agreement document.']

  Note: 'Stores the formal acquiring agreement (merchant services agreement) between the acquirer and the merchant. Captures agreement type, contract start date, contract end date, auto-renewal flag, early termination fee terms, governing law jurisdiction, signatory name and date, agreement version, and amendment history reference. Distinct from pricing plan — this is the legal contract record, not the fee configuration.'
}

Table merchant.pci_compliance {
  pci_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the PCI compliance record.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant to which this compliance record applies.']
  aoc_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or URI of the Attestation of Compliance (AOC) document submitted by the merchant.']
  assigned_qsa_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Qualified Security Assessor responsible for the merchant\'s assessment.']
  asv_scan_date date [note: 'Date when the ASV scan was performed.']
  asv_scan_status varchar [note: 'Result of the Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV) vulnerability scan.']
  compliance_document_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or URI of supporting documentation (e.g., scan reports, policies).']
  compliance_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of any fee associated with compliance monitoring or certification.']
  compliance_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the compliance fee.']
  compliance_level varchar [note: 'Level of PCI DSS compliance achieved by the merchant (e.g., SAQ A, SAQ B, SAQ D, ROC).']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, observations, or actions related to the compliance record.']
  compliance_record_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier for the compliance record, often used in reporting and audits.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance record was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the compliance review process.']
  compliance_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the compliance framework, fixed to PCI DSS for this product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the compliance status started to apply.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the compliance status ends or must be renewed.']
  is_waiver_approved boolean [note: 'True if a waiver for non‑compliance fees has been formally approved.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the merchant last completed a PCI DSS assessment.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the merchant\'s next PCI DSS assessment.']
  non_compliance_fee_waiver boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merchant has been granted a waiver for non‑compliance fees.']
  pci_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the merchant.']
  penetration_test_date date [note: 'Date when the penetration test was conducted.']
  penetration_test_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the merchant\'s penetration testing.']
  qsa_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the assigned QSA.']
  qsa_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the assigned QSA.']
  risk_score_at_assessment varchar [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned during the PCI assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the compliance record.']
  waiver_approval_date date [note: 'Date the waiver was granted.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the non‑compliance fee waiver was issued.']

  Note: 'Tracks PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) compliance status for each merchant. Stores compliance level (SAQ A, SAQ B, SAQ D, ROC), current compliance status, last assessment date, next assessment due date, AOC (Attestation of Compliance) reference, ASV scan status, penetration test date, non-compliance fee waiver status, and assigned QSA (Qualified Security Assessor). Mandatory for all card-accepting merchants per card scheme rules.'
}

Table merchant.merchant_risk_profile {
  merchant_risk_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each merchant risk profile record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Risk models use the merchant\'s operating currency; referencing the master currency list aligns risk scores with regulatory expectations.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst or system that performed the latest risk review.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant to which this risk profile belongs.']
  processing_limit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant\'s processing limit configuration.']
  risk_review_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst or system that performed the latest risk review.']
  underwriting_decision_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underwriting decision record that created the profile.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening result for the merchant.']
  average_monthly_volume decimal(15,2) [note: 'Mean monetary value of transactions per month.']
  average_ticket_size decimal(15,2) [note: 'Mean value per individual transaction.']
  business_model_risk_classification varchar [note: 'Risk classification based on the merchant\'s business model and industry exposure.']
  chargeback_rate_3m_rolling decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average chargeback rate over the trailing three‑month window.']
  chargeback_rate_current decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total transactions that have resulted in chargebacks in the most recent reporting period.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the merchant complies with applicable regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk profile record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the risk profile becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the risk profile expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  fraud_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of transactions flagged as fraudulent for the merchant.']
  is_manual_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manual risk review is required.']
  is_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether risk parameters can be overridden by authorized personnel.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current Know‑Your‑Customer verification status for the merchant.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to any field in the record.']
  last_risk_review_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent risk assessment was performed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form information about the risk profile.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for any manual override of risk settings.']
  refund_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction value refunded to cardholders within the reporting period.']
  reserve_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current monetary amount held in reserve for the merchant.']
  reserve_balance_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the reserve balance amount.']
  reserve_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the merchant\'s transaction volume that must be held as reserve.']
  reserve_requirement_type varchar [note: 'Method used to hold a financial reserve for the merchant (none, rolling, or capped).']
  risk_review_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured during the risk review.']
  risk_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the latest risk review was completed.']
  risk_review_trigger varchar [note: 'Event or condition that initiated the latest risk review.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk score derived from underwriting models (0‑100).']
  risk_score_explanation varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of factors influencing the risk score.']
  risk_score_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the risk score (e.g., model, manual).']
  risk_score_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the scoring model used.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk profile.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Overall risk tier assigned to the merchant after underwriting and ongoing assessment.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening for the merchant.']
  transaction_volume_tier varchar [note: 'Categorization of merchant based on average transaction volume.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the risk profile.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for the risk profile record.']

  Note: 'Stores the underwriting and ongoing risk assessment profile for a merchant. Captures risk tier (low, medium, high, prohibited), business model risk classification, chargeback rate (current and rolling 3-month), refund rate, fraud rate, reserve requirement type (none, rolling, capped), reserve percentage, reserve balance, last risk review date, and risk review trigger. Owned by the merchant domain as the operational risk configuration record; distinct from the enterprise risk domain\'s scoring models.'
}

Table merchant.merchant_reserve_account {
  merchant_reserve_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the reserve account.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the reserve complies with regulatory requirements (e.g., compliant, non-compliant).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reserve account record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code of the reserve balance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the reserve account becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the reserve account expires or is terminated (nullable).']
  hold_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the reserve amount is held before release eligibility.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates if the reserve parameters have been manually overridden.']
  last_release_date date [note: 'Date when the reserve was last partially or fully released.']
  merchant_reserve_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reserve account.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text for additional information or comments about the reserve account.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for any manual override of reserve settings.']
  release_schedule varchar [note: 'Frequency at which reserve funds are released back to the merchant.']
  reserve_account_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the reserve account used in reporting and external communications.']
  reserve_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current monetary amount held in the reserve account.']
  reserve_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction volume held as reserve.']
  reserve_type varchar [note: 'Classification of reserve: rolling, upfront, or capped.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score associated with the reserve account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reserve account record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Manages the rolling reserve or security reserve account held against a merchant to cover potential chargebacks, refunds, or fraud losses. Stores reserve type (rolling, upfront, capped), reserve percentage, reserve balance, currency, hold period (days), release schedule, last release date, and reserve account status. Distinct from the merchant settlement account — this is a risk mitigation financial hold, not a disbursement account.'
}

Table merchant.chargeback_threshold {
  chargeback_threshold_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the chargeback threshold record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Chargeback limits are defined per currency; referencing the master currency enables consistent enforcement across regions.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: Chargeback thresholds are merchant‑specific configuration; adding merchant_id FK to chargeback_threshold creates the proper parent‑child relationship.']
  chargeback_rate_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum chargeback rate (percentage of total transactions) permitted for the month.']
  chargeback_threshold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the threshold configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the chargeback threshold record was first created in the system.']
  current_month_chargeback_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of chargebacks recorded for the merchant in the current month.']
  current_month_chargeback_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual chargeback rate for the current month, expressed as a percentage of total processed volume.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the threshold configuration becomes operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the threshold configuration is no longer applicable (null for open‑ended).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the merchant was enrolled in the chargeback monitoring program.']
  fine_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty imposed if the merchant exceeds the defined thresholds.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the threshold configuration was last reviewed for compliance or performance.']
  monthly_chargeback_count_threshold varchar [note: 'Maximum number of chargebacks allowed in a calendar month before the merchant is flagged.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments, observations, or special handling instructions.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the monitoring program – standard (baseline) or excessive (high‑risk).']
  remediation_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or code linking to the remediation plan document or workflow associated with this threshold.']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'Name of the payment card scheme to which the chargeback monitoring program applies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the chargeback threshold record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the threshold record to support change tracking.']

  Note: 'Defines the chargeback monitoring thresholds configured for a merchant, aligned with card scheme chargeback monitoring programs (Visa VDMP, Mastercard ECM). Captures scheme name, program type (standard, excessive), monthly chargeback count threshold, chargeback rate threshold (%), current month chargeback count, current month chargeback rate, program enrollment date, remediation plan reference, and fine amount if applicable. Enables proactive chargeback program management.'
}

  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant to which the note pertains.']
  note_related_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a transaction that is contextually linked to the note, if applicable.']
  note_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a transaction that is contextually linked to the note, if applicable.']
  related_case_compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a dispute or compliance case associated with the note.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the note author.']
  author_role varchar [note: 'Business role of the author at the time of note creation.']
  body varchar [note: 'Full free‑text content of the merchant note.']

  note_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the note content.']
  risk_score_at_note decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned to the merchant at the time the note was recorded.']

  workflow_event_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the workflow event that triggered the creation of the note.']

  Note: 'Stores structured operational and relationship notes recorded against a merchant by internal teams — including underwriting notes, relationship manager comments, compliance observations, and escalation records. Captures note type, note body, author, creation timestamp, visibility level (internal, compliance-only, executive), and associated workflow event. Sourced from CRM (Salesforce) merchant account activity feed and Merchant Management System case notes.'
}

Table merchant.boarding_event {
  boarding_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant boarding event record.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant onboarding application associated with the event.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the actor (user, system, or partner) responsible for the event.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant to which this boarding event relates.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Onboarding events are subject to jurisdiction‑specific KYC/AML rules; referencing the jurisdiction master enables automated rule selection.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the first transaction that may be linked to the boarding event (e.g., first processed transaction).']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Category of the actor that performed or triggered the event.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening result associated with the event.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event triggered a compliance rule or alert.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the event\'s effect is considered active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the event is no longer considered active.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the event (e.g., KYB, MID provisioning, gateway credential issuance).']
  event_payload varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded additional data captured from the source system for the event.']
  event_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of events for the merchant onboarding funnel.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the boarding event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the boarding event was generated in the source system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The specific onboarding milestone that occurred (e.g., application received, KYB passed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: application_received|kyb_initiated|kyb_passed|mid_provisioned|gateway_credentials_issued|first_transaction_processed|go_live_confirmed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score generated by the fraud detection platform for the event.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'True if a human manually overrode an automated decision for this event.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current Know‑Your‑Customer verification status for the merchant at event time.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or comments about the event.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the operator for manually overriding the event.']
  processing_limit_exceeded boolean [note: 'True if the event indicates a processing limit breach for the merchant.']
  regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'True if the event must be included in regulatory reporting.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) associated with the event at capture time.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk tier classification derived from the risk score.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening for the merchant at the time of the event.']
  sla_actual_seconds varchar [note: 'Actual time taken to complete the onboarding step, in seconds.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'True if the actual SLA met or beat the target; otherwise false.']
  sla_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Service‑level‑agreement target time for the onboarding step, expressed in seconds.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that emitted the boarding event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the event record.']

  Note: 'Captures discrete onboarding milestone events in the merchant lifecycle — such as application received, KYB initiated, KYB passed, MID provisioned, gateway credentials issued, first transaction processed, and go-live confirmed. Each event record stores event type, event timestamp, actor, system source, and associated merchant application reference. Enables SLA tracking and onboarding funnel analysis for the Merchant Management System.'
}

Table merchant.scheme_registration {
  scheme_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each scheme registration record.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Scheme registrations are country‑specific; referencing the country master is required for compliance and reporting.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Scheme fees are charged in a designated currency; linking to the currency reference ensures accurate fee calculation and auditability.']
  primary_scheme_assigned_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the card scheme to the merchant for reporting and interchange qualification.']
  tertiary_scheme_sponsor_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sponsoring PayFac or ISO when registration_type is sponsored.']
  compliance_check_date date [note: 'Date the most recent compliance verification was performed for this registration.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the registration meets all mandatory scheme compliance requirements.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance outcome for the registration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the registration record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the registration becomes effective for transaction processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the registration expires or is scheduled to be terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external scheme or partner systems to reference this registration.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates if this is the merchant\'s primary scheme registration (true) or an auxiliary one (false).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or operational review.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code that classifies the merchant\'s primary business activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the registration.']
  program_enrollments varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of scheme programs the merchant is enrolled in (e.g., Visa Checkout, Mastercard SecureCode).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region identifier (e.g., NA, EMEA, APAC) for reporting aggregation.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date the merchant registration was initially submitted to the scheme.']
  registration_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged by the scheme for the registration.']
  registration_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the registration fee.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scheme registration.']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the merchant registers directly with the scheme or via a sponsor such as a PayFac.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for renewal of the scheme registration.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the registration based on compliance and fraud considerations.']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'Name of the card scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) with which the merchant is registered.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the registration record.']
  sponsor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sponsoring entity (PayFac, ISO, or None).']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current registration status (e.g., reason for suspension).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date the registration was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the registration record.']

  Note: 'Records the formal registration of a merchant with each card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, UnionPay). Captures scheme name, registration type (direct, sponsored via PayFac), scheme-assigned merchant ID, registration date, registration status, scheme program enrollments (e.g., Visa Checkout, Mastercard SecureCode), and scheme-level compliance flags. Required for scheme reporting and interchange qualification.'
}

Table merchant.risk_rule_override {
  risk_rule_override_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the merchant_risk_rule_override association']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant']
  risk_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk_rule']
  custom_threshold_value decimal [note: 'Custom threshold that overrides the rule\'s default for this merchant']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the custom override becomes active']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the custom override expires (null if open‑ended)']

  Note: 'Represents an operational override of a risk decisioning rule for a specific merchant, capturing custom threshold values and effective dates.. Existence Justification: Merchants can have custom overrides for individual risk decisioning rules. Each override records a custom threshold value and the period during which it is effective, and a single risk rule can be overridden for many merchants while a merchant can have overrides for many rules.'
}

Table merchant.terminal_assignment {
  terminal_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the terminal_assignment association']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the merchant entity']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the POS terminal entity']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual termination date of the assignment, if earlier than expected']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the assignment (active, suspended, terminated)']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Type of assignment (e.g., exclusive, shared, temporary)']
  expected_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the assignment']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the terminal assignment to the merchant begins']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual assignment of a POS terminal to a merchant. Each record captures the specific assignment details that are not intrinsic to either the merchant or the terminal alone.. Existence Justification: Merchants can be assigned multiple POS terminals and a single POS terminal can be shared among multiple merchants under contractual sharing agreements. The assignment is actively managed, with attributes such as assignment type, status, and start/end dates recorded for each merchant‑terminal pairing.'
}

Table merchant.portfolio {
  portfolio_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for portfolio']
  parent_portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on portfolio (parent_portfolio_id)']
  settlement_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the financial account where portfolio‑related settlements are posted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in the portfolio.']
  daily_volume_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate transaction amount per day for the portfolio.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the portfolio purpose, pricing rules, and target merchant segment.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the portfolio expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the portfolio becomes effective for merchant transactions.']
  is_tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the portfolio is exempt from transaction‑level taxes.']
  portfolio_code varchar [note: 'External business code or identifier assigned to the portfolio by the merchant onboarding system.']
  portfolio_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the portfolio used in reporting and UI.']
  portfolio_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the portfolio based on service tier or product offering.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate fees for merchants under this portfolio.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the portfolio record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the portfolio record.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the portfolio based on merchant risk profiling.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the portfolio.']
  transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum single transaction amount allowed for merchants in this portfolio.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for portfolio. Referenced by portfolio_id.'
}

Table merchant.payment_facilitator {
  payment_facilitator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_facilitator']
  parent_payment_facilitator_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_facilitator (parent_payment_facilitator_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the payment facilitator\'s headquarters.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the payment facilitator\'s headquarters.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the payment facilitator\'s headquarters.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment facilitator record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the payment facilitator.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the payment facilitator agreement becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the payment facilitator agreement ends (null if open‑ended).']
  facilitator_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment facilitator (e.g., PayFac, ISO, Sub‑merchant, Partner).']
  kyc_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when KYC verification was completed.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current Know‑Your‑Customer verification status of the payment facilitator.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Official legal name of the payment facilitator as registered with authorities.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Standard MCC assigned to the payment facilitator for transaction classification.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable display name of the payment facilitator.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes or comments.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the payment facilitator was onboarded into the platform.']
  payfac_code varchar [note: 'Internal reference code used to uniquely identify the payment facilitator within the organization.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the payment facilitator\'s headquarters.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing structure applied to the payment facilitator.']
  pricing_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric rate associated with the pricing model (e.g., percentage or flat fee).']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting the payment facilitator.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the payment facilitator.']
  processing_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount the payment facilitator is authorized to process per transaction or period.']
  processing_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the processing limit amount.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the payment facilitator (0‑100).']
  risk_score_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent risk score calculation.']
  settlement_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number used for settlement payouts to the payment facilitator.']
  settlement_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bank holding the settlement account.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for settlement transactions.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the payment facilitator\'s headquarters.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment facilitator.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the payment facilitator.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the payment facilitator relationship was terminated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment facilitator record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the payment facilitator.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_facilitator. Referenced by payfac_id.'
}

Table cardholder.account_holder {
  account_holder_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the account holder (golden record).']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Cardholder account holder records should reference the country master for regulatory and reporting purposes. Add account_holder_country_id (FK to reference.country.country_id) and remove the duplicate']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Required for issuer settlement and regulatory reporting: each account holder must be linked to the issuing ecosystem partner that provides the account.']
  account_holder_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the account holder.']
  account_holder_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the holder is an individual consumer (B2C) or a corporate entity (B2B).']
  avs_verified boolean [note: 'Result of Address Verification System check during onboarding.']
  business_category varchar [note: 'Industry classification of business account holder (e.g., retail, services).']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the mailing address.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account holder was closed or deactivated, if applicable.']
  corporate_name varchar [note: 'Legal corporate name for business account holders.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account holder record was first created in the system.']
  cvv_last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent CVV verification event.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the individual account holder.']
  dpan varchar [note: 'Device Primary Account Number used for tokenized transactions.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the account holder.']
  failed_login_attempts varchar [note: 'Count of consecutive failed login attempts.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the account holder.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the account holder as provided during onboarding.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date when the business entity was incorporated.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current KYC verification status of the account holder.']
  kyc_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when KYC verification was completed.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last successful login to the platform.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the account holder.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the mailing address.']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the mailing address.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Merchant Category Code associated with the account holder (if applicable).']
  nationality varchar [note: 'Country of citizenship or nationality of the account holder.']
  onboarding_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the account holder was onboarded.']
  pan varchar [note: 'Primary Account Number linked to the account holder (masked for security).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the account holder.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the mailing address.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications with the account holder.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned by fraud detection platform (0-100).']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening (e.g., OFAC).']
  sca_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account holder has completed Strong Customer Authentication compliance.']
  source_of_funds varchar [note: 'Declared source of funds for the account holder.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the mailing address.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the current status (e.g., fraud_suspected, compliance_hold).']
  tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account holder is tax-exempt.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (e.g., SSN, EIN, TIN) for tax reporting.']
  tokenization_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the PAN has been tokenized.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the account holder record.']

  Note: 'SSOT master record for every consumer and business cardholder identity served by the platform. Captures full legal identity (name, DOB, nationality, tax ID), account holder type (individual B2C vs. corporate B2B), onboarding channel, lifecycle status (active, suspended, closed, dormant), preferred language, and platform-assigned cardholder reference number. This is the root anchor entity for all cardholder sub-entities and the authoritative golden record for cardholder identity across all payment channels.'
}

Table cardholder.cardholder_profile {
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the cardholder profile record.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.account_holder. Business justification: cardholder_profile extends the master account_holder; linking provides a single source of truth and allows removal of duplicated personal and KYC fields from the profile.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Links employee cardholder profiles to cost centers for expense allocation and internal reporting.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Needed for loyalty and offer management: the primary partner offering programs to the cardholder is identified for personalized marketing and compliance.']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_fee_schedule. Business justification: FX fee schedule applied per cardholder for fee calculation reports; treasury uses this to charge correct FX fees based on customer segment.']
  issuing_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: Needed for co‑branded card programs: link each cardholder to the merchant that issued the card for revenue sharing, program eligibility, and regulatory reporting.']
  issuing_participant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.participant. Business justification: Regulatory AML/KYC reporting requires linking each cardholder to their issuing bank’s settlement participant to trace funds and satisfy cross‑border settlement netting.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for assigning a relationship manager to each cardholder profile; used in account management dashboards and compliance reporting.']
  preferred_payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Personalized offers and risk scoring use the cardholder\'s preferred payment_product, necessitating a direct FK to payment_product.']
  preferred_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Needed for offering cardholder‑specific scheme preferences during new card issuance and for compliance reporting on scheme‑wise cardholder demographics.']
  rate_margin_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate_margin_config. Business justification: Custom margin config per cardholder determines FX rate margins; used in pricing engine and compliance reporting.']
  watchlist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_watchlist. Business justification: Compliance monitoring links each cardholder profile to its watchlist entry to satisfy AML and sanctions screening regulations.']
  account_lock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder\'s account is currently locked due to security policies.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the cardholder\'s street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address (optional).']
  address_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder\'s address has been validated through KYC processes.']
  cardholder_type varchar [note: 'Segment indicating whether the cardholder is an individual consumer, business entity, merchant, or partner.']
  completeness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated percentage (0‑100) reflecting how many optional profile fields are populated.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the most recent consent (e.g., marketing) was recorded.']
  created_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system or service that initially created the profile record.']
  data_retention_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates if the cardholder has opted out of data retention beyond statutory periods.']
  dpan_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized representation of the cardholder\'s device PAN used for tokenization services.']
  email_verified boolean [note: 'True when the primary email address has been confirmed via verification workflow.']
  external_customer_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external partner systems to reference this cardholder.']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score generated by the fraud detection platform for this cardholder.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑declared gender of the cardholder for demographic reporting.']
  is_test_account boolean [note: 'True if the profile is used for internal testing or sandbox environments.']
  kyc_verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the Know‑Your‑Customer verification process for the cardholder.']
  last_modified_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system that performed the most recent update.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the cardholder profile.']
  marketing_consent_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder has opted in to receive marketing emails.']
  marketing_consent_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder has opted in to receive marketing SMS messages.']
  pan_linked boolean [note: 'True if a primary account number (PAN) is linked to this profile.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'Cardholder\'s chosen channel for receiving marketing and transactional messages.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Language preference for communications and UI.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Main email address used for communication and account notifications.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for SMS alerts and voice contact.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profile record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the profile record.']
  right_to_erasure_requested boolean [note: 'True when the cardholder has submitted a request for data deletion.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification derived from fraud and compliance signals.']
  sca_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current SCA compliance state of the cardholder as required by PSD2.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created or last modified the profile.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone identifier associated with the cardholder.']
  total_failed_auth_attempts varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of consecutive failed login or authentication attempts.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking changes to the profile over time.']

  Note: 'Extended personal and contact profile for an account holder. Stores PII attributes including full legal name, date of birth, gender, primary email, primary phone, preferred communication channel, marketing consent flags, and profile completeness score. Distinct from account_holder (which owns identity and lifecycle) — this entity owns the mutable contact and preference layer that changes independently of identity status. Supports GDPR/CCPA data subject rights management including right-to-erasure flags.'
}

Table cardholder.address {
  address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the cardholder address record.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Address records belong to a cardholder profile; linking enables address lookup and removes the need for profile‑level address fields.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Address verification and AML compliance require linking address to reference.country for standardized country data.']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the address record.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the address purpose.']
  avs_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the address passed Address Verification System (AVS) check.']
  avs_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the AVS verification was performed.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or locality of the address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address record was created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the address became effective for the cardholder.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the address ceased to be effective (null if still active).']
  geocode_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate from geocoding the address.']
  geocode_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate from geocoding the address.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the primary address for the cardholder.']
  label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the address (e.g., Home, Office).']
  line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address.']
  line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address (optional).']
  line3 varchar [note: 'Third line of the street address (optional).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the address.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the address record.']
  verification_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of verification attempts performed for this address.']
  verification_source varchar [note: 'Source of address verification.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification status of the address.']

  Note: 'Physical and mailing address records associated with a cardholder. Stores structured address components (street, city, state/province, postal code, country ISO code), address type (billing, shipping, residential, registered business), AVS-verified flag, AVS verification timestamp, geocoding coordinates, and address lifecycle (primary, secondary, historical). Supports AVS (Address Verification System) checks during card-not-present authorization and KYC address validation workflows.'
}

Table cardholder.cardholder_kyc_verification {
  cardholder_kyc_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each KYC verification record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder to whom this verification applies.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder to whom this verification applies.']
  document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the stored digital document used for verification.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: KYC verification must record the regulatory jurisdiction to satisfy reporting and legal obligations.']
  aml_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of the Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening performed during verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the KYC verification record was first created in the system.']
  ctf_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of the Counter‑Terrorism Financing screening performed during verification.']
  document_hash varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 hash of the submitted document to ensure integrity.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of identity document submitted for verification.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the verification is considered expired and must be renewed.']
  is_sca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the verification satisfies Strong Customer Authentication requirements.']
  issued_at timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the KYC verification was performed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations recorded by the verification analyst.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'True if this verification must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., FinCEN, FCA).']
  reverification_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the cardholder has been re‑verified after the initial verification.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned to the cardholder after KYC evaluation.']
  sanctions_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder passed sanctions list screening.']
  score_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the verification score was calculated.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the verification request originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the KYC verification record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the KYC verification.']
  verification_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party or internal provider that performed the verification.']
  verification_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number (e.g., ARN) linking this verification to downstream audit trails.']
  verification_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical confidence score (0‑100) produced by the KYC engine.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the verification record.']

  Note: 'KYC (Know Your Customer) verification record capturing the full identity verification lifecycle for a cardholder. Stores verification method (document scan, biometric, database check, eIDV), verification provider, verification status (pending, passed, failed, expired, manual_review), risk tier assigned post-KYC, document types submitted, verification score, expiry date of verification, and regulatory jurisdiction. Supports AML/CTF compliance obligations and onboarding gating logic. Each re-verification creates a new record preserving the full audit trail.'
}

Table cardholder.kyc_document {
  kyc_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the KYC document record.']
  cardholder_kyc_verification_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent KYC verification to which this document belongs.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who performed the manual review.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who performed the manual review.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 checksum of the document file for integrity verification.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance evaluation was performed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document record was first created in the system.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the document purpose for KYC.']
  document_image_uri varchar [note: 'Secure vault URI pointing to the stored image or PDF of the document.']
  document_issue_place varchar [note: 'City or locality where the document was issued.']
  document_language varchar [note: 'Two‑letter language code of the document content.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized identifier of the document (e.g., passport number).']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the identity document submitted for verification.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the document is no longer valid.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored document image.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the stored document file in bytes.']
  is_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document meets all regulatory compliance checks.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the document was originally issued by the authority.']
  issuing_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the authority that issued the document.']
  kyc_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document record.']
  manual_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the manual review after completion.']
  manual_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the human review process for the document.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for additional remarks by reviewers or operators.']
  ocr_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence percentage (0‑100) of the OCR extraction accuracy.']
  ocr_status varchar [note: 'Current status of optical character recognition processing for the document.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the document must be deleted to satisfy regulatory retention rules.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the manual review was finalized.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Method by which the document was submitted to the platform.']
  tamper_detected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document image shows signs of tampering.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the document record for audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Individual identity document submitted as part of a KYC verification. Captures document type (passport, national ID, driver\'s license, utility bill, bank statement), issuing country, document number (tokenized/masked), issue date, expiry date, document image reference (vault URI), OCR extraction status, tamper detection flag, and manual review outcome. Linked to a parent kyc_verification record. Supports regulatory document retention requirements under BSA, PSD2, and FATF standards.'
}

Table cardholder.pan_record {
  pan_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the PAN record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key linking the PAN to its owning cardholder profile.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key linking the PAN to its owning cardholder profile.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Required for card issuance and settlement: each PAN must be associated with its payment scheme (Visa, Mastercard) to route transactions and calculate interchange fees.']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening for the PAN.']
  bin varchar [note: 'First six digits of the PAN identifying the issuing bank and card brand.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network brand associated with the PAN.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the PAN record meets current PCI‑DSS requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for transactions using this PAN. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|AUD|CAD|CHF|CNY|INR|BRL|... — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the PAN expires and can no longer be used for new authorizations.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'True if the PAN has been flagged for suspected fraudulent activity.']
  iin varchar [note: 'First eight digits of the PAN used for routing and issuer identification.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'True if this PAN is the default/primary PAN for the linked cardholder.']
  is_sca_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the PAN is eligible for Strong Customer Authentication under PSD2.']
  issuing_bank_code varchar [note: 'Surrogate key linking to the bank that issued the PAN.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current Know‑Your‑Customer verification state of the cardholder linked to this PAN.']
  last_four varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the PAN, commonly used for verification and customer service.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent successful authorization involving this PAN.']
  masked_pan varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the PAN (e.g., XXXX‑XXXX‑XXXX‑1234) for human‑readable displays.']
  pan_format varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the PAN consists of only numeric characters or includes alphanumeric characters.']
  pan_length varchar [note: 'Number of digits in the PAN (typically 16, 15, or 19).']
  pan_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the PAN within its lifecycle.']
  pan_token varchar [note: 'PCI‑DSS compliant token that represents the PAN; never stores the clear PAN.']
  pan_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the PAN is a physical PAN (FPAN) or a device‑generated token (DPAN).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the PAN record was initially persisted.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the PAN record.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑99.99) indicating fraud likelihood.']
  token_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the PAN token becomes invalid.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for generating and managing the PAN token.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to derive the token from the clear PAN.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of successful transactions processed for this PAN.']

  Note: 'Authoritative PAN (Primary Account Number) registry for cardholders. Stores the tokenized PAN reference (never the raw PAN in clear text per PCI DSS), BIN (Bank Identification Number), IIN (Issuer Identification Number), card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover), PAN type (FPAN physical, DPAN device token), PAN status (active, blocked, expired, replaced), expiry date, and the issuing bank reference. This is the SSOT for PAN-to-cardholder linkage and supports BIN-level routing and interchange qualification.'
}

Table cardholder.payment_credential {
  payment_credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the payment credential record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder who owns this credential.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder who owns this credential.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment instrument (e.g., PAN record) linked to this credential.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to authenticate the credential during a transaction.']
  biometric_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether biometric authentication is enabled for this credential.']
  biometric_type varchar [note: 'Specific biometric modality used (if enabled).']
  contactless_nfc_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'True when NFC/contactless payments are permitted for this credential.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credential record was first created in the system.']
  credential_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label assigned to the credential (e.g., "Primary Card Credential").']
  credential_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the credential record (e.g., Digital Wallet Platform).']
  credential_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the credential.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Category of the credential indicating the underlying payment instrument.']
  credential_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version number for the credential record.']
  cvv2_validation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether CVV2 validation is required for this credential.']
  cvv_hash_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the hashed CVV/CVC stored securely in the HSM.']
  dpan_reference varchar [note: 'Device Primary Account Number linked to this credential.']
  emv_chip_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMV chip authentication is supported.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the credential is no longer valid.']
  external_credential_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., token service provider) to reference this credential.']
  hsm_key_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HSM key used to encrypt credential secrets.']
  issuance_date date [note: 'Date when the credential was originally issued.']
  last_rotation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the credential secrets were last rotated.']
  otp_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one‑time‑password (OTP) authentication is active.']
  otp_last_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the most recent OTP was generated/sent.']
  pan_linkage_status varchar [note: 'Current relationship status between the credential and its PAN/DPAN.']
  pin_last_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the PIN offset was last updated.']
  pin_offset varchar [note: 'Encrypted PIN offset used for offline PIN verification (PCI‑DSS).']
  sca_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True when the credential meets PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication requirements.']
  sca_compliance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when SCA compliance was last verified.']
  three_ds_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'State of Strong Customer Authentication enrollment for this credential.']
  three_ds_method varchar [note: 'Method used for 3‑DS challenge‑response (e.g., OTP, biometric).']
  token_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the token value stored in the token vault.']
  tokenization_status varchar [note: 'State of tokenization for the underlying PAN/DPAN.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credential record.']

  Note: 'Authentication credential set associated with a cardholder\'s payment instrument. Stores CVV/CVC hash reference, CVV2 validation flag, PIN offset (for debit/ATM), 3DS enrollment status, 3DS authentication method (OTP, biometric, out-of-band), SCA compliance flag per PSD2, credential version, last rotation timestamp, and HSM key reference identifier. Distinct from pan_record (which owns the PAN identity) — this entity owns the authentication secrets and SCA compliance state. Supports EMV chip credential management and NFC contactless authentication.'
}

Table cardholder.cardholder_sca_challenge {
  cardholder_sca_challenge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Globally unique identifier for the SCA challenge event.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who received the challenge.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who received the challenge.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device used for the challenge (e.g., mobile device ID).']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment instrument (e.g., PAN or DPAN) used in the transaction.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction associated with the SCA challenge.']
  acs_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ACS that generated the challenge.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the challenge attempt for the same transaction.']
  authentication_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cryptographic authentication value returned by the ACS.']
  challenge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the challenge.']
  challenge_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the challenge was issued to the cardholder.']
  challenge_type varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the SCA challenge to the cardholder.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SCA challenge record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  eci_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator of the authentication outcome used for liability shift.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why SCA was bypassed, if applicable.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of why the challenge failed, if applicable.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device that initiated the challenge.']
  is_successful boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the challenge was successfully completed (true) or not (false).']
  latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds between challenge issuance and final response.']
  three_ds_version varchar [note: 'Version of the 3‑Domain Secure protocol applied.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SCA challenge record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string presented during the challenge.']

  Note: 'Record of each Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) challenge event issued to a cardholder under PSD2/3DS protocols. Captures challenge type (OTP SMS, OTP email, biometric, push notification, out-of-band), challenge status (issued, passed, failed, expired, bypassed), 3DS version, ACS (Access Control Server) reference, authentication value (CAVV/AAV), ECI indicator, challenge timestamp, and exemption reason if SCA was bypassed (low-value, TRA, merchant-initiated). Provides the full SCA audit trail required by PSD2 RTS.'
}

Table cardholder.cardholder_account {
  cardholder_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the cardholder account record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (person) owning the account.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (person) owning the account.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Account statements are issued in a specific currency; linking to reference.currency ensures consistent reporting and fee calculation.']
  fraud_alert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_alert. Business justification: Real‑time monitoring dashboard shows the latest fraud alert linked to an account for risk officers.']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_case. Business justification: Risk management report requires each account to reference its primary fraud case for monitoring and compliance.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Required for daily GL posting of each cardholder credit account balances to financial statements.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tracks the employee account manager responsible for each cardholder account; essential for oversight, risk monitoring, and regulatory audit.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Account statements, fee calculation, and regulatory reporting require linking each cardholder_account to its underlying payment_product (e.g., credit card product).']
  account_number varchar [note: 'External account number assigned to the cardholder account.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the account.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Tier level of the account based on eligibility and benefits.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Category of the financial account.']
  available_credit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current available credit amount.']
  billing_cycle_day varchar [note: 'Day of month when billing cycle starts (1-31).']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the account was closed, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account record was created in the system.']
  credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount authorized for the account.']
  currency_conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account supports multi-currency transactions.']
  current_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Outstanding balance on the account.']
  delinquency_status varchar [note: 'Delinquency bucket based on days past due.']
  dpan_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized device PAN (DPAN) associated with the account.']
  exchange_rate_to_base decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert foreign currency transactions to the statement currency.']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Current fraud risk score assigned to the account (0-100).']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate applied to the account (e.g., 0.1999 for 19.99%).']
  is_overlimit boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the current balance exceeds the credit limit.']
  is_suspended boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the account is currently suspended.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Know Your Customer verification status for the account holder.']
  last_fraud_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent fraud risk review for the account.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent payment.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment applied to the account.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent status change event.']
  minimum_payment_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum payment amount required for the next billing cycle.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date when the account was opened.']
  pan_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a primary account number (PAN) is linked to this account.']
  payment_due_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount due by the payment due date.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Due date for the next payment.']
  product_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the product program under which the account was issued.']
