  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account is subject to special regulatory reporting (e.g., AML).']
  reward_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account participates in a rewards program.']
  sca_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account meets Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the account record originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last update to the account record.']

  Note: 'Financial account record owned by a cardholder on the platform. Represents the account-level construct (credit card account, prepaid account, debit account, BNPL account) that aggregates one or more payment instruments. Stores account type, credit limit, available credit, current balance, billing cycle day, statement currency, account open date, account status (active, delinquent, charged-off, closed), and product program reference. This is the financial account layer sitting between the cardholder identity and individual payment instruments.'
}

Table cardholder.account_status_history {
  account_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each status transition record.']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder account whose status changed.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was created in the system.']
  correlation_code varchar [note: 'Identifier to correlate this event across multiple systems.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the new status became effective.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the status transition event occurred.']
  initiating_actor varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the status change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes about the status change.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the status changed.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human readable description of the reason for status change.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if this record represents a reversal of a previous status change.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that generated the status change event.']
  status_after varchar [note: 'The status of the account after the transition.']
  status_before varchar [note: 'The status of the account prior to the transition.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of all status transitions for a cardholder account. Records each status change event (e.g., active→suspended, suspended→closed), the reason code, initiating actor (system, agent, cardholder self-service), effective timestamp, and reversal flag. Supports regulatory audit trail requirements under SOX, PCI DSS, and CFPB complaint handling. Enables lifecycle analytics and delinquency workflow tracking without mutating the parent cardholder_account record.'
}

Table cardholder.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the cardholder segment record.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Segmentation classification is assigned per profile; linking provides direct association for analytics and targeting.']
  ml_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the machine‑learning model used for ML‑assigned segments.']
  rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule set used for rule‑based assignment.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign the segment to a cardholder (rule‑based, machine‑learning, or manual).']
  audience varchar [note: 'Target audience type for the segment (consumer, business, or both).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment record was first created in the system.']
  criteria_definition varchar [note: 'Textual definition of the rules or model logic that determines segment membership.']
  criteria_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the segment criteria rule set.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the segment definition expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the segment definition becomes active for assignments.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this segment is the default fallback for cardholders without explicit assignment.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment definition was last reviewed or updated by governance.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent cardholder assignment to this segment.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when multiple segments could apply; lower numbers indicate higher precedence.']
  region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating the geographic region the segment applies to.']
  score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric score (e.g., from ML model) representing the strength of fit for the segment.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the segment, used in reporting and integration.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the segment purpose and criteria.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the segment.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment definition.']
  segment_tier varchar [note: 'Business tier level that defines pricing or eligibility (mass market, premium, etc.).']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Category of segmentation (e.g., spend tier, risk tier, loyalty tier, product tier).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the segment record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the segment record.']

  Note: 'Segmentation classification assigned to a cardholder for product targeting, risk tiering, and pricing differentiation. Stores segment code, segment name, segment tier (mass market, premium, ultra-premium, SMB, corporate), assignment method (rule-based, ML-assigned, manual), effective date, expiry date, and the segment criteria version used. Supports product eligibility gating, MDR/MSF pricing tiers, and personalized offer targeting. A cardholder may have one active segment per segment dimension (spend tier, risk tier, loyalty tier).'
}

Table cardholder.consent {
  consent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each consent record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder to whom the consent applies.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder to whom the consent applies.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: GDPR/CCPA consent tracking per partner: required to record which partner’s data processing the cardholder has consented to.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system process that created the consent record.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Consent management is jurisdiction‑specific (GDPR, CCPA) and needs a reference to the applicable regulatory jurisdiction.']
  collection_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the consent was obtained.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consent record.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the consent was granted.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of consent captured, indicating the purpose such as GDPR data processing, CCPA sale opt‑out, marketing communications, data sharing, or biometric usage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent record was first created in the system.']
  data_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the data types covered by the consent.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Optional expiry date/time after which the consent is no longer valid.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device used at the moment of consent capture.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'Legal justification for processing under GDPR, such as consent or legitimate interest.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the data processing activities covered by the consent.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consent record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string recorded during consent collection.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent text or policy that the cardholder agreed to.']
  withdrawal_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the consent was withdrawn.']
  withdrawal_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device used when consent was withdrawn.']
  withdrawal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cardholder withdrew the consent, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Explicit consent and preference record for a cardholder covering data privacy, marketing, and regulatory consent obligations. Captures consent type (GDPR data processing, CCPA sale opt-out, marketing email, marketing SMS, data sharing with partners, biometric data), consent status (granted, withdrawn, pending), consent version, collection channel, IP address at consent time, and withdrawal timestamp. Provides the SSOT for all consent decisions required under GDPR, CCPA, and PSD2 open banking data sharing rules.'
}

Table cardholder.contact_event {
  contact_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each contact event record.']
  agent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the support agent who handled the outbound contact (if applicable).']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder associated with the contact event.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder associated with the contact event.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Contact events are reported by country for regional compliance and SLA tracking, requiring a link to reference.country.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dispute associated with the contact (if applicable).']
  dispute_dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dispute associated with the contact (if applicable).']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the support agent who handled the outbound contact (if applicable).']
  notification_preference_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cardholder\'s communication preference record governing channel consent.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of attachments included with the communication.']
  attachment_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the message included one or more attachments.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance validation was performed.']
  contact_channel varchar [note: 'Medium used for the communication with the cardholder.']
  contact_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the communication was initiated by the cardholder (inbound) or by the platform/agent (outbound).']
  contact_reason varchar [note: 'Business purpose of the contact (e.g., fraud alert, statement ready, payment due, dispute update, KYC request, promotional). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fraud_alert|statement_ready|payment_due|dispute_update|kyc_request|promotional|other] ']
  correlation_code varchar [note: 'Cross‑system identifier used to trace the contact event across multiple services.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contact event record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the outbound message delivery.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery status was recorded.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contact event occurred (e.g., when the message was sent or received).']
  fraud_alert_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contact pertains to a fraud alert or investigation.']
  is_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact complies with the cardholder\'s opt‑in/opt‑out preferences and regulatory rules.']
  is_high_priority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact was marked as high priority (e.g., fraud or regulatory urgency).']
  is_opt_out boolean [note: 'True if the cardholder has opted out of the specified contact channel.']
  kyc_request_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contact is a request for KYC documentation or verification.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code of the communication content.']
  marketing_campaign_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that generated the promotional contact.']
  message_content varchar [note: 'Full body of the communication sent to or received from the cardholder.']
  message_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier of the message (e.g., email Message‑ID, SMS transaction ID).']
  message_subject varchar [note: 'Subject line or title of the communication (relevant for email, push, in‑app messages).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by agents or system logs about the contact.']
  opt_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cardholder opted out of the channel.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Categorized priority of the contact event.']
  promotional_flag boolean [note: 'True if the communication is part of a marketing or promotional campaign.']
  response_deadline timestamp [note: 'Latest timestamp by which the cardholder should respond.']
  response_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inbound response from the cardholder was recorded.']
  response_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a reply from the cardholder is expected.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the expected response.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the contact event record must be purged in accordance with data retention policies.']
  sla_actual_seconds varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed time in seconds from contact initiation to required action completion.']
  sla_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target response time in seconds defined by service‑level agreements for this contact type.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system that originated the event (e.g., CRM, Fraud Detection Platform).']
  system_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal system that generated the contact event (e.g., CRM, Fraud Platform).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contact event record.']

  Note: 'Record of each communication or contact interaction with a cardholder — inbound and outbound. Captures contact channel (email, SMS, push notification, IVR, agent call, in-app message), contact direction (inbound/outbound), contact reason (fraud alert, statement ready, payment due, dispute update, KYC request, promotional), delivery status (sent, delivered, failed, bounced, opened), contact timestamp, and agent/system reference. Supports CFPB complaint handling, SLA tracking, and communication preference enforcement.'
}

Table cardholder.linked_account {
  linked_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the linked external account.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who owns this linked account.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who owns this linked account.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Funding accounts need a canonical currency reference for settlement, FX conversion, and reporting.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Funding source compliance: linking each external account to the partner bank that supplies the funds enables AML checks and settlement routing.']
  account_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the linked account is personal or business.']
  account_holder_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the person or entity that owns the linked account.']
  account_nickname varchar [note: 'User‑defined friendly name for the linked account.']
  account_number_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized representation of the external account number used for secure processing.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the external account (e.g., checking, savings, brokerage).']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the financial institution that holds the linked account.']
  bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC code of the bank that holds the linked account.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent AML/KYC compliance verification for the linked account.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest compliance check for the linked account.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the linked account record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  daily_debit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total amount that can be debited from the linked account per calendar day.']
  daily_debit_used decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount debited from the linked account today.']
  external_account_code varchar [note: 'Original identifier of the account in the external financial institution\'s system.']
  funding_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify and link the external account for funding.']
  iban varchar [note: 'Standardized international bank account identifier for cross‑border linked accounts.']
  is_primary_funding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this linked account is the default source for funding transactions.']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the linked account details are stored as a tokenized value.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction that used this linked account as a funding source.']
  linked_since date [note: 'Date when the external account was first linked to the cardholder.']
  linked_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the linked account within the platform.']
  linked_until date [note: 'Date when the linked account was de‑linked or became inactive (null if still active).']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned to the linked account based on fraud models.']
  routing_number_masked varchar [note: 'Masked routing number of the external account, showing only the last 4 digits.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Origin of the account linking process (e.g., Plaid, Finicity, manual entry).']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the token service provider (TSP) that issued the token for this account.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of funding transactions executed against this linked account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the linked account record.']
  verification_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of verification attempts made for this linked account.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the linked account (e.g., micro‑deposit, instant verification, manual entry).']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the account verification process.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful verification of the linked account.']

  Note: 'External bank account or financial account linked to a cardholder for funding, ACH pull, A2A transfer, or BNPL repayment purposes. Stores account type (checking, savings, brokerage), routing number (masked), account number token, bank name, account holder name, linking method (micro-deposit, instant verification via Plaid/Finicity, manual), verification status, and primary funding flag. Supports ACH debit authorization, IBAN/BIC for cross-border linked accounts, and open banking account aggregation.'
}

Table cardholder.device {
  device_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying a registered cardholder device.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: A device is registered by a specific cardholder; linking device to cardholder_profile eliminates the need for indirect look‑ups via events and enables direct device‑to‑profile queries.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.device_fingerprint. Business justification: Unified device profiling links registered cardholder device to its fraud fingerprint record for accurate risk scoring.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the most recent compliance assessment was performed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest compliance check (e.g., security posture, OS version).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the device record was created in the system.']
  device_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name assigned by the cardholder to the device (e.g., \'John’s iPhone\').']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of the device used for digital wallet or authentication.']
  dpan_provisioned boolean [note: 'True if a Device Primary Account Number has been provisioned to the device.']
  fingerprint_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash representing the device fingerprint used for risk scoring.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the device firmware, if applicable.']
  hce_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device can perform Host Card Emulation for tokenized payments.']
  imei varchar [note: 'Unique hardware identifier for mobile devices, used for device tracking and fraud detection.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address observed during the last activity of the device.']
  last_active_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent timestamp when the device was used for a transaction or authentication.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the device’s last known location (if available).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the device’s last known location (if available).']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that manufactured the device (e.g., Apple, Samsung).']
  model varchar [note: 'Model identifier of the device (e.g., iPhone 13, Galaxy S22).']
  nfc_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports Near Field Communication for contactless payments.']
  os_name varchar [note: 'Name of the operating system running on the device (e.g., iOS, Android).']
  os_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the operating system (e.g., 16.4).']
  push_notification_token varchar [note: 'Token used to send push notifications to the device.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the device registration.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the device was first registered to the cardholder.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the device record.']
  uuid varchar [note: 'Universally unique identifier assigned to the device within the platform.']

  Note: 'Mobile device or hardware token registered by a cardholder for digital wallet provisioning, HCE (Host Card Emulation), NFC payments, or 3DS out-of-band authentication. Stores device type (iOS, Android, wearable, browser), device fingerprint hash, device name, OS version, NFC capability flag, HCE capability flag, device registration status (active, deactivated, suspended), DPAN provisioning status, and last active timestamp. Distinct from wallet domain device records — this entity owns the cardholder-device relationship and authentication eligibility.'
}

Table cardholder.authentication_event {
  authentication_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each authentication event record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder whose authentication was attempted.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder whose authentication was attempted.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Authentication events use geolocation country for risk scoring; a FK to reference.country provides standardized country data.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device used for authentication.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Audit of authentication providers: each SCA event must record the authentication partner (e.g., 3DS provider) for SLA and dispute analysis.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user session associated with the authentication attempt.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the attempt within the current authentication session.']
  authentication_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the authentication request was made.']
  authentication_factor varchar [note: 'Specific factor used in the authentication (e.g., password, OTP, biometric).']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate the cardholder (e.g., password, OTP, biometric).']
  authentication_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the authentication attempt.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authentication event record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of the device (e.g., mobile, desktop, POS terminal).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the authentication attempt occurred.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for authentication failure when outcome is failure or lockout.']
  geolocation_city varchar [note: 'City inferred from the IP address or device location.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address from which the authentication request originated.']
  latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds between request receipt and response.']
  lockout_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder account was locked as a result of this attempt.']
  risk_flags varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of risk indicators (e.g., high_velocity|blacklist|suspicious_location).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk assessment generated by fraud detection engine for this attempt.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the authentication event record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string presented during authentication.']

  Note: 'Record of each cardholder authentication attempt across all channels — online banking login, mobile app login, IVR PIN entry, card activation, and password reset. Captures authentication method (password, OTP, biometric fingerprint, biometric face, FIDO2 passkey, knowledge-based), authentication outcome (success, failure, lockout), failure reason, IP address, geolocation, device reference, session ID, and risk signal flags. Supports fraud detection correlation, account takeover (ATO) detection, and PCI DSS access control audit requirements.'
}

Table cardholder.cardholder_velocity_control {
  cardholder_velocity_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the velocity control record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder to whom this control applies.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder to whom this control applies.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Country‑specific velocity limits are required for fraud prevention and regulatory caps, necessitating a link to reference.country.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: Velocity controls are often MCC‑based; a foreign key to reference.mcc enables rule management and risk scoring.']
  cardholder_velocity_control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Payment channel to which the control applies (e.g., Card-Not-Present, Card-Present).']
  control_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the control definition: issuer-set, cardholder self-service, or regulatory mandate.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Category of the velocity control (e.g., spending limit, transaction count, MCC restriction).']
  control_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the control for change tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was created.']
  daily_spent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount spent by the cardholder today under this control.']
  daily_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of transactions performed today under this control.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the control becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the control expires or is no longer applicable; null for indefinite.']
  enforcement_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority for rule evaluation when multiple controls apply; lower numbers have higher priority.']
  is_exempted boolean [note: 'Indicates if the cardholder is exempted from this control (e.g., corporate card).']
  last_evaluated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction evaluation against this control.']
  limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount limit for the control (e.g., daily spend cap).']
  limit_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the limit amount.']
  limit_period varchar [note: 'Time window for the monetary limit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information or business rationale.']
  transaction_count_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions allowed in the defined period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']
  violation_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of times the control was violated.']

  Note: 'Cardholder-level velocity and spending control configuration. Defines per-cardholder or per-account spending limits (daily, weekly, monthly), transaction count limits, single-transaction amount caps, MCC-level restrictions, geographic restrictions (allowed/blocked countries), channel restrictions (CNP, CP, ATM, online), and control source (issuer-set, cardholder self-service, regulatory mandate). Supports real-time authorization decisioning and parental/corporate card control programs. Each active control record represents a currently enforced rule.'
}

Table cardholder.notification_preference {
  notification_preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each cardholder notification preference record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder to whom this preference applies.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder to whom this preference applies.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Category of transaction event that triggers a notification.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Delivery channel selected by the cardholder for this alert type.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the preference record was initially created.']
  device_token varchar [note: 'Push notification device token used when the channel is push.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the preference becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the preference is no longer active (null if open‑ended).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Email address to which email notifications are sent when the channel is email.']
  is_sca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the notification preference complies with Strong Customer Authentication requirements.']
  last_notified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent notification sent under this preference.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional remarks or business rules related to the preference.']
  notification_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the cardholder wishes to receive notifications of this type.']
  notification_preference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the notification preference.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Phone number to which SMS notifications are sent when the channel is sms.']
  preference_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the notification preference configuration.']
  threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount that must be exceeded to trigger the alert (if applicable).']
  threshold_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the threshold amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the preference record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Optimistic‑lock version counter for concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Cardholder-configured notification and alert preferences governing when and how the platform communicates transaction events. Stores alert type (transaction above threshold, foreign transaction, declined transaction, statement ready, payment due, suspicious activity), preferred channel (push, SMS, email), threshold amounts, enabled/disabled flag, and last updated timestamp. Distinct from cardholder_consent (which governs legal consent) — this entity governs operational alert delivery preferences set by the cardholder.'
}

Table cardholder.corporate_cardholder {
  corporate_cardholder_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the corporate cardholder entity.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.account_holder. Business justification: Corporate cardholder entity represents a business linked to a master account holder record; linking provides ownership and compliance context.']
  corporate_hierarchy_parent_corporate_cardholder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediate parent corporate entity in the corporate hierarchy.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Needed for regulatory reporting and consolidation of corporate cardholder activity under its legal entity.']
  annual_revenue_band varchar [note: 'Revenue range bucket for the corporate entity.']
  authorized_signatory_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the authorized signatory.']
  authorized_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the person authorized to sign on behalf of the corporate account.']
  authorized_signatory_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the authorized signatory.']
  business_line_of_service varchar [note: 'Primary line of service or product offering for the corporate client (e.g., merchant acquiring, digital wallet).']
  corporate_account_number varchar [note: 'Internal account number assigned to the corporate cardholder within the payment platform.']
  corporate_account_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the corporate account.']
  corporate_cardholder_status varchar [note: 'Operational lifecycle status of the corporate cardholder record.']
  corporate_credit_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the corporate card program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the corporate cardholder record was first created.']
  credit_limit_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the corporate credit limit.']
  dpan_linkage_status varchar [note: 'Status of the linkage between the corporate PAN and device‑generated DPAN.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the corporate cardholder relationship becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the corporate cardholder relationship ends or is scheduled to end (null if open‑ended).']
  employee_count_band varchar [note: 'Employee head‑count range bucket for the corporate entity.']
  industry_classification_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code describing the corporate\'s primary business activity.']
  kyb_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the Know‑Your‑Business compliance assessment.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the corporate KYC verification process.']
  last_kyb_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent KYB compliance review.']
  last_kyc_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent KYC review for the corporate entity.']
  legal_business_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the corporate entity holding the card program.']
  primary_contact_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the corporate primary mailing address.']
  primary_contact_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the corporate primary mailing address (optional).']
  primary_contact_city varchar [note: 'City component of the corporate primary mailing address.']
  primary_contact_country_iso_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the corporate primary mailing address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Main email address for corporate communications.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for corporate communications.']
  primary_contact_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the corporate primary mailing address.']
  primary_contact_state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the corporate primary mailing address.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued registration identifier for the corporate entity.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the corporate entity.']
  risk_score_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the current risk score was calculated.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax ID used for reporting and compliance (e.g., EIN, VAT).']
  tokenization_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the corporate card number has been tokenized for secure storage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the corporate cardholder record.']

  Note: 'B2B corporate cardholder entity representing a business entity (company, SMB, sole trader) that holds a corporate card program account. Stores legal business name, company registration number, tax identification number (EIN/VAT), industry classification (SIC/NAICS), annual revenue band, employee count band, corporate hierarchy parent reference, authorized signatory details, and corporate credit limit. Distinct from account_holder (which covers both B2C and B2B) — this entity owns the extended B2B-specific attributes required for corporate card underwriting and KYB (Know Your Business) compliance.'
}

Table cardholder.authorized_user {
  authorized_user_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the authorized user record.']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_account. Business justification: Authorized users are attached to a primary cardholder account; linking reflects the hierarchical relationship and enables account‑level controls.']
  authorization_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of permissions granted to the authorized user on the primary account.']
  authorized_user_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the authorized user.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authorized user record was first created in the system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the authorized user, used for age verification and regulatory compliance.']
  dpan varchar [note: 'Tokenized representation of the PAN used on the device for tokenization and secure transactions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the authorized user became active on the primary account.']
  email varchar [note: 'Email address used for notifications and account recovery.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the authorized user can no longer transact; typically aligns with card expiration.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the authorized user as it appears on the issued card.']
  is_test_user boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record is a synthetic test user for QA or sandbox environments.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of Know‑Your‑Customer verification for the authorized user.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for internal comments or special handling instructions.']
  pan varchar [note: '16‑digit card number assigned to the authorized user; stored in encrypted form per PCI DSS.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Phone number for SMS alerts and two‑factor authentication.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for communicating with the authorized user.']
  primary_contact_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contact detail matching the selected primary_contact_method (e.g., email address or phone number).']
  relationship_to_primary varchar [note: 'Describes how the authorized user is related to the primary cardholder account.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Categorical risk tier derived from the risk_score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned by fraud detection models.']
  sca_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the authorized user meets Strong Customer Authentication requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the authorized user record.']
  spending_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount the authorized user may spend per transaction or period, as defined by the primary account agreement.']
  spending_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the spending limit.']
  token_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the token generated for the authorized user\'s card number in the token vault.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the authorized user record.']

  Note: 'Secondary cardholder or authorized user added to a primary cardholder account. Captures authorized user identity (name, DOB, relationship to primary), spending limit assigned to this user, card number reference (separate PAN issued), authorization scope (full access, spend-only, read-only), effective date, expiry date, and status (active, suspended, removed). Supports family card programs, corporate employee card issuance, and supplementary card management. Each authorized user has their own PAN but shares the primary account\'s credit facility.'
}

Table cardholder.program_enrollment {
  program_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the enrollment record linking a cardholder to a program.']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the program or product offering to which the cardholder is enrolled.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder (person) who is enrolled in the program.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder (person) who is enrolled in the program.']
  aml_screened_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cardholder was screened against AML watchlists during enrollment.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the enrollment (e.g., PCI DSS, AML).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the enrollment became active (may differ from enrollment_timestamp for retroactive enrollments).']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the enrollment expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended enrollments.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the cardholder enrolled (e.g., online portal, mobile app, physical branch, call center, partner).']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment.']
  enrollment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the enrollment became effective.']
  is_primary_enrollment boolean [note: 'True if this is the cardholder\'s primary or default program enrollment.']
  is_test_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enrollment belongs to a test or sandbox cardholder.']
  kyc_status_at_enrollment varchar [note: 'Know‑Your‑Customer verification status of the cardholder at the time of enrollment.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to enrollment_status.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder consented to receive marketing communications for this program.']
  next_tier_threshold varchar [note: 'Points required to reach the next program tier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by operations or support staff about the enrollment.']
  points_earned_to_date varchar [note: 'Total reward points earned by the cardholder since enrollment.']
  points_redeemed_to_date varchar [note: 'Total reward points redeemed by the cardholder since enrollment.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the program offering.']
  program_tier varchar [note: 'Tier or level of the program assigned to the cardholder (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum).']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Category of the program (e.g., rewards, Buy‑Now‑Pay‑Later, premium tier, co‑brand, loyalty).']
  reward_points_balance varchar [note: 'Current accumulated reward points for the cardholder within this program.']
  sanctions_screened_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cardholder was screened against sanctions lists (OFAC, UN) at enrollment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that created the enrollment record (e.g., Digital Wallet Platform, CRM).']
  status_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the most recent status transition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service that created the enrollment.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a cardholder account to a specific card program or product offering (rewards program, BNPL plan, premium tier, co-brand program, loyalty scheme). Captures program reference, enrollment date, enrollment channel, enrollment status (active, pending, cancelled, expired), program tier, accumulated reward points balance, and next tier threshold. This is the instance-level enrollment record — the program definition itself lives in the product domain. Supports multi-program enrollment and program migration tracking.'
}

Table cardholder.cardholder_aml_screening_result {
  cardholder_aml_screening_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each AML screening result record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder whose information was screened.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder whose information was screened.']
  analyst_review_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the compliance analyst who reviewed the screening.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Final compliance determination for the cardholder based on screening.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Outcome of the screening result after analyst review.']
  is_adverse_media boolean [note: 'True if negative media coverage was identified.']
  is_pep boolean [note: 'True if the cardholder matches a PEP watchlist.']
  is_sanctioned boolean [note: 'True if the screening identified a sanctions match.']
  is_watchlist boolean [note: 'True if the screening matched any watchlist entry.']
  match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score (0‑100) representing the strength of the match.']
  match_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the screening found no match, a potential match, or a confirmed match.']
  matched_entity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organization identified as a match.']
  matched_list_name varchar [note: 'The specific list (e.g., OFAC SDN List) that produced the match.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations recorded by the reviewer.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the screening result record was first inserted.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the result must be reported to regulators (e.g., FinCEN SAR).']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Decision made by the analyst after reviewing the match.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the analyst finalized the disposition.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification applied to the cardholder after screening.']
  screening_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party service used to conduct the AML screening.']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the AML/sanctions screening was executed.']
  screening_type varchar [note: 'Category of screening applied (e.g., OFAC, PEP, adverse media, watchlist).']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Business process that initiated the screening.']

  Note: 'AML (Anti-Money Laundering) and sanctions screening result record for a cardholder. Captures screening type (OFAC/SDN check, PEP screening, adverse media, watchlist), screening provider, screening timestamp, match status (no_match, potential_match, confirmed_match), match score, matched entity name, matched list name, disposition (cleared, escalated, blocked), and analyst review reference. Each onboarding event and periodic re-screening generates a new record. Supports BSA/AML compliance, FinCEN SAR filing workflows, and FATF risk-based approach requirements.'
}

Table cardholder.cardholder_risk_profile {
  cardholder_risk_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cardholder risk profile record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder to which this risk profile belongs.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder to which this risk profile belongs.']
  aml_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Anti‑Money‑Laundering risk rating based on AML screening results.']
  behavioral_risk_indicators varchar [note: 'Free‑text or coded list of observed behavioral patterns that increase risk (e.g., rapid spend spikes, atypical merchant categories).']
  compliance_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manual compliance review is required for this profile.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk profile record was first created.']
  credit_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical credit risk score derived from credit underwriting models.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the risk profile became effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the risk profile expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  fraud_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical fraud risk score generated by the fraud detection engine.']
  geographic_risk_flag boolean [note: 'True when the cardholder\'s location is considered high‑risk for fraud or AML.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next risk reassessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by risk analysts.']
  overall_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score aggregating credit, fraud, AML, and other dimensions.']
  pep_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder is identified as a Politically Exposed Person (PEP).']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel used to contact the cardholder for risk‑related communications.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the risk profile instance.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if this profile must be included in regulatory reporting (e.g., SAR, AML reports).']
  risk_assessment_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the risk scoring model used to generate scores.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of risk represented by this profile.']
  risk_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk profile.']
  risk_score_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent risk score calculation.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Overall risk tier classification for the cardholder (low, medium, high, very high).']
  sanctions_flag boolean [note: 'True if the cardholder appears on any sanctions list (OFAC, UN, etc.).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the risk data (e.g., risk_engine, fraud_platform).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the risk profile.']

  Note: 'Aggregated risk profile record for a cardholder capturing the current risk posture used in authorization and underwriting decisions. Stores overall risk tier (low, medium, high, very_high), credit risk score, fraud risk score, AML risk rating, PEP (Politically Exposed Person) flag, sanctions flag, geographic risk flag, behavioral risk indicators, risk profile effective date, and next review date. This is the operational risk state record — distinct from individual screening events (aml_screening_result) and transaction-level fraud scores (owned by fraud domain). Updated by risk domain processes but owned here as the cardholder\'s risk identity.'
}

Table cardholder.relationship {
  relationship_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cardholder relationship record.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary cardholder in the relationship.']
  relationship_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary cardholder in the relationship.']
  relationship_related_cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the secondary cardholder linked to the primary cardholder.']
  compliance_requirements_met varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of compliance checks satisfied for this relationship (e.g., FinCEN_CDD, KYC, AML).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the relationship record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_expiry date [note: 'Date after which the relationship record must be purged or archived per regulatory policy.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates the directional nature of the relationship between the two cardholders.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the relationship becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the relationship ends or is scheduled to expire (null for open‑ended).']
  is_primary_relationship boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this relationship is the primary one for household‑level credit exposure aggregation.']
  is_test_relationship boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record is synthetic test data used for development or QA.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the most recent update.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations about the relationship.']
  relationship_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context about the relationship.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the relationship.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the relationship (e.g., household_member, corporate_subsidiary, guarantor, beneficial_owner, joint_account_holder, power_of_attorney). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: household_member|corporate_subsidiary|guarantor|beneficial_owner|joint_account_holder|power_of_attorney|other — promote to reference product]']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned to the relationship for AML typology detection.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the relationship record (e.g., CRM, Risk Management System).']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining the current status of the relationship.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the relationship record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Compliance verification state of the relationship for AML/CTF purposes.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the relationship verification was performed.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the relationship record.']

  Note: 'Hierarchical and associative relationship record between cardholder entities. Captures relationship type (household member, corporate subsidiary, guarantor, beneficial owner, joint account holder, power of attorney), relationship direction, effective date, expiry date, and relationship status. Supports household-level credit exposure aggregation, corporate beneficial ownership mapping (required under FinCEN CDD Rule), joint account management, and family/group product eligibility. Enables graph traversal of cardholder networks for AML typology detection.'
}

Table cardholder.authentication_session {
  authentication_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for authentication_session']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who initiated the authentication session.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Authentication sessions are performed by a cardholder; adding a direct FK to cardholder_profile improves session reporting and aligns with the profile‑centric data model.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used for the authentication attempt.']
  step_up_authentication_session_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on authentication_session (step_up_authentication_session_id)']
  authentication_channel varchar [note: 'Digital or physical channel through which the authentication request was made.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate the cardholder (e.g., password, biometric, one‑time‑passcode).']
  authentication_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score (0‑100) calculated by the fraud engine for this session.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authentication session record was first persisted.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Hashed representation of device characteristics used for fraud detection.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description when the session fails (e.g., invalid credentials, timeout).']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the request origin. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|AUS|CHN|IND|BRA — promote to reference product]']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the authentication request originated.']
  is_sca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the session satisfied regulatory SCA requirements.']
  location_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the device at authentication time, if available.']
  location_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the device at authentication time, if available.']
  mfa_type varchar [note: 'Method of multi‑factor authentication applied when mfa_used is true.']
  mfa_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether multi‑factor authentication was employed for this session.']
  sca_version varchar [note: 'Version of the SCA framework applied to the session.']
  session_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total length of the authentication session in seconds.']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the authentication session ended or was terminated.']
  session_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the authentication session began.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the authentication session.']
  session_token varchar [note: 'Opaque token generated for the session, used for subsequent authorisation calls.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the authentication session record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string presented during the session.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for authentication_session. Referenced by session_id.'
}

Table settlement.cycle {
  cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cycle']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Currency standardization is required for multi‑currency settlement accounting and compliance reporting.']
  ledger_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.ledger_config. Business justification: Accounting teams need to know which ledger configuration a settlement cycle posts to for GL mapping; new column follows default naming.']
  payment_rail_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.payment_rail. Business justification: Settlement cycles must be tied to a payment rail for rail‑specific settlement rules and regulatory reporting.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification status for the settlement cycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement cycle record was created in the system.']
  cut_off_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the cut-off deadline for transaction inclusion in this cycle.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible code identifying the settlement cycle, used in operational reporting.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement cycle.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement cycle ends; nullable for open-ended cycles.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the settlement cycle includes cross-border transactions.']
  net_position decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net position of the settlement participant after this cycle.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after fees and adjustments to be settled for the cycle.']
  processing_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement cycle is processed automatically by the system or requires manual intervention.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the cycle must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  settlement_cycle_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the settlement cycle purpose or characteristics.']
  settlement_cycle_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the settlement cycle.']
  settlement_window varchar [note: 'Human-readable representation of the time window for the cycle (e.g., \'09:00-17:00 UTC\').']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement cycle becomes effective.']
  total_fees_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees deducted from the gross amount for the cycle.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate gross amount of all transactions before fees and adjustments.']
  total_transactions varchar [note: 'Number of individual transactions included in the settlement cycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement cycle record.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which settled funds are considered available to the receiving party.']

  Note: 'Master definition of a settlement cycle — the scheduled window during which transactions are batched, cleared, and settled between acquiring banks, issuing banks, and card schemes. Owns cycle identifiers, cut-off times, settlement rail (RTGS, RTP, ACH, SWIFT), value date, cycle status, and participating network scheme. SSOT for settlement timing and cycle configuration.'
}

Table settlement.settlement_batch {
  settlement_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the settlement batch record.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Batch‑level reporting of fees and risk must reference the exact card scheme entity.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Batch totals are reported per currency; linking to currency reference ensures consistent ISO codes.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user or service that created the batch record.']
  funding_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the funding schedule that drives fund movements for the batch.']
  originating_acquirer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank that originated the batch.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle to which this batch belongs.']
  settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank that originated the batch.']
  acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was accepted by the card scheme or clearing house (nullable).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was approved for submission.']
  approval_user varchar [note: 'User who approved the batch for submission.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed audit trail record for this batch.']
  batch_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the settlement batch.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the batch (e.g., RTGS, RTP, ACH). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RTGS|RTP|ACH|BATCH|FILE|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum (e.g., SHA‑256) of the batch file for integrity verification.']
  compliance_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance review for the batch.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification status of the batch.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch record was first created in the data lake.']
  cross_border_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency code of the foreign currency involved in cross‑border settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CHF|... — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the batch becomes effective for settlement processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the batch is no longer valid (nullable).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied to convert foreign currency to settlement currency for cross‑border batches.']
  exchange_rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exchange rate was captured.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'File name of the batch file generated for clearing and settlement.']
  gross_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit (incoming) amount of all transactions in the batch before netting.']
  gross_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total debit (outgoing) amount of all transactions in the batch before netting.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the batch contains cross‑border transactions.']
  net_position_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net position (balance) of the settlement participant after this batch is applied.']
  net_position_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the net position amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CHF|CAD|... — promote to reference product]']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount to be settled after debits and credits are netted (gross_credit - gross_debit).']
  processing_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal code of the processing center handling the batch.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch is subject to regulatory reporting (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the scheme or clearing house when the batch is rejected (nullable).']
  reserve_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number of the reserve account used for net settlement buffering.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to the batch within a settlement cycle.']
  settlement_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the scheme or clearing house for processing the batch.']
  settlement_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the settlement fee amount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CHF|... — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_instructions_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the settlement instruction file associated with the batch.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to settle the batch (e.g., wire, ACH, internal transfer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WIRE|ACH|INTERNAL|SWIFT|... — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the net settlement was executed (nullable).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was submitted to the settlement engine.']
  total_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual transactions included in the batch.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch record.']

  Note: 'Represents a discrete batch of transactions submitted for clearing and settlement within a given settlement cycle. Captures batch sequence number, batch file reference, originating acquirer, card scheme, total transaction count, gross debit amount, gross credit amount, net settlement amount, currency, batch status (open, submitted, accepted, rejected, settled), and submission timestamp. Sourced from the Transaction Processing Platform batch processing module.'
}

Table settlement.instruction {
  instruction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for instruction']
  compliance_aml_screening_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_aml_screening_result. Business justification: AML screening is performed per instruction; linking the result enables SAR filing and regulatory reporting.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Currency of the instruction drives conversion rates and regulatory reporting.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: REQUIRED: Each settlement instruction for a cross‑border payment uses a specific currency pair; needed for FX rate selection and audit.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the party that receives the funds (e.g., issuing bank or settlement participant).']
  instruction_settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the party that funds the instruction (e.g., acquiring bank or merchant).']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.journal_entry. Business justification: Instruction processing generates a journal entry; linking enables traceability from instruction to accounting entry.']
  net_position_id varchar [note: 'Link to the net settlement position that aggregates multiple instructions.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Settlement instruction processing requires the payment product to calculate fees and generate regulatory reports; linking to payment_product provides the product type (card, ACH, BNPL) used for the tr']
  payment_rail_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.payment_rail. Business justification: Each settlement instruction must identify the payment rail for routing and compliance checks.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'FK to transaction.payment_txn.payment_txn_id — MUST-HAVE: Enables tracing a settlement instruction to its originating transaction — essential for settlement reconciliation and merchant payout verification.']
  receiving_party_settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the party that receives the funds (e.g., issuing bank or settlement participant).']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Audit trail linking each settlement instruction to its originating gateway request for compliance and dispute resolution.']
  reversal_of_instruction_id varchar [note: 'If this is a reversal, points to the original instruction being reversed.']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the party that funds the instruction (e.g., acquiring bank or merchant).']
  sanctions_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_check. Business justification: Regulatory requirement: each settlement instruction must be linked to its sanctions screening result for audit and transaction blocking.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement cycle (e.g., daily, hourly) that this instruction belongs to.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch file or group that contains this instruction.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of anti‑money‑laundering screening for the instruction.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount to be transferred.']
  bic varchar [note: 'BIC/SWIFT code of the receiving bank.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of any compliance‑related condition attached to the instruction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the instruction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_conversion_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the instruction required currency conversion.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied when converting between original and settlement currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any fee charged for processing the instruction.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the fee amount.']
  iban varchar [note: 'IBAN of the receiving account when applicable.']
  instruction_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier assigned to the instruction, used for tracking and reconciliation.']
  instruction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the instruction.']
  instruction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the real‑world event that triggered the instruction (e.g., value date time).']
  instruction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the instruction adds (credit) or subtracts (debit) funds.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for operational comments or special handling instructions.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount in the original transaction currency before conversion.']
  original_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the original transaction amount.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Technical processing state of the instruction within the settlement engine.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this instruction is a reversal of a prior instruction.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned by the fraud/risk engine.']
  sanction_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening for the instruction.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date the instruction was actually settled in the ledger.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to settle the instruction (netting vs. gross).']
  settlement_rail_details varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form details about the rail (e.g., SWIFT message type, ACH batch number).']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current settlement outcome of the instruction.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that generated the instruction (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the instruction record.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the funds are considered to be settled.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version of the instruction record for audit‑trail purposes.']

  Note: 'Individual fund movement instruction generated for a settlement obligation — specifying the paying party, receiving party, amount, currency, value date, payment rail (RTGS, RTP, ACH, SWIFT), IBAN/BIC routing details, and instruction status. Each instruction maps to a net settlement position and drives the actual fund transfer. Sourced from the Transaction Processing Platform settlement module.'
}

Table settlement.net_position {
  net_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the net settlement position record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Net positions are aggregated per currency for balance sheet and audit purposes.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the second participant (e.g., issuing bank or scheme) in the settlement pair.']
  primary_net_settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the first participant (e.g., acquiring bank) in the settlement pair.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle to which this net position belongs.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for any adjustment applied to the net position.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch file associated with this net position.']
  confirmation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the net position has been confirmed for fund movement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the net position record was created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the net position becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the net position expires or is superseded.']
  gross_credit_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross credit amount for the participant pair before netting.']
  gross_debit_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross debit amount for the participant pair before netting.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'Indicates if the net position has been adjusted post initial calculation.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after debits and credits are netted (credit minus debit).']
  net_position_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number for the net position.']
  net_position_source varchar [note: 'Source system or process that generated the net position (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  net_position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the net position.']
  netting_method varchar [note: 'Method used to net the positions.']
  note varchar [note: 'Free‑text note or comment regarding the net position.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Category of the net position.']
  settlement_cycle_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp representing the start of the settlement cycle.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement is scheduled to occur.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the net position record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the net position record for concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Computed net settlement position for each participant pair (acquirer–issuer, acquirer–scheme, or scheme–acquirer) within a settlement cycle. Stores gross debit total, gross credit total, net amount, currency, netting method (bilateral, multilateral), position type (transaction netting, interchange, scheme fee), position status, and confirmation flags. After merging interchange and scheme fee settlement records, this product is the unified SSOT for all settlement obligations before fund movement instructions are generated.'
}

Table settlement.funding_confirmation {
  funding_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the funding confirmation record.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Funding confirmations for disputed settlements must reference the dispute case to satisfy compliance and reporting rules.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bank that provided the funding confirmation.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle (e.g., daily, hourly) associated with this confirmation.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement instruction that this confirmation relates to.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to audit trail entry for this confirmation.']
