  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch file identifier used for the settlement batch containing this confirmation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes regarding the confirmation.']
  confirmation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the confirming bank for the fund transfer.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the confirmation was received from the confirming bank.']
  confirming_bank_account varchar [note: 'Bank account number of the confirming bank used for the fund transfer.']
  confirming_bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the confirming bank.']
  created_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that created the record (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the funding confirmation record was created in the system.']
  fund_transfer_type varchar [note: 'Method used to transfer funds.']
  funding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of funds confirmed.']
  funding_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the funding confirmation.']
  funding_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the confirmed funds.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the confirmation was processed automatically.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of reconciliation between the confirmation and settlement records.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this confirmation is required for regulatory reporting.']
  rtgs_rtp_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the RTGS or RTP transaction that moved the funds.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement is considered effective.']
  updated_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the funding confirmation record.']

  Note: 'Record of confirmed fund receipt or payment acknowledgment for a settlement instruction. Captures confirmation reference, confirming bank, confirmation timestamp, confirmed amount, currency, RTGS/RTP transaction reference, and reconciliation status. Provides the authoritative proof-of-payment for each settlement obligation. Sourced from the Transaction Processing Platform reconciliation module.'
}

Table settlement.clearing_record {
  clearing_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the clearing record.']
  clearing_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for clearing_record']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.card_program. Business justification: Clearing records must map each transaction to its card program to allocate scheme fees correctly and satisfy scheme‑specific reporting requirements.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Clearing records need the exact card scheme reference for fee calculation and scheme‑level reporting.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank that processed the transaction.']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_case. Business justification: Clearing records flagged as fraud need a direct reference to the fraud case for audit trails and compliance reporting.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that originated the transaction.']
  primary_clearing_settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank that processed the transaction.']
  response_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_response. Business justification: Reconciliation of cleared transaction with the exact gateway response to verify amounts and fraud flags.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original authorization transaction that is being cleared.']
  authorization_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the acquiring network for the original authorization.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch file that contains this clearing record.']
  cleared_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the transaction cleared before fees.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the transaction was cleared.']
  clearing_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the clearing record by the clearing system.']
  clearing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the clearing record.']
  clearing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the transaction was cleared in the processing platform.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record required additional compliance review (e.g., AML, sanctions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the clearing record was first persisted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total interchange and processing fees applied to the cleared transaction.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was flagged as fraudulent during clearing.']
  interchange_category varchar [note: 'Category used to determine interchange fees for the transaction.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clearing record represents a reversal transaction.']
  issuing_bank_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issuing bank that issued the payment instrument.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount to be settled after deducting fees.']
  original_authorization_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuer approving the original transaction.']
  processing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the clearing event was processed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: many values — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reporting_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates the reporting requirement status for this clearing record.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing why the transaction was reversed.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned by the risk engine at clearing time.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency bucket used for net settlement processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the clearing record.']

  Note: 'Individual cleared transaction record produced during the clearing phase — the post-authorization, pre-settlement representation of a payment transaction. Stores ARN (Acquirer Reference Number), original authorization code, MID, card scheme, interchange category, cleared amount, currency, clearing date, and clearing status. Bridges the transaction domain and the settlement domain. Sourced from the Transaction Processing Platform clearing module.'
}

Table settlement.reconciliation_record {
  reconciliation_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the reconciliation record.']
  account_reconciliation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.account_reconciliation. Business justification: Reconciliation records are tied to account reconciliation entries to close GL balances; essential for month‑end close.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Reconciliation must reconcile amounts in a standardized currency reference.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Reconciliation of settlement batches includes disputed transactions; a FK to the dispute case enables exception handling and audit.']
  duplicate_of_record_reconciliation_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original reconciliation record when this record is a duplicate.']
  event_log_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_event_log. Business justification: Regulatory audit requires linking each reconciliation record to the gateway event log that impacted it.']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_case. Business justification: Reconciliation records with fraud_flag must reference the associated fraud case to resolve exceptions and meet regulatory requirements.']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external party (e.g., acquiring bank, issuing bank, scheme) involved in the reconciliation.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle (e.g., daily, hourly) for this reconciliation.']
  settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external party (e.g., acquiring bank, issuing bank, scheme) involved in the reconciliation.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch associated with this reconciliation.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening result for the counterparty.']
  amount_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of fees, discounts, or other adjustments applied to the gross amount.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount before any adjustments or fees.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount after adjustments, representing the amount to be settled.']
  closing_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Closing balance of the account or statement at the end of the reconciliation period.']
  comment varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comment about the reconciliation.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance outcome of the reconciliation with respect to regulatory rules.']
  counterpart_record_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the external record (e.g., bank statement, scheme file) matched against.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the system.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation event occurred.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code categorising the type of exception, if any.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation was flagged for potential fraud.']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'True if this record is identified as a duplicate of another reconciliation record.']
  is_exception boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation record required exception handling.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Know‑Your‑Customer verification status of the counterparty.']
  last_reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful reconciliation for this record.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Opening balance of the account or statement at the start of the reconciliation period.']
  processing_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time taken to complete the reconciliation process, measured in seconds.']
  reconciliation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the reconciliation.']
  reconciliation_record_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the reconciliation record.']
  reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'External or internal reference number for the reconciliation run (e.g., batch number, instruction ID).']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Category of reconciliation (batch, instruction, nostro statement, scheme file).']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if this reconciliation must be included in regulatory reporting.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing how variances were investigated and resolved.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned to the reconciliation based on fraud or AML considerations.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement associated with this reconciliation was executed.']
  source_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external file (e.g., MT940, camt.053) used for reconciliation.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal settlement record used in the reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that produced the source record (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between internal settlement amount and external counterpart amount.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any variance detected during reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Reconciliation entry matching internal settlement records against external counterparts — including bank statements, scheme clearing files, RTGS confirmations, and nostro/vostro account statements (SWIFT MT940/MT950, camt.053). Captures reconciliation run reference, reconciliation type (batch, instruction, nostro, scheme file), matched/unmatched status, variance amount, source record reference, counterpart record reference, opening and closing balances (for statement-level reconciliation), reconciliation method, and resolution notes. SSOT for all settlement reconciliation including nostro statement matching and break management.'
}

Table settlement.settlement_account {
  merchant_settlement_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the settlement account record.']
  settlement_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the settlement_account data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Account balances are maintained in a defined ISO currency for reporting and risk.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the party (acquirer, issuer, or scheme) that owns the settlement account.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Each settlement account must be linked to a GL account for journal entry creation; essential for financial statements.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement. Business justification: settlement_account currently has no in-domain relationship; adding settlement_id creates a many-to-one link to settlement, eliminating the silo and reflecting that each settlement account can be used ']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name assigned to the settlement account for easy identification by business users.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account is a Nostro, Vostro, or internal settlement account.']
  available_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the balance that is free for settlement funding.']
  bank_city varchar [note: 'City in which the bank branch holding the settlement account is located.']
  bank_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the bank is located.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the financial institution that holds the settlement account.']
  bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT code identifying the bank that holds the settlement account.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date the settlement account was closed or de‑commissioned; null if still active.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance status of the settlement account.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement account record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of credit that can be extended to the settlement account.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current monetary balance of the settlement account.']
  daily_funding_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total amount that can be funded from this account per day.']
  daily_funding_reset_date date [note: 'Date on which the daily funding usage counter resets to zero.']
  daily_funding_used decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount funded from the account on the current day.']
  iban varchar [note: 'Standardized international bank account number used for cross‑border settlements.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent settlement reconciliation for this account.']
  nickname varchar [note: 'User‑defined short name for the settlement account, used in UI displays.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date the settlement account was opened.']
  owner_party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account belongs to an acquiring bank, issuing bank, or payment scheme.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating calculated by the fraud and risk platform.']
  rtgs_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account can be used for Real‑Time Gross Settlement transfers.']
  rtp_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account can be used for Real‑Time Payments transfers.']
  settlement_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement account.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the account participates in settlement runs.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used for moving funds to or from this settlement account.']
  status_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the current status became effective.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why the account is in its current status.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement account record.']

  Note: 'Master record of a settlement bank account used to fund or receive settlement obligations. Stores account holder (acquirer, issuer, or scheme), bank name, BIC, IBAN, account currency, account type (nostro, vostro, settlement), account status, credit limit, and current balance. SSOT for settlement account registry distinct from the general ledger account in the ledger domain.'
}

Table settlement.settlement_reserve_account {
  settlement_reserve_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the reserve account record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Reserve accounts hold funds in a specific currency; linking ensures correct valuation.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank or entity linked to the reserve.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant to which the reserve is attached.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement cycle governing this reserve account.']
  settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank or entity linked to the reserve.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the anti‑money‑laundering screening for the reserve account.']
  available_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the reserve that has been released and is available for use.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the reserve account with internal policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reserve account record was first created in the system.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total amount held in the reserve account.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the reserve agreement becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the reserve agreement expires or is scheduled to terminate (nullable for open‑ended).']
  held_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Principal amount currently held in the reserve account.']
  last_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent reserve fund release.']
  next_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the upcoming reserve fund release.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or operational comments.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the reserve account is subject to mandatory regulatory reporting.']
  release_conditions varchar [note: 'Business rules or triggers that allow reserve release (e.g., chargeback threshold met).']
  release_interval varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which reserve funds may be released.']
  reserve_account_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the reserve account for reporting and reconciliation.']
  reserve_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary cap for a capped reserve type.']
  reserve_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the reserve account (e.g., "Merchant XYZ Rolling Reserve").']
  reserve_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction volume that must be retained as reserve.']
  reserve_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reserve mechanism: rolling, capped, or fixed.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score indicating likelihood of reserve utilization.']
  sanction_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening for the reserve account.']
  settlement_reserve_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reserve account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reserve account record.']

  Note: 'Rolling reserve or security deposit account held against a merchant or acquirer to cover potential chargebacks, fraud losses, or settlement shortfalls. Captures reserve type (rolling, capped, fixed), reserve percentage, held amount, currency, release schedule, release conditions, and current balance. SSOT for reserve obligations and release tracking.'
}

Table settlement.reserve_movement {
  reserve_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each reserve account movement record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Movement amounts must be expressed in a standardized currency for audit trails.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that owns the reserve account.']
  merchant_reserve_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reserve account impacted by the movement.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle to which this reserve movement belongs.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch file that contains this movement, if applicable.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the reserve movement in the transaction currency.']
  authorization_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the original authorization that led to this reserve movement.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the movement passed all applicable compliance checks (e.g., AML, sanctions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reserve movement record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the reserve movement becomes effective for balance calculations.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied when converting the movement amount to a different currency.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the reserve movement (e.g., hold, release, forfeit, adjustment).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional information or comments about the movement.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original movement amount before currency conversion.']
  original_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code of the original amount prior to conversion.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the movement was posted to the reserve account.']
  reserve_movement_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the reserve movement purpose.']
  reserve_movement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the reserve movement.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score associated with the movement, used for fraud and compliance monitoring.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the reserve movement (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  triggering_event varchar [note: 'Business event that caused the reserve movement.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that last updated the reserve movement record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reserve movement record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the reserve movement record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every debit or credit to a reserve account — including reserve holds, releases, forfeitures, and adjustments. Captures movement type, amount, currency, effective date, triggering event (chargeback, fraud loss, scheduled release), and authorization reference. Provides full audit trail for reserve account balances.'
}

Table settlement.funding_schedule {
  funding_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the funding schedule record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Funding schedules are defined per currency to apply correct limits and fees.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Funding schedules debit/credit specific GL accounts; linking ensures correct posting of funding transactions.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant (or sub‑acquirer) that receives the funding.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle to which this funding schedule is linked.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch that generated the funding instruction.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule complies with all applicable regulatory checks (true = compliant).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was first created in the system.']
  deduction_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordered list of deductions applied to the gross amount (e.g., MDR, interchange, chargebacks, reserves).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the schedule expires or is retired (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the schedule becomes effective.']
  funding_bank_account varchar [note: 'Bank account number (masked) where funds are deposited.']
  funding_bank_account_name varchar [note: 'Name of the account holder for the funding destination.']
  funding_bank_bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC of the destination bank.']
  funding_bank_iban varchar [note: 'IBAN used for cross‑border funding where applicable.']
  funding_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transfer funds to the payee (ACH, wire, RTP, SEPA).']
  funding_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Optional narrative describing special rules or notes for the schedule.']
  funding_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the schedule.']
  hold_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days to hold settled proceeds before they become eligible for funding.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the funding involves cross‑border transfers that may incur additional fees or controls.']
  last_run_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent funding run.']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule was last executed.']
  minimum_funding_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum net proceeds required before a funding run is triggered.']
  next_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the next funding execution.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when multiple schedules compete for the same funds (lower number = higher priority).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code indicating the geographic region for regulatory or reporting purposes.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk rating (0‑100) for the schedule based on AML, fraud, and sanction checks.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible code used to reference the funding schedule in external systems and reports.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose or configuration of the schedule.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the schedule frequency or timing rule (e.g., daily, weekly, T+1).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the schedule record.']

  Note: 'Master schedule defining when and how merchants, sub-acquirers, or PayFacs are funded from settled proceeds. Stores funding frequency (daily, weekly, T+1, T+2, T+n), funding method (ACH, wire, RTP, SEPA), funding bank account reference, minimum funding threshold, hold period, deduction sequence (MDR, interchange, scheme fees, chargebacks, reserves), and schedule status. SSOT for merchant funding timing, payout configuration, and settlement-to-payout waterfall rules.'
}

Table settlement.merchant_payout {
  merchant_payout_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the merchant payout record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Payouts are issued in a specific currency; reference ensures correct conversion and reporting.']
  funding_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the funding schedule applied to this payout.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'FK to merchant.merchant.merchant_id — MUST-HAVE: Enables tracing merchant payouts to the merchant entity — essential for merchant settlement reporting and funding reconciliation.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle associated with this payout.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch containing this payout.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total chargeback adjustments deducted.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payout passed all compliance checks.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from external payment network or partner.']
  funding_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number to which funds are transferred.']
  funding_account_type varchar [note: 'Type of the funding bank account.']
  interchange_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Interchange fee deducted from the gross payout.']
  mdr_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'MDR fee deducted from the gross payout.']
  merchant_mid varchar [note: 'Merchant Identification Number of the receiving merchant.']
  msf_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'MSF fee deducted from the gross payout.']
  net_payout_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount transferred to the merchant after all deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments regarding the payout.']
  party_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant party receiving the payout.']
  payout_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount before any deductions.']
  payout_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transfer the payout funds.']
  payout_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference code assigned to the payout transaction by the system.']
  payout_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the payout.']
  payout_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payout was initiated.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payout record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payout record was last updated.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total refunds deducted from the payout.']
  reserve_hold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount held in reserve and not released.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned to the payout based on fraud detection models.']
  scheme_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Card scheme fee deducted from the gross payout.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the payout amount is to be settled.']

  Note: 'Individual payout disbursement record representing the net funds transferred to a merchant or sub-merchant after applying the funding schedule waterfall — deducting MDR, MSF, interchange fees, scheme fees, chargebacks, refunds, and reserve holds from settled transaction proceeds. Captures payout reference, merchant MID, payout amount, currency, line-item deduction breakdown, funding account, payout status (pending, released, on-hold, paid, failed), value date, and remittance advice reference. SSOT for merchant settlement payouts and funding audit trail.'
}

Table settlement.interchange_settlement {
  interchange_settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the interchange settlement record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Interchange amounts are settled per currency; linking supports aggregation and compliance.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank involved in the interchange fee settlement.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Interchange settlements are accounted to GL accounts; direct FK supports automated posting and reporting.']
  issuing_bank_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issuing bank that receives the interchange fee.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Interchange settlement calculations depend on the payment product characteristics (e.g., card vs digital wallet) for interchange qualification and fee rates.']
  reversal_of_settlement_interchange_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original settlement that this record reverses.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle to which this interchange settlement belongs.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch that groups this settlement record.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustments (e.g., discounts, rebates) applied to the gross interchange fee.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of anti‑money‑laundering screening for the settlement.']
  bin_range varchar [note: 'Bank Identification Number range (6‑8 digits) applicable to the interchange transaction.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement meets regulatory compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the settlement involves cross‑border transactions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the settlement becomes effective.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied when settlement currency differs from transaction currency.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the settlement record expires or is superseded, if applicable.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the interchange file received from the card scheme.']
  interchange_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross interchange fee amount before any adjustments.']
  interchange_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Rate applied to calculate the interchange fee (percentage expressed as decimal).']
  interchange_settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interchange settlement.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'True if this record represents a reversal of a prior settlement.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code representing the merchant category for the settled transactions.']
  net_interchange_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net interchange fee after adjustments.']
  net_position_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net position amount for the settlement after all debits and credits.']
  net_position_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the net position amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments added by operations staff.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the settlement record.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement has been included in regulatory reports.']
  reserve_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number used to hold reserve funds for the settlement.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned to the settlement based on fraud and compliance models.']
  sanction_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening for the settlement.']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Code of the card scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) for which the interchange is settled.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transfer funds for the settlement.']
  settlement_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the card scheme for this interchange settlement.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the interchange settlement was posted.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the settlement record.']
  total_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all underlying transactions in the settlement.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual transactions covered by this interchange settlement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement record.']

  Note: 'Settlement record for interchange reimbursement fees (IRF) exchanged between acquiring and issuing banks via card schemes. Captures interchange file reference, scheme (Visa, Mastercard), BIN range, MCC, interchange rate applied, interchange amount, currency, settlement cycle reference, and scheme confirmation. Sourced from the Transaction Processing Platform and cross-references the interchange domain.'
}

Table settlement.scheme_fee_settlement {
  scheme_fee_settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each scheme fee settlement record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Scheme fees are calculated in a defined currency; reference enables consistent reporting.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the acquiring bank receiving the settlement.']
  funding_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the funding schedule used for the settlement.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Scheme fee settlements need GL account mapping for fee revenue recognition and reporting.']
  issuing_bank_id varchar [note: 'FK to settlement.issuing_bank']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant whose acquiring bank is settling the fee.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Scheme fee settlements need the originating payment product to attribute fees accurately and meet scheme‑level regulatory reporting.']
  primary_scheme_settlement_participant_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the acquiring bank receiving the settlement.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement cycle governing this fee.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch that includes this fee.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the billing period for which the fee applies.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'First day of the billing period for which the fee applies.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification status of the settlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was first created in the system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the fee must be paid to avoid penalties.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate applied when converting the fee to the settlement currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount billed by the scheme before any adjustments or taxes.']
  fee_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the scheme fee (assessment, network access, cross‑border, etc.).']
  fee_currency_conversion_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the fee amount was converted from the original currency.']
  fee_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the fee purpose.']
  fee_invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice reference number issued by the card scheme for the fee.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the fee is a fixed amount or a percentage of transaction volume.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True when the fee relates to cross‑border transaction processing.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the settlement record.']
  net_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee amount after tax and any discounts, expressed in settlement currency.']
  net_position_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net position amount after applying this fee.']
  net_position_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the net position amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the settlement.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the original fee amount.']
  original_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original fee amount before any adjustments or currency conversion.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the fee has been paid to the scheme.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this settlement must be reported to regulators.']
  reserve_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number of the reserve account used for net settlement.']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the card scheme charging the fee (e.g., VISA, MASTERCARD).']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee settlement was executed.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to transfer funds for the fee settlement.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the settlement record.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the settlement transaction was executed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the record.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the fee, if applicable.']
  transaction_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., ARN) linking the fee to the underlying transaction batch.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement record.']

  Note: 'Settlement record for card scheme fees (assessment fees, network access fees, cross-border fees) billed by Visa, Mastercard, or other schemes to the acquirer. Captures scheme fee invoice reference, fee category, fee amount, currency, billing period, scheme, and payment status. Distinct from interchange — these are scheme-to-acquirer fees, not issuer-to-acquirer fees.'
}

Table settlement.settlement_exception {
  settlement_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the settlement exception record.']
  affected_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific settlement instruction linked to the exception.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Exceptions must record the currency of the affected amount for audit and remediation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal user identifier of the analyst or operator who resolved the exception.']
  net_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the net position record impacted by the exception.']
  resolved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal user identifier of the analyst or operator who resolved the exception.']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch where the exception originated.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle during which the exception occurred.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with the exception (e.g., shortfall amount).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the exception triggered compliance checks (e.g., AML, sanctions).']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of the action taken to remediate the exception.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception record was created in the data lake.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Technical error code returned by the processing engine.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable error description associated with the exception.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the exception for tracking and reference.']
  exception_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was first detected in the settlement process.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category of the settlement exception indicating the nature of the break.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a human manually intervened to resolve the exception.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the resolver for additional context.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original transaction amount before the exception was identified.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the original transaction amount.']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'True if the exception has regulatory reporting or compliance implications.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the resolution attempt.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was marked as resolved.']
  retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of automated retry attempts performed before escalation.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the underlying cause of the exception. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_mismatch|validation_error|network_failure|partner_error|regulatory_hold — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exception.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Business-assigned severity level reflecting impact on settlement operations.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception breached its Service Level Agreement target.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed hours to resolve the exception as defined by SLA.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the exception record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the exception record.']

  Note: 'Operational record of any exception, break, or failure arising during the settlement lifecycle — including unmatched clearing records, funding shortfalls, rejected instructions, and reconciliation variances. Captures exception type, severity, affected batch or instruction reference, exception amount, assigned resolver, resolution status, and SLA breach flag. SSOT for settlement break management.'
}

Table settlement.file {
  file_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for file']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: File metadata includes total amount currency; reference ensures uniform currency handling.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle to which this file belongs.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch processing run that produced the file.']
  acknowledgment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference returned by the receiving party confirming receipt or rejection of the file.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Hash (e.g., SHA‑256) used to verify file integrity during transfer.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of compliance checks (AML, sanctions, PCI) performed on the file.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement file record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  encryption_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or protocol used for encryption (e.g., AES‑256).']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying a processing or transmission error, if any.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the error associated with error_code.']
  external_file_reference varchar [note: 'Business-visible reference number assigned by the originating network or partner (e.g., Visa BASE II file reference).']
  file_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the file is generated by the firm (outbound) or received from a partner/network (inbound).']
  file_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text providing additional context or notes about the file.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'The name of the settlement file as stored in the lakehouse, including extension.']
  file_type varchar [note: 'Standardized type of settlement file based on the messaging format or network.']
  format varchar [note: 'Technical format used to encode the file contents.']
  generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the file was created or assembled by the source system.']
  is_encrypted boolean [note: 'True if the file content is encrypted at rest or in transit.']
  originating_system varchar [note: 'Source system that produced or received the settlement file (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Internal processing state of the file within the settlement pipeline.']
  record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transaction records contained in the file.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the file is subject to regulatory reporting (e.g., SAR, AML).']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the file may be archived or purged per data retention policy.']
  size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the file in bytes.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of all transactions in the file.']
  transmission_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the file\'s transmission to the downstream network or partner.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement file record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the file format or schema, incremented on changes.']

  Note: 'Master record of each settlement file generated or received — including Visa BASE II, Mastercard IPM, ACH NACHA files, and SWIFT MT940/MT950 statements. Captures file name, file type, originating system, file format standard, record count, total amount, generation timestamp, transmission status, and acknowledgment reference. Sourced from the Transaction Processing Platform batch processing module.'
}

Table settlement.participant {
  participant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for participant']
  appetite_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_appetite_framework. Business justification: Each settlement participant is governed by a risk appetite framework that defines exposure limits and tolerances.']
  correspondent_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.correspondent_bank. Business justification: REQUIRED: Participants (banks) maintain correspondent banking relationships for cross‑border routing; needed for compliance and settlement routing decisions.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Participants settle in a designated currency; linking supports settlement routing and reporting.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Required for partner-level settlement reporting and revenue sharing; each settlement participant represents a partner entity.']
  exposure_limit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.exposure_limit. Business justification: Exposure limits per participant are tracked to enforce credit and concentration caps during settlement.']
  scheme_membership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by card scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  underwriting_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.underwriting_policy. Business justification: Underwriting policies are applied to participants (e.g., merchants) to set transaction limits and reserve rates.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of participant.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of AML screening for participant.']
  bic_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC code identifying the participant\'s bank.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of participant\'s address.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if participant is currently compliant with regulatory requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where participant is based.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when participant record was created.']
  cross_border_settlement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates ability to settle cross-border transactions.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level of participant data.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when participant ceased participation (null if active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when participant became effective in settlement network.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of settlement fee.']
  is_reserve_account_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if participant holds a reserve account for settlement.']
  last_aml_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of most recent AML review.']
  last_sanction_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of most recent sanctions review.']
  netting_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if participant allows netting of settlement positions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding participant.']
  participant_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the participant (e.g., acquiring bank, issuing bank, PSP).']
  participant_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the participant.']
  participant_type varchar [note: 'Category of participant in the settlement network.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of participant\'s address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person for the participant.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for primary contact.']
  reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., partner system).']
  region_state varchar [note: 'State or region of participant\'s address.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if participant is subject to specific regulatory reporting.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk score for participant (e.g., AML, fraud).']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Domestic routing number (e.g., ABA) for settlement.']
  sanction_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening.']
  settlement_account_iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number for settlement.']
  settlement_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number used for settlement transfers.']
  settlement_cycle_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred frequency of settlement cycles.']
  settlement_fee_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Fee rate applied to settlement transactions (percentage).']
  settlement_rail_capability varchar [note: 'Rail(s) the participant can use for settlement.']
  settlement_window varchar [note: 'Preferred time window for settlement processing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where participant data originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of last update to participant record.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all entities participating in the settlement network for this platform — acquiring banks, issuing banks, card schemes, PSPs, and PayFacs. Captures participant type, BIC/routing number, settlement account references, participation status, scheme membership IDs, and settlement rail capabilities. Distinct from the partner domain master — this is the settlement-specific participation record.'
}

Table settlement.applied_rate {
  applied_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for applied_rate']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Applied rates are tied to a currency for correct FX application.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement record that consumed this rate.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original transaction for which the rate was applied.']
  applied_rate_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form text providing context or remarks about the rate.']
  applied_rate_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the rate record within the system.']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment the rate was locked and used in a settlement transaction.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the rate against internal and external policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the applied rate record was inserted into the lakehouse.']
  currency_pair varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 formatted currency pair (e.g., USD/EUR) for which the rate is applicable.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rate started to be valid for settlement calculations.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time after which the rate must no longer be used for settlements.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True when the underlying transaction involves different currency jurisdictions.']
  rate_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places stored for the rate value.']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the rate, e.g., European Central Bank, Visa, Mastercard, internal pricing engine, or other.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rate such as spot, mid‑market, scheme‑provided, or custom.']
  rate_value decimal(18,9) [note: 'The actual exchange rate applied, expressed with up to 9 decimal places.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the rate must be reported to regulatory bodies (e.g., FCA, ECB).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Technical name of the upstream system providing the rate (e.g., fx_feed_service).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the rate record.']

  Note: 'FX rate snapshot applied at settlement time for cross-border and multi-currency transactions. Captures rate type (spot, mid-market, scheme rate), currency pair, rate value, rate source (ECB, Visa, Mastercard, internal), effective timestamp, and expiry timestamp. Distinct from the fx domain rate feed — this is the rate-as-applied record locked at settlement for audit and reconciliation purposes.'
}

Table settlement.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for adjustment']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Adjustments adjust amounts in a specific currency; reference ensures accurate ledger entries.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Adjustments arising from dispute outcomes need a FK to the dispute case to satisfy audit and compliance requirements.']
  original_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement record that is being adjusted.']
  related_adjustment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a preceding adjustment that this record reverses or amends.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle to which this adjustment belongs.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch file that contains the original settlement.']
  adjusted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount applied by the adjustment (positive for credit, negative for debit).']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the adjustment.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment adds to (credit) or subtracts from (debit) the settled amount.']
  approval_reference varchar [note: 'Internal reference to the approval workflow or ticket that authorized the adjustment.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the adjustment was approved.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment has passed all regulatory compliance checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adjustment record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the adjustment becomes effective for accounting and reporting.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Conversion rate applied if the adjustment involves a currency exchange.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary fee component associated with the adjustment, if any.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the fee amount.']
  identifier varchar [note: 'Human‑readable unique code assigned to the adjustment (e.g., ADJ‑20230915‑001).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or justification for the adjustment.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The settled amount before the adjustment was applied.']
  original_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the original settled amount.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the adjustment was made.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this adjustment represents a reversal of a prior adjustment.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned to the adjustment based on fraud/risk models.']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal number of the adjustment within the same settlement batch.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the adjustment (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or service that last modified the adjustment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the adjustment record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or service that created the adjustment.']

  Note: 'Record of post-settlement adjustments applied to correct errors, process late presentments, apply fee credits, or handle scheme-mandated corrections. Captures adjustment type (debit, credit), adjustment reason code, original settlement reference, adjusted amount, currency, approval reference, and effective date. Provides full audit trail for settlement corrections.'
}

Table settlement.nostro_reconciliation {
  nostro_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the nostro reconciliation record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Nostro reconciliation tracks balances per currency; linking standardizes reporting.']
  net_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the net position record used for net settlement calculations.']
  nostro_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.nostro_account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Reconciliation of nostro balances needs a direct link to the specific nostro account to match statements and balances.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement cycle to which this reconciliation belongs.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch associated with the reconciliation.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any manual adjustments applied during reconciliation.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of anti‑money‑laundering screening for the statement entries.']
  closing_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance of the nostro account at the end of the statement period.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of compliance checks performed on the reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the reconciliation record is considered effective for reporting.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied if the statement currency differs from the reporting currency.']
  exchange_rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exchange rate was sourced.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Optional date after which the reconciliation record is archived or superseded.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the reconciliation involves cross‑border currency movements.']
  matched_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit amount that successfully matched between internal ledger and statement.']
  matched_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total debit amount that successfully matched between internal ledger and statement.']
  matched_entries_count varchar [note: 'Number of ledger entries that matched the bank statement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the reconciliation analyst.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance of the nostro account at the start of the statement period.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the reconciliation job.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier assigned to each reconciliation run.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the reconciliation process.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the reconciliation was executed.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reconciliation must be included in regulatory filings.']
  related_statement_file_name varchar [note: 'File name of the imported bank statement (e.g., MT940_20240430.txt).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk rating assigned to the reconciliation based on AML/CTF rules.']
  sanction_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening for the statement entries.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the statement data.']
  statement_checksum varchar [note: 'Hash of the statement file to ensure data integrity.']
  statement_date date [note: 'Date of the bank statement used for reconciliation.']
  statement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from the bank statement (e.g., MT940 reference).']
  total_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of fees charged on the statement (e.g., interchange, processing fees).']
  total_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate gross amount of all transactions represented in the statement.']
  unmatched_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit amount from the statement that could not be matched to internal entries.']
  unmatched_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total debit amount from the statement that could not be matched to internal entries.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the reconciliation record for audit trail.']

  Note: 'Reconciliation record matching the platform\'s nostro account ledger entries against correspondent bank statements (SWIFT MT940/MT950 or camt.053) for each settlement currency. Captures statement date, nostro account reference, opening balance, closing balance, matched entries count, unmatched debit amount, unmatched credit amount, and reconciliation status. Distinct from the general reconciliation_record — this is bank-statement-level nostro reconciliation.'
}

Table settlement.settlement {
  settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for settlement']
  batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch file that contains this settlement.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Core settlement records must reference a currency entity for audit, reporting, and FX.']
  cycle_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement cycle that groups this settlement.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary participant (e.g., acquiring bank, issuing bank, or scheme) involved in the settlement.']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_fee_schedule. Business justification: REQUIRED: Settlements apply FX‑related fee schedules; linking enables fee calculation and compliance reporting.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.journal_entry. Business justification: Audit trail linking a settlement record to its corresponding journal entry for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  net_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the net position record used for netting calculations.']
  original_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on settlement (original_settlement_id)']
  payment_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.payment_corridor. Business justification: REQUIRED: Settlement records must capture the payment corridor used for cross‑border payments for fee calculation and regulatory reporting.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Aggregated settlement records are produced per payment product for compliance, risk scoring, and product‑level profitability analysis.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: REQUIRED: Settlement may settle in a different currency; the applied FX rate must be stored for accounting and regulatory purposes.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.sla_profile. Business justification: Settlement performance reports reference the SLA profile governing gateway latency to ensure contractual compliance.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the settlement passed all regulatory compliance checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the start of the settlement cycle that includes this record.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied when converting foreign currency to the settlement currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees deducted from the gross amount.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before fees and adjustments.']
  instruction_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier linking to the original settlement instruction.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the settlement involves parties in different countries.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after fees and adjustments, transferred to the participant.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational comments or remarks.']
  processing_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement was processed in batch or real‑time mode.']
  rail_details varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form details about the rail, such as SWIFT message type or RTGS corridor.']
  rail_type varchar [note: 'Payment rail used for the settlement (e.g., RTGS, RTP, ACH, SWIFT).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the settlement must be reported to a regulator (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned by the fraud/risk engine.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement is booked in the ledger.']
  settlement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or notes for the settlement.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement is a debit or credit to the participant.']
  settlement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the settlement by the payment network or internal system.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the settlement.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement event occurred in the real world.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the settlement (e.g., netting, gross, adjusted).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that generated the settlement record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement record.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which funds become available to the participant.']
  window varchar [note: 'Time window (e.g., morning, afternoon) during which the settlement is processed.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for settlement. '
}

  bank_operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours of the bank\'s primary office (e.g., 09:00-17:00).']

  reserve_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number of the reserve account held for settlement buffering.']

  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Standard settlement timing (e.g., T+1) for transactions with the bank.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the issuing bank.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bank record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for issuing_bank. Referenced by issuing_bank_id.'
}
