  acronym varchar [note: 'Acronym or abbreviation of the acquirer.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the acquirer\'s registered address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the address (optional).']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the acquirer\'s registered address.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of compliance certifications (e.g., PCI‑DSS, ISO27001).']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the acquirer.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the acquirer.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the acquirer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the acquirer record was first created.']
  daily_settlement_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the daily settlement limit.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the acquirer became effective for settlement.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the acquirer ceases to be effective (null if ongoing).']
  is_preferred boolean [note: 'Indicates if this acquirer is the preferred partner for routing.']
  max_daily_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total settlement amount allowed per day for this acquirer.']
  name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the acquiring institution.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes regarding the acquirer.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the acquirer was onboarded into the system.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the acquirer\'s address.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the acquirer.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the acquirer.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for settlements with this acquirer.']
  settlement_cutoff_time varchar [note: 'Daily cutoff time (HH:MM) for settlement processing.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Standard settlement cycle frequency.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Primary settlement method supported by the acquirer.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Commonly used short name or brand for the acquirer.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the acquirer\'s address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the acquirer.']
  support_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the support contact.']
  support_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary support contact person for the acquirer.']
  support_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the support contact.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the relationship with the acquirer was terminated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the acquirer record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL of the acquirer.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for acquirer. Referenced by originating_acquirer_id.'
}

Table network.scheme {
  scheme_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each payment scheme record.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requires mapping each network scheme to its global card‑scheme definition to aggregate compliance metrics across Visa, Mastercard, etc.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory and industry standards the scheme must adhere to.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for scheme support inquiries.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for scheme support inquiries.']
  currency_supported varchar [note: 'Currencies in which the scheme can settle transactions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the scheme became effective for routing and processing.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'List of ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the scheme is accepted.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Legal entity that owns or governs the scheme (e.g., Visa Inc., Mastercard).']
  interchange_fee_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric fee rate applied to transactions (e.g., 0.0150 for 1.5%).']
  interchange_fee_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the interchange fee is calculated as a percentage or a fixed amount.']
  network_certification_date date [note: 'Date when the scheme achieved its most recent network certification.']
  network_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification status of the scheme within the network.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scheme record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scheme record.']
  risk_profile varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the scheme based on fraud and chargeback history.']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Canonical short code used to identify the scheme in messages and reports (e.g., VISA, MC).']
  scheme_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the scheme, its purpose and key characteristics.']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  scheme_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the scheme within the platform.']
  scheme_type varchar [note: 'Category of the scheme based on the primary instrument or service it supports.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Default currency used for settlement of scheme transactions.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to settle transactions for the scheme.']
  supported_transaction_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of transaction types the scheme supports (e.g., purchase, refund, cash‑advance).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the scheme was retired or ceased to be supported (nullable).']
  three_ds_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scheme supports 3‑Domain Secure authentication.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scheme supports tokenization of PANs.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the scheme definition (e.g., v2.1).']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL providing scheme specifications and resources.']

  Note: 'Master record for each payment card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, UnionPay, etc.). Defines the scheme brand identity, governing body, supported transaction types, geographic reach, and scheme-level operational parameters. SSOT for payment scheme identity across the platform.'
}

Table network.endpoint {
  endpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the network endpoint record.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the alternate endpoint used for failover.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Endpoints are deployed per country; FK to country supports regional SLA monitoring and regulatory jurisdiction tracking.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Required for routing and settlement reports to map each network endpoint to the owning partner (acquirer/issuer) responsible for traffic and fee allocation.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Network Operations Management: assigns a network engineer to each endpoint for incident response and performance reporting.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_region. Business justification: Network operations assign each endpoint to a gateway region to monitor SLA, latency, and regulatory data‑residency per region.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Endpoint compliance audit requires mapping each network endpoint to the regulatory obligation it satisfies (PCI‑DSS, ISO‑20022).']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Endpoints belong to a payment scheme; adding scheme_id FK normalizes the relationship and removes the redundant network_scheme string column.']
  timezone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.timezone. Business justification: Endpoint operations depend on local time zones for maintenance windows; linking to timezone master enables automated scheduling and compliance with local regulations.']
  tls_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the TLS certificate used for securing the connection.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit events related to the endpoint.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate connections to the endpoint.']
  average_latency_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean round‑trip latency observed for the endpoint, measured in milliseconds.']
  certificate_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the associated TLS certificate expires.']
  compliance_iso_20022 boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with ISO 20022 messaging standards.']
  compliance_pci_dss boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the endpoint meets PCI DSS requirements.']
  compliance_regulatory varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulatory codes (e.g., PSD2, SOX) applicable to the endpoint.']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether connections are persistent or stateless.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the endpoint record was first created.']
  data_center_location varchar [note: 'Physical data‑center location (city, facility) hosting the endpoint.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the endpoint became operationally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the endpoint is scheduled to be retired; null if indefinite.']
  encryption_protocol varchar [note: 'Encryption protocol negotiated for the endpoint.']
  endpoint_code varchar [note: 'Business‑level unique code used in operational processes.']
  endpoint_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the endpoint purpose and characteristics.']
  endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the endpoint.']
  endpoint_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the endpoint.']
  health_check_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Interval between successive health checks, in seconds.']
  health_check_timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum time to wait for a health‑check response before marking it failed.']
  health_status varchar [note: 'Current health assessment of the endpoint.']
  host_url varchar [note: 'Fully qualified URL or hostname used to reach the endpoint.']
  ip_address_range varchar [note: 'CIDR notation representing the IP range allocated to the endpoint.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this endpoint is the primary for its scheme.']
  is_tls_mutual boolean [note: 'True if mutual TLS authentication is required.']
  last_health_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful health‑check.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Planned maintenance period (e.g., "Sat 02:00‑04:00 UTC").']
  max_connections varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous connections the endpoint can sustain.']
  max_message_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Maximum size of a single message the endpoint will accept, in bytes.']
  monitoring_endpoint varchar [note: 'URL used by monitoring tools to probe endpoint health.']
  network_interface varchar [note: 'Type of network interface (e.g., Ethernet, Fiber).']
  port varchar [note: 'TCP/UDP port number on which the endpoint listens.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the messaging protocol used (e.g., ISO8583, ISO20022).']
  routing_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when selecting among multiple eligible endpoints.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes the endpoint can process.']
  supported_message_format varchar [note: 'Message format(s) the endpoint can process.']
  throughput_tps decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average number of transactions the endpoint processes per second.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the endpoint record.']

  Note: 'Defines physical and logical connectivity endpoints for each payment network — host URLs, IP ranges, port configurations, protocol versions (ISO 8583, ISO 20022), TLS certificate references, and connection health thresholds. Represents the network infrastructure layer for scheme connectivity.'
}

Table network.routing_table {
  routing_table_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the routing rule record.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.endpoint. Business justification: Routing table should link to the endpoint entity rather than storing a URL string; this creates a true relational link and removes redundant data.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Routing tables are defined per scheme; adding scheme_id FK creates parent relationship and removes need for any scheme string column (none present).']
  bin_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending BIN of the inclusive range for which this rule applies.']
  bin_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting BIN (Bank Identification Number) of the inclusive range for which this rule applies.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or justification for the most recent modification.']
  compliance_check_required boolean [note: 'Specifies if regulatory compliance checks must be performed before routing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing rule record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for which the rule is valid.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the routing rule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the routing rule expires; null if indefinite.']
  fallback_sequence varchar [note: 'Order number for fallback routing attempts.']
  fee_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Fee rate (as a decimal fraction) applied when routing through the target network.']
  is_fallback_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fallback routing is permitted if primary routing fails.']
  last_tested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent automated test of the routing rule.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit for transactions that can be routed by this rule.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Merchant category code that the rule matches.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower monetary threshold for transactions applicable to this rule.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority determining rule precedence (lower = higher priority).']
  processing_rail varchar [note: 'Processing mode used for the routed transaction.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed fraud risk score for a transaction to be routed via this rule.']
  routing_table_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the routing rule purpose and logic.']
  routing_table_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing rule.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing logic applied.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the routing rule.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the routing rule.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed surcharge amount added to the transaction fee for this routing path.']
  target_network varchar [note: 'Payment network to which the transaction should be routed.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent routing rule test.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction the routing rule applies to.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the routing rule.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Defines transaction routing rules mapping BIN ranges, MCC codes, transaction types, and currency combinations to target network endpoints and processing rails. Supports multi-network routing logic, least-cost routing, and failover sequencing. Core operational artifact for the Payment Gateway and Authorization Engine.'
}

Table network.network_routing_rule {
  network_routing_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the routing rule.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the acquirer endpoint']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tracks which employee created a routing rule for PCI‑DSS change‑log and internal audit compliance.']
  routing_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.routing_table. Business justification: Routing rules belong to a routing table; adding routing_table_id FK establishes hierarchy.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the card scheme']
  target_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.endpoint. Business justification: Routing rule must reference the actual endpoint entity; replace the free‑text column with a proper foreign key to enforce data integrity and enable joins.']
  amount_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction amount (in rule currency) for which the rule is applicable.']
  amount_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction amount (in rule currency) for which the rule is applicable.']
  bin_end varchar [note: 'Last BIN in the inclusive range that the rule applies to.']
  bin_start varchar [note: 'First BIN (Bank Identification Number) in the inclusive range that the rule applies to.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Category of payment instrument the rule targets.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the rule has been reviewed and approved against PCI DSS, PSD2, and other applicable regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing rule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for amount thresholds.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the rule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the rule is no longer applied; null indicates indefinite validity.']
  is_default_rule boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this rule is the default fallback']
  is_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule can supersede lower‑priority rules for the same criteria.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction that matched this rule.']
  match_criteria varchar [note: 'Criteria expression used to match transactions to this rule']
  network_routing_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer indicating rule precedence; lower numbers are evaluated first.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country or region code to which the rule applies.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business‑unique code used to reference the rule in configuration and logs.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the rule logic and intent.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose of the routing rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule logic (e.g., BIN range, amount threshold, geographic, or composite).']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change‑management and audit purposes.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction the rule evaluates.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service that last modified the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the routing rule.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Individual routing rule entry within a routing table. Specifies the condition set (BIN range, card type, transaction type, amount threshold, currency, region) and the target network endpoint or rail. Supports priority ordering, effective date ranges, and override flags for dynamic routing decisions.'
}

Table network.scheme_parameter {
  scheme_parameter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each scheme parameter record.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Parameters may vary by country; linking to country master supports jurisdiction‑specific compliance and reporting.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Fee and parameter rules are currency‑specific; linking to the master currency table enables accurate cross‑border fee calculations and regulatory reporting.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'FK to network.scheme']
  applies_to_card_type varchar [note: 'Card product types to which the parameter is applicable.']
  applies_to_mcc varchar [note: 'Four‑digit MCC to which the parameter is limited; null if not MCC‑specific.']
  applies_to_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Transaction operation types governed by the parameter.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the parameter value was changed.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates regulatory framework(s) the parameter satisfies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the parameter record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the parameter becomes effective for transaction processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the parameter expires; null if the parameter is open‑ended.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'True if the parameter applies to all merchants and terminals; false if scoped to specific groups.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the parameter must be enforced for all transactions.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the parameter was last reviewed for relevance or compliance.']
  parameter_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the parameter (e.g., floor_limit, velocity_threshold, cvv_requirement, avs_requirement, 3ds_mandate, sca_exemption, chargeback_time_limit, emv_fallback). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: floor_limit|velocity_threshold|cvv_requirement|avs_requirement|3ds_mandate|sca_exemption|chargeback_time_limit|emv_fallback|other — promote to reference product]']
  parameter_code varchar [note: 'Unique code assigned by the card network to identify the parameter.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the configuration parameter.']
  parameter_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Raw value of the parameter as provided by the scheme; stored as string to accommodate numeric, boolean, or textual formats.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation to the specific rule, bulletin, or standard governing the parameter (e.g., Visa Core Rules Section 5.2).']
  scheme_parameter_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the parameter purpose and usage.']
  scheme_parameter_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the parameter record.']
  source_bulletin_date date [note: 'Date of the network bulletin that introduced or updated the parameter.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit associated with the parameter value (e.g., USD, count, minutes).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the parameter record.']
  value_data_type varchar [note: 'Data type of the parameter value indicating how it should be interpreted.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the parameter record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Stores scheme-specific configuration parameters published by card networks — authorization floor limits, velocity thresholds, CVV/AVS requirement flags, 3DS mandate settings, SCA exemption rules, chargeback time limits, and EMV fallback policies. Updated per scheme bulletin cycles.'
}

Table network.scheme_bulletin {
  scheme_bulletin_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the scheme bulletin record.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Bulletins are issued by a scheme; replace string scheme column with scheme_id FK.']
  applicable_regions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the bulletin applies.']
  bulletin_number varchar [note: 'Official bulletin number or code assigned by the card scheme.']
  bulletin_type varchar [note: 'Category of the bulletin (e.g., mandate, update, advisory).']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Date by which merchants/issuers must comply with the bulletin.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific technical or operational requirements that must be met to comply.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bulletin record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the bulletin\'s changes become effective.']
  impacted_parameter_category varchar [note: 'Primary functional area(s) affected by the bulletin.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Progress of internal implementation of the bulletin requirements.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance review of the bulletin.']
  mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bulletin is mandatory (true) or advisory (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or internal comments.']
  scheme_bulletin_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the bulletin content and purpose.']
  scheme_bulletin_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bulletin.']
  source_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the original bulletin document hosted by the scheme.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the scheme bulletin.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bulletin record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the bulletin; increments when the bulletin is revised.']

  Note: 'Tracks official scheme bulletins and mandate publications issued by card networks (Visa, Mastercard, Amex). Records bulletin ID, effective date, impacted parameter categories, compliance deadline, and implementation status. Drives the scheme parameter update lifecycle and compliance tracking.'
}

Table network.network_certification_record {
  network_certification_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the certification record.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Certification records are tied to a scheme; replace associated_scheme string with scheme_id FK.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Organization or scheme that issued the certification.']
  certification_code varchar [note: 'Internal code or identifier used to reference the certification within the platform.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the certification (e.g., EMV Chip Certification).']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of certification indicating the technology or standard being certified.']
  compliance_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or functional scope covered by the certification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the certification becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the certification expires or is no longer valid.']
  is_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification record represents a renewal of a prior certification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Any supplemental information or comments about the certification.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the certification must be renewed to remain valid.']
  result_details varchar [note: 'Free‑text details describing the test outcome, observations, or notes.']
  test_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification testing was performed.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the certification test.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the certification (e.g., v1.2, 2023‑Q1).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Tracks network certification and compliance testing records for the platform — EMV chip certification, contactless NFC certification, 3DS server certification, PCI DSS attestations, and scheme-specific technical certifications. Records certification body, test results, expiry dates, and renewal status.'
}

Table network.message_log {
  message_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each network message log record.']
  compliance_aml_screening_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_aml_screening_result. Business justification: Message‑level monitoring links logs to AML screening results for SAR filing and audit trails.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Message logs need to attribute each transaction message to the originating partner for dispute handling and audit trails.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the network endpoint (e.g., acquiring bank, scheme gateway) that originated or received the message.']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_case. Business justification: Fraud Investigation Report requires linking each network log entry to the fraud case it supports, enabling auditors to trace messages used in case analysis.']
  network_certification_network_certification_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certification record that validates the network endpoint\'s compliance.']
  network_certification_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the certification record that validates the network endpoint\'s compliance.']
  network_routing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the routing rule applied to route this message.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that originated the transaction.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Settlement‑related message logs must reference the exact FX rate used for audit and reconciliation.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch file or processing batch that contains the message.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business transaction that generated this network message.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction in the smallest currency unit.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Result of the authorization attempt as reported by the network.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the message meets applicable regulatory and scheme compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the log record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount.']
  decline_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the specific reason for a decline, if applicable.']
  destination_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the receiving system (e.g., network gateway).']
  destination_port varchar [note: 'Network port on the destination system that received the message.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Error code returned by the network when a failure occurs.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the network message was observed or captured by the system.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned by the fraud detection platform for this message.']
  is_test_message boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the message was generated in a test or sandbox environment.']
  iso_message_version varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the message follows ISO 8583 or ISO 20022 formatting.']
  message_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record is a request, response, advice, or reversal message.']
  message_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA‑256) of the raw message for integrity verification.']
  message_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the message within its batch or session.']
  message_type_indicator varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code that identifies the message class, function, and origin according to ISO 8583 or ISO 20022. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 0100|0110|0200|0210|0400|0410|0420|0430|0500|0510|0800|0810 — promote to reference product]']
  network_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment scheme or network that processed the message (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  network_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of the network interaction for this message.']
  processing_code varchar [note: 'Six‑digit code that defines the transaction type and processing rules (e.g., purchase, refund, cash advance).']
  processing_time_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds taken by the system to process the message internally.']
  raw_message varchar [note: 'Full raw message content as received from the network (e.g., ISO 8583 bitmap).']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if the message must be included in regulatory reporting (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Three‑digit code returned by the network indicating approval, decline, or error conditions.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the message has been retried after a failure.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Categorized risk level derived from the fraud score.']
  round_trip_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Measured latency in milliseconds between transmission and receipt of the corresponding response.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was settled in the acquiring bank.']
  source_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the originating system that sent the message.']
  source_port varchar [note: 'Network port on the source system used for the transmission.']
  transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the message was sent from the originating system to the network.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the log record.']

  Note: 'Operational log of ISO 8583 and ISO 20022 financial messages exchanged with payment networks — authorization requests/responses, reversal messages, advice messages, and network management messages. Captures message type indicator (MTI), processing code, response code, transmission timestamp, and round-trip latency. Critical for reconciliation and dispute evidence.'
}

Table network.connectivity_event {
  connectivity_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each connectivity event record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Network downtime events are costed to specific cost centers for internal charge‑back and service‑level reporting.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Captures which employee logged the connectivity event, supporting root‑cause analysis and audit.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the network endpoint (e.g., switch, router, gateway) involved in the event.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Connectivity events are associated with a scheme; replace scheme string with scheme_id FK.']
  affected_transaction_volume varchar [note: 'Number of payment transactions impacted by the connectivity event.']
  certificate_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the TLS/SSL certificate used in the connection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the connectivity event record was first created in the system.']
  destination_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the target system for the connection.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of time the event lasted, measured in seconds.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error code returned by the network component, if any.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing details about the event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the connectivity event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the connectivity event.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Root cause or reason for a failure‑type event, if applicable.']
  is_successful boolean [note: 'True if the event resulted in a successful connection or recovery; otherwise false.']
  network_node varchar [note: 'Logical name or identifier of the network node where the event originated.']
  port varchar [note: 'Network port number associated with the event.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Transport protocol used for the connection.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve or mitigate the event.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of automatic retry attempts performed after a failure.']
  sla_actual_seconds varchar [note: 'Actual time taken to resolve the event, measured in seconds.']
  sla_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target duration defined in the SLA for the event to be resolved.']
  source_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the source system that initiated the connection.']
  tls_version varchar [note: 'Version of TLS negotiated for the session, if applicable.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system or user that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the connectivity event record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system or user that created the record.']

  Note: 'Records network connectivity events — session establishment, session teardown, link failures, failover activations, and reconnection events for each scheme endpoint. Captures event type, duration, affected endpoint, impacted transaction volume, and resolution action. Supports SLA monitoring and incident root-cause analysis.'
}

Table network.failover_config {
  failover_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the failover configuration.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Identifies the engineer configuring failover settings, needed for change management and regulatory audit.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Failover configurations are defined per scheme; replace string fields with scheme_id FK.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the configuration meets applicable regulatory and security standards (e.g., PCI DSS).']
  config_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the failover configuration.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name for the failover configuration.']
  consecutive_failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive failures required to trigger failover.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the failover configuration record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the failover configuration becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the failover configuration expires or is retired (nullable for open‑ended).']
  error_rate_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable error rate (percentage) before failover is triggered.']
  failover_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context and rationale for the failover configuration.']
  failover_config_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the failover configuration.']
  failover_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether failover is triggered automatically or requires manual intervention.']
  failover_type varchar [note: 'Type of failover strategy applied to network endpoints.']
  last_failover_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent failover event.']
  latency_threshold_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum acceptable latency in milliseconds before failover is triggered.']
  next_scheduled_test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the next automated failover test.']
  notification_email varchar [note: 'Email address to receive failover alerts and notifications.']
  notification_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number to receive failover alerts via SMS or voice call.']
  primary_endpoint varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary network endpoint used under normal operation.']
  recovery_time_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target time in seconds to recover to the primary endpoint after a failover event.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the geographic region for which the configuration applies.']
  secondary_endpoint varchar [note: 'Identifier of the secondary network endpoint used when failover occurs.']
  test_mode_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if periodic failover testing mode is enabled.']
  timeout_threshold_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum request timeout in seconds before failover is triggered.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated the configuration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the failover configuration record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the configuration.']

  Note: 'Defines failover and redundancy configurations for each network endpoint — primary/secondary endpoint pairs, failover trigger thresholds (error rate, latency, timeout), automatic vs. manual failover mode, and recovery criteria. Ensures business continuity for payment processing during network outages.'
}

Table network.sla {
  sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the SLA record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: SLA penalty and performance cost allocations are charged to internal cost centers for charge‑back reporting.']
  escalation_contact_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Associates SLA escalation contact with an employee, enabling automated alert routing and compliance reporting.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: SLA contracts must reference the specific regulatory obligation they support for compliance reporting.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: SLAs are negotiated per scheme; replace scheme_code with scheme_id FK.']
  actual_authorization_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Measured average authorization response time during the reporting period.']
  actual_batch_settlement_window_hours varchar [note: 'Observed average time taken to settle batches.']
  actual_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Observed average latency during the reporting period.']
  actual_uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured network availability percentage for the reporting period.']
  batch_settlement_window_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum time allowed to complete batch settlement after transaction capture.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the SLA with applicable regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for penalty and any monetary SLA components.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the SLA becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the SLA expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  endpoint_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the network connection endpoint used for routing.']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the person or team to notify on SLA breach.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for SLA breach escalation.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent SLA performance review.']
  max_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed network latency for transaction messages, in milliseconds.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'How often SLA performance metrics are measured and reported.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or special conditions.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the penalty for a breach, expressed in the contract currency.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing the financial penalty applied for SLA breaches.']
  sla_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA metric type (authorization response time, uptime, latency, settlement window).']
  sla_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the service‑level agreement.']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA record.']
  target_authorization_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Promised maximum time for a real‑time authorization response, expressed in milliseconds.']
  target_uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contracted network availability expressed as a percentage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SLA record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the SLA contract (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']

  Note: 'Defines SLA commitments for each payment network connection — target authorization response time (TPS), uptime percentage, maximum latency thresholds, batch settlement window, and penalty clauses. Tracks contracted vs. actual performance metrics per scheme and endpoint.'
}

  last_review_date date [note: 'Most recent date the membership agreement was reviewed for compliance.']

  network_certification_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent certification was granted.']
  network_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification status of the platform with the scheme.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or comments.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date the membership was initially onboarded into the platform.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for membership renewal consideration.']

  renewal_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the renewal fee.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Whether renewal is automatic or requires manual approval.']
  risk_profile varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the membership based on scheme requirements and historical performance.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for terminating the membership agreement.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that initially created the record.']

  Note: 'Records the formal membership and participation agreements between the platform (as acquirer, issuer, or processor) and each card scheme. Stores membership type (principal, associate, affiliate), membership ID, effective date, annual fees, and compliance obligations. SSOT for scheme participation status.'
}

Table network.scheme_fee_schedule {
  scheme_fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each scheme fee schedule record.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Fee schedules differ by country; FK to country enables localized fee enforcement and regulatory compliance.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Fee schedules are defined per currency; referencing the currency master ensures correct fee application and auditability.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Fee schedule entries are posted to specific GL accounts for revenue recognition in daily accounting.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Fee schedules are defined per scheme; replace scheme_code with scheme_id FK.']
  applicable_to_domestic boolean [note: 'True if the fee applies to domestic (same‑country) transactions.']
  applicable_to_international boolean [note: 'True if the fee applies to international (cross‑border) transactions.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment card for which the fee schedule is applicable.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the fee schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee schedule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the fee schedule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the fee schedule expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended.']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount charged per transaction when the fee is flat‑rate.']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount that can be charged for this fee per transaction.']
  fee_cap_indicator boolean [note: 'True if a fee cap is enforced for this schedule.']
  fee_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to the transaction amount for percentage‑based fees.']
  fee_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing code that uniquely identifies the fee schedule within the organization.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Category of the fee charged by the scheme (e.g., network access, processing, cross‑border assessment, value‑added service).']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the fee applies to cross‑border transactions; otherwise false.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance or policy review of the fee schedule.']
  maximum_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction amount for which the fee applies.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code representing the merchant\'s business category.']
  minimum_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction amount for which the fee applies.']
  scheme_fee_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, or special conditions of the fee schedule.']
  scheme_fee_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fee schedule.']
  surcharge_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the fee is a surcharge passed to the merchant or cardholder.']
  transaction_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the transaction (e.g., purchase, refund, cash‑advance) to which the fee applies.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last updated the fee schedule record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the fee schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the fee schedule record for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the fee schedule record.']

  Note: 'Defines the fee structures charged by card schemes for network access, authorization, clearing, and value-added services. Stores fee type (network access fee, scheme processing fee, cross-border assessment), rate, effective date range, and applicable transaction categories. Distinct from interchange (owned by interchange domain).'
}

Table network.multi_network_routing {
  multi_network_routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the routing profile.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Multi‑network routing profiles are scoped to a scheme; add scheme_id FK.']
  compliance_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional regulatory compliance checks must be performed before routing.']
  durbin_compliance_mode varchar [note: 'Specifies how the profile complies with the Durbin Amendment for debit transactions.']
  fallback_network_code varchar [note: 'Network code to use when primary networks are unavailable or fail compliance checks.']
  least_cost_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the routing engine should prioritize the lowest interchange cost.']
  network_preference_order varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of network codes (e.g., VISA,MC,DISC) in order of preference for routing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for operational comments, exceptions, or audit remarks.']
  priority_score decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric score used by the routing engine to rank this profile against others.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Business‑visible code that uniquely identifies the routing profile across systems.']
  profile_created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the routing profile.']
  profile_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the routing profile was initially created in the system.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the routing configuration used by operations and reporting.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the routing profile.']
  profile_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the latest update.']
  profile_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the routing profile.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed fraud risk score for a transaction to be routed using this profile.']
  routing_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing profile based on transaction type or market segment.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the profile is a default rule, an override, or specific to a merchant or cardholder.']
  transaction_volume_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative transaction amount (in USD) required to trigger this routing profile.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the routing profile for change management.']

  Note: 'Manages multi-network routing configurations for transactions eligible to be processed across multiple networks (e.g., Visa vs. Interlink for debit, Mastercard vs. Maestro). Stores network preference order, merchant override rules, cardholder preference flags, Durbin Amendment compliance settings, and least-cost routing logic.'
}

Table network.network_protocol_config {
  network_protocol_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each network protocol configuration record.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Protocol configurations are scheme‑specific; replace scheme_code with scheme_id FK.']
  bitmap_format varchar [note: 'Representation format of the ISO 8583 bitmap (binary or hexadecimal).']
  checksum_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to compute message integrity checksums.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the configuration meets applicable standards (PCI DSS, EMVCo, etc.).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration record was first created.']
  delimiter varchar [note: 'Character or byte sequence used to separate messages when framing rule is \'delimiter\'.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the configuration becomes effective for routing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the configuration ceases to be effective (null for open‑ended).']
  encoding varchar [note: 'Character encoding used for the message payload.']
  field_mapping_definition varchar [note: 'JSON string that maps internal field names to network message fields.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this configuration is the default for its scheme.']
  iso_20022_message_set varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ISO 20022 message set (e.g., pain.001, pacs.008) employed by the configuration.']
  iso_8583_version varchar [note: 'Version of the ISO 8583 standard used (e.g., 1987, 1993, 2003).']
  last_tested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent functional test of the configuration.']
  max_message_length varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed size of a single protocol message in bytes.']
  message_framing_rule varchar [note: 'Rule that defines how messages are delimited or length‑prefixed on the wire.']
  min_message_length varchar [note: 'Minimum expected size of a protocol message in bytes.']
  network_protocol_config_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the configuration in downstream systems.']
  network_protocol_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the protocol configuration and its purpose.']
  network_protocol_config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the protocol configuration.']
  network_protocol_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the protocol configuration.']
  protocol_type varchar [note: 'Type of messaging protocol implemented by this configuration.']
  routing_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when multiple configurations match; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes the configuration can handle.']
  supported_message_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of message type identifiers (e.g., 0100, 0200) supported.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the last test execution.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the configuration record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change‑management and audit purposes.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Stores messaging protocol configurations for each network connection — ISO 8583 version (1987/1993/2003), ISO 20022 message set, field mapping definitions, bitmap configurations, encoding (EBCDIC/ASCII), and message framing rules. Enables protocol translation between internal systems and external network endpoints.'
}

Table network.cutover {
  cutover_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each network cutover event.']
  business_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Links cutover project ownership to a specific employee, required for governance, reporting, and post‑cutover review.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Cutover events are owned by a specific partner; linking enables responsibility tracking and post‑cutover validation reporting.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the network endpoint being upgraded or migrated.']
  regulatory_reporting_req_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory report generated for this cutover.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'FK to network.scheme']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real end date‑time when the cutover completed.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real start date‑time when the cutover began.']
  affected_region_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 region codes impacted.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Chronological log of significant actions on the cutover record.']
  business_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the business owner responsible for the cutover.']
  business_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the business owner.']
  business_owner_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the business owner.']
  certification_renewal_date date [note: 'Date on which the network certification is renewed, if applicable.']
  change_control_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the change control ticket.']
  change_control_ticket_number varchar [note: 'Ticket number from the change management system linked to the cutover.']
  change_window_description varchar [note: 'Description of the maintenance window allocated for the cutover.']
  communication_plan varchar [note: 'Plan for notifying stakeholders before, during, and after cutover.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the cutover.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cutover record was first created in the system.']
  cutover_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the cutover event.']
  cutover_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cutover.']
  cutover_type varchar [note: 'Category of the cutover activity.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed minutes from actual start to actual end.']
  go_no_go_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine whether the cutover may proceed.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of business impact caused by the cutover.']
  network_endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the affected network endpoint.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations.']
  planned_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled end date‑time for the cutover.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start date‑time for the cutover.']
  post_cutover_validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when final validation was performed.']
  protocol_version_after varchar [note: 'Version of the network protocol after the cutover.']
  protocol_version_before varchar [note: 'Version of the network protocol prior to the cutover.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cutover must be reported to a regulator.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the cutover.']
  rollback_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed steps to revert the cutover if needed.']
  stakeholder_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address of the stakeholder responsible for the cutover.']
  stakeholder_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number of the stakeholder responsible for the cutover.']
  success_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the cutover met all success criteria.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cutover record.']
  validation_results varchar [note: 'Outcome of validation checks performed after cutover.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the cutover record for concurrency control.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier that created the cutover record.']

  Note: 'Tracks planned and executed network cutover events — scheme-mandated technology migrations, endpoint upgrades, protocol version changes, and certification renewals. Records cutover window, affected endpoints, rollback plan, go/no-go criteria, and post-cutover validation results.'
}

Table network.emv_parameter {
  emv_parameter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the EMV parameter set.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: EMV parameter sets are country‑specific for card acceptance; linking to country master aligns with global EMV certification.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) to which this EMV parameter set applies.']
  additional_terminal_capabilities varchar [note: 'Extended capability bitmap for the terminal.']
  aid varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal identifier of the EMV application as defined by the card scheme.']
  aid_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the AID purpose.']
  application_interchange_profile varchar [note: 'Hex value defining interchange profile rules for the application.']
  application_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the EMV application on the card.']
  card_action_code_default varchar [note: 'Hex code defined by the card issuer for default handling.']
  card_action_code_denial varchar [note: 'Hex code defined by the card issuer for denial handling.']
  card_action_code_online varchar [note: 'Hex code defined by the card issuer for online processing.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the parameter set meets the latest scheme and regulatory requirements.']
  contactless_transaction_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for contactless transactions without additional verification.']
  contactless_transaction_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the contactless transaction limit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the EMV parameter record was first created in the system.']
  cvm_required_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Transaction amount above which Cardholder Verification Method (CVM) is required.']
  cvm_required_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the CVM required limit.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the EMV parameter set becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the EMV parameter set expires or is superseded.']
  emv_parameter_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the purpose and scope of the parameter set.']
  emv_parameter_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the EMV parameter set.']
  floor_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount that can be approved offline without online authorization.']
  floor_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the floor limit amount.']
  issuer_country_code varchar [note: 'Country code of the card issuer.']
  offline_data_authentication varchar [note: 'Method used for offline data authentication (static, dynamic, or online).']
  offline_pin_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an offline PIN entry is mandatory for the transaction.']
  online_pin_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an online PIN entry is mandatory for the transaction.']
  parameter_set_code varchar [note: 'Unique code used to reference the EMV parameter set within the organization.']
  parameter_set_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the EMV parameter configuration.']
  terminal_action_code_default varchar [note: 'Hex code indicating the terminal\'s default response for unspecified conditions.']
  terminal_action_code_denial varchar [note: 'Hex code indicating the terminal\'s response when a transaction is denied.']
  terminal_action_code_online varchar [note: 'Hex code indicating the terminal\'s response when a transaction is processed online.']
  terminal_capabilities varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal bitmap describing the terminal\'s functional capabilities.']
  terminal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the terminal based on connectivity and capabilities.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency code used for the transaction amount.']
  transaction_currency_exponent varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places for the transaction currency (e.g., 2 for cents).']
  transaction_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for a single transaction under this parameter set.']
  transaction_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the transaction limit.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of transaction this parameter set applies to.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the EMV parameter record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the EMV parameter set, incremented on each change.']

  Note: 'Stores EMV chip and contactless parameter sets distributed by card schemes — application identifiers (AIDs), terminal action codes, card action codes, floor limits, TAC-denial/online/default values, and contactless transaction limits. Updated per EMVCo and scheme bulletin cycles.'
}

Table network.network_response_code {
  network_response_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each network response code. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a reference lookup, no minimum attribute categories required.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Response codes are defined per scheme; replace string fields with scheme_id FK.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or scheme‑specific compliance obligations associated with the response code (e.g., PCI DSS, ISO 8583).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the response code record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the response code is valid for processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the response code is no longer valid (null if indefinite).']
  is_retryable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction may be safely retried after this response.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the response code details were last reviewed for compliance or accuracy.']
  network_response_code_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation of what the response code means.']
  network_response_code_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the response code within the scheme.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks about the response code.']
  recommended_merchant_action varchar [note: 'Suggested action for the merchant when this response code is returned (e.g., retry, contact cardholder, decline).']
  response_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the response code.']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code assigned by the card scheme to indicate the outcome of an authorization request.']
  response_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the response code (network‑generated, issuer‑generated, or processor‑generated).']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the operational impact or urgency associated with the response.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the response code record.']

  Note: 'Reference table of authorization and network response codes defined by each card scheme — approval codes, decline codes, referral codes, and error codes. Stores code value, scheme owner, description, recommended merchant action, and retry eligibility flag. Used for authorization decisioning and decline analytics.'
}

Table network.scheme_compliance_record {
  scheme_compliance_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the scheme compliance record.']
  attestation_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the uploaded attestation or certification document.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Compliance‑related fees or penalties are posted to designated GL accounts for financial reporting.']
  scheme_bulletin_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific scheme bulletin or mandate that drives this compliance requirement.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) to which this compliance record relates.']
  attestation_received_date date [note: 'Date the scheme acknowledged receipt of the compliance attestation.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which the required compliance actions must be completed.']
  compliance_record_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the compliance record, often used in reporting and audits.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual description of the specific scheme requirements that this record addresses.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (e.g., 0‑100) representing overall compliance health for the record.']
  compliance_type varchar [note: 'Category of compliance requirement (e.g., PCI DSS, tokenization, 3‑DS, ISO 20022, AML, risk management).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance record was initially created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the compliance obligations become effective for this record.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the compliance obligations cease (nullable for open‑ended obligations).']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Progress status of implementing the scheme mandate.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the regulatory jurisdiction applicable to the compliance requirement.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of this compliance record.']
  non_compliance_notice_date date [note: 'Date a formal notice of non‑compliance was received from the scheme.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations related to the compliance record.']
  notice_details varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the non‑compliance notice content.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation, law, or standard that mandates the compliance (e.g., GDPR, PSD2, SOX).']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Date by which remediation actions must be finished.']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed plan describing actions to remediate identified non‑compliance.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation effort.']
  risk_profile varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the compliance record based on potential impact of non‑compliance.']
  scheme_compliance_record_status varchar [note: 'Current overall status of the compliance record against the scheme requirements.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the compliance evidence or attestation was submitted to the scheme.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compliance record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the compliance record, incremented on each substantive change.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier (e.g., employee ID or email) who created the record.']

  Note: 'Tracks the platform\'s compliance status against scheme rules and mandates — PCI DSS compliance attestations submitted to schemes, mandate implementation confirmations, non-compliance notices received, remediation plans, and compliance deadline tracking per scheme bulletin.'
}

Table network.volume_limit {
  volume_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the network volume limit record.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Volume limits are set per scheme; replace scheme_code with scheme_id FK.']
  alert_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of limit at which an alert is generated.']
  bin_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending Bank Identification Number (BIN) of the range the limit covers.']
  bin_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting Bank Identification Number (BIN) of the range the limit covers.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the limit has been breached.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was created.']
  daily_limit_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions allowed per day.']
  daily_limit_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total transaction amount allowed per day.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the limit becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the limit expires (null if indefinite).']
  limit_code varchar [note: 'Unique code used to reference the limit configuration.']
  limit_scope varchar [note: 'Scope level at which the limit applies.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Metric type of the limit (number of transactions, monetary amount, or velocity).']
  limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for limit_value.']
  limit_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold for the limit (e.g., max amount or max count).']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Merchant Category Code to which the limit applies.']
  monthly_limit_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions allowed per month.']
  monthly_limit_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total transaction amount allowed per month.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction the limit governs.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  usage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current accumulated transaction amount within the active window.']
  usage_counter varchar [note: 'Current count of transactions counted towards the limit within the active window.']
  usage_reset_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the usage counters reset.']
  velocity_window_seconds varchar [note: 'Time window in seconds for velocity control evaluation.']
  volume_limit_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the limit purpose and any special notes.']
  volume_limit_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name for the volume limit configuration.']
  volume_limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the limit configuration.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the record.']

  Note: 'Defines network-imposed and internally configured volume limits — daily transaction count caps, TPV thresholds, single-transaction amount limits, and velocity controls per scheme, BIN range, or merchant category. Supports real-time limit enforcement and breach alerting.'
}

Table network.scheme_event {
  scheme_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each scheme-level operational event.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) affected by the event.']
  affected_mcc_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of MCC values whose merchants are impacted.']
  affected_regions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes impacted by the event.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business or technical justification for the event, especially for emergency routing changes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first created in the data lake.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the event in minutes, calculated from start and end timestamps.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end time for maintenance windows or the resolution time of an outage.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the event was observed or recorded.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the scheme event: scheduled maintenance, unplanned outage, emergency routing change, or scheme‑initiated parameter push.']
  impact_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or functional scope of the event (e.g., global, regional, specific merchant category).']
  is_emergency boolean [note: 'True if the event required emergency handling or unscheduled routing changes.']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the scheme configuration parameter that was changed (relevant for parameter_push events).']
  parameter_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'New value assigned to the scheme parameter during a parameter_push event.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Notes on how the event was resolved, including actions taken and responsible teams.']
  scheme_event_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative describing the cause, context, and details of the event.']
  scheme_event_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the event from creation to closure.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Business impact severity of the event, used for prioritization and escalation.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start time for maintenance windows or the beginning of an outage period.']
