Captures billing period, scheme identifier, acquiring bank, issuing bank, total transaction count, gross interchange amount, scheme fee deductions, net settlement amount, currency, and billing status. Supports reconciliation against scheme invoices.'
}

Table interchange.cost_of_acceptance {
  cost_of_acceptance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cost of acceptance record.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant for whom this cost of acceptance is calculated.']
  msf_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.msf_schedule. Business justification: cost_of_acceptance aggregates fees for a merchant schedule; adds msf_schedule_id FK']
  settlement_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement period during which these costs were incurred.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that approved the cost of acceptance calculation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost of acceptance record was approved for finalization.']
  average_ticket_size decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average transaction amount calculated as gross transaction volume divided by transaction count.']
  bin_range_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct Bank Identification Number (BIN) or Issuer Identification Number (IIN) ranges represented in the transactions for this settlement period.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'The date on which the cost of acceptance calculation was performed.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'The pricing model or calculation method used to determine the cost of acceptance for this merchant.']
  card_not_present_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction volume for card-not-present (CNP) transactions processed via e-commerce, mail order, or telephone order channels.']
  card_present_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction volume for card-present (CP) transactions processed via Point of Sale (POS) or mobile Point of Sale (mPOS) terminals.']
  chargeback_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction volume associated with chargebacks initiated during the settlement period, impacting net margin calculations.']
  cost_of_acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost of acceptance record indicating its processing state.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost of acceptance record was first created in the system.']
  credit_card_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction volume processed using credit cards during the settlement period.']
  cross_border_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction volume for cross-border transactions where the issuing bank country differs from the acquiring bank country.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency in which the cost of acceptance is denominated.']
  debit_card_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction volume processed using debit cards during the settlement period.']
  domestic_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction volume for domestic transactions where the issuing bank and acquiring bank are in the same country.']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the merchant transactions are subject to Durbin Amendment interchange rate caps (True) or exempt (False).']
  effective_rate_bps decimal(8,2) [note: 'Effective cost of acceptance rate expressed in basis points (bps), calculated as (total cost / gross transaction volume) * 10000.']
  gross_transaction_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Payment Volume (TPV) representing the gross value of all transactions processed during the settlement period before any fees or deductions.']
  interchange_qualification_tier varchar [note: 'The interchange qualification tier applied to the majority of transactions in this settlement period, determining the IRF rate structure.']
  mcc varchar [note: 'Four-digit Merchant Category Code (MCC) classifying the merchants business type, which influences interchange qualification and rates.']
  mdr_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) percentage applied to the merchant for this settlement period, representing the total fee as a percentage of transaction volume.']
  net_margin decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net margin calculated as total MSF revenue minus total interchange cost and total scheme fees, representing the acquiring portfolio profitability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the cost of acceptance calculation, adjustments, or special circumstances.']
  refund_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transaction volume associated with refunds processed during the settlement period.']
  settlement_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the settlement period covered by this cost of acceptance record.']
  settlement_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the settlement period covered by this cost of acceptance record.']
  total_interchange_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Interchange Reimbursement Fee (IRF) paid to issuing banks for all transactions during the settlement period.']
  total_msf_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total Merchant Service Fee (MSF) revenue collected from the merchant for payment processing services during the settlement period.']
  total_scheme_fees decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total assessment fees charged by card schemes (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) for network usage during the settlement period.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transactions processed for the merchant during the settlement period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost of acceptance record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Calculated cost-of-acceptance record per merchant per settlement period, aggregating interchange fees, scheme assessment fees, and MSF components to derive the total payment acceptance cost. Stores gross transaction volume (TPV), total interchange cost, total scheme fees, total MSF revenue, net margin, effective rate (basis points), and period identifiers. Supports acquiring portfolio profitability analysis.'
}

Table interchange.downgrade {
  downgrade_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interchange downgrade event.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: During dispute resolution, downgrade details are needed to assess cost impact; linking downgrade to dispute case enables that process.']
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: interchange_downgrade references both original and downgraded IRF categories; adds irf_rate_category_id FK']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant whose transaction was downgraded.']
  risk_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_case. Business justification: Each downgrade may trigger a risk investigation; linking allows auditors to trace downgrade to its risk case.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transaction that experienced the interchange downgrade.']
  acquirer_reference_data varchar [note: 'Additional reference data provided by the acquiring bank for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  arn varchar [note: 'Unique 23-digit reference number assigned by the acquiring bank to track the transaction across the payment network.']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the transaction was authorized by the issuing bank.']
  avs_response_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuer indicating the result of address verification, which is often required for optimal interchange qualification.']
  bin varchar [note: 'First 6-8 digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank and card product type.']
  capture_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the authorized transaction was captured for settlement.']
  card_present_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the transaction was card-present (true) or card-not-present (false), which affects interchange qualification.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network brand that processed the transaction and applied the downgrade.']
  card_tier varchar [note: 'The card product tier or level (e.g., standard, rewards, premium, corporate) which influences interchange rates.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment card used in the transaction, which determines applicable interchange rate tables.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this downgrade record was first created in the data platform.']
  cvv_response_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuer indicating the result of CVV verification, which is often required for optimal interchange qualification in card-not-present transactions.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the interchange downgrade was identified and recorded in the system.']
  downgraded_irf_rate_bps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The actual interchange rate in basis points applied after the downgrade, representing a higher cost to the merchant.']
  durbin_regulated_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the transaction is subject to Durbin Amendment interchange rate caps for regulated debit cards.']
  emv_chip_transaction boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the transaction used EMV chip technology, which typically qualifies for lower interchange rates.']
  incremental_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The additional interchange fee cost incurred by the merchant due to the downgrade, calculated as the rate differential applied to the transaction amount.']
  incremental_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the incremental cost amount.']
  mcc varchar [note: 'Four-digit code classifying the merchant\'s type of business, which influences interchange qualification rules.']
  merchant_processing_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the transaction was processed, affecting interchange qualification criteria.']
  original_irf_rate_bps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The optimal interchange rate in basis points that would have applied if the transaction had not been downgraded.']
  preventable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downgrade could have been prevented through merchant action or system configuration changes.']
  rate_differential_bps decimal(6,2) [note: 'The difference in basis points between the downgraded rate and the original qualified rate, representing the incremental cost penalty.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the specific reason for the interchange downgrade, such as missing AVS data, late settlement, or incomplete transaction data.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable explanation of why the transaction was downgraded, providing context for merchant coaching and remediation.']
  remediation_action_recommended varchar [note: 'Suggested corrective action for the merchant to prevent future downgrades of this type, used for merchant coaching and optimization.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which the transaction was settled and the downgrade was applied during clearing.']
  settlement_delay_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between authorization and settlement, where excessive delay can trigger interchange downgrades.']
  three_ds_authentication_status varchar [note: 'Status of 3-D Secure authentication for the transaction, which can affect interchange qualification for e-commerce transactions.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross transaction amount in the transaction currency on which the interchange fees are calculated.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The date on which the original transaction was authorized or processed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this downgrade record was last modified in the data platform.']

  Note: 'Records instances where a transaction failed to qualify for the optimal interchange rate category and was downgraded to a higher-cost tier. Captures original qualified category, downgraded-to category, downgrade reason code, rate differential (basis points), incremental cost, merchant identifier, transaction reference, and scheme. Enables downgrade root-cause analysis and merchant coaching.'
}

Table interchange.scheme_invoice {
  scheme_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the card scheme invoice record. Primary key for the scheme invoice entity.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Invoice generation requires linking to the card scheme for correct fee line items.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for audit trail linking each invoice creation to the responsible employee, needed for PCI DSS and internal compliance reporting.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Regulatory reporting and reconciliation require associating a scheme invoice with the dispute case that challenges its amounts.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Required for GL posting of scheme invoices; financial reporting mandates linking each invoice to its GL account.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Scheme invoices may be issued in various currencies; linking to the FX rate enables accurate accounting and audit of converted totals.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requires each scheme invoice to reference the governing regulatory obligation for audit and compliance verification.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.cycle. Business justification: Links scheme invoices to the settlement cycle they belong to, supporting regulatory reporting of scheme fees per cycle.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount of credits, refunds, or additional charges applied to this invoice as adjustments from prior billing periods or corrections. Positive values represent additional charges; negative values represent credits.']
  assessment_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the invoice representing assessment fees charged by the card scheme based on transaction volume or value. Typically calculated as a percentage of Total Payment Volume (TPV) or Gross Merchandise Value (GMV).']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the billing period covered by this scheme invoice. Defines the end of the time window for which fees and assessments are being charged.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the billing period covered by this scheme invoice. Defines the beginning of the time window for which fees and assessments are being charged.']
  chargeback_processing_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the invoice representing fees charged by the card scheme for processing chargebacks and disputes during the billing period. Includes fees for dispute lifecycle management and arbitration services.']
  compliance_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the invoice representing penalties or fines assessed by the card scheme for non-compliance with network rules, PCI DSS requirements, or other regulatory standards. Zero if no penalties were assessed.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center or business unit code responsible for this scheme invoice expense. Used for internal cost allocation and management reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this scheme invoice record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cross_border_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the invoice representing fees charged for cross-border or international transactions where the issuing bank and acquiring bank are in different countries or regions.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'The date on which this invoice was formally disputed with the card scheme. Null if invoice has not been disputed.']
  dispute_reason_code varchar [note: 'The reason code provided if this invoice is disputed. Indicates the nature of the dispute (e.g., incorrect fee calculation, unrecognized charges, billing error). Null if invoice is not disputed.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the dispute for this invoice was resolved. Null if dispute is still open or if invoice was never disputed.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment must be received by the card scheme to avoid late fees or penalties. Defines the payment deadline for this invoice.']
  interchange_reimbursement_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the invoice representing Interchange Reimbursement Fees (IRF) paid between acquiring and issuing banks through the card scheme. This is the fee paid by the acquiring bank to the issuing bank for each transaction.']
  invoice_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this scheme invoice is denominated. All monetary amounts on this invoice are expressed in this currency.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date on which the card scheme issued this invoice. This is the principal business event timestamp representing when the billing statement was generated and sent.']
  invoice_document_url varchar [note: 'The URL or file path to the original invoice document (PDF or other format) provided by the card scheme. Used for audit trail and detailed line-item review.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique invoice number assigned by the card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) for this billing statement. This is the business identifier used for reconciliation and payment reference.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scheme invoice indicating its payment and processing state. Tracks the invoice from issuance through payment completion or dispute resolution.']
  network_participation_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the invoice representing fees charged for participation in the card scheme network. This is a recurring membership or access fee for being part of the payment network.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this scheme invoice, including special instructions, dispute details, or reconciliation observations. Used for operational communication and audit trail.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date on which payment was made to the card scheme for this invoice. Null if invoice remains unpaid.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or method used to settle this scheme invoice. Indicates how the payment was made to the card scheme.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'The unique reference number or transaction identifier for the payment made to settle this invoice. Used for payment reconciliation and audit trail. Null if invoice is unpaid.']
  reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'The reference identifier linking this scheme invoice to the internal reconciliation record or general ledger entry. Used to track the invoice through the accounts payable and financial close process.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the reconciliation process for this scheme invoice, indicating whether the invoice has been matched to internal transaction records and general ledger entries.']
  scheme_service_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the invoice representing fees for specific card scheme services such as tokenization, fraud prevention, dispute resolution, or other value-added services provided by the network.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount of taxes (VAT, GST, sales tax, or other applicable taxes) applied to this scheme invoice. Zero if no taxes are applicable.']
  total_amount_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount payable to the card scheme for this invoice, representing the sum of all line-item fees, assessments, and charges. This is the net total amount due before any adjustments or credits.']
  transaction_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of all transactions processed through the card scheme network during the billing period, expressed in the invoice currency. Represents the Total Payment Volume (TPV) or Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) for fee calculation purposes.']
  transaction_volume varchar [note: 'The total number of transactions processed through the card scheme network during the billing period covered by this invoice. Used to validate volume-based fee calculations.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this scheme invoice record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this scheme invoice record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Card scheme invoice record representing the periodic billing statement received from Visa, Mastercard, Amex, or Discover for network participation fees, assessment fees, and scheme services. Captures invoice number, scheme identifier, billing period, invoice date, due date, line-item fee categories, total amount due, currency, payment status, and reconciliation reference. SSOT for scheme payables.'
}

Table interchange.scheme_invoice_line {
  scheme_invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the scheme invoice line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the finance team member who approved this fee line for payment. Null until approval is granted. Used for segregation of duties controls and audit trail.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dispute case if this fee line is under dispute with the card scheme. Links to the dispute management system for tracking dispute lifecycle, evidence submission, and resolution. Null when no dispute exists.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the finance team member who approved this fee line for payment. Null until approval is granted. Used for segregation of duties controls and audit trail.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Each fee line must be posted to a specific GL account for accurate financial statements and audit trails.']
  original_invoice_line_scheme_invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original scheme_invoice_line_id that this adjustment line is correcting. Null for original fee charges. Used to trace adjustment history and maintain audit trail for billing corrections.']
  scheme_fee_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_fee_table. Business justification: scheme_invoice_line details a fee defined in scheme_fee_table; adds scheme_fee_table_id FK']
  scheme_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent scheme invoice that this line item belongs to. Links line-level detail to the invoice header for reconciliation and aggregation.']
  adjustment_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item represents an adjustment or correction to a previously billed fee. True for adjustments, credits, or corrections; false for original fee charges. Used to track billing accuracy and dispute resolution.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for an adjustment when adjustment_indicator is true. Examples include billing error correction, rate change retroactive application, volume correction, or dispute resolution credit. Null for non-adjustment lines.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fee line was approved for payment by the finance team. Null until approval is granted. Used for payment processing workflow and SOX audit trail.']
  authorization_method varchar [note: 'Method by which transactions were authorized. EMV chip, contactless NFC, magnetic stripe, manual key entry, ecommerce, recurring billing, or mail-order/telephone-order (MOTO). Interchange rates and fraud liability vary by authorization method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: chip|contactless|magnetic_stripe|manual|ecommerce|recurring|moto — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  billing_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this fee is billed. All monetary amounts on this line (transaction_value_basis, fee_amount) are expressed in this currency.']
  bin_range_high varchar [note: 'Upper bound of the BIN or IIN range that this fee line applies to. Used in conjunction with bin_range_low to define the card portfolio segment for this fee calculation.']
  bin_range_low varchar [note: 'Lower bound of the BIN or IIN (Issuer Identification Number) range that this fee line applies to. Used for BIN-level interchange qualification and fee segmentation. First 6-8 digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank.']
  card_product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the card product that this fee applies to. Interchange rates and scheme fees vary significantly by card product type. Examples include consumer credit, consumer debit, commercial cards, and premium card tiers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit|debit|prepaid|commercial|rewards|signature|platinum|world|world_elite — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center or business unit code for allocating this fee expense. Used for management accounting, profitability analysis, and internal chargeback to business lines or product portfolios.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this scheme invoice line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cross_border_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fee line applies to cross-border transactions (issuer and acquirer in different countries). Cross-border transactions typically incur additional scheme fees and higher interchange rates.']
  durbin_regulated_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fee line applies to transactions subject to Durbin Amendment interchange caps (US Regulation II). Durbin-regulated debit transactions from large issuers have capped interchange rates. Critical for US market compliance and revenue recognition.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert settlement_currency_code amounts to billing_currency_code amounts. Null when billing and settlement currencies are the same. Used for cross-border fee reconciliation and dynamic currency conversion (DCC) scenarios.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated fee amount for this line item in the billing currency. Represents the actual charge from the card scheme for this specific fee component. This is the primary financial value used for reconciliation and accounts payable processing.']
  fee_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the fee for reporting and cost allocation. Groups related fee types into business-meaningful categories for financial analysis and revenue recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: interchange|assessment|network|authorization|cross_border|chargeback|dispute|service|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fee_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the fee type and its business purpose. Provides context for the fee_type_code, explaining what service or transaction activity triggered this charge.']
  fee_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The rate applied to calculate this fee. May represent a percentage (e.g., 0.015 for 1.5%), a per-transaction amount, or a tiered rate depending on fee_rate_type. Used in conjunction with transaction_volume_basis or transaction_value_basis to compute fee_amount.']
  fee_rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates how the fee_rate should be interpreted and applied. Percentage rates are applied to transaction_value_basis, per-transaction rates are multiplied by transaction_volume_basis, tiered rates vary by volume thresholds, flat rates are fixed amounts, and basis points represent hundredths of a percent.']
  fee_type_code varchar [note: 'Card scheme-specific code identifying the type of fee being charged. Examples include interchange reimbursement fees (IRF), assessment fees, network fees, authorization fees, cross-border fees, and scheme service fees. Aligns with Visa and Mastercard fee code taxonomies.']
  interchange_qualification_tier varchar [note: 'Specific interchange qualification tier or program that this fee line is associated with. Examples include CPS/Retail, CPS/Card Not Present, EIRF, Standard, or scheme-specific tier names. Determines the applicable interchange reimbursement fee (IRF) rate.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the parent scheme invoice. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items in reconciliation and dispute processes.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit ISO 18245 code classifying the type of merchant or business where transactions occurred. Interchange qualification and fee structures often vary by MCC. Used for industry-specific fee segmentation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, explanations, or context about this fee line. Used by finance and operations teams to document unusual circumstances, reconciliation findings, or dispute rationale.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the payment transaction used to settle this fee line with the card scheme. Links to accounts payable payment records and bank statements. Null until payment is executed.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reconciliation process for this fee line. Tracks whether the scheme-billed fee has been matched against internal transaction records, whether variances exist, and whether the line has been approved for payment.']
  service_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the service period that this fee line covers. Defines the end of the transaction activity window used to calculate this fee. Used for period-based reconciliation and revenue recognition cutoff.']
  service_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the service period that this fee line covers. Defines the beginning of the transaction activity window used to calculate this fee. Used for period-based reconciliation and accrual accounting.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the underlying transactions were settled. May differ from billing_currency_code when cross-border or multi-currency processing is involved.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction activity that generated this fee. Different transaction types may incur different fee structures and rates. Critical for accurate fee reconciliation and revenue recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase|cash_advance|refund|reversal|chargeback|fee|adjustment — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transaction_value_basis decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of transactions that this fee line is calculated against. Represents the dollar volume basis used for percentage-based fee calculations. Expressed in the billing currency.']
  transaction_volume_basis varchar [note: 'Number of transactions or events that this fee line is calculated against. Represents the volume basis (transaction count, authorization count, or other countable unit) used to compute the fee amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this scheme invoice line record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the scheme-billed fee_amount and the internally calculated expected fee amount. Positive values indicate overbilling, negative values indicate underbilling. Null when reconciliation_status is matched. Used for dispute initiation and billing accuracy monitoring.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail of a card scheme invoice, breaking down each fee component billed by the scheme. Captures fee type code, fee description, transaction volume basis, rate applied, calculated amount, currency, and the parent scheme invoice reference. Enables granular reconciliation of scheme fees against internal transaction volumes and interchange qualification records.'
}

Table interchange.revenue_recognition_entry {
  revenue_recognition_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the revenue recognition entry. Primary key for this entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Cost‑center allocation is required for internal reporting and profitability analysis of recognized revenue.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner entity with whom revenue is shared for this entry (null if no revenue sharing applies).']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue recognition entries need a GL account reference for journal posting and revenue reporting.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant for whom revenue is being recognized. Links to the merchant master entity.']
  original_entry_revenue_recognition_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original revenue recognition entry if this entry is an adjustment or reversal (null for original entries).']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Revenue recognition entries need the FX rate used to translate fees into the reporting currency for GAAP/IFRS compliance.']
  scheme_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_invoice. Business justification: Revenue recognition entries are tied to a specific scheme invoice; adds scheme_invoice_id FK']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch that generated this revenue recognition entry.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement. Business justification: Connects revenue recognition entries to the master settlement record for consolidated financial reporting and GL posting.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'The accounting period (YYYY-MM format) to which this revenue recognition entry is posted.']
  adjustment_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for any adjustment or reversal applied to this entry.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for any adjustment or reversal applied to this revenue recognition entry (null if no adjustment).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue recognition entry record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the revenue amounts are denominated.']
  deferred_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The portion of gross revenue that is deferred to future periods under revenue recognition rules.']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this revenue entry is subject to Durbin Amendment interchange rate caps (applies to regulated debit card transactions).']
  erp_batch_reference varchar [note: 'The batch identifier in the ERP system (Oracle Financials) to which this revenue recognition entry was posted.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total gross revenue amount before any deferrals or adjustments.']
  interchange_rate_applied decimal(8,6) [note: 'The effective interchange rate (as a decimal) applied to calculate the interchange income component of this entry.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue recognition entry record was last modified or updated.']
  mdr_rate_applied decimal(8,6) [note: 'The effective Merchant Discount Rate (as a decimal) applied to calculate the MSF income component of this entry.']
  net_revenue_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net revenue amount after deducting scheme fees and other pass-through costs from recognized revenue.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context or audit trail information for this revenue recognition entry.']
  posted_to_gl_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this revenue recognition entry was posted to the General Ledger system.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the product line or service category for which revenue is recognized (e.g., acquiring, issuing, gateway services).']
  recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which the revenue is recognized in accordance with ASC 606 revenue recognition principles.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the revenue recognition entry in the accounting workflow: pending, recognized, adjusted, reversed, or finalized.']
  recognized_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The net revenue amount recognized in the current period (gross_amount minus deferred_amount).']
  revenue_share_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The absolute revenue amount shared with the partner, calculated from recognized_amount and revenue_share_percentage.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage of recognized revenue shared with the partner (as a decimal, e.g., 0.1500 for 15%).']
  revenue_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue type being recognized: interchange income, Merchant Service Fee (MSF) income, scheme fee pass-through, gateway fee, processing fee, or other.']
  scheme_fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The card scheme fee amount (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) passed through or absorbed in this revenue recognition entry.']
  settlement_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the settlement period for which revenue is being recognized.']
  settlement_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the settlement period for which revenue is being recognized.']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this entry has passed SOX compliance controls and internal audit checks.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'The number of transactions that contributed to this revenue recognition entry within the settlement period.']

  Note: 'Revenue recognition entry capturing the recognized interchange and MSF revenue for each settlement period per merchant and product line. Stores recognition date, revenue type (interchange income, MSF income, scheme fee pass-through), gross amount, deferred amount, recognized amount, accounting period, GL account code, and cost center. Supports SOX-compliant revenue recognition under ASC 606.'
}

Table interchange.rate_history {
  rate_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each interchange rate history record. Primary key for the interchange rate history audit trail.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Interchange rate history is tracked per currency pair to support multi‑currency pricing analysis and audit.']
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: interchange_rate_history records changes to an IRF category; adds irf_rate_category_id FK']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Rate changes are subject to regulatory approval; the FK ties each rate history record to the applicable regulatory obligation.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) that published this rate change.']
  superseded_by_history_rate_history_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the interchange_rate_history_id that superseded this rate record. Null if this is the current active rate.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this rate change to internal audit logs, compliance documentation, or regulatory filing records.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'The specific card brand or sub-brand this rate applies to (e.g., Visa Signature, Mastercard World Elite).']
  card_present_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate applies to card-present transactions (True) or card-not-present transactions (False).']
  card_product_type varchar [note: 'The type of card product this rate applies to (credit, debit, prepaid, commercial, purchasing).']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for the interchange rate change (e.g., annual review, regulatory mandate, market adjustment).']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the interchange rate was changed, as provided by the card scheme.']
  contactless_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless (NFC) transactions are eligible for this interchange rate (True) or not (False).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this interchange rate applies (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interchange rate history record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  cross_border_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate applies to cross-border transactions (True) or domestic transactions (False).']
  durbin_cap_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'The maximum interchange fee allowed under Durbin Amendment regulations for this rate category. Null if not Durbin-regulated.']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate is subject to Durbin Amendment debit interchange caps (True) or exempt (False).']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the new interchange rate became effective. Used for point-in-time rate lookups and transaction repricing.']
  emv_chip_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMV chip authentication is required to qualify for this interchange rate (True) or not (False).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this interchange rate expires or is superseded by a new rate. Null if the rate is currently active with no known end date.']
  installment_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether installment payment transactions are eligible for this interchange rate (True) or not (False).']
  irf_category_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the interchange category (e.g., Card Present Retail, Card Not Present E-commerce).']
  mcc_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of four-digit MCC codes that this rate applies to or excludes, depending on the restriction type.']
  mcc_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the MCC list is inclusive (rate applies only to listed MCCs), exclusive (rate applies to all except listed MCCs), or none (no MCC restrictions).']
  new_fixed_fee decimal(10,4) [note: 'The new fixed interchange fee amount that became effective with this change. Expressed in the rate currency.']
  new_rate_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'The new interchange rate percentage that became effective with this change. Used for forward-looking rate calculations.']
  prior_fixed_fee decimal(10,4) [note: 'The fixed interchange fee amount that was in effect before this change. Expressed in the rate currency.']
  prior_rate_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'The interchange rate percentage that was in effect before this change. Used for historical comparison and back-billing corrections.']
  publication_date date [note: 'The date on which the card scheme published or announced this rate change.']
  publication_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the source publication or bulletin where this rate change was announced (e.g., Visa April 2024 Bulletin, Mastercard Q2 2024 Rate Update).']
  rate_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the interchange rate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this interchange rate record (active, expired, superseded by newer rate, pending future effective date).']
  recurring_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether recurring payment transactions are eligible for this interchange rate (True) or not (False).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where this interchange rate applies (e.g., EMEA, APAC, LATAM). Used for multi-country rate structures.']
  three_ds_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3-D Secure authentication is required to qualify for this interchange rate (True) or not (False).']
  tokenization_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tokenized transactions are eligible for this interchange rate (True) or not (False).']
  transaction_max_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum transaction amount allowed to qualify for this interchange rate. Null if no maximum applies.']
  transaction_min_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum transaction amount required to qualify for this interchange rate. Null if no minimum applies.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'The type of transaction this interchange rate applies to (purchase, cash advance, balance transfer, refund).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interchange rate history record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this rate category, incremented with each rate change. Used for audit trail and version control.']

  Note: 'Historical audit trail of interchange rate changes published by card schemes, capturing prior rate, new rate, effective date, expiry date, scheme identifier, rate category code, change reason, and the source publication reference (e.g., Visa April bulletin). Enables point-in-time rate lookups for dispute resolution, back-billing corrections, and regulatory audit.'
}

Table interchange.mcc_interchange_map {
  mcc_interchange_map_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the MCC interchange mapping record. Primary key for the table.']
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: mcc_interchange_map references IRF category; adds irf_rate_category_id FK']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: Mapping table must reference canonical MCC records for accurate rate look‑ups.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the card scheme (network brand) to which this MCC interchange mapping applies. Links to the card scheme reference data.']
  superseded_by_map_mcc_interchange_map_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mcc_interchange_map_id of the newer mapping record that replaces this one. Null if this is the current active version.']
  card_not_present_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MCC interchange mapping applies to card-not-present (CNP) transactions such as e-commerce, mail order, or telephone order. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  card_present_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MCC interchange mapping applies to card-present (CP) transactions at physical point-of-sale terminals. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  contactless_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless (NFC/tap-to-pay) transactions with this MCC are eligible for the mapped interchange rate. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the geographic market or jurisdiction for which this MCC interchange mapping applies (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Null if globally applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MCC interchange mapping record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  cross_border_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cross-border transactions (where issuer and acquirer are in different countries) with this MCC are eligible for the mapped interchange rate. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  data_quality_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator of the data quality and verification status of this MCC interchange mapping record. Verified records have been validated against official scheme documentation.']
  durbin_compliance_tier varchar [note: 'Classification tier for Durbin Amendment compliance indicating whether the MCC qualifies for exemptions, small issuer treatment, or full regulation.']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions with this MCC are subject to Durbin Amendment interchange rate caps for regulated debit cards (US market). True if regulated, False otherwise.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this MCC interchange mapping ceases to be active. Null if the mapping is currently active with no defined end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this MCC interchange mapping becomes active and applicable to transactions. Part of the temporal validity range.']
  emv_chip_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMV chip authentication is required for transactions with this MCC to qualify for the mapped interchange rate. True if required, False otherwise.']
  installment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether installment payment transactions with this MCC are eligible for the mapped interchange rate. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  interchange_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the interchange fixed fee (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  interchange_fixed_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed per-transaction fee component of the interchange fee applicable to this MCC and scheme combination, in the base currency.']
  interchange_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'Base percentage rate component of the interchange fee applicable to this MCC and scheme combination. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0175 for 1.75%).']
  mapping_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MCC interchange mapping record. Active mappings are used for transaction qualification; Superseded mappings have been replaced by newer versions.']
  merchant_pricing_impact varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing impact this MCC interchange mapping has on merchant discount rate (MDR) configuration. Indicates whether the MCC qualifies for standard, reduced, premium, or penalty pricing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this MCC interchange mapping. May include scheme rule references, pricing rationale, or implementation guidance.']
  recurring_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether recurring or subscription-based transactions with this MCC are eligible for the mapped interchange rate. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the geographic region or regulatory zone for which this MCC interchange mapping applies (e.g., \'SEPA\', \'APAC\', \'LATAM\'). Null if country-specific or globally applicable.']
  revenue_recognition_category varchar [note: 'Accounting category code used for revenue recognition and general ledger posting of interchange income associated with this MCC. Supports financial reporting and cost-of-acceptance calculations.']
  scheme_fee_tier varchar [note: 'Tier or category code for scheme assessment fees applicable to this MCC. Used to determine network fees charged by the card scheme.']
  scheme_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific card scheme program or initiative associated with this MCC interchange mapping (e.g., \'Visa Traditional Rewards\', \'Mastercard World Elite\', \'Small Ticket Interchange\').']
  source_system_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or code from the source system (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform, Merchant Management System) from which this MCC interchange mapping was originally loaded or synchronized.']
  special_program_eligibility varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of special interchange programs or rate qualifications this MCC is eligible for (e.g., \'Supermarket Program\', \'Fuel Program\', \'Utility Program\', \'Government/Education\', \'Emerging Markets\').']
  three_ds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3-D Secure authentication is required for card-not-present transactions with this MCC to qualify for the mapped interchange rate. True if required, False otherwise.']
  tokenization_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tokenized payment credentials (DPAN) are eligible for this MCC interchange mapping. True if eligible, False otherwise.']
  transaction_max_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount threshold for this MCC interchange mapping to apply. Transactions above this amount may qualify for different interchange rates. Null if no maximum applies.']
  transaction_min_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount threshold for this MCC interchange mapping to apply. Transactions below this amount may qualify for different interchange rates. Null if no minimum applies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MCC interchange mapping record was last modified. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this MCC interchange mapping record. Incremented when the mapping is updated to reflect scheme rule changes or pricing adjustments.']

  Note: 'Mapping table associating Merchant Category Codes (MCC) to applicable interchange rate programs and scheme fee tiers. Captures MCC code, MCC description, card scheme, applicable IRF category codes, special program eligibility (e.g., supermarket, fuel, utilities), and effective date range. Drives MCC-based interchange qualification logic and merchant pricing configuration.'
}

Table interchange.acquiring_portfolio_pricing {
  acquiring_portfolio_pricing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each acquiring portfolio pricing record.']
  appetite_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_appetite_framework. Business justification: Portfolio pricing rules are governed by the firm’s risk appetite framework; FK provides direct reference for compliance reporting.']
  durbin_compliance_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.durbin_compliance_tier. Business justification: acquiring_portfolio_pricing may be governed by a Durbin compliance tier; adds durbin_compliance_tier_id FK']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring portfolio to which this pricing configuration applies.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.interchange_program. Business justification: Acquiring portfolio pricing can be associated with a specific interchange program; adds interchange_program_id FK']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the card scheme (network) to which the pricing rules apply.']
  acquiring_portfolio_pricing_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the pricing contract purpose and scope.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether changes to this pricing contract require formal approval.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pricing contract was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the pricing contract.']
  base_interchange_pass_through_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the base interchange fee is passed through to the merchant without markup.']
  compliance_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the latest compliance review for this pricing contract.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing contract.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the pricing contract for change tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pricing record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in the contract.']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'True if the pricing is subject to the Durbin Amendment regulations.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the pricing contract becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the pricing contract expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  is_default_pricing boolean [note: 'True if this pricing configuration is the default for the portfolio.']
  is_portfolio_level boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pricing applies at the portfolio level (vs. individual merchant level).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or performance review of the pricing contract.']
  markup_basis_points varchar [note: 'Number of basis points added as markup on top of the base interchange rate.']
  monthly_minimum_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum monthly revenue that must be generated under the pricing contract.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the pricing contract.']
  per_transaction_markup_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat amount added to each transaction as a markup.']
  pricing_application_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of transactions to which this pricing configuration applies.']
  pricing_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the most recent change to the pricing configuration.']
  pricing_contract_code varchar [note: 'Business‑visible code or reference number for the pricing contract.']
  pricing_currency_conversion_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Conversion rate used when pricing is expressed in a currency different from the settlement currency.']
  pricing_margin_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the margin is expressed as a percentage or a fixed amount.']
  pricing_margin_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the margin, interpreted according to pricing_margin_type.']
  pricing_model_type varchar [note: 'Type of pricing model applied to the portfolio (e.g., interchange‑plus, flat‑rate, tiered, volume‑based).']
  pricing_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that supplied the pricing configuration.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pricing configuration complies with applicable regulations (e.g., PCI DSS, PSD2).']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the contract\'s compliance or performance review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the pricing record.']
  volume_tier_1_markup_basis_points varchar [note: 'Markup in basis points applied when transaction volume reaches tier 1.']
  volume_tier_1_threshold varchar [note: 'Transaction count threshold for the first volume‑based pricing tier.']
  volume_tier_2_markup_basis_points varchar [note: 'Markup in basis points applied when transaction volume reaches tier 2.']
  volume_tier_2_threshold varchar [note: 'Transaction count threshold for the second volume‑based pricing tier.']

  Note: 'Acquiring portfolio-level pricing configuration defining the interchange-plus margin, flat-rate, or tiered pricing model applied to a portfolio of merchants managed by an acquirer, ISO, or PayFac. Captures portfolio identifier, pricing model type, base interchange pass-through flag, markup basis points, per-transaction markup, monthly minimum, volume tier thresholds, and contract effective dates.'
}

Table interchange.issuer_interchange_income {
  issuer_interchange_income_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the issuer interchange income record.']
  issuer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issuing bank or financial institution earning the interchange income.']
  scheme_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_invoice. Business justification: Issuer interchange income is reported on a scheme invoice; adds scheme_invoice_id FK']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total adjustments applied to interchange income (chargebacks, disputes, corrections, credits).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the interchange income settlement.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the interchange income settlement was approved.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'Card scheme or network brand (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, Amex).']
  card_product_type varchar [note: 'Type of card product for which interchange income is earned (e.g., credit, debit, prepaid, commercial). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit|debit|prepaid|commercial|rewards|platinum|signature — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the issuer\'s primary operating jurisdiction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interchange income record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interchange income includes cross-border transactions (True if transactions crossed international borders).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this record.']
  discrepancy_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of discrepancy identified during reconciliation between expected and actual interchange income.']
  discrepancy_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for any reconciliation discrepancy (e.g., timing difference, fee mismatch, transaction dispute).']
  durbin_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for exemption from Durbin Amendment caps (e.g., small issuer exemption, prepaid exemption).']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interchange income is subject to Durbin Amendment interchange rate caps (True for regulated debit cards).']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the interchange income is posted.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the interchange income was posted to the general ledger.']
  gross_interchange_earned decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross interchange revenue earned by the issuer before any deductions or fees.']
  interchange_qualification_tier varchar [note: 'Interchange rate tier or category that the transactions qualified for (e.g., standard, enhanced, premium).']
  issuer_bin varchar [note: 'Bank Identification Number or Issuer Identification Number (IIN) representing the issuer\'s card range.']
  issuer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the issuing bank or financial institution.']
  net_interchange_income decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net interchange income after all scheme fees, processing fees, and adjustments have been deducted from gross interchange.']
  network_assessment_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Assessment fee charged by the card network for transaction processing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this interchange income record, including any special circumstances or exceptions.']
  portfolio_segment varchar [note: 'Issuer portfolio segment or business line (e.g., consumer, commercial, small business, premium).']
  processing_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Processing fees deducted from interchange income for transaction handling and settlement services.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of reconciliation between issuer records and scheme settlement reports.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for the issuer (e.g., EMEA, APAC, LATAM, North America).']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which the interchange income is recognized as revenue for accounting purposes.']
  scheme_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees charged by the card scheme (network fees, assessment fees) deducted from gross interchange.']
  scheme_settlement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number provided by the card scheme for this settlement period.']
  settlement_cycle_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the settlement cycle or batch (e.g., monthly, weekly cycle code).']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the interchange income was settled to the issuer\'s account.']
  settlement_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the settlement period for which interchange income is calculated.']
  settlement_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the settlement period for which interchange income is calculated.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the interchange income settlement (e.g., pending, settled, reconciled, disputed).']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transactions contributing to the interchange income for this settlement period.']
  transaction_volume decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total monetary volume of transactions processed during the settlement period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interchange income record was last updated.']

  Note: 'Issuer-side interchange income record capturing the interchange revenue earned by the issuing bank for each settlement period. Stores issuer identifier, card product type, settlement period, transaction count, gross interchange earned, scheme fee deductions, net interchange income, currency, and reconciliation status. Supports issuer portfolio revenue recognition and profitability reporting.'
}

Table interchange.interchange_reconciliation {
  interchange_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interchange reconciliation record. Primary key.']
  acquirer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the acquiring bank (merchant\'s bank) involved in the interchange transactions.']
  acquiring_bank_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Reconciliation reports must reference the acquiring bank\'s legal entity to resolve variance and audit discrepancies.']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor or manager who approved the reconciliation results and any adjustments.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dispute case opened with the card scheme if the variance could not be internally resolved.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or analyst who performed the reconciliation and investigation.']
  issuing_bank_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the issuing bank (cardholder\'s bank) involved in the interchange transactions.']
  primary_interchange_ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the acquiring bank (merchant\'s bank) involved in the interchange transactions.']
  primary_interchange_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or analyst who performed the reconciliation and investigation.']
  scheme_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_invoice. Business justification: Interchange reconciliation aligns with a scheme invoice; adds scheme_invoice_id FK']
  settlement_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement period during which the interchange transactions occurred.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Any adjustment amount applied to reconcile the variance, either credited or debited based on dispute resolution.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for any adjustment applied to this reconciliation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the reconciliation was approved by the authorized supervisor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this reconciliation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this reconciliation record.']
  cycle_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier for the reconciliation cycle or batch run during which this record was processed.']
  dispute_filed_date date [note: 'The date on which a formal dispute was filed with the card scheme regarding this reconciliation variance.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the scheme dispute was resolved and final settlement amounts were agreed.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which interchange reconciliation amounts are posted.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the reconciliation results and any adjustments were posted to the general ledger.']
  internal_calculated_interchange_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total interchange amount calculated internally based on qualification rules and transaction data for the settlement period.']
  internal_settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which internal settlement processing was completed for this period.']
  reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the reconciliation was completed and status was finalized.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reconciliation record indicating whether internal and scheme amounts are aligned.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this reconciliation record for tracking and audit purposes.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the investigation, resolution actions taken, and final outcome for this reconciliation record.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue or cost associated with this interchange reconciliation is recognized for financial reporting.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause identified for any variance in this reconciliation.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the root cause identified for the variance, including specific transaction or rule issues.']
  scheme_identifier varchar [note: 'The payment card scheme (network brand) for which this reconciliation is performed.']
  scheme_reported_interchange_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total interchange amount reported by the card scheme for the same settlement period.']
  scheme_settlement_date date [note: 'The date on which the card scheme settled the interchange amounts.']
  settlement_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the settlement period covered by this reconciliation.']
  settlement_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the settlement period covered by this reconciliation.']
  transaction_count_internal varchar [note: 'The total number of transactions included in the internal interchange calculation for this period.']
  transaction_count_scheme varchar [note: 'The total number of transactions reported by the card scheme for this period.']
  transaction_count_variance varchar [note: 'The difference in transaction counts between internal records and scheme reports.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this reconciliation record was last modified.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The absolute difference between internal calculated interchange and scheme reported interchange (internal minus scheme).']
  variance_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The variance expressed as a percentage of the scheme reported interchange amount.']
  variance_threshold_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The absolute monetary threshold above which variances must be investigated and resolved.']
  variance_threshold_exceeded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the variance amount or percentage exceeds the acceptable tolerance threshold requiring investigation.']
  variance_threshold_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage threshold above which variances must be investigated and resolved.']

  Note: 'Reconciliation record matching internal interchange qualification records against scheme-reported interchange amounts for a given settlement period. Captures settlement period, scheme identifier, internal calculated interchange, scheme-reported interchange, variance amount, variance percentage, reconciliation status (matched, unmatched, disputed), and resolution notes. Critical for financial close and scheme dispute management.'
}

Table interchange.payfac_sub_merchant_pricing {
  payfac_sub_merchant_pricing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the PayFac sub-merchant pricing configuration record.']
  acquiring_portfolio_pricing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.acquiring_portfolio_pricing. Business justification: PayFac sub‑merchant pricing belongs to a portfolio pricing configuration; adds acquiring_portfolio_pricing_id FK']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that approved this pricing configuration for activation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that approved this pricing configuration for activation.']
  payfac_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Payment Facilitator managing this sub-merchant pricing configuration.']
  sub_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sub-merchant onboarded under the PayFac master Merchant Identification Number (MID). This sub-merchant operates under the PayFac umbrella for payment processing.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pricing configuration was approved and authorized for use.']
  base_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The base percentage rate applied to transaction volume under this pricing configuration. For interchange-plus models, this represents the markup over interchange; for flat-rate models, this is the total rate charged.']
  batch_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged per batch settlement submission. Applicable when sub-merchant submits transaction batches for clearing and settlement.']
  card_type_tier varchar [note: 'Specifies which card types this pricing configuration applies to. Enables differential pricing based on card product type (debit, credit, premium, commercial).']
  chargeback_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the sub-merchant for each chargeback dispute processed, covering administrative and operational costs of dispute management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pricing configuration record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all fees and rates are denominated for this pricing configuration.']
  durbin_regulated_pricing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing configuration applies Durbin Amendment regulated debit interchange rates (True) or standard rates (False). Applicable to US debit card transactions from regulated issuers.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this pricing configuration expires or is superseded. Null indicates an open-ended configuration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this pricing configuration becomes active and applicable to the sub-merchant transactions.']
  interchange_pass_through_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether actual interchange costs are passed through to the sub-merchant (True) or absorbed into blended pricing (False). Critical for interchange-plus pricing models.']
  mcc_restriction_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Merchant Category Codes (MCC) to which this pricing applies or is restricted. Used to enforce industry-specific pricing or risk controls.']
  monthly_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly service fee charged to the sub-merchant for account maintenance, platform access, and support services.']
  monthly_minimum_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum monthly fee threshold. If calculated fees fall below this amount, the sub-merchant is charged the minimum to ensure baseline revenue.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special terms, exceptions, or operational notes related to this pricing configuration.']
  per_transaction_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed fee charged per transaction processed for this sub-merchant, regardless of transaction amount. Typically expressed in the settlement currency.']
  pricing_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the legal or commercial agreement governing this pricing configuration. Links pricing to contractual terms.']
  pricing_configuration_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned code or identifier for this specific pricing configuration, used for internal reference and reporting.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'The pricing model applied to the sub-merchant. Flat rate applies a single percentage to all transactions; tiered applies different rates based on card type or volume; interchange-plus (IC-plus) passes through interchange costs plus a markup; blended combines multiple fee components into a single rate; subscription charges a fixed periodic fee; custom represents bespoke pricing arrangements.']
  pricing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing configuration. Draft indicates pending approval; active indicates in use; suspended indicates temporarily inactive; expired indicates past effective end date; terminated indicates permanently closed.']
  pricing_tier_level varchar [note: 'Named tier or level within a tiered pricing structure (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum). Used for volume-based or relationship-based pricing differentiation.']
  refund_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the sub-merchant for processing refund transactions. May be waived or reduced depending on pricing tier.']
  revenue_split_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of net revenue (after interchange and scheme fees) retained by the PayFac. The remainder is the sub-merchant net settlement. Used for internal revenue allocation and partner reporting.']
  risk_reserve_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of transaction volume withheld as a rolling reserve to cover potential chargebacks, refunds, or fraud losses. Released to sub-merchant after a defined holding period.']
  scheme_fee_pass_through_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether card scheme fees (Visa, Mastercard network fees) are passed through to the sub-merchant (True) or included in the pricing (False).']
  settlement_delay_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between transaction authorization and funds settlement to the sub-merchant. Used for risk management and cash flow control.']
  transaction_count_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions allowed per month under this pricing configuration. Used for volume-based pricing tier enforcement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pricing configuration record was last modified.']
  volume_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction volume allowed per month under this pricing configuration. Exceeding this cap may trigger pricing tier changes or require approval.']

  Note: 'Sub-merchant pricing configuration managed by a Payment Facilitator (PayFac) for each sub-merchant onboarded under its master MID. Captures sub-merchant identifier, PayFac identifier, pricing model (flat rate, tiered, IC-plus), applicable rate, per-transaction fee, monthly fee, volume cap, and effective date. Enables PayFac revenue split and interchange cost allocation to sub-merchants.'
}

Table interchange.program {
  program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the interchange program.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Program approval must be signed off by a designated employee; this link supports compliance reporting and program governance.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Program eligibility and rate structures must comply with specific regulatory obligations; linking enables compliance checks and reporting.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'True if enrollment into the program requires manual approval.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the program was approved for activation.']
  card_not_present_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'True if card‑not‑present (e‑commerce) transactions are eligible.']
