  card_present_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'True if card‑present (in‑store) transactions are eligible.']
  card_type_eligibility varchar [note: 'Card types that qualify for the program.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Internal code categorizing the program (e.g., Utility, Merit, Purchasing).']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Regulatory or compliance related observations for the program (e.g., PCI DSS, PSD2).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the program record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'True if transactions crossing national borders qualify for the program.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values in the program.']
  data_quality_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates the confidence level of the program data.']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program is subject to the Durbin Amendment regulations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the program expires or is superseded. Null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the program becomes effective.']
  eligibility_criteria_description varchar [note: 'Detailed rules that determine transaction qualification for the program.']
  enrollment_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual description of the criteria a merchant or issuer must satisfy to enroll in the program.']
  fixed_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Flat fee amount charged per transaction under this program.']
  installment_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'True if installment‑based transactions qualify.']
  mcc_eligibility varchar [note: 'Merchant category codes (MCC) that are eligible for the program. Multiple codes may be stored as a delimited list.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or internal remarks about the program.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'External code used to identify the interchange program in scheme documentation.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the interchange program.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interchange program.']
  qualification_criteria_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing a detailed rule set that defines qualification logic.']
  rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction amount charged as interchange under this program.']
  rate_structure_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the program uses a percentage rate, a fixed fee, or a combination.']
  recurring_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'True if recurring (subscription) transactions qualify.']
  scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network that defines the program.']
  scheme_bulletin_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the scheme bulletin that announced the program.']
  scheme_publication_date date [note: 'Date when the scheme published the program details.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the program record (e.g., Merchant Management System).']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Tier classification that determines rate structure.']
  tokenization_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'True if tokenized card transactions are eligible under the program.']
  transaction_amount_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction amount for eligibility.']
  transaction_amount_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction amount (in program currency) for eligibility.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the program record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the program definition, incremented on each change.']

  Note: 'Card scheme interchange program master defining special interchange programs (e.g., Visa Utility, Mastercard Merit, Amex OptBlue, Visa Purchasing) with their eligibility criteria, qualifying transaction attributes, rate structures, and enrollment requirements. Captures program code, program name, scheme, card type eligibility, MCC eligibility, transaction size thresholds, and program tier levels.'
}

Table interchange.cross_border_fee_rule {
  cross_border_fee_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cross-border fee rule record. Primary key for this entity.']
  acquiring_portfolio_pricing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.acquiring_portfolio_pricing. Business justification: Cross‑border fee rules are defined within a portfolio pricing configuration; adds acquiring_portfolio_pricing_id FK']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, etc.) to which this cross-border fee rule applies.']
  superseded_by_rule_cross_border_fee_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cross-border fee rule that replaces this rule when it is superseded. Supports rule lineage and historical analysis.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'The payment card brand to which this cross-border fee rule applies. Determines which network rules govern the transaction.']
  card_present_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cross-border fee rule applies to card-present transactions (physical POS) or card-not-present transactions (e-commerce, MOTO).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cross-border fee rule record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  cross_border_assessment_rate_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Percentage-based cross-border assessment fee applied to the transaction amount when the issuing country differs from the acquiring country. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 1.25000 for 1.25%).']
  cross_border_fixed_fee_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary fee applied per cross-border transaction, independent of transaction amount. Used in conjunction with or as an alternative to percentage-based fees.']
  currency_conversion_fee_rate_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Percentage-based fee applied when currency conversion occurs in a cross-border transaction. Represents the FX markup over the wholesale exchange rate.']
  dcc_fee_rate_percent decimal(8,5) [note: 'Fee rate applied when Dynamic Currency Conversion is offered at the point of sale, allowing the cardholder to see the transaction amount in their home currency.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the destination country (typically the issuing country or cardholder country of residence).']
  destination_region_code varchar [note: 'Regional grouping code for the destination country used for regional cross-border fee structures and compliance segmentation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this cross-border fee rule is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this cross-border fee rule becomes active and applicable to qualifying transactions. Part of the temporal validity window.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the cross-border fees are denominated and assessed.']
  mcc_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of four-digit MCC codes to which this cross-border fee rule applies or excludes, based on the MCC restriction type.']
  mcc_restriction_type varchar [note: 'Defines how the MCC list is applied: all (applies to all MCCs), include (applies only to listed MCCs), exclude (applies to all except listed MCCs).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional business context, special conditions, or operational notes related to this cross-border fee rule configuration.']
  originating_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the transaction originates (typically the acquiring country or merchant location country).']
  originating_region_code varchar [note: 'Regional grouping code for the originating country (e.g., EEA, NAFTA, APAC) used for regional cross-border fee structures.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple cross-border fee rules match a transaction. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cross-border fee rule is subject to specific regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., PSD2 in EU, Durbin Amendment caps).']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory framework or jurisdiction-specific regulation governing this cross-border fee rule (e.g., PSD2, Regulation II, local central bank rules).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique code identifying this cross-border fee rule configuration. Used for operational reference and configuration management.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the cross-border fee rule for business user identification and reporting purposes.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cross-border fee rule. Controls whether the rule is applied in production fee calculation processes.']
  scheme_bulletin_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the official card scheme bulletin or publication that announced or mandated this cross-border fee rule.']
  scheme_program_code varchar [note: 'Specific card scheme program or product identifier (e.g., Visa Signature, Mastercard World Elite) to which this cross-border fee rule applies.']
  swift_correspondent_fee_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Fixed fee component for SWIFT messaging and correspondent banking services used in cross-border settlement. Applies when international wire transfers are required for settlement.']
  transaction_max_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount threshold for this cross-border fee rule to apply. Transactions above this amount may be subject to different fee structures or caps.']
  transaction_min_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount threshold for this cross-border fee rule to apply. Transactions below this amount may be subject to different fee structures.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment transaction to which this cross-border fee rule applies. Different transaction types may have different cross-border fee structures.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this cross-border fee rule record. Supports change management and audit requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cross-border fee rule record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this cross-border fee rule configuration. Incremented with each modification to support change tracking and audit trails.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this cross-border fee rule record. Supports audit trail and accountability requirements.']

  Note: 'Cross-border and international transaction fee rules defining the additional fees applied when the issuing country differs from the acquiring country. Captures originating country, destination country, card scheme, cross-border assessment rate, currency conversion fee rate, DCC (Dynamic Currency Conversion) fee, SWIFT/correspondent banking fee component, and effective date range. Supports FX cost modeling in cross-border payment flows.'
}

Table interchange.pricing_exception {
  pricing_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pricing exception record. Primary key for the pricing exception entity.']
  acquiring_portfolio_pricing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.acquiring_portfolio_pricing. Business justification: pricing_exception applies to a specific portfolio pricing configuration; adds acquiring_portfolio_pricing_id FK']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who approved this pricing exception. Links to user or employee master record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the partner organization (ISO, PayFac, aggregator) to which this exception applies. Used when exceptions are negotiated at partner level.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who approved this pricing exception. Links to user or employee master record.']
  mdr_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.mdr_config. Business justification: pricing_exception is tied to an MDR configuration; adds mdr_config_id FK']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the merchant receiving the pricing exception. Links to the merchant master record.']
  portfolio_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the merchant portfolio or segment to which this exception applies. Used when exceptions are granted at portfolio level rather than individual merchant level.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the executive or committee that authorized this pricing exception (e.g., Chief Revenue Officer, Pricing Committee, Regional VP).']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this pricing exception was formally approved by the designated authority.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pricing exception automatically renews at the end of its term. True means exception continues unless terminated, false means it expires on effective_end_date.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the business rationale for granting this exception, including competitive factors, strategic value, volume commitments, or relationship considerations.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'The payment card network to which this exception applies. Value \'all\' indicates exception applies across all schemes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visa|mastercard|amex|discover|jcb|unionpay|all — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  card_type varchar [note: 'The card product type to which this exception applies. Value \'all\' indicates exception applies to all card types.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance review of this exception to ensure it meets regulatory requirements, internal policy, and audit standards.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number of the merchant processing agreement, partner contract, or amendment that documents this pricing exception.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this pricing exception record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this pricing exception expires. Null for open-ended exceptions subject to contract termination only.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this pricing exception becomes active and applicable to qualifying transactions.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code identifying this pricing exception for reference in contracts, invoices, and reporting.']
  exception_fixed_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The negotiated per-transaction fixed fee granted under this exception. May be zero for full waiver.']
  exception_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the pricing exception, typically referencing the merchant, partner, or program name.']
  exception_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'The negotiated exception rate granted to the merchant, portfolio, or partner. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.0225 for 2.25%).']
  exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing exception. Draft is under construction, pending_approval awaits authorization, active is in force, suspended is temporarily inactive, expired has passed end date, terminated was cancelled before expiry.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category of pricing exception granted. MDR discount reduces merchant discount rate, MSF waiver eliminates merchant service fees, IRF adjustment modifies interchange reimbursement fee pass-through, volume incentive provides tiered pricing based on transaction volume, promotional is temporary campaign pricing, custom is bespoke arrangement.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all fee amounts in this exception record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum transaction amount for which this exception applies. Null if no maximum threshold.']
  mcc_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of four-digit MCC codes to which this exception applies. Null if mcc_scope is \'all\'.']
  mcc_scope varchar [note: 'Defines whether the exception applies to specific MCC codes, a range of MCCs, or all merchant categories.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum transaction amount required for this exception to apply. Null if no minimum threshold.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional context, special conditions, or operational notes related to this pricing exception.']
  rate_discount_bps varchar [note: 'The discount granted expressed in basis points (1 bps = 0.01%). Calculated as (standard_rate_percent - exception_rate_percent) * 10000. Used for revenue impact analysis.']
  revenue_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the revenue impact estimate.']
  revenue_impact_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated annual revenue impact (reduction) of granting this exception, calculated at time of approval. Used for financial planning and exception portfolio management.']
  standard_fixed_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The standard per-transaction fixed fee (Merchant Service Fee component) that would normally apply before the exception.']
  standard_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'The standard Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) or percentage-based fee that would normally apply before the exception. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.0275 for 2.75%).']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to terminate this pricing exception, as specified in the contract.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'The transaction channel or entry mode to which this exception applies. Value \'all\' indicates exception applies to all transaction types.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this pricing exception record was last modified.']
  volume_tier_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the volume tier threshold.']
  volume_tier_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum monthly or annual transaction volume required to qualify for this exception. Used for volume-based incentive pricing.']

  Note: 'Records negotiated pricing exceptions and custom interchange arrangements granted to specific merchants, portfolios, or partners that deviate from standard MDR or MSF schedules. Captures exception type, merchant or portfolio identifier, standard rate, exception rate, basis point discount, approval authority, effective date, expiry date, and business justification. Supports audit and contract compliance tracking.'
}

Table interchange.volume_tier_threshold {
  volume_tier_threshold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the volume tier threshold configuration record.']
  acquirer_id varchar [note: 'Acquiring bank or payment service provider identifier for acquirer-specific tier configurations. Null indicates tier applies across all acquirers.']
  acquiring_portfolio_pricing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.acquiring_portfolio_pricing. Business justification: Volume tier thresholds are part of a portfolio pricing configuration; adds acquiring_portfolio_pricing_id FK']
  issuer_id varchar [note: 'Issuing bank identifier for issuer-specific tier configurations. Null indicates tier applies across all issuers.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Card scheme or payment network identifier (e.g., VISA, MASTERCARD, AMEX, DISCOVER) to which this tier applies. Null indicates tier applies across all schemes.']
  superseded_by_tier_volume_tier_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the volume_tier_threshold_id that replaces this configuration when a new version is published. Null if this is the current active version.']
  applicable_fixed_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed fee amount per transaction that applies when this tier threshold is met.']
  applicable_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'Interchange rate percentage or MDR percentage that applies when this tier threshold is met. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.0250 for 2.50%).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this tier configuration before it becomes effective.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who approved this tier configuration for production use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tier configuration was approved.']
  card_present_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier applies to card-present transactions (True), card-not-present transactions (False), or both (Null).']
  card_product_tier varchar [note: 'Card product tier or brand level (e.g., standard, gold, platinum, signature, infinite, world elite). Null indicates tier applies to all product tiers.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment card this tier applies to (debit, credit, prepaid, commercial). Null indicates tier applies to all card types.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the geographic market this tier applies to. Null indicates tier applies globally.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this volume tier threshold record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier applies to cross-border transactions (True), domestic transactions (False), or both (Null).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary thresholds (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null for non-monetary thresholds.']
  discount_rate_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'Discount percentage applied to standard rates when merchant qualifies for this volume tier. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.0050 for 0.50% discount).']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier applies to Durbin Amendment regulated debit transactions (True), non-regulated transactions (False), or both (Null).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this volume tier threshold configuration expires and is no longer applied. Null indicates no expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this volume tier threshold configuration becomes effective and begins applying to merchant or issuer volume calculations.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Type of fee structure this tier applies to: IRF (Interchange Reimbursement Fee), MDR (Merchant Discount Rate), MSF (Merchant Service Fee), scheme fee, or assessment fee.']
  lower_bound decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum volume threshold value required to qualify for this tier (inclusive). Null indicates no lower limit.']
  measurement_period varchar [note: 'Time period over which volume is measured to determine tier qualification (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annual, rolling 30 days). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|annual|rolling_30_days|rolling_90_days — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the threshold bounds (count for transaction volume, currency_amount for TPV, percentage for rate-based thresholds).']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four-digit MCC that this tier applies to. Null indicates tier applies across all MCCs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, business rationale, or special instructions for this volume tier threshold configuration.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple tier configurations could apply to the same transaction. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rebate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed rebate amount provided to merchant or issuer when this volume tier is achieved during the measurement period.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Regional market identifier for multi-country tier configurations (e.g., EMEA, APAC, LATAM). Null indicates no regional restriction.']
  threshold_type varchar [note: 'Type of volume measurement used to determine tier qualification (transaction count, TPV amount, monthly volume, annual volume, or quarterly volume).']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the volume tier (e.g., TIER_1, TIER_GOLD, TIER_PLATINUM).']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of the tier within the pricing structure, where lower numbers typically represent entry-level tiers and higher numbers represent premium tiers.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the volume tier (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum).']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the volume tier threshold configuration.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction this tier applies to (purchase, refund, cash advance, balance inquiry). Null indicates tier applies to all transaction types.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this volume tier threshold record was last modified.']
  upper_bound decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum volume threshold value for this tier (exclusive). Null indicates no upper limit.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this tier configuration, incremented with each modification to support change tracking and audit requirements.']

  Note: 'Volume tier threshold configuration defining the transaction volume or TPV (Total Payment Volume) bands that trigger tiered interchange rates, MDR discounts, or scheme fee rebates. Captures tier level, lower volume bound, upper volume bound, applicable rate or discount, fee type, scheme or acquirer identifier, and effective date. Enables dynamic pricing adjustments based on merchant volume performance.'
}

Table interchange.interchange_dispute {
  interchange_dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interchange dispute record. Primary key for the interchange dispute entity.']
  billing_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the interchange billing period that is the subject of the dispute, if the dispute pertains to aggregated billing rather than a single transaction.']
  compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_compliance_case. Business justification: Interchange disputes are investigated as compliance cases; linking provides case tracking, resolution status, and auditability.']
  disputed_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific transaction that is the subject of the interchange dispute, if the dispute pertains to a single transaction.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the acquiring bank or merchant acquirer involved in the interchange dispute.']
  issuing_bank_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the issuing bank or card issuer involved in the interchange dispute.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant associated with the disputed transaction or billing period.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the card scheme entity involved in the interchange dispute.']
  scheme_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_invoice. Business justification: Interchange disputes are linked to the underlying scheme invoice; adds scheme_invoice_id FK']
  arn varchar [note: 'Unique acquirer reference number for the disputed transaction, used for tracing and reconciliation across the payment network.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by the interchange dispute, applicable when the dispute relates to aggregated billing.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by the interchange dispute, applicable when the dispute relates to aggregated billing.']
  bin varchar [note: 'The first 6-8 digits of the card number (BIN or IIN) associated with the disputed transaction, used for interchange qualification.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network brand or card scheme involved in the interchange dispute.']
  claimed_interchange_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Interchange fee amount claimed by the disputing party as the correct amount that should have been applied.']
  claimed_irf_category_code varchar [note: 'The interchange rate category code that the disputing party claims should have been applied to the transaction or billing period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the interchange dispute record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the disputed transaction involved cross-border processing, which affects interchange qualification.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in the interchange dispute.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interchange dispute in the resolution workflow.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interchange dispute based on the nature of the disagreement.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The difference between the claimed and scheme-reported interchange amounts, representing the financial value in dispute.']
  durbin_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the disputed transaction or billing period is subject to Durbin Amendment interchange fee caps for regulated debit cards.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation level of the interchange dispute within the card scheme dispute resolution hierarchy.']
  filing_date date [note: 'Date when the interchange dispute was formally filed with the card scheme or counterparty.']
  filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the interchange dispute was submitted into the dispute management system.']
  financial_adjustment_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The final financial adjustment amount resulting from the dispute resolution, which may be positive (credit) or negative (debit).']
  initiator varchar [note: 'Identifies which party initiated the interchange dispute (acquirer, issuer, payment platform, or card scheme).']
  mcc varchar [note: 'Four-digit merchant category code associated with the disputed transaction, relevant for interchange qualification rules.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or internal observations related to the interchange dispute.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific reason for the interchange dispute, aligned with card scheme dispute reason taxonomies.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual explanation of the reason for the interchange dispute, providing context beyond the reason code.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the interchange dispute, used for communication with card schemes, acquirers, and issuers.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the interchange dispute was resolved or closed, marking the end of the dispute lifecycle.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the interchange dispute resolution process, indicating whether the dispute was upheld, denied, or partially resolved.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the interchange dispute resolution was recorded in the system.']
  respondent varchar [note: 'Identifies which party is responding to or defending against the interchange dispute.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the respondent must provide a response to the interchange dispute, per card scheme rules.']
  scheme_applied_irf_category_code varchar [note: 'The interchange rate category code that was actually applied by the card scheme, which is being disputed.']
  scheme_case_number varchar [note: 'Case or dispute tracking number assigned by the card scheme for this interchange dispute.']
  scheme_reported_interchange_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Interchange fee amount reported or calculated by the card scheme, which is being disputed.']
  supporting_documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or location of supporting documentation, evidence, or attachments related to the interchange dispute.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the interchange dispute record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Interchange-specific dispute record tracking disagreements between acquirers and issuers (or between the platform and card schemes) over interchange fee amounts, rate category misapplication, or downgrade decisions. Captures dispute reference, disputed transaction or billing period, claimed interchange amount, scheme-reported amount, dispute reason code, filing date, resolution date, resolution outcome, and financial adjustment amount.'
}

Table interchange.program_fee_schedule_assignment {
  program_fee_schedule_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ProgramFeeScheduleAssignment association']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx_fee_schedule']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange_program']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this mapping expires (null if open‑ended)']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this program‑fee‑schedule mapping becomes active']
  fee_type_override varchar [note: 'Specific fee type override for this program‑schedule pair, if any']

  Note: 'This association captures the mapping between an interchange program and an FX fee schedule, including the period the mapping is effective and any fee‑type overrides specific to that combination.. Existence Justification: An interchange program can be linked to multiple FX fee schedules, and a single FX fee schedule can apply to multiple interchange programs. The business actively manages this mapping, capturing effective dates and optional fee‑type overrides for each program‑schedule pair.'
}

Table interchange.pricing_tier {
  pricing_tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pricing_tier']
  fallback_pricing_tier_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pricing_tier (fallback_pricing_tier_id)']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the tier is applicable.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Card network to which the tier rules are applicable.']
  compliance_tier varchar [note: 'Indicates Durbin Amendment compliance classification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pricing tier record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the tier\'s purpose and rules.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the pricing tier becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the pricing tier expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage component of the interchange fee for this tier.']
  flat_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary component of the fee for this tier.']
  interchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interchange rate for reporting and compliance.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier is the default for new merchants.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction amount for which the tier applies.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code representing the merchant\'s business type.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction amount for which the tier applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or operational notes.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Model used to calculate fees (percentage, flat, or hybrid).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing tier.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surcharge is applied under this tier.']
  surcharge_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage surcharge applied when surcharge_applicable is true.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the pricing tier in external systems.']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating the hierarchy of the tier (e.g., 1 = highest).']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing tier.']
  tier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tier such as volume‑based, flat‑fee, or hybrid.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of transaction the tier applies to.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the pricing tier record.']
  volume_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Transaction volume amount that triggers this tier.']
  volume_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the volume threshold.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pricing_tier. Referenced by pricing_tier_id.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_case {
  fraud_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the fraud case record.']
  assigned_investigator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst or investigator assigned to the case.']
  billing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.interchange_billing. Business justification: Billing adjustments for chargebacks require linking each fraud case to the billing period record that captured the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder linked to the suspicious activity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Internal cost allocation of fraud investigation resources is tracked via cost centers for budgeting and performance reporting.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Case loss amounts are reported in a specific currency; linking to reference.currency ensures consistent currency metadata.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device fingerprint record linked to the event.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Link needed for chargeback reconciliation reports that map each confirmed fraud case to its dispute case.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partner Fraud Risk Dashboard aggregates fraud cases per acquiring partner; linking case to ecosystem_partner enables required reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst or investigator assigned to the case.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule or model that generated the alert.']
  fraud_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_type. Business justification: fraud_case needs a classification of the case type; linking to fraud_type provides hierarchy and risk weighting.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Regulatory filing and jurisdictional reporting of fraud cases require linking each case to the responsible legal entity.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant associated with the case.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Required for regulatory fraud reporting and risk scoring that depend on the specific payment product (card, BNPL, A2A) used in the transaction.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'FK to transaction.payment_txn.payment_txn_id — MUST-HAVE: Enables linking a fraud case to its triggering transaction — essential for fraud investigation, loss calculation, and network reporting.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.pos_terminal. Business justification: Required for fraud case investigation reports that must identify the POS terminal where the suspicious transaction originated.']
  qualification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.interchange_qualification. Business justification: Fraud loss reporting needs the original IRF rate applied to the fraudulent transaction to calculate lost interchange revenue.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory report associated with the case.']
  regulatory_report_regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory report associated with the case.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Investigation reports require linking each fraud case to the original gateway request that initiated the disputed transaction.']
  response_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_response. Business justification: Fraud case analysis needs the gateway response details (auth code, decline reason) to verify outcome and support regulatory reporting.']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Risk assessment reports require linking each fraud case to the party\'s risk profile to correlate fraud outcomes with risk scores.']
  routing_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.routing_decision. Business justification: Case reviews often examine the routing decision that selected the acquirer to understand risk exposure.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Regulatory loss and risk reporting are often aggregated by card scheme; associating each fraud case with its scheme enables accurate reporting.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the fraud alert.']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.wallet_transaction. Business justification: Required for fraud case investigations to drill down to the exact wallet transaction that triggered the case, enabling detailed audit and remediation.']
  watchlist_fraud_watchlist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_watchlist. Business justification: fraud_case may reference a watchlist entry that triggered the case; linking to fraud_watchlist enables traceability.']
  aml_alert boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case triggered an AML screening alert.']
  blocklist_hit boolean [note: 'True if the entity matched an internal or external blocklist.']
  blocklist_name varchar [note: 'Name of the blocklist that produced the hit.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable case number used in investigations and reporting.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the fraud case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Category of fraud scenario prompting the case.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the suspicious transaction originated.']
  chargeback_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the chargeback associated with the case.']
  chargeback_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the chargeback amount.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case reached a terminal state (e.g., resolved, rejected).']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the regulatory compliance review for the case.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the fraud case record was first created in the system.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'System or channel that initially flagged the suspicious activity.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the suspicious event was detected.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the case was escalated.']
  exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary loss if fraud is successful.']
  fraud_category varchar [note: 'High‑level fraud taxonomy for the case.']
  investigation_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the investigation was concluded.']
  investigation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the formal investigation of the case began.']
  is_blocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the associated account or card was blocked pending investigation.']
  is_chargeback_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a chargeback was filed related to the case.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'True when the case has been escalated to senior investigators or external teams.']
  is_fraudulent boolean [note: 'True if the case was concluded as confirmed fraud.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case is flagged as high risk for immediate action.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Analyst free‑form notes documenting investigation details.']
  payment_instrument_token varchar [note: 'Token representing the payment credential used in the transaction.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Operational priority assigned to the case for handling.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount successfully recovered from fraudster or merchant.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome after case investigation.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score generated by scoring models (0‑100).']
  sar_filed boolean [note: 'True if a Suspicious Activity Report was filed for this case.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined severity rating indicating potential impact.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent time the case record was modified.']

  Note: 'Core operational record representing a fraud investigation lifecycle from initial detection through remediation. Tracks case type (card-not-present, account takeover, synthetic identity, first-party fraud, etc.), case status, assigned investigator, detection source, financial exposure, recovery amount, and resolution outcome. SSOT for fraud case management in the Fraud Detection and Prevention Platform.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_alert {
  fraud_alert_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the fraud alert record.']
  assigned_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst or user currently handling the alert.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder (primary account holder).']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder (primary account holder).']
  compliance_aml_screening_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_aml_screening_result. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requires attaching AML screening results to each fraud alert for audit trails and SAR/STR filing.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Geo country code drives jurisdictional AML checks; FK to reference.country provides authoritative country data.']
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.cross_border_payment. Business justification: Real‑time fraud alerts for cross‑border payments need the payment ID to fetch transaction details for immediate response.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Transaction currency must map to reference.currency for regulatory reporting and FX conversion thresholds.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device fingerprint record associated with the transaction.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the downstream fraud case linked to this alert.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Regulatory and SLA alerts are sent to the partner owning the merchant; linking alerts to partner supports AML/Fraud notification workflow.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst or user currently handling the alert.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_endpoint. Business justification: Forensic analysis of alerts needs the exact network endpoint that processed the transaction, supporting root‑cause investigations.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule that triggered the alert, if rule‑based.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: MCC code is used for fraud risk scoring and reporting; linking to reference.mcc enables standardized risk categories.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant involved in the transaction.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ML model that produced the fraud score, if applicable.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Alerts must reference the payment product to apply product‑specific risk thresholds and generate compliance reports.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.pos_terminal. Business justification: Real‑time fraud alerts need terminal context to apply device‑specific risk rules and for audit trails in compliance reporting.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Alert triage uses the exact gateway request payload to reproduce the event and assess fraud indicators.']
  response_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_response. Business justification: Linking alerts to the gateway response enables auditors to see the response code and fraud flag that triggered the alert.']
  sanctions_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sanctions_check. Business justification: Sanctions screening is executed as part of fraud alert processing; linking records the check result for regulatory filing.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the transaction that triggered the alert.']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.wallet_transaction. Business justification: Alerts generated from wallet monitoring need a direct link to the wallet transaction for analysts to view transaction context and take action.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Category of the alert indicating the source logic (rule, machine‑learning score, velocity control, etc.).']
  auth_response_code varchar [note: 'ISO‑8583 response code returned by the issuer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 00|05|12|13|14|15|30|41|43|51|54|55|57|58|62|63|65|75|76|77|78|79|80|81|82|83|84|85|86|87|88|89|90|91|92|93|94|95|96|97|98|99 — 42 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  channel varchar [note: 'Digital or physical channel through which the transaction was initiated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert record was first created in the data lake.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the issuer for a declined transaction, if applicable.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fraud alert was generated by the detection engine.']
  fraud_alert_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the alert within the case management workflow.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City name of the transaction source location, if available.']
  geo_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the transaction source.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the transaction originated.']
  is_fraudulent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the transaction is confirmed as fraudulent after review.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the ML model used for scoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes added by analysts during investigation.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used for the transaction (e.g., card, bank transfer).']
  payment_network varchar [note: 'Card scheme or network that processed the transaction.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Recommended action taken by the system or analyst in response to the alert.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rule that caused the alert.']
  score_threshold decimal(10,4) [note: 'Configured threshold that the triggered_score must exceed to raise an alert.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined severity indicating potential impact of the alert.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that emitted the alert (e.g., gateway, fraud_engine).']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction in the transaction currency.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the underlying transaction occurred.']
  triggered_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric risk score generated by the detection engine.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the alert record.']

  Note: 'Real-time fraud alert generated by the fraud detection engine when a transaction or account activity breaches a scoring threshold or rule condition. Captures alert type, severity level, triggering rule or model identifier, alert status (open, reviewed, escalated, closed), and the associated transaction or account reference. Feeds the case management workflow.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_rule {
  fraud_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the fraud rule.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal user who originally created the rule.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: FX‑specific fraud rules are defined per currency pair; the rule must reference the pair for correct applicability.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal user responsible for the rule\'s lifecycle and maintenance.']
  fraud_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_channel. Business justification: fraud_rule applies to specific channels; a FK to fraud_channel replaces the free‑text channel_applicability and enforces valid channel references.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Rules are defined per payment product to enforce product‑specific controls and satisfy product‑level compliance policies.']
  primary_fraud_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal user responsible for the rule\'s lifecycle and maintenance.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each fraud rule is derived from a specific regulatory obligation; linking enables compliance impact analysis and rule audits.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Payment schemes have distinct fraud rule sets; linking rules to scheme_id allows scheme‑specific rule activation and compliance reporting.']
  tertiary_fraud_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal user who performed the latest update.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal user who performed the latest update.']
  wallet_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.wallet_scheme. Business justification: Fraud rules are often scoped to specific card schemes; associating a rule with a wallet scheme supports rule management and regulatory reporting.']
  action varchar [note: 'Action taken when the rule condition evaluates to true.']
  condition_expression varchar [note: 'Executable logical expression evaluated by the rule engine (e.g., DSL or SQL‑like syntax).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the rule becomes active in production.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the rule is no longer applied (null for open‑ended).']
  fraud_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  is_test_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is deployed in a test environment only.']
  max_decline_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit for automatic decline actions.']
  max_decline_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the max decline amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional comments or operational guidance.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple rules match (lower = higher priority).']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum fraud risk score required for the rule to fire.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business-friendly short code used to reference the rule in operational dashboards and logs.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule\'s purpose, scope and business rationale.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fraud detection rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rule defining its evaluation logic.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Business impact rating of the rule when triggered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']
  velocity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions allowed within the velocity window.']
  velocity_window_seconds varchar [note: 'Time window over which transaction count is evaluated for velocity rules.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version of the rule definition.']

  Note: 'Configuration record for a fraud detection rule deployed in the rule engine. Defines rule name, rule type (velocity, threshold, pattern, blocklist), condition logic expression, action (decline, flag, challenge, review), effective date range, channel applicability (POS, CNP, ATM, digital wallet), and rule owner. Supports versioned rule lifecycle management.'
}

Table fraud.rule_version {
  rule_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each version of a fraud rule configuration.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that initially created the rule version record.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent fraud rule to which this version belongs.']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_fee_schedule. Business justification: Certain fraud rule versions need to reference the applicable FX fee schedule for cost‑based risk calculations.']
  rate_margin_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate_margin_config. Business justification: Rule versions may depend on margin configuration parameters that affect FX pricing and fraud thresholds.']
  action_parameters varchar [note: 'Serialized parameters that define actions taken when the rule fires (e.g., block, challenge, alert).']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule version became effective for evaluation.']
  applies_to_payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument categories that the rule evaluates.']
  applies_to_region varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the geographic region where the rule is active.']
  applies_to_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Transaction event types to which the rule is applied.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the analyst who approved this rule version.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule version received formal approval.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual list of regulatory or internal compliance controls that this rule satisfies (e.g., PCI DSS, AML, PSD2).']
  condition_logic varchar [note: 'Serialized representation (e.g., JSON or DSL) of the rule\'s conditional expressions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when this rule version record was first created in the system.']
  deactivation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule version was retired or superseded (nullable if still active).']
  is_fallback_rule boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this rule serves as a fallback when no other rule matches.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the most recent update.']
  max_amount_per_day decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary ceiling for cumulative transaction amount in a day that, if exceeded, triggers the rule.']
  max_transactions_per_minute varchar [note: 'Velocity control limit for the number of transactions allowed per minute before the rule triggers.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold that the computed risk score must exceed to trigger the rule.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fraud rule.']
  rule_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the rule definition (built in‑house, supplied by a partner, or mandated by regulation).']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rule logic (e.g., velocity control, device fingerprint, 3‑DS outcome, behavioral analytics, machine‑learning model, or custom).']
  rule_version_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the rule purpose and scope.']
  rule_version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rule version.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the risk score threshold (points or percent).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to this rule version record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the rule configuration.']

  Note: 'Versioned history of fraud rule configurations, capturing each published version of a fraud rule with its full condition logic, action parameters, activation timestamp, deactivation timestamp, and the analyst who approved the version. Enables audit trail and rollback capability for rule changes.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_velocity_control {
  fraud_velocity_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the velocity control configuration.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Velocity controls often vary by currency pair, requiring a FK to identify the pair for each control.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the control.']
  notification_template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the template used for alerts generated by this control.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Velocity controls vary by product type; linking enables enforcement of product‑specific transaction limits.']
  primary_fraud_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the control.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who last modified the control.']
  applicable_channels varchar [note: 'Payment channels to which the control applies.']
  applicable_payment_methods varchar [note: 'Payment instrument types covered by the control.']
  breach_action varchar [note: 'Action taken when the threshold is exceeded: block transaction, flag for review, invoke step‑up authentication, or generate an alert.']
  breach_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times the control has been breached since activation.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates regulatory compliance relevance of the control.']
  control_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable unique code used to reference the control in operational interfaces.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the velocity control rule.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the measurement type the control evaluates: transaction count, monetary amount, distinct merchant count, or distinct device count.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was first created.']
  effective_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the control expires; null for indefinite.']
  effective_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the control becomes effective.']
  fraud_velocity_control_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the control\'s purpose and business rationale.']
  fraud_velocity_control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the velocity control.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control applies globally across all jurisdictions (true) or is jurisdiction‑specific (false).']
  jurisdiction_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating the regulatory jurisdiction for the control.']
  last_breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent occurrence where the control was breached.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Relative priority (1‑10) used when multiple controls conflict; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  risk_score_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weight applied to the control\'s outcome when calculating an overall fraud risk score.']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the control definition (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  scope_entity varchar [note: 'Entity to which the velocity limit applies (e.g., individual cardholder, merchant, BIN, device, IP address, or channel).']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the threshold value: number of transactions or monetary currency.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric limit that triggers the breach action; interpreted according to threshold_unit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']
  window_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of the rolling time window over which the control is evaluated, expressed in seconds.']

  Note: 'Velocity control configuration defining transaction frequency and volume limits applied per cardholder, merchant, BIN, or device within a rolling time window. Captures control type (count, amount, unique merchant), window duration, threshold value, breach action (block, flag, step-up auth), and applicability scope. Core fraud prevention mechanism for CNP and POS channels.'
}

Table fraud.velocity_breach {
  velocity_breach_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the velocity control breach event.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Breach amount currency must be tied to reference.currency for loss reporting and risk aggregation.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device fingerprint used in risk assessment.']
  fraud_velocity_control_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the velocity control rule that was breached.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: MCC drives velocity thresholds; FK to reference.mcc provides standardized category data.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual transaction that caused the breach.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'Automated or manual response applied after the breach (e.g., block, flag, challenge).']
  action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the response action was executed.']
  bin_number varchar [note: 'First six digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank.']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breach event.']
  breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the velocity breach was detected.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Origin channel of the transaction that triggered the breach.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates if the breach has regulatory or internal compliance implications.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the breached velocity control.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Category of the control – e.g., transaction count, monetary amount, frequency, or custom value.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach record was first persisted.']
  detection_engine varchar [note: 'Engine that evaluated the transaction – real‑time rule engine, batch processor, or ML model.']
  entity_identifier varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the entity such as masked PAN (last 4), MID, or device token.']
  entity_reference varchar [note: 'System identifier of the entity (e.g., cardholder_id, merchant_id) that caused the breach.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity that triggered the breach (cardholder, merchant, device, BIN, or account).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address observed for the transaction that triggered the breach.']
  is_fraudulent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was classified as fraudulent.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the breach originated or is subject to regulation.']
  observed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual count or monetary amount observed that exceeded the threshold.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score associated with the transaction at detection time.']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the velocity control rule at the time of breach.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system that generated the breach record.']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the threshold – either a simple count or a currency amount.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Configured limit for the control (count or amount) that should not be exceeded.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction that caused the breach.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the breach record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or client user‑agent string associated with the transaction.']
  velocity_breach_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing the context or rationale of the breach.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a velocity control breach event, capturing which velocity control was breached, the entity (cardholder, merchant, device, BIN) that triggered the breach, the observed count or amount versus the configured threshold, the breach timestamp, and the resulting action taken (block, flag, challenge). Feeds fraud alert generation.'
}

Table fraud.device_fingerprint {
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the device fingerprint record.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Device geo country is used in fraud detection models; linking to reference.country standardizes country risk scores.']
  app_version varchar [note: 'Version of the mobile or desktop application used.']
  blocklist_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device is present on an internal or external blocklist.']
  blocklist_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source list that flagged the device (e.g., internal, external vendor).']
  browser_name varchar [note: 'Name of the web browser or embedded web view.']
  browser_version varchar [note: 'Version of the browser.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the device fingerprint record was created in the data store.']
  device_fingerprint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the device fingerprint record.']
  device_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label or nickname assigned to the device for operational reference.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of the device used in the transaction.']
  fingerprint_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash representing the unique digital fingerprint of the device.']
  first_seen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the device fingerprint was first observed in the system.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City name derived from geolocation.']
  hce_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports Host Card Emulation.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address observed at the time of device interaction.']
  last_seen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent observation of the device fingerprint.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the device location.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the device location.']
  nfc_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device has Near Field Communication capability.']
  os_name varchar [note: 'Name of the operating system running on the device.']
  os_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the operating system.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Categorical risk classification derived from the risk score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score assigned to the device based on fraud models.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the device fingerprint record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Full user‑agent string captured from the device request.']

  Note: 'Device fingerprint record capturing the unique digital identity of a device used in a payment or authentication event. Stores device fingerprint hash, device type (mobile, desktop, POS terminal, mPOS), OS, browser or app version, IP address, geolocation, HCE flag, NFC capability, first-seen and last-seen timestamps, and risk classification. Used for device-based fraud detection and account takeover prevention.'
}

Table fraud.auth_3ds_result {
  auth_3ds_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each 3-D Secure authentication outcome record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: 3‑DS authentication records need currency context for compliance and settlement reporting.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.device_fingerprint. Business justification: auth_3ds_result stores device fingerprint as a string; linking to device_fingerprint table normalizes device data and removes redundant column.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant involved in the transaction.']
  primary_auth_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction to which this 3DS result belongs.']
  tertiary_auth_acs_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the Access Control Server (ACS) for this authentication flow.']
  acquirer_bin varchar [note: 'BIN of the acquiring bank that processed the transaction.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction associated with this 3DS authentication.']
  authentication_attempts varchar [note: 'Count of how many authentication attempts were made for this transaction.']
  authentication_error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error code returned when authentication fails.']
  authentication_error_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable error message describing the authentication failure.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used for authentication: frictionless, challenge, or fallback.']
  authentication_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the 3-D Secure authentication attempt.']
  authentication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the 3-D Secure authentication event occurred.']
  authentication_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cryptographic authentication value returned by the ACS (CAVV for 3DS 1.x, AAV for 3DS 2.x).']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network brand of the card used (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  cardholder_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the cardholder at the time of authentication.']
  challenge_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a challenge was required, requested, or not needed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the authentication outcome meets internal and external compliance standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this 3DS result record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  eci_indicator varchar [note: 'ECI code indicating the level of liability shift and authentication strength.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned by the fraud engine for this authentication event.']
  is_successful boolean [note: 'True if the authentication resulted in a successful liability shift.']
  issuer_bin varchar [note: 'BIN of the issuing bank that issued the card.']
  liability_shift boolean [note: 'True if liability for the transaction shifted to the issuer per 3DS outcome.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or analyst comments.']
  processing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time elapsed between authentication request and final response, measured in milliseconds.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if this authentication must be included in regulatory reporting (e.g., AML, PCI).']
  risk_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision based on fraud score and business rules.']
  sca_exemption_type varchar [note: 'Type of SCA exemption applied to the transaction, if any.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the 3DS result (e.g., gateway, digital_wallet).']
  three_ds_version varchar [note: 'Version of the 3-D Secure protocol used for the authentication.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']

  Note: '3-D Secure authentication outcome record for a payment transaction. Captures 3DS version (1.0, 2.1, 2.2), authentication status (authenticated, attempted, failed, not enrolled), ECI indicator, authentication value (CAVV/AAV), DS transaction ID, ACS transaction ID, challenge indicator, SCA exemption type applied, and authentication timestamp. SSOT for 3DS outcomes in the fraud domain.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_sca_challenge {
  fraud_sca_challenge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SCA challenge record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (customer) subject to the SCA challenge.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (customer) subject to the SCA challenge.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: SCA challenge amount currency is required for risk scoring and cross‑border regulation.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.device_fingerprint. Business justification: fraud_sca_challenge stores device fingerprint as a string; linking to device_fingerprint table normalizes device data and removes redundant column.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud rule that triggered the SCA challenge.']
  gateway3ds_authentication_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway3ds_authentication. Business justification: SCA challenge records must be tied to the corresponding 3‑DS authentication session for full compliance audit.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant involved in the transaction.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal that initiated the transaction.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction that triggered the SCA challenge.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the underlying transaction.']
  auth_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code returned by the issuer after successful challenge.']
  challenge_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the attempt for the same transaction.']
  challenge_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the SCA challenge: passed, failed, or abandoned by the cardholder.']
  challenge_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in milliseconds between initiation and completion of the challenge.']
  challenge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the challenge.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SCA challenge was completed (success, failure, or abandonment).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SCA challenge record was first created in the data lake.']
  exemption_type varchar [note: 'PSD2 exemption applied to the transaction, if any.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of why the challenge failed, if applicable.']
  idempotency_key varchar [note: 'Unique key to ensure the challenge request is processed exactly once.']
  initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SCA challenge was presented to the cardholder.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address of the device that performed the SCA challenge.']
  is_challenge_abandoned boolean [note: 'True when the cardholder abandoned the challenge.']
  is_challenge_blocked boolean [note: 'True when the challenge was blocked by policy or system error.']
  is_challenge_exempted boolean [note: 'True when an SCA exemption was applied and no challenge was required.']
