  is_challenge_successful boolean [note: 'True when the challenge outcome is passed.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s business type.']
  payment_instrument_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment instrument used for the transaction.']
  regulatory_context varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the challenge, e.g., PSD2.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned by the fraud detection engine prior to the challenge.']
  sca_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the SCA challenge (One‑Time Password, biometric, push notification, knowledge‑based).']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the SCA challenge was presented (web, mobile app, POS terminal, API).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SCA challenge record.']

  Note: 'Strong Customer Authentication challenge record capturing the SCA method used (OTP, biometric, push notification, knowledge-based), challenge outcome (passed, failed, abandoned), SCA exemption applied (low-value, TRA, trusted beneficiary, recurring), PSD2 regulatory context, challenge initiation timestamp, and completion timestamp. Supports PSD2 SCA compliance reporting.'
}

Table fraud.blocklist_entry {
  blocklist_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each blocklist record.']
  related_fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud case that triggered this blocklist entry, if applicable.']
  block_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the entity was placed on the blocklist.']
  blocking_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the block action (e.g., decline transaction, flag for review, step‑up authentication, monitor only).']
  blocklist_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the blocklist record.']
  blocklist_version varchar [note: 'Version number for the blocklist record to support change tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the blocklist entry was initially created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the block becomes active.']
  entity_identifier varchar [note: 'Tokenized or hashed value that uniquely identifies the blocked entity within the system.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity being blocked (e.g., PAN, BIN, IP address, email, phone, merchant MID).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the block expires or is scheduled to be removed (nullable for indefinite blocks).']
  is_global boolean [note: 'True if the block applies across all jurisdictions; false if limited to a specific jurisdiction.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the jurisdiction where the block applies.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent periodic review of the blocklist entry.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or comments about the block.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest review (e.g., reviewed, not reviewed, escalated).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned to the entity at the time of blocklist entry (higher indicates greater risk).']
  source_of_addition varchar [note: 'Origin of the blocklist entry (e.g., manual entry, automated fraud rule, network advisory).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the blocklist entry.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the blocklist entry.']

  Note: 'Blocklist record for an entity (PAN, BIN, device fingerprint, IP address, email, phone, merchant MID, IBAN) that has been flagged for blocking or enhanced scrutiny. Captures entity type, entity identifier (tokenized/hashed), blocklist reason code, source of addition (manual, automated rule, network advisory), effective date, expiry date, and blocking scope (decline, flag, step-up). SSOT for fraud blocklist management.'
}

Table fraud.blocklist_audit {
  blocklist_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each audit log entry of a blocklist record.']
  blocklist_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the blocklist entry whose lifecycle event is being recorded.']
  related_case_fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud case linked to this blocklist event, if any.']
  related_fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud case linked to this blocklist event, if any.']
  blocklist_type varchar [note: 'Category of the value being blocked (e.g., IP address, PAN, device identifier, token, email, phone).']
  blocklist_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual value placed on the blocklist (e.g., PAN, IP address, token).']
  comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments added by the performer.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards driver for the blocklist action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was first created in the data store.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'System error code returned if the blocklist action failed.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable error description for a failed blocklist operation.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the blocklist lifecycle action occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of audit event: addition, modification, removal, or expiry of a blocklist entry.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the blocklist entry is set to expire, if applicable.']
  is_successful boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the blocklist operation completed successfully.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why the blocklist action was taken, required for regulatory audit.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Status of the blocklist entry after the event.']
  performed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the analyst, system, or automated process that executed the action.']
  performed_by_role varchar [note: 'Role of the actor that performed the blocklist action.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Status of the blocklist entry before the event.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code representing the geographic region associated with the blocklist value.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from the risk score (low, medium, high).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score assigned to the blocklist entry at the time of the event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the blocklist event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this audit record.']

  Note: 'Audit trail for blocklist entry lifecycle events — additions, modifications, removals, and expiry actions. Captures the previous and new state of the blocklist entry, the action type, the analyst or automated process that performed the action, and the business justification. Supports regulatory audit and PCI DSS compliance requirements.'
}

Table fraud.score_event {
  score_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Globally unique identifier for the fraud scoring event record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (or account holder) involved in the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (or account holder) involved in the transaction.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Score events reference transaction currency; FK to reference.currency enables unified currency analytics.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device fingerprint record associated with the transaction.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the transaction.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Score events need product context for analytics and to trigger product‑specific actions.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Fraud scoring models incorporate the FX rate at transaction time to assess risk of currency conversion anomalies.']
  risk_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_model. Business justification: Fraud scoring uses a specific risk model; linking enables audit of model version per score event for compliance.']
  rule_set_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule set configuration applied during scoring.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the fraud scoring.']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.wallet_transaction. Business justification: Fraud scoring engine processes wallet transactions; linking score events to the wallet transaction provides traceability for compliance audits.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Digital or physical channel through which the transaction originated.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the event complies with applicable regulatory requirements.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Actual decision taken by downstream systems after applying the recommended action.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'Subsystem that generated the fraud scoring event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the fraud scoring evaluation was performed.']
  feature_set_hash varchar [note: 'Hash of the input feature set used for the scoring run, enabling reproducibility.']
  fraud_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric fraud risk score produced by the model (higher indicates greater risk).']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code derived from the IP address.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address of the transaction request.']
  is_blocked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was automatically blocked by the system.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction is classified as high risk.']
  latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds taken to compute the fraud score.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the fraud scoring model.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to fund the transaction.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the primary reason for the fraud decision.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Suggested operational action based on the fraud evaluation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fraud score event record was first persisted.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fraud score event record.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if the event must be included in regulatory reporting feeds.']
  rule_set_version varchar [note: 'Version of the rule set used for the evaluation.']
  score_band varchar [note: 'Categorical band derived from the fraud score for operational handling.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction being evaluated.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a fraud scoring evaluation performed on a transaction or account event. Captures the scoring model identifier, model version, input feature set hash, composite fraud score, score band (low, medium, high, critical), scoring latency in milliseconds, and the recommended action output. This is an OPERATIONAL event record — not an analytics aggregate — representing each real-time scoring invocation.'
}

Table fraud.investigation {
  investigation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fraud investigation record.']
  audit_trail_id varchar [note: 'Reference to audit trail record for this investigation.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Investigations may focus on a compromised digital wallet; linking the investigation to the wallet enables case‑level reporting and remediation tracking.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Investigation outcomes are reported in dispute case files; linking enables the Investigation‑to‑Dispute summary report.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the investigator assigned to the case.']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_case. Business justification: An investigation belongs to a single fraud case; adding investigation.fraud_case_id enables direct navigation and satisfies the requirement that every product participates in a relationship.']
  investigation_assigned_investigator_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  investigation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the investigator assigned to the case.']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the investigator was assigned.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'External case number assigned by the fraud case management system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigation record was created.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the investigation data must be retained per policy.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the investigation was concluded.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the investigation was escalated, if applicable.']
  evidence_details varchar [note: 'Detailed evidence data (e.g., transaction logs, device data) stored as JSON string.']
  evidence_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of evidence collected during investigation.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigation.']
  investigation_type varchar [note: 'Category of investigation.']
  investigator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the assigned investigator.']
  is_privileged boolean [note: 'Indicates if the investigation involves privileged accounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments by investigator.']
  outcome_decision varchar [note: 'Result of the investigation.']
  outcome_recommendation varchar [note: 'Recommended action based on investigation outcome.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the case must be reported to regulators.']
  reporting_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Jurisdiction for regulatory reporting (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score associated with the case, as calculated by fraud scoring engine.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the investigation.']
  sla_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigation met SLA requirements.']
  sla_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Target date/time for SLA compliance.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the investigation work began.']
  steps varchar [note: 'Chronological description of steps taken during investigation.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief title or description of the investigation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Detailed investigation record linked to a fraud case, tracking the investigator assigned, investigation steps taken, evidence collected (transaction logs, device data, cardholder statements), escalation history, SLA compliance status, and investigation outcome recommendation. Supports the full case investigation workflow within the FDPP case management module.'
}

  encryption_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the evidence is stored encrypted and the encryption state.']

  evidence_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or component that produced the evidence.']

  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the evidence file in bytes.']
  format varchar [note: 'File format or serialization of the stored evidence.']
  is_sensitive boolean [note: 'True if the evidence contains personally identifiable or regulated data.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction governing the evidence, expressed as ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the evidence must be archived or securely destroyed per regulatory policy.']
  storage_uri varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., S3 URI, file path) to the physical location of the stored evidence.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or service account that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the evidence record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or service account that created the evidence record.']

  Note: 'Evidence artifact record associated with a fraud investigation, capturing evidence type (transaction record, device log, cardholder statement, network advisory, 3DS result, SCA log), evidence source, collection timestamp, storage reference, chain-of-custody status, and admissibility flag. Supports structured evidence management for dispute representment and law enforcement referrals.'
}

Table fraud.loss {
  loss_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the fraud loss record.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Financial loss records must reference the dispute that generated the loss for regulatory loss‑tracking filings.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partner Chargeback Reconciliation Report needs to attribute loss amounts to the responsible partner for settlement and liability.']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the confirmed fraud case to which this loss is linked.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Financial reporting of fraud loss expense requires posting each loss to a GL expense account for SOX compliance and audit trails.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., 2023-Q1) to which the loss is booked for financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loss record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency in which the loss is measured.']
  gross_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the fraudulent transaction before any recovery.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the regulatory jurisdiction governing the loss.']
  loss_category varchar [note: 'Category describing how the fraud occurred (e.g., card‑present, CNP, account takeover).']
  loss_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the circumstances of the fraud loss.']
  loss_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the loss record.']
  net_loss_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final loss after subtracting recovered amount from gross loss.']
  occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the fraudulent loss was realized in the payment flow.']
  recovered_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount successfully recovered through chargeback, insurance, or law‑enforcement actions.']
  recovery_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to recover funds from the fraudulent loss.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference code for the loss, used in reporting and audit trails.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the loss has been reported to a regulator (true) or not (false).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the loss record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the loss record.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'Date the loss was formally written off in the accounting system.']

  Note: 'Financial loss record associated with a confirmed fraud case, capturing gross fraud loss amount, recovered amount, net loss, loss currency, loss category (card-present, CNP, account takeover, synthetic identity), write-off date, recovery method (chargeback, insurance, law enforcement), and accounting period. SSOT for fraud financial impact tracking and regulatory loss reporting.'
}

Table fraud.network_fraud_advisory {
  network_fraud_advisory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the network fraud advisory record.']
  related_case_fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_case. Business justification: network_fraud_advisory often relates to a specific fraud case; linking provides context and auditability.']
  advisory_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text details describing the nature of the fraud advisory.']
  advisory_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the advisory indicating potential impact.']
  advisory_severity_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score representing the advisory\'s risk magnitude, used for internal prioritization.']
  advisory_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the advisory within the fraud system.']
  advisory_type varchar [note: 'Category of fraud advisory received from a card network or industry body.']
  affected_entity_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the affected entity (e.g., compromised BIN number, PAN range, merchant ID).']
  affected_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity impacted by the advisory (e.g., BIN, PAN, merchant, account).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the advisory record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the advisory is considered active for monitoring.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the advisory is no longer applicable.']
  external_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number provided by the issuing network for traceability.']
  internal_action_taken varchar [note: 'Action performed internally in response to the advisory.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the advisory is currently active in the system.']
  network_name varchar [note: 'Card network or industry body that issued the advisory.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the advisory was received by the fraud platform.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the advisory record.']

  Note: 'Fraud advisory record received from card network schemes (Visa, Mastercard) or industry bodies, containing advisory type (compromised BIN, compromised PAN range, merchant compromise, account takeover campaign), affected entity identifiers, advisory severity, issuing network, receipt timestamp, and internal action taken. Enables proactive fraud prevention based on network intelligence.'
}

Table fraud.compromised_credential {
  compromised_credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the compromised credential record.']
  related_case_fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud case linked to this compromised credential.']
  related_fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud case linked to this compromised credential.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Applicable regulatory or compliance obligations (e.g., PCI DSS, GDPR) triggered by the compromise.']
  compromise_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the credential was determined to be compromised.']
  compromise_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the compromise information indicating how the credential breach was discovered.']
  compromised_credential_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the compromised credential record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compromised credential record was first created.']
  credential_identifier_hash varchar [note: 'Hashed representation of the compromised credential value to protect sensitive data while enabling lookup.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment credential that was compromised (e.g., PAN, DPAN, IBAN, account number, token).']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to detect the compromise (real‑time scoring, batch analysis, or manual review).']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the compromise was detected by the system.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the compromised credential record is currently active in the system.']
  issuer_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the issuing bank or entity responsible for the compromised credential.']
  issuer_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the issuing institution has been notified of the compromise.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating the regulatory jurisdiction relevant to the compromise.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the last modification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or comments about the compromise.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the notification to the issuing bank or cardholder.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notification was sent (or attempted).']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Planned remediation step to address the compromised credential.']
  remediation_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which remediation was successfully completed.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the remediation process.']
  remediation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent remediation activity.']
  risk_score_at_compromise decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned to the credential at the time of compromise detection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Record of a compromised payment credential (PAN, DPAN, IBAN, account number) identified through network advisories, dark web intelligence, or internal detection. Captures credential type, tokenized/hashed credential identifier, compromise source, compromise date, notification status to issuer, remediation action (reissue, block, monitor), and remediation completion date.'
}

Table fraud.case_status_history {
  case_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each status transition record in the fraud case lifecycle.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Status history location determines regulatory jurisdiction; FK to reference.country supports compliance reporting.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the device fingerprint record associated with the event.']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the fraud case to which this status transition belongs.']
  actor varchar [note: 'Name or system identifier of the entity (analyst, automated system, or external partner) that performed the status change.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the actor that performed the transition.']
  case_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the fraud type associated with the case.']
  case_priority varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority level of the fraud case at the time of the transition.']
  case_status_history_status varchar [note: 'The status of the fraud case after the transition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|under_investigation|escalated|pending_recovery|closed_fraud|closed_no_fraud|referred_to_law_enforcement — promote to reference product]']
  channel varchar [note: 'Origin channel of the transaction or event that led to the status transition.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transition complies with relevant regulatory or internal policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this status history record was first inserted into the data store.']
  fraud_rule_triggered varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud rule or model that influenced the status change, if applicable.']
  investigation_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the investigation at the time of this transition.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address observed for the transaction or event.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'True if the case was escalated to a higher tier or external authority during this transition.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured at the time of the status transition.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The status of the fraud case before the transition occurred.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if this status change triggers a regulatory filing (e.g., SAR, STR).']
  risk_score_at_transition decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score associated with the case when the status change was recorded.']
  sla_deadline_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Target date‑time by which the status transition should be completed to meet service‑level agreements.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transition occurred within the SLA deadline (true) or missed it (false).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that generated the status transition record (e.g., Fraud Detection Platform).']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the status change was recorded.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this status history record.']

  Note: 'Status transition history for a fraud case, recording each status change (new, under investigation, escalated, pending recovery, closed-fraud, closed-no-fraud, referred-to-law-enforcement), the timestamp of the transition, the actor (analyst, automated system), and any status change notes. Enables SLA tracking and case lifecycle audit.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_type {
  fraud_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each fraud type. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a reference lookup for fraud classification.']
  parent_fraud_type_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent fraud type for hierarchical classification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fraud type record was created.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Primary detection technique used for this fraud type.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the fraud type definition becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the fraud type definition expires (null if indefinite).']
  fraud_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category grouping similar fraud types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: card_not_present|card_present|account_takeover|synthetic_identity|merchant_fraud|money_mule|phishing|first_party — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fraud_type_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the fraud type (e.g., CNP for Card-Not-Present).']
  fraud_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the fraud scenario and typical characteristics.']
  fraud_type_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fraud type.']
  fraud_type_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the fraud type definition.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fraud type is currently active in the system.']
  mitigation_action varchar [note: 'Standard remediation action taken when this fraud type is detected.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel where the fraud type is observed.']
  regulatory_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used in regulatory reports (e.g., Visa reason code, Mastercard reason code).']
  requires_manual_review boolean [note: 'Indicates if cases of this fraud type automatically trigger manual investigation.']
  risk_score_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default weight applied to this fraud type in aggregate risk scoring models.']
  typical_loss_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monetary loss historically associated with this fraud type.']
  typical_loss_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the typical loss amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fraud type record.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of fraud types recognized by the platform, including type code, type name (card-not-present, card-present counterfeit, lost-and-stolen, account takeover, synthetic identity, first-party, merchant fraud, money mule, phishing), parent category, applicable payment channel, and regulatory reporting code mapping (e.g., Visa reason code, Mastercard reason code). Used for consistent fraud categorization across cases and reporting.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_channel {
  fraud_channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each payment channel used in fraud monitoring.']
  applicable_rule_categories varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of fraud rule categories that apply to this channel (e.g., "velocity|device_fingerprint|geolocation").']
  average_fraud_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Historical average fraud rate for the channel expressed as a proportion (e.g., 0.0125 = 1.25%).']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Short, unique alphanumeric code that identifies the payment channel.']
  channel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the channel\'s characteristics and typical use cases.']
  channel_group varchar [note: 'Higher‑level category used to aggregate channels (e.g., "Card", "Bank Transfer", "Digital").']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment channel (e.g., "Card‑Present POS").']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the channel (e.g., card‑present, card‑not‑present, ATM, digital wallet, P2P, A2A, ACH, BNPL, QR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: card_present|card_not_present|atm|digital_wallet|p2p|a2a|ach|bnpl|qr — promote to reference product]']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or standards requirements that apply to the channel (e.g., PCI DSS, PSD2).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel record was first inserted.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the channel definition is retired; null if still active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the channel definition becomes active for fraud monitoring.']
  fraud_channel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the channel definition.']
  is_3ds_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3‑Domain Secure authentication is supported for the channel.']
  is_sca_required boolean [note: 'True if Strong Customer Authentication is mandatory for transactions on this channel.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating the primary regulatory jurisdiction for the channel.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text comments or observations about the channel.']
  risk_score_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the channel’s base fraud score to reflect relative risk.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or service that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the channel record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or service that created the channel record.']

  Note: 'Reference data defining the payment channels in scope for fraud monitoring (card-present POS, CNP e-commerce, CNP MOTO, ATM, digital wallet, P2P, A2A, ACH, BNPL, QR code). Captures channel code, channel name, applicable fraud rule categories, 3DS applicability flag, SCA requirement flag, and average fraud rate benchmark. Enables channel-specific fraud rule targeting.'
}

Table fraud.false_positive_review {
  false_positive_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the false positive review record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit standard role categories and is modeled as a custom record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (customer) linked to the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (customer) linked to the transaction.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud case associated with the alert.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device fingerprint record associated with the transaction.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partners require a false‑positive review report to assess impact on their transaction volume and risk scores.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the analyst who performed the review.']
  fraud_alert_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud alert that was reviewed.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant involved in the transaction.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: False‑positive reviews reference the payment product to assess impact and report product‑level false‑positive rates.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the analyst who performed the review.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the original alert.']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the cardholder.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the review outcome complies with internal fraud policy and regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the false positive review record was created in the system.']
  customer_impact varchar [note: 'Effect of the false positive on the customer, such as a declined legitimate transaction or account freeze.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for escalating the review.']
  false_positive_reason_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing why the alert was deemed a false positive.']
  false_positive_reason_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation providing additional context for the false positive classification.']
  feedback_action_detail varchar [note: 'Additional details about the corrective action applied.']
  feedback_action_taken varchar [note: 'Action performed as a result of the false positive review to improve fraud detection (e.g., model tuning).']
  impact_detail varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific customer impact experienced.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address observed for the transaction.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'True if the false positive review was escalated to senior fraud management.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the jurisdiction governing the transaction.']
  original_alert_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud score value that originally triggered the alert.']
  original_alert_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the original fraud alert.']
  original_alert_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the original fraud alert was generated.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the transaction that triggered the alert.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if the false positive review required regulatory reporting (e.g., SAR filing).']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review was marked as resolved.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Analyst notes captured during the false positive review.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the review indicating whether the alert was a false positive, true positive, or requires further investigation.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the false positive review.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the false positive review was performed.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the analyst who cleared the alert as a false positive.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the original transaction was initiated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the review record.']

  Note: 'Record of a fraud alert or case that was reviewed and determined to be a false positive, capturing the original alert or case reference, the review outcome, the analyst who cleared it, the false positive reason category, the customer impact (declined legitimate transaction, account freeze), and any feedback loop action taken to tune the scoring model or rule. Supports model calibration and customer experience improvement.'
}

Table fraud.watchlist {
  watchlist_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each watchlist entry.']
  watchlist_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.watchlist_entry. Business justification: Fraud detection references the master watchlist maintained by compliance; FK provides authoritative entity details for alerts.']
  assigned_analyst varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud analyst responsible for monitoring this watchlist entry.']
  contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred contact channel for the watchlist entity used for notifications or escalations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the watchlist entry was initially created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the watchlist entry becomes effective.']
  entity_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the entity (e.g., PAN, MID, device fingerprint, IP address, email domain).']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity placed on the watchlist (e.g., cardholder, merchant, device, IP range, email domain).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the watchlist entry expires or is scheduled for removal.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the watchlist entry applies globally across all jurisdictions.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the regulatory jurisdiction relevant to the watchlist entry.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review action performed on the entry.']
  monitoring_level varchar [note: 'Intensity of monitoring applied to the entity (passive = observation only, active = heightened scrutiny, step_up = requires step‑up authentication).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the watchlist entry.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for placing the entity on the watchlist (e.g., high velocity, suspicious geography, known fraud pattern).']
  review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next manual review of the watchlist entry.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned to the entity at the time of watchlist inclusion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the watchlist entry.']
  watchlist_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the watchlist entry (internal risk team, external network advisory, law‑enforcement notification).']
  watchlist_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the watchlist entry.']

  Note: 'Watchlist of entities (cardholders, merchants, devices, IP ranges, email domains) under enhanced fraud monitoring without full blocking. Captures entity type, entity identifier, watchlist reason, monitoring level (passive, active, step-up auth required), watchlist source (internal, network advisory, law enforcement), effective date, review date, and assigned analyst. Distinct from blocklist — watchlist entities are monitored but not blocked.'
}

Table fraud.rule_test {
  rule_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each fraud rule test execution record.']
  audit_trail_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit trail entry capturing changes to this test record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the test record.']
  rate_snapshot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate_snapshot. Business justification: Rule testing uses historical FX rate snapshots to validate rule behavior against real market data.']
  rule_set_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule set applied during the test.']
  rule_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific fraud rule version being evaluated.']
  test_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dataset (historical or live) used for the simulation.']
  actual_fraud_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Observed monetary loss from fraud during the test period.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result was formally approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the test.']
  average_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Mean monetary value of transactions in the test sample.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance constraints applicable to the test (e.g., PCI DSS, GDPR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts in the test.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Assessment of the underlying data quality used for the test.']
  estimated_false_negative_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected proportion of fraudulent transactions missed by the rule.']
  estimated_false_positive_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected proportion of legitimate transactions incorrectly flagged as fraud.']
  expected_fraud_loss decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary loss from fraud that the rule aims to prevent.']
  expected_transaction_volume varchar [note: 'Projected number of transactions the rule would process if deployed.']
  financial_impact_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of the net financial effect (savings vs. loss) if the rule were deployed.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test record is currently active in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the test execution.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the test (higher value = higher priority).']
  region_scope varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code(s) indicating geographic coverage of the test.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test results must be included in regulatory reporting.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score level that the rule must exceed to trigger a fraud decision during the test.']
  rule_test_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, design, and expectations of the test.']
  simulated_hit_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected proportion of fraudulent transactions correctly identified by the rule during the test.']
  test_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the test result.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the rule being tested (e.g., velocity, device fingerprint, amount).']
  test_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraud rule test execution completed.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Deployment environment where the test was run.']
  test_execution_mode varchar [note: 'Mode in which the test was executed: live, historical replay, or synthetic data.']
  test_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the fraud rule test scenario.']
  test_result_summary varchar [note: 'Concise textual summary of test outcomes and key observations.']
  test_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative of the business scope (e.g., transaction types, payment methods) covered by the test.']
  test_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fraud rule test execution began.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the test execution.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the test methodology (A/B, champion‑challenger, baseline).']
  transaction_sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of transactions included in the test dataset.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the test record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the test record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version counter for the test record, incremented on each update.']

  Note: 'Record of a fraud rule simulation or A/B test run against historical or live transaction data before production deployment. Captures test name, rule version under test, test dataset reference, test period, simulated hit rate, estimated false positive rate, estimated false negative rate, financial impact estimate, and test approval status. Supports safe rule deployment and champion-challenger testing.'
}

Table fraud.mule_account {
  mule_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the mule account record.']
  related_case_fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_case. Business justification: mule_account records should be tied to the fraud case they are associated with for investigation and reporting.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account based on its underlying payment instrument or ownership model.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'Operational response applied to the mule account after detection.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address linked to the mule account for communication and investigation.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number associated with the mule account, used for verification and outreach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the mule account record was first created in the data lake.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'The process or system that first identified the account as a mule.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the mule account was first flagged by the detection system.']
  display_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name associated with the mule account, as provided during onboarding or investigation.']
  is_blocked boolean [note: 'True if the account is currently blocked from processing transactions.']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the underlying account number has been tokenized (true) or stored in clear (false).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the primary legal jurisdiction for the mule account.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the mule account was last reviewed by fraud analysts or compliance.']
  mule_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the mule account within the fraud management process.']
  mule_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the mule is knowingly participating (witting), unaware (unwitting), or a repeat offender (established).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for analyst comments, case details, or additional context.']
  reporting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a SAR has been filed, law‑enforcement referral made, or no reporting required.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the mule account by fraud scoring models.']
  risk_score_band varchar [note: 'Categorical bucket derived from the numeric risk score.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the mule account record.']
  tokenized_account_reference varchar [note: 'Tokenized representation of the account number used by the mule, stored to protect the underlying PAN.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the mule account record.']

  Note: 'Record of an identified or suspected money mule account used to receive and transfer fraudulent funds. Captures account identifier (tokenized), account type, mule category (witting, unwitting, established), detection method, linked fraud cases, reporting status (SAR filed, law enforcement referred), account action taken (freeze, close, monitor), and detection timestamp. Supports AML-fraud convergence investigations.'
}

Table fraud.fraud_notification {
  fraud_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each outbound fraud notification.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Notifications about fraud events often need to include the related dispute case for customer communication compliance.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partner‑facing notification system delivers fraud case updates; FK needed for routing and audit.']
  fraud_alert_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the alert that caused the notification.']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud case linked to this notification.']
  message_template_id varchar [note: 'Lookup to the template defining the notification body.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Notifications include product details for stakeholder communication and regulatory filing per product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the notification entry was first persisted.']
  delivery_attempt_count varchar [note: 'How many times the system tried to send the notification.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Medium used to deliver the notification.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the notification delivery.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time the notification was dispatched.']
  fraud_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the fraudulent activity occurred.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Marks notifications that require immediate attention.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Language in which the notification was composed.']
  message_content varchar [note: 'Full textual content delivered to the recipient.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Category of the notification sent to the recipient.']
  originating_system varchar [note: 'Name of the internal service that generated the notification.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Urgency level of the notification.']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'Email address used for email channel notifications.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number used for SMS or IVR channel notifications.']
  recipient_type varchar [note: 'Business entity that receives the notification.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'True if delivery occurred before SLA target.']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Deadline by which the notification must be delivered to meet SLA.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the notification entry was last modified.']

  Note: 'Outbound notification record sent to a cardholder, merchant, or internal team regarding a fraud event. Captures notification type (fraud alert, account freeze notice, transaction decline explanation, reissuance notice), delivery channel (SMS, email, push, IVR), recipient type, delivery status, delivery timestamp, and the triggering fraud case or alert reference. Supports customer communication compliance and SLA tracking.'
}

Table fraud.loss_allocation {
  loss_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the fraud_loss_allocation association']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fraud case']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the GL account']
  gross_loss_amount decimal [note: 'Total loss amount before recoveries']
  loss_category varchar [note: 'Category of the loss (e.g., chargeback, reserve)']
  loss_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the loss amounts']
  loss_occurrence_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loss posting was recorded']
  loss_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business reference for the loss posting']
  loss_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the loss posting (e.g., pending, posted)']
  net_loss_amount decimal [note: 'Net loss after recoveries']
  recovered_amount decimal [note: 'Amount recovered from the fraudster or merchant']
  recovery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recover the loss (e.g., chargeback, settlement)']

  Note: 'Represents the allocation of a fraud loss from a fraud_case to a GL account. Each record captures the specific loss posting details that belong to the relationship, linking one fraud_case to one gl_account.. Existence Justification: Each fraud case may generate a financial loss that is posted to one or more general‑ledger expense accounts. The organization records each posting with details such as loss amount, currency, and recovery method, creating a many‑to‑many linkage. Conversely, a GL account can receive loss postings from many fraud cases.'
}

Table fraud.case_payment_link {
  case_payment_link_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the case_payment_link association']
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the cross‑border payment']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the fraud case']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was linked to the fraud case']
  role varchar [note: 'Role of the payment in the investigation (e.g., primary, supporting, related)']

  Note: 'Association product representing the link between a fraud case and a cross‑border payment. It records when the payment was linked to the case and the role of the payment in the investigation.. Existence Justification: Investigators link multiple cross‑border payments to a single fraud case, and a single payment can be part of multiple fraud investigations. The link is managed as a separate record that captures the detection timestamp and the role of the payment in the investigation.'
}

Table fraud.audit_trail {
  audit_trail_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit_trail']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the user, service account, or system that performed the action.']
  parent_audit_trail_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit_trail (parent_audit_trail_id)']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Unique ID assigned by the source system to the inbound request.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the event to a specific user session.']
  action varchar [note: 'Specific operation that was executed and is being audited.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Denotes whether the actor is a human user, an automated system, or a micro‑service.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the audit entry was first persisted.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the audit entry.']
  blocklist_flag boolean [note: 'True if the event involved a blocklisted entity.']
  correlation_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used to trace a series of related events across systems.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Unique hash representing the device used in the transaction.']
  event_payload varchar [note: 'Raw data or JSON payload associated with the event (e.g., request body, response code).']
  event_source varchar [note: 'Logical identifier of the system, device, or service that emitted the event (e.g., transaction_id, device_id).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the audited event happened in the source system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the event for fraud monitoring and audit purposes.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address from which the event originated.']
  location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code representing the geographic origin of the event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional context or comments recorded by fraud analysts.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the action succeeded or failed.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective step performed after the event.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Qualitative classification of the risk score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk assessment (0.00‑99.99) assigned to the event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Logical name of the application or service that emitted the audit event.']
  status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the audit entry within the audit pipeline.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or client identifier from which the request originated.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_trail. Referenced by audit_trail_id.'
}

Table fraud.message_template {

  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent compliance review.']

  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the template for processing order.']
  regulatory_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory domain(s) the template must satisfy.']
  requires_opt_out boolean [note: 'True if the message must include an opt‑out mechanism per regulation.']

  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the template.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Subject line used for email‑type messages.']
  template_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the template purpose.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the template record.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times the template has been used.']
  version_notes varchar [note: 'Summary of changes introduced in this version.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the template; increments on each change.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier that created the template.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for message_template. Referenced by message_template_id.'
}

  velocity_window_seconds varchar [note: 'Time window in seconds used for velocity calculations.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier (e.g., 1.0.3) for change management.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for rule_set. Referenced by rule_set_id.'
}

Table risk.risk_profile {
  risk_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the risk profile record.']
  appetite_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_appetite_framework. Business justification: Each risk profile aligns to a single risk appetite framework; replace string alignment with FK.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Required to associate each cardholder\'s risk profile used in AML/fraud scoring and regulatory reporting; risk profile is generated per cardholder profile.']
  compliance_kyc_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_kyc_verification. Business justification: Risk profiles incorporate KYC verification results to adjust risk scores per AML regulations.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Risk profile reporting needs authoritative currency metadata for exposure limits; linking to reference.currency enables accurate conversion and compliance.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Risk scoring per digital wallet for AML/fraud monitoring requires linking each risk_profile to its digital_wallet.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the counterparty (merchant, cardholder, partner, issuer, or acquirer) to which this risk profile belongs.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: Regulatory AML/KYC reporting requires linking each merchant\'s risk profile to the merchant record for audit and monitoring.']
  partner_ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Required for Partner Risk Profiling report, linking each partner\'s risk profile to its ecosystem partner record for compliance and monitoring.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Required for product‑specific risk profiling used in the Product Risk Tiering report, which assigns risk scores per payment product.']
  risk_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk model (e.g., model name or UUID) that produced the risk_score.']
  risk_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk policy governing this profile.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Risk scoring varies by card scheme; risk profiles must reference scheme for compliance reporting and scheme‑specific fraud rules.']
  aml_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) reflecting anti‑money‑laundering risk based on screening and transaction patterns.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall compliance rating derived from regulatory checks, KYC, AML, and sanctions results.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk profile record was first created.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) indicating likelihood of fraudulent activity associated with the counterparty.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of Know‑Your‑Customer verification.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent comprehensive risk assessment.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled risk review.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the counterparty indicating its role in the payment ecosystem.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel used to reach the counterparty for risk communications.']
  primary_contact_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contact detail corresponding to the selected primary_contact_method (e.g., email address or phone number).']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is subject to special regulatory reporting.']
  risk_approval_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the risk approval.']
  risk_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manual risk approval is required for this counterparty.']
  risk_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required risk approval.']
  risk_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk approval decision was made.']
  risk_assessment_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to generate the risk score.']
  risk_assessment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the risk assessment data.']
  risk_decision_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the risk decision applied to the counterparty.']
  risk_exposure_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary exposure the organization is willing to accept for this counterparty.']
  risk_exposure_used decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current monetary exposure incurred by the counterparty.']
  risk_flags varchar [note: 'Delimited list of risk flags or alerts (e.g., \'high_velocity|geo_mismatch\').']
  risk_last_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any risk‑related field.']
  risk_limit_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the risk_exposure_limit that is currently utilized.']
  risk_model_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the risk model used for scoring.']
  risk_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version of the risk policy applied to this profile.']
  risk_profile_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the risk profile record.']
  risk_review_notes varchar [note: 'Analyst comments and observations from the latest risk review.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score (0‑100) derived from underwriting models, fraud signals, AML checks, and other risk inputs.']
  risk_score_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the current risk_score was calculated.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from risk_score thresholds used for decisioning and monitoring.']
  sanction_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening for the counterparty.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version number for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Master record for each counterparty (merchant, cardholder, partner) capturing aggregated risk scores, risk tier classification, risk appetite alignment, and overall risk posture. SSOT for entity-level risk assessment used in underwriting, authorization decisioning, and ongoing portfolio monitoring.'
}

Table risk.appetite_framework {
  appetite_framework_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the risk appetite framework record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the framework record.']
  appetite_framework_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the framework\'s purpose, scope, and methodology.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the framework received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User or committee identifier that approved the framework.']
  classification_or_type varchar [note: 'Category of the framework indicating the primary risk dimension it governs.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance posture of the framework against internal policies and external regulations.']
  concentration_limit_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed concentration as a percentage of total exposure.']
  counterparty_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of counterparties (e.g., consumer, enterprise) covered by the framework.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the framework record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the framework becomes operationally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the framework expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  exposure_limit_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate exposure permitted under the framework.']
  exposure_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency for the exposure limit amount.']
  framework_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the framework in external systems.']
  framework_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the risk appetite framework as used by the risk committee.']
  framework_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the framework, following semantic versioning (e.g., v1.0).']
  geography varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code or region identifier for the framework\'s applicability.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the framework.']
  max_loss_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Absolute monetary loss limit that the organization is willing to tolerate for the defined scope.']
  max_loss_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the maximum loss amount.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the framework\'s limits are monitored and reported.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next formal review of the framework.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, exceptions, or operational notes related to the framework.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Business product line or service to which the framework is scoped.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory sign‑off is required for this framework.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required regulatory approval.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval for formal review of the framework.']
  risk_capacity_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total risk capacity the organization can absorb for the defined scope.']
  risk_capacity_currency varchar [note: 'Currency for the risk capacity amount.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High‑level risk domain that the appetite limits apply to.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score value above which transactions are flagged for additional review.']
  risk_score_type varchar [note: 'Type of risk score used in the threshold definition.']
  risk_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'Weight applied to the risk factor when calculating aggregate risk exposure.']
  tolerance_band varchar [note: 'Qualitative band indicating the severity of tolerance (e.g., low, medium).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the framework record.']

  Note: 'Defines the enterprise risk appetite thresholds, tolerance bands, and risk capacity limits approved by the risk committee. Captures maximum acceptable loss levels, exposure concentration limits, and risk-reward parameters by product line, geography, and counterparty segment. Governs all downstream risk decisioning rules and underwriting policies.'
}

Table risk.underwriting_policy {
  underwriting_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the underwriting policy record.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Underwriting policies are defined per card scheme; linking enables scheme‑aware merchant onboarding and policy enforcement.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions exceeding thresholds require manual underwriting approval.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual summary of regulatory and internal compliance obligations tied to the policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary limits defined in the policy.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the policy expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended policies.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the policy becomes binding.']
  max_merchant_discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound on the merchant discount rate (MDR) that can be applied under this policy.']
  mcc_allowed varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of MCCs that the merchant is permitted to process under this policy.']
  mcc_blocked varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of MCCs that are prohibited for the merchant under this policy.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'External business code or number used to reference the policy in contracts and communications.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the policy purpose, scope and applicable merchant segments.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the underwriting policy.']
  policy_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or team responsible for maintaining the policy.']
  policy_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for periodic review and re‑certification of the policy.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Category of the policy based on the type of entity it governs.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required regulator sign‑off for the policy.']
  reserve_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction amount held as a reserve to mitigate chargeback risk.']
  risk_appetite_category varchar [note: 'Business‑defined risk appetite tier that the policy aligns with.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum risk score a merchant may have to qualify under this policy.']
  rolling_reserve_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Dynamic reserve percentage that can adjust based on merchant performance trends.']
  tpv_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total payment volume (in policy currency) a merchant may reach before additional review is required.']
  transaction_amount_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative monetary value of transactions permitted under the policy for the defined period.']
  transaction_volume_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions allowed per defined period (e.g., per month).']
  underwriting_decision_logic varchar [note: 'Reference to the algorithmic model or rule set used for automated decisions.']
  underwriting_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy.']
  underwriting_rules_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the rule set applied by this policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the policy record.']

  Note: 'Defines underwriting rules, eligibility criteria, and approval conditions for merchant onboarding, credit extension, and payment product access. Captures MCC-based restrictions, TPV thresholds, reserve requirements, rolling reserve rates, and risk-based pricing parameters. Used by the Merchant Management System and Risk and Compliance Management System to automate underwriting decisions.'
}

Table risk.risk_rule {
  risk_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the risk decisioning rule.']
  dispute_rule_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.rule_config. Business justification: Each risk rule can be mapped to a specific dispute rule configuration used by the dispute engine.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Team or individual responsible for the rule\'s maintenance.']
  fraud_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_rule. Business justification: Some risk rules are also fraud rules; linking supports unified rule governance and cross‑domain impact analysis.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: Rule engine evaluates transaction MCCs; FK to reference.mcc provides standardized category and risk score for rule evaluation.']
  network_routing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_routing_rule. Business justification: Specific routing rules may have associated risk rules for fraud checks; FK ties risk rule to the routing rule it governs.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Compliance reporting requires mapping rule regulatory references to official jurisdiction records.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory compliance requires each risk rule to be traceable to its governing regulatory obligation for audit and reporting.']
  risk_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk scoring model that utilizes this rule.']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Risk rules often depend on transaction type; FK ensures consistent type codes for scoring and audit.']
  action varchar [note: 'Automated action taken when the rule condition is met.']
  applicable_channel varchar [note: 'Payment channel (e.g., online, mobile, POS) where the rule is evaluated.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating geographic scope of the rule.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes for audit or change‑management purposes.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the rule with its referenced regulation.']
  concentration_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Limit on concentration of transactions to a single merchant or channel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code associated with monetary thresholds.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date on which the rule was officially deprecated.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes active for evaluation.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires or is no longer applied (null if open‑ended).']
  exposure_limit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative monetary exposure limit that triggers the rule.']
  is_compliance_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule enforces regulatory compliance.']
  is_fraud_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is part of the fraud detection engine.']
  last_evaluated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction evaluation against this rule.']
  logic varchar [note: 'Executable expression or script that defines the rule condition.']
  max_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound amount for which the rule applies (optional).']
  min_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound amount for which the rule applies (optional).']
  parameters varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded parameter set used by the rule logic.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple rules fire (higher = higher priority).']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the rule definition is retained for audit purposes.']
  risk_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the rule logic and purpose.']
  risk_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
