  risk_score_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weight applied to the rule\'s contribution to the overall risk score (0‑100).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the rule for reference in operational systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the risk rule used for display and reporting.']
  rule_source varchar [note: 'Originating system that created or manages the rule (e.g., Fraud Detection Platform).']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rule defining the underlying risk model (e.g., velocity, exposure).']
  threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the threshold value (e.g., currency, count, percent).']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric threshold that must be exceeded for the rule to fire.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the rule record.']
  velocity_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions allowed within the velocity window.']
  velocity_window_seconds varchar [note: 'Time window in seconds over which transaction count is evaluated for velocity rules.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number of the rule definition.']

  Note: 'Risk decisioning rule definitions including rule type (velocity, exposure, concentration, behavioral, threshold-based), evaluation logic, parameterized threshold values, action triggers (approve, decline, review, hold), and rule versioning. Supports real-time transaction rules and batch portfolio rules. Includes threshold configurations that drive automated risk actions.'
}

Table risk.risk_model {
  risk_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each risk scoring model.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_region. Business justification: Risk models are deployed per geographic/ regulatory region; linking model to gateway region supports correct model selection.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Model validation reports must reference the specific regulatory obligation the model addresses, enabling oversight of model compliance.']
  algorithm varchar [note: 'Machine‑learning algorithm used (e.g., logistic_regression, xgboost, neural_network).']
  algorithm_parameters varchar [note: 'Serialized hyper‑parameter settings for the algorithm (JSON string).']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Reference or summary of audit events related to model changes.']
  champion_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the model is the champion, challenger, or inactive in the A/B testing framework.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of compliance controls the model satisfies (e.g., PCI DSS, GDPR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model record was first created.']
  deployment_state varchar [note: 'Current deployment state of the model.']
  deployment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model was deployed to production.']
  deprecated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the model is deprecated and should not be used for new decisions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the model ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the model becomes effective for decisioning.']
  exposure_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary exposure allowed under this model.']
  exposure_limit_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the exposure limit.']
  feature_set_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the features used by the model.']
  input_data_source varchar [note: 'Primary data source feeding the model (e.g., transaction_data, merchant_profile).']
  last_retrained_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent retraining cycle.']
  output_metric varchar [note: 'Name of the metric produced by the model (e.g., risk_score).']
  owner_team varchar [note: 'Business team responsible for the model\'s governance.']
  performance_auc decimal(5,4) [note: 'AUC metric for model performance.']
  performance_gini decimal(5,4) [note: 'Gini coefficient measuring model discrimination power.']
  performance_ks decimal(5,4) [note: 'KS statistic indicating separation between good and bad outcomes.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory sign‑off for the model.']
  retirement_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the model was retired.']
  retirement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the model was retired (nullable).']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High‑level risk domain the model addresses (e.g., credit_risk, operational_risk).']
  risk_model_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the model in operational systems.']
  risk_model_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the model\'s purpose and scope.']
  risk_model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the risk model.']
  risk_model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the model.']
  risk_model_type varchar [note: 'Category of underwriting model (e.g., credit, behavioral, velocity, concentration, custom).']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'Score above which a transaction is declined or flagged.']
  score_max decimal(5,4) [note: 'Maximum possible value of the model\'s risk score.']
  score_min decimal(5,4) [note: 'Minimum possible value of the model\'s risk score.']
  score_units varchar [note: 'Units or scale of the risk score (e.g., points, percentile).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created or maintains the model record.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason explaining the current status.']
  training_dataset_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the dataset used to train the model.']
  training_date date [note: 'Date on which the model was originally trained.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the model record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version string of the model (e.g., v1.2.3).']
  version_notes varchar [note: 'Notes describing changes introduced in this version.']

  Note: 'Registry of risk scoring models deployed in the risk engine — including model type (credit, behavioral, velocity, concentration), model version, champion/challenger status, training dataset reference, feature set, performance metrics (Gini, KS, AUC), and deployment lifecycle state. Distinct from fraud domain ML models — these are risk underwriting and exposure models.'
}

Table risk.exposure_limit {
  exposure_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the exposure limit record.']
  appetite_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_appetite_framework. Business justification: Exposure limits belong to a risk appetite framework; one framework can have many exposure limits. Added exposure_limit.risk_appetite_framework_id.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Exposure limits are allocated to internal cost centers for charge‑back, budgeting, and performance measurement.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or role that approved the current limit version.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether changes to the limit require formal approval.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the limit was last approved.']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current breach status of the limit.']
  business_identifier varchar [note: 'External identifier or code used by the risk system to reference the limit.']
  counterparty_type varchar [note: 'Type of counterparty to which the limit applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the limit record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the limit amount.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the limit becomes effective.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the limit expires; null for open‑ended limits.']
  exposure_limit_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the limit record.']
  geography varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where the limit is applicable.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the limit.']
  limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed exposure amount for the defined scope.']
  limit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the limit (e.g., “US Merchant Daily TPV Limit”).']
  limit_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the limit – defines the aggregation window or transaction type.']
  limit_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that originated the limit record (e.g., Risk Management System, Merchant Management System).']
  next_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the next scheduled review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or exceptions related to the limit.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Product or service category the limit governs.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory limit reviews.']
  risk_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the risk model used to calculate the limit.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score associated with the counterparty at the time of limit assignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the limit record.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the limit that has been utilized (utilized_amount / limit_amount * 100).']
  utilized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current amount of exposure already used within the active period.']

  Note: 'Maximum exposure and credit limit assignments by counterparty, counterparty type (merchant, cardholder, institutional partner), limit scope (TPV, settlement, credit line, intraday, FX), geography, and product type. Captures limit amount, currency, utilization, breach status, and review cycle. Enforced during real-time authorization and batch settlement.'
}

Table risk.threshold {
  threshold_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the risk threshold configuration.']
  appetite_framework_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_appetite_framework. Business justification: Risk thresholds are defined within a risk appetite framework; added FK to associate each threshold with its framework.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Thresholds are defined per currency; linking to reference.currency provides official code, symbol, and rounding rules.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: MCC‑based thresholds need standardized MCC data for accurate risk limits per merchant category.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual description of regulatory or internal compliance rules tied to this threshold.']
  concentration_limit decimal(20,2) [note: 'Maximum exposure amount to a single merchant or risk factor before additional controls apply.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the threshold record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the threshold becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the threshold expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  escalation_action varchar [note: 'Automated action taken when the threshold is breached.']
  is_system_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this threshold is a built‑in default provided by the platform.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the threshold was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit for a single transaction when this threshold is active.']
  max_transaction_volume varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions allowed within the threshold’s evaluation period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional comments or audit remarks.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Channel used to notify stakeholders of a threshold breach.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple thresholds apply (lower number = higher priority).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country or region code where the threshold is applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|GBR|CAN|AUS|DEU|FRA|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the threshold with respect to regulatory bodies.']
  reserve_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction amount to be held in reserve when the threshold is triggered.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory reviews of the threshold configuration.']
  risk_appetite_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the threshold within the organization’s risk appetite framework.']
  risk_dimension varchar [note: 'The specific risk metric that this threshold governs (e.g., chargeback ratio, fraud rate).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the threshold record.']
  threshold_code varchar [note: 'Business-friendly code used to reference the threshold in policies and rules.']
  threshold_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose and application of the threshold.']
  threshold_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the risk threshold.']
  threshold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the threshold configuration.']
  threshold_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the threshold value is an absolute number, a percentage, or a banded range.']
  threshold_value decimal(20,6) [note: 'Numeric value that defines the limit for the selected risk dimension.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the threshold value is expressed (e.g., percent, count, currency).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the threshold record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the threshold configuration for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Granular risk threshold configurations for specific risk dimensions — chargeback ratio thresholds, fraud rate thresholds, decline rate bands, reserve trigger levels, and concentration risk limits. Linked to risk rules and underwriting policies. Provides the parameterized values that drive automated risk actions and escalation workflows.'
}

Table risk.underwriting_decision {
  underwriting_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each underwriting decision record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cardholder (or consumer) associated with the decision, if applicable.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cardholder (or consumer) associated with the decision, if applicable.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the human underwriter who approved or reviewed the decision, if applicable.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant to which the underwriting decision applies.']
  partner_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a partner entity (e.g., PSP, TSP) involved in the decision context.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Underwriting decisions are evaluated per payment product, supporting the Product‑Level Credit Approval workflow.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the underwriting evaluation.']
  underwriter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the human underwriter who approved or reviewed the decision, if applicable.']
  underwriting_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underwriting policy applied to this decision.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the decision was made by an automated engine or a human underwriter.']
  audit_trail_ref varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed audit log entry capturing the decision workflow.']
  compliance_requirements_met varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or internal compliance checks satisfied by this decision.']
  conditions_applied varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any special conditions (e.g., reserve rate, TPV cap) imposed by the decision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the underwriting decision record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary limits.']
  decision_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the decision (e.g., "new_merchant", "risk_adjustment").']
  decision_confidence decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence level (0‑1) associated with the automated decision engine\'s output.']
  decision_expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'If the decision is conditional, the time after which it expires and must be re‑evaluated.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the underwriting evaluation: approved, declined, conditional, or referred.']
  decision_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a human can override the automated decision.']
  decision_override_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided when a decision is manually overridden.']
  decision_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the decision outcome.']
  decision_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for periodic review of the underwriting decision.']
  decision_score_components varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of individual score components used in the final risk score.']
  decision_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the decision request: real‑time engine, batch process, or manual entry.']
  decision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the underwriting decision.']
  decision_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated tags for categorizing the decision (e.g., "new_merchant,high_volume").']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the underwriting decision was made, representing the business event moment.']
  decision_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this is the initial underwriting, a reassessment, or a post‑settlement review.']
  is_fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the decision was influenced by a fraud detection signal.']
  is_high_risk_flag boolean [note: 'True when the decision falls into a high‑risk category requiring extra monitoring.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum single transaction amount permitted under the decision.']
  max_transaction_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total transaction volume allowed for the merchant over a defined period.']
  mcc_allowed varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of MCCs that the merchant is permitted to process.']
  mcc_blocked varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of MCCs that are prohibited for the merchant.']
  policy_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the underwriting policy in effect.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required regulatory sign‑off for the underwriting decision.']
  reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount placed in reserve for the merchant based on the decision.']
  reserve_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of transaction amount held in reserve as a risk mitigation measure.']
  risk_appetite_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the decision within the enterprise risk appetite framework.']
  risk_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numerical risk score calculated at decision time, higher values indicate greater risk.']
  risk_score_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the risk scoring model used for this decision.']
  tpv_cap decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative payment volume allowed for the merchant within the policy period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the underwriting decision record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each underwriting decision made for a merchant, cardholder, or partner — capturing decision outcome (approved, declined, conditional, referred), applied policy version, risk score at decision time, conditions imposed (reserve rate, TPV cap, restricted MCCs), decision timestamp, and approving authority (automated engine or human underwriter). SSOT for underwriting audit trail.'
}

Table risk.assessment {
  assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each risk assessment record.']
  assessor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the risk analyst or automated engine that generated the assessment.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Assessments calculate risk exposure in a currency; linking to reference.currency ensures consistent currency data across reports.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Risk assessments are performed per wallet to evaluate compliance and fraud risk, requiring a direct wallet reference.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the risk analyst or automated engine that generated the assessment.']
  exposure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_exposure. Business justification: FX exposure snapshots are needed in risk assessments to calculate overall exposure and regulatory capital.']
  partner_ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Enables the Partner Risk Assessment dashboard, associating partner IDs with risk assessment outcomes for regulatory reporting.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant, cardholder, or partner being assessed.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Periodic risk assessments are performed per payment product to satisfy the Product Risk Dashboard compliance report.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Real‑time risk assessment is performed per gateway request; linking enables audit trails and compliance reporting of each assessment.']
  risk_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underwriting policy governing limits and thresholds for this assessment.']
  risk_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_profile. Business justification: Risk assessments are performed for a specific risk profile; added FK to link assessment to profile.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the risk assessment was conducted.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the assessment, used in reporting and audit trails.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the workflow state of the assessment from creation through completion or rejection.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the assessment is an initial onboarding review, a scheduled periodic review, or a review triggered by a specific event.']
  average_transaction_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Mean monetary value per transaction for the party in the last 30 days.']
  compliance_flag_aml boolean [note: 'True if the assessment included an Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening.']
  compliance_flag_sanctions boolean [note: 'True if the assessment incorporated sanctions list checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the assessment entry was first persisted.']
  credit_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score reflecting creditworthiness of the assessed party.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Boolean flag set when the overall risk tier meets or exceeds the high‑risk threshold.']
  is_manual_review boolean [note: 'True when a human analyst intervened in the assessment process.']
  mcc_category varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s primary business activity.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the subsequent risk assessment, supporting periodic review cycles.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Narrative comments, observations, or justification provided by the assessor.']
  operational_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score measuring operational risk factors such as process failures or system outages.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final decision resulting from the assessment, guiding downstream processing.']
  overall_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score derived from individual risk dimension scores.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the subject of the assessment as a merchant, cardholder, or partner entity.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Prescribed next steps for the assessed party (e.g., increase limits, impose controls, terminate relationship).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating the primary operating region of the assessed party.']
  regulatory_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting compliance and regulatory exposure of the party.']
  reputational_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assessing potential damage to brand or public perception.']
  risk_exposure_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial exposure associated with the assessed risk, expressed in the reporting currency.']
  risk_model_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scoring model version applied during the assessment.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical risk tier used for underwriting decisions and monitoring.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or process that triggered the assessment.']
  transaction_amount_last_30d decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of transaction monetary values for the party over the preceding 30‑day window.']
  transaction_volume_last_30d decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate count of transactions processed for the party over the preceding 30‑day window.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Periodic or event-triggered risk assessment records for merchants, cardholders, and partners — capturing assessment type (initial onboarding, periodic review, triggered review), risk dimensions evaluated (credit, operational, reputational, regulatory), assessment outcome, risk tier assigned, and recommended actions. Distinct from real-time scoring — these are structured review records with human or model-driven conclusions.'
}

Table risk.risk_event {
  risk_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the risk event record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder involved in the event.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder involved in the event.']
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.cross_border_payment. Business justification: Mitigation actions often stem from cross‑border payments; linking provides traceability from the event to the payment.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Event amounts are recorded in a currency; FK to reference.currency supports reporting, settlement, and AML analysis.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Investigation event logs are tied to the dispute case they belong to, enabling end‑to‑end traceability.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Loss events are booked to GL accounts so that risk‑related losses appear in financial reporting.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the event.']
  message_log_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_message_log. Business justification: Fraud investigations require tracing the exact network message that triggered a risk event; FK provides audit linkage.']
  partner_ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Links risk events that affect a partner to the partner record, essential for incident response, fraud investigation, and regulatory incident reporting.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Needed to capture the payment product context in fraud event logs for the Regulatory Fraud Reporting process.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.pos_terminal. Business justification: Required for Terminal Fraud Monitoring Report: links each risk event to the terminal where the suspicious transaction originated, enabling terminal‑level risk analytics.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Required for FX volatility risk events to reference the exact rate used, enabling audit and compliance reporting.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Each fraud or compliance event originates from a specific gateway request; the link is required for incident investigation and remediation.']
  risk_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk rule or policy that fired the event.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch in which the event was recorded.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the risk event.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.wallet_device. Business justification: Device‑based fraud detection records the wallet_device used in a risk_event for device fingerprint analysis.']
  affected_entity_ref varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the affected entity (e.g., transaction_id, merchant_id).']
  affected_entity_type varchar [note: 'The type of business entity that the event pertains to.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value tied to the event (e.g., transaction amount, chargeback amount).']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing the audit log of changes to the event record.']
  confidence_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Probability confidence of the risk prediction (0‑1 range).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk event record was first persisted.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of escalation required based on severity and impact.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the event within the escalation workflow.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'System or process that generated the risk event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact point in time when the risk event occurred in the business domain.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the risk event (e.g., fraud, compliance, operational).']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a human manually overrode the automated decision.']
  is_threshold_exceeded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the observed value surpassed the defined threshold.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the jurisdiction impacted.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version of the machine‑learning model or rule set used for scoring.']
  payload varchar [note: 'Raw data or JSON payload describing the event details.']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'True if the event must be reported to a regulator (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Recommended or executed action to mitigate the risk event.']
  risk_dimension varchar [note: 'Specific risk domain impacted by the event (e.g., AML, sanctions, exposure).']
  risk_event_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text providing additional context or notes about the event.']
  risk_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk event record.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the risk severity as calculated by the risk engine.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined severity of the event, used for prioritization and escalation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the event data.']
  threshold_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Configured threshold that, when exceeded, triggers the risk event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the risk event record.']

  Note: 'Captures discrete risk events that trigger risk management actions — including chargeback ratio breaches, fraud rate spikes, exposure limit violations, sanctions hits, and behavioral anomalies. Each event records the triggering condition, severity level, affected entity, risk dimension, and escalation status. Feeds the risk case management workflow.'
}

Table risk.risk_case {
  risk_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the risk investigation case.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (customer) linked to the case.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (customer) linked to the case.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier who created the case record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Case investigations track monetary impact; linking to reference.currency ensures consistent currency attributes for audit.']
  detection_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fraud/AML rule that triggered the case.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dispute.case. Business justification: Internal risk investigations are often opened for a dispute; linking them enables unified case management.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk analyst responsible for the case.']
  fraud_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.fraud_case. Business justification: When a fraud incident triggers a broader risk investigation, the risk case references the originating fraud case for traceability.']
  last_updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last modified the case record.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant involved in the case.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Risk case investigations reference the affected payment product for the Case Management system and regulatory audit.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.pos_terminal. Business justification: Needed for Terminal Incident Investigation Report: associates each risk case with the specific POS terminal involved, supporting root‑cause analysis and remediation tracking.']
  primary_risk_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk analyst responsible for the case.']
  related_case_risk_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a linked case for cross‑reference or follow‑up.']
  response_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_response. Business justification: Cases are often opened on declined or flagged gateway responses; linking provides root‑cause context for investigators.']
  tertiary_risk_last_updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last modified the case record.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the risk case.']
  aml_status_at_time timestamp [note: 'AML screening status of the party at case creation.']
  case_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the case subject.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable case number used for tracking and reference.']
  case_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the case creation.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Category of the risk case indicating the originating risk domain.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case reached a final closed state.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the compliance review process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case record was first created in the system.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the triggering rule fired.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case was escalated to higher authority.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of escalation applied to the case.']
  evidence_count varchar [note: 'Number of evidence items attached to the case.']
  fraud_indicator_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of fraud indicator codes associated with the case.']
  investigation_summary varchar [note: 'Free‑text summary of investigative findings and actions.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'True if the case is flagged as high risk based on scoring thresholds.']
  kyc_status_at_time timestamp [note: 'KYC verification status of the party when the case was opened.']
  mitigation_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the remediation or mitigation action applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by analysts.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case was officially opened for investigation.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the case for handling urgency.']
  regulatory_flag boolean [note: 'True if the case requires regulatory reporting or review.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the case (e.g., approved, rejected, dismissed).']
  risk_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount representing the potential financial exposure associated with the case.']
  risk_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the risk model used for scoring.']
  risk_score_at_creation decimal(10,4) [note: 'Risk score calculated at the moment the case was created.']
  risk_score_current decimal(10,4) [note: 'Most recent risk score reflecting ongoing assessment.']
  sanction_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening at case creation.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating reflecting potential impact.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'More detailed classification within the main category.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Free‑form tags for ad‑hoc categorisation and search.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the case record.']

  Note: 'Operational case management for risk investigations — capturing case type, status lifecycle (open, under review, escalated, resolved, closed), assigned analyst, investigation evidence, mitigation actions taken, resolution outcome, and effectiveness review. SSOT for risk case workflow and intervention tracking.'
}

Table risk.concentration_exposure {
  concentration_exposure_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each concentration exposure record.']
  risk_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk model used to calculate the risk_score.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if the exposure exceeds its defined limit, otherwise false.']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Textual status describing the breach condition of the exposure.']
  concentration_exposure_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the exposure record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exposure record was first created in the system.']
  dimension_code varchar [note: 'Code or identifier representing the specific value within the chosen dimension (e.g., MCC code, ISO country code, merchant ID, BIN number, rail identifier).']
  dimension_type varchar [note: 'Category of concentration risk dimension (e.g., Merchant Category Code, geographic region, single merchant, BIN, settlement rail).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the exposure measurement becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the exposure measurement expires; null for open‑ended exposures.']
  exposure_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the current exposure for the dimension, expressed in the reporting currency.']
  exposure_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the exposure amount.']
  exposure_limit decimal(20,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed exposure for the dimension before breach, expressed in the reporting currency.']
  last_assessed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent risk assessment run that produced the current exposure values.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text field for comments, remediation actions, or analyst observations.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if this exposure must be included in regulatory capital or compliance reports.']
  risk_model_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the risk model applied to this exposure.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score associated with the concentration exposure, derived from the enterprise risk model.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the exposure data (e.g., Risk Management System, Transaction Processing Platform).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the exposure record.']
  utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the limit that is currently utilized (exposure_amount / exposure_limit * 100).']

  Note: 'Tracks portfolio-level concentration risk exposures by dimension — MCC concentration, geographic concentration, single-merchant TPV concentration, BIN-level exposure, and settlement rail concentration. Captures current exposure value, limit, utilization percentage, and breach status. Used for portfolio risk monitoring and regulatory capital adequacy reporting.'
}

Table risk.risk_exception {
  risk_exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the risk exception record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the requesting entity.']
  requestor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the requesting entity.']
  risk_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk policy that the exception overrides.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the approval process.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was approved.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authority that approved the exception.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Additional audit trail notes regarding the exception.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates compliance status of the exception with regulatory requirements.']
  conditions_attached varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or constraints attached to the exception.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exception record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code applicable to monetary thresholds.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the exception expires or is no longer valid.']
  exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exception.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Category of exception, e.g., policy deviation, model override, threshold waiver, manual approval.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes related to the exception.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of applying the exception to transactions.']
  overridden_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the overridden threshold.']
  overridden_threshold_value decimal(20,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the risk threshold that is overridden by the exception.']
  rationale varchar [note: 'Business justification for granting the exception.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception must be reported to regulators.']
  requestor_entity varchar [note: 'Entity that requested the exception.']
  requestor_role varchar [note: 'Role of the requestor within the requesting entity.']
  risk_score_after decimal(10,4) [note: 'Risk score after applying the exception.']
  risk_score_before decimal(10,4) [note: 'Risk score that would have applied without the exception.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the exception becomes effective.']
  transaction_amount_limit_override decimal(20,4) [note: 'Override for the maximum transaction amount under the exception.']
  transaction_volume_limit_override varchar [note: 'Override for the maximum number of transactions allowed under the exception.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that last updated the exception record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the exception record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the exception record.']

  Note: 'Records approved exceptions to standard risk policies and underwriting rules — capturing exception type, requesting entity, exception rationale, approving authority, exception validity period, conditions attached, and exception outcome. Enables governance oversight of risk policy deviations and supports regulatory audit of exception management.'
}

Table risk.indicator {
  indicator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each risk indicator record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Indicators may be measured in specific currencies; FK provides official currency details for dashboards.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary owner or steward of the indicator.']
  owner_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary owner or steward of the indicator.']
  risk_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk model that produces or influences this indicator.']
  breach_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the indicator has breached thresholds within the current reporting window.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value currently violates any threshold.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the current breach, if any.']
  breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent breach occurrence.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards framework that mandates tracking of this indicator.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the indicator record was first created.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) reflecting the quality of source data for this indicator.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplies the raw data for the indicator.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the indicator definition was retired (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the indicator definition became active.']
  indicator_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what the indicator measures and why it matters.']
  indicator_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the risk indicator.']
  indicator_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the indicator definition.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'True if the indicator record is archived and excluded from active reporting.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the indicator definition was last reviewed.']
  measured_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual value recorded for the indicator at the measurement timestamp.']
  measurement_formula varchar [note: 'Mathematical or logical expression used to calculate the indicator value.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the indicator is measured or refreshed.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the indicator value was captured.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the indicator.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for the indicator.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the indicator must be reported to a regulator.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the indicator definition is formally reviewed.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High‑level risk domain to which the indicator belongs.']
  risk_model_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the associated risk model.']
  threshold_amber_high decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the amber (caution) range for the indicator.']
  threshold_amber_low decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the amber (caution) range for the indicator.']
  threshold_green_high decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the green (acceptable) range for the indicator.']
  threshold_green_low decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the green (acceptable) range for the indicator.']
  threshold_red_high decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the red (breach) range for the indicator.']
  threshold_red_low decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the red (breach) range for the indicator.']
  trend_direction varchar [note: 'Observed direction of change compared to previous periods.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit applied to the indicator value.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the indicator record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Key Risk Indicator (KRI) definitions and their time-series measurement readings. Captures indicator name, measurement formula, data source, frequency, threshold bands (green/amber/red), measured values, trend direction, and breach history. Provides operational data for KRI dashboards and risk committee reporting.'
}

Table risk.credit_limit {
  credit_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the credit limit record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Credit limits are currency‑specific; linking to reference.currency enables correct limit calculations and reporting.']
  partner_ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Supports credit limit management per partner, required for credit exposure limits and AML/KYC compliance reporting.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant or cardholder to which the credit limit applies.']
  underwriting_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underwriting policy that governed the limit decision.']
  available_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining credit available for new transactions (limit_amount minus utilization_amount).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit limit record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the credit limit becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the credit limit expires or is scheduled to be terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount approved for the party, expressed in the specified currency.']
  limit_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the credit limit (amount, type, or status).']
  limit_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the credit limit is scheduled to expire if not renewed.']
  limit_reset_date date [note: 'Date on which a revolving credit limit resets its utilization cycle.']
  limit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for periodic review of the credit limit.']
  limit_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the credit limit record (e.g., system‑generated, manual entry, API import).']
  limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit limit.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Category of credit limit: revolving, installment, or buy‑now‑pay‑later.']
  risk_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the risk scoring model applied at limit assignment.']
  risk_score_at_assignment decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk model score used when the credit limit was originally approved.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credit limit record.']
  utilization_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current amount of credit that has been used against the limit.']

  Note: 'Credit limit assignments for merchants and cardholders — capturing approved credit line amount, currency, credit limit type (revolving, installment, BNPL), utilization amount, available credit, limit review date, and limit change history trigger. Owned by risk domain as the underwriting output that governs payment authorization credit decisions. Distinct from exposure_limit which governs TPV and settlement exposure.'
}

Table risk.mitigation_action {
  mitigation_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the risk mitigation action record.']
  action_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team responsible for executing the mitigation action.']
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.cross_border_payment. Business justification: Mitigation actions are often triggered by specific cross‑border payments; linking enables end‑to‑end audit of actions.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Mitigation actions may adjust limits in a currency; FK ensures accurate before/after amounts.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User or system that created the record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Financial impact of mitigation actions is posted to a GL account for audit, financial statements, and cost tracking.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Mitigation actions are tied to the specific payment product they remediate, required for the Product‑Specific Risk Mitigation KPI.']
  primary_employee_id varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update.']
  related_mitigation_action_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a related mitigation action, if any.']
  risk_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk event or case that triggered this mitigation action.']
  risk_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk model used to evaluate the risk before action.']
  risk_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific risk rule that triggered the action.']
  action_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the mitigation action.']
  action_reference varchar [note: 'Business reference code for the mitigation action, used for external reporting.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Type of risk mitigation action taken.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the action complies with regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the mitigation action record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the mitigation action becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the mitigation action expires or is scheduled for review.']
  effectiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score assessing the effectiveness of the mitigation action.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the mitigation action was performed manually (true) or automatically (false).']
  limit_after_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary exposure limit after action.']
  limit_before_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary exposure limit before action.']
  mitigation_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the mitigation action and its purpose.']
  mitigation_action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the mitigation action.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the mitigation action.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of regulatory approval for the mitigation action.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date when the mitigation action effectiveness was reviewed.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Notes from the effectiveness review.']
  risk_score_after decimal(10,4) [note: 'Risk score after the mitigation action was applied.']
  risk_score_before decimal(10,4) [note: 'Risk score of the entity before the mitigation action was applied.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the mitigation action originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Records risk mitigation actions taken in response to risk events, assessment findings, or KRI breaches — capturing action type (reserve increase, TPV cap reduction, MCC restriction, account suspension, enhanced monitoring), action status, triggering risk event or case, implementation date, and effectiveness review date. Provides the operational record of risk management interventions.'
}

Table risk.model_validation {
  model_validation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each risk model validation record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Validation reports include currency‑based thresholds; linking to reference.currency standardizes reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier that created the validation record.']
  risk_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk model that was validated.']
  auc_roc decimal(5,4) [note: 'Area under the Receiver Operating Characteristic curve for the model.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of compliance controls the model must satisfy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the validation record was first created in the system.']
  data_snapshot_date date [note: 'Date of the data snapshot used for validation.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the data used for validation.']
  exposure_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary exposure the model is allowed to approve.']
  gini_coefficient decimal(5,4) [note: 'Statistical measure of model discrimination power.']
  governance_signoff varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person/committee that signed off the validation.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the validation was performed automatically by the platform.']
  ks_statistic decimal(5,4) [note: 'Maximum distance between cumulative distributions of scores for good vs. bad outcomes.']
  model_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithmic technique employed (e.g., XGBoost, Neural Network).']
  model_deployment_environment varchar [note: 'Environment where the model is deployed.']
  model_feature_set_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the feature set used to train the model.']
  model_input_schema_version varchar [note: 'Version of the input data schema expected by the model.']
  model_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or team responsible for the model.']
  model_training_data_version varchar [note: 'Version of the training dataset used for the model.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the risk model at the time of validation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations.']
  outcome_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the validation outcome, including any conditions or concerns.']
  performance_summary varchar [note: 'Free‑text summary of validation results and observations.']
  psi decimal(5,4) [note: 'Metric indicating shift in score distribution between training and validation data.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required regulatory sign‑off for the validation.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification derived from validation results.']
  risk_model_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the risk model (e.g., credit, fraud).']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(10,2) [note: 'Score threshold used by the model to trigger risk actions.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of records evaluated during validation.']
  signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the governance sign‑off was recorded.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user identifier that last modified the validation record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the validation record.']
  validation_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total runtime of the validation process in seconds.']
  validation_error_count varchar [note: 'Number of errors encountered during validation.']
  validation_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the methodology (e.g., back‑testing, hold‑out, statistical test) used for the validation.']
  validation_outcome varchar [note: 'Governance decision after validation.']
  validation_report_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed validation report document.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the validation record.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the validation activity was executed.']
  validation_type varchar [note: 'Category of validation activity performed.']
  validation_warning_count varchar [note: 'Number of warnings generated during validation.']

  Note: 'Records of risk model validation activities — capturing validation type (initial validation, periodic review, champion/challenger test), validation methodology, performance metrics assessed (Gini coefficient, KS statistic, PSI, AUC-ROC), validation outcome (approved, conditionally approved, rejected), and model governance sign-off. Supports model risk management (MRM) governance requirements.'
}

Table risk.counterparty_risk_limit {
  counterparty_risk_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each counterparty risk limit record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Counterparty limits are set per currency; FK to reference.currency provides authoritative currency attributes.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User or system that created the record.']
  forward_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_forward_contract. Business justification: Counterparty limits are hedged with forward contracts; linking tracks which contract backs each limit.']
  risk_model_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the risk model used to calculate the risk score.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the risk limit agreement.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the limit was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person/role that approved the limit.']
  collateral_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty must provide collateral for the limit.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'Type of collateral pledged against the risk limit.']
  collateral_value decimal(20,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the pledged collateral.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Textual description of compliance conditions attached to the limit.']
  counterparty_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the counterparty (e.g., acquiring bank, issuing bank, PSP).']
  counterparty_risk_limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the risk limit agreement.']
  counterparty_type varchar [note: 'Category of the counterparty entity involved in the risk limit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk limit record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the risk limit becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the risk limit expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  last_utilization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to limit utilization.']
  limit_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Approved maximum exposure amount for the counterparty.']
  limit_code varchar [note: 'External reference code or number assigned to the risk limit agreement.']
  limit_tenor_days varchar [note: 'Maximum duration, in days, that the limit is valid for a single exposure.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the exposure the limit governs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the limit.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory body’s sign‑off status for the limit.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk score assigned to the counterparty for this limit.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  utilization_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Current amount of the limit that has been utilized.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the approved limit that is currently used.']

  Note: 'Bilateral risk limit agreements with acquiring banks, issuing banks, card schemes, and PSP partners — capturing counterparty entity, limit type (settlement exposure, intraday credit, FX exposure), approved limit amount, currency, limit tenor, collateral requirements, and limit utilization. Distinct from exposure_limit (which governs merchant/cardholder limits) — this governs institutional counterparty risk.'
}

Table risk.rule_assignment {
  rule_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the risk_rule_assignment association']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the payment_product entity']
  risk_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the risk_rule entity']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule assignment becomes active for the payment product']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule assignment expires or is no longer applied']
  rule_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule‑product assignment (e.g., active, inactive, pending)']

  Note: 'This association captures the operational assignment of risk decisioning rules to payment products. Each record links one risk_rule to one payment_product and stores the effective period and current status of that assignment.. Existence Justification: Risk rules are actively assigned to payment products by risk operations. A single risk_rule can be applied to many payment_product records, and each payment_product can have multiple risk_rule assignments, each with its own effective dates and status.'
}

Table risk.model_currency_pair_assignment {
  model_currency_pair_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ModelCurrencyPairAssignment association']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the currency pair']
  risk_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the risk model']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the model becomes active for the currency pair']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the model ceases to be active for the currency pair']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the risk model used for this assignment']

  Note: 'This association product captures the assignment of a risk scoring model to a specific currency pair, including the period during which the model is effective and the version of the model applied. Each record links one risk_model to one currency_pair and stores relationship‑specific attributes.. Existence Justification: Risk models are actively assigned to multiple currency pairs, and each currency pair can be evaluated by several risk models over time. The assignment is managed as a distinct operational record that includes effective dates and model version information. This many‑to‑many relationship is therefore a business concept that is created, updated, and retired by risk‑management teams.'
}

Table risk.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for party']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the party\'s primary physical address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the party\'s primary physical address (optional).']
  annual_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all transactions for the party in the most recent calendar year.']
  annual_transaction_volume varchar [note: 'Total number of transactions processed for the party in the most recent calendar year.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the party\'s primary address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the party\'s primary address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the party record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for monetary amounts associated with the party.']
  customer_since_date date [note: 'Date when the party first became a customer of Payments Fintech.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of an individual party.']
  external_party_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external partner or legacy system to reference the same party.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry classification (e.g., NAICS) for the party.']
  is_blacklisted boolean [note: 'True if the party is flagged for fraud or regulatory blacklisting.']
  kyc_completed_date date [note: 'Date when KYC verification was successfully completed.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the party\'s KYC verification process.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the party as registered with governing authorities.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'National identity document number (e.g., SSN, NIN).']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party indicating its role in the payments ecosystem.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the party\'s primary address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Main email address used for electronic communication with the party.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for reaching the party.']
  region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the party\'s primary address.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number for corporate or legal entities.']
  risk_last_assessed timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent risk evaluation for the party.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Categorical risk tier derived from the risk score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk rating (0‑100) used for underwriting and transaction decisioning.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment or market cohort the party belongs to.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream operational system that supplied the party record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the party within the payments ecosystem.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the party.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Commonly used business name or brand under which the party operates.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the party record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for party. Referenced by party_id.'
}

Table risk.risk_policy {
  risk_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for risk_policy']
  risk_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the risk scoring model used by this policy.']
  superseded_risk_policy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on risk_policy (superseded_risk_policy_id)']
  applicable_merchant_category_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of MCC codes to which the policy applies.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions exceeding thresholds require manual approval.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit actions or manual overrides related to the policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary limits defined in the policy.']
  daily_exposure_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary exposure limit for a merchant or account within a single day.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the policy purpose and scope.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the policy becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the policy expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended policies.']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the person or team to notify when policy breaches occur.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for escalation notifications.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) (e.g., country codes) where the policy is enforced.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the policy was last reviewed for relevance and compliance.']
  monthly_exposure_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary exposure limit for a merchant or account within a calendar month.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the policy in external systems.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the risk policy.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Category of risk policy indicating the primary risk domain it governs.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory policy reviews.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold for the composite risk score that triggers policy enforcement.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy.']
  transaction_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value allowed per individual transaction under this policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the policy record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the policy for change management.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for risk_policy. Referenced by risk_policy_id.'
}

Table dispute.chargeback {
  chargeback_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for the chargeback record in the dispute domain. Serves as the SSOT identifier for all chargeback lifecycle tracking per card scheme rules (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover).']
  authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.authorization. Business justification: Original authorization data is required to validate the auth amount, response codes, and 3‑DS results during chargeback assessment.']
  capture_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.capture. Business justification: Capture details (final amount, fees) are essential for reconciling chargeback amounts and settlement adjustments.']
  chargeback_reason_code_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the card scheme reason code record in the reference domain that classifies the basis for the chargeback (e.g., fraud, not as described, duplicate processing, credit not processed). Each card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover) maintains its own reason code taxonomy.']
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.cross_border_payment. Business justification: Cross‑border chargebacks must reference the originating payment to satisfy audit, compliance, and settlement reconciliation.']
  dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent dispute case record that initiated this chargeback filing. A dispute may progress through retrieval request, first chargeback, second chargeback, and arbitration stages — this links the chargeback to its overarching dispute case.']
  dispute_dispute_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent dispute case record that initiated this chargeback filing. A dispute may progress through retrieval request, first chargeback, second chargeback, and arbitration stages — this links the chargeback to its overarching dispute case.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the issuing bank (cardholder\'s bank) that initiated the chargeback on behalf of the cardholder. The issuing bank is the party that files the chargeback against the acquiring bank per card scheme rules.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the General Ledger entry in Oracle Financials (ERP) recording the financial adjustment associated with this chargeback. Enables reconciliation between the dispute management system and the financial accounting system for SOX compliance and revenue recognition.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchant record against whom the chargeback has been filed. The Merchant Identification Number (MID) is the acquiring bank-assigned identifier for the merchant in the payment network.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Chargeback performance dashboard requires product‑level metrics for risk analysis and scheme reporting; linking each chargeback to its payment_product enables this.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: Chargeback investigations need the detailed payment transaction record for settlement, network, and device info – a standard compliance and reporting requirement.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.pos_terminal. Business justification: Chargeback Terminal Performance Report requires linking each chargeback to its originating POS terminal to monitor terminal risk and compliance.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Chargeback settlement report requires original FX rate for currency conversion of the chargeback amount.']
  response_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_response. Business justification: Chargeback reconciliation needs the original acquirer response to verify decline reason, authorization code, and fraud flags.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Chargebacks are filed under a specific card scheme; scheme_id enables scheme‑level chargeback reporting required by Visa/Mastercard compliance.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement. Business justification: Chargeback settlement reports must link to the settlement record that includes the chargeback amount for traceability.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating payment transaction record in the transaction domain that is the subject of this chargeback filing. Links the chargeback to the full transaction detail including authorization, capture, and settlement data.']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.wallet_transaction. Business justification: Chargeback processing for wallet transactions requires linking each chargeback to the specific wallet transaction that was disputed, enabling accurate reconciliation and reporting.']
  acquirer_reference varchar [note: 'Acquiring bank\'s internal reference number for this chargeback case. Used by the acquiring bank\'s operations team to track the chargeback in their internal systems and correlate with the card scheme case identifier.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net financial adjustment amount posted to the merchant\'s account as a result of this chargeback. Includes the chargeback amount plus any applicable scheme fees, processing fees, or interchange adjustments. Negative value indicates a debit to the merchant; positive value indicates a credit (e.g., upon successful representment).']
  amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross amount of the chargeback as filed by the issuing bank in the transaction currency. Represents the disputed amount that will be debited from the merchant\'s account if the chargeback is upheld. May differ from the original transaction amount in cases of partial chargebacks.']
  arbitration_deadline timestamp [note: 'Scheme-mandated deadline for filing an arbitration case with the card scheme after a failed pre-arbitration or second chargeback cycle. Arbitration is the final escalation path where the card scheme adjudicates the dispute and assigns liability. Missing this deadline forfeits the right to arbitration.']
  arbitration_filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the arbitration case was filed with the card scheme. Populated only when the chargeback has been escalated to the arbitration stage (cycle_stage = ARBITRATION).']
  arn varchar [note: 'Acquirer Reference Number uniquely identifying the originating payment transaction across the card scheme network.
