  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the notification template.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority for sending the notification.']
  schedule_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for sending the notification when using scheduled delivery.']
  sender_email varchar [note: 'Email address used as the sender for email notifications.']
  sender_name varchar [note: 'Name shown as the sender of the notification.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the template.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Subject text used for email or push notifications.']
  type varchar [note: 'Channel or medium for which the template is designed (e.g., email, SMS, push notification).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the template.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the template for change management.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the template record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for notification_template. Referenced by template_id.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_aml_screening_result {
  compliance_aml_screening_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each AML screening result record.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Needed for AML reporting to map residence country to standardized country reference for regulatory filings.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Regulatory AML screening reports are generated per wallet account; linking enables reporting and audit of wallet‑specific AML outcomes.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the screened entity (transaction, cardholder, merchant, or partner).']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Internal AML screening of employees; link needed to identify screened staff for SAR reporting.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: AML screening results are governed by a regulatory obligation; linking enables obligation‑centric reporting.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: AML screening result must be traceable to the originating gateway request for audit and regulatory reporting.']
  analyst_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an analyst manually overrode the automated disposition.']
  analyst_override_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation provided by the analyst for the override.']
  compliance_aml_screening_result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the screening result record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the screening result record was first created in the data lake.']
  customer_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the individual associated with the screened entity.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Outcome of the screening: pass, requires manual review, or block.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of business entity that was screened.']
  is_sar_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) must be filed.']
  is_str_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) must be filed.']
  match_type varchar [note: 'Category of data element that triggered a match during screening.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations related to the screening.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned by the AML engine (0.00 – 100.00).']
  sar_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the SAR filing process.']
  sar_filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SAR was filed, if applicable.']
  screening_engine varchar [note: 'Name of the AML screening engine or vendor used (e.g., World-Check, LexisNexis).']
  screening_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the rule set applied during the screening.']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the AML screening was performed.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or component within the source system that executed the screening.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system that generated the screening result (e.g., Risk and Compliance Management System).']
  str_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the STR filing process.']
  str_filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the STR was filed, if applicable.']
  typology varchar [note: 'AML typology or pattern identified (e.g., structuring, smurfing, terrorist financing).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the screening result record.']

  Note: 'SSOT for Anti-Money Laundering screening outcomes. Records each AML screening event against a transaction, cardholder, or merchant entity — including the screening engine used, risk score assigned, matched typology, disposition (pass/review/block), analyst override, and regulatory obligation reference. Sourced from the Risk and Compliance Management System AML module.'
}

Table compliance.sanctions_check {
  sanctions_check_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each sanctions screening event.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Sanctions screening is performed on each wallet; FK provides direct traceability for compliance investigations and regulator filings.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (cardholder, merchant, partner, beneficiary) screened.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Name of the compliance officer responsible for reviewing the hit.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Sanctions screening must record which payment product was used to support product‑level risk analytics.']
  primary_sanctions_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with this screening, if applicable.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Enables linking sanction checks to jurisdiction metadata for compliance reporting and risk scoring.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch in which the screening was executed, if applicable.']
  watchlist_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.watchlist_entry. Business justification: Sanctions checks reference entries in the watchlist master; linking provides source of the hit.']
  block_action_taken varchar [note: 'Action taken as a result of the hit (e.g., blocked transaction, allowed, sent for review).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sanctions check record was first created.']
  false_positive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hit was later determined to be a false positive.']
  hit_details varchar [note: 'Details of the matched sanctioned entity (e.g., name, address).']
  hit_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the screening produced a potential match against the sanctions list.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the screening result is considered high‑risk and requires immediate attention.']
  list_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sanctions list used for the screening (e.g., OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated, UN).']
  list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the sanctions list at the time of screening.']
  match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical confidence score (0‑100) indicating the strength of the match.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes added by analysts or reviewers.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the screened party.']
  reporting_obligation varchar [note: 'Regulatory filing triggered by the hit (Suspicious Activity Report, Suspicious Transaction Report, or None).']
  reporting_obligation_filed boolean [note: 'True if the required regulatory report was filed.']
  review_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the compliance review.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance officer completed the review.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk score combining match score and other risk factors.']
  screening_method varchar [note: 'Algorithmic method used for the screening.']
  screening_source varchar [note: 'System or module that executed the screening (e.g., Risk and Compliance Management System).']
  screening_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the screening.']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the sanctions screening was performed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sanctions check record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Records each OFAC/SDN and global sanctions screening event performed against a party (cardholder, merchant, partner, beneficiary). Captures the list version screened (SDN, EU Consolidated, UN), match score, hit details, false-positive determination, blocking action taken, and OFAC reporting obligation triggered. SSOT for sanctions screening history.'
}

Table compliance.sar_filing {
  sar_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SAR filing record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Standardizes currency for SAR filing amounts, supporting multi‑currency reporting and conversion.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance officer who filed the SAR.']
  filing_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance officer who filed the SAR.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: SAR filings often relate to a specific payment product (e.g., card vs ACH) for product‑level AML reporting.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Provides authoritative jurisdiction reference for SAR filing jurisdiction field, required for regulator reports.']
  related_case_compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a related internal case or investigation.']
  related_compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a related internal case or investigation.']
  activity_end_date date [note: 'Date when the suspicious activity ended.']
  activity_start_date date [note: 'Date when the suspicious activity began.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the suspicious activity.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the amendment for this SAR.']
  amount_involved decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value associated with the suspicious activity.']
  attachment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documents attached to the filing.']
  compliance_review_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance review was completed.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the internal compliance review of the SAR.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification level indicating data sensitivity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SAR record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_retention_expiry date [note: 'Date after which the SAR record must be purged per retention policy.']
  filing_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SAR was submitted to the regulator.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'External tracking number assigned by BSA for the filing.']
  filing_officer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the officer responsible for the filing.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the SAR with the regulator.']
  is_amended boolean [note: 'True if this filing is an amendment to a prior SAR.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing is flagged as high risk.']
  narrative_summary varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative describing the suspicious activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional remarks.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority to which the SAR was submitted.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score generated by the compliance scoring engine.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the SAR (e.g., FraudDetectionPlatform).']
  subject_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the subject entity.']
  subject_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the subject\'s residence.']
  subject_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual or organization subject to the SAR.']
  subject_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the subject entity.']
  subject_identifier varchar [note: 'Government or tax identifier of the subject (e.g., SSN, EIN).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SAR record.']

  Note: 'Suspicious Activity Report filing record — the authoritative record of each SAR submitted to FinCEN or equivalent regulator. Captures the subject entity, suspicious activity type, activity date range, filing date, BSA tracking number, narrative summary, filing officer, and acknowledgement receipt. Supports regulatory examination and audit trail requirements under BSA.'
}

Table compliance.str_filing {
  str_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each STR filing.']
  compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal investigation case associated with the filing.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Ensures STR filing amounts use a normalized currency reference for cross‑border reporting.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the legal entity that submitted the filing.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: STR filings need the payment product context to assess transaction‑type risk and regulatory metrics.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Links STR filing jurisdiction to reference jurisdiction for consistent regulatory reporting.']
  acknowledgment_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier returned by the regulator confirming receipt of the filing.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time the regulator confirmed receipt of the filing.']
  aml_flag boolean [note: 'True if the filing is primarily driven by AML considerations.']
  compliance_officer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the compliance officer overseeing the filing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the filing record was initially captured.']
  ctf_flag boolean [note: 'True if the filing involves potential counter‑terrorism financing concerns.']
  description_of_suspicion varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative explaining the reasons for suspicion.']
  filing_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the filing was officially submitted to the regulator.']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the regulator or internal system for the filing.']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the filing according to the governing regulatory regime.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'True if the filing has been escalated as high risk.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary comments or observations added by compliance analysts.']
  prior_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of an earlier filing that this record amends or follows up.']
  regulator_name varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority (e.g., FCA, AUSTRAC) to which the STR is filed.']
  reporting_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the organization filing the STR.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that originated the filing.']
  str_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the STR filing.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time the filing was transmitted to the regulator.']
  suspicious_indicator_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of indicator codes that describe why the transaction is considered suspicious. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AML|CTF|STRUCTURING|SMURFING|UNUSUAL_ACTIVITY|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial amount involved in the suspicious transaction.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the suspicious transaction occurred.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the suspicious activity report (e.g., ARN, MID).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the filing record.']

  Note: 'Suspicious Transaction Report filing record for jurisdictions outside the US (e.g., FCA UK, AUSTRAC, MAS Singapore). Captures the reporting entity, subject transaction references, suspicious indicators, filing date, regulator acknowledgement number, and cross-border CTF flags. Distinct from SAR due to differing regulatory schemas and jurisdictional requirements.'
}

  primary_contact_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Primary contact detail (email address) used for KYC communication.']
  provider_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier assigned by the verification provider.']
  re_verification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the KYC must be refreshed or re‑verified.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned based on KYC results.']
  sanctions_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of OFAC/SDN sanctions screening.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the KYC verification record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the KYC verification.']
  verification_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the verification after processing.']
  verification_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party provider performing verification.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the KYC verification process.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when verification was completed.']

  Note: 'Know Your Customer identity verification record for cardholders, merchants, and business entities. Captures verification method (document check, biometric, database lookup), identity document type and number, verification provider, outcome (pass/fail/pending), risk tier assigned, re-verification due date, and CDD/EDD classification. SSOT for KYC status across the platform.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_obligation {
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the regulatory obligation record.']
  amendment_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the obligation has been amended since its original issuance.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with the obligation is mandatory, optional, or voluntary.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance outcome for the obligation.']
  control_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit, department, or role responsible for ensuring compliance with the obligation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the obligation record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the official regulatory document or guidance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the obligation becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the obligation expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  enforcement_penalty varchar [note: 'Description of penalties, fines, or sanctions associated with non‑compliance.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the authority that issued the obligation (e.g., PCI Security Standards Council, EU PSD2, OFAC).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the jurisdiction to which the obligation applies.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the obligation was most recently reviewed.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the obligation (active, inactive, pending, retired).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next compliance review.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used internally to reference the obligation.']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the regulatory obligation as defined by the governing body.']
  regulation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the regulatory instrument (law, directive, standard, or guideline).']
  regulatory_obligation_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the regulatory requirement, including scope and key provisions.']
  review_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the obligation is reviewed for relevance and compliance.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the obligation based on potential impact and likelihood of non‑compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the obligation record (e.g., Risk and Compliance Management System).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the obligation record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the obligation (e.g., v1.0, v2.3).']

  Note: 'Master registry of all regulatory obligations applicable to the business — laws, directives, rules, and standards (PCI DSS, PSD2, BSA, GDPR, CCPA, Reg E, Reg II, OFAC, SOX). Each record defines the obligation name, governing body, jurisdiction, effective date, applicability scope, control owner, and review cadence. Serves as the compliance obligation inventory for control mapping.'
}

Table compliance.control {
  control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the compliance control.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Compliance control budgeting uses cost centers for internal financial reporting; linking enables allocation of control costs to the appropriate cost center.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Posting expenses or adjustments from compliance activities requires a GL account; this FK supports accurate financial statements and audit trails.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Control ownership assigned to a specific employee; needed for control accountability reports.']
  owner_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Control ownership tied to org unit; needed for departmental responsibility reporting.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each compliance control implements one or more regulatory obligations; linking provides direct mapping.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Reference to audit log entries related to the control.']
  control_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the control across systems.']
  control_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the control\'s purpose and scope.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the compliance control.']
  control_scope varchar [note: 'Business area or process that the control applies to.']
  control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the control based on its nature: preventive, detective, or corrective.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was created in the system.']
  criticality varchar [note: 'Criticality level of the control for business continuity.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the control becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the control expires or is superseded, if applicable.']
  evidence_collected boolean [note: 'True if required evidence has been collected for the latest assessment.']
  evidence_required varchar [note: 'Description of the evidence needed to demonstrate control execution.']
  evidence_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored evidence artifact.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the control is performed or reviewed.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'True if the control is performed automatically by systems.']
  is_key_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control is considered a key control for regulatory reporting.']
  is_manual boolean [note: 'True if the control requires manual execution.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent assessment of the control.']
  last_assessment_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent control assessment.']
  next_assessment_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next control assessment.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) that the control satisfies.']
  regulatory_requirement_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific regulatory requirement mapped to this control.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Planned action to address control deficiencies.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remediation effort.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the control based on its impact and likelihood.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the control\'s risk level.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the control information.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated tags for categorizing the control (e.g., finance, security, privacy).']
  testing_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to test the effectiveness of the control.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the control definition.']

  Note: 'Defines each internal compliance control implemented to satisfy one or more regulatory obligations. Captures control ID, control name, control type (preventive/detective/corrective), frequency, owner, mapped obligation references, testing methodology, and last assessment outcome. Forms the control library used in PCI DSS, SOX, and regulatory examination responses.'
}

Table compliance.control_assessment {
  control_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each control assessment record.']
  assessor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Assessment performed by an employee; required for audit of control testing.']
  control_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the compliance control being assessed.']

  assessment_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency with which the control is assessed.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the assessment was performed.']
  assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail result of the control assessment.']
  assessment_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal or calendar period the assessment covers (e.g., Q1‑2023).']
  assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time (with timezone) when the assessment activity occurred.']
  assessor_role varchar [note: 'Role or function of the assessor within the assessment process.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed audit log for this assessment.']
  control_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the assessment record.']
  control_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory or framework category to which the control belongs.']
  control_effectiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the assessed effectiveness of the control.']
  control_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or individual accountable for the control.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the control based on its function.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  evidence_collected varchar [note: 'Description of documentation, logs, or artifacts gathered as proof of control operation.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an exception to the control was granted.']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why an exception was allowed.']
  finding_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating assigned to any deficiency identified during the assessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations recorded by the assessor.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment record.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory regime governing the control.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed corrective activity to resolve the finding.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which remediation must be completed.']
  remediation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective action is required to address a finding.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating derived from the assessment findings.']
  testing_approach varchar [note: 'Methodology used to test the control (e.g., walkthrough, sampling, automated scan).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assessment record.']

  Note: 'Records each periodic testing or assessment of a compliance control — including assessment date, assessor (internal audit, QSA, external auditor), testing approach, evidence collected, finding severity, remediation required flag, and pass/fail outcome. Provides the audit trail for control effectiveness over time and supports PCI DSS QSA assessments and SOX testing cycles.'
}

Table compliance.pci_dss_audit {
  pci_dss_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each PCI DSS audit record.']
  audit_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier used by the QSA for the audit report document.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.pos_terminal. Business justification: PCI DSS audit reports must reference the exact POS terminal audited for compliance reporting and remediation.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: PCI DSS audits are performed to satisfy regulatory obligations; linking enables obligation‑centric audit tracking.']
  assessment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the on‑site assessment concluded.']
  assessment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the on‑site assessment began.']
  assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp of the assessment completion event.']
  audit_document_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA‑256) of the stored audit report.']
  audit_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the audit (e.g., "2024 Annual ROC").']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the QSA or internal tracking system.']
  audit_source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated the audit record (e.g., Risk and Compliance Management System).']
  audit_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level narrative summarizing audit results.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the audit is a Report on Compliance (ROC) or a Self‑Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ).']
  audit_version varchar [note: 'Version string for the audit (e.g., "v1.0", "v2.1").']
  cde_boundary_description varchar [note: 'Technical description of the CDE boundaries assessed.']
  compensating_controls_count varchar [note: 'Count of approved compensating controls for unmet requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Internal classification of the audit data.']
  findings_count varchar [note: 'Total count of non‑conformities discovered.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the primary jurisdiction.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the audit record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information.']
  overall_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric compliance score expressed as a percentage.']
  overall_compliance_status varchar [note: 'High‑level compliance outcome of the audit.']
  qsa_firm_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the QSA firm contact.']
  qsa_firm_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the QSA firm contact.']
  qsa_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the QSA firm that performed the audit.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'PCI standard or related framework governing the audit.']
  remediation_deadline date [note: 'Target date for completing all remediation actions.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation activities.']
  report_file_name varchar [note: 'File name of the submitted audit report.']
  report_file_path varchar [note: 'Path or URI where the audit report is stored.']
  report_submission_date date [note: 'Date the ROC or SAQ report was submitted to the acquiring bank or regulator.']
  requirements_tested varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of PCI DSS requirement numbers examined.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating derived from audit findings.']
  saq_type varchar [note: 'Specific SAQ variant applied when audit_type = SAQ.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the CDE scope covered by the audit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record.']

  Note: 'PCI DSS compliance audit record capturing each annual or interim assessment cycle — QSA firm, assessment scope (SAQ type or full ROC), cardholder data environment (CDE) boundaries assessed, requirements tested, findings, compensating controls, and Report on Compliance (ROC) or Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) submission details. SSOT for PCI DSS compliance posture.'
}

  Note: 'Records each GDPR or CCPA data subject rights request received — request type (access, erasure, portability, rectification, restriction, objection), requestor identity, verification status, request receipt date, regulatory response deadline (30-day GDPR / 45-day CCPA), fulfillment actions taken, and closure date. SSOT for data subject rights compliance lifecycle.'
}

Table compliance.consent_record {
  consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each consent record.']
  consent_id varchar [note: 'External identifier for the consent agreement, often a UUID or reference number used in audit and reporting.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Maps consent geo‑country code to reference country for jurisdiction‑specific consent compliance.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Consent record creation logged by employee; needed for data governance audit.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device (e.g., mobile device ID) used during consent capture.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the data subject (cardholder or merchant) who gave the consent.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Consent records are collected to meet specific regulatory obligations; linking clarifies which obligation the consent satisfies.']
  collection_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the consent was obtained (e.g., website, mobile app, in‑store terminal, call centre, email).']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consent record.']
  consent_text_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the consent text version to ensure integrity and support audit.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the consent was recorded.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of consent covering marketing, data sharing, open banking, or Strong Customer Authentication (SCA).']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the consent text to support change management and re‑consent tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  data_processing_purpose varchar [note: 'Narrative description of why the data is being processed under this consent.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the data may be retained after consent, as defined by policy.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent becomes legally effective.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Optional expiry timestamp; null if consent is open‑ended.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'Higher‑level region or jurisdiction (e.g., EU, APAC) associated with the consent capture.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device used to provide consent, required for audit and fraud investigations.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code of the consent text presented to the data subject.']
  lawful_basis varchar [note: 'Legal justification for processing under GDPR/CCPA (e.g., consent, contract, legal obligation, legitimate interest).']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the consent was provided by a cardholder (consumer) or a merchant (business).']
  reconsent_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the data subject must provide renewed consent, if required.']
  reconsent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a new consent must be obtained due to regulatory or policy changes.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Describes the data processing activities covered by the consent (e.g., marketing, analytics, third‑party sharing, risk assessment).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that captured the consent (e.g., Digital Wallet Platform, Merchant Management System).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or service account that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the consent record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string providing context about the client environment.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason provided by the data subject for withdrawing consent.']
  withdrawal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the data subject withdrew the consent, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of data processing consent obtained from cardholders and merchants under GDPR, PSD2, and CCPA. Captures consent type (marketing, data sharing, open banking, SCA), consent version, collection channel, timestamp, IP address, withdrawal status, and re-consent triggers. Supports lawful basis documentation and data subject rights fulfillment.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_filing {
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory filing record.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory filing overseen by compliance officer employee; essential for filing responsibility tracking.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Normalizes filing currency to reference table for accurate financial aggregation.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Provides a stable jurisdiction key for regulatory filing metadata required by regulators.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory filings are made to satisfy specific obligations; linking enables tracking of filing status per obligation.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when acknowledgment was received from the regulator.']
  attached_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of supporting documents attached to the filing.']
  audit_trail_hash varchar [note: 'Hash of the audit trail for integrity verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the filing record was created in the system.']
  filing_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount reported in the filing, if applicable (e.g., transaction total for CTR).']
  filing_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the filing for reporting purposes.']
  filing_deadline_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the filing must be completed or responded to.']
  filing_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing context and details of the filing.']
  filing_effective_date date [note: 'Effective date of the filing as recognized by the regulator.']
  filing_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the filing.']
  filing_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the regulator for this filing.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the filing.']
  filing_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title or name of the filing.']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Category of regulatory filing such as FinCEN Currency Transaction Report (CTR), PSD2 incident report, Reg E notice, etc.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing contains confidential information.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the filing after regulator review.']
  outcome_description varchar [note: 'Additional details about the filing outcome.']
  regulator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority to which the filing is submitted (e.g., FinCEN, ECB, CFPB).']
  related_case_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of any internal case linked to this filing.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which a response to the regulator is required.']
  response_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulator\'s response was received.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system of record for the filing (e.g., Risk and Compliance Management System).']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the filing was submitted to the regulator.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the filing record.']

  Note: 'Master record of all regulatory filings submitted to governing bodies — FinCEN CTR (Currency Transaction Reports), PSD2 incident reports to ECB/NCAs, Reg E error resolution notices, CFPB complaint responses, and SWIFT gpi compliance reports. Captures filing type, regulator, submission date, filing reference number, status, and acknowledgement. Distinct from SAR/STR which have dedicated entities.'
}

Table compliance.examination_request {
  examination_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the regulatory examination request.']
  compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_compliance_case. Business justification: Each examination request is handled by a compliance case; linking provides case context and eliminates silo.']
  internal_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Examination request owned by internal employee; needed for case management.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Links examination request jurisdiction to reference for regulator‑driven examination tracking.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded audit trail capturing key actions and timestamps for compliance purposes.']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Regulatory domain to which the examination pertains.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the examination request record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code for the currency of any monetary penalty.']
  documents_provided varchar [note: 'List or description of the documents submitted in response to the request.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for escalation, if applicable.']
  examination_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the examination request.']
  examination_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the examination (routine, targeted, or enforcement).']
  examiner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the regulator\'s examiner.']
  examiner_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulator\'s examiner or point of contact.']
  examiner_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the regulator\'s examiner.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Brief narrative of the regulator\'s findings.']
  follow_up_actions varchar [note: 'Actions the organization must take as a result of the examination.']
  information_requested varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the data, reports, or documentation the regulator is requesting.']
  internal_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the internal owner.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the examination has been escalated to senior management.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the examination request.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the examination (e.g., compliant, findings, penalties).']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed by the regulator, if any.']
  regulator varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority that issued the examination request.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date the examination request was received from the regulator.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the examination request.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the organization must provide the requested information.']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Date the organization submitted its response to the regulator.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Internal risk rating assigned to the examination request.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the examination request record.']

  Note: 'Tracks regulatory examination and supervisory requests received from regulators (OCC, CFPB, FCA, ECB, FinCEN). Captures the regulator, examination type (routine, targeted, enforcement), request date, information requested, response due date, documents provided, examiner contacts, and examination outcome. SSOT for regulatory examination support and response management.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_case {
  compliance_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance_compliance_case data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  assigned_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance officer responsible for the case.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Compliance cases often originate from wallet‑level fraud or policy breaches; linking the case to the wallet enables case dashboards and risk metrics.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Compliance cases involving partner misconduct need a direct FK to the partner to generate case reports, regulatory filings, and remediation actions.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance officer responsible for the case.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Regulatory case reporting (AML, SAR) must capture the legal entity involved for filing with authorities; experts expect a direct link to the entity record.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Compliance cases are frequently triggered by issues with a particular payment product, requiring direct linkage.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.pos_terminal. Business justification: Compliance cases often originate from suspicious activity on a specific terminal; linking enables case‑to‑terminal traceability.']
  primary_compliance_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the case, if applicable.']
  primary_compliance_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that prompted the case, if applicable.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Associates each compliance case with a canonical jurisdiction for risk scoring and reporting.']
  risk_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to risk.risk_case. Business justification: Compliance investigations often originate from a risk case; linking preserves the provenance of the compliance case.']
  action_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance action was executed.']
  case_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the case (e.g., regulatory, operational, conduct).']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the case for tracking and reference.']
  case_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating reflecting potential impact.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the case.']
  case_subtype varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the main category.']
  case_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of user‑defined tags for search and categorisation.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Category of the case such as AML, sanctions, PCI, GDPR, conduct, fraud, risk, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AML|sanctions|PCI|GDPR|conduct|fraud|risk|other — promote to reference product]']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case was closed or resolved (nullable).']
  compliance_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the remedial or enforcement action performed.']
  compliance_case_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form narrative describing the issue, findings, and context.']
  compliance_officer_notes varchar [note: 'Notes entered by the compliance officer during investigation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case record was created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Enterprise data classification applied to the case record.']
  evidence_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the evidence log or repository containing supporting documents.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by an external regulator or reporting body.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the case contains confidential information requiring restricted handling.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the case was escalated to higher authority.']
  is_false_positive boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the case was determined to be a false positive.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the last update.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case was initially opened.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business-assigned priority indicating urgency.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulation or standard governing the case.']
  regulatory_report_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a required regulatory report has been filed.']
  report_filing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory report was filed.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the case after investigation.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the case resolution was recorded.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk assessment score for the case.']
  source_trigger varchar [note: 'Origin that triggered the creation of the case.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the case record.']

  Note: 'Compliance investigation case record — opened when a potential violation, breach, or regulatory concern is identified. Captures case type (AML, sanctions, PCI, GDPR, conduct), case status, assigned compliance officer, source trigger (SAR, audit finding, regulator inquiry, internal alert), investigation timeline, evidence log references, and resolution outcome. Distinct from fraud_case in the fraud domain.'
}

Table compliance.breach_notification {
  breach_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the breach notification record.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Breach notification handled by compliance officer employee; needed for breach response accountability.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.pos_terminal. Business justification: Data breach notifications must identify the terminal where the breach occurred for incident response and regulator reporting.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Maps breach notification jurisdiction to reference jurisdiction for regulator notification requirements.']
  affected_data_categories varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of data categories compromised (e.g., PAN, personal email, authentication credentials).']
  affected_systems varchar [note: 'List of internal systems, applications, or services impacted by the breach.']
  board_report_date date [note: 'Date the breach was formally reported to the board.']
  breach_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the breach (e.g., internal employee, external attacker, third‑party vendor).']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breach investigation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach notification record was first created in the system.']
  data_loss_indicator boolean [note: 'True if data was confirmed to be exfiltrated or accessed without authorization.']
  discovery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the security incident was first discovered.']
  encryption_status_at_time timestamp [note: 'Encryption state of the affected data at the moment of breach.']
  estimated_cardholder_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of individual cardholders whose data was impacted.']
  estimated_merchant_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of merchants affected by the breach.']
  is_reported_to_board boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breach has been escalated to the board of directors.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'Legal justification for processing personal data at the time of breach, per GDPR. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: consent|contract|legal_obligation|legitimate_interest|vital_interest|public_task|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the investigation team.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulator (or required authority) was formally notified.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to notify affected individuals or regulators.']
  notification_trigger varchar [note: 'Regulatory or policy trigger that initiates the breach notification (e.g., GDPR 72‑hour rule, state breach law, PCI DSS Requirement 12.10).']
  regulator_notified varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority that was notified about the breach.']
  remediation_action_summary varchar [note: 'High‑level description of actions taken to contain and remediate the breach.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Internal risk rating (0‑100) reflecting severity and potential impact.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the breach notification record.']

  Note: 'Records each data breach or security incident notification event — breach discovery date, notification trigger (GDPR 72-hour rule, state breach laws, PCI DSS Requirement 12.10), affected data categories, estimated cardholder/merchant count impacted, regulator notified, notification date, and remediation actions. SSOT for breach notification compliance lifecycle.'
}

Table compliance.ctf_control {
  ctf_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the Counter-Terrorism Financing control record.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Links CTF control jurisdiction to reference for consistent FATF and AML reporting.']
  applies_to_channel varchar [note: 'Payment channels (e.g., online, POS) where the control is enforced.']
  applies_to_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Transaction types to which the control is applied.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit observations or change rationale.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) that mandate the control.']
  control_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned unique code for the CTF control, used for lookup and reporting.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the CTF control.']
  control_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team responsible for the control.']
  control_owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the control.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Category of the CTF control, indicating the typology it addresses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was first created in the system.']
  ctf_control_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and mechanics of the CTF control.']
  ctf_control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the CTF control becomes operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the CTF control ceases to be active; null for indefinite.']
  effectiveness_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Quantitative rating (0‑1) of how effective the control is, based on internal assessments.']
  fatf_recommendation varchar [note: 'Specific FATF recommendation number (e.g., Recommendation 10) that the control implements.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control is mandatory under regulatory requirements.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last formal review of the control\'s effectiveness.']
  regulatory_reporting_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific reporting obligation (e.g., SAR, STR) linked to the control.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled reviews of the control.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk score assigned to the control, reflecting its importance in the CTF risk model.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Technical description of the rule logic (e.g., pseudocode or reference to model version).']
  threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary threshold that triggers the control (if applicable).']
  threshold_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the threshold amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']

  Note: 'Counter-Terrorism Financing control record defining specific CTF measures implemented — transaction monitoring rules targeting terrorism financing typologies, high-risk jurisdiction controls, PEP (Politically Exposed Person) screening configurations, and correspondent banking due diligence requirements. Captures control type, applicable FATF recommendation, jurisdiction scope, and effectiveness rating.'
}

Table compliance.psd2_incident_report {
  psd2_incident_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the PSD2 incident report.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: PSD2 incident report assigned to compliance officer employee; required for regulator communication.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: PSD2 incident reports frequently involve a particular wallet (e.g., SCA failure); linking supports regulatory reporting and remediation tracking.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.pos_terminal. Business justification: PSD2 incident reports require the affected terminal ID to satisfy regulatory incident filing and risk analysis.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Provides a normalized jurisdiction key for PSD2 incident reporting to supervisory authorities.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: PSD2 incident reports are triggered by regulatory requirements; linking provides direct reference to the obligation.']
  affected_services varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of payment services or APIs impacted by the incident.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident report record was created in the system.']
  data_subjects_impacted varchar [note: 'Count of individual data subjects whose personal data was affected.']
  estimated_financial_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of the financial loss attributable to the incident, expressed in EUR.']
  final_report_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the final incident report was submitted to the regulator.']
  incident_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the incident by the reporting entity.']
  incident_severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the incident based on impact and risk.']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the incident event occurred.']
  incident_title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title summarising the incident.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incident (e.g., operational, security, fraud).']
  interim_report_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the interim incident report was submitted to the regulator.']
  is_reported_to_nca boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident has been formally reported to the NCA.']
  is_sar_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Suspicious Activity Report is required for this incident.']
  mitigation_actions varchar [note: 'Description of actions taken to mitigate or resolve the incident.']
  nca_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the NCA for this incident.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident was first reported to the compliance team or regulator.']
  psd2_incident_report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incident report.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Regulatory body to which the incident is reported.']
  report_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulator report submission process.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score assigned to the incident by the risk engine.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying cause of the incident.']
  sar_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current filing status of the SAR, if required.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that generated the incident record (e.g., Fraud Detection Platform).']
  str_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current filing status of the Suspicious Transaction Report, if required.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the incident report.']
  user_impact_count varchar [note: 'Number of end‑users (cardholders, merchants, etc.) affected by the incident.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or process that created the incident report record.']

  Note: 'PSD2 major operational or security incident report submitted to the relevant National Competent Authority (NCA) or ECB. Captures incident classification (operational, security), initial notification timestamp, root cause, affected payment services, estimated financial impact, user impact count, interim and final report submission dates, and NCA reference number. Required under PSD2 Article 96.'
}

Table compliance.sca_exemption {
  sca_exemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SCA exemption record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who initiated the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who initiated the transaction.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance officer who reviewed or approved the exemption.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance officer who reviewed or approved the exemption.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant acquiring the transaction.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the payment transaction to which the exemption applies.']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the SCA exemption was granted for the transaction.']
  audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the detailed audit log for this exemption.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exemption record was first created in the system.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of why the exemption was granted.']
  exemption_reference varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the exemption for external tracking and reporting.']
  exemption_requested_by varchar [note: 'Party that initiated the exemption request (e.g., PSP, acquiring bank, issuing bank, merchant).']
  exemption_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status indicating whether the exemption is active, approved, rejected, revoked, or expired.']
  exemption_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount for which a low‑value exemption can be applied.']
  exemption_threshold_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the exemption threshold amount.']
  exemption_type varchar [note: 'Category of exemption as defined by PSD2 RTS (e.g., low‑value, recurring, merchant‑initiated).']
  exemption_valid_from date [note: 'Start date of the exemption\'s validity period.']
  exemption_valid_until date [note: 'End date of the exemption\'s validity period (nullable if open‑ended).']
  is_exemption_revoked boolean [note: 'True if the exemption was later revoked; otherwise false.']
  liability_shift varchar [note: 'Entity that bears liability for the transaction after the exemption is applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary information or comments regarding the exemption.']
  payment_instrument_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment instrument used in the transaction.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Specific method of payment (e.g., credit card, debit card, prepaid, virtual, contactless).']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exemption must be reported to regulators (e.g., PSD2 supervisory reporting).']
  reporting_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the exemption must be reported to the appropriate regulator.']
  revocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the exemption was revoked, if applicable.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from the risk score (low, medium, high).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score (e.g., 0‑100) reflecting the risk associated with granting the exemption.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that originated the exemption (e.g., Payment Gateway, Risk & Compliance System).']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the payment transaction before any fees or discounts.']
  transaction_arn varchar [note: 'Reference number generated by the acquirer for reconciliation and dispute purposes.']
  transaction_auth_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuing bank indicating approval of the transaction.']
  transaction_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency of the transaction amount.']
  transaction_mcc varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s business type.']
  transaction_mid varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the merchant by the acquiring bank.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the payment transaction.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the underlying payment transaction was initiated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the exemption record.']

  Note: 'Records each Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) exemption applied to a payment transaction under PSD2 RTS. Captures exemption type (low-value, low-risk TRA, recurring, merchant-initiated, whitelisted payee), transaction reference, exemption requestor (PSP/acquirer/issuer), liability shift outcome, and regulator-mandated monitoring thresholds. Supports PSD2 SCA compliance reporting.'
}

Table compliance.watchlist_entry {
  watchlist_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each watchlist entry.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Provides a normalized jurisdiction reference for watchlist entries used in AML/CTF screening.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the watchlist entry.']
  address varchar [note: 'Physical address associated with the entity on the watchlist.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the watchlist entry was first loaded into the system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of an individual entity, if applicable.']
  entity_aliases varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of alternative names, spellings, or known aliases for the entity.']
  entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual, organization, or vessel as listed on the watchlist.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Category of the watchlist entity: individual, organization, or vessel.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the watchlist entry is scheduled to be removed or become inactive.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Gender of the individual, if known.']
  identification_number varchar [note: 'Official identifier such as passport number, national ID, or tax ID.']
  identification_type varchar [note: 'Type of the identification number provided.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the entity is considered high risk based on internal scoring.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent manual review of the watchlist entry.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the watchlist entry.']
  list_entry_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used by the source list to reference this entry (e.g., SDN ID).']
  list_source varchar [note: 'Originating sanctions or watchlist source for the entry.']
  list_version_date date [note: 'Date of the version of the source list that contains this entry.']
  match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score indicating similarity between this entry and a screened party.']
  nationality varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the entity\'s nationality or country of citizenship.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional context about the entry.']
  place_of_birth varchar [note: 'Geographic location where the individual was born.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the entity\'s compliance review.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned to the entity by the source list or internal assessment.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score (0‑100) assigned by the compliance team.']
  sanctions_type varchar [note: 'Primary type of sanction applied to the entity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the watchlist data (e.g., OFAC Feed, UN Sanctions Feed).']
  source_url varchar [note: 'Web address or document reference where the entry was originally published.']

  Note: 'Reference master of watchlist entries used in AML, sanctions, and CTF screening — sourced from OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Consolidated List, HM Treasury, PEP databases, and adverse media feeds. Captures entity name, aliases, entity type (individual/organization/vessel), list source, list version date, risk category, and active status. Feeds the sanctions_check and aml_screening_result processes.'
}

Table compliance.attestation {
  attestation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the compliance attestation record.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the compliance officer responsible for the attestation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the compliance officer responsible for the attestation.']
  gateway_api_credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.api_credential. Business justification: Regulatory attestation requires certifying each API credential used for gateway integration meets PCI‑DSS and security standards.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Links attestation jurisdiction to reference for auditability and regulator verification.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory obligation that this attestation satisfies.']
  attached_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the PDF or supporting documentation for the attestation.']

  attestation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the attestation.']
  attestation_type varchar [note: 'Category of regulatory or internal compliance attestation (e.g., PCI DSS Attestation of Compliance, SOX Section 302).']
  attesting_party_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual or entity signing the attestation.']
  attesting_party_role varchar [note: 'Role of the attesting party within the organization.']
  control_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team that owns the compliance control covered by the attestation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the attestation record was first created in the system.']
  digital_signature_reference varchar [note: 'Pointer (e.g., URL or hash) to the stored digital signature for the attestation.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the attestation becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the attestation expires or is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended attestations).']

  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the attestation was last reviewed.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next review of the attestation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations related to the attestation.']
  review_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the review process.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the attestation was reviewed and signed.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with the attestation based on compliance exposure.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the regulatory or policy scope covered by the attestation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the attestation record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the attestation record.']

  Note: 'Formal compliance attestation record — captures periodic sign-offs by compliance officers, business unit heads, or board members confirming adherence to specific regulatory obligations or internal policies. Includes attestation type (annual PCI DSS AOC, SOX 302/906 certification, AML program attestation), attesting party, attestation date, scope, and digital signature reference.'
}

Captures policy name, version, effective date, expiry date, owner, approval authority, applicable regulatory obligation references, and distribution scope.'
