Table compliance.jurisdiction_profile {
  jurisdiction_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the jurisdiction profile record.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Jurisdiction profiles define the regulatory environment; linking to obligations ties profiles to applicable rules.']
  amendment_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the record reflects an amendment to a prior version.']
  aml_ctf_regime varchar [note: 'Classification of the jurisdiction\'s anti‑money‑laundering and counter‑terrorism financing regime (e.g., FATF, Non‑FATF).']
  applicable_regulations varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulation codes that apply in this jurisdiction (e.g., PSD2, Reg E, APRA CPS 234).']
  classification varchar [note: 'Category of the jurisdiction (e.g., sovereign, non‑sovereign, special economic zone).']
  compliance_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the internal owner responsible for this jurisdiction\'s compliance data.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the jurisdiction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the jurisdiction profile record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Default currency used for transactions in the jurisdiction.']
  data_protection_law varchar [note: 'Primary data‑privacy legislation governing the jurisdiction (e.g., GDPR).']
  data_residency_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific data residency or localisation obligations imposed by the jurisdiction.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the jurisdiction profile becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the jurisdiction profile ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  enforcement_penalty decimal(18,2) [note: 'Typical monetary penalty for non‑compliance in this jurisdiction.']
  fatf_mutual_evaluation_rating varchar [note: 'Latest FATF mutual‑evaluation rating for the jurisdiction.']
  is_cross_border_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cross‑border payments are permitted under local regulations.']
  jurisdiction_profile_description varchar [note: 'Extended textual description of the jurisdiction profile.']
  jurisdiction_profile_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the jurisdiction (e.g., United States, European Union).']
  jurisdiction_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the jurisdiction profile.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Specific type classification of the jurisdiction.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the jurisdiction profile was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  legal_entity_identifier varchar [note: '20‑character global identifier for legal entities operating in the jurisdiction.']
  max_daily_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Regulatory limit on total daily transaction volume for a participant.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Regulatory ceiling for a single transaction amount in the jurisdiction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the jurisdiction.']
  privacy_regulation varchar [note: 'Specific privacy regulation reference applicable in the jurisdiction.']
  regulatory_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic regulatory zone grouping (e.g., EU, APAC).']
  reporting_obligation varchar [note: 'Description of mandatory regulatory reporting requirements (e.g., SAR, STR).']
  review_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the jurisdiction profile must be reviewed.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall compliance risk rating assigned to the jurisdiction.']
  sanctions_regime_membership varchar [note: 'Sanctions lists or regimes the jurisdiction participates in.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the jurisdiction data.']
  tax_identification_number_format varchar [note: 'Pattern or description of the tax ID format used in the jurisdiction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the jurisdiction profile.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the jurisdiction profile record.']

  Note: 'Reference master of regulatory jurisdictions in which the business operates — country, regulatory zone (EU, EEA, APAC), applicable payment regulations (PSD2, Reg E, APRA CPS 234), AML/CTF regime classification, FATF mutual evaluation rating, data residency requirements, and sanctions regime membership. Used to determine applicable obligations per transaction corridor and entity domicile.'
}

Table compliance.case_transaction_link {
  case_transaction_link_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the case_transaction_link association']
  compliance_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance_compliance_case']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet_transaction']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the link between the case and transaction was recorded']
  link_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the transaction was linked to the compliance case (e.g., AML suspicion, sanction hit)']

  Note: 'This association product represents the link between a compliance investigation case and a wallet transaction. It captures the reason for linking and the timestamp when the link was detected. Each record links one compliance case to one wallet transaction with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: A compliance investigation case may involve multiple wallet transactions, and a single wallet transaction can be referenced by multiple compliance cases when it triggers separate investigations. The business records the reason for each link and the timestamp when the association was detected, meaning the relationship itself carries data.'
}

  residency_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO country code where the party currently resides.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk rating (0‑100) derived from AML, fraud, and regulatory checks.']
  sanctions_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of sanctions identifiers that matched this party, if any.']
  source_of_funds varchar [note: 'Declared origin of the party\'s funds (e.g., salary, investment, loan).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream operational system that originated the party record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the party record.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status of the party.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the party record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for party. Referenced by party_id.'
}

Table product.payment_product {
  payment_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the payment product.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.acquirer_endpoint. Business justification: Primary acquirer endpoint assigned to a payment product for settlement and processing, required for settlement reporting.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Regulatory reporting and settlement require authoritative currency data; linking payment_product to reference.currency replaces denormalised currency_code.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Regulatory reporting of product issuance requires linking each payment product to its issuing partner for compliance and revenue sharing.']
  gateway_api_credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.api_credential. Business justification: Credential management ties each payment product to the API credential used for authentication in gateway calls, needed for compliance audit.']
  irf_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_table. Business justification: Required for pricing engine to map each payment product to the specific IRF table that defines its interchange rates, used in rate calculation reports.']
  promotional_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a promotional offer linked to the product.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each payment product is subject to specific regulatory obligations; mapping enables compliance monitoring.']
  revenue_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue Posting: Each payment product’s transaction fees and interchange income must be posted to a specific GL account for financial reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Required for mapping each payment product to its card scheme for interchange fee calculation, compliance reporting and settlement routing.']
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_settlement_cycle. Business justification: Settlement processing uses defined cycles; linking to reference_settlement_cycle provides cycle parameters for each product.']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether AML screening is performed on transactions of this product.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification state of the product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the product becomes legally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the product ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which merchants or cardholders may use the product.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for contactless (NFC) transactions.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for cross‑border payments.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for online e‑commerce transactions.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product supports tokenization of sensitive data.']
  kyc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether KYC verification is mandatory for using the product.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper limit for a single transaction processed with this product.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower limit for a single transaction processed with this product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for internal comments or special handling instructions.']
  payment_product_category varchar [note: 'Target market segment for the product.']
  payment_product_code varchar [note: 'Business‑unique alphanumeric code used to reference the product in downstream systems.']
  payment_product_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the product, its purpose and key features.']
  payment_product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment product.']
  payment_product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the payment product.']
  product_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for the product definition.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory regimes that apply to the product.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the product based on fraud and compliance exposure.']
  supported_rails varchar [note: 'Payment networks or clearing rails the product can be processed on.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed surcharge amount applied when surcharge_applicable is true.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'True if a surcharge may be added to the transaction fee.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to generate tokens for the product.']
  transaction_fee_fixed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat fee amount applied per transaction, in the product\'s currency.']
  transaction_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variable percentage fee applied to each transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the product record.']
  version_effective_from date [note: 'Date when this product version became effective.']
  version_effective_until date [note: 'Date when this product version was superseded; null if current.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all payment products offered by the platform — card programs (credit, debit, prepaid), BNPL offerings, P2P transfer products, A2A rail products, virtual account products, and digital wallet products. Each record defines a distinct product type with its commercial identity, lifecycle status, supported rails, and regulatory classification. SSOT for product identity across merchant and cardholder agreements.'
}

Table product.card_program {
  card_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the card program.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.acquirer_endpoint. Business justification: Card program must reference issuing acquirer endpoint for BIN allocation and settlement.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Card issuance must reference a specific card scheme for compliance and interchange rules; replace scheme string with FK.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Card program fees are charged in a specific currency; authoritative currency reference needed for reporting.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Card programs are owned and managed by a partner bank; linking enables program governance, fee allocation, and audit trails.']
  program_fee_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Fee Accounting: Card program fees (annual, network, surcharge) are allocated to a dedicated GL account to track expense and revenue per program.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Card programs are issued under a specific scheme; linking enables eligibility checks, regulatory approval tracking, and scheme‑specific fee structures.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit‑related observations or manual overrides.']
  bin_end varchar [note: 'Last six digits of the BIN range allocated to the program.']
  bin_start varchar [note: 'First six digits of the BIN range allocated to the program.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the program.']
  contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless (NFC) payments are enabled for the program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the program record was first created in the system.']
  daily_spend_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate spend allowed per cardholder per day, expressed in the program currency.']
  digital_wallet_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the program can be provisioned to digital wallets.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the program is terminated or expires; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the program becomes legally binding and operational.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which merchants or cardholders may enroll in the program.']
  emv_configuration varchar [note: 'EMV chip specifications and version used for the program.']
  fee_structure varchar [note: 'Description of the fee model applied to merchants (e.g., flat, tiered, volume‑based).']
  interchange_qualification varchar [note: 'Description of the interchange qualification rules applicable to the program.']
  is_3ds_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3‑Domain Secure authentication is required.']
  is_fraud_detection_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if real‑time fraud detection rules are applied to the program.']
  is_tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program supports tokenization of PANs.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper bound for a single transaction amount.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Default MCC assigned to transactions under this program.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower bound for a single transaction amount.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the card program, including its value proposition and target market.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the card program as marketed to merchants and cardholders.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the card program.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the program (e.g., credit, debit, prepaid, commercial, virtual, gift).']
  program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the program configuration (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the program received all required regulatory approvals.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) assigned by the fraud risk engine.']
  spending_control_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if program‑level spending controls (e.g., daily limits) are active.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether surcharges are allowed on the program.']
  surcharge_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage surcharge applied to qualifying transactions.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the token service provider used for the program.']
  transaction_type_supported varchar [note: 'Types of transactions the program permits.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the program record.']

  Note: 'Defines a card program as a distinct business entity — a branded card product issued under a specific BIN range, card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), and issuing bank arrangement. Captures program type (credit, debit, prepaid, commercial), co-brand partnerships, EMV configuration, contactless enablement, and program-level spending controls. Links to BIN registry and interchange qualification rules.'
}

Table product.bnpl_plan {
  bnpl_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each BNPL plan template.']
  creator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Compliance audit requires tracking which employee created each BNPL plan for regulatory reporting.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: BNPL installment calculations and compliance depend on a validated currency; link to reference.currency.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: BNPL plans are provided by a partner; the link supports partner‑specific risk scoring, KYC, and revenue‑share calculations.']
  interest_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Interest Revenue: BNPL plans generate interest income that must be recorded in a designated GL account for accurate revenue recognition and compliance.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: BNPL plans are defined for a specific payment product; child BNPL plan references parent payment_product.']
  allowed_countries varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the plan is offered.']
  bnpl_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the plan, including marketing copy and eligibility notes.']
  bnpl_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  compliance_regulation_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulation identifiers (e.g., PCI_DSS, PSD2) that this plan must satisfy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan record was first created in the system.']
  disallowed_countries varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the plan is prohibited.']
  early_repayment_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fee charged as a percentage of the outstanding balance if the cardholder repays early.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the plan is retired or expires; null for open‑ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes available for enrollment.']
  eligibility_cardholder_segments varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of cardholder segmentation codes (e.g., premium, student) that qualify.']
  eligibility_merchant_category_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of MCCs for merchants that may offer this plan.']
  eligibility_merchant_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of merchant identifiers (MID) permitted to use this plan.']
  fee_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of all fees associated with the plan.']
  installment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of equal payments the cardholder must make.']
  installment_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between successive installment due dates.']
  interest_rate_annual_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard annual interest rate applied to the outstanding balance after any promotional period.']
  is_promotion_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is currently being marketed as a promotion.']
  late_payment_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat fee applied when an installment is missed or paid after the due date.']
  late_payment_grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the due date before a late fee is assessed.']
  max_installment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound on the amount of any single installment.']
  max_total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total purchase amount that can be financed under this plan.']
  maximum_credit_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound on the total credit exposure a cardholder can have under this plan.']
  min_installment_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower bound on the amount of any single installment.']
  min_total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum total purchase amount required to qualify for the plan.']
  minimum_purchase_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount required for a purchase to qualify for this BNPL plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier used in merchant and cardholder agreements.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the BNPL installment plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of BNPL offering: installment, revolving credit, or hybrid.']
  promotional_apr_end_date date [note: 'Last date the promotional APR is applied; after this the standard APR applies.']
  promotional_apr_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Reduced APR offered during the promotional window.']
  promotional_apr_start_date date [note: 'First date the promotional APR becomes effective.']
  repayment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate installment amounts.']
  requires_credit_check boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a creditworthiness assessment is performed before approval.']
  requires_kyc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Know‑Your‑Customer verification is mandatory for enrollment.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum risk score a cardholder must have to be eligible for this plan.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or process that last updated the plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the plan record.']

  Note: 'Master definition of Buy Now Pay Later installment plan structures offered to cardholders and merchants. Captures installment count, interest rate, promotional APR windows, minimum purchase threshold, maximum credit limit, merchant eligibility criteria, and repayment schedule logic. Distinct from a cardholder\'s active BNPL enrollment — this is the plan template in the product catalog.'
}

Table product.product_fee_schedule {
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each fee schedule record.']
  creator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Fee schedule creation is governed by finance staff; linking creator enables audit of fee policy changes.']
  fee_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Fee schedules must use a standard currency for accounting and audit; FK replaces free‑text fee_currency.']
  fee_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Fee Booking: Each product fee schedule defines fees that are posted to a specific GL account to ensure proper expense/revenue tracking.']
  merchant_pricing_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing plan that uses this fee schedule.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: A fee schedule belongs to a specific payment product; linking enables direct access to product details and removes need for duplicate product attributes in fee schedule.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Fee schedules can be governed by regulatory rules; linking captures which obligations drive fee structures.']
  scheme_fee_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_fee_table. Business justification: Fee schedules for products must align with scheme fee tables; FK ensures consistency and supports fee audit reports.']
  applicable_card_type varchar [note: 'Card types eligible for the fee.']
  applicable_mcc varchar [note: 'MCC values for which the fee applies.']
  applicable_product_code varchar [note: 'Product code to which this fee schedule applies.']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where the fee is valid.']
  applicable_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Transaction message types to which the fee applies.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to compute the fee.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) governing the fee (e.g., PSD2, PCI DSS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PSD2|PCI_DSS|SOX|GDPR|CCPA|FATF|OFAC|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee schedule record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount charged per applicable event, expressed in the fee_currency.']
  fee_application_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the fee.']
  fee_category varchar [note: 'Category of the fee such as transaction, monthly, setup, chargeback, FX, BNPL, interchange, MDR, MSF. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transaction|monthly|annual|setup|chargeback|fx|bnpl|interchange|mdr|msf|other — promote to reference product]']
  fee_code varchar [note: 'Business‑defined unique code for the fee schedule entry.']
  fee_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the fee purpose and calculation.']
  fee_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fee (e.g., "MDR Tier 1", "FX Markup").']
  fee_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Percentage‑based rate applied to the transaction amount (e.g., 0.0250 for 2.5%).']
  fee_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the fee cost.']
  fee_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee is subject to tax.']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'True if the fee is part of a promotional offer.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee recurs on a schedule.']
  max_fee_cap decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper limit on the fee amount that can be charged.']
  min_fee_floor decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound on the fee amount that will be charged.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or business rationale for the fee.']
  product_fee_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fee schedule.']
  promotional_end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional fee expires.']
  promotional_start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotional fee becomes effective.']
  recurrence_interval varchar [note: 'Interval at which a recurring fee is applied.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the fee has been validated against applicable regulations (e.g., PSD2, PCI).']
  tax_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (e.g., 0.0700 for 7%).']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Ordinal number of the tier when the fee uses a tiered structure.']
  tier_max_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum transaction volume or amount for this tier.']
  tier_min_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum transaction volume or amount that qualifies for this tier.']
  tier_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'Rate applied within this tier (percentage or flat).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fee schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master fee schedule defining all fee types, rates, and calculation methods applicable to a payment product or pricing plan. Covers MDR tiers, MSF rates, interchange pass-through configurations, monthly maintenance fees, transaction fees, chargeback fees, FX markup fees, and BNPL origination fees. Each schedule is versioned and effective-dated to support regulatory and commercial changes.'
}

Table product.fee_schedule_line {
  fee_schedule_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each fee schedule line record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Each fee line may be charged in a specific currency; linking ensures consistency with master currency list.']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fee schedule to which this line belongs.']
  amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount applied when rate_type is flat.']
  applicable_product_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product (e.g., card program, BNPL) for which the fee is applicable.']
  applicable_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction that triggers this fee line.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates if the fee line is subject to specific compliance regimes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fee schedule line was first inserted.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the fee line\'s validity period.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date of the fee line\'s validity period; null indicates indefinite.']
  fee_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the fee (e.g., interchange, network, service).']
  fee_component_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the fee component (e.g., "MDR Flat Fee").']
  fee_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing context for the fee component.']
  fee_schedule_line_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the fee line.']
  fee_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the fee definition (e.g., internal policy, partner agreement, regulatory mandate).']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'True if the fee is subject to tax, otherwise false.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified the record.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential position of the line within the fee schedule.']
  max_cap decimal(18,4) [note: 'Upper bound on the fee amount for this line.']
  min_cap decimal(18,4) [note: 'Lower bound on the fee amount for this line.']
  percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage applied to the transaction amount when rate_type is percentage.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Calculation method for the fee line.']
  regulatory_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory rule or standard that mandates or influences the fee.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage tax rate applied to the fee when is_taxable is true.']
  tier_end_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount that qualifies for this tier.']
  tier_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage fee applied to transactions whose amount falls within the tier range.']
  tier_start_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount that qualifies for this tier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the fee line.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the fee line for audit and change management.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item entries within a fee schedule, each representing a single fee component with its own rate type (flat, percentage, tiered, blended), applicable transaction type, currency, minimum/maximum cap, and effective date range. Enables granular fee modeling for complex MDR structures, interchange-plus pricing, and tiered volume discounts.'
}

Table product.product_pricing_plan {
  product_pricing_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the pricing plan record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Pricing plans are defined per currency; FK to reference.currency supports multi‑currency pricing and compliance.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Pricing plans are negotiated with partners; the FK supports partner‑specific pricing rules and audit.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Product pricing plans are defined for a payment product; child pricing plan references parent payment_product.']
  pricing_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Pricing Impact: Pricing plans affect transaction fees and discounts; linking to a GL account enables automated posting of pricing-related revenue and expense.']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule definition applied by the plan.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.interchange_program. Business justification: Pricing plans are built on specific interchange programs; linking allows automated plan generation and regulatory reporting of program‑based rates.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the plan received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow state of the plan.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that approved the plan.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Result of the compliance review for the plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pricing plan record was first created in the system.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Discount applied to fees within the tier, expressed as a decimal fraction.']
  discount_tier_end decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction volume for a discount tier.']
  discount_tier_start decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction volume for a discount tier.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the pricing plan ceases to be binding (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the pricing plan becomes binding for the counterparties.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which merchants or cardholders may use the plan.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external partners or legacy systems to reference the plan.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed fee component of the plan, expressed in the plan currency.']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum fee that can be charged under the plan per defined period.']
  fee_cap_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the fee cap (per transaction, monthly, or annual).']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Variable fee component expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.0250 = 2.50%).']
  is_default_plan boolean [note: 'True if this plan is the default offering for its category.']
  is_test_plan boolean [note: 'True if the plan is used for testing or sandbox environments.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction value for which the plan fees apply.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction value for which the plan fees apply.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the plan.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the product offering (e.g., transaction‑based, subscription, BNPL).']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the pricing plan, used in contracts and merchant agreements.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the plan applies to merchants, cardholders, or both.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Pricing methodology used in the plan.']
  product_pricing_plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the plan, including purpose and scope.']
  product_pricing_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the plan.']
  promotional_discount_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Discount rate applied during the promotional window.']
  promotional_window_end date [note: 'End date of any promotional pricing period.']
  promotional_window_start date [note: 'Start date of any promotional pricing period.']
  regulatory_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan has passed all required regulatory approvals.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the plan was offered to the counterparties.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the plan was terminated, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for plan termination (e.g., regulatory, business decision).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the pricing plan record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the plan for change management.']

  Note: 'Commercial pricing plan assigned to merchants or cardholders, bundling a fee schedule with interchange model (flat-rate, interchange-plus, tiered, blended), volume discount thresholds, and promotional pricing windows. Tracks plan version, effective dates, approval status, and the sales channel through which the plan was offered. SSOT for the commercial pricing agreement at the plan level.'
}

Table product.promotional_offer {
  promotional_offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each promotional offer.']
  creator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Marketing promotions must be linked to the employee who created them for compliance and performance tracking.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Promotions are offered in a defined currency; linking ensures consistency across offers and reporting.']
  discount_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Discount Expense: Promotional offers create discount expenses that must be booked to a GL account for financial statements and audit trails.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Promotional offers are often sponsored by a partner; linking enables tracking sponsor revenue and compliance.']
  applicable_product_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product or program codes to which the offer applies.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded log of significant changes to the offer record for compliance purposes.']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance sign‑off status for the offer.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance team completed its review.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the offer record was first created in the system.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary discount applied by the offer (e.g., $5 off).']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage‑based discount applied by the offer (e.g., 10%).']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that define which cardholders, merchants or transactions qualify for the offer.']
  end_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the promotional offer expires or is de‑activated.']
  fee_waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offer waives applicable processing fees.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic extent of the offer\'s applicability.']
  intro_apr decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual Percentage Rate offered for the introductory period.']
  intro_apr_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the introductory APR is in effect.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the offer can be combined with other offers.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the most recent update.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the offer record.']
  marketing_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which the offer is communicated to customers.']
  max_redemption_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit for total redemptions across all users.']
  max_redemption_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times the offer can be redeemed overall.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for internal comments or special handling instructions.']
  offer_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the offer mechanics, benefits and any conditions.']
  offer_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the promotional offer.']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Category of the promotional offer, e.g., introductory APR, cashback, fee waiver, etc.']
  promotional_offer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the offer.']
  promotional_period_days varchar [note: 'Length of the promotional window expressed in days.']
  redemption_limit_per_user varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single cardholder may redeem the offer.']
  redemption_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which a customer redeems the offer.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the offer must be reported to regulators (e.g., AML, PSD2).']
  remaining_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value still available for redemption before hitting the max amount.']
  remaining_redemptions varchar [note: 'Number of redemptions still available before the offer reaches its max count.']
  sponsor_type varchar [note: 'Entity that sponsors the offer – issuer, merchant, or the fintech platform.']
  stackable_with_offer_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of other promotional_offer_id values that may be stacked.']
  start_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the promotional offer becomes active.']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of customers targeted by the offer.']
  total_redemption_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all redemptions.']
  total_redemptions varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of redemptions that have occurred.']
  zero_fee_flag boolean [note: 'True when the offer results in a zero transaction fee for eligible transactions.']

  Note: 'Defines time-limited promotional offers applied to payment products — introductory APR periods, zero-fee processing windows, cashback incentive programs, waived annual fees, and BNPL promotional interest-free periods. Captures offer mechanics, eligibility criteria, redemption limits, start/end dates, and the sponsoring entity (issuer, merchant, or platform). Distinct from a standing pricing plan.'
}

Table product.bundle {
  bundle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product bundle.']
  creator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Bundle creation is a product‑management activity; linking creator supports governance and audit trails.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Bundles have a base price in a defined currency; linking ensures correct pricing and compliance.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Bundles are marketed by partners; linking records which partner owns the bundle for revenue‑share and compliance.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Bundle Revenue: Sales of product bundles are recognized in a dedicated GL account to separate bundle revenue from individual product revenue.']
  allowed_countries varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3 country codes where the bundle may be offered.']
  allowed_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes accepted for the bundle.']
  base_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard price of the bundle before discounts or overrides.']
  bundle_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the bundle offering.']
  bundle_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the bundle in contracts and pricing systems.']
  bundle_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the bundle offering.']
  bundle_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the bundle offering.']
  bundle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the bundle.']
  bundle_tier varchar [note: 'Tier level indicating feature depth and pricing.']
  bundle_type varchar [note: 'Category of the bundle based on the primary service(s) it combines.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales or delivery channel for the bundle.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission percentage earned by partners for selling the bundle.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance assessment.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary discount applied when discount_type = amount.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied when discount_type = percentage.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'How discounts are applied to the base price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the bundle becomes effective for customers.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the bundle expires or is retired (null for open‑ended).']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which merchants or cardholders may subscribe to the bundle.']
  fee_structure varchar [note: 'How fees are applied to the bundle (e.g., per transaction or subscription).']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bundle is a limited‑time promotional offering.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value for a single transaction under the bundle.']
  max_transactions_per_month varchar [note: 'Upper bound on the number of transactions allowed under the bundle each month.']
  minimum_commitment_months varchar [note: 'Minimum contract length required for the bundle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or internal comments.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the bundle price.']
  promotional_end_date date [note: 'End date of the promotional period.']
  promotional_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the promotional period.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bundle record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bundle record.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory review for the bundle.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Composite risk rating assigned by the fraud detection platform.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Textual description of service level commitments for the bundle.']
  sla_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied if SLA targets are not met.']
  sla_target_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Target response or processing time promised in the SLA, expressed in minutes.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Primary market segment for which the bundle is designed.']
  termination_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged if the merchant terminates the bundle before the commitment period.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management.']

  Note: 'Defines bundled service packages that combine multiple payment products and ancillary services into a single commercial offering — e.g., a merchant acquiring bundle combining gateway access, POS terminal service, fraud screening, and chargeback management under one MSF. Captures bundle composition, pricing override rules, minimum commitment terms, and eligibility constraints.'
}

Table product.bundle_component {
  bundle_component_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bundle component record.']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product bundle to which this component belongs.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Component pricing overrides are currency‑specific; FK provides authoritative currency reference.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who created the bundle component record.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment product or service that is part of the bundle.']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule that governs this component\'s charges.']
  promotional_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a promotional offer linked to this component.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who last updated the bundle component record.']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening is required for this component.']
  bundle_component_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the component and any special conditions.']
  bundle_component_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the component within the bundle.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the component configuration.']
  component_role varchar [note: 'Role of the component within the bundle (primary, add‑on, or optional).']
  component_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the component within the bundle composition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bundle component record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when this component configuration becomes effective for the bundle.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when this component configuration expires (null if open‑ended).']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine when the component can be applied to a merchant or cardholder.']
  interchange_fee_category varchar [note: 'Category of interchange fee applicable to this component.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the component supports contactless transactions.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the component is available for cross‑border transactions.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the component must be present in every bundle enrollment.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the component can be used in online payment channels.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Specifies if the component is subject to tax.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'Specifies whether the component can be tokenized for secure storage.']
  kyc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if Know‑Your‑Customer verification is required for this component.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the component.']
  pricing_override_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount used to override the component\'s standard fee.']
  pricing_override_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage used to adjust the component\'s standard fee.']
  pricing_override_type varchar [note: 'Method used to override default pricing for this component.']
  quantity_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units of this component allowed in a bundle enrollment.']
  quantity_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units of this component that must be included in a bundle enrollment.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which settlements for this component are processed.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed surcharge amount applied when surcharge_applicable is true.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surcharge is applied to this component.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate for the component when taxable.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used for tokenizing the component, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bundle component record.']
  version_effective_from date [note: 'Start date for this version of the component configuration.']
  version_effective_until date [note: 'End date for this version of the component configuration (null if current).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the component configuration for change management.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking individual payment products or services to a product bundle, capturing the role of each component (primary, add-on, optional), component-level pricing overrides, quantity limits, and sequencing rules. Enables flexible bundle composition and supports partial bundle enrollment tracking.'
}

Table product.eligibility_rule {
  eligibility_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the eligibility rule.']
  creator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Eligibility rules are authored by risk analysts; linking creator enables audit of rule provenance.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment product or pricing plan governed by this rule.']
  promotional_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional offer to which this rule is attached.']
  bin_range_end varchar [note: 'Last six digits of the issuing BIN that marks the end of the applicable range.']
  bin_range_start varchar [note: 'First six digits of the issuing BIN that marks the start of the applicable range.']
  cardholder_segment varchar [note: 'Segment label of cardholders (e.g., consumer, corporate) to which the rule applies.']
  condition_expression varchar [note: 'Logical expression (e.g., SQL‑like or DSL) that defines the rule criteria.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for geographic eligibility; empty when not a geography rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the rule becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the rule expires; null for open‑ended.']
  eligibility_rule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the rule purpose and business rationale.']
  eligibility_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  is_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an authorized user can override the rule for a specific case.']
  kyc_tier varchar [note: 'KYC verification tier required for eligibility.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'MCC that the rule filters on; empty when not an MCC‑type rule.']
  merchant_segment varchar [note: 'Segment label of merchants (e.g., enterprise, SMB) to which the rule applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the rule.']
  override_authority varchar [note: 'Entity authorized to perform an override when allowed.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer indicating rule precedence when multiple rules apply (lower number = higher priority).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Sub‑national region or state code used for finer geographic filtering.']
  risk_score_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest risk‑score value (inclusive) for which the rule applies.']
  risk_score_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest risk‑score value (inclusive) for which the rule applies.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of what the rule applies to (e.g., merchant, cardholder, segment).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the rule within the product catalog.']
  rule_logic varchar [note: 'Indicates whether matching entities are included or excluded by the rule.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rule used in UI and reports.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of eligibility filter expressed by the rule.']
  tpv_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative TPV amount that must be met or exceeded for the rule to be applicable.']
  transaction_amount_max decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction amount for which the rule applies.']
  transaction_amount_min decimal(18,2) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction amount (in the product currency) for which the rule applies.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last updated the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the rule definition; increments on each change.']

  Note: 'Business rules governing which merchants, cardholders, or segments are eligible for a given payment product, pricing plan, or promotional offer. Captures rule type (MCC restriction, BIN range filter, geography, TPV threshold, KYC tier, risk score band), rule logic (include/exclude), effective dates, and override authority. Used by onboarding and agreement workflows to validate product assignment.'
}

Table product.version {
  version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product version record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Versioned product definitions include currency; FK ensures version history aligns with master currency data.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Product versions track changes to a payment product; child version references parent payment_product.']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether AML screening is performed for transactions using this product.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual/committee that approved the version.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the version was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the approval workflow for this version.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining why the product definition was changed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the product version.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the version record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when this product version becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when this product version ceases to be binding (null for open‑ended).']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which merchants or cardholders may use the product.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Primary monetary fee associated with the product version.']
  interchange_fee_category varchar [note: 'Category of interchange fee applied to the product.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for contactless transactions.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for cross‑border payments.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used for online e‑commerce transactions.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product supports tokenization of payment credentials.']
  kyc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Know‑Your‑Customer verification is required for this product.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed transaction amount for this product version.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum allowed transaction amount for this product version.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the product version.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the product in external systems.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment product.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment product (e.g., card, BNPL, P2P, A2A, virtual account, digital wallet).']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory category (e.g., PCI‑DSS, PSD2, AML) applicable to the product.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the product version.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Standard settlement timing for the product (e.g., T+0, T+1).']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed surcharge amount applied when surcharge_applicable is true.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surcharge may be applied to the product.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used for tokenizing payment data (e.g., DPAN, HCE, Token Service Provider).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the version record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the product definition.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product version.']

  Note: 'Tracks the versioned lifecycle of a payment product definition — capturing each material change to product terms, fee structures, regulatory classifications, or feature sets. Stores version number, change reason, approval authority, effective date, and the delta from the prior version. Supports regulatory audit trails and backward-compatible product agreement references.'
}

Table product.rate_tier {
  rate_tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the rate tier definition.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Rate tiers are defined per currency; linking to reference.currency guarantees correct financial calculations.']
  product_pricing_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_pricing_plan. Business justification: Rate tiers are defined within a pricing plan; linking rate_tier to product_pricing_plan clarifies hierarchy and eliminates the redundant payment_product_id column.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of internal compliance review for the tier definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tier record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the tier becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the tier expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules or conditions that must be satisfied for a merchant or portfolio to qualify for this tier.']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum fee that can be charged under this tier, if a cap applies.']
  fee_cap_type varchar [note: 'Frequency or basis of the fee cap (e.g., per transaction, per month, per year).']
  is_default_tier boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this tier is the default when no other tier matches.']
  max_volume decimal(20,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the volume band for this tier.']
  min_volume decimal(20,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the volume band that triggers this tier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the tier.']
  rate_tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tier.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the tier rate is a percentage of the transaction amount or a fixed monetary amount.']
  rate_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the rate applied within the tier (percentage expressed as decimal or fixed amount).']
  regulatory_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tier has received required regulatory sign‑off.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the tier in pricing rules.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing tier.']
  tier_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve overlapping tiers; lower numbers have higher precedence.']
  tier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tier based on the metric it governs (e.g., volume‑based, transaction‑based, value‑based).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tier record.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit used for the volume thresholds (e.g., transaction count or currency).']

  Note: 'Volume-based rate tier definitions used within pricing plans and fee schedules — specifying TPV bands (e.g., $0–$10K, $10K–$100K, $100K+) and the corresponding MDR or MSF rate applicable at each tier. Supports both merchant-level and portfolio-level tiered pricing models. Effective-dated to support periodic rate reviews.'
}

Table product.interchange_qualification {
  interchange_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each interchange qualification configuration record.']
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: Needed for product qualification rules to reference the exact interchange rate category, enabling compliance checks and pricing simulations.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: Interchange qualification rules depend on MCC; linking to reference.mcc provides validated category data.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Interchange qualification settings apply to a payment product; child qualification references parent payment_product.']
  authorization_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to obtain transaction authorization.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Type of card instrument the qualification applies to.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the qualification against applicable standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the qualification becomes effective for new transactions.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the qualification is no longer applicable (null for open‑ended).']
  entry_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which card data was entered for the transaction.']
  interchange_category varchar [note: 'Resulting interchange rate category that the qualification maps to.']
  interchange_qualification_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the qualification logic and any special notes.']
  interchange_qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the qualification configuration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or operational comments.']
  qualification_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used in downstream systems to reference this qualification configuration.']
  qualification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the qualification rule set (e.g., "Visa CPS Retail Qualification").']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Category of product to which the qualification applies (e.g., card program, BNPL offering, P2P transfer).']
  regulatory_approval_flag boolean [note: 'True if the qualification has received formal regulatory approval.']
  sca_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction flow meets Strong Customer Authentication requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the qualification record.']
  version_effective_from date [note: 'Date when this version of the qualification became effective.']
  version_effective_until date [note: 'Date when this version ceased to be effective (null if current).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version identifier for change management.']

  Note: 'Product-level interchange qualification configuration mapping card program and transaction attributes (card type, entry mode, MCC, authorization method, SCA compliance) to the expected interchange rate category (e.g., Visa CPS Retail, MC Merit III). Distinct from the interchange rate table owned by the interchange domain — this entity defines the qualification logic for a product\'s expected interchange outcome.'
}

Table product.p2p_product {
  p2p_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the P2P product.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: P2P products are typically provided by a partner; the FK enables partner‑level fee schedules and fraud monitoring.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: P2P transfer products are a type of payment product; child P2P product references parent payment_product.']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if Anti-Money Laundering screening is performed for transfers.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product record was created.']
  cross_border_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if cross-border transfers are permitted.']
  currency_supported varchar [note: 'Currency in which transfers are settled.']
  daily_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative amount allowed per day.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the product becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the product ceases to be effective, if applicable.']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum fee that can be charged per transfer.']
  fee_fixed_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed fee amount applied to each transfer.']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage fee applied to each transfer.']
  funding_sources varchar [note: 'Supported funding source types for the P2P product.']
  fx_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates if foreign exchange is supported for cross-border transfers.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if contactless transfers (e.g., NFC) are supported.']
  is_default_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates if this product uses the default pricing plan.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the product supports international transfers.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the product can be used via online channels.']
  is_test_product boolean [note: 'Indicates if this product is for testing purposes only.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'Indicates if the product supports tokenization of payment credentials.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Primary jurisdiction where the product is offered. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|GBR|CAN|AUS|DEU|FRA|JPN|SGP — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  kyc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if Know Your Customer verification is required for using the product.']
  monthly_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative amount allowed per month.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes about the product.']
  p2p_product_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code for the P2P product.']
  p2p_product_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the P2P product offering.']
  p2p_product_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the P2P product offering.']
  p2p_product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product.']
  per_transaction_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed per individual transfer.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Type of P2P transfer offered.']
  promotional_offer_code varchar [note: 'Code of any promotional offer linked to the product.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the product per jurisdiction.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used for tokenizing payment credentials.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product record was last updated.']

  Note: 'Master definition of Peer-to-Peer transfer products offered on the platform — including instant P2P, scheduled P2P, and cross-border P2P variants. Captures supported funding sources (debit card, bank account, wallet balance), transfer limits, FX eligibility, SLA for fund availability, and regulatory classification per jurisdiction. Distinct from A2A rail products which are bank-to-bank.'
}

Table product.a2a_product {
  a2a_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the A2A product.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Account‑to‑account products settle in a specific currency; FK needed for compliance and settlement.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Account‑to‑account products are offered by a partner institution; linking is required for settlement routing and compliance reporting.']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule governing this product.']
  promotional_offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a promotional offer linked to this product.']
  a2a_product_code varchar [note: 'Business‑oriented code used to reference the product in external systems.']
  a2a_product_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the product offering.']
  a2a_product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the A2A transfer product.']
  a2a_product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the product.']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether AML screening is performed on transactions using this product.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the product becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the product ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules that determine which parties may use the product.']
  interchange_fee_category varchar [note: 'Category of interchange fee applicable to the product.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if contactless transactions are allowed.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if cross‑border transfers are permitted.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the product can be used for online transfers.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product supports tokenization of payment credentials.']
  iso_20022_version varchar [note: 'Version of the ISO 20022 message schema used for this product.']
  kyc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether KYC verification is mandatory for using this product.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit per transaction for this product.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Lower monetary limit per transaction for this product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra information.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Category of the A2A rail (e.g., ACH, RTP, RTGS, SWIFT, Open Banking).']
  product_version varchar [note: 'Human‑readable version label for the product.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Classification for regulatory reporting purposes.']
  regulatory_reporting varchar [note: 'Regulatory frameworks applicable to the product.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification for the product based on fraud exposure.']
  settlement_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between transaction initiation and settlement.']
  stp_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions can be processed end‑to‑end without manual intervention.']
  supported_rails varchar [note: 'List of transfer rails supported by this product.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed surcharge amount applied when surcharge_applicable is true.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Whether a surcharge may be applied to the transaction.']
  surcharge_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage‑based surcharge applied when surcharge_applicable is true.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used for tokenization when supported.']
  transaction_fee_fixed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary fee applied to each transaction.']
  transaction_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage‑based fee applied to each transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the product record.']
  version_effective_from date [note: 'Date when this version becomes effective.']
  version_effective_until date [note: 'Date when this version expires (null if ongoing).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for product changes.']

  Note: 'Master definition of Account-to-Account transfer rail products — ACH (standard and same-day), RTP, RTGS, SWIFT, and open banking A2A variants. Captures supported rails, settlement windows, transaction limits, STP eligibility, ISO 20022 message schema version, and regulatory reporting obligations (Reg E, PSD2). Distinct from P2P products which are consumer-wallet-funded.'
}

Table product.virtual_account_product {
  virtual_account_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the virtual account product.']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the issuing program or card program to which this virtual account belongs.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Virtual accounts are denominated in a currency; linking to reference.currency supports reporting and limits.']
  product_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule that defines fees applicable to this product.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Virtual accounts inherit scheme rules (tokenization, KYC, fee models); a FK provides accurate scheme association for audit and compliance.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the virtual account becomes active and usable.']
  allowed_mcc_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of Merchant Category Codes permitted for transactions on this virtual account.']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'Specifies if Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening is performed on the account holder.']
  bin_end varchar [note: 'Last six digits of the Bank Identification Number range assigned to this product.']
  bin_start varchar [note: 'First six digits of the Bank Identification Number range assigned to this product.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification that groups the product for reporting and eligibility rules.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the product meets all applicable compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product record was first created in the system.']
  daily_spend_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate spend allowed per calendar day.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the product becomes effective for issuance.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the product ceases to be offered (null if open‑ended).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the virtual account automatically expires if not used.']
