  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the virtual account can be used for contactless transactions.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the virtual account can be used for cross‑border payments.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the virtual account can be used for online e‑commerce transactions.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the virtual account number can be tokenized for secure usage.']
  issuance_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'One‑time fee charged when the virtual account is created.']
  kyc_required boolean [note: 'Specifies if Know‑Your‑Customer verification is mandatory for activation.']
  maintenance_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Recurring fee for maintaining the virtual account product.']
  max_balance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper bound on the cumulative balance that can be held in the virtual account.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Absolute ceiling for any single transaction processed with this virtual account.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Floor amount below which a transaction will be declined.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Business‑defined alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the product in catalogs and pricing systems.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the virtual account is single‑use, multi‑use, or a virtual IBAN.']
  promo_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product can be included in promotional offers.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approval for the product.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification used for underwriting and fraud monitoring.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which funds are settled to the underlying account.']
  spend_control_rules varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded rules that define merchant category restrictions, geographic limits, and velocity controls.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surcharge may be applied to transactions using this product.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party token service provider, if tokenization is enabled.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to tokenize the virtual account number (Token Service Provider, Host Card Emulation, or none).']
  transaction_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for a single transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the product record.']
  virtual_account_product_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the virtual account product, including marketing and functional details.']
  virtual_account_product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the virtual account product.']
  virtual_account_product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the product.']

  Note: 'Master definition of virtual account products — single-use virtual card numbers, multi-use virtual accounts, and virtual IBANs issued for B2B payables, expense management, or marketplace disbursements. Captures account type, BIN assignment, spending controls, expiry logic, tokenization method (TSP/HCE), and the issuing program it belongs to.'
}

Table product.feature {
  feature_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product feature.']
  fee_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Feature fee impact must be expressed in a standard currency; FK ensures correct financial reporting.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Features may be mandated by particular regulations; linking tracks required regulatory obligations per feature.']
  activation_rules varchar [note: 'Business rules or conditions that must be satisfied for the feature to be activated (e.g., eligibility criteria, risk thresholds).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the feature record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the feature becomes available for activation.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the feature is retired or no longer available (null if indefinite).']
  feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the feature in configurations and contracts.']
  feature_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the feature, its purpose and behavior.']
  feature_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the feature.']
  feature_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the feature.']
  feature_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the feature based on its functional role.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount charged when the feature is enabled (applicable when fee_impact_type = \'fixed\').']
  fee_impact_type varchar [note: 'How the feature influences pricing (no impact, fixed fee, percentage fee, or tiered fee).']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of transaction amount charged when the feature is enabled (applicable when fee_impact_type = \'percentage\').']
  is_enabled_by_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the feature is enabled by default on new product instances.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the feature.']
  regulatory_dependency varchar [note: 'Regulations or standards that govern the use of this feature.']
  requires_3ds boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3‑D Secure authentication is required when the feature is used.']
  requires_aml boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening is required for the feature.']
  requires_contactless boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless (NFC) capability is required for the feature.']
  requires_dcc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Dynamic Currency Conversion is required for the feature.']
  requires_international boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the feature can be used for cross‑border or international payments.']
  requires_kyc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Know‑Your‑Customer verification is required to enable the feature.']
  requires_online boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the feature is applicable only to online transactions.']
  requires_tokenization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tokenization of payment credentials is mandatory for the feature.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating associated with the feature (higher values indicate greater risk).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the feature record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the feature definition, incremented on each change.']

  Note: 'Catalog of discrete features and capabilities that can be enabled or disabled on a payment product — e.g., contactless NFC, 3DS authentication, DCC, instalment conversion, rewards accrual, spend controls, real-time notifications, and open banking data sharing. Each feature has its own activation rules, regulatory dependencies, and fee implications. Supports modular product configuration.'
}

Table product.feature_assignment {
  feature_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each product-feature assignment record.']
  feature_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the feature (e.g., 3DS, DCC, tokenization) being enabled for the product.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment product or card program to which the feature is assigned.']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the feature became active for the product.']
  agreement_code varchar [note: 'Reference code of the merchant or cardholder agreement that triggered the feature activation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the governance review for enabling the feature.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the approval decision was recorded.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that approved the feature activation.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or source through which the feature was activated (e.g., online portal, API call, partner integration).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the feature assignment.']
  configuration_parameters varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded key‑value pairs for feature‑specific settings (e.g., 3DS version, DCC markup rate).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the assignment record was first created in the system.']
  deactivation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the feature was de‑activated or expired; null if still active.']
  feature_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the feature assignment.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the feature is the default setting for the product.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or audit trail notes related to the assignment.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with enabling the feature for the product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the assignment record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking changes to the assignment record.']

  Note: 'Association entity recording which features are enabled on a specific payment product or card program, including activation date, configuration parameters (e.g., 3DS version, DCC markup rate), approval status, and the channel or agreement that triggered the feature activation. Enables per-product feature matrix management.'
}

Table product.geography {
  geography_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each product geography record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Geography‑specific pricing and surcharge rules are currency‑dependent; link to reference.currency.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment product to which this geographic availability applies.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the product in the geography.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest compliance review for the product in this geography.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance posture of the product in the geography.']
  country_specific_variant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying a product variant that applies only to this country.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product geography record was first created.']
  geography_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the jurisdiction where the product is offered.']
  is_cross_border_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product can be used for cross‑border transactions from this geography.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when the product became available in the geography.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upper limit on a single transaction amount for the product in this geography.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Lower limit on a single transaction amount for the product in this geography.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or operational notes about the product geography configuration.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Logical region grouping of countries for reporting and pricing (e.g., EMEA, APAC).']
  regulatory_approval_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when regulatory approval was granted for the product in the geography.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory regime governing the product in this geography.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk classification for the product in this geography based on fraud, regulatory, and operational considerations.']
  sunset_date date [note: 'Date when the product will be retired or no longer offered in the geography (nullable if indefinite).']
  supported_rails varchar [note: 'Payment rails that are enabled for the product in this geography.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Flat surcharge amount applied when surcharge_applicable is true.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surcharge is applied to transactions in this geography.']
  transaction_type_supported varchar [note: 'Types of transactions that the product can process in the geography.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the product geography record.']
  variant_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any geography‑specific product variant or customization.']
  variant_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a geography‑specific product variant overrides default product settings.']

  Note: 'Defines the geographic availability and regulatory applicability of each payment product — mapping products to supported countries, currency zones, card scheme regions, and regulatory frameworks (PSD2, Reg E, GDPR). Captures launch date, sunset date, and any country-specific product variant overrides. Supports cross-border product eligibility checks.'
}

Table product.product_channel {
  product_channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product channel configuration.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Channel configurations (e.g., limits, fees) are defined per currency; FK provides authoritative currency data.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment product to which this channel configuration applies.']
  aml_screening_required boolean [note: 'True if Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening must be performed for transactions on this channel.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the channel (e.g., POS, MPOS, EC, INAPP).']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the distribution or acceptance channel (e.g., "Retail POS", "Mobile App").']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Category of the channel based on interface and network; additional types are available via reference lookup.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the channel with PCI DSS, regulatory, and scheme rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the channel configuration becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the channel configuration expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  interchange_fee_category varchar [note: 'Classification of interchange fee structure applicable to the channel.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless (NFC) transactions are accepted on this channel.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'True if cross‑border or foreign‑currency transactions are permitted on this channel.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'True if the channel supports online (e‑commerce) transaction flows.']
  is_tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'True if the channel can process tokenized payment credentials.']
  kyc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Know‑Your‑Customer verification is required for merchants using this channel.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit for a single transaction processed via this channel.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Lower monetary threshold for a transaction on this channel.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks about the channel.']
  product_channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the channel configuration.']
  requires_3ds boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3‑DS authentication is required for online transactions on this channel.']
  requires_emv boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMV chip compliance is mandatory for transactions on this channel.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned to the channel based on fraud and regulatory exposure.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Standard settlement timing for transactions processed via this channel.']
  sla_response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Service‑Level‑Agreement target response time for transaction authorizations on this channel, expressed in seconds.']
  supported_entry_modes varchar [note: 'Payment entry methods supported on this channel; additional modes are defined in a reference table.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the token service provider (TSP) used when tokenization is enabled for this channel.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the channel record.']

  Note: 'Defines the distribution and acceptance channels through which each payment product is offered or used — POS, mPOS, eCommerce, in-app, ATM, SWIFT, ACH, UPI, QR code, open banking API. Captures channel-specific configuration (e.g., EMV required for POS, 3DS required for eCommerce), SLA commitments, and supported entry modes per channel.'
}

Table product.pricing_plan_assignment {
  pricing_plan_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each pricing plan assignment record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Assignments of pricing plans to parties must reference a specific currency for correct fee calculation.']
  merchant_pricing_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pricing plan that is assigned.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (merchant, cardholder, or partner) to which the pricing plan is assigned.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Pricing plan assignments need to be linked to the payment product they apply to; child assignment references parent payment_product.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the pricing plan assignment.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment received final approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that approved the assignment.']
  assigned_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity (person or system) that performed the assignment.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date the pricing plan was assigned to the party.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assign the pricing plan.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the compliance review process for the assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was first created.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount rate applied by the pricing plan, expressed as a percentage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the pricing plan ceases to be effective; null for open‑ended assignments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the pricing plan becomes effective for the party.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary component of the pricing plan (if any).']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage component of the pricing plan (if any).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this pricing plan is the default for the party.']
  is_test_plan boolean [note: 'Indicates if the assignment is for testing purposes only.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or audit notes related to the assignment.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why a standard pricing plan was overridden.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Type of party receiving the pricing plan (merchant, cardholder, or partner).']
  pricing_plan_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing plan assignment.']
  regulatory_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment has received required regulatory approval.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assignment record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a pricing plan being assigned to a specific merchant or cardholder account, capturing assignment date, assigned-by (sales rep, automated rule, or API), effective start/end dates, override reason, and approval workflow status. Provides the audit trail for all pricing plan changes and supports dispute resolution for billing discrepancies.'
}

Table product.promotional_offer_redemption {
  promotional_offer_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each promotional offer redemption record.']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who redeemed the promotional offer.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who redeemed the promotional offer.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Redemption transactions must be settled in the offer\'s currency; FK validates currency used.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant that redeemed the promotional offer.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Promotional offer redemptions should be traceable to the payment product that generated the offer; child redemption references parent payment_product.']
  promotional_offer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional offer that was redeemed.']
  qualifying_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that qualified for the promotional offer redemption.']
  applied_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any additional fee charged in conjunction with the redemption (e.g., processing fee).']
  applied_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount applied to the redemption transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the redemption record was first created in the system.']
  eligibility_criteria_met varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction met the promotional offer eligibility rules.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score assigned by the fraud detection system for this redemption.']
  is_fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the redemption was flagged for potential fraud.']
  net_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final transaction amount after applying the promotional discount, fees, and taxes.']
  offer_value_applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount or benefit applied from the promotional offer.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the redemption was performed by a merchant, cardholder, or partner.']
  promotional_offer_redemption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the redemption (e.g., pending, completed, declined).']
  qualifying_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the qualifying transaction before any promotional discount.']
  qualifying_transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the transaction that triggered the promotional offer eligibility.']
  redemption_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the promotional offer was redeemed (e.g., online, in‑store, mobile app).']
  redemption_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments captured by the operator regarding the redemption.']
  redemption_quota_remaining varchar [note: 'Remaining number of redemptions available for the promotional offer after this transaction.']
  redemption_quota_total varchar [note: 'Total number of times the promotional offer can be redeemed under the agreement.']
  redemption_reference varchar [note: 'External reference code or number assigned to the redemption event for tracking and reconciliation.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the promotional offer was redeemed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the redemption record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a promotional offer being redeemed by a merchant or cardholder — capturing redemption timestamp, qualifying transaction or enrollment event, offer value applied, redemption channel, and remaining redemption quota. Enables offer utilization tracking, budget burn monitoring, and fraud detection on promotional abuse.'
}

Table product.limit {
  limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each product limit definition.']
  creator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Limit definitions are set by product owners; linking creator supports limit‑change audit.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Limits (e.g., daily, monthly) are expressed in a specific currency; FK ties limits to master currency list.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.payment_product']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Numeric value representing the primary monetary threshold for the limit.']
  atm_withdrawal_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cash withdrawal amount per ATM transaction.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes capturing audit actions or change rationale.']
  bnpl_credit_exposure_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit exposure allowed for a BNPL product.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the limit has been reviewed and approved for compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the limit record was first created.']
  cross_border_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount allowed for cross‑border transactions.']
  daily_cumulative_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for all transactions within a calendar day.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the limit becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the limit expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  enforcement_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the limit is a hard block (transaction declined) or a soft alert.']
  installment_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between successive BNPL installments.']
  is_cross_border_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the limit applies to cross‑border transactions.']
  limit_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the limit, indicating the business purpose.']
  limit_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the limit in downstream systems.']
  limit_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context for the limit.']
  limit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the limit (e.g., "Daily Transaction Cap").']
  limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the limit definition.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the limit applies per transaction (single) or over a period (cumulative).']
  max_installments varchar [note: 'Maximum number of installments allowed for BNPL plans.']
  monthly_cumulative_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for all transactions within a month.']
  period varchar [note: 'Time window to which a cumulative limit applies.']
  regulatory_rationale varchar [note: 'Explanation of the regulatory requirement that drives this limit (e.g., AML, PSD2).']
  risk_rationale varchar [note: 'Business or risk‑based justification for the limit (e.g., fraud exposure).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the limit record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version for optimistic concurrency control.']
  weekly_cumulative_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for all transactions within a week.']

  Note: 'Defines transaction and velocity limits configured at the product level — single transaction maximum, daily/weekly/monthly cumulative limits, cross-border limits, ATM withdrawal caps, and BNPL credit exposure limits. Captures limit type, currency, enforcement mode (hard block vs. soft alert), and the regulatory or risk rationale for each limit. Distinct from cardholder-level spending controls.'
}

Table product.scheme_product_mapping {
  scheme_product_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the scheme product mapping record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Mapping between scheme products and currencies requires a validated currency reference for fee calculations.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'FK to reference.mcc']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Scheme product mapping is specific to a payment product; replace product_code with direct FK to payment_product and remove redundant code column.']
  promotional_offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a promotional offer linked to this mapping.']
  scheme_fee_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.scheme_fee_table. Business justification: Mapping product to scheme requires authoritative fee definitions; linking to scheme_fee_table provides source of fee rates for compliance and fee calculation.']
  scheme_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Scheme Accounting: Mapping a payment scheme to a product requires a GL account for scheme-specific interchange and fee posting.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'FK to network.scheme']
  bin_range_end varchar [note: 'Last BIN in the range allocated to the product.']
  bin_range_start varchar [note: 'First BIN (Bank Identification Number) in the range allocated to the product.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance review.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the mapping.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the mapping record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the mapping becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the mapping expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  eligibility_rule varchar [note: 'Business rule that defines which merchants or cardholders may use this product.']
  fee_billing_indicator varchar [note: 'Method used to bill scheme fees for this product.']
  interchange_category varchar [note: 'Category used by the scheme to determine interchange fees.']
  interchange_fee_category varchar [note: 'Category used for interchange fee calculation.']
  interchange_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Base interchange rate expressed as a percentage.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'True if contactless transactions are allowed.']
  is_default_mapping boolean [note: 'True if this mapping is the default for the associated product.']
  is_international_supported boolean [note: 'True if cross‑border usage is permitted.']
  is_online_supported boolean [note: 'True if the product can be used in online channels.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'True if the product supports tokenization.']
  mapping_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the mapping configuration.']
  network_fee_indicator boolean [note: 'True if a network‑level fee applies to this product.']
  product_tier varchar [note: 'Tier level of the product as defined by the scheme.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the payment product.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the mapping received regulatory sign‑off.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned to the product‑scheme combination.']
  scheme_product_code varchar [note: 'Unique code assigned by the scheme to the product tier.']
  scheme_product_mapping_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the mapping.']
  scheme_product_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the product as defined by the scheme.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Standard settlement timing for the product.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat surcharge amount applied when surcharge_applicable is true.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a surcharge may be applied.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to generate tokens for the product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the mapping record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier for changes to the mapping.']

  Note: 'Maps each card program or payment product to the specific card scheme product codes and program identifiers used by Visa, Mastercard, Amex, and other networks — e.g., Visa Infinite, MC World Elite, Amex Platinum. Captures scheme product code, program tier, scheme-assigned BIN range, and the interchange category the scheme assigns to this product. Required for accurate scheme fee billing and interchange qualification.'
}

Table product.product_lifecycle_event {
  product_lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each product lifecycle event record.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment product to which this lifecycle event applies.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance assessment of the product after the event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the lifecycle change becomes effective for the product.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the lifecycle event occurred or was recorded.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the lifecycle change (e.g., launch, pilot, general availability, deprecation, regulatory suspension, end of life).']
  impacted_population_size varchar [note: 'Number of merchants or cardholders affected by the event.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the migration action is mandatory (true) or optional (false).']
  migration_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of required migration steps or actions for the affected parties.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the event.']
  product_lifecycle_event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the lifecycle event record.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework or authority relevant to the event.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the product post‑event.']
  triggering_authority varchar [note: 'Organizational entity that initiated the lifecycle event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the event record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version of the event record for audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Operational event log capturing significant lifecycle milestones for payment products — launch, pilot, general availability, feature deprecation, regulatory suspension, and end-of-life. Each event records the triggering authority (regulatory body, product committee, risk team), effective date, impacted merchant/cardholder population size, and required migration actions. Supports product governance and regulatory audit.'
}

Table product.terminal_authorization {
  terminal_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the product_terminal_authorization association']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment_product']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pos_terminal']
  support_end_date date [note: 'Date when the payment product support ends on the terminal']
  support_start_date date [note: 'Date when the payment product support begins on the terminal']

  Note: 'This association captures the authorization of a payment product on a specific POS terminal, including the effective support period. Each record links one payment_product to one pos_terminal with attributes that belong only to this relationship.. Existence Justification: A payment product can be authorized on many POS terminals, and a POS terminal can support multiple payment products. The mapping is actively managed by product and terminal teams and includes start and end dates for each authorization period.'
}

Table product.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for party']
  address_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the primary address has been verified.']
  business_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of the market the party belongs to.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the party\'s primary address.']
  communication_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has opted in to receive communications.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the party\'s primary address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the party record was initially created.']
  customer_since_date date [note: 'Date when the party first became a customer.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of an individual party.']
  display_name varchar [note: 'Commonly used name or brand name for the party.']
  email_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the primary email address has been verified.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used for the party in an external partner system.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry classification code (e.g., NAICS) for the party.']
  is_blacklisted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the party is blacklisted for compliance or risk reasons.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Know‑Your‑Customer verification status of the party.']
  last_kyc_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent KYC review for the party.']
  last_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the party record.']
  legal_entity_type varchar [note: 'Legal form of the organization (if party is an entity).']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has opted in to receive marketing messages.']
  name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the party (individual or organization).']
  national_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued personal identifier (e.g., SSN, passport).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the party.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date the party was first onboarded into the system.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party (e.g., individual, organization, merchant, issuer, partner).']
  phone_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the primary phone number has been verified.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the party\'s primary address.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications with the party.']
  primary_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the party\'s primary mailing address.']
  primary_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the party\'s primary mailing address, if applicable.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for communication with the party.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the party.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number assigned by the jurisdiction.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the party (0‑100).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system that supplied the party data.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the party\'s primary address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the party.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (e.g., EIN, TIN) for the party.']
  tax_residency_country varchar [note: 'Country of tax residency for the party.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL associated with the party.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for party. Referenced by party_id.'
}

Table partner.ecosystem_partner {
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the ecosystem partner.']

  currency_id varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for settlements with the partner.']
  default_currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Partner defines a default currency pair for all cross‑border payments, needed for routing, DCC offers and regulatory reporting.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Maps each partner to its legal entity for regulatory reporting, KYC/AML compliance, and consolidated financial statements.']
