  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the partnership contract became effective.']
  contract_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the partnership contract expires, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the partner record was first created.']
  data_privacy_agreement_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner has signed the required data privacy agreement.']
  fraud_monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real‑time fraud monitoring is active for this partner.']
  is_approved_for_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the partner is authorized to process cross‑border transactions.']
  is_global_partner boolean [note: 'True if the partner operates in multiple jurisdictions globally.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit for the partner.']
  network_participation_flags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated flags indicating participation in specific payment networks or schemes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the partner.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the partner completed onboarding and became active.']
  onboarding_stage varchar [note: 'Lifecycle stage of the partner onboarding process.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the partner within the ecosystem.']
  pci_dss_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI DSS compliance status of the partner.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary individual contact for the partner.']
  primary_contact_role varchar [note: 'Role of the primary contact within the partner organization.']
  registration_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the partner is legally registered.']
  regulatory_license_number varchar [note: 'Official license or registration number issued by the relevant financial regulator.']
  revenue_sharing_model varchar [note: 'Model used to share revenue with the partner.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the partner based on AML, fraud, and credit assessments.']
  sla_commitments varchar [note: 'Textual description of Service Level Agreement commitments with the partner.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the partner.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the partnership was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the partner record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all ecosystem partners in the payments network — acquiring banks, issuing banks, card schemes, PSPs, ISOs, PayFacs, aggregators, TSPs, and technology partners. Owns the authoritative partner identity including legal entity name, partner type classification, registration jurisdiction, regulatory license numbers, PCI DSS compliance status, network participation flags, operational status, and onboarding lifecycle stage. SSOT for partner identity across the enterprise.'
}

Table partner.partner_profile {
  partner_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the partner profile record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partner profile extends ecosystem partner; adding partner_id FK creates the required parent‑child relationship and eliminates the silo.']
  partner_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_type. Business justification: Add FK to reference partner_type lookup, replace redundant string column and enable consistent type classification.']
  primary_contact_partner_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.partner_contact. Business justification: Replace duplicated contact details with FK to partner_contact, ensuring single source of truth for contact information.']
  annual_tpv_tier varchar [note: 'Tier of total payment volume processed annually.']
  api_capability_authentication boolean [note: 'Indicates if partner supports authentication APIs (e.g., 3‑DS).']
  api_capability_settlement boolean [note: 'Indicates if partner supports settlement APIs.']
  api_capability_tokenization boolean [note: 'Indicates if partner supports tokenization APIs.']
  business_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of the partner\'s business activities.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the partner.']
  contract_effective_end date [note: 'Contract end date (nullable if open‑ended).']
  contract_effective_start date [note: 'Contract start date.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique contract identifier governing the partnership.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the partner profile record was first created.']
  data_privacy_compliance varchar [note: 'Applicable data privacy and security compliance frameworks.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days partner data is retained per policy.']
  dispute_resolution_contact varchar [note: 'Contact name for dispute resolution communications.']
  dispute_resolution_email varchar [note: 'Email address for dispute resolution contact.']
  geographic_footprint varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where the partner operates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: list of many countries — promote to reference product]']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the partner\'s headquarters street address.']
  headquarters_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the headquarters address (optional).']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City of the partner\'s headquarters.']
  headquarters_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of headquarters.']
  headquarters_state varchar [note: 'State or province of the headquarters.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates if the partner profile is currently active.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'KYC verification status of the partner.']
  last_risk_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent risk review for the partner.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the partner.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the partner was onboarded.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the ecosystem partner.']
  partner_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the partner profile.']
  partner_tier varchar [note: 'Partner tier classification based on revenue sharing and SLA commitments.']
  revenue_sharing_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue shared with the fintech per transaction.']
  risk_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned to the partner based on AML and fraud assessments.']
  service_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Service fee percentage charged to the partner.']
  sla_commitment_days varchar [note: 'Number of days guaranteed for SLA response time.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes supported by the partner.']
  supported_payment_rails varchar [note: 'Payment rails supported by the partner (e.g., ACH, SWIFT, RTP, CARD, NFC, QR, UPI, B2B, P2P). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACH|SWIFT|RTP|CARD|NFC|QR|UPI|B2B|P2P — promote to reference product]']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the partnership was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the partner profile.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the partner.']

  Note: 'Extended business profile for each ecosystem partner capturing operational details beyond identity — headquarters address, primary contact information, website, business description, annual TPV tier, supported payment rails (ACH, SWIFT, RTP, card), geographic footprint (countries served), supported currencies, API capability flags, and partner tier classification (Gold, Silver, Bronze). Complements ecosystem_partner with rich operational metadata.'
}

Table partner.agreement {
  agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each partner agreement.']
  contract_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Legal/contract team designates an employee owner for each partner agreement; needed for responsibility tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for any monetary amounts in the agreement.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the ecosystem partner associated with this agreement.']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_fee_schedule. Business justification: Agreements often embed an FX fee schedule governing cross‑border fees; required for invoicing and compliance.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Links revenue‑share agreements to a GL account for accurate revenue recognition and compliance reporting.']
  partner_contact_id varchar [note: 'FK to partner.partner_contact']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction whose law governs the agreement (e.g., US, EU, UK).']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Agreements are often scheme‑specific (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) and the scheme context is needed for fee, rule, and regulatory compliance calculations.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the agreement, often referenced in legal and operational documents.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the agreement.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Category of the partnership contract (e.g., acquiring bank, ISO/PayFac, Token Service Provider, technology partner, network participation).']
  amendment_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the latest amendment to the agreement (e.g., v1.2).']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent anti‑money‑laundering screening for the partner.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured during audits or reviews of the agreement.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement automatically renews at expiry.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or internal compliance obligations tied to the agreement.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to the agreement.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL pointing to the stored signed contract document.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'System user identifier who created the agreement record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agreement record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the agreement data must be retained for regulatory compliance.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement expires or terminates; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the agreement becomes legally binding.']
  irf_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'IRF rate applicable to the partner, expressed as a percentage.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner has exclusive rights for the covered product or service.']
  kyc_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Know‑Your‑Customer verification has been completed for the partner.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'System user identifier who last modified the agreement record.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum single transaction amount permitted for the partner.']
  max_transaction_volume decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate transaction volume the partner is allowed to process under the agreement.']
  mdr_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard MDR applied to the partner\'s transactions, expressed as a percentage.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiry that a renewal notice must be issued.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction value shared with the partner when revenue_share_type = percentage.']
  revenue_share_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate revenue sharing (percentage of volume, fixed fee, or tiered structure).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the partner based on underwriting criteria.']
  settlement_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between transaction processing and settlement for the partner.']
  signatory_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the signatory for correspondence and audit trails.']
  signatory_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual signing the agreement on behalf of the partner.']
  signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the signatory within the partner organization.']
  sla_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the SLA tier committed to the partner.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Textual description of the conditions under which the agreement may be terminated.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date the agreement was terminated, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the agreement record.']

  Note: 'Contractual agreement records between Payments Fintech and ecosystem partners — covering acquiring agreements, ISO/PayFac sponsorship agreements, network participation agreements, TSP licensing agreements, and technology partner contracts. Captures agreement type, effective date, expiration date, auto-renewal flag, governing law jurisdiction, signatory details, MDR/IRF revenue share terms reference, SLA tier reference, termination clauses, and agreement status lifecycle (draft, active, suspended, terminated).'
}

Table partner.onboarding_application {
  onboarding_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the partner onboarding application.']
  assigned_analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the onboarding analyst responsible for this application.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Onboarding application belongs to a partner; adding partner_id FK links the application to its ecosystem partner and resolves the silo.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the onboarding analyst responsible for this application.']
  aml_screening_date date [note: 'Date the AML screening was performed.']
  aml_screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of Anti-Money Laundering screening.']
  api_credentials_provided boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether API credentials have been provisioned for the partner.']
  applicant_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for communication with the applicant.']
  applicant_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the applicant organization.']
  applicant_legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the partner organization applying for onboarding.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date the onboarding application was submitted.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the onboarding application.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the onboarding application.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding record was first created in the system.']
  due_diligence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score reflecting the outcome of due‑diligence checks.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date the partnership agreement terminates or expires (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date the partnership agreement becomes effective.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Planned date when the partner becomes operational in production.']
  integration_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score indicating technical integration readiness.']
  kyc_completion_date date [note: 'Date KYC verification was completed.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Progress status of Know Your Customer verification.']
  last_status_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent status change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by analysts during review.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Category of partner being onboarded (e.g., acquiring bank, PSP, PayFac).']
  pci_attestation_date date [note: 'Date the PCI DSS attestation was recorded.']
  pci_dss_attestation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the partner\'s PCI DSS compliance attestation.']
  regulatory_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of regulatory compliance screening (e.g., OFAC, sanctions).']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why an application was rejected.']
  revenue_sharing_model varchar [note: 'Model used to calculate revenue share with the partner.']
  risk_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned after assessment.']
  sanction_check_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of sanctions list checking.']
  sla_commitment_level varchar [note: 'Level of service‑level agreement committed to the partner.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the onboarding record.']

  Note: 'Partner onboarding application record tracking the full lifecycle of a new partner joining the ecosystem — from initial application submission through KYC/KYB verification, due diligence, compliance screening (OFAC/AML), technical integration assessment, and final approval or rejection. Captures application date, applicant legal entity, partner type requested, assigned onboarding analyst, KYC status, AML screening result, PCI DSS attestation status, approval decision, rejection reason codes, and go-live date.'
}

Table partner.onboarding_document {
  onboarding_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the onboarding document record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner to which this document belongs.']
  checksum_sha256 varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 hash used to verify file integrity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding document record was first created in the system.']
  document_language varchar [note: 'Language in which the document content is written.']
  document_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the submitted document.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned reference number or code for the document.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the onboarding document indicating its purpose and regulatory relevance.']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version label or number of the document, if multiple revisions are submitted.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the document is no longer considered valid for onboarding.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored document.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in bytes.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'True if the document contains confidential or restricted information.']
  is_original boolean [note: 'True if the uploaded file is the original source document; false if it is a copy or scan.']
  onboarding_document_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the document content or purpose.']
  onboarding_document_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the document within the onboarding workflow.']
  retention_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how long the document must be retained for compliance.']
  storage_path varchar [note: 'Secure URI or file system path where the document file is stored.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the document was initially submitted by the partner.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the onboarding document record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date the document was reviewed and its compliance status confirmed.']
  verifying_analyst varchar [note: 'Name of the analyst who performed the document verification.']

  Note: 'Documents submitted as part of partner onboarding and ongoing compliance — articles of incorporation, PCI DSS certificates of compliance (CoC), AML policy documents, network membership certificates, bank account verification letters, and regulatory license copies. Tracks document type, submission date, expiration date, verification status, verifying analyst, document storage reference, and associated onboarding application.'
}

Table partner.partner_api_credential {
  partner_api_credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner API credential.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the credential.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner to which the credential is issued.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the credential.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credential record was created in the system.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Type of credential issued to the partner.']
  credential_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The secret value of the credential (e.g., API key, client secret, certificate).']
  environment varchar [note: 'Deployment environment for which the credential is valid.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credential expires.']
  ip_allowlist varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IP addresses or CIDR blocks allowed to use the credential.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credential was issued.']
  last_rotation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent credential rotation.']
  partner_api_credential_description varchar [note: 'Human readable description of the credential purpose.']
  partner_api_credential_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credential.']
  permission_scopes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of permission scopes granted to the credential.']
  rate_limit_tier varchar [note: 'Tier defining the allowed request rate for the credential.']
  revocation_status varchar [note: 'Revocation state of the credential.']
  sdk_version varchar [note: 'Version of the SDK associated with the credential.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credential record.']
  usage_quota decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum number of API calls allowed per billing period.']
  usage_reset_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which usage quota resets.']

  Note: 'API integration credentials issued to partners for accessing Payments Fintech platform services — gateway APIs, tokenization APIs, reporting APIs, and webhook endpoints. Captures credential type (API key, OAuth2 client, mTLS certificate), environment (sandbox, production), issued date, expiration date, last rotation date, permission scopes, rate limit tier, IP allowlist, revocation status, and associated SDK version. Critical for partner integration security and access governance.'
}

Table partner.api_credential_rotation {
  api_credential_rotation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each API credential rotation event.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the ecosystem partner whose API credentials are being rotated.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rotation record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this rotation record.']
  credential_scope varchar [note: 'Logical grouping or API surface that the credential grants access to.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credential being rotated.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Error identifier returned if the rotation failed.']
  new_credential_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the newly issued credential.']
  new_credential_issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the new credential became active.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations related to the rotation event.']
  old_credential_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the credential value before rotation (used for audit verification).']
  partner_acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Current acknowledgment state of the partner after rotation.']
  rollback_window_expiry timestamp [note: 'Timestamp after which the old credential can no longer be used for rollback.']
  rotating_actor varchar [note: 'Name or system identifier of the analyst or automated process that performed the rotation.']
  rotation_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of why the rotation was required (e.g., key compromise, policy update).']
  rotation_success_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rotation completed successfully.']
  rotation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the credential rotation was performed.']
  rotation_trigger varchar [note: 'Reason that initiated the rotation: scheduled, security incident, or manual request.']

  Note: 'Audit trail of API credential rotation events for partner integrations — capturing rotation trigger (scheduled, security incident, manual request), old credential reference hash, new credential issued timestamp, rotating analyst or automated process, partner acknowledgment status, and rollback window expiry. Supports PCI DSS key management compliance and security incident response.'
}

Table partner.revenue_share_schedule {
  revenue_share_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the revenue share schedule record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the master agreement that governs the revenue share terms.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ecosystem partner associated with this revenue share schedule.']
  bonus_structure_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any bonus or performance‑based components of the schedule.']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance review outcome for the schedule.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance approval was performed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue share schedule record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for all monetary values in the schedule.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the revenue share schedule becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the revenue share schedule expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  incentive_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary volume threshold that triggers additional incentive payments.']
  irf_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Pass‑through rate applied to the Interchange Reimbursement Fee (expressed as a decimal fraction of the fee).']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the schedule.']
  mdr_split_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the MDR that is allocated to the partner.']
  msf_tier varchar [note: 'Tier identifier (e.g., A, B, C) that determines the MSF sharing percentage.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or operational comments.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'How often revenue share amounts are calculated and paid to the partner.']
  residual_income_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rate applied to residual income calculations for referral partners (e.g., ISO/PayFac).']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the revenue share schedule.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Category of revenue sharing mechanism (e.g., Interchange Reimbursement Fee pass‑through, Merchant Discount Rate split, Merchant Service Fee tier, bonus, or incentive).']
  tier_logic_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining how volume bands map to rates and percentages.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the revenue share schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the schedule for change tracking.']
  volume_threshold varchar [note: 'Transaction volume (count) required to qualify for the defined tier or incentive.']

  Note: 'Revenue sharing schedule defining the economic terms between Payments Fintech and a partner — IRF pass-through rates, MDR split percentages, MSF sharing tiers, volume-based incentive thresholds, residual income rates for ISO/PayFac referrals, and bonus structures. Captures effective date, expiration date, tiering logic (volume bands), currency, payment frequency (monthly, quarterly), and the associated partner agreement. SSOT for partner economics.'
}

Table partner.revenue_share_statement {
  revenue_share_statement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the revenue share statement record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the revenue‑share agreement governing the calculation.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ecosystem partner (acquiring bank, PSP, etc.) to whom the statement applies.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Allows posting of revenue‑share statements to the general ledger, required for financial consolidation and audit.']
  adjustments_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of chargebacks, refunds, penalties, and other adjustments affecting the partner\'s share.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the statement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  gross_interchange_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total interchange fees earned from transactions processed through the partner before any deductions.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the invoice generated for the partner payout.']
  irf_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total IRF paid to card schemes or networks on behalf of the partner.']
  mdr_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total MDR collected from merchants and attributed to the partner.']
  net_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue after subtracting MDR and IRF from gross interchange.']
  partner_share_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of net revenue allocated to the partner as per agreement.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the partner must be paid for the statement.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payout to the partner.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes related to the statement.']
  revenue_share_statement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the statement.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the payout was settled in the financial system.']
  statement_date timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the statement was generated.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the statement, e.g., RSS-2023-07-001.']
  statement_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar date covered by the statement period.']
  statement_period_start date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the statement period.']
  statement_type varchar [note: 'Frequency of the statement issuance.']
  total_payable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable to the partner after all adjustments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the statement record.']

  Note: 'Periodic revenue share statement generated for each partner — capturing the calculation period, total TPV processed through the partner, gross interchange earned, MDR collected, IRF paid to networks, net revenue, partner share amount, adjustments (chargebacks, refunds, penalties), and final payable amount. Supports partner billing reconciliation and Oracle Financials AP integration for partner payouts.'
}

Table partner.sla_commitment {
  sla_commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the SLA commitment record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.agreement. Business justification: Link SLA commitment to its governing agreement for traceability; agreement_number string is redundant.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the ecosystem partner to which this SLA applies.']
  breach_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times the SLA has been breached.']
  breach_last_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent SLA breach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA commitment record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the SLA commitment expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the SLA commitment becomes effective.']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the escalation contact.']
  escalation_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact for SLA escalations.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the escalation contact.']
  last_measured_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent metric measurement for this SLA.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the metric is measured within the measurement window.']
  measurement_frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for measurement frequency (day, hour, minute).']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method or system used to capture the metric (e.g., real‑time monitoring, batch report).']
  measurement_window varchar [note: 'Length of the time window over which the metric is measured.']
  measurement_window_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the measurement window (day, hour, minute).']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'Name of the performance metric measured against the SLA (e.g., "Uptime Percentage").']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments regarding the SLA commitment.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied for each breach of the SLA.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of penalty terms and conditions.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often SLA performance reports are generated.']
  reporting_frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for reporting frequency (day, hour, minute).']
  reporting_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which SLA performance is reported to the partner (e.g., API, dashboard, email).']
  sla_commitment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA commitment.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Category of the SLA commitment (e.g., uptime guarantee, authorization response time).']
  target_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target value (e.g., percent, seconds).']
  target_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric target that the partner must achieve for the metric.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SLA commitment record.']

  Note: 'SLA commitments defined for each partner relationship — covering uptime guarantees, authorization response time targets (TPS thresholds), settlement cutoff SLAs, dispute resolution turnaround times, API availability SLAs, and support response tiers. Captures SLA type, metric name, target value, measurement unit, measurement window, breach penalty terms, and effective period. Distinct from generic product SLAs — these are partner-specific negotiated commitments.'
}

Table partner.sla_breach_event {
  sla_breach_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SLA breach event record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the ecosystem partner associated with the breach.']
  penalty_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice generated for the penalty.']
  remediation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the remediation plan created to address the breach.']
  sla_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.sla_commitment. Business justification: Associate breach events with the specific SLA commitment they violate, enabling detailed reporting.']
  actual_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The observed value of the metric at the time of breach.']
  breach_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the breach circumstances.']
  breach_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of time the SLA metric remained out of compliance, measured in seconds.']
  breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breach event.']
  breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the SLA breach was detected by the monitoring system.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Any supplemental notes or remarks about the breach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach event record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for the penalty amount.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'How the breach was identified (e.g., automated monitoring).']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Internal escalation tier applied to the breach.']
  financial_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty triggered by the breach, before tax.']
  partner_notified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time when the partner was formally notified of the breach.']
  penalty_triggered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the financial penalty was actually applied.']
  recorded_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the system or user that logged the event.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach was considered resolved.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of why the SLA breach occurred.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business impact rating assigned to the breach.']
  sla_metric varchar [note: 'The specific SLA metric that was not met (e.g., transaction processing time).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the breach data.']
  target_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contractual target value for the SLA metric.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the breach event record.']

  Note: 'Recorded instances where a partner SLA commitment was breached — capturing breach detection timestamp, SLA metric breached, actual measured value, target value, breach duration, root cause category, financial penalty triggered, partner notification timestamp, remediation plan reference, and resolution timestamp. Supports partner performance management and penalty billing workflows.'
}

Table partner.referral_record {
  referral_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the referral record.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.account_holder. Business justification: Referral attribution: linking the referring cardholder to the partner referral record enables incentive calculations and performance reporting.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the partner who made the referral.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system process that created the record.']
  primary_referral_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system process that created the record.']
  referred_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant (or sub‑merchant) that was referred.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system process that performed the latest update.']
  compliance_review_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance review was completed.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of any compliance or AML review performed on the referral.']
  conversion_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the referral was converted into an active merchant (if applicable).']
  converted_mid varchar [note: 'MID assigned to the merchant after successful conversion.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the referral record was first created in the system.']
  fee_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the referral qualifies for a commission based on program rules.']
  is_test_referral boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the referral is a test record used for onboarding or QA.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks added by the partner or internal staff.']
  referral_campaign_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the marketing or incentive campaign associated with the referral.']
  referral_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the referral was received.']
  referral_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the partner submitted the referral.']
  referral_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount payable to the referring partner for a successful conversion.']
  referral_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the referral fee.']
  referral_number varchar [note: 'External reference code assigned to the referral for tracking across systems.']
  referral_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the referral.']
  referral_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the geographic region of the referral.']
  referral_type varchar [note: 'Category of the referring partner (ISO, PayFac, Agent, etc.).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the referral record (e.g., CRM, Partner Portal).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the referral record.']

  Note: 'Referral tracking records for ISO, PayFac, and agent partners who refer merchants or sub-merchants to Payments Fintech. Captures referral date, referring partner, referred merchant or entity, referral channel, referral campaign code, conversion status (pending, converted, rejected), conversion date, MID assigned upon conversion, and referral fee eligibility flag. Feeds into revenue_share_statement for referral commission calculation.'
}

Table partner.network_participation {
  network_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each network participation record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the ecosystem partner (acquirer, issuer, PSP, etc.) associated with this network participation.']
  bin_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending value of the BIN range allocated to the partner.']
  bin_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting value of the BIN range allocated to the partner for routing.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the authority or network that issued the certification.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date when the partner\'s certification on the network became effective.']
  certification_expiry date [note: 'Date when the partner\'s certification on the network expires (nullable if open‑ended).']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Type of certification granted to the partner (full, provisional, or revoked).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the participation with network rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the participation record was first created in the system.']
  iin_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending IIN assigned to the partner.']
  iin_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting IIN (Issuer Identification Number) assigned to the partner.']
  is_primary_network boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this network is the primary routing network for the partner.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last compliance audit performed on this participation record.']
  network_member_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the network to the partner (e.g., Visa MID, Mastercard ID).']
  network_name varchar [note: 'Name of the payment network or rail (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, ACH, SWIFT, UPI).']
  network_participation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the participation record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the participation agreement.']
  participation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the participation agreement for tracking and reporting.']
  participation_role varchar [note: 'Role of the partner within the network (acquirer, issuer, processor, or sponsor).']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction revenue shared with the partner under this network participation.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned to the partner\'s participation based on fraud and risk models.']
  sla_commitment varchar [note: 'SLA tier agreed for the participation (e.g., gold, silver).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the participation agreement was terminated.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for terminating the network participation, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the participation record.']

  Note: 'Network participation records defining which payment card schemes and rails a partner is certified and active on — Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, ACH, RTP, SWIFT, UPI. Captures network name, participation role (acquirer, issuer, processor, sponsor), BIN range assignments, IIN registrations, certification date, certification expiry, network-assigned member ID, and participation status. Critical for routing decisions and interchange qualification.'
}

  Note: 'BIN/IIN sponsorship records where an acquiring or issuing bank partner sponsors BIN ranges on behalf of PayFacs, ISOs, or sub-processors. Essential for PayFac and ISO program management.'
}

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contact record.']

  Note: 'Contact persons associated with partner organizations — primary business contacts, technical integration contacts, compliance officers, finance/billing contacts, and executive sponsors.
