}

Table partner.partner_hierarchy {
  partner_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each partner hierarchy record.']
  child_partner_ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the downstream (child) partner in the hierarchy.']
  primary_ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the upstream (parent) partner in the hierarchy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hierarchy record was first created in the system.']
  depth varchar [note: 'Number of levels from the top‑most parent to this child partner (root = 0).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the hierarchical relationship becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the hierarchical relationship ends or is scheduled to end; null if open‑ended.']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the relationship between parent and child partners.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hierarchy relationship is currently active.']
  relationship_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context or notes about the hierarchy relationship.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the parent‑child relationship.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the hierarchy record (e.g., CRM, ERP).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the hierarchy record.']

  Note: 'Organizational hierarchy relationships between partner entities — parent-child relationships for bank holding companies, ISO sub-agent networks, PayFac sub-merchant aggregators, and technology partner subsidiaries. Captures parent partner, child partner, hierarchy type (ownership, sponsorship, referral network), effective date, and hierarchy depth level. Enables roll-up reporting of TPV and revenue across partner networks.'
}

Table partner.integration_endpoint {
  integration_endpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the integration endpoint configuration.']
  partner_api_credential_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the stored credential (API key, certificate, secret) used for authentication.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to authenticate calls to the endpoint.']
  change_log varchar [note: 'Free‑form log of configuration changes and version history.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance posture of the endpoint configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the endpoint record was first created.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date when the endpoint is scheduled to be retired.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to technical documentation or integration guide.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the endpoint configuration becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the endpoint configuration expires or is superseded (null if indefinite).']
  encryption_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used for payload encryption.']
  endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Full URL or network address used to invoke the partner API or messaging service.']
  environment varchar [note: 'Operational environment where the endpoint is provisioned.']
  error_handling_policy varchar [note: 'Policy dictating how errors from the endpoint are processed.']
  health_check_url varchar [note: 'Endpoint used by monitoring tools to verify service health.']
  integration_endpoint_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the endpoint purpose and usage.']
  integration_endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the integration endpoint.']
  integration_endpoint_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the endpoint.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address of the endpoint (if applicable).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the endpoint is currently enabled for traffic.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the endpoint has been deprecated.']
  last_test_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent connectivity test.']
  max_retries varchar [note: 'Maximum number of retry attempts allowed.']
  message_format varchar [note: 'Standard format of messages exchanged on the endpoint.']
  monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether continuous health monitoring is active.']
  monitoring_endpoint varchar [note: 'URL or address of the monitoring service for this endpoint.']
  network_zone varchar [note: 'Logical network zone where the endpoint resides.']
  owner_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the internal owner responsible for the endpoint.']
  owner_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the internal owner responsible for the endpoint.']
  payload_encryption boolean [note: 'Indicates whether request/response payloads are encrypted.']
  port varchar [note: 'TCP/UDP port used for the connection.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Communication protocol used by the endpoint (e.g., REST, SOAP, ISO 8583, ISO 20022, SFTP, Webhook).']
  rate_limit_per_minute varchar [note: 'Maximum number of requests allowed per minute.']
  retry_policy varchar [note: 'Strategy applied when a request fails.']
  sla_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed response time per service level agreement.']
  sla_uptime_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Targeted service availability expressed as a percentage.']
  supported_operations varchar [note: 'Directionality of traffic the endpoint supports.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the last connectivity test.']
  timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum time in seconds to wait for a response before aborting.']
  tls_version varchar [note: 'Transport Layer Security version required for the connection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the endpoint record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label of the endpoint configuration (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'Technical integration endpoint configurations for partner API and messaging connections — REST API endpoints, ISO 8583 socket connections, ISO 20022 messaging endpoints, SFTP batch file exchange endpoints, and webhook delivery URLs. Captures endpoint URL, protocol type, message format standard (ISO 8583, ISO 20022, JSON REST), environment (sandbox/production), TLS version, authentication method, timeout settings, retry policy, and health check URL.'
}

Table partner.partner_certification_record {
  partner_certification_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the partner certification record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the partner to which this certification applies.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification assessment was performed.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official identifier or number assigned by the issuing body to this certification.']
  certification_document_url varchar [note: 'Secure link to the stored certification document or PDF.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the certification (e.g., PCI DSS QSA Assessment, EMVCo Type Approval).']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of certification; possible values include PCI_DSS, EMVCo, 3DS, PA-DSS, Visa_Ready, Mastercard_Engage, AML, KYC. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PCI_DSS|EMVCo|3DS|PA-DSS|Visa_Ready|Mastercard_Engage|AML|KYC — promote to reference product]']
  compliance_level varchar [note: 'Internal rating of compliance maturity for this certification (e.g., level1‑basic to level4‑advanced).']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing overall compliance assessment result.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  document_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA‑256) of the certification document for integrity verification.']
  expiration_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for certification expiration if status is expired (e.g., lapse, revocation).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the certification expires and must be renewed.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'True if the certification is currently valid (i.e., not expired or revoked).']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the certification, such as PCI Security Standards Council, EMVCo, Visa, Mastercard, FinCEN, or other.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the regulatory jurisdiction governing the certification.']
  next_assessment_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next required assessment or re‑certification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or observations about the certification.']
  partner_certification_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation or standard (e.g., PCI DSS v4.0, EMVCo 3DS 2.2).']
  remediation_details varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of required remediation actions, if any.']
  remediation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any remediation actions are required to maintain compliance.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk rating associated with the partner\'s certification status, derived from internal risk models.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the functional or geographic scope covered by the certification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the certification (e.g., v1.2, rev‑3).']

  Note: 'Partner certification and compliance attestation records — PCI DSS QSA assessments, EMVCo type approval certifications, 3DS server certifications, PA-DSS validations, network scheme certifications (Visa Ready, Mastercard Engage), and AML program certifications. Captures certification type, issuing body, certificate number, assessment date, expiry date, scope description, compliance level, and remediation items if any.'
}

Table partner.partner_event {
  partner_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner lifecycle event record.']
  actor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system component that initiated the event.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the partner to which this event relates.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system component that initiated the event.']
  related_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner agreement that is directly affected by this event, if applicable.']
  related_agreement_partner_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner agreement that is directly affected by this event, if applicable.']
  related_application_onboarding_application_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner onboarding or integration application linked to the event.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the event was triggered by an automated system process or a human user.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary driver for the state change.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative describing the reason or context for the event.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'System or application module that generated the event (e.g., Partner Management System, Compliance Engine).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event actually occurred in the business process.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the partner lifecycle event.']
  new_state varchar [note: 'Partner lifecycle status after the event was applied.']
  payload varchar [note: 'Structured or semi‑structured data captured with the event (e.g., JSON snapshot of changed fields).']
  previous_state varchar [note: 'Partner lifecycle status before the event was applied.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this event record (e.g., after enrichment).']

  Note: 'Significant lifecycle events recorded against a partner relationship — partner status changes (activation, suspension, termination), agreement amendments, ownership changes, regulatory action notifications, network membership changes, and escalation events. Captures event type, event timestamp, triggering actor (system or user), previous state, new state, event description, and linked agreement or application reference. Provides a full audit trail of partner relationship history.'
}

Table partner.volume_commitment {
  volume_commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each volume commitment.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the volume commitment entry.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ecosystem partner (acquiring bank, PSP, etc.) associated with this commitment.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the volume commitment entry.']
  actual_tpv_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total payment volume actually processed by the partner in the measurement period.']
  actual_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of transactions the partner actually processed during the measurement period.']
  commitment_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable contract number or code assigned to the volume commitment.']
  commitment_type varchar [note: 'The type of volume commitment (e.g., annual, quarterly, monthly, or one‑time).']
  committed_tpv_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total payment volume (in the measurement currency) the partner guarantees for the commitment period.']
  committed_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of individual transactions the partner must achieve.']
  compliance_review_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the volume commitment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the volume commitment record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The start date when the volume commitment takes effect.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The termination date of the commitment; null if the agreement is open‑ended.']
  incentive_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reward amount payable to the partner for surpassing the committed volume thresholds.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the commitment was last reviewed for compliance and performance.']
  measurement_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the currency for TPV amounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|AUD|CAD|CHF|CNY|INR|BRL — promote to reference product]']
  measurement_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the period over which actual TPV and transaction counts are measured.']
  measurement_start_date date [note: 'First day of the period over which actual TPV and transaction counts are measured.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments, special conditions, or remarks related to the volume commitment.']
  overachievement_flag boolean [note: 'True if actual TPV or transaction count exceeds the committed thresholds.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial penalty charged to the partner for under‑performance relative to the committed volume.']
  shortfall_flag boolean [note: 'True if actual TPV or transaction count is below the committed thresholds.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the volume commitment record.']
  volume_commitment_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the volume commitment.']

  Note: 'Volume commitment records negotiated with partners — minimum annual TPV guarantees, transaction volume floors for preferential pricing, and volume-based incentive targets. Captures commitment period, committed TPV amount, committed transaction count, measurement currency, penalty for shortfall, incentive for overachievement, and actual-vs-committed tracking reference. Supports commercial negotiations and partner performance reviews.'
}

Table partner.partner_settlement_account {
  partner_settlement_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the partner settlement account record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the partner that owns this settlement account.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Required for mapping each partner settlement transaction to a GL account for financial reporting and audit compliance.']
  nostro_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.nostro_account. Business justification: Settlement accounts are linked to internal Nostro accounts for liquidity management of cross‑border flows.']
  account_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the settlement account.']
  account_holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual or entity that holds the settlement account.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Local bank account number (PCI‑DSS protected).']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the settlement account for accounting and payout purposes.']
  bank_branch varchar [note: 'Specific branch of the bank (if applicable) where the account is maintained.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution where the settlement account is held.']
  bic_swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code identifying the bank for international transfers.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance posture of the settlement account with regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement account record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the settlement account data must be retained to satisfy regulatory obligations.']
  designated_use varchar [note: 'Business purpose for which payouts are made to this account.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the settlement account is no longer valid for payouts (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the settlement account became effective for payouts.']
  iban varchar [note: 'Standardized international account identifier used for cross‑border payouts.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance or risk audit performed on the account.']
  partner_settlement_account_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the settlement account.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the settlement account based on AML/KYC and fraud monitoring.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Domestic routing number used for ACH transfers in the United States.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for disbursing funds to the settlement account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement account record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement account was successfully verified.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the account verification process.']

  Note: 'Bank account and settlement account details registered for partner payouts — revenue share disbursements, referral commissions, and incentive payments. Captures account holder name, bank name, account type, IBAN, BIC/SWIFT code, routing number (ACH), account currency, account verification status, verification date, and designated use (revenue share, penalty, refund). Distinct from merchant settlement accounts — these are partner-level financial accounts.'
}

Table partner.partner_fee_schedule {
  partner_fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each partner fee schedule record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fee amount.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner to which this fee schedule applies.']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_fee_schedule. Business justification: Partner‑specific fee schedules reference the master FX fee schedule to calculate per‑transaction FX fees.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Enables posting of partner fee accruals to the general ledger, essential for fee revenue recognition and audit trails.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Fee schedule changes are managed by a specific employee; linking enables change‑approval audit and compliance checks.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Fee schedules are defined per card scheme; linking enables accurate fee lookup and reporting per scheme.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the fee is billed to the partner.']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance review outcome for the fee schedule.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance approval was performed.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee schedule record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule becomes binding.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the fee schedule ends; null for open‑ended.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount for the fee (if applicable).']
  fee_application_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the fee is applied to partner activity.']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum fee amount that can be charged in a billing period.']
  fee_minimum_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum fee amount that will be charged in a billing period.']
  fee_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to the fee base (e.g., 0.0250 for 2.5%).']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Category of fee charged to the partner.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'True if the fee recurs on each billing cycle.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who last modified the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the fee schedule.']
  partner_fee_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the fee schedule purpose and scope.']
  partner_fee_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fee schedule.']
  regulatory_reference_code varchar [note: 'Code linking the fee schedule to a specific regulatory requirement (e.g., PCI_DSS, PSD2).']
  sla_commitment_days varchar [note: 'Number of days partner is given to comply with fee schedule changes per SLA.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee is exempt from tax.']
  tier_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the volume tier thresholds.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fee schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the fee schedule record for change management.']
  volume_tier_max varchar [note: 'Maximum transaction volume (count) for this tier; null if no upper bound.']
  volume_tier_min varchar [note: 'Minimum transaction volume (count) required for this tier.']

  Note: 'Fee schedule defining charges billed TO partners by Payments Fintech — platform access fees, per-transaction processing fees, monthly minimum fees, chargeback handling fees, API call overage fees, and compliance program fees. Captures fee type, fee amount or rate, billing frequency, effective date, expiration date, volume tier thresholds, and currency. Distinct from revenue_share_schedule which defines amounts paid OUT to partners.'
}

Table partner.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each partner invoice record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Supports allocation of partner invoice amounts to internal cost centers for budgeting and cost analysis.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Invoices must record the employee who generated them for internal accounting, dispute resolution, and audit trails.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the invoice amounts.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner to whom the invoice is issued.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Necessary to post partner invoices to a GL account for revenue accounting and financial statement preparation.']
  payment_rail_id varchar [note: 'Method by which settlement to the partner is performed.']
  reversal_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice that reverses this invoice, if applicable.']
  ar_reference_number varchar [note: 'Accounts Receivable reference number in Oracle Financials linked to this invoice.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by the invoice.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by the invoice.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the invoice record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discount amount applied to the invoice, if any.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment for the invoice is due.']
  exchange_rate_to_usd decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert invoice currency to USD for reporting.']
  external_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number provided by the partner (if they issue a reciprocal invoice).']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of platform and processing fees included in the invoice.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was generated and issued to the partner.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the invoice by the Payments Fintech system.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|paid|partial|overdue|voided|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Category of fee or charge represented by the invoice.']
  is_credit_memo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is a credit memo (negative amount).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the invoice record.']
  last_payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent payment applied to the invoice.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of line items included in the invoice.']
  net_amount_due decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount payable after taxes, fees, penalties, and discounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments related to the invoice.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the partner is expected to settle the invoice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|bank_transfer|ach|wire|digital_wallet|crypto — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment settlement status of the invoice.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the partner.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Penalty or late fee amount assessed on the invoice, if any.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for settlement of the invoice with the partner.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the invoice is exempt from tax.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross total amount billed on the invoice before any adjustments.']
  total_quantity varchar [note: 'Aggregate quantity of units across all line items (e.g., total transaction count).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']
  vat_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable VAT rate applied to the invoice, if tax is not exempt.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for voiding the invoice.']
  voided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice has been voided.']

  Note: 'Invoices issued to partners for platform fees, processing charges, and penalty assessments — distinct from revenue share statements which represent outbound payments. Captures invoice number, billing period, invoice date, due date, line items (fee type, quantity, unit rate, amount), total amount due, currency, payment status, and Oracle Financials AR reference. Supports the ERP AR module integration for partner billing.'
}

Table partner.partner_type {
  partner_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each partner type record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code used for settlements with this partner type.']
  payment_rail_id varchar [note: 'Preferred settlement method for transactions involving this partner type.']
  aml_screening_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening is required for partners of this type.']
  compliance_last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance review for this partner type.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the partner type record was first created in the system.']
  data_privacy_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the partner type is required to meet data‑privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR, CCPA).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the partner type definition becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the partner type definition is retired or superseded (null if indefinite).']
  fee_structure_type varchar [note: 'Type of fee structure applied to this partner type.']
  governing_body_standards varchar [note: 'Applicable industry standards or regulatory frameworks governing this partner type (e.g., PCI DSS, EMVCo, PSD2, ISO 20022). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PCI_DSS|EMVCo|PSD2|ISO_20022|FATF|SOX|GDPR|CCPA — promote to reference product]']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner type is currently in active use within the organization.']
  kyc_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Know‑Your‑Customer verification is mandatory for partners of this type.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last compliance or governance review of this partner type.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum single transaction amount permitted for this partner type.']
  max_transaction_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate transaction volume (in currency_code) allowed for this partner type per reporting period.']
  network_role varchar [note: 'Specific functional role(s) the partner plays in the payment network (e.g., acquiring, issuing, settlement, tokenization). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acquiring_bank|issuing_bank|card_scheme|psp|iso|payfac|aggregator|tsp|technology_partner|bnpl_provider|digital_wallet_provider|cross_border_specialist — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or operational notes about the partner type.']
  partner_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the partner for regulatory and risk purposes.']
  partner_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the partner type, its role in the payments ecosystem, and typical services offered.']
  revenue_share_model varchar [note: 'Model used to calculate revenue share with partners of this type.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the partner type based on regulatory and operational risk assessments.']
  sla_commitment_days varchar [note: 'Number of days within which service level agreements must be fulfilled for this partner type.']
  surcharge_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'True if transaction surcharges may be applied to this partner type under regulatory rules.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Compact alphanumeric code used to reference the partner type in system integrations.']
  type_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the partner type (e.g., Acquiring Bank, Issuing Bank, PSP).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the partner type record.']

  Note: 'Reference classification of partner types in the payments ecosystem — Acquiring Bank, Issuing Bank, Card Scheme, PSP, ISO, PayFac, Aggregator, TSP, Technology Partner, BNPL Provider, Digital Wallet Provider, and Cross-Border Specialist. Captures type code, type name, regulatory classification, typical network roles, and applicable governing body standards. Used for partner segmentation, routing logic, and compliance rule application.'
}

Table partner.integration_test_result {
  integration_test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each integration test result record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ecosystem partner being certified.']
  test_case_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the individual test case within the suite.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test satisfied all regulatory and compliance checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result record was first created in the system.']
  execution_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time taken to execute the test case, measured in seconds.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the test case was executed.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation when the test result is a failure or block.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'True if the test case was executed automatically without manual intervention.']
  logs_uri varchar [note: 'Location (URL or storage path) of detailed execution logs and artifacts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or observations.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the test case execution.']
  risk_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the risk impact of any failure observed.']
  sign_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test result has been formally signed off for go‑live.']
  sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the sign‑off was recorded.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the partner\'s software component being validated.']
  test_case_actual_outcome varchar [note: 'The observed result from the test execution.']
  test_case_category varchar [note: 'Broad functional area the test case belongs to.']
  test_case_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the test case was originally created in the test management system.']
  test_case_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the test case purpose and scope.']
  test_case_expected_outcome varchar [note: 'The anticipated result defined for the test case.']
  test_case_last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent modification time of the test case definition.']
  test_case_owner varchar [note: 'Team or individual responsible for the test case definition.']
  test_case_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the test case.']
  test_case_result_code varchar [note: 'Machine‑readable code representing the result outcome (e.g., ERR001, PASS).']
  test_case_result_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable message accompanying the result code.']
  test_data_set_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the data set used during the test (e.g., sample transaction file).']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Environment in which the test was run (e.g., sandbox, staging, production).']
  test_suite_name varchar [note: 'Name of the test suite that groups related test cases for partner integration certification.']
  test_tool_name varchar [note: 'Name of the testing framework or tool used to execute the test.']
  test_tool_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the testing framework or tool.']
  tester_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or automated agent that performed the test.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the test result record.']
  validation_schema_version varchar [note: 'Version of the schema or contract against which the test was validated.']

  Note: 'Results of partner integration certification testing — sandbox API testing, ISO 8583 message format validation, EMV chip transaction testing, 3DS authentication flow testing, and settlement file format validation. Captures test suite name, test case ID, test execution date, pass/fail result, failure reason, tester identity, environment, software version under test, and sign-off status. Required before partner go-live approval.'
}

Table partner.performance_period {
  performance_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key uniquely identifying each partner performance snapshot for a measurement period.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ecosystem partner whose performance is being recorded.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Partner performance reviews are performed by a designated employee; linking supports performance dashboards and compliance reporting.']
  authorization_approval_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction authorizations that were approved for the partner (approved / total attempts).']
  chargeback_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of settled transactions that resulted in a chargeback during the period.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the partner with respect to regulatory and internal policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance record was first inserted.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that supplied the raw performance data (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  fraud_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transactions flagged as fraudulent for the partner in the period.']
  measurement_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reporting period (inclusive).']
  measurement_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reporting period (inclusive).']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the performance snapshot was generated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the partner\'s performance for the period.']
  partner_tier varchar [note: 'Current commercial tier classification of the partner based on volume and performance.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the reporting period; either monthly or quarterly.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk rating for the partner based on fraud, compliance, and operational metrics.']
  settlement_timeliness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) measuring how promptly the partner\'s settlements were completed relative to SLA targets.']
  sla_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score (0‑100) reflecting the partner\'s adherence to agreed Service Level Agreement metrics for the period.']
  total_tpv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary volume of all transactions processed for the partner during the period, expressed in the settlement currency.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual payment transactions attributed to the partner in the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the performance record.']
  volume_commitment_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner met its contractual transaction volume commitment for the period.']

  Note: 'Periodic partner performance measurement records — capturing a partner\'s actual TPV, transaction count, authorization approval rate, chargeback rate, SLA compliance score, fraud rate, and settlement timeliness for a given measurement period (monthly or quarterly). Feeds partner tier reviews, volume commitment tracking, and commercial renegotiation triggers. Distinct from individual SLA breach events — this is the aggregated performance snapshot per period.'
}

Table partner.sub_merchant_registration {
  sub_merchant_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sub-merchant registration record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Sub‑merchant may have a default currency pair for DCC transactions; the pair drives conversion rates shown to cardholders.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PayFac or aggregator partner sponsoring the sub‑merchant.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Sub‑merchant onboarding must record the card scheme under which the merchant will process transactions for compliance and settlement.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the sub‑merchant registration.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the sub‑merchant\'s registered location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of the anti‑money‑laundering screening for the sub‑merchant.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the sub‑merchant\'s registered address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance posture of the sub‑merchant with regulatory and internal policies.']
  contract_effective_end date [note: 'Date the contract expires or is scheduled to terminate.']
  contract_effective_start date [note: 'Date the contract became legally effective.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the contractual agreement between the sub‑merchant and the sponsor partner.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the registered address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the registration record was first created in the system.']
  data_privacy_agreement_signed boolean [note: 'True if the sub‑merchant has signed the data‑privacy agreement required for processing.']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'Trade name under which the sub-merchant operates.']
  irf_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Interchange fee rate applicable to the sub‑merchant\'s transactions.']
  is_global_partner boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor partner operates globally (true) or regionally (false).']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Know‑Your‑Customer verification process.']
  last_kyc_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent KYC review or re‑verification.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the sub-merchant entity.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code that classifies the sub‑merchant\'s business activity.']
  mdr_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard fee rate charged to the sub‑merchant per transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or comments about the registration.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the registered address.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date the sub‑merchant registration became effective.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of gross revenue allocated to the sponsor partner.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) derived from fraud and AML assessments.']
  sanctions_check_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of sanctions list checks for the sub‑merchant.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for settlement of the sub‑merchant\'s funds.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to settle funds to the sub‑merchant.']
  sla_tier_code varchar [note: 'Tier level of service‑level commitments agreed with the sponsor partner.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the registered address.']
  sub_mid varchar [note: 'Merchant Identification Number assigned to the sub‑merchant within the PayFac ecosystem.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the sub‑merchant registration was terminated, if applicable.']
  transaction_limit_tier varchar [note: 'Tier defining the maximum transaction volume or amount the sub‑merchant is permitted.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the registration record.']

  Note: 'Sub-merchant registration records managed by PayFac and aggregator partners on behalf of their sub-merchants. Captures sub-merchant legal name, DBA name, MCC, registered address, PayFac sponsor partner, assigned sub-MID, registration date, KYC verification status, transaction limit tier, and active status. Distinct from the merchant domain\'s full merchant master — this is the partner-domain view of sub-merchant registrations owned by PayFac partners.'
}

Table partner.manager_assignment {
  manager_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the partner_manager_assignment association']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the ecosystem partner']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the employee stopped managing the partner (null if current)']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the employee started managing the partner']
  manager_role varchar [note: 'The role of the employee in managing the partner (e.g., Regional Manager, Product Specialist)']

  Note: 'This association product represents the assignment of account‑manager employees to ecosystem partners. It captures which employee manages which partner, the role of the employee in that relationship, and the effective period of the assignment.. Existence Justification: An ecosystem partner can be serviced by multiple account‑manager employees and an employee can manage multiple ecosystem partners. The business actively creates, updates, and deletes these assignments, tracking the manager role and the active period for each assignment.'
}

Table partner.rail_onboarding {
  rail_onboarding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the partner_rail_onboarding association']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecosystem_partner']
  payment_rail_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment_rail']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of the partner for the specific payment rail']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date the partner was onboarded to the payment rail']

  Note: 'This association product represents the onboarding contract between an ecosystem partner and a payment rail. It captures the date a partner was onboarded to a rail and the compliance status of that partnership. Each record links one ecosystem_partner to one payment_rail with attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: An ecosystem partner can be onboarded to multiple payment rails, and each payment rail serves many ecosystem partners. The onboarding process records an onboarding date and a compliance status for each partner‑rail pairing. This relationship is actively managed by the business as a distinct entity.'
}

Table partner.integration_test_case {
  integration_test_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for integration_test_case']
  depends_on_integration_test_case_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on integration_test_case (depends_on_integration_test_case_id)']
  associated_feature varchar [note: 'Product or platform feature that this test case validates.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards requirement covered by the test case.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the test case record was first created.']
  data_volume_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical size of request/response payloads in megabytes.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date on which the test case becomes deprecated.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to detailed documentation or runbook for the test case.']
  execution_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the test case is scheduled to run.']
  expected_response_code varchar [note: 'HTTP response code(s) expected from a successful execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 200|201|400|401|403|404|500 — promote to reference product]']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Root cause description when the last execution failed.']
  http_method varchar [note: 'HTTP verb used when invoking the API.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test case is executed automatically by CI/CD pipelines.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether failure of this test case is considered critical to operations.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test case is marked for deprecation.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the test case was last executed.']
  last_execution_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent test execution.']
  max_retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of automatic retries on failure.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text notes or comments.']
  notification_email varchar [note: 'Email address to receive test execution alerts.']
  notification_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether notifications are sent for this test case.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Team or individual responsible for maintaining the test case.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the test case.']
  regression_impact varchar [note: 'Potential impact on regression suites if this test case changes.']
  related_api_endpoint varchar [note: 'Full URL of the API endpoint exercised by the test case.']
  request_payload_schema varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the JSON schema of the request payload.']
  retry_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Delay between retry attempts.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with test case failure.']
  sla_actual_seconds varchar [note: 'Observed execution time of the most recent run.']
  sla_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed execution time for the test case as defined in the service level agreement.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test case.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Free‑form tags for ad‑hoc categorisation.']
  test_case_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the test case within the catalog.']
  test_case_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the integration test case.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business domain the test case belongs to.']
  test_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Typical execution time for the test case.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Target environment where the test case is executed.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Category of test performed (e.g., functional, performance).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated the test case record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the test case record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version of the test case definition.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the test case record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for integration_test_case. Referenced by test_case_id.'
}

Table gateway.gateway_api_credential {
  gateway_api_credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the API credential record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partner onboarding requires each gateway API credential to be linked to the owning partner for billing, compliance audit, and credential lifecycle management.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service account that created the credential.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: API credentials are issued per merchant; linking enables authentication audit, usage reporting, and revocation per merchant.']
  allowed_ip_ranges varchar [note: 'Network CIDR blocks from which the credential may be used; empty means unrestricted.']
  audience varchar [note: 'Recipient(s) that the token is intended for, often a service identifier.']
  audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier linking to a detailed audit trail stored in the compliance system.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Specifies the protocol or mechanism by which the credential authenticates API calls.']
  certificate_thumbprint varchar [note: 'SHA‑1/256 hash of the public certificate associated with the credential.']
  client_identifier varchar [note: 'Public identifier for an OAuth client used during token acquisition.']
  client_secret varchar [note: 'Confidential secret associated with an OAuth client credential.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the credential meets current PCI DSS, GDPR, and internal security policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credential record was first created in the system.']
  credential_key varchar [note: 'Public API key or client identifier presented by the merchant/partner when invoking gateway APIs.']
  credential_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name assigned to the credential for easy identification by operations and security teams.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the technical mechanism of the credential (e.g., static API key, OAuth client, HMAC secret, X.509 certificate).']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Organizational classification indicating handling and access controls for the credential data.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Calendar date on which the credential is permitted to be used.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Calendar date after which the credential must no longer be accepted; null indicates no fixed expiry.']
  encryption_algorithm varchar [note: 'Cryptographic algorithm employed to protect the credential material.']
  environment varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the credential is intended for live production traffic or testing/sandbox environments.']
  gateway_api_credential_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the credential is usable, disabled, revoked, or awaiting activation.']
  hash_algorithm varchar [note: 'Digest algorithm used for HMAC or signature verification.']
  hmac_secret varchar [note: 'Shared secret used to compute HMAC signatures for request authentication.']
  issuer varchar [note: 'Name of the authority or system that issued the credential.']
  key_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the cryptographic key, used in token headers.']
  last_rotated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credential was most recently rotated.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the last successful API call authenticated with this credential.']
  max_concurrent_sessions varchar [note: 'Upper limit on simultaneous active sessions or tokens for this credential.']
  next_rotation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the next required rotation of the credential.']
  rate_limit_per_minute varchar [note: 'Maximum number of API calls permitted in any rolling 60‑second window.']
  regulatory_review_date date [note: 'Date when the credential was last examined for regulatory compliance.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation for why the credential was revoked.']
  revoked_flag boolean [note: 'True if the credential has been revoked and must no longer be accepted.']
  revoked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the revocation became effective.']
  rotation_interval_days varchar [note: 'Policy‑driven interval after which the credential must be regenerated.']
  scopes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of permission scopes granted to the credential (e.g., transactions:read, settlements:write).']
  token_endpoint varchar [note: 'OAuth token endpoint used by the client to obtain access tokens.']
  token_expiry_seconds varchar [note: 'Lifetime of issued access tokens, expressed in seconds.']
  token_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the token follows Bearer or MAC token semantics.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service account that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the credential record.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Running total of successful API calls made with this credential.']
  usage_quota decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total number of allowed invocations or monetary value before the credential is throttled or blocked.']

  Note: 'Master record for merchant and partner API authentication credentials used to access the payment gateway. Stores API keys, OAuth client IDs/secrets, HMAC signing keys, certificate thumbprints, credential rotation schedules, permission scopes, and revocation status. SSOT for gateway authentication identity — every inbound API call is authenticated against this record.'
}

Table gateway.merchant_integration {
  merchant_integration_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the merchant integration configuration record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Integration records must capture the partner that facilitated merchant onboarding, used in partner performance dashboards and revenue‑share calculations.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: Required for onboarding: each merchant integration config must be linked to its merchant for routing, reporting, and compliance.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Merchant integration configuration is product‑specific, linking integration to the payment product it supports for onboarding and monitoring.']
  api_key varchar [note: 'Secret API key credential used for authentication when authentication_method = api_key.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to authenticate calls from the merchant (API key, OAuth, or mutual TLS).']
  compliance_last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit or review for this integration.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the integration with PCI DSS and other regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this integration record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the integration configuration becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the integration configuration expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Fully qualified URL of the merchant\'s API endpoint or callback address.']
  environment varchar [note: 'Operational environment for the integration – sandbox for testing or production for live traffic.']
  gateway_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or data‑center where the integration is hosted (e.g., us-east-1).']
  integration_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category of the integration (e.g., payment, refund).']
  integration_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name given to this integration profile by the merchant or operations team.']
  integration_type varchar [note: 'Technical style of the integration (e.g., REST API, SOAP web service, ISO 8583 message, native SDK).']
  is_rate_limited boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether rate limiting is enforced for this integration.']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'Indicates if the integration uses tokenization for cardholder data.']
  last_tested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent connectivity or compliance test performed on the integration.']
  merchant_integration_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the integration purpose, scope, or special notes.']
  merchant_integration_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the integration profile.']
  oauth_client_identifier varchar [note: 'Client identifier issued to the merchant for OAuth‑based authentication.']
  oauth_scopes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of OAuth scopes granted to the merchant integration.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the merchant\'s onboarding workflow for this integration.']
  pci_dss_compliance_tier varchar [note: 'PCI DSS compliance tier assigned to the integration based on data handling scope.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the integration protocol or API specification used.']
  rate_limit_per_minute varchar [note: 'Maximum number of API calls allowed per minute for this integration.']
  support_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the merchant\'s technical support contact for this integration.']
  support_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the merchant\'s technical support contact for this integration.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest integration test (passed, failed, or not yet tested).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this integration record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the integration configuration (e.g., release tag).']
  webhook_secret varchar [note: 'Shared secret used to sign webhook payloads for integrity verification.']
  webhook_url varchar [note: 'URL to which the gateway will POST event notifications for this integration.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record for each merchant\'s API or SDK integration with the payment gateway. Captures integration type (REST, SOAP, ISO 8583, SDK), protocol version, endpoint URLs, MID linkage, integration environment (sandbox/production), onboarding status, PCI DSS compliance tier, and assigned gateway region. One merchant may have multiple integration profiles across channels.'
}

Table gateway.gateway_routing_rule {
  gateway_routing_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the routing rule.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Primary acquirer endpoint to which matching transactions are routed.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Rules may target a specific card scheme; linking to reference_card_scheme provides scheme details and compliance data.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Routing rules are applied per currency; linking to reference.currency enables validation, reporting, and compliance per ISO currency.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partners often have custom routing rules; linking enables rule‑ownership reporting, SLA monitoring, and regulatory routing audits.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User or system that created the rule.']
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: Routing: rules need to reference IRF rate categories to apply correct interchange rates per regulatory and scheme requirements.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: MCC‑based routing decisions require the authoritative MCC reference for accurate categorisation and risk scoring.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Routing engine uses product_id to select routing rule for each payment product, required for rule‑based acquirer selection.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Required for scheme‑based routing decisions; the routing engine must know which card scheme a rule applies to for compliance reporting.']
  target_acquirer_acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Primary acquirer endpoint to which matching transactions are routed.']
  amount_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper bound of transaction amount for rule matching.']
  amount_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lower bound of transaction amount for rule matching.']
  bin_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending BIN of the rule\'s matching range.']
  bin_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting BIN (Bank Identification Number) of the rule\'s matching range.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the transaction originates.']
  compliance_check_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the transaction must pass additional compliance checks before routing.']
  compliance_rule_set varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance rule set applied to transactions routed by this rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the rule expires (null for indefinite).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes active.']
  external_rule_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rule in an external system, if any.']
  failover_acquirer_sequence varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of acquirer IDs used for failover routing.']
  fraud_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum fraud risk score required for the rule to be applied.']
  gateway_routing_rule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the rule purpose and logic.']
  gateway_routing_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  is_default_rule boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule serves as the fallback/default routing rule.']
  is_rate_limited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule enforces a transaction rate limit.']
  load_balancing_weight varchar [note: 'Relative weight for load‑balancing among multiple target acquirers.']
  match_criteria varchar [note: 'JSON representation of complex matching conditions not covered by individual columns.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes for operational teams.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Evaluation order; lower numbers are evaluated first.']
  rate_limit_per_second varchar [note: 'Maximum number of transactions per second allowed under this rule.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country/region code for geographic routing.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification associated with the rule.']
  routing_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to select among multiple target acquirers.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the rule in external systems.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the routing rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rule logic (e.g., least‑cost, scheme‑preference, geographic, custom).']
  sla_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Target maximum response time for routing decisions, in milliseconds.']
  tokenization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the transaction must be tokenized before routing.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Type of transaction the rule applies to.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rule.']

  Note: 'Business-managed transaction routing rule defining how inbound payment requests are directed to acquirer endpoints. Captures rule priority, matching criteria (BIN range, MCC, currency, amount threshold, card scheme, region), target acquirer endpoint, failover sequence, load-balancing weight, effective date range, and rule status. Supports least-cost routing, scheme-preference routing, and geographic routing strategies.'
}

Table gateway.acquirer_endpoint {
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the acquirer endpoint configuration.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partner‑specific acquirer endpoints are configured for settlement and risk management; the FK ties endpoint health and compliance to the responsible partner.']
  failover_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the secondary endpoint used for failover.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Fee accounting process maps each acquirer endpoint to a GL account for posting acquisition fees and settlement adjustments.']
  acquirer_bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC of the acquirer, 8‑ or 11‑character code.']
  acquirer_bin varchar [note: 'First six digits of the acquirer\'s card number range.']
  acquirer_endpoint_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the endpoint purpose, notes, or special handling instructions.']
  acquirer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the acquiring financial institution.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory frameworks applicable to this endpoint (e.g., PCI_DSS, PSD2, EMVCo).']
  connection_pool_size varchar [note: 'Maximum number of simultaneous connections maintained to the acquirer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the endpoint record was first created.']
  cross_border_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional fee charged for cross‑border transactions, expressed in the settlement currency.']
  currency_conversion_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the acquirer can process cross‑currency transactions for this endpoint.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the endpoint configuration becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the endpoint configuration expires or is retired (null if open‑ended).']
  endpoint_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the endpoint in operational processes.']
  endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the acquirer connection endpoint.']
  failover_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic failover to a secondary endpoint is active.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where this endpoint is active. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|AUS|CHN|IND|BRA — promote to reference product]']
  health_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent automated health check.']
  host_url varchar [note: 'Fully qualified domain name or IP address of the acquirer endpoint.']
  is_tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the acquirer supports tokenized card data for this endpoint.']
  last_health_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent health‑check execution.']
  max_concurrent_connections varchar [note: 'Hard limit on concurrent active sessions allowed for this endpoint.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the endpoint.']
  port varchar [note: 'TCP port used for the connection.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Technical protocol used for communication with the acquirer.']
  rate_limit_per_sec varchar [note: 'Allowed number of requests per second to the acquirer.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency used for settlement with the acquirer.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether settlement is net‑based or gross‑based.']
  supported_card_schemes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of card scheme identifiers (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, Amex) that this endpoint can process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Visa|Mastercard|Amex|Discover|JCB|UnionPay — promote to reference product]']
  timeout_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a response before timing out.']
  tls_certificate_expiration date [note: 'Date on which the TLS certificate expires.']
  tls_certificate_thumbprint varchar [note: 'SHA‑1 thumbprint of the TLS certificate presented by the acquirer.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the token service provider (TSP) associated with this endpoint, if any.']
  transaction_fee_fixed_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary fee applied per transaction, expressed in the settlement currency.']
  transaction_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage fee applied to each transaction processed through this endpoint.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the endpoint record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each registered acquirer connection endpoint available to the gateway. Stores acquirer BIC/BIN, connection protocol (ISO 8583, REST, SWIFT), host URL, port, TLS certificate details, connection pool size, timeout thresholds, supported card schemes, settlement currency, geographic coverage, and operational status. SSOT for acquirer connectivity configuration within the gateway domain.'
}

Table gateway.gateway_protocol_config {
  gateway_protocol_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each protocol configuration record.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_region. Business justification: Gateway protocol configurations are region-specific; linking to gateway_region enables regional management and compliance.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate inbound API calls for this configuration.']
  bitmap_configuration varchar [note: 'Definition of bitmap layout for ISO‑8583 messages.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change request that introduced or modified this configuration.']
  character_set varchar [note: 'Specific character set applied to the message (e.g., ISO‑8859‑1).']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard that this configuration adheres to.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the protocol configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the configuration record was first created in the system.']
  deprecation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation why the configuration was deprecated, if applicable.']
  deprecation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration was officially deprecated.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the technical documentation for this configuration.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration becomes active for routing.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration is retired; null if indefinite.']
  encoding_scheme varchar [note: 'Character encoding used for message payloads (e.g., UTF-8, ASCII).']
  error_handling_mode varchar [note: 'Defines how parsing or transformation errors are treated.']
  field_mapping_definitions varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded rules that map source fields to target fields for the protocol.']
  gateway_protocol_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and scope of the configuration.']
  gateway_protocol_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the configuration.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this configuration is the default for its protocol.']
  last_tested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful end‑to‑end test of the configuration.']
  max_payload_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Upper limit on the size of a single message payload.']
  mti_mappings varchar [note: 'Mapping definition between source and target MTI values for the protocol.']
  owner_team varchar [note: 'Business team responsible for maintaining the configuration.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used when multiple configurations match; lower numbers have higher precedence.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Messaging standard that this configuration translates to and from.']
  rate_limit_per_second varchar [note: 'Maximum number of messages that can be processed per second under this configuration.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if deployment of this configuration requires formal regulatory sign‑off.']
  retry_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how failed message deliveries are retried.']
  supported_message_versions varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of protocol version identifiers that this configuration supports.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the configuration is intended for sandbox or production use.']
  timeout_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum time to wait for a response before considering the transaction timed out.']
  transformation_pipeline varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing pipeline that applies additional transformations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the configuration record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the protocol specification (e.g., 1.0, 2.1).']

  Note: 'Configuration record defining protocol translation parameters for each supported messaging standard (ISO 8583, ISO 20022, REST, SOAP, GraphQL). Captures message format version, field mapping definitions, encoding scheme, character set, MTI (Message Type Indicator) mappings, bitmap configuration, and transformation pipeline reference. Enables the gateway to translate between merchant-facing and acquirer-facing protocols.'
}

Table gateway.request {
  request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inbound API request record.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Required for transaction monitoring report linking each gateway request to the originating cardholder profile for fraud detection and compliance.']
  client_application_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client application (e.g., mobile SDK, web app) making the request.']
  contactless_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that generated the request.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Geo‑country code in request drives jurisdictional risk checks; linking to reference.country enables AML/OFAC screening.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Incoming requests include a currency code; FK to reference.currency validates the code and supports settlement reporting.']
  device_fingerprint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fraud.device_fingerprint. Business justification: Request logs reference captured device fingerprint to feed fraud risk models and support device‑based investigations.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Required for linking each payment request originating from a digital wallet to the wallet, enabling fraud monitoring, transaction traceability, and settlement reporting.']
  gateway_api_credential_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the API credential (key/secret) used to authenticate the request.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant (MID) that originated the request.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Transaction request includes payment product identifier to apply correct fee schedule and routing logic.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that generated the request.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: FX rate lookup needed during processing of multi‑currency transactions; request stores the applied rate for compliance and reporting.']
  rate_limit_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate‑limit rule that applied (if any).']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.wallet_transaction. Business justification: Directly ties the gateway request to the wallet transaction it represents, supporting real‑time monitoring and end‑to‑end transaction audit.']
  client_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the client application.']
