  correlation_identifier varchar [note: 'Client‑provided identifier for tracing the request across systems.']
  endpoint_path varchar [note: 'API endpoint path invoked by the request (e.g., /v1/authorize).']
  headers varchar [note: 'Serialized HTTP headers of the inbound request.']
  http_method varchar [note: 'HTTP verb used for the request.']
  idempotency_key varchar [note: 'Client‑provided key to ensure idempotent processing of the request.']
  is_rate_limited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request was blocked or throttled by rate‑limit rules.']
  latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time elapsed from request receipt to completion of initial validation, measured in milliseconds.']
  payload_truncated varchar [note: 'First 256 characters of the request payload for quick inspection (full payload stored elsewhere).']
  processing_node_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the gateway server node that processed the request.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Protocol used for the inbound request (e.g., ISO8583, REST, SOAP).']
  raw_message_hash varchar [note: 'Hash (e.g., SHA‑256) of the raw request message for integrity verification.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the gateway received the API request (event time).']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Business operation the request is attempting (authorization, capture, void, refund, inquiry).']
  schema_version varchar [note: 'Version of the API request schema used by the client.']
  size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the raw request payload in bytes.']
  source_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the client that initiated the request.']
  source_port varchar [note: 'Network port on the client side used for the request.']
  tls_version varchar [note: 'TLS protocol version negotiated for the request.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'User‑agent string supplied by the client application.']
  validation_error_code varchar [note: 'Error code returned when validation_outcome is \'rejected\' or \'error\'.']
  validation_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the gateway\'s initial syntactic/semantic validation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every inbound API request received by the payment gateway before authorization processing. Stores request timestamp, originating MID, TID, API credential reference, request type (authorization, capture, void, refund, inquiry), raw message hash, protocol used, source IP, request size, TLS version, and initial validation outcome. Serves as the immutable entry-point audit record for every gateway interaction.'
}

Table gateway.response {
  response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gateway response record.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Card scheme in response is needed for scheme‑specific settlement rules; reference link provides authoritative data.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Enables reconciliation of response data with the cardholder profile for dispute resolution and regulatory audit of transaction outcomes.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Response includes currency; FK to reference.currency ensures accurate reporting and reconciliation.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Needed to associate the gateway\'s response with the originating digital wallet for reconciliation, dispute handling, and compliance reporting.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.journal_entry. Business justification: Required for daily settlement posting: each gateway response must be linked to the corresponding journal entry to record revenue and reconcile transaction outcomes.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant associated with the transaction.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Response records the payment product to enable product‑level reconciliation and reporting.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that originated the request; sensitive PCI data.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Response records the FX rate used for the transaction to support post‑settlement audit and dispute handling.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating gateway request that generated this response.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch that includes this transaction for settlement.']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.wallet_transaction. Business justification: Associates the response with the specific wallet transaction for settlement reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  acquirer_response_code varchar [note: 'Response code returned by the acquiring bank or processor.']
  amount_cents varchar [note: 'Transaction amount expressed in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents).']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Issuer-provided code confirming successful authorization; sensitive PCI data.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the card (credit, debit, prepaid, commercial).']
  compliance_check_details varchar [note: 'Free‑text details of any compliance issues detected.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of regulatory compliance checks (e.g., AML, sanctions).']
  correlation_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier used to correlate this response with upstream request logs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the response record was first persisted.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reason why the transaction was declined, if applicable.']
  destination_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the merchant endpoint that received the response.']
  destination_port varchar [note: 'Network port on the merchant side used for the response.']
  entry_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which card data was captured.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Code representing any internal processing error that occurred.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the internal error.']
  final_disposition varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the response after retries (e.g., success, failed, partial).']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned by the fraud engine.']
  is_fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was flagged as potentially fraudulent.']
  is_test_transaction boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was processed in test mode.']
  message varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text message included in the response.']
  network_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Measured network round‑trip time in milliseconds.']
  processing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds from request receipt to response generation.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Protocol used to deliver the response to the merchant.']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Standard response code indicating approval, decline, or error (e.g., ISO 8583 response codes).']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the gateway sent the response to the merchant.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of retry attempts performed before delivering the final response.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was settled with the acquiring bank.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the response met the defined SLA latency threshold.']
  source_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the gateway node that sent the response.']
  source_port varchar [note: 'Network port on the gateway used for the response.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Business purpose of the transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the response record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the gateway\'s outbound response for each processed request. Stores response timestamp, linked gateway_request_id, response code, authorization code, decline reason, acquirer response code, processing latency (ms), response protocol, retry count, and final disposition. Enables SLA measurement, decline analysis, and end-to-end latency tracking.'
}

Table gateway.routing_decision {
  routing_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the routing decision record.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank or payment network selected (or considered) for the transaction.']
  gateway_routing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the routing rule that was evaluated to produce this decision.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that initiated the payment request.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Routing decisions consider real‑time FX rates for currency‑based routing rules; storing the rate links decision to the rate source.']
  alternative_acquirer_endpoints varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of other eligible acquirer endpoints evaluated during routing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing decision record was first persisted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the request amount.']
  decision_error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error code if the routing decision failed.']
  decision_error_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable error message associated with a failed routing decision.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the routing decision as applied to the transaction.']
  decision_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation or code describing why the particular endpoint was selected.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the routing decision was evaluated and made.']
  failover_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a failover path was taken due to primary endpoint failure.']
  load_balancing_outcome varchar [note: 'Algorithmic outcome used to select the acquirer endpoint.']
  request_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction that triggered the routing decision.']
  request_protocol varchar [note: 'Protocol used by the originating request (e.g., ISO8583, REST, SOAP, JSON).']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment request arrived (e.g., web, mobile app, POS terminal).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score generated by the fraud detection platform for this request.']
  routing_decision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing decision.']
  routing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Measured time in milliseconds from request receipt to routing decision output.']
  selected_acquirer_endpoint varchar [note: 'Endpoint (e.g., URL, host:port) of the acquirer that was chosen to receive the transaction.']
  sla_achieved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual routing latency met the SLA target.']
  sla_target_ms varchar [note: 'Configured service‑level‑agreement target latency for routing decisions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the routing decision record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the real-time routing decision made for each payment request. Records which routing rule was applied, the selected acquirer endpoint, evaluated alternative endpoints, routing latency, load-balancing outcome, failover triggered flag, and the reason for endpoint selection. Provides full auditability of routing logic for dispute resolution, performance tuning, and regulatory review.'
}

Table gateway.failover_event {
  failover_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each failover event record.']
  gateway_routing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the routing rule applied during the failover.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant whose transactions were impacted.']
  network_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment network (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) involved.']
  primary_failover_acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary (failed) acquirer endpoint.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment network (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) involved.']
  target_endpoint_acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the secondary (fallback) acquirer endpoint.']
  affected_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of the affected transactions (currency assumed USD).']
  affected_transaction_volume varchar [note: 'Count of individual transactions impacted by the failover.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Error code returned by the gateway when the primary endpoint failed.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the failover was triggered by the gateway.']
  failover_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time from failover start to successful recovery, measured in seconds.']
  failover_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the failover event.']
  network_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment network.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or investigator comments.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the failover event record was first inserted.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the failover event record.']
  recovery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when traffic was successfully restored to the target endpoint.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code representing the geographic region of the impacted transactions.']
  retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Count of automatic retry attempts made on the primary endpoint prior to failover.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if the failover duration exceeded the SLA threshold.']
  source_endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the failed primary endpoint.']
  target_endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fallback endpoint.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of transactions impacted by the failover.']
  trigger_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the primary endpoint was considered unavailable.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each gateway failover occurrence — when a primary acquirer endpoint becomes unavailable and traffic is rerouted to a secondary. Captures failover trigger timestamp, failed endpoint, failover target endpoint, trigger reason (timeout, connection refused, error threshold breach), affected transaction volume, failover duration, and recovery timestamp. Critical for SLA breach analysis and infrastructure resilience reporting.'
}

Table gateway.webhook_subscription {
  webhook_subscription_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the webhook subscription record.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the subscription.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner (e.g., payment service provider) associated with the subscription, if applicable.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the subscription.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that owns this webhook subscription.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate webhook calls to the target URL.']
  authentication_secret varchar [note: 'Secret key or token used with the selected authentication method (e.g., HMAC secret, OAuth bearer token).']
  backoff_strategy varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate delay between successive retries.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription becomes active.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subscription expires or is scheduled to terminate (null for indefinite).']
  encryption_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payload is encrypted at rest or in transit.']
  encryption_key_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the encryption key used to protect the payload.']
  event_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of event identifiers (e.g., authorization.approved,capture.completed) the subscriber wishes to receive.']
  failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive delivery failures.']
  ip_allowlist varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of IP address ranges permitted to receive webhook calls.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subscription is for testing purposes only.']
  last_failure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent delivery failure.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the subscription.']
  max_payload_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed size of the webhook payload in bytes.']
  max_retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of retry attempts before the subscription is marked failed.']
  notification_endpoint_type varchar [note: 'Transport mechanism for the webhook (e.g., HTTP, HTTPS, message queue).']
  oauth_client_identifier varchar [note: 'Client identifier when OAuth Bearer authentication is used.']
  oauth_scopes varchar [note: 'Space‑separated list of OAuth scopes granted to the webhook client.']
  payload_format varchar [note: 'Format of the webhook payload delivered to the target URL.']
  rate_limit_per_minute varchar [note: 'Maximum number of webhook calls allowed per minute for this subscription.']
  retry_backoff_seconds varchar [note: 'Base number of seconds used in backoff calculations between retries.']
  retry_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how failed webhook deliveries are retried.']
  sla_response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Target maximum time (in seconds) for the webhook endpoint to acknowledge a delivery.']
  subscription_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing code used to reference the subscription in external systems and documentation.']
  subscription_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the webhook subscription.']
  subscription_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the subscription purpose.']
  target_url varchar [note: 'Fully qualified HTTPS endpoint where event notifications are delivered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subscription record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number incremented on each configuration change.']
  webhook_secret_hash varchar [note: 'Hash of the secret used for HMAC verification of incoming webhook calls.']
  webhook_subscription_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form description providing context about the subscription purpose.']

  Note: 'Master record for each merchant or partner webhook subscription registered with the gateway. Stores target URL, subscribed event types (authorization.approved, capture.completed, dispute.opened, etc.), authentication method (HMAC-SHA256, OAuth bearer), retry policy, maximum retry attempts, backoff strategy, active status, and subscription creation/expiry dates. SSOT for outbound event notification configuration.'
}

Table gateway.webhook_delivery {
  webhook_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each webhook delivery attempt.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the HTTP request sent to the merchant.']
  webhook_subscription_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant subscription that defines the webhook endpoint and event filters.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the delivery attempt for this webhook event.']
  correlation_identifier varchar [note: 'UUID used to correlate this delivery with upstream processing logs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery record was first created in the data lake.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the webhook delivery attempt.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the webhook delivery attempt was initiated.']
  endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Full URL of the merchant\'s webhook endpoint.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Error details returned by the merchant endpoint when delivery fails.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the business event that triggered the webhook notification.']
  http_status_code varchar [note: 'HTTP response status code returned by the merchant endpoint.']
  is_final_attempt boolean [note: 'True if this attempt is the last allowed retry.']
  max_attempts varchar [note: 'Configured maximum number of retry attempts allowed for the webhook.']
  next_retry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the next retry attempt when the previous delivery failed.']
  payload_hash varchar [note: 'SHA-256 hash of the webhook payload to verify integrity.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Communication protocol used for the webhook delivery.']
  response_body_snippet varchar [note: 'Truncated portion of the HTTP response body for diagnostic purposes.']
  response_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Round‑trip time in milliseconds between sending the webhook and receiving the HTTP response.']
  retry_policy varchar [note: 'Configured retry strategy for failed webhook deliveries.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the webhook event.']
  success_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the webhook delivery was successful (true) or not (false).']
  throttling_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether delivery was delayed due to rate‑limiting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each webhook notification delivery attempt made by the gateway. Captures delivery timestamp, linked webhook_subscription_id, event type, payload hash, HTTP response code, response latency, delivery attempt number, retry sequence, success/failure status, and next retry scheduled time. Enables merchant support teams to diagnose missed notifications and replay failed deliveries.'
}

Table gateway.rate_limit_policy {
  rate_limit_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the rate limit policy.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Rate‑limit policies are negotiated per partner contract; linking supports enforcement reporting and partner‑level usage billing.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user or service that created the policy.']
  burst_limit varchar [note: 'Number of requests allowed in a short burst exceeding the steady‑state quota.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance rules that the policy must satisfy (e.g., PCI DSS, GDPR).']
  consumer_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consumer subject to the policy (e.g., API key, MID, or IP range).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the policy record was first created.']
  daily_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum total requests allowed in a calendar day.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy becomes effective.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the policy expires; null for indefinite.']
  enforcement_action varchar [note: 'Action taken when limits are breached: throttle (delay) or block (reject).']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rate limit policy.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Category of consumer scope the policy applies to.']
  rate_limit_policy_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and scope of the policy.']
  rate_limit_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the policy.']
  request_quota_rpm varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed requests per minute for the consumer.']
  request_quota_tps varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed requests per second for the consumer.']
  throttle_response_code varchar [note: 'HTTP status code returned when the consumer exceeds the quota.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the service tier (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold) associated with the policy.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or service that last updated the policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the policy.']

  Note: 'Business-managed rate limiting policy applied to API consumers accessing the gateway. Defines the consumer scope (per API credential, per MID, per IP range), request quota (TPS, RPM, daily cap), burst allowance, throttle response code, policy effective period, and enforcement action (throttle vs. block). Supports tiered service levels and abuse prevention without impacting legitimate high-volume merchants.'
}

Table gateway.rate_limit_breach {
  rate_limit_breach_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each rate limit breach event.']
  rate_limit_policy_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the rate‑limit policy that was violated.']
  alert_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an operational alert was generated for the breach.']
  alert_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert for the breach was sent.']
  breach_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the context or notes of the breach.']
  breach_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of time the breach condition persisted before remediation.']
  breach_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the rate limit breach was detected by the gateway.']
  client_user_agent varchar [note: 'User‑agent string of the client making the request.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the breach event complies with internal and regulatory policies.']
  correlation_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier used to trace the request across distributed systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach record was first persisted in the data lake.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level assigned to the breach record for governance purposes.']
  endpoint_path varchar [note: 'REST endpoint path that was accessed when the breach occurred.']
  enforcement_action varchar [note: 'Action taken by the gateway when the breach occurred.']
  exceeded_metric varchar [note: 'Metric (TPS or RPM) that exceeded the configured threshold.']
  is_test_environment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request originated from a test/sandbox environment.']
  observed_rpm varchar [note: 'Number of transactions per minute observed at the moment of breach.']
  observed_tps varchar [note: 'Number of transactions per second observed at the moment of breach.']
  originating_ip varchar [note: 'IP address from which the request that triggered the breach originated.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the rate‑limit policy applied to the source.']
  rate_limit_breach_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breach record.']
  rate_limit_window varchar [note: 'Time window granularity for the applied rate‑limit policy.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the geographic region of the request.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Post‑breach action taken to address the underlying cause.']
  request_method varchar [note: 'HTTP verb used for the API call that exceeded the rate limit.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach was resolved or automatically cleared.']
  source_identifier varchar [note: 'The actual identifier of the source that caused the breach (e.g., API key value, merchant ID, or IP address).']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity that triggered the breach (API credential, merchant identifier, or IP address).']
  threshold_rpm varchar [note: 'Configured RPM limit for the applicable policy.']
  threshold_tps varchar [note: 'Configured TPS limit for the applicable policy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the breach record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each rate limit breach event detected by the gateway. Captures breach timestamp, offending API credential or MID, applied policy reference, observed TPS/RPM at breach, enforcement action taken (throttled, blocked, alerted), breach duration, and resolution timestamp. Used for capacity planning, merchant communication, and abuse pattern detection.'
}

Table gateway.sla_profile {
  sla_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SLA profile.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank or network endpoint covered by the SLA.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: SLAs can be defined per currency; linking to reference.currency supports currency‑specific availability targets.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Partners have SLA agreements with the gateway; the FK enables SLA compliance tracking and breach penalty calculations per partner.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant to which the SLA applies.']
  breach_notification_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum time to notify the merchant after an SLA breach.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA profile record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the SLA becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the SLA expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  error_rate_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed error/decline rate expressed as a percentage.']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the contact to notify on SLA breach.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the escalation contact.']
  last_measured_availability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed availability percentage during the last measurement window.']
  last_measured_error_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed error/decline rate in the last measurement window.']
  last_measured_latency_p95_ms varchar [note: 'Observed 95th percentile latency in the last measurement window.']
  last_measured_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent SLA performance measurement.']
  latency_p50_ms varchar [note: '50th percentile (median) authorization response time in milliseconds.']
  latency_p95_ms varchar [note: '95th percentile authorization response time in milliseconds.']
  latency_p99_ms varchar [note: '99th percentile authorization response time in milliseconds.']
  measurement_metric varchar [note: 'Primary metric used for SLA measurement.']
  measurement_window_minutes varchar [note: 'Time window over which SLA metrics are aggregated.']
  monitoring_endpoint varchar [note: 'URL of the endpoint used to monitor SLA metrics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the SLA.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied for SLA breach.']
  remediation_obligation varchar [note: 'Description of remediation steps or penalties applied when SLA is breached.']
  sla_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the SLA agreement, used in contracts and reporting.']
  sla_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA.']
  sla_revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for tracking changes to the SLA.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA (e.g., merchant integration, acquirer endpoint, API service, partner).']
  sla_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the SLA contract (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  target_availability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Committed service availability expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.95).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SLA profile.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the gateway SLA commitments for a merchant integration or acquirer endpoint. Specifies target availability percentage, maximum authorization latency (p50/p95/p99 in ms), maximum error rate threshold, measurement window, breach notification SLA, and remediation obligations. Linked to merchant agreements and used as the baseline for SLA performance measurement.'
}

Table gateway.sla_measurement {
  sla_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each SLA measurement entry.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SLA profile against which this measurement is evaluated.']
  availability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated availability of the gateway service expressed as a percentage of the measurement window.']
  breach_severity varchar [note: 'Categorical severity of the SLA breach, where higher levels indicate greater impact.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA measurement record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  error_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of total requests that failed, expressed as a percentage of the measurement window.']
  latency_p50_ms decimal(10,3) [note: 'Median (p50) request latency observed during the measurement window, expressed in milliseconds.']
  latency_p95_ms decimal(10,3) [note: '95th percentile request latency observed during the measurement window, expressed in milliseconds.']
  latency_p99_ms decimal(10,3) [note: '99th percentile request latency observed during the measurement window, expressed in milliseconds.']
  measurement_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the measurement data, e.g., real‑time gateway, batch processing, external monitoring service.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the SLA measurement observation occurred.']
  measurement_window_end timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the end of the period over which the SLA metrics were aggregated.']
  measurement_window_start timestamp [note: 'Timestamp marking the beginning of the period over which the SLA metrics were aggregated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional textual comments providing context, anomalies, or manual observations related to the measurement.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if any measured metric fell below the SLA target thresholds for the window.']
  tps decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average number of successful transactions processed per second during the measurement window.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this SLA measurement record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing periodic SLA performance measurements against defined SLA profiles. Stores measurement window start/end, measured availability percentage, p50/p95/p99 latency values, error rate, TPS throughput, SLA breach flag, breach severity, and measurement source. Provides the time-series operational data needed for SLA reporting, breach detection, and contractual compliance.'
}

Table gateway.sla_breach {
  sla_breach_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each SLA breach event.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SLA contract/profile that defines the metrics and thresholds.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the full audit log that captured the breach lifecycle events.']
  breach_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the breach calculated as end minus start, expressed in seconds.']
  breach_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the SLA metric returned to within the contracted threshold.']
  breach_reference varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference code assigned to the breach for tracking and communication.']
  breach_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the SLA metric first fell outside the contracted threshold.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the breach remediation complies with internal policies and external regulations.']
  contracted_unit varchar [note: 'Unit associated with the contracted SLA value.']
  contracted_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'The SLA‑defined threshold that should not be exceeded (or must be met).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the breach record was first inserted into the data store.']
  credit_applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the credit obligation was settled.']
  credit_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code (e.g., USD, EUR) for the credit obligation.']
  credit_obligation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial credit amount the provider must remit to the impacted party as per SLA contract.']
  impacted_party_reference varchar [note: 'Surrogate key referencing the party that experienced the SLA breach.']
  impacted_party_type varchar [note: 'Classifies whether the breach affected a merchant, acquiring bank, partner, or other entity.']
  is_credit_applied boolean [note: 'True if the contractual credit has been posted to the impacted party.']
  measured_unit varchar [note: 'Unit associated with the measured value (e.g., percent, ms).']
  measured_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric value recorded for the metric while the breach was active.']
  metric varchar [note: 'The specific SLA metric (e.g., availability, latency) that triggered the breach.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True when the breach triggers mandatory regulatory reporting (e.g., AML, PCI).']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective steps performed to resolve the breach.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of why the SLA breach occurred.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Business‑defined severity level used for escalation and penalty calculation.']
  sla_breach_description varchar [note: 'Additional narrative details about the breach, context, or observations.']
  sla_breach_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the breach is still open, under investigation, resolved, or closed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the breach record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each confirmed SLA breach event against a gateway SLA profile. Captures breach start/end timestamps, breached metric (availability, latency, error rate), measured vs. contracted value, impacted merchant or acquirer, breach severity classification, root cause category, remediation action taken, and credit obligation triggered. Feeds contractual penalty calculations and merchant communication workflows.'
}

Table gateway.sdk_version {
  sdk_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each SDK version record.']
  api_compatibility_version varchar [note: 'Version of the gateway API that this SDK is compatible with.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'Organization that performed the PCI DSS certification (e.g., PCI Security Standards Council).']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date when the SDK achieved PCI DSS certification.']
  changelog_summary varchar [note: 'Brief description of functional and bug‑fix changes introduced in this SDK version.']
  checksum_sha256 varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 hash of the SDK package for integrity verification.']
  checksum_type varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to generate the checksum.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SDK version record was first created in the data lake.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date after which the SDK version is considered deprecated and should no longer be used for new integrations.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the SDK developer documentation.']
  download_url varchar [note: 'Secure URL from which the SDK package can be downloaded.']
  end_of_life_date date [note: 'Final date after which the SDK version will be retired and unsupported.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the SDK binary/package in bytes.']
  is_mandatory_update boolean [note: 'Indicates whether merchants must upgrade to this SDK version to remain compliant.']
  language_support varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of programming languages or locales supported by the SDK.']
  minimum_os_version varchar [note: 'Lowest operating system version required for the SDK to function.']
  minimum_runtime_version varchar [note: 'Minimum version of the runtime environment (e.g., Java, .NET) required.']
  pci_dss_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI DSS compliance certification status of the SDK.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Technology platform the SDK targets (e.g., iOS, Android, JavaScript, Java, .NET, PHP, Python).']
  release_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the SDK version was first made publicly available.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Full release notes detailing changes, bug fixes, and migration guidance.']
  sdk_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SDK based on deployment model.']
  sdk_version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SDK version.']
  support_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for technical support related to this SDK version.']
  support_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for technical support related to this SDK version.']
  supported_features varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of major features (e.g., tokenization, 3‑DS, fraud scoring) available in this SDK version.']
  supported_payment_methods varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of payment methods (e.g., card, ACH, digital wallet) the SDK can process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: card|ach|digital_wallet|bank_transfer|crypto|other — promote to reference product]']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SDK version record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Human‑readable version label (e.g., "v2.3.1"). Serves as the identity label for the SDK resource.']
  version_code varchar [note: 'Canonical version code used internally and in external contracts; uniquely identifies the SDK release.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each published version of the payment gateway SDK (iOS, Android, JavaScript, Java, .NET, PHP, Python). Stores SDK platform, version number, release date, deprecation date, end-of-life date, minimum OS/runtime requirements, supported payment methods, changelog summary, download URL, and PCI DSS compliance certification status. SSOT for SDK lifecycle management and merchant upgrade tracking.'
}

Table gateway.merchant_sdk_adoption {
  merchant_sdk_adoption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each SDK adoption record linking a merchant integration to a specific SDK version.']
  merchant_integration_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant integration (API/SDK configuration) that is adopting the SDK version.']
  sdk_version_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SDK version being used by the merchant integration.']
  adoption_date date [note: 'Date when the merchant integration first started using the SDK version.']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'True if the SDK version must meet specific regulatory or PCI DSS compliance criteria for the merchant.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the SDK version for the merchant integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the adoption record was initially created in the data lake.']
  current_sdk_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the SDK version as observed for the merchant integration.']
  forced_upgrade_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the merchant must upgrade to a supported SDK version to remain compliant with PCI DSS or internal policies.']
  last_seen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful transaction or heartbeat observed for this SDK version on the merchant integration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the gateway team regarding the adoption, exceptions, or special handling.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score (0‑100) assigned to the SDK version based on vulnerability scanning and fraud exposure.']
  sdk_release_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the SDK version was released to merchants.']
  sdk_release_notes_url varchar [note: 'Web address linking to the release notes or changelog for the SDK version.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the adoption record.']
  upgrade_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a notification to upgrade to a newer SDK version has been sent to the merchant.']

  Note: 'Association record tracking which SDK version each merchant integration is actively using. Captures merchant_integration_id, sdk_version_id, adoption date, last seen timestamp, upgrade notification sent flag, and forced upgrade deadline. Enables the gateway team to identify merchants on deprecated SDK versions, drive upgrade campaigns, and enforce PCI DSS compliance cutoffs.'
}

Table gateway.endpoint_health {
  endpoint_health_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each health check observation of an acquirer endpoint.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the registered acquirer endpoint that was health‑checked.']
  check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the health check was performed.']
  circuit_breaker_state varchar [note: 'Current state of the circuit‑breaker controlling traffic to the endpoint.']
  consecutive_failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of back‑to‑back failed health checks for this endpoint.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health‑check record was first inserted.']
  endpoint_health_status varchar [note: 'Result of the health check – success indicates the endpoint responded as expected.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable error description when a health check fails.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code categorising why a health check failed.']
  health_check_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism used for the health check (e.g., TCP ping, ISO 8583 echo, REST heartbeat).']
  health_score varchar [note: 'Composite score (0‑100) reflecting overall endpoint health based on recent checks.']
  last_success_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful health check for this endpoint.']
  protocol varchar [note: 'Communication protocol exposed by the acquirer endpoint.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating the region where the endpoint resides.']
  response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Measured round‑trip time of the health check in milliseconds.']
  sla_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum response time promised in the SLA for this endpoint.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this health‑check record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing periodic health check results for each registered acquirer endpoint. Stores check timestamp, endpoint reference, health check type (TCP ping, ISO 8583 echo, REST heartbeat), response time (ms), success/failure status, consecutive failure count, circuit breaker state (closed/open/half-open), and health score. Drives automated failover decisions and capacity management.'
}

Table gateway.tls_certificate {
  tls_certificate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the TLS/SSL certificate record.']
  acquirer_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.acquirer_endpoint. Business justification: TLS certificates are bound to specific acquirer endpoints; FK replaces free-text endpoint reference.']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate is set to renew automatically before expiry.']
  certificate_profile varchar [note: 'Level of validation performed by the CA (Extended Validation, Organization Validation, Domain Validation, Self‑Signed).']
  certificate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the certificate purpose.']
  compliance_pci_dss varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate meets PCI DSS requirements for encryption.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter ISO 3166‑1 country code from the certificate subject.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certificate record was first created in the data lake.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Email address listed in the certificate for administrative contact.']
  expiration_alert_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an expiry alert has been sent to the operations team.']
  fingerprint_sha256 varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal SHA-256 hash of the certificate, used for integrity verification.']
  is_wildcard boolean [note: 'True if the certificate covers a wildcard domain (e.g., *.paymentsfintech.com).']
  issuer varchar [note: 'Distinguished Name of the Certificate Authority (CA) that issued the certificate.']
  key_algorithm varchar [note: 'Cryptographic algorithm used for the public key, e.g., RSA or ECDSA.']
  key_length_bits varchar [note: 'Size of the public key in bits, e.g., 2048, 3072, 4096.']
  last_checked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful health‑check of the certificate\'s endpoint.']
  locality varchar [note: 'City component of the certificate subject.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text information or operational comments about the certificate.']
  organization_name varchar [note: 'Legal organization name extracted from the certificate subject.']
  organizational_unit varchar [note: 'Organizational unit (OU) component of the certificate subject.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the certificate should be renewed to avoid service interruption.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reason why the certificate was revoked, if applicable.']
  revocation_status varchar [note: 'Current revocation state of the certificate.']
  revocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the certificate was revoked.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the issuing CA, used to identify the certificate.']
  state_or_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the certificate subject.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Distinguished Name (DN) representing the entity the certificate is issued to, e.g., CN=api.paymentsfintech.com.']
  thumbprint varchar [note: 'Legacy SHA-1 hash of the certificate, often used by older systems.']
  tls_certificate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certificate within the gateway.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certificate record.']
  usage varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the certificate is used for inbound, outbound, or both traffic.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date on which the certificate becomes valid.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date on which the certificate expires and must be renewed.']

  Note: 'Master record for TLS/SSL certificates used to secure gateway connections — both inbound (merchant-facing) and outbound (acquirer-facing). Stores certificate subject, issuer CA, serial number, fingerprint (SHA-256), valid-from/valid-to dates, key algorithm, key length, associated endpoint or integration, auto-renewal flag, and revocation status. Enables proactive certificate expiry management and PCI DSS compliance tracking.'
}

Table gateway.ip_allowlist {
  ip_allowlist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each allowlist or blocklist record.']
  gateway_api_credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key hint to the API credential (client) that this IP rule protects.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the rule meets current PCI DSS network access control requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the allowlist entry was initially persisted.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The calendar date from which the IP rule is enforced.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The calendar date after which the IP rule is no longer applied.']
  enforcement_action varchar [note: 'Specifies the system response: reject the request, monitor for review, or raise an alert.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Region used for regulatory or risk‑based routing decisions.']
  ip_allowlist_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is currently enforced, awaiting activation, or revoked.']
  ip_range varchar [note: 'The IPv4 address or CIDR block that the rule applies to.']
  is_rate_limited boolean [note: 'True if this IP rule includes a custom rate‑limit configuration.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining why the IP address or range is allowed or blocked.']
  list_type varchar [note: 'Specifies if the IP range is permitted (allow) or denied (block) for gateway access.']
  merchant_mid varchar [note: 'The merchant\'s unique identifier to which the IP rule applies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional context or remarks entered by administrators.']
  rate_limit_per_second varchar [note: 'Maximum number of API calls allowed per second for this IP range when rate‑limit override is active.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system (e.g., Gateway Config Service) that supplied the data.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or process that last modified the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the allowlist entry.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or process that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Master record defining IP address allowlists and blocklists applied to gateway API access. Stores the associated API credential or MID, IP address or CIDR range, list type (allow/block), geographic region, effective date range, enforcement action, and the business justification for the entry. Supports PCI DSS network access control requirements and fraud prevention at the network perimeter.'
}

Table gateway.event_log {
  event_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique immutable identifier for each gateway event log record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Event logs must attribute actions to the responsible partner for audit trails, dispute resolution, and regulatory reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the actor (system user, service account, or automated process) that performed the action.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SLA profile applicable at the time of the event.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the actor (e.g., user, service, system).']
  after_state varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded snapshot of the entity\'s state after the event.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was first persisted in the data lake.']
  before_state varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded snapshot of the entity\'s state prior to the event.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the event complies with applicable regulations and internal policies.']
  correlation_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier used to correlate this event with related events across systems.']
  entity_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the specific entity instance that the event relates to.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Business entity type impacted by the event (e.g., api_credential, routing_rule, endpoint).']
  event_log_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description summarizing the event.']
  event_payload varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form data or context associated with the event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the event occurred in the gateway domain.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorical discriminator indicating the kind of lifecycle event captured.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the event originated, used for security and audit purposes.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the event execution.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level indicating the impact or urgency of the event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system or component that generated the event (e.g., gateway, fraud_platform).']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'User‑agent header value identifying client software or device.']

  Note: 'Immutable business-level event log capturing significant lifecycle events within the gateway domain — credential rotations, routing rule activations, endpoint registrations, SLA profile changes, webhook subscription modifications, and failover activations. Stores event type, actor (system or user), affected entity type and ID, before/after state summary, event timestamp, and source system. Distinct from technical system logs — this is the business audit trail required for PCI DSS and regulatory compliance.'
}

Table gateway.threeds_config {
  threeds_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each 3-D Secure configuration record.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: 3‑DS settings are scheme‑specific; linking to reference_card_scheme provides scheme metadata for compliance.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: 3‑DS configuration may vary by currency; FK to reference.currency enables correct rule selection.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: MCC overrides in 3‑DS flow require authoritative MCC data to apply correct exemption rules.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_region. Business justification: 3DS configuration varies by processing region; linking to gateway_region provides regional control.']
  acquirer_bin varchar [note: 'Six‑digit BIN of the acquiring bank used for routing the 3‑DS flow.']
  audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit log entry that records changes to this configuration.']
  authentication_flow varchar [note: 'Method used to present the 3‑DS challenge to the cardholder.']
  challenge_preference varchar [note: 'Merchant‑specified preference for challenge flow during authentication.']
  challenge_timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Time limit in seconds for the challenge response.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) governing this configuration.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest compliance validation.']
  config_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the configuration in external systems.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the 3‑DS configuration.']
  config_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the configuration follows a standard template or is custom‑built for a merchant.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the configuration becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the configuration is no longer active (null for open‑ended).']
  exemption_rules varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of exemption criteria applied to the configuration.']
  frictionless_flow_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transactions can be processed without a challenge under this configuration.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this configuration is the default for the merchant.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance review of the configuration.']
  max_challenge_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of challenge attempts allowed before transaction decline.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the 3‑DS protocol applied (e.g., 1.0 or 2.0).']
  requestor_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity (typically the merchant) that initiates the 3‑DS authentication request.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum risk score allowed for frictionless flow; higher scores trigger challenge.']
  sca_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of PSD2 SCA compliance evaluation for the configuration.']
  threeds_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and scope of the configuration.']
  threeds_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the 3‑DS configuration.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of transaction to which the configuration applies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the configuration.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version string (e.g., 1.2.3) for the configuration.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record for 3-D Secure (3DS v1 and v2) authentication settings applied per merchant integration. Stores 3DS protocol version, requestor ID, acquirer BIN, merchant category code override, challenge preference (no-preference, no-challenge, challenge-requested), exemption rules (TRA, low-value, trusted-beneficiary), frictionless flow eligibility, and SCA compliance status under PSD2. Governs how the gateway invokes 3DS for each merchant.'
}

Table gateway.gateway3ds_authentication {
  gateway3ds_authentication_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each 3‑DS authentication attempt.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder whose payment instrument was used.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder whose payment instrument was used.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant involved in the transaction.']
  primary_gateway3ds_ds_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the Directory Server for this authentication flow.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original payment request that triggered the 3‑DS authentication.']
  acs_response_code varchar [note: 'Response code returned by the Access Control Server.']
  authentication_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds between authentication request and final response.']
  authentication_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable message describing the authentication outcome.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used for authentication (static, dynamic, frictionless, or challenge).']
  authentication_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the 3‑DS authentication: Y=success, N=failed, A=attempted, U=unavailable, R=retry, C=challenge.']
  authentication_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the authentication record.']
  authentication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the 3‑DS authentication was performed.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'Brand of the payment card used in the transaction.']
  cavv varchar [note: 'Cryptographic value generated by the cardholder\'s device for authentication.']
  challenge_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a challenge is required (01‑05 per 3‑DS spec).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authentication record was first inserted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction amount.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Hashed identifier of the device used for authentication.']
  eci_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'ECI code indicating the level of authentication and liability shift.']
  exemption_applied varchar [note: 'Exemption code applied to bypass full authentication (e.g., low‑value, trusted‑merchant).']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the authentication was flagged as fraudulent.']
  liability_shift varchar [note: 'Entity (merchant, issuer, or none) that bears liability after authentication.']
  liability_shift_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the liability shift outcome, if applicable.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk assessment generated by fraud engine for this authentication.']
  source_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client device that initiated the authentication.']
  three_ds_version varchar [note: 'Version of the 3‑DS protocol used for this authentication (e.g., 2.1.0).']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the underlying transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the authentication record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each 3-D Secure authentication attempt processed through the gateway. Captures authentication timestamp, linked gateway_request_id, 3DS version, authentication result (Y/N/A/U/R/C), ECI value, CAVV/AAV, DS transaction ID, ACS transaction ID, challenge indicator, exemption applied, authentication latency, and liability shift outcome. SSOT for 3DS authentication events within the gateway.'
}

Table gateway.tokenization_request {
  tokenization_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the tokenization request record.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Supports tokenization audit linking each token request to the cardholder profile, satisfying PCI‑DSS tokenization reporting and risk analysis.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device to which the token is bound (e.g., mobile device ID).']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Tokenization requests are issued by a specific wallet; linking enables audit of token issuance per wallet and regulatory token‑lifecycle tracking.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant (MID) that originated the tokenization request.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_region. Business justification: Tokenization requests are processed per gateway region; linking enables region-level reporting.']
  assurance_level varchar [note: 'Level of confidence in the tokenization process, reflecting authentication strength.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request satisfied all applicable PCI/DSS and regulatory compliance checks.']
  compliance_rule_set varchar [note: 'Identifier of the compliance rule set applied during tokenization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tokenization request record was first created in the data lake.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of why a tokenization request failed, if applicable.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'True if the request was flagged as potentially fraudulent.']
  fraud_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or rule that triggered the fraud flag.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City name associated with the request IP address.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 country code of the request origin.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address of the tokenization request.']
  outcome_status varchar [note: 'Result of the tokenization request: success, failure, or partial (e.g., token created but with warnings).']
  pan_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the PAN; stored to avoid retaining raw PAN data.']
  processing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds taken to process the tokenization request.']
  request_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the request was made (web, mobile app, POS, mPOS).']
  request_reference varchar [note: 'External reference supplied by the merchant to correlate the tokenization request with their own systems.']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the request call: API, SDK, or webhook.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the tokenization request was received by the gateway.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score assigned by the fraud detection engine for this request.']
  sla_achieved_flag boolean [note: 'True if processing latency met or beat the SLA target.']
  sla_target_ms varchar [note: 'Service‑level‑agreement target latency for tokenization processing.']
  token_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the issued token becomes invalid.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name or code of the token service provider that issued the token.']
  token_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the category of token generated: network token, gateway token, or device PAN (DPAN).']
  token_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual token string issued to replace the PAN; treated as PCI-sensitive data.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to generate the token: client‑side, server‑side, or hybrid.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tokenization request record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each network tokenization or gateway tokenization request processed through the gateway. Captures request timestamp, originating MID, PAN hash (never raw PAN), token type (DPAN, gateway token, network token), TSP reference, tokenization outcome, token expiry, device binding reference, and assurance level. Supports PCI DSS scope reduction by tracking the tokenization event without storing sensitive cardholder data.'
}

Table gateway.region {
  region_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gateway processing region.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Each gateway region is subject to a specific regulatory jurisdiction; linking enables jurisdiction‑based compliance reporting.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the region.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest audit (passed, failed, or pending).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the region against applicable regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the region record was first created.']
  cross_border_fee_applicable boolean [note: 'True if cross‑border transaction fees are applied for this region.']
  data_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the data center role for the region.']
  data_residency_requirements varchar [note: 'Statement of data residency constraints (e.g., local‑only, allowed cross‑border).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the region definition becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the region definition expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  encryption_standard varchar [note: 'Encryption protocol used for data in transit within the region.']
  jurisdiction_data_protection_law varchar [note: 'Primary data‑protection legislation governing the region.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit for the region.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle stage of the region within the gateway ecosystem.']
  max_transactions_per_second varchar [note: 'Peak transaction throughput the region is provisioned to handle.']
  network_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Average network latency observed from the region to major acquirers.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the region.']
  primary_data_center_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location (city, country) of the primary data center serving the region.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code identifying the processing region (e.g., NA1, EU2).']
  region_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the region\'s purpose and characteristics.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the processing region.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority or framework governing payments in the region (e.g., FCA, OCC, PSD2).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating for the region based on operational, regulatory, and security factors.']
  secondary_data_center_location varchar [note: 'Location of the disaster‑recovery / secondary data center for the region.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Default currency used for settlement of transactions processed in the region.']
  sla_target_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed response time for gateway operations in this region, per SLA.']
  supported_card_schemes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of card schemes (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, Amex) supported in this region. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Visa|Mastercard|Amex|Discover|JCB|UnionPay|Other — promote to reference product]']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes accepted in the region. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|AUD|CAD|CHF|CNY|INR|Other — promote to reference product]']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the region (e.g., America/New_York).']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tokenization services are available in the region.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the region record.']

  Note: 'Reference record defining the geographic processing regions in which the gateway operates. Stores region code, region name, primary data center location, secondary (DR) data center location, supported card schemes, supported currencies, regulatory jurisdiction, data residency requirements, and active status. Used to assign merchant integrations and acquirer endpoints to the correct processing region for latency optimization and data sovereignty compliance.'
}

Table gateway.api_version {
  api_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the API version record.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Service‑Level‑Agreement profile governing performance expectations for this API version.']
  api_documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the formal OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for this version.']
  api_version_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the purpose and scope of the API version.']
  api_version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the API version.']
  authentication_methods varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of authentication mechanisms supported by this API version.']
  backward_compatibility_matrix varchar [note: 'Structured description (e.g., JSON) of which prior API features remain compatible.']
  breaking_change_flag boolean [note: 'True if the version introduces breaking changes compared with the prior version.']
  changelog_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of changes introduced in this version.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance assessment for this API version.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI‑DSS compliance status of the API version.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the API version record was first created in the data lake.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date after which the API version is considered deprecated and should no longer be used for new integrations (nullable if not scheduled).']
  deprecation_reason varchar [note: 'Business or technical rationale for deprecating this API version.']
  documentation_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the accompanying API documentation set.']
  feature_flags varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded map of feature toggles enabled for this API version.']
  is_beta boolean [note: 'True if the version is a beta release intended for early adopters.']
  is_mandatory_update boolean [note: 'True if merchants must upgrade to this version to remain compliant.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit performed on this API version.']
  migration_guide_url varchar [note: 'Web address of the migration guide that explains how to move from prior versions to this version.']
  minimum_os_version varchar [note: 'Minimum operating system version required for SDKs or clients using this API version.']
  minimum_runtime_version varchar [note: 'Minimum runtime (e.g., Java, .NET) version required for this API version.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of platforms (e.g., web, mobile, POS) that can invoke this API version.']
  rate_limit_per_minute varchar [note: 'Maximum number of API calls allowed per minute for this version.']
  rate_limit_per_second varchar [note: 'Maximum number of API calls allowed per second for this version.']
  release_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the API version was first made publicly available.']
  release_notes_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed release notes for this API version.']
  release_type varchar [note: 'Semantic classification of the release (e.g., major, minor, patch, beta, release candidate).']
  sunset_date date [note: 'Final date on which the API version will be retired and cease to operate.']
  supported_languages varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of programming languages for which SDKs are provided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: java|python|ruby|go|csharp|nodejs|php|swift|kotlin — promote to reference product]']
  supported_payment_methods varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of payment instrument types accepted by this API version.']
  supported_protocols varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of transport protocols the API version can use.']
  supported_request_types varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of request operation types that this API version supports.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Intended primary consumer of the API version.']
  test_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of automated test coverage for this API version.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the API version record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Human‑readable version string (e.g., "v2.1.0") that uniquely identifies the published API version.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the record.']

  Note: 'Reference record for each published version of the gateway API. Stores API version identifier, release date, deprecation date, sunset date, supported request types, breaking change flag, migration guide URL, and backward-compatibility matrix. Enables the gateway team to manage multi-version API coexistence, enforce deprecation timelines, and communicate breaking changes to merchant integrations.'
}

Table gateway.sdk_adoption {
  sdk_adoption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the sdk_adoption association']
  merchant_integration_id varchar [note: 'Links to the merchant integration that adopts the SDK']
  sdk_version_id varchar [note: 'Links to the SDK version being adopted']
  adoption_date date [note: 'Date the SDK version was first adopted for the integration']
  compliance_required varchar [note: 'Indicates whether compliance checks are required for this SDK adoption']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the SDK within this integration']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record creation timestamp']
  current_sdk_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the SDK for this integration (e.g., ACTIVE, DEPRECATED)']
  forced_upgrade_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which the integration must upgrade to a newer SDK version']
  last_seen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent timestamp the SDK version was observed in use']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes about the adoption decision or issues']
  risk_score decimal [note: 'Risk score assigned to this SDK adoption based on security and compliance factors']
  sdk_release_date date [note: 'Release date of the SDK version being adopted']
  sdk_release_notes_url varchar [note: 'URL to the release notes for the adopted SDK version']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Record last update timestamp']

  Note: 'Represents the adoption relationship between a merchant integration and a specific SDK version. Each record captures when the SDK was adopted, compliance details, risk score, and other lifecycle attributes that belong to the adoption event.. Existence Justification: Merchants manage which SDK version their integration uses and may upgrade or downgrade over time. Each adoption is recorded with dates, compliance status, and risk information, and the same SDK version can be used by many merchant integrations. The relationship itself is a managed entity that carries lifecycle attributes.'
}

Table terminal.payment_instrument {
