  payment_instrument_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each payment instrument record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Instrument-level currency must align with reference.currency for transaction routing and compliance with currency-specific rules.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Required for Issuer‑Partner reporting: links each payment instrument to the issuing partner for settlement, KYC, AML, and revenue‑share calculations.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Required for posting instrument fees and balances to the General Ledger; finance teams need a direct GL account per payment instrument for accurate accounting.']
  irf_rate_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_rate_category. Business justification: Needed to determine the applicable interchange rate category for a payment instrument during transaction processing and fee calculation.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Required for fee schedule, compliance and reporting: each funding instrument is issued under a specific payment product.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: REQUIRED: Interchange fee calculation and compliance reporting need to know the network scheme of each instrument.']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the most recent transaction linked to this instrument.']
  available_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current spendable balance after pending authorizations.']
  bin varchar [note: 'First six digits of the PAN identifying the issuing bank and card brand.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network brand associated with the card (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual to whom the instrument is issued.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment instrument record was first created in the system.']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate amount allowed per day.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the instrument is no longer valid.']
  fraud_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for fraud flagging (e.g., stolen, counterfeit, suspicious_activity).']
  iin varchar [note: 'Extended identifier (first 6‑8 digits) used for routing and fraud checks.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment instrument (e.g., physical card, virtual card, ACH account, UPI ID, digital wallet).']
  is_3ds_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the instrument is enrolled in 3‑Domain Secure authentication.']
  is_contactless boolean [note: 'True if the instrument supports contactless (NFC) transactions.']
  is_fraud_flagged boolean [note: 'True if the instrument has been flagged for suspected fraud.']
  is_hce_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the instrument can be used via HCE on mobile devices.']
  is_nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates NFC hardware support for the instrument.']
  is_sca_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the instrument meets PSD2 SCA requirements.']
  is_virtual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the instrument is a virtual (non‑physical) representation.']
  issuance_date date [note: 'Date the payment instrument was issued to the holder.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful transaction using the instrument.']
  limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount authorized for the instrument.']
  monthly_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate amount allowed per calendar month.']
  network_brand varchar [note: 'Underlying interchange network used for transaction routing.']
  pan_masked varchar [note: 'Human‑readable masked PAN (e.g., 1234******5678) for display in UI and reports.']
  pan_token varchar [note: 'PCI‑DSS token representing the full PAN; used for internal processing without exposing the actual number.']
  payment_instrument_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the instrument.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) generated by fraud detection models.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Entity that issued the token for the instrument.']
  token_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the token\'s usage scope.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment instrument record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all payment instruments issued or managed by the platform — physical cards, virtual cards, ACH account credentials, UPI IDs, and alternative payment methods. Stores instrument type, status, issuance date, expiry, PAN (tokenized/masked), BIN, IIN, card scheme, network brand, currency, and PCI DSS classification. SSOT for instrument identity across the enterprise.'
}

Table terminal.card {
  card_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the card record.']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the card program that defines pricing, limits, and rules for this card.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual or entity that owns the card.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual or entity that owns the card.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date the card became active for transactions.']
  available_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current available balance for prepaid or debit cards.']
  card_art_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the visual design (artwork) applied to the card.']
  card_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the card.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Category of the card product (e.g., credit, debit, prepaid, commercial, virtual).']
  contactless boolean [note: 'True if the card can be used for contactless (NFC) transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the card record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the card\'s monetary values.']
  cvv_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the CVV value is stored, masked, or unavailable.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date the card was deactivated or closed (nullable).']
  embossed_name varchar [note: 'Name printed on the front of the physical card.']
  emv_capable boolean [note: 'True if the card supports EMV chip technology.']
  expiry_month varchar [note: 'Two‑digit month (01‑12) when the card expires.']
  expiry_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year when the card expires.']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'True if the card number is stored only as a token.']
  is_virtual boolean [note: 'True if the card exists only in digital form (no physical card).']
  issuance_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the card was provisioned.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the card was issued to the cardholder.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent status transition.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction or activity using the card.']
  limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit limit authorized for the card (applicable to credit cards).']
  magnetic_stripe boolean [note: 'True if the card includes a magnetic stripe.']
  number_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the card number, displayed for identification purposes.']
  pan_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized representation of the card\'s Primary Account Number, used for secure processing.']
  pin_change_date date [note: 'Date the PIN was last changed.']
  pin_set boolean [note: 'True if a personal identification number (PIN) has been set for the card.']
  scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network scheme associated with the card.']
  token varchar [note: 'Token value representing the card number for transaction processing.']
  tokenization_service varchar [note: 'Name of the token service provider that issued the token for this card.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the card record.']

  Note: 'Physical and virtual card master entity representing a specific card product instance — credit, debit, prepaid, or commercial card. Captures card number (PAN masked/tokenized), card type, card program reference, embossed name, expiry date (MM/YY), CVV indicator, EMV chip capability, contactless (NFC) flag, magnetic stripe flag, card art reference, and card status lifecycle (active, suspended, expired, lost, stolen, cancelled).'
}

Table terminal.wallet_bin_range {
  wallet_bin_range_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the BIN range record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Bin range may be currency-specific; linking to reference.currency supports cross-border fee calculations.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Bin range configuration is defined per wallet; linking to digital_wallet enables lookup of scheme and limits.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Internal or external identifier for the issuing bank.']
  issuing_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Bin range issuing country compliance requires reference.country for sanctions and regulatory checks.']
  partner_profile_id varchar [note: 'Internal or external identifier for the issuing bank.']
  bin_length varchar [note: 'Length of the BIN prefix (number of digits).']
  bin_prefix varchar [note: 'Numeric prefix (6-11 digits) identifying the issuing institution.']
  bin_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending numeric value of the BIN/IIN range.']
  bin_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting numeric value of the BIN/IIN range.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network brand associated with the BIN.']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the card product.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of compliance regimes applicable to the BIN.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  dpan_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if Device PAN (DPAN) is issued for this BIN.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the BIN range becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the BIN range expires or is retired (nullable).']
  funding_type varchar [note: 'Whether the card is funded directly from an account or prepaid.']
  interchange_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the BIN is eligible for interchange fee processing.']
  interchange_fee_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard interchange fee percentage for the BIN.']
  issuing_bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the issuing financial institution.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed transaction amount for this BIN (currency unspecified).']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum allowed transaction amount for this BIN.']
  network varchar [note: 'Network through which transactions are routed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Visa|Mastercard|Amex|Discover|JCB|UnionPay|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the card product.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment classification for the BIN range.']
  surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Whether surcharges may be applied to transactions using this BIN.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the BIN supports tokenization services.']
  transaction_routing_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates typical channels for routing transactions using this BIN.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system or user that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  wallet_bin_range_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BIN range.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system or user that created the record.']

  Note: 'BIN/IIN range registry defining the authoritative mapping of Bank Identification Number (BIN) and Issuer Identification Number (IIN) ranges to issuing institutions, card schemes, card types, and product categories. Stores BIN prefix (6–11 digits), range start/end, card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover), card type (credit/debit/prepaid), issuing country, issuing bank, funding type, and interchange eligibility flags. Critical for transaction routing and authorization decisions.'
}

Table terminal.wallet_card_scheme {
  wallet_card_scheme_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each card scheme record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Card scheme reference is scoped to a specific wallet; linking provides direct access to scheme details.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for scheme operator communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for scheme operator communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the scheme record was initially loaded.']
  cross_border_supported boolean [note: 'True if the scheme is authorized for international transactions.']
  dispute_resolution_policy varchar [note: 'Identifier or URL of the policy governing dispute handling for the scheme.']
  dynamic_currency_conversion_supported boolean [note: 'True if the scheme supports dynamic currency conversion at point of sale.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the scheme version started to apply.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'List of ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the scheme operates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: many values — promote to reference product]']
  interchange_fee_model varchar [note: 'Methodology for determining interchange fees for the scheme.']
  operator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that governs the card scheme.']
  pci_dss_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the scheme meets PCI DSS security standards.']
  psd2_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the scheme complies with the Payment Services Directive 2.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the scheme version ceased to be active (nullable if still active).']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Identifier for the version of the scheme\'s processing rules (e.g., 2023‑01).']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the card network scheme.']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the card network (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  scheme_type varchar [note: 'Category of the scheme (e.g., network, brand, regional). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: network|brand|regional|private|co‑branded|other — promote to reference product]']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for settlement of interchange and fees.']
  supported_card_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of card types the scheme issues.']
  supported_transaction_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of transaction types allowed under the scheme.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'True if the scheme allows tokenized card representations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the scheme record.']
  wallet_card_scheme_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing additional context about the scheme.']
  wallet_card_scheme_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the scheme version.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official web address for the scheme operator.']

  Note: 'Reference master for payment card network brands and schemes — Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, UnionPay, RuPay, JCB, etc. Stores scheme code, scheme name, scheme operator, supported card types, supported transaction types, geographic coverage, settlement currency, and scheme-specific rule version references. Used for routing, interchange qualification, and compliance alignment.'
}

Table terminal.emv_config {
  emv_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each EMV configuration record.']
  card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.card. Business justification: EMV configuration belongs to a specific card; linking enables card‑level EMV parameter retrieval.']
  aid varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal identifier of the EMV application stored on the chip.']
  app_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the EMV application (e.g., 1.0.3).']
  compliance_pci_dss_version varchar [note: 'Version of the PCI DSS standard to which this configuration complies.']
  config_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the EMV configuration in downstream systems.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the EMV configuration.']
  config_type varchar [note: 'Category of the EMV configuration based on supported interface types.']
  contactless_capability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless transactions are permitted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the EMV configuration record was first created.']
  cryptogram_type varchar [note: 'Type of cryptogram generated during transaction (Authorization Request Cryptogram, Transaction Cryptogram, or Application Authentication Cryptogram).']
  cumulative_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Overall cumulative amount limit over a defined period (e.g., monthly).']
  cvm_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Cardholder Verification Method is mandatory for this configuration.']
  cvm_requirements varchar [note: 'Rules describing required CVM (e.g., PIN, signature, biometrics) for the configuration.']
  daily_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative transaction amount limit for a cardholder within a calendar day.']
  dynamic_currency_conversion_supported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the configuration supports Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC).']
  dynamic_data_authentication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Dynamic Data Authentication (DDA) is enabled.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the EMV configuration becomes effective for processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the EMV configuration ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  emv_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context for the configuration.']
  emv_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the EMV configuration.']
  emvco_spec_version varchar [note: 'Version of the EMVCo specification governing this configuration.']
  floor_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount that can be approved offline without online authorization.']
  issuer_action_codes varchar [note: 'Issuer‑defined action codes governing risk and decline decisions.']
  issuer_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the issuing bank or entity.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance or policy review of the configuration.']
  offline_data_auth_method varchar [note: 'Method used for offline data authentication of the chip (Static, Dynamic, or Combined DDA).']
  offline_pin_verification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates support for offline PIN verification.']
  online_pin_verification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates support for online PIN verification.']
  personalization_profile_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chip personalization profile linked to this EMV configuration.']
  risk_score_threshold varchar [note: 'Numeric threshold above which a transaction is flagged for additional risk checks.']
  static_data_authentication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Static Data Authentication (SDA) is enabled.']
  supported_card_schemes varchar [note: 'List of payment network schemes supported by this EMV configuration.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Currency codes for which the configuration is valid.']
  terminal_action_codes varchar [note: 'Concatenated terminal action codes controlling transaction flow (e.g., TAC‑Default, TAC‑Denial).']
  terminal_capability varchar [note: 'Capabilities of the terminal (e.g., offline, online, contactless) as defined by EMV.']
  tokenization_supported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if tokenization of PAN is enabled for this configuration.']
  transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for a single transaction under this EMV configuration.']
  transaction_type_supported varchar [note: 'Types of transactions that this EMV configuration can process.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the EMV configuration record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number incremented on each change to the configuration.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'EMV chip configuration master for card instruments — stores EMV application identifiers (AIDs), application version numbers, terminal action codes (TAC), issuer action codes (IAC), cryptogram types (ARQC, TC, AAC), offline data authentication method (SDA, DDA, CDA), risk management parameters, and chip personalization profile references. Governs how EMV chip transactions are processed at POS terminals per EMVCo specifications.'
}

Table terminal.token {
  token_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the token record.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Token usage analytics and fraud detection depend on linking each token to the cardholder profile that generated it.']
  fpan_holder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Government or internal identifier of the PAN holder (e.g., SSN, national ID).']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Tokens are generated for a specific payment product; linking enables token lifecycle management and compliance tracking.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: REQUIRED: Token‑scheme mapping is required for token‑registry reporting and scheme‑specific risk analytics.']
  token_requestor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (e.g., merchant, app) that requested token creation.']
  assurance_level varchar [note: 'Risk/verification level assigned to the token (e.g., based on KYC/AML checks).']
  device_binding_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the device (e.g., device ID) to which the token is bound.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the token is no longer valid.']
  fpan_account_type varchar [note: 'Type of account linked to the Funding PAN.']
  fpan_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code of the underlying Funding PAN.']
  fpan_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the underlying Funding PAN.']
  fpan_hash varchar [note: 'Secure hash of the underlying Funding PAN stored for verification.']
  fpan_holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual or entity that owns the Funding PAN.']
  fpan_issuer varchar [note: 'Bank or institution that issued the underlying Funding PAN.']
  fpan_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the underlying Funding PAN for reference and reconciliation.']
  fpan_network varchar [note: 'Payment network (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) of the underlying Funding PAN.']
  fpan_tokenization_algorithm varchar [note: 'Cryptographic algorithm used for token generation.']
  fpan_tokenization_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance standards satisfied (e.g., PCI DSS, EMVCo).']
  fpan_tokenization_key_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cryptographic key used in tokenization.']
  fpan_tokenization_key_version varchar [note: 'Version of the cryptographic key used.']
  fpan_tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to tokenize the Funding PAN (e.g., EMVCo, PCI DSS, proprietary).']
  fpan_tokenization_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes about the tokenization process or exceptions.']
  fpan_tokenization_status varchar [note: 'Result of the tokenization process.']
  fpan_tokenization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the Funding PAN was tokenized.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the token was provisioned and became active.']
  last_used_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location (city, country code) of the most recent token use.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction where the token was presented.']
  provisioning_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the token was provisioned.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the token record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the token record.']
  scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network or scheme associated with the token.']
  service_provider varchar [note: 'Entity that performed the tokenization service.']
  token_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the token.']
  token_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the token based on its generation method: Device Primary Account Number (DPAN), Host Card Emulation (HCE), or cloud‑based token.']
  token_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Alphanumeric token string that replaces the underlying PAN for transactions.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of successful transactions using the token.']
  wallet_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the digital wallet platform (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay) that holds the token.']

  Note: 'Payment token master record representing a DPAN (Device Primary Account Number) or network token mapped to an underlying FPAN (Funding PAN). Stores token value, token type (DPAN, HCE, cloud-based), token requestor ID, token service provider (TSP), token status, token expiry, device binding reference, wallet provider, provisioning channel (NFC, in-app, e-commerce), and assurance level. Supports PCI DSS tokenization and EMVCo token framework compliance.'
}

Table terminal.token_lifecycle_event {
  token_lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the token lifecycle event record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet instance associated with the token.']
  token_id varchar [note: 'Unique token value assigned to the payment instrument.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event record was first created in the data store.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this event record.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the event was triggered.']
  compliance_check_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific compliance rule evaluated.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the token passed compliance checks at the time of the event.']
  device_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device involved in the event, such as a POS terminal or mobile device.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the token lifecycle event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the lifecycle event for the token.']
  initiating_actor varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the token lifecycle event.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the event originated.']
  is_restricted_token boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the token is subject to additional restrictions (e.g., high-value transactions).']
  is_test_token boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the token is used for testing purposes.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City name of the event location.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the event location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: list of country codes — promote to reference product]']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Status of the token after the event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments associated with the event.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Status of the token before the event.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that recorded the event.']
  token_activation_date date [note: 'Date when the token was initially activated.']
  token_deletion_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for token deletion, if applicable.']
  token_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the token is set to expire.']
  token_scheme varchar [note: 'Tokenization scheme or standard applied to the token.']
  token_suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for token suspension, if applicable.']
  token_type varchar [note: 'Type of token representing the underlying payment instrument.']
  token_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the token record, incremented on each lifecycle change.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every state transition in a payment token\'s lifecycle — provisioning, activation, suspension, resumption, deletion, and expiry. Stores event type, event timestamp, initiating actor (cardholder, wallet provider, issuer, TSP), previous status, new status, reason code, device reference, and channel. Provides full audit trail for token lifecycle management per EMVCo and PCI DSS requirements.'
}

Table terminal.ach_account {
  ach_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the ACH account record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: ACH account can be used as a funding source for a wallet; linking enables wallet‑level aggregation.']
  account_number_masked varchar [note: 'Account number stored in masked form (first six digits hidden, last four visible) for security.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Category of the ACH account based on its usage and ownership.']
  ach_account_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the ACH account.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the bank\'s physical address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the bank\'s physical address (optional).']
  authorization_type varchar [note: 'Standard ACH entry class code indicating the purpose of the transaction.']
  balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current monetary balance associated with the ACH account (if tracked).']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the financial institution that holds the ACH account.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the bank\'s address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the ACH account.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the bank\'s location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the ACH account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the account (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the ACH account became effective for transactions.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the ACH account is scheduled to be closed or become inactive (nullable).']
  holder_id_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued or tax identifier for the account holder (e.g., SSN, EIN).']
  holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual or organization that owns the ACH account.']
  holder_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account holder is an individual person or a business entity.']
  is_ach_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ACH transactions are currently permitted for this account.']
  last_verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful verification of the ACH account.']
  micro_deposit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of the micro‑deposit used for account verification (if applicable).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the bank\'s address.']
  prenote_status varchar [note: 'State of the prenote verification process for the ACH account.']
  routing_number varchar [note: '9‑digit routing number identifying the financial institution that holds the ACH account.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the bank\'s address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the ACH account record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify ownership of the ACH account.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification state of the ACH account.']

  Note: 'ACH bank account credential master for electronic fund transfer instruments. Stores account holder name, routing number (ABA), account number (masked), account type (checking, savings, corporate), bank name, account status, prenote status, verification method (micro-deposit, instant verification), and ACH authorization type (PPD, CCD, WEB, TEL). SSOT for ACH instrument credentials used in A2A transfers, payroll, and bill payment rails.'
}

Table terminal.upi {
  upi_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the UPI virtual payment address record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: UPI virtual address is owned by a wallet; linking provides direct wallet context.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PSP that hosts or processes the UPI address.']
  linked_bank_account_linked_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bank account that funds are drawn from or credited to for this VPA.']
  linked_linked_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bank account that funds are drawn from or credited to for this VPA.']
  partner_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PSP that hosts or processes the UPI address.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of AML screening performed on the VPA holder.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this UPI record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for limits and settlements.']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate value of transactions allowed per calendar day for this VPA.']
  device_binding_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the device (e.g., mobile phone) bound to the VPA for authentication.']
  failed_auth_attempts varchar [note: 'Count of consecutive failed authentication attempts since the last successful login.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current state of KYC verification for the VPA holder.']
  last_successful_auth_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful authentication (e.g., PIN entry, biometric) for the VPA.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this UPI record.']
  max_failed_attempts varchar [note: 'Configured threshold after which the VPA is locked pending reset.']
  monthly_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate value of transactions allowed per calendar month for this VPA.']
  nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether NFC‑based tap‑to‑pay is enabled for this VPA.']
  pin_set boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has configured a UPI PIN for authorising transactions.']
  privacy_consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the VPA holder has consented to data processing under GDPR/CCPA.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the VPA registration process.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the VPA was initially registered.']
  sanction_check_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of sanctions list screening for the VPA holder.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the VPA was registered.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current registration or verification status.']
  tokenization_status varchar [note: 'State of tokenization for the VPA\'s underlying payment credentials.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the VPA verification (e.g., OTP, micro‑deposit) process.']
  vpa varchar [note: 'The UPI ID in the format address@bank, used for peer-to-peer and peer-to-merchant payments.']
  vpa_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the VPA is for a personal or business entity.']

  Note: 'UPI (Unified Payments Interface) virtual payment address (VPA) master record. Stores UPI ID (VPA string), linked bank account reference, PSP handle, registration status, verification status, daily transaction limit, monthly transaction limit, UPI PIN set flag, device binding reference, and registration timestamp. Supports P2P and P2M UPI payment flows per NPCI UPI specifications.'
}

Table terminal.alt_payment_method {
  alt_payment_method_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each alternative payment method record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Alternative payment method is attached to a wallet; replace virtual_wallet_id with proper FK to digital_wallet.']
  alt_payment_method_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the payment method and its use cases.']
  alt_payment_method_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the payment method.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall regulatory compliance status (e.g., PCI DSS, AML).']
  contactless_type varchar [note: 'Technology used for contactless interaction.']
  country_of_issuance varchar [note: 'Country where the alternative payment method was issued or registered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment method record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the payment method.']
  daily_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative amount allowed in a calendar day.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the payment method becomes active for transactions.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the payment method expires or is de‑activated (null if open‑ended).']
  external_account_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the external provider for the linked account or wallet.']
  fee_fixed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat fee charged per transaction, expressed in the method\'s currency.']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage fee applied to each transaction using this method.']
  is_contactless boolean [note: 'True if the method supports contactless transactions (NFC, QR, etc.).']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment method data is stored as a token.']
  is_virtual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the method exists only in digital form (no physical token).']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current KYC verification state of the payment method.']
  last_aml_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the last AML screening was performed.']
  last_sanction_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the last sanctions screening was performed.']
  method_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to uniquely reference the payment method within the platform.']
  method_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment method (e.g., "Buy Now Pay Later – 30 Days").']
  method_type varchar [note: 'Category of the alternative payment method.']
  monthly_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative amount allowed in a calendar month.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external service provider offering the payment method.']
  requires_kyc boolean [note: 'True if the method requires Know‑Your‑Customer verification before activation.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from the risk score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned by the fraud platform (0‑100).']
  sanction_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent sanctions screening.']
  supporting_documents varchar [note: 'List of document types required for onboarding (e.g., "business_license, tax_id").']
  tokenization_scheme varchar [note: 'Standard used for tokenizing the payment method.']
  transaction_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount allowed per single transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment method record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the payment method record.']

  Note: 'Alternative payment method master covering non-card, non-ACH, non-UPI instruments — BNPL accounts, QR code payment handles, digital vouchers, cryptocurrency wallets, open banking payment initiation credentials, and stored value accounts. Stores method type, provider name, external account reference, currency, country of issuance, status, and applicable transaction limits. Enables the platform to support diverse global payment rails beyond traditional card networks.'
}

Table terminal.instrument_status_history {
  instrument_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each status change event of a payment instrument.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the payment instrument (card, token, digital wallet) whose status transition is recorded.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing why the status change was triggered (e.g., fraud_alert, KYC_failure, customer_request).']
  change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the status transition occurred in the source system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the status change event.']
  initiating_actor varchar [note: 'Entity or system that initiated the status change.']
  is_manual_change boolean [note: 'True if a human operator performed the change; false for automated system actions.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The status of the instrument after the change event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text comments providing additional context for the status transition.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The status of the instrument before the change event.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this history record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this history record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that generated the status change event.']
  supporting_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related case, dispute, compliance event, or other artifact that justified the status change.']

  Note: 'Audit history table recording every status change for a payment instrument throughout its lifecycle — activation, suspension, reactivation, expiry, cancellation, loss/theft reporting, and replacement. Stores previous status, new status, change timestamp, change reason code, initiating actor (cardholder, issuer, fraud system, compliance), and supporting reference (case ID, dispute ID, compliance event ID). Enables full lifecycle traceability for regulatory and fraud investigations.'
}

Table terminal.card_personalization {
  card_personalization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each card personalization specification record.']
  card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.card. Business justification: Personalization specifications apply to a specific card; adding card_id creates proper parent‑child relationship and allows removal of duplicated card attributes stored in personalization.']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the card program to which this personalization belongs.']
  card_art_template_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the graphic template used for card front design.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the personalization record was first created.']
  cvv_cvc_generation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to generate the card verification value.']
  cvv_cvc_key varchar [note: 'Secret key material for CVV generation; PCI‑DSS protected.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the personalization specification becomes active.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the personalization specification is retired.']
  personalization_batch_number varchar [note: 'External batch identifier assigned by the personalization bureau.']
  personalization_bureau_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number supplied by the card personalization bureau.']
  personalization_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the personalization profile.']
  personalization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the personalization record.']
  personalization_type varchar [note: 'Category of personalization workflow (e.g., standard, instant issuance, virtual, tokenized).']
  pin_offset varchar [note: 'Derived value used by the chip to validate the cardholder PIN; PCI‑DSS protected.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned during personalization based on fraud models.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the personalization record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Card personalization specification record defining the data elements written to a card during manufacturing and personalization — embossed name format, magnetic stripe track data layout, EMV chip data elements, PIN offset, CVV/CVC generation parameters, card art template, and personalization bureau reference. Links card programs to their physical or virtual personalization profiles. Critical for card manufacturing and instant issuance workflows.'
}

Table terminal.pin_management {
  pin_management_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each PIN lifecycle record.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the card or token to which this PIN applies.']
  hsm_key_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HSM key used to encrypt/decrypt the PIN block.']
  pin_block_format varchar [note: 'Format used to encrypt the PIN as defined by ISO 9564.']
  pin_blocked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PIN is currently blocked due to exceeded attempts.']
  pin_blocked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PIN became blocked.']
  pin_change_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the PIN has been changed since creation.']
  pin_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance assessment of the PIN against PCI and regulatory rules.']
  pin_encryption_algorithm varchar [note: 'Cryptographic algorithm used to protect the PIN block.']
  pin_failed_attempts varchar [note: 'Count of consecutive failed PIN entry attempts since last successful entry.']
  pin_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the PIN (e.g., "Primary PIN").']
  pin_last_changed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent successful PIN change.']
  pin_last_failed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent failed PIN entry.']
  pin_last_successful_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent successful PIN entry.']
  pin_reset_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to reset the PIN.']
  pin_reset_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PIN was last reset.']
  pin_set_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the PIN was initially set for the instrument.']
  pin_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the PIN.']
  pin_tries_remaining varchar [note: 'Number of remaining allowed PIN entry attempts before the PIN is blocked.']
  pin_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the PIN based on its usage scenario.']
  pin_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the PIN record for optimistic concurrency control.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the PIN management record was first created.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the PIN management record.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the latest update.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'PIN (Personal Identification Number) management record for card instruments — stores PIN set status, PIN change history count, PIN block format (ISO 9564), HSM key reference, PIN tries remaining, PIN blocked flag, PIN blocked timestamp, and PIN reset method (IVR, online, branch). Does NOT store the PIN value itself — only metadata for PIN lifecycle management per PCI PIN Security Requirements.'
}

Table terminal.credential_vault {
  credential_vault_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each credential vault entry.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.payment_instrument. Business justification: Credential vault stores credentials for a payment instrument; linking enforces one‑to‑many relationship.']
  tokenization_mapping_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this vault entry to the tokenization mapping record.']
  access_control_policy_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the policy governing who may read or modify this credential.']
  audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference string to the detailed audit log entry for this credential.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the immutable audit log for this credential entry.']
  business_identifier varchar [note: 'External business reference code for the credential, e.g., merchant‑provided token ID.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the credential according to PCI standards.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the credential with PCI DSS requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the credential vault record was created.']
  credential_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credential entry.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of credential stored, such as PAN, CVV, token, or PIN.']
  credential_vault_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the credential entry.']
  cvv_vault_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the secure vault location where the CVV/CVC is stored.']
  data_sensitivity_level varchar [note: 'Organizational data classification for the credential record.']
  encryption_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to encrypt the credential data.']
  encryption_key_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the HSM‑protected encryption key used to encrypt the credential data.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time after which the credential is considered expired and must not be used.']
  hsm_module_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific HSM module used for encryption.']
  is_hsm_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential is stored in a Hardware Security Module.']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stored credential is a tokenized representation.']
  key_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the associated encryption key expires.']
  key_management_service varchar [note: 'Service responsible for managing encryption keys.']
  key_rotation_interval_days varchar [note: 'Configured interval in days for periodic key rotation.']
  key_rotation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent key rotation for this credential.']
  key_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the encryption key for key‑rotation tracking.']
  last_accessed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or service that last accessed the credential.']
  last_accessed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent access to the credential data.']
  owner_party_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (merchant, cardholder, or partner) that owns the credential.']
  pan_token_reference varchar [note: 'Token that represents the Primary Account Number; actual PAN is never stored.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the geographic region of the credential.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the credential must be retained before mandatory deletion.']
  tokenization_scheme varchar [note: 'Standard or scheme used for tokenizing the credential.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the credential vault record.']
  vault_location varchar [note: 'Logical location or container identifier within the secure vault where the credential reference is stored.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version counter for the credential record, incremented on each change.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that created the credential vault entry.']

  Note: 'Secure credential storage registry for sensitive payment instrument data managed under PCI DSS controls — stores references to HSM-protected cryptographic keys, encrypted PAN storage pointers, CVV/CVC vault references, and tokenization mapping pointers. Does NOT store raw PANs or CVVs — stores vault location references, encryption key IDs, key rotation timestamps, and access control metadata. Provides the PCI DSS-compliant credential management layer for the instrument domain.'
}

Table terminal.instrument_limit {
  instrument_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each instrument limit configuration record.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment instrument to which this limit applies.']
  atm_withdrawal_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum cash withdrawal amount allowed at ATMs for the instrument.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of the latest regulatory compliance validation for the limit configuration.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the most recent compliance check was performed.']
  contactless_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount for a single contactless (tap‑and‑go) transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the limit record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which limits are expressed.']
  daily_spend_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate spend allowed for the instrument in a single calendar day.']
  daily_spend_used_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount spent on the instrument today, used to compare against daily_spend_limit.']
  ecommerce_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount for a single online purchase transaction.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the limit configuration expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the limit configuration becomes active.']
  enforcement_mode varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the limit is applied: hard (transaction declined), soft (warning), or dynamic (adjusted by risk engine).']
  fraud_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the reason for a fraud flag, aligned with internal fraud taxonomy.']
  instrument_limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the limit configuration.']
  international_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed for cross‑border transactions.']
  is_fraud_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the instrument has been flagged for fraud during limit evaluation.']
  is_override_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a merchant or cardholder can request a temporary limit increase.']
  last_enforced_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction where this limit was evaluated.']
  limit_group_code varchar [note: 'Logical grouping identifier for related limit records (e.g., program‑wide, regional).']
  limit_scope varchar [note: 'Entity level at which the limit is applied: individual instrument, card program, cardholder, or merchant.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the limit, indicating the transaction scenario it governs.']
  limit_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version number for change‑control and audit.']
  monthly_spend_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate spend allowed for the instrument in a calendar month.']
  monthly_spend_used_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount spent on the instrument this month, used to compare against monthly_spend_limit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or operational notes about the limit configuration.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for why an override was granted or requested.']
  per_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount permitted for a single transaction using the instrument.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk assessment (0‑100) associated with the instrument at the time of limit definition.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created or last modified the limit configuration.']
  transaction_count_month varchar [note: 'Number of transactions processed this month for the instrument.']
  transaction_count_today varchar [note: 'Number of transactions processed today for the instrument.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the limit record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the limit record.']

  Note: 'Transaction and velocity limit configuration for a payment instrument — stores daily spend limit, monthly spend limit, per-transaction limit, ATM withdrawal limit, international transaction limit, contactless transaction limit, and e-commerce transaction limit. Limits can be set at instrument level, card program level, or cardholder-requested level. Supports real-time authorization limit enforcement and risk-based limit adjustments.'
}

Table terminal.instrument_feature {
  instrument_feature_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the instrument feature record.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment instrument to which this feature applies.']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the feature was activated for the instrument.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this feature record was initially created.']
  deactivation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the feature was deactivated for the instrument.']

  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the feature expires or is scheduled to be deactivated; null if indefinite.']
  feature_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the feature (e.g., contactless, international usage). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: contactless|international|ecommerce|atm|recurring|3ds|dcc|bnpl — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  feature_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the feature in the internal feature catalog.']
  feature_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the feature.']
