  limit_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the limit amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes regarding the feature configuration or special conditions.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk score assigned to the feature usage for this instrument.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this feature record.']
  usage_counter varchar [note: 'Number of times the feature has been used since activation.']
  usage_reset_period varchar [note: 'Period after which the usage_counter resets.']

  Note: 'Feature and capability enablement record for a payment instrument — tracks which features are enabled or disabled per instrument: contactless payments, international usage, e-commerce transactions, ATM access, recurring billing, 3DS enrollment, DCC opt-in/opt-out, and BNPL eligibility. Supports cardholder self-service feature management and issuer-controlled feature provisioning.'
}

Table terminal.card_replacement {
  card_replacement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the card replacement event.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder requesting the replacement.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder requesting the replacement.']
  primary_original_card_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the card being replaced.']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replacement card became active for transactions.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the replacement card was actually delivered to the cardholder.']
  card_replacement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the replacement process.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the replacement request passed AML/KYC compliance checks.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance check was performed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the replacement record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking identifier for the shipped replacement card.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Projected date on which the replacement card will be delivered.']
  fee_waiver_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the replacement fee was waived for this request.']
  fee_waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the replacement fee was waived (e.g., promotional, dispute).']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the replacement card to the cardholder.']
  is_replacement_card_contactless boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the replacement card supports contactless transactions.']
  is_replacement_card_hce_enabled boolean [note: 'True if Host Card Emulation is enabled for the replacement card.']
  is_replacement_card_nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'True if NFC (tap‑to‑pay) is enabled on the replacement card.']
  is_replacement_card_tokenized boolean [note: 'True if the replacement card is issued as a token rather than a physical PAN.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by agents or system regarding the replacement.']
  replacement_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary fee charged for the card replacement, if applicable.']
  replacement_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the replacement fee.']
  replacement_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why the card is being replaced (e.g., lost, stolen, damaged).']
  replacement_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the replacement request for tracking and customer communication.']
  request_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the replacement request was submitted.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the replacement request was received.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the replacement request based on fraud detection models.']
  rush_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the replacement was expedited for faster delivery.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Entity providing tokenization services for the replacement card.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the replacement record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that created the replacement record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for card replacement events — covers lost/stolen replacements, damaged card replacements, expiry renewals, and upgrade replacements. Stores replacement reason code, original card reference, replacement card reference, request channel, request timestamp, fulfillment method, estimated delivery date, rush delivery flag, and replacement fee waiver indicator. Tracks the full replacement workflow from request to activation.'
}

Table terminal.instrument_verification {
  instrument_verification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each instrument verification event record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (person) linked to the instrument.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (person) linked to the instrument.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the verification (if applicable).']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment instrument (card, token, bank account) that was verified.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transaction that triggered the verification (if any).']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device used to perform the verification.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the verification attempt for the same instrument.']
  avs_response_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the Address Verification System (e.g., Y, N, A).']
  biometric_match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0‑100) for biometric verification.']
  compliance_pci_dss boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the verification process adheres to PCI DSS requirements.']
  compliance_psd2_sca boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the verification satisfies Strong Customer Authentication requirements under PSD2.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the verification record was first created in the system.']
  cvv_response_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating result of CVV/CVC check (e.g., M, N, P).']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time after which the verification request is considered expired.']
  fraud_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the reason for fraud flagging (e.g., high_risk_score, mismatched_address).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address from which the verification request originated.']
  is_fraud_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the verification triggered a fraud alert.']
  location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 country code of the requestor\'s location.']
  max_attempts_allowed varchar [note: 'Configured maximum number of verification attempts before lockout.']
  micro_deposit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of the micro‑deposit used for ACH account verification.']
  micro_deposit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the micro‑deposit verification process.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments added by operators or automated systems.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the verification record.']
  risk_score_at_verification decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned to the instrument at the moment of verification (0‑100 scale).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that generated the verification event.']
  three_ds_authentication_status varchar [note: 'Result of 3‑Domain Secure authentication flow.']
  tokenization_status varchar [note: 'Current tokenization state of the instrument after verification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the verification record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'User‑agent string of the client application initiating verification.']
  verification_channel varchar [note: 'Interface or channel through which the verification was initiated.']
  verification_method_detail varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the method or technology used for verification (e.g., HCE, NFC, OTP).']
  verification_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the verification: pass, fail, or partial (e.g., address matched but CVV did not).']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the verification attempt occurred.']
  verification_type varchar [note: 'Category of verification performed (e.g., Address Verification System, Card Verification Value, 3‑DS, Biometric, Micro‑Deposit, Tokenization).']

  Note: 'Verification event record for payment instrument authentication checks — AVS (Address Verification System), CVV/CVC verification, 3DS authentication, biometric verification, and micro-deposit verification for ACH accounts. Stores verification type, verification timestamp, verification result (pass/fail/partial), verification channel, risk score at time of verification, and verification method details. Supports fraud prevention and SCA compliance under PSD2.'
}

Table terminal.instrument_block {
  instrument_block_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each instrument block record.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment instrument (card, token, etc.) to which the block applies.']
  affected_transaction_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of transaction types that the block restricts.']
  block_amount_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary ceiling that may still be processed under the block (e.g., limit for low‑value transactions).']
  block_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the block used for reporting and analytics.']
  block_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the reason for the block.']
  block_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number or code assigned to the block for audit and lookup.']
  block_type varchar [note: 'Category indicating why the instrument is blocked.']
  blocking_authority varchar [note: 'Source system or party that initiated the block.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the block record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code associated with block_amount_limit.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the block expires or is lifted; null if indefinite.']
  instrument_block_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the block record.']
  is_cardholder_requested boolean [note: 'True if the block was requested directly by the cardholder (e.g., travel block).']
  is_compliance_triggered boolean [note: 'True if the block was generated to satisfy regulatory or AML requirements.']
  is_fraud_triggered boolean [note: 'True if the block was generated by the fraud detection engine.']
  is_issuer_requested boolean [note: 'True if the issuing bank placed the block.']
  is_temporary boolean [note: 'True if the block is intended to be temporary (has an end timestamp).']
  mcc_restriction_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of MCC codes that are blocked for this instrument.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional context, comments, or audit information related to the block.']
  regulatory_hold boolean [note: 'True if the block is due to a regulatory or sanctions requirement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the block (e.g., Fraud Detection Platform).']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the block starts to apply.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or service that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the block record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or service that initially created the block.']

  Note: 'Instrument blocking and restriction record capturing active blocks placed on a payment instrument — fraud-triggered blocks, compliance-triggered blocks, cardholder-requested blocks (travel, merchant category), and regulatory holds. Stores block type, block reason, blocking authority (fraud system, compliance, cardholder, issuer), block start timestamp, block end timestamp, affected transaction types, and MCC restriction list. Enables granular instrument-level controls beyond simple status flags.'
}

Table terminal.secure_element {
  secure_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each secure element record.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.wallet_device. Business justification: Secure element is provisioned to a specific wallet device; linking reflects the ownership relationship.']
  compliance_emvco boolean [note: 'True if the secure element complies with EMVCo standards.']
  compliance_pci_dss boolean [note: 'True if the secure element configuration meets PCI DSS requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the secure element record was first created.']
  cross_border_supported boolean [note: 'True if the secure element can be used for cross‑border transactions.']
  cryptographic_key_set_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the set of cryptographic keys associated with the secure element.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for transaction limits.']
  decommission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the secure element was retired or decommissioned.']
  device_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the mobile device hosting the secure element (e.g., IMEI).']
  dynamic_currency_conversion_supported boolean [note: 'True if the secure element supports Dynamic Currency Conversion.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the firmware running on the secure element.']
  hce_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the secure element is provisioned for Host Card Emulation.']
  is_test_se boolean [note: 'True if the record represents a test or sandbox secure element.']
  key_set_expiry date [note: 'Date on which the current key set expires and must be rotated.']
  key_set_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the cryptographic key set.']
  last_provisioned_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the last provisioning action.']
  last_provisioned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent provisioning event.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle stage of the secure element.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit for a single transaction processed via this secure element.']
  nfc_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports Near‑Field Communication for contactless payments.']
  os_version varchar [note: 'Version of the host operating system that interacts with the secure element.']
  provisioning_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the secure element provisioning workflow.']
  provisioning_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the secure element was last provisioned.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the secure element based on security posture.']
  se_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor that produced the secure element hardware.']
  se_model varchar [note: 'Model identifier of the secure element hardware.']
  se_serial_number varchar [note: 'Factory‑assigned serial number of the secure element.']
  se_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the secure element technology: embedded, SIM‑based, or cloud‑hosted HCE.']
  secure_element_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the secure element.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Overall security classification of the secure element based on protection mechanisms.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the secure element can store tokenized payment credentials.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the secure element record.']
  wallet_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the digital‑wallet provider that owns the secure element.']

  Note: 'Secure element (SE) and HCE (Host Card Emulation) provisioning record for NFC-enabled mobile payment instruments. Stores SE type (embedded SE, SIM-based SE, cloud HCE), device identifier, SE application identifier, provisioning status, provisioning timestamp, wallet provider, NFC capability flag, and cryptographic key set reference. Manages the hardware and software security modules that store payment credentials on mobile devices per EMVCo and GlobalPlatform standards.'
}

Table terminal.instrument_expiry_schedule {
  instrument_expiry_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each expiry schedule entry.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the payment instrument (card, token, etc.) whose expiry is being tracked.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'True when the system should automatically issue a replacement before expiry.']
  block_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason for instrument blockage.']
  compliance_check_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the specific compliance rule evaluated.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'True if the instrument satisfied all regulatory checks (PCI DSS, AML, etc.) at schedule creation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the schedule record was first created.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the payment instrument expires.']
  instrument_expiry_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the expiry schedule entry.']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment instrument (e.g., physical card, digital wallet token).']
  is_blocked boolean [note: 'True when the instrument is blocked for fraud or compliance reasons.']
  last_renewal_attempt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest attempt to renew the instrument.']
  next_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for the next scheduled activity related to the instrument.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations about the expiry schedule.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'True when the renewal reminder has been communicated.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time the renewal reminder was dispatched.']
  reissuance_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Configured lead time used for scheduling renewal batches.']
  renewal_attempt_count varchar [note: 'Number of times renewal has been tried for this schedule.']
  renewal_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the batch job that will process the renewal.']
  renewal_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'True if the instrument meets business rules for renewal.']
  renewal_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Business code describing why a renewal attempt failed.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the renewal operation.']
  renewal_suppression_flag boolean [note: 'True when renewal should be skipped (e.g., instrument is blocked).']
  renewal_window_end_date date [note: 'Latest date by which renewal must be completed.']
  renewal_window_start_date date [note: 'Earliest date on which renewal actions may be initiated.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating (0‑99.99) used to prioritize renewal actions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the schedule data.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the schedule record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service that created the record.']

  Note: 'Expiry and renewal schedule master for payment instruments — tracks upcoming expiry dates, auto-renewal eligibility, renewal batch assignment, reissuance lead time, and renewal suppression flags (for inactive or blocked instruments). Enables proactive card renewal operations, batch reissuance planning, and cardholder communication scheduling ahead of expiry. Supports operational SLA management for card lifecycle teams.'
}

Table terminal.pan_alias {
  pan_alias_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the PAN alias record.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the underlying payment instrument (card, token, etc.) that this alias represents.']
  alias_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description or notes about the alias purpose or usage.']
  alias_purpose varchar [note: 'Intended business use of the alias, such as customer‑facing display, statement line, receipt line, or internal processing.']
  alias_type varchar [note: 'Category of the alias representation, e.g., masked PAN, last four digits, display token, or reference number.']
  alias_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual alias string presented to downstream systems; may be a masked PAN, last‑four digits, token, or reference number.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit entry for creation was recorded.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit entry for the latest modification was recorded.']
  compliance_check_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the specific compliance rule or test applied.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of the most recent PCI/DSS compliance validation for the alias.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the alias record was created in the system.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the alias becomes invalid or must be retired.']
  is_pci_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the alias complies with PCI DSS requirements for storage and display.']
  is_restricted_alias boolean [note: 'True if the alias is subject to additional access controls or usage limits.']
  is_test_alias boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alias is used for testing or sandbox environments.']
  is_tokenized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the underlying PAN has been tokenized.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or operational notes related to the alias.']
  pan_alias_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the alias.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the alias record.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Provider responsible for issuing or managing the token associated with the alias.']
  token_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the token (e.g., single‑use, multi‑use, session‑based, device‑bound).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or process that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the alias record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the alias record for optimistic concurrency control.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or process that created the alias record.']

  Note: 'PAN alias and surrogate identifier registry mapping masked PANs, last-four-digit references, and display tokens to underlying instrument records. Stores alias type (masked PAN, last four, display token, reference number), alias value, alias purpose (customer display, statement, receipt), creation timestamp, and expiry. Ensures PCI DSS compliance by providing safe display representations of card numbers without exposing the full PAN in downstream systems.'
}

Table terminal.pos_terminal {
  pos_terminal_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated surrogate primary key for the POS terminal record.']
  acceptance_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal_acceptance_type. Business justification: Terminal specifies acceptance type; no redundant columns.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: REQUIRED: Expense allocation and maintenance cost reporting assign each terminal to a cost center for internal budgeting.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Regulatory compliance reporting requires mapping each terminal to its operating country for AML and tax jurisdiction analysis.']
  dcc_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.dcc_config. Business justification: Required for Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) processing at the POS; each terminal must reference the DCC configuration defining margin, currency pairs, and disclosure rules.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Required for Acquirer Ownership Report: each terminal must be linked to its acquiring partner for settlement, compliance, and risk reporting.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_endpoint. Business justification: During terminal provisioning, assigning a network endpoint defines routing path and settlement routing for each terminal.']
  ledger_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.ledger_config. Business justification: REQUIRED: Transaction posting rules (chart of accounts, currency) are defined per ledger config; terminals must reference the config they use.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: REQUIRED: Regulatory and tax reporting require each terminal to be linked to its owning legal entity.']
  merchant_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical site where the terminal is installed.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_region. Business justification: Assigning each POS terminal to a gateway region determines routing, latency SLA, and regulatory jurisdiction for transactions.']
  sla_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.sla_profile. Business justification: Linking terminal to an SLA profile enables monitoring of terminal availability and latency against contractual service levels.']
  software_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal_software. Business justification: Direct link to installed software; removes duplicated version fields.']
  terminal_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Daily settlement report posts terminal revenue to a specific GL account; terminals need a direct GL account reference for accurate posting.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI/EMV compliance status of the terminal.']
  connectivity_type varchar [note: 'Primary network connectivity method used by the terminal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the terminal record was first created in the data lake.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the terminal was retired or removed from service (null if still active).']
  deployment_date date [note: 'Date the terminal was first installed at a merchant location.']
  device_certification_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the terminal\'s security or compliance certification.']
  emv_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process EMV chip card transactions.']
  form_factor varchar [note: 'Physical deployment style of the terminal.']
  hardware_model varchar [note: 'Model name/number of the terminal hardware as defined by the OEM.']
  hce_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal supports Host Card Emulation for mobile wallet payments.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address assigned to the terminal for network communication.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit performed on the terminal.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date the terminal last underwent preventive maintenance.']
  last_software_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent software/firmware update applied to the terminal.']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Media Access Control address of the terminal\'s network interface.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current maintenance state of the terminal.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the terminal was manufactured.']
  nfc_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal supports Near Field Communication for contactless payments.']
  pci_pts_cert_level varchar [note: 'PCI Point‑to‑Point Encryption (PTS) certification level achieved by the terminal.']
  pos_terminal_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the terminal.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑issued serial number that uniquely identifies the physical terminal hardware.']
  tamper_seal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the physical tamper‑evident seal on the terminal.']
  terminal_capabilities varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of functional capabilities supported by the terminal.']
  terminal_city varchar [note: 'City where the terminal is installed.']
  terminal_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to each point‑of‑sale terminal by the acquiring network.']
  terminal_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the terminal.']
  terminal_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the terminal.']
  terminal_owner_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal is owned by the fintech, a partner, or a third‑party provider.']
  terminal_region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the terminal\'s location.']
  terminal_security_cert_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the terminal\'s security certificate used for TLS/SSL connections.']
  terminal_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status (e.g., reason for maintenance or decommission).']
  tokenization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal is configured to use tokenization for card data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the terminal record.']

  Note: 'Master record for every physical or virtual point-of-sale terminal deployed across the merchant estate. Owns the TID (Terminal Identification Number), device serial number, hardware model, form factor (countertop, mPOS, unattended kiosk, SoftPOS), NFC capability flag, EMV chip capability, PCI PTS certification level, tamper-evident seal status, manufacture date, and current operational lifecycle stage. This is the SSOT for terminal identity within the payments-fintech platform.'
}

Table terminal.deployment {
  deployment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each terminal deployment event.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Deployment records must associate each terminal installation site with a country to calculate local tax, compliance, and shipping logistics.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Deployment cost fields are recorded in a specific currency; linking to the currency master enables accurate financial reporting and multi‑currency settlement.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who performed the installation.']
  installation_technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who performed the installation.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant where the terminal is deployed.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the terminal device being deployed.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the terminal became active for processing transactions.']
  compliance_audit_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent compliance audit was performed.']
  compliance_audit_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the compliance audit.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest PCI PTS compliance check for the terminal.']
  cost_adjustment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Adjustments (discounts, fees) applied to the gross deployment cost.']
  cost_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross cost incurred for the deployment before any adjustments.']
  cost_net decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net cost after adjustments for the deployment.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the terminal was removed from service, if applicable.']
  decommission_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for terminal decommissioning (e.g., hardware failure, upgrade, merchant closure).']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the deployment.']
  deployment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the deployment event occurred (e.g., when the terminal was installed).']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terminal firmware installed at deployment.']
  installation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the technician or point of contact for installation.']
  installation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the technician or point of contact for installation.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Actual date when the terminal was installed at the merchant site.']
  installation_technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the installation technician.']
  is_contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless payment (e.g., NFC) is enabled on the terminal.']
  is_emv_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMV chip processing is enabled on the terminal.']
  is_nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Near Field Communication is enabled for tap‑to‑pay.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured during deployment (e.g., observations, special instructions).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the deployment record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the deployment record.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the deployment event for tracking and reporting.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the deployment based on fraud and security assessments.']
  schedule_date date [note: 'Planned date for the terminal deployment.']
  shipping_carrier varchar [note: 'Logistics provider responsible for transporting the terminal to the merchant site.']
  site_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the merchant site address where the terminal is installed.']
  site_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the merchant site address (optional).']
  site_city varchar [note: 'City of the merchant site address.']
  site_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the merchant site address.']
  site_state varchar [note: 'State or province of the merchant site address.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terminal application software.']
  terminal_model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the terminal hardware.']
  terminal_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number of the terminal device.']
  terminal_type varchar [note: 'Category of terminal based on form factor and usage.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking identifier for the terminal shipment.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the physical deployment or redeployment of a terminal to a merchant location. Tracks deployment date, installation technician reference, merchant MID, physical site address, deployment status (scheduled, in-transit, installed, activated, decommissioned), shipping carrier, tracking number, and acceptance sign-off. Represents the operational event of placing a terminal into service at a specific merchant site.'
}

Table terminal.terminal_config {
  terminal_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the terminal configuration profile.']
  contactless_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.contactless_profile. Business justification: Config uses contactless profile; redundant flags removed.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Needed for Partner‑Specific Configuration Management: configs are provisioned per acquiring partner to enforce network routing and regulatory settings.']
  emv_parameter_set_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.emv_parameter_set. Business justification: Config references EMV parameters.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant to which this configuration is assigned.']
  receipt_template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal_receipt_template. Business justification: Terminal configuration should reference a receipt template to centralize receipt layout attributes; the new FK consolidates receipt formatting and removes duplicated columns.']
  software_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal_software. Business justification: Config specifies software version; move to software catalog.']
  terminal_group_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the terminal group (fleet) that uses this configuration.']
  accepted_card_schemes varchar [note: 'List of payment card schemes the terminal is permitted to process.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest audit: passed, failed, or not applicable.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI/EMV compliance state of the configuration.']
  config_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the configuration purpose and scope.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the configuration profile.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency used for limits and amounts.']
  dcc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether DCC is offered on the terminal.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the configuration becomes active for assigned terminals.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the configuration expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  encryption_key_version varchar [note: 'Version of the encryption key used for PIN and data protection.']
  hardware_model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model identifier for the terminal device.']
  idle_screen_timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Number of seconds of inactivity before the terminal returns to idle screen.']
  is_default_config boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profile is the default for new terminals.']
  language_locale varchar [note: 'Locale code (e.g., en-US) for UI language and formatting.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit for this configuration.']
  max_retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of authorization retries before decline.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit for any single transaction processed under this configuration.']
  offline_floor_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount that can be authorized offline before requiring online verification.']
  pin_entry_mode varchar [note: 'Mode in which PIN entry is accepted: online, offline, or both.']
  requires_pin_entry boolean [note: 'Specifies if PIN entry is mandatory for all transactions.']
  retry_interval_seconds varchar [note: 'Delay in seconds between successive authorization retries.']
  supports_emv boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal is EMV‑chip capable.']
  supports_nfc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal hardware supports NFC contactless.']
  terminal_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the configuration profile.']
  terminal_type varchar [note: 'Physical form factor or deployment category of the terminal.']
  tip_prompt_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal prompts the cardholder for a tip.']
  tip_prompt_options varchar [note: 'Configuration of tip prompting method offered to the cardholder.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the token service provider (TSP) used for tokenization.']
  tokenization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether card data is tokenized on the terminal.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the configuration.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change management of the configuration.']

  Note: 'Master configuration profile applied to a terminal, defining operational parameters such as accepted card schemes, contactless transaction limits, offline floor limits, PIN entry mode, tip prompting rules, DCC enablement, receipt printing preferences, idle screen settings, and language locale. A single config profile may be applied to many terminals, enabling fleet-wide configuration management.'
}

Table terminal.emv_parameter_set {
  emv_parameter_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each EMV parameter set record.']
  additional_terminal_capabilities varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal byte providing extended capability information.']
  aid varchar [note: 'Application Identifier for the EMV application on the card.']
  application_interchange_profile varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal value indicating the card\'s capabilities and restrictions.']
  application_transaction_counter varchar [note: 'Initial value of the transaction counter for the application.']
  application_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the EMV application on the card.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the parameter set with applicable regulations.']
  contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless (NFC) functionality is enabled on the terminal.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the jurisdiction of the parameter set.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for which the parameters apply.']
  cvm_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of supported CVMs for the terminal.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the parameter set becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the parameter set expires; null if indefinite.']
  emv_parameter_set_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description or notes about the parameter set.']
  emv_parameter_set_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the EMV parameter set.']
  floor_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum transaction amount that can be approved offline without online authorization.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the default EMV parameter set for the scheme.']
  kernel_version varchar [note: 'Version of the EMV kernel software loaded on the terminal.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent regulatory compliance audit.']
  offline_data_authentication varchar [note: 'Supported method for offline data authentication.']
  offline_pin_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the terminal can perform offline PIN verification.']
  online_pin_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the terminal can perform online PIN verification.']
  random_selection_percentage varchar [note: 'Percentage of transactions randomly selected for online processing to mitigate risk.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk score assigned based on fraud monitoring of this parameter set.']
  scheme varchar [note: 'Card scheme to which this EMV parameter set applies.']
  tac_default varchar [note: 'Default Terminal Action Code applied when no other TAC matches.']
  tac_denial varchar [note: 'Terminal Action Code used when a transaction is denied offline.']
  tac_online varchar [note: 'Terminal Action Code used when a transaction requires online authorization.']
  terminal_capabilities varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal byte indicating the terminal\'s functional capabilities.']
  transaction_type_support varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of transaction types the terminal can process.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  velocity_check_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of offline transactions allowed before a mandatory online check.']
  velocity_check_time_window varchar [note: 'Time window in minutes for the velocity check limit.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for change management of the parameter set.']

  Note: 'Defines the full set of EMV chip parameters loaded onto a terminal for each supported card scheme (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover, UnionPay). Includes AID (Application Identifier), TAC (Terminal Action Codes) for denial/online/default, terminal capabilities byte, additional terminal capabilities, floor limit, random transaction selection percentage, velocity check parameters, and EMV kernel version. Critical for correct chip transaction processing and scheme compliance.'
}

Table terminal.contactless_profile {
  contactless_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the contactless profile configuration.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the profile.']
  contactless_fallback_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic fallback to chip is enabled after contactless failures.']
  contactless_fallback_timeout_ms varchar [note: 'Timeout in milliseconds before fallback to chip after a contactless attempt.']
  contactless_profile_description varchar [note: 'Free-form description of the contactless profile.']
  contactless_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profile.']
  contactless_transaction_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount allowed for a single contactless transaction under this profile.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profile record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction limit.']
  cvm_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount for which a specific Cardholder Verification Method (CVM) is required.']
  cvm_type varchar [note: 'Type of CVM applied for transactions under this profile.']
  dual_interface_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal supports both contactless and chip interfaces.']
  dual_interface_routing_rule varchar [note: 'Routing rule applied when both contactless and chip interfaces are available.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the profile ceases to be effective (null if open-ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the profile becomes effective for terminals.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terminal firmware associated with this profile.']
  kernel_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the contactless kernel software.']
  max_transaction_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of contactless attempts before fallback to chip.']
  nfc_antenna_gain_db decimal(5,2) [note: 'Antenna gain in decibels for NFC communication.']
  nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if NFC (Near Field Communication) is enabled on the terminal.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding the profile.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the contactless profile within the system.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name for the contactless configuration profile.']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the profile (e.g., default, custom, partner specific).']
  qr_payment_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if QR code payments are enabled for the terminal.']
  qr_payment_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether static or dynamic QR codes are used.']
  security_certification_level varchar [note: 'Security certification level of the terminal (e.g., PCI PTS, PCI PTS A, PCI PTS B).']
  supported_kernels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of contactless kernel identifiers supported (e.g., Visa payWave, Mastercard PayPass, Amex ExpressPay, Discover D-PAS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Visa payWave|Mastercard PayPass|Amex ExpressPay|Discover D-PAS|UnionPay QuickPass|JCB J/Speedy — promote to reference product]']
  terminal_software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the terminal software that implements this profile.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated the profile.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profile record was last updated.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the profile.']

  Note: 'Configuration entity defining NFC and contactless payment parameters for a terminal, including supported contactless kernels (Visa payWave, Mastercard PayPass/Tap & Go, Amex ExpressPay, Discover D-PAS), CVM (Cardholder Verification Method) limits, contactless transaction ceiling limits, dual-interface routing rules, and QR code payment enablement flags. Governs how the terminal handles tap-to-pay and QR-based transactions.'
}

Table terminal.software {
  software_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each terminal software record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Software Licensing Audit: linking software releases to the supplying partner enables license tracking, version compliance, and audit trails.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date on which the software received its most recent certification.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification state of the software with respect to PCI/EMV standards.']
  checksum_sha256 varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 hash of the software package for integrity verification.']
  compliance_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards body that issued the certification (e.g., PCI SSC, EMVCo).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first inserted into the catalog.']
  deployment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the software in the terminal fleet.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date after which the software should no longer be deployed.']
  digital_signature varchar [note: 'Cryptographic signature used to verify authenticity of the package.']
  end_of_support_date date [note: 'Date after which the vendor will no longer provide support or patches.']
  file_location_path varchar [note: 'Filesystem or object‑store path where the binary is stored.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Binary size of the software package.']
  hardware_model_compatibility varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of terminal hardware models that can run this software.']
  is_contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the software enables contactless transaction processing.']
  is_firmware boolean [note: 'True if the software package is firmware rather than an application layer.']
  is_mandatory_update boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deployment of this version is required for compliance or security.']
  is_test_version boolean [note: 'True if the version is intended for testing environments only.']
  pci_validation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent PCI validation.']
  pci_validation_status varchar [note: 'PCI validation outcome for the software package.']
  regulatory_compliance_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of regulatory codes applicable to this software (e.g., PCI|EMV|PSD2).']
  release_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the software version was made publicly available.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing changes, bug fixes, and enhancements for this version.']
  release_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the release according to versioning semantics.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned by the security team (0.00‑99.99).']
  scheme_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from payment schemes (e.g., Visa, Mastercard) confirming approval.']
  software_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the software\'s purpose and functionality.']
  software_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the software package.']
  software_type varchar [note: 'Category of the software component.']
  source_repository varchar [note: 'URL of the source code repository or binary storage location.']
  supported_payment_schemes varchar [note: 'Payment schemes that the software version supports.']
  supported_terminal_features varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of terminal capabilities enabled by this software (e.g., NFC, EMV, QR).']
  update_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority for rolling out this software version.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that supplied the software.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier following the vendor\'s versioning scheme (e.g., 1.2.3).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of terminal application software versions available for deployment across the terminal fleet. Tracks software package name, version number, target hardware model compatibility, payment application type (payment kernel, PIN pad firmware, OS), PCI PA-DSS or PCI P2PE validation status, certification date, scheme approval references (Visa, Mastercard), and release notes. Serves as the authoritative software version registry.'
}

Table terminal.software_update {
  software_update_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each software update event.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or automated process that approved the software update.']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or automated process that approved the software update.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the POS terminal or device being updated.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real date and time when the update process finished.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The real date and time when the update process began on the terminal.']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the operator gave approval for the update.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error identifier when the update fails.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the failure condition.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations captured by the operator or system.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the update after execution.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'When the software update record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'When the software update record was last modified.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time by which the update was expected to complete.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the update was scheduled to begin.']
  software_version_after varchar [note: 'The firmware or software version installed on the terminal after the update completes.']
  software_version_before varchar [note: 'The firmware or software version installed on the terminal prior to the update.']
  update_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the software package was delivered to the terminal.']
  update_reference varchar [note: 'Business-visible reference code or batch number for the software update.']
  update_status varchar [note: 'State of the update process from scheduling through completion or failure.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a software update or firmware push event executed on a terminal. Captures the target terminal, source and target software versions, update method (OTA, USB, network download), scheduled update window, actual start and completion timestamps, update outcome (success, failed, rolled back), error codes on failure, and operator who authorized the update. Enables full audit trail of software change management across the terminal fleet.'
}

Table terminal.key_injection {
  key_injection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the key injection event record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician or automated system that performed the key injection.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal on which the key injection was performed.']
  technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician or automated system that performed the key injection.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured during the key injection event.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the injection meets PCI PTS compliance requirements.']
  hsm_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HSM that generated or protected the injected key.']
  injection_location varchar [note: 'Code representing the physical or logical location where the key injection took place.']
  injection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to load the key onto the terminal, either via a physical Key Injection Facility (KIF) or remote loading.']
  injection_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the key injection attempt, indicating whether it succeeded or failed.']
  injection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cryptographic key was injected into the terminal.']
  key_check_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Six‑character hexadecimal check value used to verify the integrity of the injected key.']
  key_expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the injected key must no longer be used.']
  key_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the injected key: Terminal Master Key (TMK), Terminal Encryption Key (TEK), or PIN Encryption Key.']
  key_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the injected cryptographic key.']
  load_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the order of key loads for the terminal.']
  outcome_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the reason for a failure outcome, if applicable.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this key injection record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this key injection record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that logged the key injection event.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a cryptographic key injection event performed on a terminal, including the key type (TMK — Terminal Master Key, TEK — Terminal Encryption Key, PIN encryption key), injection method (KIF — Key Injection Facility, remote key loading), HSM reference, key check value (KCV), injection timestamp, technician or automated system identifier, and injection outcome. Critical for PCI PTS compliance and secure PIN transaction processing.'
}

  merchant_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the terminal is installed.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.pos_terminal. Business justification: Associate TID provisioning with terminal; duplicate hardware info removed.']
  acquiring_bin varchar [note: 'Bank Identification Number of the acquiring bank that processes the terminal\'s transactions.']
  activation_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the terminal became active for processing transactions.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the terminal with PCI DSS and other regulations.']
  contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless payment is enabled on the terminal.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the terminal is registered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the provisioning record was first created in the data lake.']
  deactivation_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the terminal was deactivated or deregistered.']
  deployment_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the terminal was physically installed at the location.']
  emv_capability varchar [note: 'EMV support level of the terminal (contact, contactless, or both).']
  hardware_model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number of the terminal hardware.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address assigned to the terminal (if applicable).']
  last_software_update timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent software/firmware update applied to the terminal.']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Hardware MAC address of the terminal\'s network interface.']
  mid varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant to which the terminal is assigned.']
  provisioning_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the terminal was provisioned in the system.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk rating assigned by the fraud detection platform based on terminal behavior.']
  scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network scheme for which the terminal is registered (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  scheme_registration_status varchar [note: 'Current registration status of the terminal with the payment scheme.']
  security_certification varchar [note: 'Security certification level of the terminal per PCI PTS standards.']
  terminal_type varchar [note: 'Category of terminal based on form factor and usage.']
  tid varchar [note: 'Unique terminal identifier assigned by the acquiring network.']
  tid_provisioning_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the terminal.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal supports tokenization of card data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the provisioning record.']

  Note: 'Master record governing the provisioning and registration of a TID (Terminal Identification Number) within the payment network. Tracks TID value, associated MID, acquiring BIN, card scheme registration status per network (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), provisioning date, TID status (active, suspended, deregistered), and the network registration reference numbers returned by each scheme. Bridges terminal identity to network-level routing and settlement.'
}

Table terminal.terminal_certification {
  terminal_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each terminal certification record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the certification became effective.']
  body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the certification (e.g., PCI SSC, EMVCo, Visa, Mastercard, national scheme).']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the certification body to this certification.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the certification (e.g., hardware only, software only, combined, contactless, tokenization).']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Type of certification such as PCI PTS, PA‑DSS, P2PE, EMV Level 1, EMV Level 2, Contactless Kernel.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes from compliance reviewers about the certification.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score (0‑100) reflecting overall compliance posture.']
  conditions_or_restrictions varchar [note: 'Any conditions, limitations, or restrictions imposed by the certifying body.']
  contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal supports contactless transactions.']
  cvv_capture_supported boolean [note: 'True if the terminal can capture CVV for card‑not‑present transactions.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the terminal was retired or taken out of service.']
  deployment_date date [note: 'Date the terminal was first deployed in the field.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored certification document.']
  emv_level varchar [note: 'EMV certification level achieved by the terminal.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certification expires or must be renewed.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the terminal firmware.']
  hardware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the terminal hardware.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address assigned to the terminal.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether certification is mandatory for the terminal\'s operating region.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the jurisdiction governing the certification.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit for this terminal.']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Media Access Control address of the terminal\'s network interface.']
  magnetic_stripe_supported boolean [note: 'True if the terminal can read magnetic stripe cards.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that manufactured the terminal.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number assigned by the manufacturer.']
  pci_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI compliance status of the terminal.']
  pin_entry_supported boolean [note: 'True if the terminal supports PIN entry for cardholder verification.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework under which the certification was obtained.']
  renewal_notice_date date [note: 'Date on which a renewal notice should be sent to the responsible team.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'True if the certification is approaching expiry and requires renewal.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the terminal based on certification and security posture.']
  secure_element_type varchar [note: 'Type of secure element used (e.g., HCE, TPM, dedicated SE).']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the terminal device.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the terminal operating software.']
  terminal_model varchar [note: 'Human‑readable model name or identifier for the terminal hardware.']
  terminal_type varchar [note: 'Category of terminal based on deployment context.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal is capable of tokenizing card data.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the certification record (used for change tracking).']

  Note: 'Master record of terminal type approvals and certifications obtained for a hardware/software combination. Tracks certification body (PCI SSC, EMVCo, Visa, Mastercard, national scheme), certification type (PCI PTS, PA-DSS, P2PE, EMV Level 1/Level 2, contactless kernel), certificate number, approval date, expiry date, certification scope, and any conditions or restrictions. Enables compliance tracking and renewal management across the terminal estate.'
}

Table terminal.inventory {
  inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each terminal inventory record.']
  assigned_merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant currently using or assigned to the terminal.']
