  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inventory record was first created.']
  current_location_code varchar [note: 'Identifier (e.g., warehouse code) of the terminal\'s present location.']
  current_location_type varchar [note: 'Physical location category where the terminal resides.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the terminal was removed from service, if applicable.']
  deployment_date date [note: 'Date the terminal was installed at a merchant site.']
  device_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status or any recent change.']
  emv_capability varchar [note: 'Supported EMV capabilities of the terminal.']
  encryption_key_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the encryption key used for data-at-rest encryption on the terminal.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address assigned to the terminal (if applicable).']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which preventive maintenance was performed.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the terminal asset.']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Media Access Control address of the terminal\'s network interface.']
  maintenance_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval in days between routine maintenance activities.']
  network_connectivity_type varchar [note: 'Supported network connection method for the terminal.']
  nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the terminal can read NFC-enabled devices.']
  pci_pts_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status with PCI Point‑to‑Point Encryption standards.']
  physical_condition varchar [note: 'Observed condition of the terminal hardware.']
  power_requirement_w varchar [note: 'Electrical power consumption in watts.']
  security_certification_date date [note: 'Date when the terminal received its latest security certification.']
  shipment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code for the shipment that delivered the terminal to inventory.']
  temperature_rating_c varchar [note: 'Maximum operating temperature in degrees Celsius.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can generate payment tokens.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Purchase cost of a single terminal unit in the functional currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inventory record.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the supplier that provided the terminal.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty ends.']

  Note: 'Tracks the physical inventory of terminal hardware units held in warehouses, staging facilities, and field stock. Records device serial number, hardware model, current inventory location (warehouse, in-transit, merchant site, repair depot), inventory status (available, reserved, deployed, faulty, decommissioned), acquisition date, unit cost, and assigned batch or shipment reference. Supports device lifecycle management from procurement through decommission.'
}

Table terminal.assignment {
  assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each terminal assignment record.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant to which the terminal is assigned.']
  merchant_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical site or store location where the terminal is deployed.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the POS terminal being assigned.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date when the assignment actually ended (null if still active).']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the physical address where the terminal is installed.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the address (optional).']
  assigned_mid varchar [note: 'The merchant\'s identification number as used in payment networks.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assignment.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the terminal assignment: permanent, temporary, seasonal, or test.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the terminal installation address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the terminal at the location.']
  contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless payment functionality is enabled on the terminal.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the terminal location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was created.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Planned date for terminal removal or retirement.']
  decommission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal is scheduled for decommission.']
  emv_capability varchar [note: 'EMV support level of the terminal: contact, contactless, or both.']
  expected_end_date date [note: 'Planned date when the assignment is expected to end.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware/software version installed on the terminal.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection.']
  lane_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the checkout lane or POS position within the location (e.g., Lane A, Lane 1).']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or security inspection of the terminal.']
  last_software_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent firmware or software update applied to the terminal.']
  network_provider varchar [note: 'Payment network provider associated with the terminal (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes about the terminal assignment.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the installation site.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the terminal assignment (e.g., new store opening, seasonal promotion).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score (0‑99.99) for the terminal assignment based on fraud and security factors.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the terminal assignment becomes effective.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the address.']
  terminal_model_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the terminal model/type (e.g., TPOS-1000).']
  terminal_owner varchar [note: 'Internal department or business unit responsible for the terminal.']
  terminal_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number of the terminal hardware.']
  tokenization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal is configured to generate payment tokens.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assignment record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the assignment record.']

  Note: 'Association entity recording the active assignment of a terminal to a specific merchant, merchant location, and POS lane or checkout position. Captures assignment start date, expected end date, assignment type (permanent, temporary, seasonal), assigned MID, site address, lane identifier, and assignment status. Enables fleet management queries such as \'which terminals are assigned to which merchants\' and supports redeployment planning.'
}

Table terminal.status_event {
  status_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the terminal status change event.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal whose status changed.']
  status_actor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Status Change Audit logs the employee who performed the status change, needed for operational accountability.']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Category of entity that triggered the status change.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this status event record was first created in the data lake.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this status event record.']
  change_request_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the change‑request record that authorized the status transition.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the actor about the status change.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI PTS compliance state of the terminal.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the status change event was generated.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terminal firmware after the event.']
  hardware_model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number of the terminal device.']
  incident_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of any incident or ticket linked to the status change.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Operational state of the terminal after the change.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Operational state of the terminal before the change.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the POS application software after the event.']
  status_change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the status transition occurred (e.g., fraud_suspicion, hardware_failure, compliance, upgrade, merchant_request).']
  terminal_location varchar [note: 'Physical location identifier (e.g., store code or address) where the terminal is installed.']

  Note: 'Transactional log of terminal lifecycle status change events, capturing each transition in terminal operational state (e.g., provisioned → active → suspended → decommissioned). Records the previous status, new status, event timestamp, triggering actor (system, operator, merchant), reason code, and any associated incident or change request reference. Provides a complete audit trail of terminal state changes for compliance and operational troubleshooting.'
}

Table terminal.tamper_event {
  tamper_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tamper event record.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst handling the investigation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst handling the investigation.']
  merchant_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location (store/branch) of the terminal.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the terminal where tamper was detected.']
  alert_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alert was sent.']
  compliance_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the compliance report generated for this event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tamper event record was created in the data lake.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Source mechanism that generated the tamper detection.']
  event_details varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the tamper event.']
  event_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the event for the terminal on a given day.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the tamper event was detected.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of terminal firmware at time of event.']
  investigation_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when investigation of the tamper event was concluded.']
  investigation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when investigation of the tamper event began.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the investigation into the tamper event.']
  is_alert_sent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether an alert notification was sent to operations.']
  is_compliance_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tamper event was reported to regulatory compliance.']
  is_test_event boolean [note: 'Indicates if the event was generated from a test or simulation environment.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Final resolution outcome of the tamper event.']
  response_action varchar [note: 'Action taken by the terminal in response to the tamper event.']
  sensor_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific sensor that triggered the tamper detection.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating assigned to the tamper event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the event record.']
  tamper_type varchar [note: 'Category of tamper detected.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tamper event record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a physical or logical tamper detection event triggered by a terminal\'s PCI PTS security mechanisms. Captures event timestamp, tamper type (case open, voltage attack, temperature anomaly, logical intrusion), terminal response action (key zeroization, self-destruct, alert), detection source (hardware sensor, software check), and subsequent investigation status. Critical for PCI PTS compliance and fraud prevention.'
}

Table terminal.maintenance {
  maintenance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the terminal maintenance record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Maintenance cost amounts are stored in a currency; FK to currency master supports consolidated expense reporting and audit of cross‑border maintenance fees.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who performed the maintenance.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that owns the terminal.']
  partner_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity (in‑house team or third‑party) that performed the maintenance.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the terminal device that received maintenance.']
  technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who performed the maintenance.']
  terminal_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the terminal resides.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance activity met internal compliance requirements.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base cost charged for the maintenance service before taxes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the maintenance record was first created in the system.']
  downtime_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the terminal was brought back online.']
  downtime_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the terminal went offline for maintenance.']
  fault_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the fault or issue that triggered the maintenance.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the terminal firmware at the time of maintenance.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance record.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Category of maintenance performed on the terminal.']
  mean_time_to_repair_minutes varchar [note: 'Average time in minutes required to repair similar faults, captured for performance analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the technician or service manager.']
  part_replaced_details varchar [note: 'Details of the part(s) replaced, including part numbers and serials.']
  part_replaced_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any hardware part was replaced during the service.']
  provider_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance was performed by internal staff or an external third‑party.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference number for the maintenance activity.']
  repair_actions varchar [note: 'Detailed actions taken to resolve the fault.']
  replacement_parts varchar [note: 'List of parts replaced during the maintenance activity.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current resolution state of the maintenance request.']
  return_to_service_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the terminal was returned to operational service after maintenance.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the maintenance activity.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which the maintenance service was performed.']
  sla_actual_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes taken to complete the maintenance.']
  sla_target_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes defined by SLA for completing the maintenance.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the terminal software/OS at the time of maintenance.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax applied to the maintenance cost.']
  terminal_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the terminal device.']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount billed for the maintenance (cost plus tax).']
  total_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes the terminal was unavailable due to maintenance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the maintenance record.']
  warranty_claim_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the warranty claim associated with this maintenance, if applicable.']
  warranty_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance activity is covered under the terminal\'s warranty.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a maintenance or repair activity performed on a terminal device. Tracks maintenance type (preventive, corrective, emergency), fault description, repair actions taken, replacement parts used, maintenance provider (in-house, third-party), service date, resolution status, and return-to-service timestamp. Supports device health management and SLA tracking for terminal uptime commitments.'
}

Table terminal.terminal_batch {
  terminal_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the terminal batch record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Batch settlement totals are expressed in a currency; linking to currency master ensures correct conversion, reporting, and compliance with settlement regulations.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant owning the terminal.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal that generated the batch.']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement_batch. Business justification: Required for Batch Reconciliation Report that matches each terminal batch to the settlement batch that settles it.']
  acquiring_host_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the acquiring host for the corresponding settlement batch.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total of fees, discounts, or other adjustments applied to the batch.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Indicates how the batch was created: automatic day‑end, shift‑based, or manual entry.']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the batch was closed on the terminal.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of why a batch failed settlement, if applicable.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of approved transaction amounts before fees or adjustments.']
  is_batch_reconciled boolean [note: 'True if the batch has been successfully reconciled with downstream accounting.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after applying adjustments to the gross amount.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the batch was opened on the terminal.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the batch reconciliation was completed.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the batch record.']
  reference_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the batch by the acquiring host or settlement system.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric sequence that orders batches chronologically for a given terminal.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the batch within the settlement process.']
  total_declined_count varchar [note: 'Number of transactions within the batch that were declined.']
  total_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual transactions captured in the batch.']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing a settlement batch opened and closed at a terminal. Captures batch open timestamp, batch close timestamp, total transaction count, total approved amount, total declined count, batch sequence number, settlement status (open, closed, submitted, settled, failed), and the acquiring host batch reference. Links terminal-level batch activity to the settlement domain for end-of-day reconciliation.'
}

Table terminal.terminal_reconciliation {
  terminal_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each terminal reconciliation record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Reconciliation amounts are in a currency; FK enables consistent financial statements and audit trails across currencies.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service that created the record.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant that owns the terminal.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the POS terminal whose totals are being reconciled.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement run against which the terminal totals are compared.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement run against which the terminal totals are compared.']
  terminal_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the terminal is installed.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch that contains the transactions for this reconciliation.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or service that performed the last update.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable batch identifier used by back‑office operations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of compliance checks (e.g., PCI DSS) for the reconciliation batch.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first inserted.']
  host_reported_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of transactions recorded by the host system.']
  host_reported_count varchar [note: 'Number of transactions recorded by the host/settlement system for the period.']
  is_manual_adjustment boolean [note: 'True if a manual adjustment was applied to resolve the variance.']
  manual_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any manual correction applied.']
  manual_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why a manual adjustment was made.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date covered by the reconciliation period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the reconciliation period.']
  processing_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing center or hub that executed the host settlement.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reconciliation process.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reconciliation was performed.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the reconciliation batch for audit and tracking.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes documenting investigation findings and actions taken.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any investigation or remediation activity.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk rating assigned by the fraud/risk engine for this reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the reconciliation data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PaymentGateway|TransactionProcessing|MerchantManagement|FraudPlatform|DigitalWallet|RiskCompliance|DisputeManagement|DataWarehouse — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  terminal_firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the firmware running on the terminal at reconciliation time.']
  terminal_reported_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of transactions reported by the terminal (in transaction currency).']
  terminal_reported_count varchar [note: 'Number of transactions reported by the terminal for the period.']
  terminal_software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the application software on the terminal at reconciliation time.']
  terminal_status_at_reconciliation varchar [note: 'Operational status of the terminal when the reconciliation was performed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary difference between terminal and host totals (terminal - host).']
  variance_count varchar [note: 'Difference between terminal and host transaction counts (terminal - host).']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the primary cause of the variance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the reconciliation outcome between terminal-reported totals and host-processed totals for a given batch or settlement period. Records terminal-reported count and amount, host-reported count and amount, variance amount, reconciliation status (balanced, out-of-balance, pending investigation), and resolution notes. Supports acquirer back-office reconciliation and dispute prevention.'
}

Table terminal.terminal_model {
  terminal_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the terminal hardware model.']
  capability_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal_capability. Business justification: Model references capability profile; duplicate capability fields removed.']
  terminal_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to terminal.terminal_certification. Business justification: Model linked to its certification; redundant certification fields removed.']
  certification_date date [note: 'Date the terminal model received its certification.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state with PCI and regulatory standards.']
  connectivity_options varchar [note: 'Network connectivity methods available on the terminal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the terminal model record was first created.']
  dimensions_mm varchar [note: 'Width x Height x Depth in millimetres (e.g., 150x80x30).']
  display_type varchar [note: 'Screen technology and size (e.g., LCD 4", OLED 5").']
  end_of_life_date date [note: 'Date after which the terminal model is no longer supported.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terminal firmware.']
  form_factor varchar [note: 'Physical deployment style of the terminal.']
  hardware_specifications varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of key hardware components.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that manufactures the terminal hardware.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Vendor‑assigned part number for the terminal model.']
  memory_capacity_mb varchar [note: 'RAM size in megabytes.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the terminal model.']
  operating_temperature_celsius varchar [note: 'Supported ambient temperature range, e.g., -10 to 45°C.']
  pci_pts_version varchar [note: 'PCI PIN Transaction Security version the terminal is certified to.']
  pin_pad_type varchar [note: 'Type of PIN entry device (e.g., integrated, external, keypad).']
  power_requirements_watts decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum power consumption in watts.']
  processor_type varchar [note: 'CPU or microcontroller model used in the terminal.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date the terminal model was first made available to customers.']
  security_features varchar [note: 'Key security capabilities (e.g., tamper‑detect, encryption, secure boot).']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terminal operating software.']
  storage_capacity_mb varchar [note: 'Non‑volatile storage size in megabytes.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes the terminal can handle.']
  supported_interfaces varchar [note: 'List of payment interfaces the terminal supports (e.g., EMV contact, EMV contactless, NFC, MSR, QR).']
  supported_schemes varchar [note: 'Payment card networks supported by the terminal.']
  terminal_model_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the terminal model\'s capabilities and intended use cases.']
  terminal_model_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the terminal model record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the terminal model record.']
  weight_grams varchar [note: 'Weight of the terminal unit in grams.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of terminal hardware models supported by the platform. Defines manufacturer, model name, form factor (countertop, portable, mPOS, unattended, SoftPOS), supported interfaces (EMV contact, EMV contactless, NFC, MSR, QR), PIN pad type, display type, connectivity options (Ethernet, Wi-Fi, 4G, Bluetooth), PCI PTS version certified, and end-of-life date. Used to standardize device procurement and configuration management.'
}

Table terminal.capability {
  capability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the terminal capability profile.']
  accessibility_features varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of accessibility options (e.g., audio prompts, high‑contrast UI). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: audio_prompt|high_contrast|large_font|braille|visual_alert] — promote to reference product']
  capability_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the capability profile used in configuration and reporting.']
  capability_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the functional and technical characteristics of the capability.']
  capability_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the terminal capability profile.']
  capability_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capability profile.']
  capability_type varchar [note: 'Category of terminal that the capability applies to (e.g., point‑of‑sale, ATM, mobile POS).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI/DSS compliance status of the capability profile.']
  contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless (tap‑to‑pay) functionality is supported.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the capability record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the maximum transaction amount.']
  dcc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Dynamic Currency Conversion is available on the terminal.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the capability profile becomes effective for assignment.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the capability profile expires or is retired (null if indefinite).']
  emv_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMV chip transaction processing is supported.']
  encryption_key_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the encryption key version used for PIN and data encryption.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terminal firmware associated with this capability.']
  hardware_model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model identifier for the terminal hardware.']
  hce_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal supports Host Card Emulation for mobile wallets.']
  idle_screen_timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Number of seconds of inactivity before the terminal screen returns to idle mode.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capability profile is the default for newly provisioned terminals of its type.']
  language_locale varchar [note: 'Locale code (e.g., en_US, fr_FR) for the terminal user interface language.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit for this capability.']
  max_retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of authorization retries allowed before transaction is declined.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount that can be authorized on a single transaction using this capability.']
  multi_application_support boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can host multiple payment applications simultaneously.']
  nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Near Field Communication (NFC) for mobile wallets is supported.']
  qr_payment_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether QR code payment method is supported.']
  receipt_printing_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of receipt output supported by the terminal.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score assigned to the capability based on fraud and security considerations.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terminal application software associated with this capability.']
  supported_card_schemes varchar [note: 'Card network schemes supported by the terminal (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visa|mastercard|amex|discover|jcb|unionpay] — promote to reference product']
  supported_payment_methods varchar [note: 'List of payment methods supported (e.g., chip, swipe, contactless, QR, NFC wallet). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: chip|swipe|contactless|qr|nfc_wallet|magstripe|tokenized] — promote to reference product']
  terminal_type varchar [note: 'Physical form factor of the terminal (e.g., fixed, mobile, handheld, integrated).']
  tip_prompt_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can prompt for tip entry during a transaction.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can perform tokenization of PAN data.']
  transaction_limit_per_day decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for transactions per day for terminals with this capability.']
  transaction_limit_per_month decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for transactions per month for terminals with this capability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the capability record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management of the capability profile.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining the functional capability profile of a terminal model or individual terminal, specifying supported payment methods (chip, contactless, MSR swipe, PIN, signature, QR, NFC wallet), supported card schemes, maximum transaction amount, supported currencies, multi-application support flag, and accessibility features. Used during merchant onboarding and terminal assignment to match terminal capabilities to merchant acceptance requirements.'
}

Table terminal.terminal_alert {
  terminal_alert_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each terminal alert event.']
  acknowledger_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Alert Management Process requires recording which employee acknowledged the alert for traceability.']
  merchant_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the site or store where the terminal is installed.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal or device that originated the alert.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the alert was acknowledged by an operator or automated process.']
  alert_category varchar [note: 'Broad category used for reporting and routing (operational, security, hardware, or software).']
  alert_description varchar [note: 'Narrative text providing additional context or diagnostic information about the alert.']
  alert_source varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the alert was raised by an external monitoring system or reported by the terminal itself.']
  alert_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the alert event occurred on the terminal.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the alert (e.g., connectivity loss, battery low, paper out, key expiry, certification expiry, abnormal decline rate spike).']
  battery_level_percent varchar [note: 'Remaining battery charge expressed as a percentage.']
  cert_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the terminal\'s security certification (e.g., PCI PTS) expires.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal met regulatory and security compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the alert record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  decline_spike_percent varchar [note: 'Percentage increase in decline rate compared to baseline, triggering a decline_spike alert.']
  device_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number of the terminal hardware.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑form text explaining why the alert was escalated.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the alert was escalated to a higher tier.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terminal firmware when the alert was generated.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'True when the alert required escalation beyond first‑line support.']
  key_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the terminal\'s encryption key is scheduled to expire.']
  last_communication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the terminal last successfully communicated with the gateway.']
  last_maintenance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent scheduled or unscheduled maintenance.']
  network_status varchar [note: 'Current network state of the terminal when the alert occurred.']
  paper_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether receipt paper is available or depleted.']
  payload varchar [note: 'Structured or unstructured data captured from the terminal or monitoring system.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the underlying issue was fixed and the alert closed.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑99.99) generated by the risk management system.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the seriousness of the alert for prioritization.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terminal application software at alert time.']
  terminal_alert_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the processing state of the alert from creation to closure.']
  transaction_volume_last_hour varchar [note: 'Number of transactions processed by the terminal in the hour before the alert.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the alert record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of an operational alert generated for a terminal, such as connectivity loss, battery low, paper out, key expiry approaching, certification expiry warning, or abnormal decline rate spike. Captures alert type, severity level, alert timestamp, terminal identifier, alert source (monitoring system, terminal self-report), acknowledgement status, acknowledged by, and resolution timestamp. Supports proactive terminal fleet health management.'
}

Table terminal.connectivity {
  connectivity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each terminal connectivity record.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_endpoint. Business justification: Operational monitoring maps each terminal\'s connectivity profile to the specific network endpoint it uses for latency and SLA compliance.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the terminal to which this connectivity record belongs.']
  apn_name varchar [note: 'Access Point Name used for cellular data connections.']
  average_latency_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average network latency in milliseconds observed over the reporting window.']
  backup_channel_type varchar [note: 'Backup communication channel used by the terminal.']
  backup_gateway_ip varchar [note: 'Gateway IP for the backup network.']
  backup_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address assigned to the backup channel.']
  backup_ip_assignment_type varchar [note: 'Method used to assign the backup IP address.']
  backup_subnet_mask varchar [note: 'Subnet mask for the backup network.']
  cellular_signal_strength_dbm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured cellular signal strength in decibel‑millwatts.']
  configuration_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the connectivity configuration applied to the terminal.']
  connectivity_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the connectivity setup (single, dual, or redundant channels).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the connectivity record was first created.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the terminal firmware governing network interfaces.']
  last_config_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the connectivity configuration.']
  last_heartbeat_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent heartbeat received from the terminal.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the online_status last changed.']
  network_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the telecom or ISP providing the connectivity service.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for operational comments or troubleshooting details.']
  online_status varchar [note: 'Current connectivity status of the terminal.']
  primary_channel_type varchar [note: 'Primary communication channel used by the terminal.']
  primary_gateway_ip varchar [note: 'Gateway IP for the primary network.']
  primary_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address assigned to the primary channel.']
  primary_ip_assignment_type varchar [note: 'Method used to assign the primary IP address.']
  primary_subnet_mask varchar [note: 'Subnet mask for the primary network.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the connectivity profile in external systems.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the connectivity configuration profile.']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Service Level Agreement compliance status for connectivity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the connectivity record.']
  uptime_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of time the terminal remained connected during the monitoring period.']
  vpn_tunnel_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the VPN tunnel used for secure connectivity.']
  wifi_signal_strength_dbm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured Wi‑Fi signal strength in decibel‑milliwatts.']
  wifi_ssid varchar [note: 'Service Set Identifier of the Wi‑Fi network used as primary or backup.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking the network connectivity configuration and real-time connectivity status of a terminal. Captures primary and backup communication channels (Ethernet, Wi-Fi SSID, 4G APN, dial-up), IP address assignment type (static/DHCP), VPN tunnel reference, last heartbeat timestamp, average latency, connectivity uptime percentage, and current online/offline status. Supports SLA monitoring and network troubleshooting for the terminal estate.'
}

Table terminal.terminal_fee_schedule {
  terminal_fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the terminal fee schedule record.']
  applicable_terminal_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a logical group of terminals sharing this fee schedule.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Fee schedule amounts are defined per currency; linking to currency master supports pricing consistency and regulatory fee disclosures.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Fee schedules assign specific GL accounts for each fee type; linking enables automated journal entry generation.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the terminal for which the fee schedule is directly applicable.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the detailed audit log for changes to this fee schedule.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the merchant is invoiced for the fee schedule.']
  billing_day_of_month varchar [note: 'Numeric day of the month (1‑31) on which the invoice is generated.']
  chargeback_handling_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee assessed for processing and managing chargeback events.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent regulatory compliance assessment was performed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fee schedule record was first created in the system.']
  discount_end_date date [note: 'Date when the discount expires.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the total fee amount during the discount period.']
  discount_start_date date [note: 'Date when the discount becomes effective.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The calendar date on which the fee schedule starts to apply.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The calendar date on which the fee schedule ceases to apply (null for open‑ended).']
  fee_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper limit on total fees that may be billed within the defined fee cap period.']
  fee_cap_period varchar [note: 'Timeframe over which the fee cap amount applies.']
  fee_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the fee schedule for reporting and UI display.']
  fee_schedule_code varchar [note: 'External code or number used to reference the fee schedule in contracts and pricing catalogs.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Category of fees contained in the schedule (e.g., rental, transaction, PCI compliance, chargeback handling, surcharge, discount).']
  is_tax_included boolean [note: 'True if fees are tax‑inclusive; false if tax is applied on top of fees.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fee schedule was last reviewed for compliance or pricing accuracy.']
  monthly_rental_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Recurring flat fee charged to the merchant for terminal rental each month.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary information or comments about the fee schedule.']
  pci_compliance_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee charged to cover PCI DSS compliance costs associated with the terminal.']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'Tier of pricing plan associated with the fee schedule.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the fee schedule has passed all applicable regulatory checks.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'State of the most recent review process for the fee schedule.']
  surcharge_fixed_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat surcharge applied to the terminal fee schedule, independent of transaction volume.']
  surcharge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage‑based surcharge applied to transaction fees.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary tax amount derived from tax_rate and taxable fees.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage tax rate applied to taxable fee components.']
  terminal_fee_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing context or special conditions for the fee schedule.']
  terminal_fee_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Operational status indicating whether the fee schedule is currently in effect.']
  transaction_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount charged for each processed transaction under this schedule.']
  transaction_fee_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction fee is a fixed amount per transaction or a percentage of the transaction value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the fee schedule record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the fee structure applicable to a terminal or terminal group, including monthly terminal rental fee, transaction-based fees, PCI compliance fee, chargeback handling fee, and any terminal-specific surcharge configurations. Links to the merchant agreement and product pricing catalog. Enables billing of terminal-related charges to merchants as part of the acquiring relationship.'
}

Table terminal.acceptance_type {
  acceptance_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each terminal acceptance type. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a reference lookup classification table, no minimum categories required.']
  acceptance_type_code varchar [note: 'Compact code used to identify the acceptance type in system integrations and reporting.']
  acceptance_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the acceptance type and its typical use cases.']
  acceptance_type_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the acceptance type (e.g., "Card‑Present Attended").']
  acceptance_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the acceptance type.']
  associated_fee_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Base interchange or service fee rate (as a decimal) associated with this acceptance type.']
  attended_indicator varchar [note: 'Specifies if the terminal is operated by a merchant employee (attended) or used by the consumer alone (unattended).']
  card_presence varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the card was physically present during the transaction.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of compliance standards applicable to this acceptance type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PCI_DSS|EMV|SCA|3DS|PSD2|FIPS] — promote to reference product']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the acceptance type record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for monetary limits.']
  default_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this acceptance type is the default for newly provisioned terminals.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the acceptance type becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the acceptance type is no longer valid (null if indefinite).']
  entry_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which card data is entered at the terminal.']
  firmware_version_required varchar [note: 'Minimum firmware version required for the terminal to support this acceptance type.']
  hce_supported boolean [note: 'True if HCE‑based mobile payments are permitted.']
  interchange_qualification varchar [note: 'Interchange eligibility status for the acceptance type.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last compliance audit performed on this acceptance type.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed transaction amount for this acceptance type.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the acceptance type.']
  regulatory_code varchar [note: 'Code linking the acceptance type to specific regulatory rules (e.g., PCI, PSD2).']
  requires_pin boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PIN entry is mandatory for this acceptance type.']
  risk_profile varchar [note: 'Risk classification used for fraud scoring and interchange qualification.']
  security_cert_level varchar [note: 'PCI PTS certification level required for terminals using this acceptance type.']
  software_version_required varchar [note: 'Minimum software version required for the terminal to support this acceptance type.']
  supports_contactless boolean [note: 'True if contactless transactions are permitted under this acceptance type.']
  supports_emv boolean [note: 'True if EMV chip transactions are allowed.']
  supports_nfc boolean [note: 'True if NFC (near‑field communication) is enabled for this acceptance type.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tokenization of card data is allowed.']
  transaction_environment varchar [note: 'Broad environment where the transaction originates, aligning with interchange rules.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the acceptance type record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management of the acceptance type definition.']

  Note: 'Reference entity classifying the payment acceptance environment and entry mode for a terminal, including card-present vs card-not-present, attended vs unattended, POS entry mode codes (chip, contactless, swipe, manual key entry, fallback), and the associated risk and interchange qualification implications. Used to correctly classify transactions for interchange optimization and fraud risk assessment.'
}

Table terminal.p2pe_scope {
  p2pe_scope_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each P2PE scope record.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that owns or operates the terminal.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal to which this P2PE scope applies.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the P2PE certificate.']
  compliance_reduction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal\'s validated P2PE scope reduces the merchant\'s PCI DSS scope (true = reduction).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the P2PE scope record was initially created.']
  decryption_environment varchar [note: 'Environment where encrypted transaction data is decrypted (HSM, secure module, or cloud service).']
  encryption_domain varchar [note: 'Scope of encryption implementation for the terminal (hardware, software, or cloud based).']
  encryption_key_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the encryption key used by the terminal.']
  hsm_location varchar [note: 'Physical or logical location of the Hardware Security Module that stores decryption keys.']
  key_management_approach varchar [note: 'Method used to manage encryption keys for the P2PE solution.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes regarding the P2PE scope, exceptions, or special handling.']
  p2pe_certificate_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the P2PE compliance certificate assigned to the terminal.']
  p2pe_solution_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the specific P2PE solution offering.']
  p2pe_solution_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor that supplies the validated Point‑to‑Point Encryption solution.']
  p2pe_solution_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the P2PE solution used for the terminal.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score assigned to the terminal based on P2PE compliance and security posture.']
  scope_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current P2PE scope validation expires, if applicable.']
  scope_last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the terminal\'s P2PE scope.']
  scope_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the P2PE scope for the terminal.']
  scope_validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the terminal\'s P2PE scope was validated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the P2PE scope record.']
  validation_audit_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking to the audit report that validated the P2PE scope.']
  validation_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the validation audit was performed.']

  Note: 'Master record documenting the PCI P2PE (Point-to-Point Encryption) scope and configuration for a terminal, including the P2PE solution provider, solution listing reference, encryption domain, decryption environment, key management approach, and scope validation date. Tracks whether a terminal is within a validated P2PE solution, which directly impacts the merchant\'s PCI DSS scope reduction. Critical for compliance and merchant PCI program management.'
}

Table terminal.surcharge_config {
  surcharge_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the terminal surcharge configuration record.']

  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal to which this surcharge configuration applies.']
  applicable_card_scheme varchar [note: 'Card network(s) to which the surcharge configuration applies.']
  applicable_product_type varchar [note: 'Card product category (e.g., consumer, commercial) for which the surcharge is valid.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the configuration against PCI DSS, Visa, Mastercard, and regional regulations.']
  config_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the surcharge configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the surcharge configuration record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the surcharge configuration becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the surcharge configuration expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this configuration is the default for the terminal when multiple configs exist.']
  jurisdiction_state_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter state code defining the jurisdictional scope of the surcharge (U.S. only).']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper transaction amount threshold beyond which the surcharge is not applied.']
  merchant_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merchant has satisfied disclosure compliance for this surcharge configuration.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes for operational or compliance comments.']
  regulatory_disclosure_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory rules require the merchant to disclose the surcharge to the cardholder.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating (0‑100) reflecting regulatory or fraud risk associated with the surcharge configuration.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount applied when surcharge_mode is \'fixed_amount\'.']
  surcharge_application_rule varchar [note: 'Rule defining how the surcharge is aggregated across transactions.']
  surcharge_cap_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum total surcharge amount that can be applied to a single transaction.']
  surcharge_cap_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Maximum surcharge expressed as a percentage of the transaction amount.']
  surcharge_config_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the surcharge rules and purpose.']
  surcharge_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the surcharge configuration.']
  surcharge_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the surcharge is calculated as a percentage of the transaction amount or as a fixed monetary amount.']
  surcharge_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to the transaction amount when surcharge_mode is \'percentage\'.']
  surcharge_type varchar [note: 'Category of payment instrument for which the surcharge is defined.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the surcharge configuration.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management and audit.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record defining surcharging rules applied at a terminal, including surcharge type (credit card surcharge, convenience fee), surcharge rate or fixed amount, applicable card schemes and product types, regulatory jurisdiction constraints (e.g., state-level surcharge restrictions in the US), merchant disclosure compliance flag, and effective date range. Governs how surcharges are applied and disclosed at the point of sale.'
}

  line_item_separator varchar [note: 'Character or string used to separate individual line items.']
