  receipt_format varchar [note: 'Delivery format of the receipt – printed paper, digital only, or both.']
  receipt_template_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the template.']
  sms_template_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the SMS template used for digital receipt delivery.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the template in configuration and reporting.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the receipt layout template.']
  template_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the template – standard provided by the platform, custom built for a merchant, or partner‑specific.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the template.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the receipt template for change management.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the receipt layout and content template configured for a terminal, including header/footer text, merchant name and address display, itemized field inclusion (transaction amount, tip, total, card scheme logo, masked PAN, approval code, AID, TVR), language locale, paper width, and digital receipt delivery options (email, SMS). Supports regulatory receipt requirements and merchant branding.'
}

Table terminal.downtime {
  downtime_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each terminal downtime record.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the terminal resides.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal experiencing downtime.']
  reporter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Downtime Incident Report captures the employee who reported the outage, supporting root‑cause analysis and SLA reporting.']
  actual_affected_transactions varchar [note: 'Measured number of transactions missed or failed due to the downtime.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the downtime record was first created.']
  downtime_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the downtime cause.']
  downtime_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the downtime detection (automated system, manual report, or partner).']
  downtime_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the downtime record.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the terminal downtime ended or was resolved.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of escalation triggered for the downtime incident.']
  estimated_affected_transactions varchar [note: 'Projected number of transactions that could not be processed during the downtime.']
  external_incident_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the incident in an external ticketing or monitoring system.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Firmware version installed on the terminal at the time of downtime.']
  is_critical_terminal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal is classified as critical for business operations.']
  network_connectivity_type varchar [note: 'Primary network connection type used by the terminal.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by the support team.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reason for downtime.']
  report_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time when the downtime was reported into the system.']
  reported_via varchar [note: 'Channel through which the downtime was reported.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve the downtime incident.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current resolution state of the downtime incident.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time when the downtime was marked as resolved.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk score assigned to the downtime event based on impact and frequency.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying cause of the downtime.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the root cause.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Impact severity of the downtime on operations.']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual downtime duration in minutes measured against SLA target.']
  sla_compliance varchar [note: 'Whether the downtime met or breached the SLA target.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed downtime duration in minutes as per SLA.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Software version (including OS) running on the terminal during the incident.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the terminal downtime began.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Internal support ticket number associated with the downtime.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the downtime record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing planned or unplanned terminal downtime periods, including downtime start and end timestamps, downtime category (hardware fault, software failure, connectivity loss, planned maintenance, key expiry), impact severity, affected transaction count estimate, resolution action taken, and root cause classification. Supports SLA reporting, uptime calculation, and operational incident management for the terminal fleet.'
}

Table terminal.digital_wallet {
  digital_wallet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each digital wallet record.']
  account_holder_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the person or entity that owns the wallet.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the person or entity that owns the wallet.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Required for regulatory reporting and settlement; mapping wallet currency to reference.currency enables currency conversion rates and compliance.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to partner.ecosystem_partner. Business justification: Needed to attribute each digital wallet to its issuing partner for regulatory compliance, KYC/AML oversight, and partner‑level usage analytics.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Payroll Disbursement: Salary is credited to an employee\'s digital wallet, requiring a direct link from wallet.digital_wallet.employee_id to workforce.employee.employee_id for reconciliation reports.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Regulatory reporting mandates mapping each digital wallet to its owning legal entity for AML/KYC and financial statements.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchant.merchant. Business justification: Required for linking each merchant to its own digital wallet for payout processing, settlement reporting, and KYC compliance; merchants own a wallet that holds funds.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.interchange_program. Business justification: Digital wallets enroll in specific interchange programs that define fee structures; enrollment must be tracked for compliance and revenue recognition.']
  balance_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wallet is eligible to hold a stored‑value balance.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the wallet record was first created in the system.']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for transactions within a 24‑hour period.']
  device_provisioning_status varchar [note: 'Status of the device provisioning process for the wallet.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the wallet ceased to be effective (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the wallet became effective for use.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wallet has been flagged for suspected fraud.']
  fraud_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the reason for a fraud flag (e.g., suspicious_activity, stolen_device).']
  is_3ds_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wallet is enrolled in 3‑Domain Secure authentication.']
  is_contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wallet supports contactless payments.']
  is_hce_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wallet uses Host Card Emulation for tokenized payments.']
  is_nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Near‑Field Communication is enabled for the wallet.']
  is_sca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with SCA requirements for the wallet.']
  issuer_bin varchar [note: 'First six digits of the issuing bank\'s number range that issued the underlying payment instrument.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent successful transaction or interaction with the wallet.']
  last_tokenization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the wallet was most recently tokenized or re‑tokenized.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when any attribute of the wallet record was last modified.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum single transaction amount allowed for this wallet.']
  monthly_transaction_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary limit for transactions within a calendar month.']
  pci_dss_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wallet meets PCI DSS security standards.']
  provisioning_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the wallet was provisioned (e.g., mobile app, web portal, partner integration, API).']
  psd2_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wallet complies with EU PSD2 regulatory requirements.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating the primary regulatory jurisdiction for the wallet.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the wallet based on fraud and compliance models.']
  stored_value_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current monetary balance held in the wallet for stored‑value transactions.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party provider that issues tokens for the wallet.']
  tokenization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of tokenization for the wallet.']
  wallet_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the wallet instance.']
  wallet_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the wallet, often shown to the cardholder in UI.']
  wallet_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the wallet instance, used in communications with partners and regulators.']
  wallet_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network scheme associated with the wallet (e.g., Visa, Mastercard).']
  wallet_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the wallet instance.']
  wallet_tier varchar [note: 'Business tier of the wallet, reflecting service level and fee structure.']
  wallet_type varchar [note: 'Category of wallet based on the provider or technology (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay, proprietary).']
  wallet_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the wallet software/firmware configuration.']

  Note: 'Master record for a consumer or business digital wallet instance — the primary entity in the wallet domain. Captures wallet type (Apple Pay, Google Pay, proprietary), wallet status (active, suspended, closed), wallet scheme, issuer BIN, cardholder reference, provisioning channel, wallet tier, currency, balance eligibility flag, creation timestamp, last activity timestamp, and regulatory jurisdiction. SSOT for wallet identity and lifecycle.'
}

Table terminal.wallet_token {
  wallet_token_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the wallet token record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the issuing bank or entity for the underlying FPAN.']
  partner_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the issuing bank or entity for the underlying FPAN.']
  token_requestor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay) that requested the token.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device to which the token is bound (if applicable).']
  assurance_level varchar [note: 'Risk‑based confidence level assigned to the token (e.g., low, medium, high).']
  compliance_check_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the outcome or reason of the latest compliance check.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of the most recent compliance validation (e.g., PCI, PSD2) for the token.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the token record was first created in the system.']
  fpan_bin varchar [note: 'First six digits of the underlying Financial PAN identifying the issuing bank and card scheme.']
  fpan_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the underlying Financial PAN for reference and reconciliation.']
  is_restricted_token boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the token is subject to additional regulatory or contractual restrictions.']
  is_test_token boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the token is used for testing purposes only.']
  last_used_location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the token was last used.']
  provisioning_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the token was provisioned onto the device or cloud environment.']
  token varchar [note: 'Masked Device Primary Account Number (DPAN) representing the tokenized payment credential stored in the digital wallet.']
  token_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the token is scheduled to expire and become invalid.']
  token_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the token was initially provisioned.']
  token_last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent transaction that utilized this token.']
  token_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network scheme associated with the token.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Organization that issued and manages the token.']
  token_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the token.']
  token_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the token based on its provisioning model: device‑bound, cloud‑hosted, or ecommerce.']
  token_usage_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of successful transactions performed with this token.']
  token_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the token record, incremented on each re‑issuance or update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the token record.']

  Note: 'TSP-issued payment token (DPAN) record representing a tokenized payment credential stored in a digital wallet. Captures DPAN value (masked), FPAN reference (masked), token requestor ID, token requestor name (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay), token type (device, cloud HCE, ecommerce), token status (active, suspended, deleted), token expiry, assurance level, last four of FPAN, BIN, issuer ID, device ID reference, provisioning timestamp, and last used timestamp. Core to the tokenization platform.'
}

Table terminal.token_requestor {
  token_requestor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the token requestor.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the token service provider that issues DPANs for this requestor.']
  partner_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the token service provider that issues DPANs for this requestor.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the token requestor\'s physical address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the token requestor\'s physical address, if applicable.']
  allowed_token_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of token types (e.g., DPAN, TPAN) the requestor is authorized to request.']
  api_endpoint varchar [note: 'Base URL of the requestor\'s API used for token request calls.']
  certificate_fingerprint varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 fingerprint of the TLS certificate presented by the requestor.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the token requestor\'s address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the requestor with applicable regulations and standards.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for communications with the token requestor.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the token requestor.']
  contractual_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the legal agreement governing token requestor activities.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the token requestor\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the token requestor record was first created in the system.']
  daily_token_request_count varchar [note: 'Running count of token requests submitted by the requestor for the current day.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the requestor\'s token request data must be retained per regulatory policy.']
  is_test_requestor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requestor is a test/sandbox entity used for development.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the requestor\'s operational status.']
  max_token_requests_per_day varchar [note: 'Upper limit on the number of token requests the requestor may submit in a single day.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date the requestor was approved and added to the token requestor registry.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the token requestor\'s address.']
  privacy_consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requestor has provided required privacy consent for data processing.']
  requestor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requestor based on its business role.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the requestor based on historical behavior and risk models.']
  scheme_affiliation varchar [note: 'Payment scheme with which the requestor is affiliated for token issuance.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the token requestor\'s address.']
  token_requestor_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the token requestor organization or entity.']
  token_requestor_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the token requestor within the tokenization ecosystem.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the token requestor record.']

  Note: 'Master registry of authorized token requestors (TRs) permitted to request DPANs from the TSP token vault. Captures token requestor ID (EMVCo-assigned), requestor name, requestor type (wallet provider, merchant, issuer), scheme affiliation (Visa VTS, Mastercard MDES), API endpoint, certificate fingerprint, onboarding date, status, and contractual agreement reference. Governs which entities can initiate tokenization requests.'
}

Table terminal.wallet_device {
  wallet_device_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the wallet device record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet to which this device is bound.']
  binding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the device was first linked to the wallet.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of compliance checks passed/failed (e.g., pci_dss|psd2).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the device record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the device was retired or removed from service.']
  device_fingerprint_hash varchar [note: 'Hash of the device fingerprint used for fraud risk analytics.']
  device_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique device identifier linked to the DPAN; used for tokenization and device‑level risk scoring.']
  device_owner_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the device is owned by an individual consumer or a business entity.']
  device_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number for the device.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of the consumer device (e.g., mobile phone, wearable, tablet, IoT).']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the device firmware at the time of last update.']
  geolocation_city varchar [note: 'City name associated with the device\'s last known location.']
  geolocation_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where the device was last observed.']
  hce_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the device can perform Host Card Emulation for tokenized payments.']
  imei varchar [note: 'Unique hardware identifier for mobile devices.']
  is_test_device boolean [note: 'True if the device is used in testing environments and not for production transactions.']
  last_seen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent activity or heartbeat received from the device.']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Network interface MAC address of the device.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model designation of the device (e.g., iPhone 13, Galaxy S22).']
  nfc_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the device supports Near Field Communication for tap‑to‑pay.']
  os_name varchar [note: 'Name of the operating system running on the device (e.g., Android, iOS).']
  os_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the operating system (e.g., 14.4, 12).']
  provisioning_status varchar [note: 'Current status of secure element provisioning for the device.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) derived from device behavior and fraud models.']
  secure_element_type varchar [note: 'Type of secure element used for cryptographic operations (embedded SE, Host Card Emulation, SIM‑based).']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status (e.g., reason for suspension).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the device record was last modified.']
  wallet_device_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the device within the wallet ecosystem.']

  Note: 'Consumer payment device registered to a digital wallet — mobile phone, wearable, tablet, or IoT device. Captures device ID (DPAN-linked), device type, device OS and version, device model, NFC capability flag, secure element type (embedded SE, HCE, SIM-based), device fingerprint hash, device status (active, suspended, lost, stolen), last seen timestamp, geolocation country, and device binding timestamp. Supports device lifecycle management and fraud risk signals.'
}

Table terminal.provisioning_request {
  provisioning_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the provisioning request event.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the digital wallet receiving the tokenized credential.']
  token_requestor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Token Service Provider (TSP) that initiated the tokenization request.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device (e.g., mobile, POS) from which the provisioning request originated.']
  compliance_check_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific compliance rule or policy evaluated for the request.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request satisfied all applicable AML/KYC/PCI compliance checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the provisioning request record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for any monetary amounts associated with the request.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the issuer rendered its decision for the provisioning request.']
  decline_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why the issuer declined the provisioning request.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Hash or token representing the unique characteristics of the device used for provisioning.']
  external_request_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier supplied by the originating system or partner for cross‑system traceability.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary fee charged by the token service provider for processing the provisioning request.']
  fpan_masked varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the Primary Account Number (e.g., 1234******5678) used for audit and display while complying with PCI DSS.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the provisioning request was initiated.']
  is_test_request boolean [note: 'True if the provisioning request was generated in a test or sandbox environment.']
  issuer_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision returned by the issuing bank: approved, declined, or step‑up required.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the requester\'s location.']
  provisioning_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the provisioning request was submitted: over‑the‑air (OTA), in‑app, or manual entry.']
  provisioning_method varchar [note: 'Operational path used for provisioning: green (standard), yellow (conditional), or red (exception) flow.']
  provisioning_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the provisioning request.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the provisioning request was initially submitted.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating assigned to the request by the fraud detection engine.']
  sca_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the SCA check required for the provisioning request.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the provisioning request record.']
  step_up_method varchar [note: 'Method used to satisfy a step‑up requirement: one‑time password, biometric, or call‑center verification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the provisioning request record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each tokenization provisioning request submitted to the TSP — the event of adding a payment credential to a digital wallet. Captures request ID, wallet ID, device ID, FPAN reference (masked), token requestor ID, provisioning channel (OTA, in-app, manual), provisioning method (green path, yellow path, red path), issuer decision (approved, declined, step-up required), step-up method (OTP, biometric, call center), decision timestamp, decline reason code, and SCA outcome. Critical audit trail for PCI DSS and EMVCo compliance.'
}

Table terminal.wallet_transaction {
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the wallet transaction record.']
  authorization_response_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.authorization_response_code. Business justification: Authorization response codes map to reference.authorization_response_code for dispute handling and reporting.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Card scheme identification links to reference.reference_card_scheme for scheme-specific processing and compliance.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Dispute handling and compliance reporting need the cardholder profile associated with each wallet transaction.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Transaction currency reporting and FX calculations rely on reference.currency to fetch exchange rates and compliance data.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet used for the transaction.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Corporate Transaction Audit: Internal audit needs to know which employee authorized or performed a wallet transaction, linking wallet.wallet_transaction.employee_id to workforce.employee.employee_id.']
  irf_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to interchange.irf_table. Business justification: Required for interchange fee reporting: each wallet transaction must reference the IRF rate record used to calculate fees for settlement and audit.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.journal_entry. Business justification: Audit and reconciliation require each wallet transaction to be linked to the corresponding GL journal entry that records the financial impact.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: Transaction MCC classification uses reference.mcc for risk scoring and reporting.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant where the transaction occurred.']
  network_routing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_routing_rule. Business justification: REQUIRED: Compliance checks need the specific network routing rule that governed the transaction for traceability.']
  original_transaction_wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original transaction for reversals or adjustments.']
  payment_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.payment_corridor. Business justification: Transaction routing and fee logic depend on the payment corridor definition; linking each transaction to its corridor enables accurate fee schedules and regulatory checks.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Transaction processing uses a payment product; needed for product‑level revenue, risk and regulatory reporting.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Required for settlement audit: each transaction must reference the exact FX rate record used for currency conversion, enabling regulatory reporting and dispute resolution.']
  routing_table_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.routing_table. Business justification: REQUIRED: Transaction routing audit report must capture which routing table rule was applied to each transaction.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to settlement.settlement. Business justification: Required for regulatory reconciliation reports that map each wallet transaction to the settlement record that cleared it, enabling audit of funds flow.']
  token_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the token representing the payment instrument used.']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Transaction type mapping to reference.transaction_type enables processing rules and analytics.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device (e.g., mobile, POS terminal) that initiated the transaction.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the transaction in the transaction currency.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Code assigned by the issuer for the authorized transaction.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the transaction was processed.']
  compliance_check_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the result of compliance checks.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the transaction passed compliance screening (AML, sanctions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the system.']
  cryptogram varchar [note: 'Masked cryptographic value used for transaction authentication.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any dispute related to the transaction.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if currency conversion occurred.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the platform for processing the transaction.']
  fraud_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the reason for fraud flag.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device initiating the transaction.']
  is_fraud_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was flagged for potential fraud.']
  is_successful boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was successful.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City of the transaction origin.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the transaction origin.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after fees, in the transaction currency.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the transaction is a reversal.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned to the transaction.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transaction was settled.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number for the transaction as provided by the payment network.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transaction was initiated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']
  wallet_transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the transaction.']

  Note: 'Wallet-layer transaction record capturing each payment event initiated from a digital wallet — NFC tap-to-pay, QR code payment, in-app purchase, or P2P transfer. Captures wallet transaction ID, wallet ID, token ID, device ID, transaction amount, currency, merchant reference, MCC, transaction type (NFC, QR, in-app, P2P), cryptogram value (masked), transaction timestamp, authorization response code, and wallet scheme. Complements the transaction domain\'s authorization record with wallet-specific context.'
}

Table terminal.hce_credential {
  hce_credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the HCE credential record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet to which this credential belongs.']
  token_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the token associated with this HCE credential.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit trail record for this credential.']
  compliance_pci_dss_version varchar [note: 'Version of the PCI DSS standard applicable to this credential.']
  compliance_psd2_version varchar [note: 'Version of the PSD2 regulatory framework applicable.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the credential.']
  contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if contactless payments are permitted with this credential.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credential record was created.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Type of HCE credential, indicating its usage model.']
  credential_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the credential, incremented on each rotation.']
  cross_border_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the credential can be used for cross‑border payments.']
  device_binding_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device bound to this credential.']
  dynamic_currency_conversion_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates support for Dynamic Currency Conversion during transactions.']
  fraud_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the reason for fraud flagging.']
  hce_credential_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the HCE credential.']
  hce_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if Host Card Emulation is enabled for this credential.']
  is_fraud_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential has been flagged for fraud.']
  is_test_credential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credential is used for testing purposes only.']
  issuer_script_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the issuer script used during credential provisioning.']
  key_algorithm varchar [note: 'Cryptographic algorithm of the HCE key.']
  key_expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the key becomes invalid.']
  key_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cryptographic key expires.']
  key_index varchar [note: 'Index of the cryptographic key within the HCE key set.']
  key_length varchar [note: 'Length of the cryptographic key in bits.']
  key_rotation_interval_days varchar [note: 'Configured interval in days after which the key must be rotated.']
  key_rotation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the key was last rotated.']
  key_usage_counter varchar [note: 'Number of times the key has been used for a transaction.']
  key_usage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed uses for the key before it must be replenished or rotated.']
  last_replenishment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent key replenishment event.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction that used this credential.']
  nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if NFC communication is supported for this credential.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the credential.']
  provisioning_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the credential was provisioned.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the region where the credential is issued.']
  replenishment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the key has been replenished.']
  replenishment_threshold varchar [note: 'Number of remaining uses that triggers automatic key replenishment.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score assigned to the credential by the fraud detection engine.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tokenization is supported for this credential.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credential record.']
  usage_reset_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the usage counters were last reset.']

  Note: 'Host Card Emulation credential record for software-based NFC payments without a physical secure element. Captures HCE credential ID, wallet ID, token ID, credential type (limited-use key, session key), key index, key expiry, replenishment threshold, replenishment count, last replenishment timestamp, credential status, and issuer script reference. Manages the limited-use cryptographic key lifecycle for HCE-based contactless payments.'
}

Table terminal.upi_registration {
  upi_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the UPI registration record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wallet to which the UPI registration belongs.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PSP that processes the UPI transactions.']
  partner_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PSP that processes the UPI transactions.']
  aml_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of AML screening for the registration.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance verification for the registration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this registration record was first captured.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the VPA\'s settlement currency.']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate transaction amount allowed per day for this VPA.']
  default_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this VPA is the default address for the wallet.']
  deregistration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the VPA was deregistered or deactivated.']
  device_binding_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device bound to the VPA for authentication.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the VPA registration becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the VPA registration remains effective (null for open-ended).']
  failed_auth_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive failed authentication attempts.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Result of the KYC verification for the linked account holder.']
  last_successful_auth_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful authentication for the VPA.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this registration record.']
  last_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful verification of the VPA.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the VPA registration.']
  linked_bank_account_masked varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the bank account linked to the VPA.']
  max_failed_attempts varchar [note: 'Configured limit for failed authentication attempts before lockout.']
  monthly_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate transaction amount allowed per month for this VPA.']
  nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether NFC-based payments are enabled for this VPA.']
  privacy_consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the user has consented to privacy terms for UPI usage.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the NPCI for this registration.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the registration request, such as self-service or customer support.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the UPI registration lifecycle.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the VPA registration was created.']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'Specifies if the VPA is the primary or a secondary address for the user.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk score assigned to the VPA registration based on fraud and compliance signals.']
  sanction_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening for the registration.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the VPA registration was initiated.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for the current registration status.']
  tokenization_status varchar [note: 'Whether the VPA is associated with a tokenized payment instrument.']
  upi_handle varchar [note: 'PSP-specific suffix appended to the VPA (e.g., @bank).']
  upi_pin_set boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a PIN has been configured for the VPA.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the verification process for the VPA.']
  vpa varchar [note: 'The UPI ID assigned to the user for receiving payments.']
  vpa_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the VPA is for a personal or business account.']

  Note: 'UPI (Unified Payments Interface) Virtual Payment Address (VPA) registration record linking a cardholder\'s bank account to a UPI ID within the wallet platform. Captures UPI registration ID, wallet ID, VPA (UPI ID), linked bank account reference (masked IBAN/account), NPCI registration status, UPI handle (PSP suffix), registration timestamp, last verified timestamp, default flag, and deregistration timestamp. SSOT for UPI identity within the wallet domain.'
}

Table terminal.wallet_funding_source {
  wallet_funding_source_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the wallet funding source record.']
  added_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Funding Source Management: Compliance requires tracking which employee added or verified a funding source, so wallet.wallet_funding_source.added_by_employee_id references workforce.employee.employee_i']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.cardholder_profile. Business justification: Funding source management reports need to associate each funding source with the cardholder profile that owns it for risk scoring and compliance.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Funding source currency mapping to reference.currency supports compliance and limit enforcement per currency.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet to which this funding source is linked.']
  linked_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to cardholder.linked_account. Business justification: Transaction routing and reconciliation require mapping a wallet funding source to the specific linked bank account of the cardholder.']
  payment_instrument_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment instrument (card, bank account, BNPL line) that backs the wallet.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: Top‑up (funding) transactions must reference the funding source record for audit and reconciliation.']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the most recent transaction that used this funding source.']
  available_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current available balance on the funding source, if applicable.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the funding source (PCI, PSD2, AML).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the wallet funding source record was first created.']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total value of transactions allowed per day for this funding source.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'When the funding source became effective for the wallet.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'When the funding source is scheduled to cease being effective (nullable for open‑ended).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the funding source expires or becomes invalid.']
  fraud_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the reason for a fraud flag.']
  funding_source_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status (e.g., reason for removal).']
  instrument_type varchar [note: 'Category of the funding instrument (e.g., debit card, credit card, ACH account, BNPL line).']
  is_3ds_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the funding source is enrolled in 3‑Domain Secure authentication.']
  is_contactless_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the funding source supports contactless transactions.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this funding source is the default for the wallet.']
  is_fraud_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the funding source has been flagged for fraud.']
  is_hce_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if Host Card Emulation is enabled for the funding source.']
  is_nfc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if NFC (Near Field Communication) is enabled for the funding source.']
  is_sca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication requirements.']
  issuer_bin varchar [note: 'Bank Identification Number of the issuing bank for the funding instrument.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the funding source was last used in a wallet transaction.']
  limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount that can be drawn from the funding source per defined period.']
  link_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the funding source was linked to the wallet.']
  masked_reference varchar [note: 'Masked representation (last four characters) of the instrument number (PAN or IBAN).']
  monthly_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum total value of transactions allowed per month for this funding source.']
  nickname varchar [note: 'User‑defined friendly name for the funding source.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score assigned to the funding source by fraud/risk models.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the funding source record.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the token service provider that issued the token for this funding source.']
  tokenization_status varchar [note: 'Whether the funding source has been tokenized for secure storage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the wallet funding source record was last modified.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the funding source (e.g., micro‑deposit, instant verification).']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the funding source verification process.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the verification status was last updated.']
  wallet_funding_source_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the funding source within the wallet.']

  Note: 'Funding source linked to a digital wallet — the payment instrument (card, bank account, BNPL line) that backs wallet transactions. Captures funding source ID, wallet ID, instrument type (debit card, credit card, ACH account, BNPL), masked instrument reference (last four / masked IBAN), issuer BIN, funding source status (active, expired, removed), default flag, verification status, verification method (micro-deposit, instant verification), and link timestamp. Manages the wallet-to-instrument binding.'
}

Table terminal.wallet_balance {
  wallet_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each wallet balance record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Balance currency must reference reference.currency for accurate reporting and multi-currency balance management.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the wallet to which this balance belongs.']
  available_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the total balance that is free for spend or transfer.']
  balance_category varchar [note: 'Business segment to which the balance applies.']
  balance_ceiling_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable balance for the wallet as defined by product rules or regulatory caps.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the balance record.']
  balance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stored‑value product (e.g., prepaid, e‑money, gift, loyalty).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the balance with regulatory rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the balance record was first created in the system.']
  e_money_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification of the e‑money balance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the balance became effective for the wallet.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the balance ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  external_balance_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number used by partner systems for this balance.']
  is_frozen boolean [note: 'True when the balance is temporarily frozen due to compliance or risk actions.']
  is_overdraft_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wallet permits overdraft usage.']
  last_credit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent credit transaction applied to the balance.']
  last_debit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent debit transaction applied to the balance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for operational comments or audit remarks.']
  overdraft_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Authorized amount that can be drawn beyond the available balance, if overdraft is permitted.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if this balance must be included in statutory reporting (e.g., AML, PSD2).']
  reserved_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount held for pending authorizations or holds.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the balance record (e.g., Digital Wallet Platform).']
  total_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of available and reserved balances representing the full stored value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent time the balance record was modified.']

  Note: 'Stored-value balance record for wallets that hold a prepaid or e-money balance (proprietary wallet schemes, prepaid wallets). Captures balance ID, wallet ID, currency, available balance, reserved balance, total balance, last credit timestamp, last debit timestamp, balance ceiling limit, regulatory e-money classification, and balance status. Distinct from funding sources — represents actual stored value held within the wallet platform.'
}

Table terminal.balance_movement {
  balance_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each balance movement record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet that owns the balance.']
  settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch that includes this movement.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch that includes this movement.']
  wallet_balance_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the stored-value balance to which this movement applies.']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'External identifier of the originating transaction (e.g., authorization or settlement ID).']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the movement in the transaction currency.']
  balance_movement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the movement record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the movement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for the movement amount.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the balance or movement expires, if applicable.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary fee deducted as part of the movement, if applicable.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the fee amount, using ISO 4217 three‑letter code.']
  initiating_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the movement was initiated (mobile app, web, point‑of‑sale, API).']
  is_test_movement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record was generated for testing purposes.']
  movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the movement occurred in the real world.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the balance change: credit, debit, reversal, or expiry.']
  post_movement_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance amount after the movement was applied.']
  pre_movement_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance amount before the movement was applied.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing why a reversal, expiry, or fee was applied.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that produced the movement record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the movement record.']

  Note: 'Transactional ledger record for every credit or debit movement against a wallet stored-value balance — top-ups, payments, refunds, fee deductions, and expiry write-offs. Captures movement ID, balance ID, wallet ID, movement type (credit, debit, reversal, expiry), amount, currency, reference transaction ID, initiating channel, pre-movement balance, post-movement balance, and movement timestamp. Provides the full audit trail for e-money regulatory reporting.'
}

Table terminal.nfc_tap_event {
  nfc_tap_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each NFC tap interaction record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet instance linked to the tap.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant (MID) associated with the transaction.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal (TID) where the tap occurred.']
  token_id varchar [note: 'Token representing the payment credential used in the tap.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical device that performed the NFC tap.']
  application_transaction_counter varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing counter from the card/emulated token used to detect replay attacks.']
  cryptogram_type varchar [note: 'Type of cryptographic value generated during the tap (e.g., Authorization Request Cryptogram).']
  cryptogram_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Masked representation of the cryptogram generated for the transaction, stored for audit and fraud analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction amount.']
  fraud_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the reason for fraud flagging (e.g., high velocity, mismatched CVM).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the terminal or gateway that recorded the tap.']
  is_fraud_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tap event was flagged as potentially fraudulent.']
  kernel_version varchar [note: 'Version of the EMV contactless kernel used for processing the tap.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City name of the terminal location for the tap event.']
  location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the tap was performed.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tap event record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tap event record.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk assessment assigned to the tap event by fraud detection models.']
  tap_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the tap attempt as reported by the terminal.']
  tap_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the NFC tap was captured.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount authorized for the tap transaction.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the transaction (e.g., purchase, refund).']

  Note: 'Granular NFC contactless tap interaction record capturing each physical tap-to-pay event at a POS terminal. Captures tap event ID, device ID, token ID, wallet ID, terminal TID, merchant MID, tap timestamp, cryptogram type (ARQC, TC), cryptogram value (masked), ATC (Application Transaction Counter), tap result (authorized, declined, fallback), contactless kernel version, and transaction amount. Provides device-level NFC telemetry for fraud detection and EMV compliance.'
}

Table terminal.wallet_sca_challenge {
  wallet_sca_challenge_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) challenge event.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet to which the SCA challenge is associated.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device used to receive or respond to the challenge.']
  challenge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SCA challenge.']
  challenge_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the SCA challenge was issued to the wallet holder.']
  challenge_type varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the SCA challenge (e.g., one‑time password via SMS, email, biometric verification, push notification, or call‑center).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the wallet holder received the challenge.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the challenge reached a terminal state (completed, failed, or expired).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the SCA challenge record was first created in the data lake.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Textual code or description explaining why the challenge failed or expired, if applicable.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client device at the time of challenge issuance.']
  is_test_challenge boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the challenge was generated in a test or sandbox environment.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code representing the geographic location of the device.']
  request_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier linking the challenge to the originating provisioning request or transaction (e.g., transaction ID, provisioning request ID).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk assessment (0‑100) generated by the fraud detection engine for this SCA challenge.']
  sca_exemption_applied varchar [note: 'Regulatory exemption that justified bypassing or simplifying the SCA requirement for this transaction.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System or process name that performed the latest update to the challenge record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the SCA challenge record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or application user‑agent string reported by the client device.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System or process name that created the challenge record.']

  Note: 'Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) challenge event record for wallet provisioning and high-value wallet transactions under PSD2. Captures challenge ID, wallet ID, provisioning request or transaction reference, challenge type (OTP SMS, OTP email, biometric, push notification, call center), challenge status (sent, completed, failed, expired), challenge timestamp, completion timestamp, failure reason, and SCA exemption applied (if any). Supports PSD2 SCA compliance audit trail.'
}

  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the enrollment record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the enrollment record.']

  Note: 'Cardholder wallet enrollment event — the business record of a cardholder registering and activating a digital wallet on the platform. Captures enrollment ID, wallet ID, cardholder reference, enrollment channel (mobile app, web, IVR, branch), enrollment timestamp, KYC verification status at enrollment, terms and conditions version accepted, consent flags (data sharing, marketing), activation timestamp, and enrollment source campaign reference. SSOT for wallet onboarding lifecycle.'
}

Table terminal.p2p_transfer {
  p2p_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the P2P transfer record.']
  primary_p2p_digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the wallet from which funds are debited.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: P2P transfers may involve currency conversion; storing the applied FX rate record is needed for fee calculation, compliance, and audit trails.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.transaction. Business justification: Regulatory reporting of peer‑to‑peer transfers requires linking each P2P record to its underlying transaction.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to initiate the transfer.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Originating channel through which the transfer was initiated.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the transfer reached a terminal state (completed, failed, or reversed).']
  compliance_check_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific compliance rule evaluated.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer satisfied AML/KYC and other regulatory checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transfer record was first persisted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the transfer amount.']
  external_transfer_reference varchar [note: 'External or partner-provided reference number for the transfer, if any.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why a transfer failed or was reversed.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary fee charged for processing the transfer.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the fee amount; typically matches transfer currency.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transfer was flagged as potentially fraudulent.']
  initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the transfer request was created.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client at the time of transfer initiation.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'True if the transfer is part of a recurring series.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the transfer was initiated.']
  recipient_identifier varchar [note: 'Value of the recipient identifier (e.g., phone number, email address, UPI VPA, or wallet ID).']
  recipient_identifier_type varchar [note: 'Method used to identify the recipient (phone number, email address, UPI VPA, or internal wallet ID).']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Pattern defining how a recurring transfer repeats.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk assessment (0‑100) generated by fraud detection engine.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned execution time for a scheduled transfer; null for instant transfers.']
  transfer_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Principal amount transferred between wallets, before fees.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transfer.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer is immediate, scheduled for a future date, or part of a recurring series.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the transfer record.']

  Note: 'Peer-to-peer (P2P) money transfer record between two wallet holders on the platform. Captures transfer ID, sender wallet ID, recipient wallet ID, transfer amount, currency, transfer type (instant, scheduled, recurring), transfer status (initiated, pending, completed, failed, reversed), initiation timestamp, completion timestamp, fee amount, fee currency, recipient identifier type (phone, email, UPI VPA, wallet ID), and failure reason code. Core transactional entity for P2P payment flows.'
}

Table terminal.wallet_limit {

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the limit record was first created.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the limit becomes active.']
  enforcement_mode varchar [note: 'Defines how strictly the limit is enforced.']

  limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the limit expressed in the specified currency.']

  limit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the limit (e.g., "Daily Spend Cap").']
  limit_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies which transaction channels the limit applies to.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the limit governing spending or transaction behavior.']
  limit_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the limit configuration for change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional information or business comments.']

  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory or scheme rule that mandates or influences this limit.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating (0‑100) associated with the limit configuration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the limit record.']
  wallet_limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the limit configuration.']

  Note: 'Operational spending and velocity limit configuration applied to a digital wallet — daily spend caps, per-transaction limits, NFC contactless limits, P2P transfer limits, and top-up limits. Captures limit ID, wallet ID, limit type (daily spend, per-transaction, contactless, P2P, top-up), limit amount, currency, limit scope (all channels, NFC only, online only), effective date, expiry date, override flag, and regulatory basis (PSD2 contactless limit, scheme rule). Governs wallet risk controls.'
}

Table terminal.token_pan_mapping {
  token_pan_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each DPAN to FPAN mapping record.']
  partner_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the card‑issuing institution (e.g., IIN or internal issuer code).']
  token_requestor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity (merchant, PSP, or partner) that requested the tokenization.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the immutable audit‑log entry that captures the full change history for this mapping.']
  bin varchar [note: 'First six digits of the card number identifying the issuing bank and card brand.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of compliance indicators applicable to the mapping (e.g., pci_dss, psd2).']
  dpan_masked varchar [note: 'Masked tokenized primary account number used in digital wallet transactions; only the first six and last four digits are visible.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the mapping becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the mapping expires; null if indefinite.']
  fpan_reference varchar [note: 'Secure reference (e.g., vault pointer) to the underlying real PAN stored in the token vault.']
  is_restricted_mapping boolean [note: 'True if the mapping is subject to additional security controls (e.g., high‑value cards).']
  is_test_mapping boolean [note: 'True if the mapping is created for testing or sandbox environments.']
  last_detokenization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent detokenization (FPAN retrieval) event.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the mapping record.']
  mapping_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the mapping record was initially created.']
  mapping_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the DPAN‑FPAN mapping.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk rating (0‑99.99) associated with this token mapping.']
  tokenization_algorithm varchar [note: 'Name of the cryptographic algorithm applied during token generation.']
  tokenization_compliance varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance standards satisfied by the tokenization process.']
  tokenization_key_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cryptographic key used for token creation.']
  tokenization_key_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the tokenization key.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to generate the token value.']
  update_reason varchar [note: 'Code describing why the mapping record was last updated (e.g., status_change, compliance_review).']

  Note: 'Secure DPAN-to-FPAN mapping record maintained in the TSP token vault — the authoritative link between a tokenized DPAN and the underlying FPAN. Captures mapping ID, DPAN (masked), FPAN (masked/vaulted reference), BIN, issuer ID, token requestor ID, mapping status (active, suspended, deleted), mapping creation timestamp, and last detokenization timestamp. Critical for transaction authorization, dispute resolution, and fraud investigation requiring FPAN recovery.'
}

Table terminal.detokenization_request {
  detokenization_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each detokenization request.']
  token_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the token (DPAN) that is being detokenized.']
  token_requestor_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the party (issuer, fraud platform, or dispute system) that initiated the detokenization request.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device that initiated the detokenization request.']
  authorization_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the authorization check for the detokenization request.']
  compliance_check_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific compliance rule or policy evaluated for the request.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request passed all regulatory compliance checks (e.g., AML, PCI).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the detokenization request record was first created in the system.']
  detokenization_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the detokenization request.']
  dpan_masked varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the DPAN (e.g., first 6 and last 4 digits visible).']
  fpan_masked varchar [note: 'Masked representation of the FPAN when it is not fully returned, complying with PCI DSS masking rules.']
  fpan_returned_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the full Primary Account Number (FPAN) was returned (true) or not (false).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the detokenization request originated.']
  location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the request origin.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information related to the request.']
  request_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the detokenization request.']
  request_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number used by business processes to track the request.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the detokenization request was received.']
  requestor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requesting party.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the detokenization response (FPAN) was returned.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk assessment score assigned to the request by the fraud detection platform.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system (e.g., Payment Gateway, Fraud Platform) that generated the request.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the detokenization request record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each DPAN-to-FPAN detokenization request made by authorized parties (issuers, fraud teams, dispute investigators). Captures request ID, token ID, DPAN reference (masked), requesting party ID, requesting party type (issuer, fraud platform, dispute system), request reason (authorization, fraud investigation, dispute), authorization outcome, FPAN returned flag, request timestamp, and response timestamp. Provides PCI DSS-compliant audit trail for all detokenization events.'
}

Table terminal.wallet_notification {
  wallet_notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each wallet notification record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet that receives the notification.']
  wallet_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device (mobile, wearables, etc.) through which the notification is delivered.']
  correlation_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used to correlate this notification with related processing events or transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the notification record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Medium used to deliver the notification to the wallet holder.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the notification delivery lifecycle.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time when the notification was successfully delivered to the end‑device.']
  is_test_notification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the notification was generated in a test or sandbox environment.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑2 three‑letter code representing the language of the notification.']
  message_content varchar [note: 'Rendered notification message sent to the user (may contain placeholders resolved at send time).']
  message_template_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the template used to render the notification content.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the notification event was generated by the system.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Category of the notification indicating its business purpose.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority indicating urgency of the notification.']
  read_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recipient opened or read the notification (if applicable).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the notification (e.g., payment_gateway, fraud_engine).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the notification record (e.g., status change).']

  Note: 'Push notification and alert record sent to wallet holders for transaction confirmations, provisioning events, security alerts, and balance updates. Captures notification ID, wallet ID, device ID, notification type (transaction alert, provisioning confirmation, security alert, balance low, limit breach), delivery channel (push, SMS, email, in-app), delivery status (sent, delivered, failed, read), notification timestamp, delivery timestamp, and message template reference. Supports cardholder communication audit trail.'
}

Table terminal.wallet_consent {
  wallet_consent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the consent record.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet to which this consent applies.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Consent must be tracked against reference.regulatory_jurisdiction for GDPR/PSD2 compliance reporting.']
  biometric_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder consented to use biometric authentication.']
  consent_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the consent was obtained.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consent.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of consent granted by the cardholder (e.g., data processing, marketing, third‑party sharing, open banking, biometric).']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent text or policy that was accepted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent record was first created in the system.']
  data_processing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder consented to processing personal data.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the consent automatically expires, if a time‑bound consent.']
  grant_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the consent was granted by the cardholder.']
  marketing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder consented to receive marketing communications.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the consent record.']
  open_banking_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder consented to open‑banking data sharing under PSD2.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the consent data.']
  third_party_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder consented to share data with third‑party service providers.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that last updated the consent record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consent record.']
  withdrawal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the consent was withdrawn, if applicable.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that created the consent record.']

  Note: 'Cardholder consent and data-sharing preference record for a digital wallet — GDPR, CCPA, and PSD2 open banking consent management. Captures consent ID, wallet ID, consent type (data processing, marketing, third-party data sharing, open banking, biometric), consent status (granted, withdrawn, expired), consent version, consent channel, grant timestamp, withdrawal timestamp, and regulatory jurisdiction. SSOT for wallet-level privacy and consent compliance.'
}

Table terminal.loyalty_account {
  loyalty_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each loyalty account.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet to which this loyalty account is attached.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the account with AML/KYC and loyalty program rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty account record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code representing the currency used for point valuation, if applicable.']
  is_test_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is a test or sandbox record used for development or QA.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent activity (earn, redeem, tier change) on the loyalty account.']
  loyalty_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the loyalty account.']
  points_balance varchar [note: 'Current net balance of loyalty points available for redemption.']
  points_balance_last_update timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the points balance.']
  points_earned_last_12_months varchar [note: 'Cumulative points earned over the past 12 calendar months.']
  points_earned_last_30_days varchar [note: 'Total points earned in the most recent 30‑day window.']
  points_earned_total varchar [note: 'Cumulative total of points ever earned by the account (including redeemed and expired).']
  points_expiration_date date [note: 'Specific calendar date when the current points balance will expire (if fixed policy).']
  points_expiry_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how points expire – rolling (based on activity), fixed date, or none.']
