  points_pending varchar [note: 'Points that have been earned but are not yet available for redemption (e.g., pending settlement).']
  points_redeemed_last_12_months varchar [note: 'Cumulative points redeemed over the past 12 calendar months.']
  points_redeemed_last_30_days varchar [note: 'Total points redeemed in the most recent 30‑day window.']
  points_redeemed_total varchar [note: 'Cumulative total of points that have been redeemed from the account.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the loyalty or rewards program.']
  program_sponsor varchar [note: 'Entity (e.g., bank, merchant, brand) that sponsors the loyalty program.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Category of the loyalty program – points, airline miles, cash‑back, or voucher based.']
  reward_redemption_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account currently meets all criteria to redeem rewards.']
  reward_redemption_eligibility_reason varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation why the account is or is not eligible for redemption.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk rating (0‑100) derived from fraud and compliance models for this account.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the loyalty account record.']
  tier_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current tier status expires if not renewed.']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Current tier of the account holder (e.g., Standard, Silver, Gold, Platinum).']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the tier, may include marketing label.']
  tier_points_threshold varchar [note: 'Number of points required to attain or maintain the current tier.']
  tier_qualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the account qualified for its current tier.']
  tier_qualification_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the account qualified for its current tier.']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current qualification status of the tier (e.g., qualified, pending renewal, unqualified).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the loyalty account record.']

  Note: 'Loyalty and rewards account linked to a digital wallet — points balances, tier status, and reward redemption eligibility for wallet-integrated loyalty programs. Captures loyalty account ID, wallet ID, program name, program sponsor, points balance, tier level (standard, silver, gold, platinum), tier qualification date, tier expiry date, lifetime points earned, points expiry policy, and last activity timestamp. Supports wallet-embedded loyalty and rewards schemes.'
}

Table terminal.loyalty_transaction {
  loyalty_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty transaction record.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to initiate the loyalty transaction.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital wallet that holds the loyalty account.']
  loyalty_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty account associated with this transaction.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant where the loyalty activity originated.']
  risk_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program rule applied to calculate points.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement record linked to this loyalty transaction.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch in which this transaction was settled.']
  wallet_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment or activity that triggered the loyalty transaction.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the loyalty activity was captured.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance outcome for the transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty transaction record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary equivalent.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which earned points are scheduled to expire, if applicable.']
  fraud_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing the reason for fraud flagging, if applicable.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the transaction was initiated.']
  is_fraud_flagged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was flagged for potential fraud.']
  is_test_transaction boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the transaction was generated for testing purposes.']
  location_city varchar [note: 'City name of the transaction\'s geographic origin.']
  location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the transaction originated.']
  monetary_equivalent decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the points transacted, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the transaction.']
  points_amount varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty points earned (positive) or redeemed/adjusted (negative) in this transaction.']
  points_balance_after varchar [note: 'Loyalty points balance of the account after this transaction is applied.']
  points_balance_before varchar [note: 'Loyalty points balance of the account prior to this transaction.']
  program_rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the rule that governed point accrual or redemption.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score assigned to the transaction by fraud detection models.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the loyalty transaction (e.g., wallet, gateway).']
  transaction_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee charged for processing the loyalty transaction, if any.']
  transaction_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the transaction fee, expressed as ISO 4217 code.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from the source system for this loyalty transaction.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the loyalty transaction.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty transaction event occurred.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the loyalty transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the loyalty transaction record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of loyalty points earned, redeemed, adjusted, or expired within a wallet-linked loyalty account. Captures loyalty transaction ID, loyalty account ID, wallet transaction reference, transaction type (earn, redeem, adjust, expire, transfer), points amount, monetary equivalent, merchant reference, program rule applied, transaction timestamp, and expiry date of earned points. Provides the full points ledger for wallet loyalty programs.'
}

Table terminal.terminal_group {
  terminal_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for terminal_group']
  parent_terminal_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on terminal_group (parent_terminal_group_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the physical address for the group’s primary site.']
  average_transaction_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monetary volume processed per terminal in the group (currency‑agnostic).']
  average_uptime_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average operational uptime of terminals in the group expressed as a percent.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the primary site for the terminal group.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the terminal group with industry regulations.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the group’s operational contact.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the group’s operational contact.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the terminal group is primarily deployed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the terminal group record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level governing access to the group’s data.']
  deployment_mode varchar [note: 'Infrastructure deployment model for the terminals in this group.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the terminal group’s purpose and characteristics.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the terminal group becomes active for business use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the terminal group is scheduled to be retired or become inactive; null if open‑ended.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Standard firmware version deployed across terminals in the group.']
  group_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the group within the organization.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the terminal group used in reporting and UI.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Category of merchants or environments the group serves.']
  is_contactless_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether contactless payment capability is enabled for the group.']
  is_emv_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMV chip functionality is enabled for the group.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance audit performed on the group.']
  max_terminals varchar [note: 'Maximum number of individual terminals that can be assigned to this group.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal remarks or operational notes.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the primary site.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Internal risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the terminal group based on fraud and compliance metrics.']
  security_cert_level varchar [note: 'PCI PTS certification level applicable to the terminals in this group.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the terminal group.']
  supported_payment_methods varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of payment method types the group supports.']
  terminal_group_category varchar [note: 'Logical categorisation used for reporting (e.g., group, cluster, fleet).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the terminal group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for terminal_group. Referenced by terminal_group_id.'
}

Table fx.rate {
  rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each FX rate record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Each fx_rate belongs to a currency pair; linking eliminates the free‑text pair field and enforces referential integrity.']
  rate_feed_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate_feed. Business justification: fx_rate records are produced by a specific rate feed and belong to a currency pair; linking removes duplicated source fields and enables traceability to the feed and pair.']
  settlement_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Regulatory settlement reports require linking each FX rate to the settlement currency master record for audit and compliance.']
  ask_price decimal(18,9) [note: 'Price at which the market maker is willing to sell the base currency.']
  base_currency varchar [note: 'Currency being quoted (the numerator in the pair).']
  bid_price decimal(18,9) [note: 'Price at which the market maker is willing to buy the base currency.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Provider‑assigned confidence level (0‑100) indicating reliability of the rate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FX rate record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  cross_border_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the rate is used for a cross‑border transaction.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rate becomes effective for transaction conversion.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rate ceases to be valid; null for open‑ended rates.']
  external_rate_code varchar [note: 'Reference code used by the external provider to identify this specific rate.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rate is currently active for use in transaction processing.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate record.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region of the market where the rate is sourced or applied.']
  mid_price decimal(18,9) [note: 'Average of bid and ask prices; often used as the reference rate.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional remarks or manual adjustments.']
  precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places provided for the rate value.']
  quote_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used to express the rate (the denominator in the pair).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the FX rate indicating its purpose and calculation method.']
  rate_value decimal(18,9) [note: 'Primary exchange rate value expressed as the amount of quote currency per one unit of base currency.']
  spread decimal(18,9) [note: 'Difference between ask and bid prices, expressed in quote currency units.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the FX rate record.']

  Note: 'Master record for foreign exchange rates published and consumed by the payments platform. Captures spot rates, mid-market rates, bid/ask spreads, and indicative rates for every currency pair supported across 200+ countries. Sourced from primary rate feed providers (Reuters, Bloomberg, ECB) and internal treasury desks. Serves as the authoritative rate reference for DCC pricing, cross-border transaction conversion, and settlement FX calculations. Includes rate type classification (spot, forward, indicative, guaranteed), effective timestamp, expiry timestamp, rate source, and confidence band.'
}

Table fx.rate_feed {
  rate_feed_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each FX rate feed configuration.']
  gateway_api_credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.api_credential. Business justification: Required for authenticating FX rate feeds using stored API credentials; FX team uses gateway\'s credential store for secure feed access.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Feed operations are overseen by a feed manager; required for feed performance audit and SLA compliance.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to authenticate the connection to the external feed source.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of regulations governing the feed (e.g., PCI DSS, GDPR, PSD2).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rate feed record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  credential_type varchar [note: 'Category of credential used (e.g., API key, client certificate). Actual secret values are stored in a vault, not in this table.']
  data_format varchar [note: 'File or message format in which rates are delivered.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the feed configuration starts being used for rate ingestion.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the feed configuration is no longer active; null indicates indefinite validity.']
  encryption_protocol varchar [note: 'Specific encryption protocol negotiated for the feed connection.']
  failover_priority varchar [note: 'Rank used to select an alternate feed when the primary feed is unavailable (lower = higher priority).']
  feed_endpoint varchar [note: 'Network address (URL, host:port, or file path) where the rate feed is accessed.']
  feed_protocol varchar [note: 'Technical protocol through which the FX rates are delivered to the platform.']
  is_encrypted boolean [note: 'True if the feed connection uses encryption (TLS/SSH).']
  last_error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the reason for the most recent feed failure.']
  last_error_message varchar [note: 'Detailed message describing the most recent feed retrieval error.']
  last_failure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent poll that resulted in an error.']
  last_success_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the last successful poll of the external rate feed.']
  polling_frequency_seconds varchar [note: 'How often the platform requests new rates from the provider.']
  provider_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code assigned to the provider for contract and reporting purposes.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the external FX rate feed provider (e.g., Reuters Refinitiv, Bloomberg FX).']
  rate_feed_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description providing context, notes, or special handling instructions for the feed.']
  rate_feed_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the rate feed configuration.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the FX rate (spot, forward, mid‑market, inter‑bank).']
  sla_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Target maximum latency for a successful feed retrieval as defined in the provider SLA.']
  sla_uptime_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable availability of the feed over a month, expressed as a percent.']
  supported_currency_pairs varchar [note: 'List of currency pairs (e.g., EUR/USD, GBP/JPY) that the feed supplies.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier (e.g., UTC, Europe/London) used for timestamps supplied by the provider.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the rate feed record.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record for each external FX rate feed provider integrated into the platform. Defines the provider identity (Reuters Refinitiv, Bloomberg FX, ECB reference rates, internal treasury), feed protocol (REST, FIX, SFTP), polling frequency, supported currency pairs, rate type delivered, SLA thresholds, failover priority, and connectivity credentials metadata. Governs how the platform ingests, validates, and routes rate data from each upstream source. Critical for rate feed health monitoring and provider SLA management.'
}

Table fx.currency_pair {
  currency_pair_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying a currency pair record.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Pair risk management report assigns a senior trader to each currency pair; treasury needs manager for oversight.']
  primary_base_currency_id varchar [note: 'FK to reference.currency']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification of the pair (major, minor, or exotic).']
  cross_border_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the pair may be used for cross‑border payment routing.']
  currency_pair_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the currency pair.']
  currency_pair_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the currency pair.']
  dcc_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the pair is eligible for DCC transactions.']
  decimal_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places supported for pricing the pair.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pair is the default for a given corridor or product.']
  last_rate_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent rate update for the pair.']
  market_convention varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the pair is quoted directly (base/quote) or indirectly.']
  min_tradeable_unit decimal(20,8) [note: 'Smallest amount of the base currency that can be traded or converted.']
  pair_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable concatenation of base and quote currency codes.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the currency pair record was created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which settlement amounts are settled.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Settlement speed category for the pair.']
  source_feed varchar [note: 'Origin of the FX rate feed for the pair.']
  source_feed_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the rate feed source.']
  standard_pip_size decimal(10,8) [note: 'Standard pip size used for price quoting of the pair.']
  trading_end_time timestamp [note: 'UTC time when the pair stops being tradable each business day.']
  trading_start_time timestamp [note: 'UTC time when the pair becomes tradable each business day.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all supported currency pairs traded or converted on the platform. Defines the base currency, quote currency, ISO 4217 currency codes, standard pip size, minimum tradeable unit, decimal precision, market convention (direct/indirect quote), trading hours, and active status. Serves as the canonical reference for FX rate lookups, DCC eligibility checks, and cross-border routing decisions. Includes classification of major, minor, and exotic pairs relevant to the payments corridor.'
}

Table fx.dcc_transaction {
  dcc_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the DCC transaction record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who performed the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who performed the transaction.']
  dcc_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.dcc_config. Business justification: A DCC transaction is governed by a DCC configuration; the FK captures the config used for the transaction.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant where the DCC transaction occurred.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: DCC fee and margin calculations depend on the underlying payment product (card) used, so the product must be recorded.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: A DCC transaction is a specific payment; linking allows settlement, chargeback, and compliance reporting.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: DCC processing requires the card scheme to apply correct margin and compliance rules.']
  settlement_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: DCC settlement must be reconciled against the official currency list for tax and fee calculations.']
  terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the point‑of‑sale terminal that captured the transaction.']
  acquirer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the acquiring bank for reconciliation.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuer confirming authorization.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment network brand of the card used in the transaction.']
  cardholder_home_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the cardholder\'s billing/home currency.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the transaction was initiated.']
  compliance_check_details varchar [note: 'Free‑text details of any compliance issues identified.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of regulatory compliance checks (e.g., AML, sanctions) for the transaction.']
  converted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount after applying the DCC exchange rate, expressed in the cardholder\'s home currency.']
  created_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the record (e.g., gateway, processing).']
  dcc_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the DCC offer was presented to the cardholder.']
  dcc_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Flat fee charged for the DCC service, if any, expressed in the cardholder\'s currency.']
  dcc_margin_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the fixed DCC margin amount, if applicable.']
  dcc_margin_fixed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed margin amount applied when margin type is fixed.']
  dcc_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage margin applied by the issuer/acquirer on top of the base exchange rate.']
  dcc_margin_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the DCC margin is a percentage or a fixed amount.']
  dcc_offer_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cardholder accepted the DCC offer (true) or declined (false).']
  dcc_offer_decline_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why a DCC offer was declined, if applicable.']
  dcc_transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the DCC transaction.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Technology used to capture the transaction.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the original amount to the cardholder\'s currency.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was flagged for potential fraud.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score generated by the fraud detection platform for this transaction.']
  fx_rate_source varchar [note: 'Source of the exchange rate used for conversion.']
  fx_rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exchange rate was retrieved.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address of the transaction request.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s business type.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text field for additional remarks or manual adjustments.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount in the original currency before DCC conversion.']
  original_transaction_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the transaction before conversion.']
  processing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Time in milliseconds from transaction receipt to DCC offer decision.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DCC transaction record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this DCC transaction record.']
  scheme_compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates compliance of the DCC transaction with Visa/Mastercard DCC rules.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final settled amount after conversion and fees.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction was settled in the cardholder\'s currency.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'External reference such as Acquirer Reference Number (ARN) that uniquely identifies the transaction across systems.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the transaction (e.g., purchase, refund).']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every Dynamic Currency Conversion event executed at the point of sale or online checkout. Records the original transaction currency, cardholder home currency, exchange rate applied, DCC margin percentage, converted amount, cardholder acceptance or decline of DCC offer, merchant MID, terminal TID, acquirer reference number (ARN), and scheme DCC compliance flags. Supports DCC revenue reporting, scheme compliance audits (Visa/Mastercard DCC rules), and cardholder dispute resolution for currency conversion disagreements.'
}

Table fx.conversion_audit {
  conversion_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the conversion audit record.']
  cardholder_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (customer) associated with the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder (customer) associated with the transaction.']
  device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used for the transaction (e.g., token ID).']
  input_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Audit of conversion inputs requires a foreign key to the currency master for validation and regulatory reporting.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant involved in the transaction.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Currency conversion audits need to trace back to the payment product that initiated the conversion for compliance traceability.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Conversion audit references the exact FX rate applied; linking removes the free‑text pair and provides a stable reference.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch or instruction related to this conversion.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement batch or instruction related to this conversion.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating payment transaction linked to this conversion.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit record was first created in the data lake.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this audit record (e.g., after reprocessing).']
  calculated_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Resulting amount after applying conversion rate and rounding.']
  calculation_status varchar [note: 'Outcome status of the conversion calculation.']
  client_ip varchar [note: 'IP address of the client initiating the transaction (if captured).']
  conversion_pnl decimal(18,6) [note: 'Profit or loss impact of the conversion on the company\'s FX P&L.']
  conversion_rate decimal(18,12) [note: 'FX rate applied for the conversion (target per unit of source).']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the currency conversion was performed.']
  conversion_type varchar [note: 'Category of conversion operation.']
  engine_version varchar [note: 'Version of the conversion calculation engine or service.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for conversion failure if status is failure.']
  input_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Original monetary amount before conversion.']
  is_rate_validated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applied FX rate passed compliance validation checks.']
  is_test_conversion boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the conversion was performed in a test or sandbox environment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks or manual adjustments.']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate source (e.g., Bloomberg, internal feed, central bank).']
  rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FX rate was retrieved or published.']
  rate_validation_rule varchar [note: 'Name of the compliance rule applied for rate validation.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this conversion must be reported to regulatory authorities (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  rounding_method varchar [note: 'Rounding algorithm applied to the converted amount.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System or module that generated the conversion event (e.g., Payment Gateway, Digital Wallet).']
  target_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the resulting amount.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit trail record for every currency conversion calculation performed by the platform, whether for DCC, cross-border settlement, or multi-currency wallet operations. Captures the input amount, source currency, target currency, rate applied, rate source reference, conversion timestamp, calculation engine version, rounding method applied, and resulting converted amount. Provides the forensic evidence chain required for regulatory inquiries, cardholder disputes, and internal reconciliation of FX P&L. Distinct from dcc_transaction in that it covers ALL conversion events, not just DCC-offered ones.'
}

Table fx.correspondent_bank {
  correspondent_bank_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the correspondent bank record.']
  swift_bic_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.swift_bic. Business justification: BIC codes are centrally managed; linking enables validation and sanctions screening of correspondent banks.']
  aml_kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current anti‑money‑laundering and know‑your‑customer compliance status of the bank.']
  compliance_documentation varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the stored compliance documents (e.g., AML policies, contracts).']
  contact_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address of the bank\'s primary office for correspondence.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the bank\'s relationship manager.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the bank\'s relationship manager.']
  correspondent_bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the correspondent bank.']
  correspondent_fee_fixed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat fee charged per transaction, in the bank\'s settlement currency.']
  correspondent_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variable fee expressed as a percentage of the transaction amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the correspondent bank record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure the fintech is permitted to have with the correspondent bank, expressed in US dollars.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the relationship ended or is scheduled to end (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the correspondent relationship became effective.']
  iban_format_supported varchar [note: 'Pattern or description of IBAN structures the bank can process (e.g., country-specific length).']
  is_iban_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bank can receive or send payments using IBAN format.']
  is_swift_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SWIFT messaging is supported for this bank.']
  is_tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bank participates in tokenization services for digital wallets.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or risk review of the relationship.']
  nostro_account_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code of the nostro account (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  nostro_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number held by the fintech in the correspondent bank for settlement purposes.']
  payment_corridors varchar [note: 'Geographic or network corridors (e.g., US‑EU, APAC‑LATAM) the bank supports for cross‑border payments.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Country or region whose financial regulations govern the correspondent relationship.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the correspondent banking relationship.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the correspondent bank based on AML, credit, and operational risk assessments.']
  settlement_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Daily cut‑off time (local bank time) after which transactions are processed in the next business day.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Primary mechanism used for settling transactions with the correspondent bank.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes the bank can settle for.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the correspondent bank.']

  Note: 'Master record for correspondent banking relationships maintained by the platform to facilitate cross-border payment routing. Captures the correspondent bank name, BIC/SWIFT code, IBAN format supported, nostro account details, supported currencies, supported payment corridors, settlement cut-off times, correspondent bank fees, regulatory jurisdiction, AML/KYC status, and relationship status. Serves as the SSOT for correspondent banking counterparty data used in SWIFT routing, cross-border settlement instructions, and treasury liquidity management.'
}

Table fx.nostro_account {
  nostro_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the nostro account record.']
  correspondent_bank_id varchar [note: 'FK to fx.correspondent_bank']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Nostro balances must be tied to the official currency list for accounting and regulatory reporting.']
  account_label varchar [note: 'Human‑friendly label or nickname for the account used in treasury dashboards.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Unique account number assigned by the correspondent bank for the nostro account.']
  account_purpose varchar [note: 'Primary business purpose of the nostro account.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nostro account based on its operational purpose.']
  aml_watchlist_status varchar [note: 'Result of AML watchlist screening for the account.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the detailed audit record for the account.']
  available_balance decimal(20,2) [note: 'Balance available for new transactions after holds and overdraft considerations.']
  bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC of the correspondent bank holding the nostro account.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date on which the nostro account was closed, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account record was first created in the system.']
  cumulative_daily_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total value of transactions processed on the current day.']
  current_balance decimal(20,2) [note: 'Current total balance of the nostro account.']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(20,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate transaction amount permitted per calendar day.']
  daily_usage_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the daily transaction limit that has been used.']
  fee_schedule varchar [note: 'Description of fees applicable to the account.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number used for cross‑border transactions.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate applied to the account balance.']
  is_pre_funded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is pre‑funded for anticipated transactions.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction governing the account.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent audit performed on the account.']
  last_reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent reconciliation with the correspondent bank statement.']
  last_statement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent bank statement received for the account.']
  liquidity_limit decimal(20,2) [note: 'Maximum liquidity allocation permitted for the account.']
  minimum_balance_threshold decimal(20,2) [note: 'Minimum balance that must be maintained to avoid fees or penalties.']
  nostro_account_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the nostro account.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date on which the nostro account was opened with the correspondent bank.']
  opening_balance decimal(20,2) [note: 'Balance of the account at the moment it was opened.']
  overdraft_limit decimal(20,2) [note: 'Authorized overdraft amount for the account.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account is subject to specific regulatory reporting obligations.']
  settlement_instructions varchar [note: 'Instructions for processing settlements using this account.']
  treasury_desk varchar [note: 'Internal treasury desk responsible for managing the account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the account record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each nostro account held by the platform at correspondent banks in foreign currencies. Captures the account number, correspondent bank reference, currency denomination, account type, opening balance, current balance, minimum balance threshold, overdraft limit, account status, and the treasury desk responsible for management. Nostro accounts are the operational funding pools for cross-border payment execution. Supports real-time liquidity monitoring, pre-funding adequacy checks, and reconciliation against correspondent bank statements.'
}

Table fx.nostro_balance {
  nostro_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each nostro balance snapshot record.']
  nostro_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the nostro account to which this balance belongs.']
  available_balance decimal(20,6) [note: 'Portion of the closing balance that is not on hold and can be used for payments.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the snapshot has been reconciled against correspondent bank statements.']
  closing_balance decimal(20,6) [note: 'Balance amount at the end of the value date, after all posted movements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the balance record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  credit_movements decimal(20,6) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all credit transactions posted to the account on the value date.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code representing the account\'s currency.']
  debit_movements decimal(20,6) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all debit transactions posted to the account on the value date.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Fixed value indicating this row represents a balance snapshot event.']
  hold_balance decimal(20,6) [note: 'Funds that are reserved or blocked and cannot be used for outgoing payments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or remarks about the balance snapshot.']
  opening_balance decimal(20,6) [note: 'Balance amount at the beginning of the value date, expressed in the account currency.']
  pending_credits decimal(20,6) [note: 'Sum of credit amounts that are authorized but not yet finalised.']
  pending_debits decimal(20,6) [note: 'Sum of debit amounts that are authorized but not yet finalised.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the balance snapshot was recorded.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that produced the balance snapshot.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the balance record.']
  value_date date [note: 'The business date for which the balance snapshot is taken.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing point-in-time balance snapshots and movements on each nostro account. Records the nostro account reference, value date, opening balance, closing balance, credit movements, debit movements, pending credits, pending debits, and reconciliation status against correspondent bank statements. Enables intraday liquidity monitoring, end-of-day reconciliation, and pre-funding adequacy validation before cross-border payment execution. Distinct from nostro_account (the master) as it captures the time-series balance history.'
}

Table fx.swift_message {
  swift_message_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the SWIFT message record.']
  gpi_tracking_swift_gpi_tracker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier used for tracking the message in SWIFT gpi network.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: Provides end‑to‑end traceability from SWIFT messages to the originating payment transaction for audit and dispute handling.']
  sender_swift_bic_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.swift_bic. Business justification: Sender BIC validation against master BIC table is required for AML and GPI tracking.']
  swift_gpi_tracker_id varchar [note: 'Identifier used for tracking the message in SWIFT gpi network.']
  aml_sar_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the transaction triggered a Suspicious Activity Report.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the transaction in the specified currency.']
  beneficiary_customer_account varchar [note: 'IBAN of the beneficiary customer\'s account.']
  beneficiary_customer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the party receiving the payment.']
  charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of charges applied to the transaction.']
  charge_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the charges amount.']
  charges_instruction varchar [note: 'Indicates which party bears the transaction charges: SHA (shared), OUR (sender), BEN (beneficiary).']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework applicable to the transaction (e.g., PSD2, AML, OFAC).']
  correspondent_bank_chain varchar [note: 'Ordered list of intermediary banks (BICs) involved in routing the payment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SWIFT message record was created in the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the transaction amount.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Code representing any error encountered during processing.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human readable description of the processing error.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied if currency conversion occurs.']
  is_duplicate boolean [note: 'Indicates if this message is a duplicate of a previously received message.']
  is_gpi boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the message is part of SWIFT gpi (True) or not (False).']
  is_test_message boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the message is a test (True) or production (False).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SWIFT message record.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the message within the platform.']
  message_reference varchar [note: 'Reference assigned by the sender to correlate messages.']
  message_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the SWIFT message.']
  message_type varchar [note: 'Type of SWIFT message according to SWIFT or ISO 20022 standards.']
  message_version varchar [note: 'Version of the message format (e.g., ISO 20022 version).']
  ordering_customer_account varchar [note: 'IBAN of the ordering customer\'s account.']
  ordering_customer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the party ordering the payment.']
  processing_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when processing of the message completed.']
  processing_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when processing of the message began.']
  receiver_bic varchar [note: 'BIC of the receiving financial institution.']
  regulatory_sanctions_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions screening for the transaction.']
  remittance_information varchar [note: 'Additional information for the beneficiary to reconcile the payment.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction is settled in the receiving bank.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the SWIFT message (e.g., Payment Gateway, Transaction Processing Platform).']
  transaction_purpose varchar [note: 'Narrative purpose of the payment as provided by the ordering customer.']
  uetr varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier for the transaction per SWIFT gpi.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the funds are to be made available to the beneficiary.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for every SWIFT financial message sent or received by the platform for cross-border payment instructions. Captures the SWIFT message type (MT103, MT202, MT199, ISO 20022 pacs.008), sender BIC, receiver BIC, UETR (Unique End-to-End Transaction Reference per SWIFT gpi), message reference, value date, currency, amount, ordering customer, beneficiary customer, correspondent bank chain, charges instruction (SHA/OUR/BEN), and message status. Supports SWIFT gpi tracking, cross-border payment tracing, and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table fx.payment_corridor {
  payment_corridor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the payment corridor record.']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fee schedule applicable to this corridor.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Compliance officer owns each corridor; needed for corridor risk monitoring and regulatory reporting.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Each payment corridor must comply with particular regulatory obligations; the FK ties corridor definitions to the governing obligation for audit and risk checks.']
  source_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Payment corridor reporting requires the source country to be a foreign key to the country master for regulatory stats.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the corridor record was first created.']
  currency_conversion_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether automatic currency conversion is permitted for this corridor.']
  dcc_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if DCC (Dynamic Currency Conversion) is offered for transactions in this corridor.']
  destination_bank_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of BIC codes of banks in the destination country that can receive payments.']
  destination_country_iso varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the destination country for the corridor.']
  destination_currency_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes that can be received in this corridor.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the corridor becomes active and usable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the corridor is retired or no longer available (null if indefinite).']
  estimated_settlement_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Typical time in minutes from transaction initiation to settlement for this corridor.']
  exchange_rate_source varchar [note: 'Source of FX rates used for conversion in this corridor.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount allowed per transaction in the corridor, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary amount required per transaction in the corridor.']
  ofac_screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether OFAC or other sanctions screening must be performed for transactions in this corridor.']
  payment_corridor_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the corridor, including any special handling notes.']
  payment_corridor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the corridor.']
  payment_corridor_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the corridor based on usage or risk profile.']
  regulatory_restrictions varchar [note: 'Textual description of any regulatory or compliance constraints that apply to the corridor (e.g., AML limits, country sanctions).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the corridor based on AML, sanctions, and fraud exposure.']
  settlement_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for settlement processing (e.g., netting, gross, timing).']
  source_bank_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of BIC codes of banks in the source country that can originate payments.']
  source_currency_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes that can be originated in this corridor.']
  supported_payment_rails varchar [note: 'Payment rail(s) available for routing transactions through this corridor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the corridor record.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record defining a supported cross-border payment corridor between a source country and destination country. Captures the source country ISO code, destination country ISO code, supported currencies, available payment rails (SWIFT, local ACH, RTP, SEPA), regulatory restrictions, OFAC/sanctions screening requirements, estimated settlement time, corridor status, and applicable fee schedule reference. Serves as the routing decision table for cross-border payment execution and corridor availability checks.'
}

Table fx.iban_routing {
  iban_routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each IBAN routing rule record.']
  correspondent_bank_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.correspondent_bank. Business justification: IBAN routing rules are specific to a correspondent bank; linking removes the need to store bank details redundantly.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: IBAN routing rules depend on the country; linking to country master enables validation and compliance checks.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Routing may be limited to specific currencies; foreign key ensures correct currency association.']
  account_number_end_position varchar [note: 'Zero‑based end index (inclusive) of the account number segment within the BBAN.']
  account_number_start_position varchar [note: 'Zero‑based start index of the account number segment within the BBAN.']
  bank_id_end_position varchar [note: 'Zero‑based end index (inclusive) of the national bank identifier within the BBAN.']
  bank_id_start_position varchar [note: 'Zero‑based start index of the national bank identifier within the BBAN.']
  bban_pattern varchar [note: 'Regular‑expression pattern describing the BBAN structure (excluding country code and check digits).']
  branch_code_end_position varchar [note: 'Zero‑based end index (inclusive) of the branch code segment within the BBAN.']
  branch_code_start_position varchar [note: 'Zero‑based start index of the branch code segment within the BBAN, if applicable.']
  check_digit_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate and validate the IBAN check digits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the routing rule becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the routing rule is no longer valid (null if indefinite).']
  iban_example varchar [note: 'A valid example IBAN for the country, useful for testing and documentation.']
  iban_length varchar [note: 'Total number of characters in a valid IBAN for the country.']
  iban_routing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing rule (active, inactive, or deprecated).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments about the routing rule.']
  routing_bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC of the primary correspondent bank used for routing payments to this country.']
  sepa_member_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country participates in the Single Euro Payments Area (true = member).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the IBAN routing data (e.g., SWIFT, internal registry).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the routing record.']

  Note: 'Reference master for IBAN structure validation rules and routing configurations by country. Captures the country ISO code, IBAN length, BBAN format pattern, check digit algorithm, national bank identifier position, branch code position, account number position, SEPA membership flag, and routing bank BIC for each country. Used by the payment processing engine to validate IBANs on inbound cross-border payment instructions and to derive the correct correspondent bank routing path from an IBAN.'
}

Table fx.forward_contract {
  forward_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the FX forward contract record.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external bank or financial institution counterpart to the contract.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: FX forward contracts are defined for a specific currency pair; linking removes duplicated currency code fields.']
  source_feed_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rate feed used for spot and forward rates.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract received internal approval.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to audit trail record for this contract.']
  collateral_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Amount of collateral posted for the contract.']
  collateral_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the posted collateral.']
  collateral_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if collateral is required for the contract.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework applicable to the contract.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Unique external reference code for the FX forward contract.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FX forward contract.']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the contract record for amendment tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was created in the system.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract automatically expires if not settled.']
  forward_points decimal(12,6) [note: 'Difference between forward and spot rates expressed in points.']
  forward_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Agreed exchange rate for settlement on the value date.']
  hedge_designation varchar [note: 'Accounting hedge classification per IFRS 9/ASC 815.']
  internal_accounting_code varchar [note: 'Internal accounting code used for GL posting.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal comments and audit notes.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the contract involves cross-border currency exchange.']
  last_rate_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent rate update from the source feed.']
  maturity_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires if not settled earlier.']
  notional_amount decimal(20,2) [note: 'Principal amount of the base currency to be exchanged.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract must be reported to regulatory authorities.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the contract based on credit and market risk.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which settlement will be made.']
  settlement_instructions varchar [note: 'Textual instructions for settlement (e.g., bank account, SWIFT details).']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transfer settlement funds.']
  spot_rate_at_inception decimal(18,8) [note: 'Spot exchange rate observed at contract initiation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the currency exchange is settled.']

  Note: 'Master record for FX forward contracts entered into by the platform treasury to hedge future cross-border settlement obligations. Captures the contract reference, counterparty bank, base currency, quote currency, notional amount, contracted forward rate, spot rate at inception, forward points, value date, maturity date, hedge designation (cash flow hedge, fair value hedge), contract status, and settlement instructions. Supports treasury FX risk management, hedge accounting under IFRS 9/ASC 815, and settlement obligation pre-funding.'
}

Table fx.exposure {
  exposure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each FX exposure snapshot record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: FX exposure is measured per currency pair; linking normalizes the currency reference.']
  monitor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Exposure limits are monitored by a risk analyst; daily exposure limit report references this analyst.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: FX exposure limits are set per payment product; linking exposure records to the product enables risk monitoring.']
  risk_limit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the risk limit definition applied to this exposure.']
  treasury_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal treasury unit responsible for the exposure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exposure record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the exposure.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the exposure is a long (positive) or short (negative) position.']
  exposure_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the exposure amount, such as pending settlements, nostro balances, forward contracts, etc.']
  exposure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exposure record.']
  gross_notional_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total notional amount of the FX position before any hedging offsets.']
  hedging_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the exposure is fully hedged, partially hedged, or unhedged.']
  limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable exposure amount defined by the risk limit.']
  limit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the risk limit governing this exposure.']
  limit_utilization_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Absolute amount of the risk limit currently utilized by this exposure.']
  net_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Exposure amount after applying hedging instruments, expressed in the reporting currency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the exposure snapshot.']
  risk_limit_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the assigned risk limit that is currently utilized by this exposure.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the exposure snapshot was captured.']
  unhedged_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the exposure that remains without hedge coverage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the exposure record.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the exposure is expected to settle or be realized.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the platform\'s open FX exposure position by currency at a point in time. Records the currency, exposure direction (long/short), gross notional exposure, net exposure after hedges, unhedged exposure, value date, exposure source (pending settlements, nostro balances, forward contracts), and risk limit utilization percentage. Enables treasury to monitor real-time FX risk positions, trigger hedging actions when exposure breaches thresholds, and report FX risk to the board and regulators.'
}

Table fx.cross_border_payment {
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cross-border payment record.']
  beneficiary_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party receiving the funds.']
  beneficiary_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party receiving the funds.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Cross‑border payments can be initiated from a digital wallet; linking to the originating wallet supports KYC/AML checks and source‑of‑funds reporting.']
  origin_country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Origin country is required for AML, sanctions, and reporting; linking to country master provides authoritative data.']
  origin_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Origin currency must be validated against the currency master for conversion and reporting.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requires linking each cross‑border payment to its payment product to aggregate volumes by product type.']
  payment_txn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to transaction.payment_txn. Business justification: Cross‑border payment records must be tied to the underlying payment transaction for reconciliation and AML reporting.']
  request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to gateway.gateway_request. Business justification: Enables reconciliation of cross‑border payments with the original gateway request for audit and settlement reporting.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.scheme. Business justification: Regulatory reporting of cross‑border payments must capture the card scheme used for compliance (e.g., PSD2, SWIFT reporting).']
  sender_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (individual or organization) sending the funds.']
  settlement_instruction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the settlement instruction record governing fund transfer.']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of Anti‑Money‑Laundering screening for the transaction.']
  beneficiary_bank_bic varchar [note: 'Bank Identifier Code of the beneficiary\'s financial institution.']
  beneficiary_iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number of the beneficiary\'s account.']
  chargeback_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the payment resulted in a chargeback.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes related to compliance checks or regulatory observations.']
  converted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount in the destination currency after applying the FX rate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cross-border payment.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the beneficiary\'s country.']
  destination_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the amount received by the beneficiary.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment instruction was initiated by the sender.']
  exchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the FX rate (e.g., spot, forward).']
  exchange_rate_validity_end timestamp [note: 'End of the time window during which the FX rate is valid.']
  exchange_rate_validity_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the time window during which the FX rate is valid.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fees charged for processing the cross‑border payment.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the fee amount.']
  fx_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert the original amount to the destination currency.']
  fx_rate_source varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the rate feed provider (e.g., Bloomberg, Reuters).']
  fx_rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FX rate was sourced or locked for the transaction.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the payment status.']
  ofac_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of Office of Foreign Assets Control sanctions screening.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount in the origin currency before conversion.']
  payment_purpose_code varchar [note: 'ISO 20022 purpose code describing the reason for the payment.']
  payment_rail varchar [note: 'Network or scheme used to route the cross‑border payment.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the originating institution or platform for tracking the payment.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction must be reported to regulators.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the payment record represents a reversal of a prior transaction.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment is settled between banks.']
  swift_uetr varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier assigned by SWIFT for tracking the payment.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of original amount and any applicable fees, expressed in the origin currency.']
  total_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the total amount field.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether funds are being sent (debit) or received (credit).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment record.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the converted amount becomes effective for accounting.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record for every cross-border payment instruction initiated through the platform. Captures the payment reference, originating country, destination country, sender identity, beneficiary identity, beneficiary bank BIC, beneficiary IBAN/account number, payment amount, origination currency, destination currency, FX rate applied, converted amount, payment rail selected (SWIFT, SEPA, local ACH), SWIFT UETR, payment purpose code, regulatory reporting flags, OFAC screening status, and payment lifecycle status. The SSOT for cross-border payment execution within the FX domain.'
}

Table fx.multicurrency_settlement {
  multicurrency_settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the multicurrency settlement record.']
  counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant or financial institution on the opposite side of the settlement.']
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_fee_schedule. Business justification: A settlement may be subject to a fee schedule; linking enables fee‑schedule lookup from the settlement.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Settlement amounts are calculated using a specific FX rate; linking preserves the exact rate used.']
  batch_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement batch record was initially created in the processing system.']
  batch_processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was sent to the settlement rail for execution.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of compliance checks applied to the settlement batch.']
  correspondent_bank_swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC identifier of the correspondent bank used for the settlement.']
  currency_pair varchar [note: 'ISO representation of the FX pair used for conversion (e.g., USD/EUR).']
  exchange_rate_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places used for the FX rate.']
  exchange_rate_source varchar [note: 'Source system or provider that supplied the FX rate.']
  fee_amount decimal(20,6) [note: 'Total fees (e.g., interchange, processing, regulatory) deducted from the gross amount.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which fees are assessed; typically matches settlement currency.']
  fx_rate_applied decimal(18,9) [note: 'Foreign‑exchange rate used to convert the gross amount to the settlement currency.']
  gross_settlement_amount decimal(20,6) [note: 'Total amount of the batch before FX conversion and fees, expressed in origination currency.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the settlement involves currency conversion across borders.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(20,6) [note: 'Amount after FX conversion and deduction of fees, expressed in settlement currency.']
  nostro_account_debited varchar [note: 'Account number of the fintech\'s Nostro account from which funds are debited.']
  origination_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the original cross‑border transaction(s) before conversion.']
  rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FX rate was captured or published.']
  reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the external reconciliation file or message confirming settlement.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this settlement record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this settlement record.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this settlement batch must be reported to a regulator (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  settlement_batch_reference varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the settlement batch, used in reporting and reconciliation.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the batch is settled.']
  settlement_currency_minor_units varchar [note: 'Number of minor units (e.g., cents) for the settlement currency.']
  settlement_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement batch was executed in the payment rail.']
  settlement_instructions varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing any special handling or routing instructions for the batch.']
  settlement_rail varchar [note: 'Payment rail used to move the settled funds.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement batch.']
  settlement_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current settlement status, especially when failed or pending.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement was processed as a batch, real‑time, or scheduled operation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that originated the settlement record.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual transactions aggregated into this settlement batch.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement amount becomes effective for accounting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for multi-currency settlement instructions generated for cross-border transaction batches. Captures the settlement batch reference, settlement currency, origination currency, gross settlement amount, FX rate applied, net settlement amount after fees, correspondent bank, nostro account debited, value date, settlement rail (RTGS, SWIFT, local), settlement status, and reconciliation confirmation reference. Distinct from the settlement domain\'s core settlement records as this entity specifically tracks the FX conversion and multi-currency netting aspects of cross-border settlement.'
}

Table fx.dcc_config {
  dcc_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the DCC configuration record.']
  cardholder_disclosure_text_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the legal disclosure text shown to cardholders for DCC offers.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes related to regulatory or scheme compliance requirements for this configuration.']
  config_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the DCC configuration, used in reporting and operational dashboards.']
  config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the DCC configuration purpose and any special notes.']
  config_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DCC configuration (e.g., standard, premium, custom).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the DCC configuration record was first created.']
  dcc_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DCC configuration.']
  dcc_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage margin applied by the merchant or acquirer over the base FX rate for DCC transactions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the DCC configuration expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the DCC configuration becomes active.']
  enrolled_currency_pairs varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of currency pairs (e.g., USD/EUR|USD/GBP) that the merchant participates in for DCC offers.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry code that classifies the merchant\'s business type.']
  merchant_mid varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant to which this DCC configuration applies.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum transaction amount (in merchant\'s settlement currency) required for a DCC offer to be presented.']
  offer_presentation_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the DCC offer is presented to the cardholder.']
  opt_in_default boolean [note: 'Default opt‑in (true) or opt‑out (false) setting applied when a merchant enrolls in DCC.']
  scheme_compliance varchar [note: 'Indicates which card scheme(s) the DCC configuration complies with.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the DCC configuration record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version used for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record governing DCC program parameters for each merchant or merchant category enrolled in the DCC service. Captures the merchant MID, enrolled currency pairs, DCC margin percentage, minimum transaction amount for DCC offer, DCC offer presentation method (terminal, online, IVR), scheme compliance settings (Visa DCC, Mastercard Currency Conversion), cardholder disclosure text reference, opt-in/opt-out default, and configuration effective dates. Drives the DCC eligibility and rate calculation logic at transaction time.'
}

Table fx.fx_fee_schedule {
  fx_fee_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each FX fee schedule record.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Fee schedules are subject to regulatory frameworks; linking to the obligation ensures fee calculations respect compliance rules.']
  applicable_currency_pair varchar [note: 'Currency pair (e.g., USD/EUR) to which the fee schedule applies.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the fee – flat amount, percentage of transaction amount, or tiered structure.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) that govern the fee schedule, e.g., PSD2, FCA, or local AML rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee schedule record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of customers for which the fee schedule is valid.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee schedule becomes active.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the fee schedule ceases to be active; null for open‑ended schedules.']
  fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Core fee value used when calculation_method is flat; for percentage or tiered methods this stores the base rate or tier reference.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the fee is expressed.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Category of FX service the fee applies to, such as Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) or SWIFT wire.']
  fee_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number used for change management and audit.']
  fx_fee_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context, business rules, or notes about the fee schedule.']
  fx_fee_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fee schedule.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule is the default for its fee_type and segment.']
  maximum_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Highest fee that can be charged under this schedule, expressed in fee_currency.']
  minimum_fee decimal(15,2) [note: 'Lowest fee that can be charged under this schedule, expressed in fee_currency.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the fee schedule across systems.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the fee schedule used for reporting and UI display.']
  tier_details varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded or delimited representation of tier thresholds and rates when calculation_method is tiered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fee schedule record.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the fee structures applied to FX and cross-border payment services offered by the platform. Captures the fee schedule name, applicable service type (DCC, cross-border transfer, currency conversion, SWIFT wire), fee calculation method (flat, percentage, tiered), fee currency, minimum fee, maximum fee, applicable currency pairs or corridors, customer segment applicability, effective date, and expiry date. Serves as the pricing reference for FX revenue calculation and merchant/cardholder fee disclosure.'
}

Table fx.rate_margin_config {
  rate_margin_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the FX margin configuration record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the currency pair to which this margin applies.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who approved the configuration.']
