  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent update.']
  primary_rate_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who approved the configuration.']
  applicable_region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the region where the margin applies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA|CAN|AUS|... — promote to reference product]']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration was approved.']
  audit_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for audit notes or change justification.']
  base_rate_source_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source providing the underlying interbank rate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration record was first created.']
  customer_tier_code varchar [note: 'Segment of customers (e.g., platinum, gold) for which the margin is defined.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the margin configuration becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the margin configuration expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this configuration is the default for its scope.']
  margin_basis varchar [note: 'Unit used to express the margin (basis points or percent).']
  margin_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Margin expressed as a percentage when margin_type is percentage_markup.']
  margin_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the markup on the interbank rate.']
  margin_value_bps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Margin expressed in basis points when margin_type is fixed_spread.']
  product_type_code varchar [note: 'Type of payment product or service the margin applies to.']
  rate_margin_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context and business rationale.']
  rate_margin_config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the margin configuration.']
  rate_margin_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the margin configuration.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the configuration satisfies PSD2 transparency requirements.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk rating associated with this margin configuration (higher scores may justify higher margins).']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the margin is settled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CHF|CAD|... — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the configuration record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for change management.']

  Note: 'Master configuration record defining the FX margin (spread) applied on top of the interbank mid-market rate for each customer segment, product type, or payment corridor. Captures the margin type (fixed spread, percentage markup, tiered by volume), base rate source, margin basis points, applicable currency pair or corridor, customer tier, product type, effective date, and approval authority. Governs how the platform marks up interbank rates to generate FX revenue while remaining competitive and compliant with PSD2 transparency requirements.'
}

Table fx.rate_snapshot {
  rate_snapshot_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each FX rate snapshot record.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Rate snapshots are taken for a specific currency pair; linking removes duplicated pair fields.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.rate. Business justification: Rate snapshot records are point‑in‑time copies of a rate. Adding rate_snapshot.rate_id FK to rate.rate_id eliminates duplicated rate columns and enables joins to the authoritative rate record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the snapshot record was first persisted in the data lake.']
  cross_border_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the rate snapshot is used for a cross‑border payment scenario.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time from which the quoted rates are considered effective for settlement.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time after which the rates are no longer valid; nullable for open‑ended rates.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'True if the rates are derived estimates rather than directly observed market rates.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market region (e.g., EMEA, APAC, AMER) applicable to the snapshot.']
  rate_feed_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the rate feed used for this snapshot.']
  rate_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places used to represent the rates for this currency pair.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the snapshot is required for regulatory reporting (e.g., Basel III, AML).']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the transaction settlement will be performed.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the FX rate snapshot was captured.']
  snapshot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the snapshot: end‑of‑day (EOD), intraday, or regulatory reporting.']
  source_feed_session_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the feed session that produced this snapshot.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the snapshot record (e.g., correction of metadata).']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing scheduled end-of-day or intraday FX rate snapshots used for settlement valuation, regulatory reporting, and accounting purposes. Captures the snapshot timestamp, snapshot type (EOD, intraday, regulatory), currency pair, mid rate, bid rate, ask rate, rate source, and the rate feed session reference. Distinct from fx_rate (the live rate master) as snapshots are immutable point-in-time records used for valuation and audit purposes rather than real-time transaction pricing.'
}

Table fx.sanctions_screening_result {
  sanctions_screening_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each sanctions screening result record.']
  analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Compliance analyst reviews flagged sanctions hits; required for investigation report and audit.']
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.cross_border_payment. Business justification: Screening results are tied to the payment they screened; linking provides direct access to the payment record.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch in which the screening was performed.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment transaction that generated this screening result.']
  analyst_review_status varchar [note: 'Current review state of the screening result by compliance analysts.']
  compliance_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this screening result must be included in regulatory filings (e.g., FinCEN SAR).']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final compliance decision: allow transaction, block transaction, send for further review, or escalate to senior management.']
  eu_consolidated_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the European Union consolidated sanctions list was screened.']
  hmt_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the UK HM Treasury sanctions list was screened.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'True if the underlying payment is a test transaction and not subject to regulatory hold.']
  match_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0‑100) indicating the likelihood of a match.']
  match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the screening: \'clear\' (no match), \'potential\' (possible match), or \'confirmed\' (definite match).']
  matched_entity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or organization that triggered the match, if any.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments added by analysts or automated processes.']
  ofac_sdn_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) list was included in the screening.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original amount of the cross‑border payment being screened.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'ISO‑4217 three‑letter currency code of the payment (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number of the cross‑border payment instruction that was screened.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the screening result record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the screening result (e.g., after analyst review).']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'True if the transaction is placed on hold pending regulatory clearance.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall AML/CTF risk score associated with the payment, as calculated by the screening engine.']
  screening_engine varchar [note: 'Name or code of the sanctions screening engine or vendor that executed the check (e.g., OFAC‑API, Accuity, World‑Check).']
  screening_result_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the screening result record to support updates and re‑screening.']
  screening_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the sanctions screening was performed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that produced the screening record (e.g., Payment Gateway, AML Platform).']
  un_sanctions_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the United Nations sanctions list was screened.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the OFAC/SDN and international sanctions screening result for each cross-border payment instruction processed by the platform. Records the payment reference, screening timestamp, screening engine used, lists checked (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated, UN Sanctions, HMT), match status (clear, potential match, confirmed match), match score, matched entity name, analyst review status, disposition decision, and regulatory hold flag. Required for BSA/AML compliance, FinCEN reporting, and OFAC regulatory obligations on cross-border payments.'
}

Table fx.regulatory_reporting_req {
  regulatory_reporting_req_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory reporting requirement record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user responsible for the requirement.']
  internal_owner_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user responsible for the requirement.']
  payment_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.payment_corridor. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requirements are often scoped to a payment corridor; linking enables direct association.']
  payment_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.payment_product. Business justification: Regulatory reporting often requires product‑level breakdowns, so the requirement must reference the specific payment product.']
  regulatory_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requirements are derived from a specific regulatory obligation; linking enables the reporting engine to reference the exact obligation it satisfies.']
  applicable_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 4217 currency codes to which the requirement applies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CHF|Other — promote to reference product]']
  applicable_payment_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of payment methods (e.g., ACH, SWIFT, Card) that fall under this requirement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACH|SWIFT|Card|Crypto|Other — promote to reference product]']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the reporting requirement configuration.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requirement was approved or rejected.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent change to the requirement configuration.']
  compliance_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether each transaction must be validated against this requirement before processing.']
  contact_point_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the compliance contact responsible for this requirement.']
  contact_point_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the compliance contact responsible for this requirement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requirement record was first created.']
  data_retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days records related to this requirement must be retained for compliance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the reporting obligation becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the reporting obligation expires or is superseded (null if indefinite).']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority that issues the reporting requirement.']
  internal_owner_department varchar [note: 'Business unit that owns the reporting requirement configuration.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the requirement applies only to cross‑border FX transactions.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with this reporting requirement is mandatory (true) or optional (false).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Country or region whose regulator mandates the reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the requirement record.']
  regulation_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the regulation governing the reporting obligation.']
  regulatory_filing_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the triggering event by which the report must be filed.']
  regulatory_reporting_req_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose and scope of the reporting obligation.']
  regulatory_reporting_req_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reporting requirement.']
  reporting_channel varchar [note: 'Method used to submit the regulatory report.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the report must be submitted.']
  reporting_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount that triggers the reporting obligation.']
  reporting_threshold_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the threshold amount.']
  reporting_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'Currency unit used for the reporting threshold.']
  reporting_type varchar [note: 'Category of regulatory filing (e.g., Currency Transaction Report, Suspicious Activity Report).']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'True if the reporting configuration must be approved by a compliance officer.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'File or message format required for the regulatory filing.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the requirement record for change management.']

  Note: 'Master record defining each regulatory reporting obligation applicable to cross-border payments and FX operations across the jurisdictions the platform operates in. Captures the regulation name, governing body (FinCEN, ECB, FCA, FATF), reporting type (CTR, SAR, CMIR, cross-border wire transfer report), reporting threshold (amount, frequency), applicable payment types, reporting frequency, submission format, and jurisdiction. Serves as the compliance configuration driving automated regulatory report generation for cross-border FX activity.'
}

Table fx.cross_border_fee {
  cross_border_fee_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cross‑border fee record.']
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.cross_border_payment. Business justification: Each cross‑border fee is charged against a specific payment; linking provides direct navigation from fee to its payment.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent payment transaction to which this fee is attached.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the fee is collected from the payer.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) applicable to this fee.']
  created_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that created the fee record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee record was first created in the system.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount of the fee in the fee currency before any conversion.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the fee amount is expressed.']
  fee_currency_conversion_rate decimal(18,8) [note: 'Rate used to convert fee_original_amount to fee_amount.']
  fee_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the fee purpose or context.']
  fee_effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fee became effective (e.g., when applied to the transaction).']
  fee_original_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fee amount expressed in the transaction’s settlement currency before conversion.']
  fee_original_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the fee as originally charged before any conversion.']
  fee_payer varchar [note: 'Party responsible for paying the fee.']
  fee_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of fee units applied; typically 1.']
  fee_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference code for the fee (e.g., ARN, SWIFT code).']
  fee_settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fee was settled with the correspondent bank or internal ledger.']
  fee_type varchar [note: 'Category of the fee applied to the cross‑border payment.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee is subject to tax.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last modified the fee record.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the fee line within the transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text comments or audit notes related to the fee.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the fee must be reported to regulatory authorities (e.g., PSD2).']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fee has been reversed or cancelled.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fee reversal was processed.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current settlement state of the fee.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax amount applied to the fee, if taxable.']
  tax_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the tax amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fee record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every fee charged in connection with a cross-border payment or FX conversion event. Records the payment reference, fee type (SWIFT wire fee, correspondent bank charge, FX conversion fee, DCC margin, lifting charge), fee amount, fee currency, fee payer (sender/receiver/shared), fee collection method, and settlement status of the fee. Supports FX revenue recognition, correspondent bank fee reconciliation, and transparent fee disclosure to customers as required by PSD2 and Regulation E.'
}

Table fx.fx_reconciliation {
  fx_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each FX reconciliation record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder involved in the FX transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder involved in the FX transaction.']
  correspondent_bank_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the correspondent bank used for settlement.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the FX transaction.']
  reconciler_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Reconciliation tasks are assigned to a treasury analyst; audit trail requires analyst identifier.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch processing run that included this reconciliation.']
  audit_created_by varchar [note: 'User or system account that created the reconciliation record.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the detailed audit log entry for this reconciliation.']
  audit_updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system account that last updated the reconciliation record.']
  base_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the source currency.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation passed all regulatory compliance checks.']
  confirmed_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'FX amount confirmed by external counterparties (e.g., correspondent bank) in quote currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_pair varchar [note: 'Currency pair in ISO format (e.g., USD/EUR) for the conversion being reconciled.']
  exchange_rate_used decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied to convert the base amount to the quote amount for this reconciliation.']
  expected_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'FX amount that the internal system expects after conversion, expressed in quote currency.']
  fx_rate_source varchar [note: 'Name of the source providing the FX rate used in the conversion.']
  fx_rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FX rate was retrieved from the source.']
  fx_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the reconciliation record.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the record originates from a test or production environment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or explanations by the reconciliation analyst.']
  processing_time_ms varchar [note: 'Elapsed time in milliseconds for the reconciliation process to complete.']
  quote_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the target currency.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date on which the FX reconciliation was performed.']
  regulatory_report_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this reconciliation must be included in regulatory reporting (e.g., AML, SAR).']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve a variance or discrepancy.']
  rounding_method varchar [note: 'Method used to round the conversion result.']
  run_reference varchar [note: 'External reference code for the reconciliation run, used for audit and reporting.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the settlement amount is recorded.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settled FX transaction was posted to the ledger.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that generated the source FX data (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  total_transaction_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all original transaction amounts before conversion, expressed in base currency.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual FX conversion transactions included in this reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Difference between expected and confirmed amounts (expected – confirmed).']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variance expressed as a percentage of the expected amount.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why a variance occurred.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking the reconciliation of FX conversion amounts between the platform\'s internal records and external confirmations from correspondent banks, card schemes, and settlement agents. Captures the reconciliation run reference, reconciliation date, currency pair, expected conversion amount, confirmed conversion amount, variance amount, variance reason code, reconciliation status (matched, unmatched, in-dispute), and resolution action. Ensures FX P&L accuracy and supports audit requirements for cross-border settlement integrity.'
}

Table fx.beneficiary {
  beneficiary_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each beneficiary record.']
  account_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Beneficiary account currency must be tied to the currency master for compliance and conversion accuracy.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Beneficiary country is needed for sanctions screening and jurisdictional reporting.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Domestic bank account number of the beneficiary.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the beneficiary\'s street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the beneficiary\'s street address, if applicable.']
  bank_bic_swift varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC identifier of the beneficiary\'s bank.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the bank holding the beneficiary\'s account.']
  beneficiary_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the beneficiary (individual or corporate).']
  beneficiary_status varchar [note: 'Overall status indicating whether the beneficiary is active, inactive, suspended, or closed.']
  beneficiary_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the beneficiary is an individual, corporate, government, or nonprofit entity.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the beneficiary\'s address.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for communications with the beneficiary.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the beneficiary.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the beneficiary record was first created.']
  external_beneficiary_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned to the beneficiary by external correspondent banks or partners.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number of the beneficiary.']
  is_preferred boolean [note: 'True if the beneficiary is marked as preferred for recurring cross‑border payments.']
  last_payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent successful payment to this beneficiary.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the beneficiary\'s address.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score (0‑99.99) assigned based on AML and fraud analytics.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of sanctions list screening for the beneficiary.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the beneficiary data.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Tax ID number for corporate beneficiaries, used for reporting and compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the beneficiary record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of identity verification for the beneficiary.']

  Note: 'Master record for beneficiary entities registered on the platform for recurring cross-border payment execution. Captures the beneficiary name, beneficiary type (individual, corporate), country of residence, bank name, bank BIC/SWIFT code, account number or IBAN, account currency, beneficiary address, identity verification status, sanctions screening status, and last payment date. Serves as the trusted beneficiary registry enabling faster cross-border payment processing by pre-validating and pre-screening recurring payment recipients.'
}

Table fx.liquidity_position {
  liquidity_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the liquidity position record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Liquidity positions are reported per currency; FK to currency master enables consolidated risk metrics.']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.currency_pair. Business justification: Liquidity positions are tracked per currency; linking to the currency pair provides a normalized reference for the currency.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Treasury manager oversees liquidity positions; needed for daily liquidity reporting and limit monitoring.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether net liquidity fell below the minimum threshold.']
  committed_outflows decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total amount of pending outbound cross-border payments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was initially created in the data lake.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of liquidity event captured.']
  expected_inflows decimal(20,4) [note: 'Total amount of incoming settlements expected.']
  minimum_liquidity_threshold decimal(20,4) [note: 'Configured minimum liquidity threshold for the currency.']
  net_available_liquidity decimal(20,4) [note: 'Net liquidity after accounting for outflows and inflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments regarding the liquidity snapshot.']
  position_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the liquidity position snapshot was recorded.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the liquidity position record.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this snapshot is required for regulatory liquidity reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the liquidity snapshot (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform).']
  total_available_balance decimal(20,4) [note: 'Aggregate available balance across all nostro accounts for the currency.']
  treasury_action_taken varchar [note: 'Action taken by treasury in response to liquidity breach or status (e.g., pre-funding, hold).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the platform\'s intraday and end-of-day liquidity position across all currencies and nostro accounts. Records the position timestamp, currency, total available balance across nostro accounts, committed outflows (pending cross-border payments), expected inflows (incoming settlements), net available liquidity, minimum liquidity threshold, breach flag, and treasury action taken. Enables real-time treasury liquidity management, pre-funding decisions, and regulatory liquidity reporting.'
}

Table fx.swift_gpi_tracker {
  swift_gpi_tracker_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the SWIFT gpi tracking record.']
  transaction_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: GPI tracking requires the transaction currency to be validated against the currency master for reporting.']
  actual_credit_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when funds were actually credited to the beneficiary account.']
  beneficiary_bank_bic varchar [note: 'BIC of the beneficiary (receiving) bank.']
  beneficiary_iban varchar [note: 'IBAN of the receiving account.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Digital or physical channel through which the payment was initiated.']
  compliance_check_details varchar [note: 'Free‑text details of any compliance issues or rule matches.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of automated compliance screening (e.g., AML, sanctions).']
  conversion_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount after foreign exchange conversion, expressed in conversion_currency.']
  conversion_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the converted amount.']
  correlation_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used to correlate this record with upstream system logs.']
  current_processing_bank_bic varchar [note: 'BIC of the bank currently processing the payment in the gpi chain.']
  estimated_credit_time timestamp [note: 'System‑estimated timestamp when funds will be credited to the beneficiary.']
  exchange_rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FX rate was sourced.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which total_fees are expressed.']
  fx_rate_applied decimal(12,6) [note: 'Effective foreign exchange rate applied at the conversion point.']
  fx_rate_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the FX rate provider used for conversion.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True for payments that cross national borders.']
  is_test_transaction boolean [note: 'True if the transaction was generated for testing purposes.']
  number_of_hops varchar [note: 'Count of distinct banks or intermediaries the payment passed through.']
  originating_bank_bic varchar [note: 'BIC of the bank that originated the payment.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument or network used to move funds.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Merchant or sender supplied reference for the payment.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tracking record was first created in the data warehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this tracking record.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction must be reported to regulatory authorities.']
  sender_iban varchar [note: 'IBAN of the originating account sending the funds.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount credited to the beneficiary after fees and FX.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the settlement amount.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment was settled in the receiving bank.']
  status_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the current status was recorded.']
  swift_gpi_tracker_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the gpi transaction.']
  total_fees decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate fees deducted across all processing hops.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original amount of the payment in the transaction currency.']
  transaction_initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was initially submitted to the gpi network.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the payment type per ISO 20022.']
  uetr varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier assigned to each gpi transaction per SWIFT standards.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking the real-time status of cross-border payments through the SWIFT gpi (Global Payments Innovation) network. Captures the SWIFT UETR, payment reference, originating bank BIC, current processing bank BIC, beneficiary bank BIC, payment status (processing, credited, returned), status timestamp, deducted fees at each hop, FX rate applied at each conversion point, and estimated credit time. Enables end-to-end cross-border payment visibility for customers and operations teams per SWIFT gpi standards.'
}

Table fx.psd2_fx_disclosure {
  psd2_fx_disclosure_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each PSD2 FX disclosure record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who performed the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the cardholder who performed the transaction.']
  cross_border_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.cross_border_payment. Business justification: PSD2 FX disclosures are required per payment; linking associates each disclosure with its payment.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: PSD2 disclosure must reference the official currency master to show accurate exchange rates to cardholders.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the merchant acquiring the payment.']
  cardholder_acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Result of the cardholder\'s interaction with the disclosure (acknowledged, declined, or not shown).']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'Result of automated compliance validation for the disclosure (true if passed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the disclosure record was first persisted.']
  disclosure_channel varchar [note: 'Medium through which the FX disclosure was presented (online, mobile app, or POS terminal).']
  disclosure_reference varchar [note: 'Business‑visible reference code for the FX disclosure presented to the payer.']
  disclosure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the FX rate and fee disclosure was presented to the payer.']
  disclosure_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the disclosure template or rule set used.']
  estimated_recipient_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected amount the payee will receive after conversion, expressed in the recipient\'s currency.']
  exchange_rate_source varchar [note: 'Provider of the FX rate used for the disclosure.']
  exchange_rate_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the FX rate was retrieved from the source.']
  fee_amount_disclosed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fee amount disclosed alongside the FX rate, expressed in payer currency.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the disclosed fee amount is expressed.']
  mid_market_rate_shown decimal(18,9) [note: 'Mid‑market exchange rate displayed to the payer before the transaction.']
  payment_instrument_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment instrument used for the transaction.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment transaction to which this disclosure applies.']
  psd2_fx_disclosure_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the disclosure record.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this disclosure must be included in regulatory reporting feeds.']
  total_charges_disclosed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount (in payer currency) of all fees and margins disclosed alongside the FX rate.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original transaction amount before FX conversion, in the payer\'s currency.']
  transaction_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the transaction was initiated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the disclosure record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the mandatory pre-transaction FX rate and fee disclosure presented to payers for cross-border payments under PSD2 Article 45 and Regulation (EU) 2019/518. Records the disclosure reference, payment reference, disclosure timestamp, mid-market rate shown, total charges disclosed, estimated recipient amount, disclosure channel (online, mobile, terminal), cardholder acknowledgement status, and disclosure version. Provides the regulatory evidence that transparent FX cost disclosure was made prior to transaction execution as required by PSD2.'
}

Table fx.currency_pair_enrollment {
  currency_pair_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the currency_pair_enrollment association']
  currency_pair_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the currency pair reference entity']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the merchant master entity']
  currency_pair_enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current enrollment status (e.g., active, suspended, terminated)']
  dcc_margin_percentage decimal [note: 'Margin percentage applied to the merchant for this currency pair']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the merchant enrolled in the currency pair for DCC']

  Note: 'Represents the enrollment of a merchant in a specific currency pair for DCC services. Each record links one merchant to one currency pair and captures enrollment_date, dcc_margin_percentage, and status that are specific to the merchant‑pair relationship.. Existence Justification: Merchants actively enroll in one or more currency pairs for Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) and each currency pair can be offered to many merchants. The enrollment is managed as a distinct business entity with attributes such as enrollment date, DCC margin percentage, and status. This many‑to‑many relationship is therefore operational and requires its own association table.'
}

Table fx.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for party']
  parent_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent party in hierarchical relationships (e.g., corporate group).']
  beneficial_owner_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ownership percentage held by the ultimate beneficial owner.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the primary address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing with regulatory requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code of the party\'s primary location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: list of ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 codes — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the party record was first created in the system.']
  currency_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred settlement currency for the party.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to the party record.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of an individual party.']
  dcc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if Dynamic Currency Conversion is enabled for the party.']
  display_name varchar [note: 'Commonly used name or brand for the party.']
  external_party_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external partners or networks to reference the party.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number associated with the party.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date the organization was legally incorporated.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry classification code (e.g., NAICS).']
  is_blacklisted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party is on a sanctions or blacklist.']
  kyc_completion_date date [note: 'Date when KYC verification was completed.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Know Your Customer verification status.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last observed activity (transaction, login, etc.) for the party.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the most recent update.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the party as registered.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks or comments about the party.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Category of the party indicating its role in the payments ecosystem.']
  payment_limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed transaction amount per day for the party.']
  payment_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the payment limit amount.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the primary address.']
  primary_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the party\'s primary physical address.']
  primary_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the address (optional).']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for electronic communications.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number in E.164 format.']
  region varchar [note: 'State, province or region of the primary address.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official business registration number.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the party based on compliance and transaction history.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for settling payments.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system of the party record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the party record.']
  swift_bic varchar [note: 'SWIFT BIC code for the party\'s bank (if applicable).']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (e.g., EIN, VAT) for the party.']
  ultimate_beneficial_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the ultimate beneficial owner for the party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the party record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for party. Referenced by sender_party_id.'
}

Table fx.treasury_unit {
  treasury_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for treasury_unit']
  parent_treasury_unit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on treasury_unit (parent_treasury_unit_id)']
  balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current monetary balance held by the treasury unit in its functional currency.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the treasury unit with regulatory requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the primary jurisdiction for the treasury unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the treasury unit record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure authorized for the treasury unit.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the functional currency used by the treasury unit.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the treasury unit became operationally effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the treasury unit ceased to be effective; null if still active.']
  is_cross_border_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the treasury unit can process cross‑border FX transactions.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit performed on the treasury unit.']
  manager_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the treasury unit manager for operational communications.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the treasury unit.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region where the treasury unit primarily operates.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the treasury unit based on regulatory and internal assessments.']
  settlement_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days defining the settlement cycle for the treasury unit.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the treasury unit.']
  treasury_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal manager responsible for the treasury unit.']
  unit_code varchar [note: 'External or legacy code used to reference the treasury unit in operational systems.']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the treasury unit indicating its primary function.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the treasury unit record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for treasury_unit. Referenced by treasury_unit_id.'
}

Table fx.counterparty {
  counterparty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for counterparty']
  parent_counterparty_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on counterparty (parent_counterparty_id)']
  address varchar [note: 'Full mailing address of the counterparty, including street, city, postal code, and country.']
  aml_last_checked timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the AML watchlist status was last verified.']
  aml_watchlist_flag boolean [note: 'True if the counterparty appears on any anti‑money‑laundering watchlist.']
  blacklisting_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the counterparty was blacklisted.']
  compliance_last_checked timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent compliance verification.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall regulatory compliance status of the counterparty.']
  counterparty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the counterparty based on its role in the payments ecosystem.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the counterparty\'s primary jurisdiction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the counterparty record was first created in the system.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for electronic communications.']
  is_blacklisted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is prohibited from transacting.']
  kyc_last_updated timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent KYC status change.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Know‑Your‑Customer verification status of the counterparty.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent risk/compliance review of the counterparty.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name of the counterparty as per legal documents.']
  limit_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount allowed per the defined limit period.']
  limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the transaction limit amount.']
  limit_effective_from date [note: 'Start date when the transaction limit becomes active.']
  limit_effective_until date [note: 'End date when the transaction limit expires (null if open‑ended).']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Temporal granularity of the transaction limit.']
  name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the counterparty (person or organization).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the counterparty.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the counterparty was first onboarded to the platform.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for voice contact.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for primary communications with the counterparty.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification assigned to the counterparty.']
  risk_rating_date date [note: 'Date when the current risk rating was assigned.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk score (0‑99.99) derived from internal risk models.']
  settlement_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number used for settlement of funds with the counterparty.']
  settlement_bank_iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number for the settlement bank.']
  settlement_bank_swift varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC identifier of the settlement bank.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for settlement transactions with the counterparty.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream operational system that supplied the record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the counterparty record.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status, if applicable.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is exempt from tax withholding.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for tax exemption status, if applicable.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the counterparty.']
  tax_residency_country varchar [note: 'Country of tax residence for withholding and reporting purposes.']
  trading_name varchar [note: 'Public-facing or brand name used by the counterparty in transactions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the counterparty record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for counterparty. Referenced by counterparty_id.'
}

Table ledger.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the GL account record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Consolidated financial statements require each GL account to reference a currency entity for accurate reporting and conversion.']
  parent_account_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent account in the hierarchical chart of accounts.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping label used for reporting roll‑ups.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the GL account.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the GL account as used in accounting systems.']
  account_owner varchar [note: 'Organizational unit or department responsible for the account.']
  account_subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing more granular detail (e.g., cash, accounts receivable, prepaid expense).']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the account according to accounting fundamentals.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the cost center to which the account is allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the GL account record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the account is retired; null for open‑ended accounts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the account becomes active for posting.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the account in an external financial system (e.g., legacy ERP).']
  financial_statement varchar [note: 'Indicates which primary financial statement the account belongs to.']
  gl_account_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or usage of the account.']
  gl_account_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the GL account.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the account within the chart of accounts hierarchy (0 = top level).']
  is_budgeted boolean [note: 'True if the account is included in the formal budgeting process.']
  is_consolidation boolean [note: 'True if the account participates in legal entity consolidation reporting.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for intercompany transactions.']
  normal_balance varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit or credit balance.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the profit center associated with the account.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for consolidated reporting of this account.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the business segment (e.g., retail, corporate) linked to the account.']
  sox_control_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates the SOX compliance requirement for the account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the GL account record.']

  Note: 'Chart of accounts master record defining every general ledger account used across the enterprise. Captures account number, account type (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense), normal balance direction, account hierarchy level, parent account reference, financial statement mapping (P&L vs balance sheet), currency, consolidation flag, intercompany flag, and SOX control classification. This is the SSOT for all GL account definitions in Oracle Financials and the authoritative reference for all journal entry postings.'
}

Table ledger.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each cost center record.']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate expenses to the cost center.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total budget allocated to this cost center when part of a shared pool.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center budget was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the cost center budget.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned monetary budget allocated to the cost center for the defined period.']
  budget_period varchar [note: 'Frequency of the budget allocation.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the budget amount applies.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the cost center with internal policies and external regulations.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used in accounting and reporting systems.']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the cost center.']
  cost_center_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of cost centers for consolidated reporting.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost center.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center for reporting and allocation purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the cost center record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the budget.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level applied to the cost center data for governance.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department responsible for the cost center.']
  division varchar [note: 'Division within the business unit to which the cost center belongs.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center becomes effective for budgeting and reporting.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center in external ERP or financial systems.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'GL account number associated with the cost center for posting transactions.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of the cost center within the organizational hierarchy.']
  is_shared boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost center is shared across multiple business units.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center was last reviewed for budgeting or compliance.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the cost center manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the manager responsible for the cost center.']
  owning_business_unit varchar [note: 'Top‑level business unit that owns the cost center.']
  parent_cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the immediate parent cost center in the hierarchy, if any.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for the cost center based on financial exposure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the cost center record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the cost center record.']

  Note: 'Organizational cost center master record representing a financial responsibility unit within the payments fintech enterprise. Captures cost center code, name, owning business unit, division, department, manager, active status, budget period, currency, and allocation basis. Used for expense allocation, P&L reporting by business unit, and management accounting. Aligns with Oracle Financials cost center segment in the accounting flexfield.'
}

  consolidation_group_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.consolidation_group']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: KYC and regulatory filings require each legal entity to be linked to its country of registration.']
  parent_entity_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent legal entity, if any.']
  primary_ultimate_parent_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the top‑most parent in the corporate hierarchy.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: AML/CTF compliance mandates mapping each entity to the governing regulatory jurisdiction.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the legal entity.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  aml_screening_status varchar [note: 'Result of anti‑money‑laundering screening for the entity.']
  annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue reported for the most recent fiscal year.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the legal entity record was first created in the system.']

  dissolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the entity was legally dissolved, if applicable.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the entity became operational for reporting purposes.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for official communications.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Number of full‑time equivalent employees employed by the entity.']
  financial_reporting_period varchar [note: 'Standard period used for financial statements (Fiscal Year or Quarter).']
  functional_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for day‑to‑day operations of the entity.']
  iban varchar [note: 'Standardized bank account number for international transfers.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date on which the entity was legally formed.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'Standard industry classification code for the entity\'s primary business.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance or financial audit.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Common name used to refer to the legal entity in business contexts.']
  legal_entity_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the entity within the organization.']
  legal_entity_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the legal structure of the entity.']
  ofac_sanctions_status varchar [note: 'Result of OFAC sanctions screening for the entity.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the entity.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the entity\'s address.']
  registration_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Country or region where the entity is legally registered.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration identifier assigned by the jurisdiction of incorporation.']
  regulatory_license_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the license authorizing the entity to provide payment services.']
  regulatory_license_type varchar [note: 'Category of the regulatory licence held by the entity.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which the entity reports financial results for consolidation.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Internal risk assessment category for the entity.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the entity\'s address.']
  swift_bic varchar [note: 'International bank identifier used for cross‑border payments.']
  tax_id_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the tax identification number format.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the entity (e.g., EIN, VAT number).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the legal entity record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the legal entity.']

  Note: 'Legal entity master record representing each incorporated company, subsidiary, or branch within the payments fintech corporate group. Captures legal entity name, registration number, jurisdiction of incorporation, tax identification number, functional currency, consolidation group, SWIFT BIC, regulatory license references, and OFAC/sanctions screening status. Serves as the top-level organizational anchor for all financial reporting and intercompany accounting.'
}

Table ledger.accounting_period {
  accounting_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the accounting period record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Period‑level reporting and closing balances depend on the period\'s functional currency, mandated by accounting standards.']
  ledger_config_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ledger to which this accounting period belongs.']
  ledger_ledger_config_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ledger to which this accounting period belongs.']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the period was closed for posting.']
  closing_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total ledger balance recorded at the moment the period was closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accounting period record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date on which the accounting period ends.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year to which the period belongs, e.g., 2023.']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period is used for post‑close adjustments rather than regular transaction posting.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period has been archived for historical reference and is read‑only.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'True if this period is the active period for posting at the current point in time.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the period was opened for posting.']
  period_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the period\'s primary purpose, e.g., financial reporting, tax reporting, or internal reporting.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Business-facing code that uniquely identifies the period within the fiscal calendar, often used in journal entries and reporting.']
  period_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text description providing additional context about the period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the period, e.g., "FY2023 Q1" or "2023‑01".']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the period within its fiscal year (1‑12 for months, 1‑4 for quarters).']
  period_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric order of the period within the fiscal year, useful for sorting (e.g., 1 for Jan, 2 for Feb).']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the period: open (accepting postings), closed (postings stopped), locked (no changes allowed), or pending (not yet active).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the period granularity: month, quarter, year, or adjustment.']
  period_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the period record to support re‑open/re‑close scenarios and audit trails.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar date on which the accounting period begins.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last modified the accounting period record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the accounting period record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the accounting period record.']

  Note: 'Financial accounting period master defining the fiscal calendar used for GL posting, period-end close, and financial reporting. Captures period name, fiscal year, period number, period type (month, quarter, year), open/closed status, period open date, period close date, adjustment period flag, and the ledger it belongs to. Controls which periods accept journal entry postings and drives the financial period close workflow.'
}

Table ledger.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the journal entry record.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Journal entries are posted to a specific accounting period; FK supports period‑level queries.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the journal entry for posting.']
  approver_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the journal entry for posting.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Journal entries are posted in a specific currency; linking enables automated FX conversion.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who prepared the journal entry.']
  primary_journal_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who prepared the journal entry.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Required for FX gain/loss reconciliation report linking each journal entry to the exact FX rate used in the conversion.']
  related_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the counter‑party (merchant, cardholder, or other) associated with the entry.']
  reversal_of_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original journal entry that this entry reverses, if applicable.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch that contains this entry.']
  accounting_date date [note: 'The fiscal date to which the entry is posted for financial reporting.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for the adjustment.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the cost center charged by the entry.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry record was first created in the system.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department associated with the entry.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the journal entry, often used in accounting reports.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the journal entry source such as payment processing, settlement, manual adjustment, intercompany, or recurring.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied when the entry involves a foreign currency.']
  exchange_rate_date date [note: 'Date for which the exchange rate is applicable.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year of the entry.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'True if the entry records an intercompany transaction.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'True if the entry represents an accounting adjustment.']
  journal_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or nature of the journal entry.']
  journal_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the journal entry.']
  ledger_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ledger (e.g., general, subsidiary) where the entry is posted.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and adjustments, used for reporting.']
  period varchar [note: 'Accounting period identifier (e.g., 2023‑04) to which the entry belongs.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entry was posted to the general ledger.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entry is a reversal of a prior entry.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Analytical segment identifier for reporting (e.g., product line, geography).']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module within the source system that produced the entry.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the journal entry.']
  sox_control_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SOX control that governs this entry.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component captured in the entry, if applicable.']
  total_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate credit amount for the journal entry, expressed in the entry currency.']
  total_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate debit amount for the journal entry, expressed in the entry currency.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the underlying business event occurred (e.g., payment authorization).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the journal entry record.']

  Note: 'General ledger journal entry header record representing a balanced accounting transaction posted to the GL. Captures journal entry number, journal source (payment processing, settlement, manual, intercompany, recurring), journal category, accounting date, currency, total debit amount, total credit amount, posting status, reversal flag, reversal period, preparer, approver, SOX control reference, and description. The primary transactional record for all financial activity in Oracle Financials GL module.'
}

Table ledger.journal_line {
  journal_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each line item within a general ledger journal entry.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Journal lines inherit the period of their parent entry; explicit FK improves traceability.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: SOX audit requires tracking which employee created each journal line; adding created_by_employee_id links to employee.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.gl_account']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent journal entry to which this line belongs.']
  journal_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent journal entry to which this line belongs.']
  rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fx.fx_rate. Business justification: Needed for detailed audit of each journal line recording foreign exchange amounts, supporting regulatory FX reporting.']
  transaction_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Line‑level transactions may have a distinct transaction currency; required for accurate posting.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the adjustment line (e.g., correction, reversal, write‑off).']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business unit owning the transaction for internal reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the cost center to which the transaction cost is allocated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the journal line was initially created in the system.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount credited on this line, expressed in the functional currency of the entity.']
  debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount debited on this line, expressed in the functional currency of the entity.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the functional currency used for accounting.']
  fx_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the transaction amount from transaction currency to functional currency for this line.']
  fx_rate_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the foreign‑exchange rate used for conversion.']
  fx_rate_source varchar [note: 'Name of the provider or system that supplied the foreign‑exchange rate.']
  intercompany_entity_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related inter‑company entity for intercompany journal entries.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'True if the line represents an adjustment (correction, reversal, write‑off) rather than a regular transaction.']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (e.g., subsidiary) owning the transaction.']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Narrative text describing the purpose of the line item.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the line within the journal entry.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status indicating whether the line is posted, pending, reversed, or encountered an error.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the line is posted to the general ledger.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Code of the project or initiative associated with the line for cost allocation.']
  reconciliation_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier (such as ARN, MID, or payment token) used to reconcile this line with source systems.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Code used to group lines for segment‑level financial reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream operational system that originated the journal line.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary tax amount associated with the line, expressed in functional currency.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax rule or jurisdiction applicable to this line.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or automated process that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the journal line.']

  Note: 'Individual debit or credit line within a GL journal entry. Captures line number, GL account, cost center, legal entity, project, intercompany entity, debit amount, credit amount, functional currency amount, transaction currency amount, FX rate, line description, tax code, and reconciliation reference. Each journal entry must have balanced lines (total debits = total credits). Supports drill-through from financial statements to source transactions.'
}

Table ledger.gl_balance {
  gl_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each GL balance snapshot.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center associated with the balance for internal reporting.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Balance calculations and revaluations need a normalized currency reference for audit trails.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the GL account for which the balance is recorded.']
  ledger_config_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ledger (e.g., primary ledger, sub‑ledger) to which the balance belongs.']
  ledger_ledger_config_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ledger (e.g., primary ledger, sub‑ledger) to which the balance belongs.']
  balance_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the balance (e.g., posted after close, provisional during interim).']
  balance_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the balance is in functional currency or reporting currency.']
  closing_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance after applying period debits and credits; used for financial statements.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Rate applied to convert functional currency balance to reporting currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Numeric period within the fiscal year (e.g., 1‑12 for monthly, 1‑4 for quarterly).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year of the accounting period (e.g., 2024).']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'True if the balance aggregates multiple legal entities; otherwise false.']
  last_reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the balance was last validated against source ledgers.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance amount carried forward from the prior period.']
  period_credits decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate credit amount posted to the account in the period.']
  period_debits decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate debit amount posted to the account in the period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reporting period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reporting period.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent reconciliation process.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the balance snapshot was initially persisted.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the balance record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform) that produced the record.']
  translated_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Closing balance expressed in the reporting currency after applying exchange rate.']

  Note: 'Period-end GL account balance record capturing the opening balance, period activity (debits and credits), and closing balance for each GL account per accounting period per ledger. Supports financial statement generation, variance analysis, and period-over-period comparisons. Captures account, period, ledger, currency type (functional/reporting), beginning balance, period debit, period credit, ending balance, and translated balance for multi-currency reporting.'
}

Table ledger.ledger_config {
  ledger_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the ledger configuration record.']
  functional_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Ledger configuration defines the functional currency used for all postings, required for regulatory compliance.']
  accounting_calendar varchar [note: 'Accounting calendar associated with the ledger.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent audit of the ledger configuration (e.g., passed, failed, pending).']
  audit_trail_enabled boolean [note: 'Specifies whether changes to this ledger are captured in an audit trail.']
  chart_of_accounts varchar [note: 'Reference to the chart of accounts used by the ledger.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) that this ledger must satisfy (e.g., SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ledger configuration record was created.']
  currency_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places used for monetary amounts in the ledger.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the ledger becomes effective.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the ledger ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  fiscal_year_start_month varchar [note: 'Month (1‑12) that marks the start of the fiscal year for this ledger.']
  intercompany_settlement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether intercompany transactions are settled in this ledger.']
  is_consolidation_ledger boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ledger is used for consolidation reporting.']
