  is_multi_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates if the ledger supports multiple functional currencies.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the ledger configuration.']
  ledger_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the ledger.']
  ledger_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the ledger purpose and scope.']
  ledger_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ledger.']
  ledger_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the ledger.']
  ledger_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or department responsible for the ledger.']
  ledger_timezone varchar [note: 'Timezone in which ledger processing timestamps are recorded.']
  ledger_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ledger (e.g., primary, secondary, reporting).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of accounting periods used by the ledger.']
  posting_rule varchar [note: 'Rule that determines when transactions are recognized in the ledger.']
  primary_legal_entity varchar [note: 'Legal entity that owns the ledger.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for external reporting from this ledger.']
  retained_earnings_account varchar [note: 'Account code where retained earnings are posted.']
  subledger_method varchar [note: 'Method used for subledger accounting.']
  tax_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the ledger is used for tax reporting purposes.']
  translation_method varchar [note: 'Method applied to translate foreign currency balances.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ledger configuration.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the ledger configuration for change management.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the ledger configuration.']

  Note: 'Ledger configuration master defining each accounting ledger (primary, secondary, reporting) within Oracle Financials. Captures ledger name, ledger type, chart of accounts, accounting calendar, functional currency, subledger accounting method, consolidation ledger flag, reporting currency, translation method, and retained earnings account. A payments fintech enterprise typically maintains multiple ledgers for different legal entities and regulatory reporting jurisdictions.'
}

Table ledger.subledger_entry {
  subledger_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the subledger entry record.']
  cardholder_cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder involved in the transaction.']
  cardholder_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cardholder involved in the transaction.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Sub‑ledger entries must be tied to a currency for correct posting and settlement.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with the transaction.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement run associated with this entry.']
  transaction_batch_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch that includes this entry.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating transaction that generated this subledger entry.']
  accounting_date date [note: 'Date on which the entry is accounted in the ledger, per accounting period.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the adjustment, if applicable.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing audit events for the entry.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates if the entry triggered any compliance checks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the subledger entry record was created in the data lake.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Credit monetary amount for the entry.']
  debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Debit monetary amount for the entry.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the subledger entry, often sequential per source system.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the underlying business event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of accounting event represented by this subledger entry.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied if transaction involves currency conversion.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'Chart of accounts code to which this entry is posted.']
  gl_derivation_rule varchar [note: 'Rule applied to derive the GL account from transaction attributes.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry is an adjustment (true) or original transaction (false).']
  is_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry has been reconciled with the GL.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence order of the subledger entry within its parent transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or remarks.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original transaction amount before any conversion.']
  original_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the original amount before conversion.']
  payment_instrument_token varchar [note: 'Token representing the payment instrument used, per tokenization standards.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of posting this entry to the General Ledger.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric quantity associated with the entry, e.g., number of units or amount of service.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the entry was reconciled.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned to the transaction at time of entry.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module within the source system (e.g., Authorization Engine, Settlement Engine).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the subledger entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the subledger entry record.']

  Note: 'Subledger accounting entry record generated by operational subsystems (payment gateway, settlement engine, merchant billing, dispute management) before posting to the GL. Captures subledger application (AP, AR, FA, payment processing), event type, event date, accounting date, transaction reference, GL account derivation rule applied, debit/credit amounts, currency, and transfer-to-GL status. Provides the audit trail linking operational transactions to GL postings per SOX requirements.'
}

Table ledger.revenue_recognition_schedule {
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the revenue recognition schedule record.']
  audit_trail_id varchar [note: 'Reference to audit trail entry for changes.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying contract or agreement generating the revenue.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Revenue schedules allocate amounts in a specific currency; compliance with ASC 606/IFRS 15 requires currency linkage.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the schedule.']
  performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific performance obligation within the contract.']
  primary_revenue_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the schedule.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last updated the schedule.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Financial accounting period associated with the schedule.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of total price allocated to this schedule.']
  business_identifier varchar [note: 'External reference number or code for the revenue schedule as used by finance.']
  classification_or_type varchar [note: 'Category of revenue type for the schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition starts according to the contract.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when revenue recognition ends; null if indefinite.']
  frequency_interval varchar [note: 'Numeric interval for the frequency (e.g., every 2 months).']
  is_partial_recognition_allowed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if partial recognition is permitted before full amount.']
  is_prorated boolean [note: 'Indicates if the schedule uses prorated amounts.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the revenue schedule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the schedule.']
  recognition_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date for the final recognition event.']
  recognition_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which revenue is recognized.']
  recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue.']
  recognition_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the first recognition event.']
  recognized_to_date_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount recognized to date.']
  total_transaction_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contract transaction price allocated to this performance obligation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule.']

  Note: 'Revenue recognition schedule master defining the rules and timeline for recognizing deferred revenue in compliance with ASC 606 / IFRS 15. Captures contract reference, performance obligation description, total transaction price, allocated amount, recognition method (point-in-time vs over-time), recognition start date, recognition end date, recognition frequency, and recognized-to-date amount. Critical for payments fintech revenue streams including MDR, SaaS platform fees, and BNPL origination fees.'
}

Table ledger.revenue_recognition_event {
  revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the revenue recognition event.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Each recognition event records currency to calculate realized revenue and tax impact.']
  gl_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GL journal entry created for this revenue recognition.']
  revenue_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the revenue line item associated with this event.']
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the revenue schedule to which this recognition event belongs.']
  reversal_event_revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [note: 'If this event reverses a prior recognition, reference to that event.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying payment transaction linked to this revenue event.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period code (e.g., 2023Q1) for the recognized revenue.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the adjustment, if applicable.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the revenue recognition event.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the event was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User or system that approved the revenue recognition.']
  audit_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form comment for audit or review purposes.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates any compliance action required for this event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue recognition event record was created.']
  cumulative_recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount recognized to date for the schedule, including this event.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied if the revenue is recorded in a foreign currency.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event is an adjustment to a prior recognition.']
  is_foreign_currency boolean [note: 'True if the recognized amount was originally in a foreign currency.']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'True if the event was entered manually rather than automatically.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the underlying transaction before conversion.']
  posted_date date [note: 'Date the revenue recognition entry was posted to the accounting system.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the revenue recognition event.']
  recognition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue was recognized.']
  recognition_trigger varchar [note: 'Mechanism that caused the revenue to be recognized (time‑based, milestone, or usage).']
  recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount recognized in this event.']
  regulatory_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for regulatory reporting of the revenue event.']
  remaining_deferred_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Deferred revenue remaining after this recognition event.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for reversing the revenue recognition.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the revenue was settled in the ledger.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating record in the source system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that generated the revenue recognition event (e.g., ERP, Data Warehouse).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the recognized revenue.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Standard tax code applied to the revenue.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the revenue recognition event record.']

  Note: 'Individual revenue recognition event record capturing each instance of revenue being recognized against a schedule. Captures schedule reference, recognition date, accounting period, recognized amount, cumulative recognized amount, remaining deferred amount, GL journal entry reference, recognition trigger (time-based, milestone, usage), and recognition status. Provides the transactional audit trail for ASC 606 compliance and SOX revenue controls.'
}

Table ledger.intercompany_transaction {
  intercompany_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the intercompany transaction record.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Intercompany transactions are settled within a fiscal period; FK enables period‑based reconciliation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center associated with the transaction for internal reporting.']
  primary_intercompany_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity that originated the intercompany transaction.']
  receiving_entity_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity that is the counter‑party to the transaction.']
  related_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original transaction when this record is a reversal or adjustment.']
  transaction_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Intercompany amounts are settled in a transaction currency; proper FX handling needs a currency reference.']
  amount_adjustment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of fees, taxes, or other adjustments applied to the gross amount.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value before any adjustments, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after adjustments; the amount that is settled between entities.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the internal approval process for the transaction.']
  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'True when the transaction should be eliminated during consolidation to avoid double counting.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied when converting from transaction currency to reporting currency, if applicable.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any fee charged as part of the intercompany transaction (e.g., service fee).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1‑4) of the transaction.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the transaction belongs.']
  intercompany_account_pair varchar [note: 'Combined GL account codes representing the debit and credit sides of the intercompany posting (e.g., "4000-5000").']
  intercompany_transaction_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or nature of the intercompany transaction.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the transaction within the intercompany workflow.']
  netting_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction is eligible for netting against other intercompany balances.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction is posted to the general ledger.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to external documents such as an Acquirer Reference Number (ARN) or internal voucher.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the transaction is a reversal of a previously posted intercompany entry.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the intercompany amount is settled between entities.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the transaction (e.g., ERP, Payment Gateway).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the transaction, if applicable.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the transaction (e.g., journal reference).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the intercompany transaction was recorded in the source system.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the intercompany activity such as loan, service charge, royalty, cost allocation, or settlement.']

  Note: 'Intercompany transaction record capturing financial activity between legal entities within the payments fintech corporate group. Captures transaction type (loan, service charge, royalty, cost allocation, settlement), initiating entity, receiving entity, transaction date, currency, amount, intercompany account pair, netting eligibility, elimination flag, and approval status. Essential for consolidated financial reporting and intercompany reconciliation across the global payments network.'
}

Table ledger.period_close_task {
  period_close_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the period close task record.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Period tasks belong to a specific accounting period; linking enables period‑based reporting and scheduling.']
  dependent_task_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a prerequisite task that must be completed first.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team responsible for executing the task.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or team responsible for executing the task.']
  actual_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Recorded effort spent on the task, expressed in hours.']
  blocking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task blocks downstream close activities.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the task was finished.']
  compliance_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the task requires a compliance or regulatory review.']
  due_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the task should be completed.']
  estimated_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected effort required to complete the task, expressed in hours.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the task is deemed critical for timely close.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the latest update.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the task.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Business role of the task owner.']
  period_close_task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the task.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the task.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the task record was initially created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the task record.']
  regulatory_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required regulatory review for the task.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the task\'s impact on the close process.']
  sign_off_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the sign‑off record or approval document for the task.']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work to be performed for the task.']
  task_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the close task (e.g., "Month-End Revenue Reconciliation").']
  task_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the task within the overall close workflow.']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Category of the task within the financial close workflow.']

  Note: 'Financial period close task record representing a discrete step in the month-end, quarter-end, or year-end close checklist. Captures task name, task type (reconciliation, accrual, allocation, consolidation, reporting), assigned owner, due date, completion date, status, dependency on prior tasks, blocking flag, and sign-off reference. Supports the structured financial close workflow required for SOX compliance and timely financial reporting in a payments fintech enterprise.'
}

Table ledger.account_reconciliation {
  account_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the account reconciliation record.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Account reconciliation records are generated per accounting period; FK provides direct period context.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Reconciliation reports must state the currency of the GL account to validate balances.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the General Ledger account being reconciled.']
  prepared_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Reconciliation process records the employee who prepared the reconciliation; required for internal control reports.']
  account_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reconciliation record.']
  adjusted_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance after applying reconciling adjustments.']
  aging_days varchar [note: 'Number of days unresolved reconciling items have been outstanding.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'SOX‑compliant approval outcome for the reconciliation.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation was approved or rejected.']
  closed_reconciling_items_count varchar [note: 'Count of reconciling items that have been resolved.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) governing the reconciliation (e.g., SOX).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to translate foreign‑currency balances to the functional currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Numeric period within the fiscal year (e.g., month number).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the reconciliation belongs.']
  gl_balance_book decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance of the GL account as recorded in the books for the period.']
  is_auto_reconciled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the reconciliation was performed automatically by the system.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the GL account is part of a consolidated reporting hierarchy.']
  last_reconciled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful reconciliation run for this account/period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations entered by the preparer or reviewer.']
  open_reconciling_items_count varchar [note: 'Count of reconciling items that remain unresolved.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the reconciliation period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the reconciliation period.']
  reconciliation_cycle varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency of the reconciliation (e.g., monthly).']
  reconciliation_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the reconciliation.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Business‑visible identifier or reference number for the reconciliation run.']
  reconciling_items_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all reconciling items identified for the period.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who reviewed the reconciliation.']
  sign_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the reconciliation was formally signed off.']
  supporting_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., URL or document ID) to supporting evidence for the reconciliation.']
  total_reconciling_items_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of monetary values of all reconciling items for the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between book balance and adjusted balance.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the variance (e.g., timing difference, error).']

  Note: 'GL account reconciliation record capturing the formal balance reconciliation performed for each GL account at period end. Captures account, accounting period, GL balance per books, reconciling items, adjusted balance, supporting documentation reference, preparer, reviewer, approval status, and sign-off date. Mandatory SOX control for all balance sheet accounts. Tracks open reconciling items and aging of unresolved differences.'
}

Table ledger.accrual_entry {
  accrual_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the accrual journal entry.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center associated with the accrual.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GL account to which the accrual is posted.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (company) that owns the accrual.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reporting segment (e.g., business line or geography) for the accrual.']
  accrual_basis varchar [note: 'Accounting basis applied to the entry (accrual, cash, or adjustment).']
  accrual_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the accrual entry.']
  accrual_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accrual entry.']
  accrual_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier or code for the accrual entry, used for tracking and reconciliation.']
  accrual_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the accounting period for which the accrual is recognized.']
  accrual_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the accounting period for which the accrual is recognized.']
  accrual_type varchar [note: 'Category of accrual such as expense, revenue, interchange income, scheme fee, or settlement float.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Adjustment component (e.g., tax, fee, discount) applied to the gross amount.']
  calculation_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the calculation method or model used to derive the accrual amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the accrual entry record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the accrual amounts.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the business event that triggered the accrual (e.g., period‑end processing).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied when converting foreign currency amounts to the functional currency.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before any adjustments, expressed in the entry currency.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'True if the accrual relates to an intercompany transaction.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accrual amount is an estimate (true) or a final calculated amount (false).']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after adjustments, the amount posted to the GL.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the accrual entry is posted to the ledger.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the accrual entry is posted.']
  reversal_journal_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the journal entry that reverses this accrual, if applicable.']
  reversal_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period when the accrual is scheduled to be reversed.']
  reversal_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period when the accrual is scheduled to be reversed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the accrual entry (e.g., Transaction Processing Platform, Payment Gateway, ERP).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the accrual, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the accrual entry record.']

  Note: 'Accrual journal entry record capturing period-end accruals for expenses and revenues not yet invoiced or received. Captures accrual type (expense accrual, revenue accrual, interchange accrual, scheme fee accrual), accrual basis, accrual amount, currency, GL account, cost center, accrual period, reversal period, reversal journal reference, and supporting calculation methodology. Payments fintech-specific accruals include interchange income, scheme fee accruals, and settlement float accruals.'
}

Table ledger.expense_allocation {
  expense_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each expense allocation record.']
  allocation_journal_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the journal entry created for this allocation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the GL account used for the allocation.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the journal entry created for this allocation.']
  source_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the cost center from which the expense is allocated.']
  target_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the cost center receiving the allocated expense.']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Basis used to calculate the allocation (headcount, total payment volume, revenue, or fixed percentage).']
  allocation_basis_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric value associated with the allocation basis (e.g., percentage or factor).']
  allocation_journal_reference varchar [note: 'External reference (e.g., journal number) for the allocation journal entry.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation was generated automatically by the system or entered manually.']
  allocation_note varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or justification of the allocation.']
  allocation_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the fiscal period for which the expense is allocated.']
  allocation_period_start date [note: 'First day of the fiscal period for which the expense is allocated.']
  allocation_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the allocation for audit and traceability.']
  allocation_rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the allocation rule applied (e.g., "Headcount Pro Rata").']
  allocation_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the allocation rule to support rule changes over time.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the allocation.']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the allocation was executed in the system.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework governing the allocation record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocation amounts (e.g., "USD").']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Date‑time from which the allocation becomes effective for reporting.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Date‑time after which the allocation is no longer effective (nullable).']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation is between legal entities within the same corporate group.']
  target_allocation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount allocated to the target cost center.']
  total_allocation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary amount allocated from the source cost center.']
  transfer_pricing_flag boolean [note: 'True if the allocation is subject to transfer‑pricing rules for cross‑border reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation record.']

  Note: 'Expense allocation record capturing the distribution of shared costs across cost centers, legal entities, or business units using defined allocation rules. Captures allocation rule name, source cost center, target cost centers, allocation basis (headcount, TPV, revenue, fixed percentage), allocation period, total amount allocated, per-target amounts, GL accounts, and allocation journal reference. Supports management P&L reporting and transfer pricing compliance for cross-border payments entities.'
}

Table ledger.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each fixed asset record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Asset acquisition cost and depreciation are currency‑specific; regulatory asset registers require a currency FK.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.vendor']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recorded to date for the asset.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original cost incurred to acquire the asset, expressed in the functional currency.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was purchased or otherwise acquired.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the asset type.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional details about the asset.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the asset (e.g., "POS Terminal 12A").']
  asset_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number of the asset.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric tag assigned to the asset for inventory tracking.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Specific type within the category (e.g., "POS Terminal", "HSM", "Server", "Software License").']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier to which the asset expense is charged.']
  custodian varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team responsible for day‑to‑day care of the asset.']
  depreciation_end_date date [note: 'Projected date when the asset will be fully depreciated.']
  depreciation_expense_current_period decimal(18,2) [note: 'Depreciation expense recognized for the current accounting period.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to calculate depreciation.']
  depreciation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation calculations begin.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the asset was removed from service or sold.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of the asset.']
  disposal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the disposal process.']
  fixed_asset_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the asset.']
  geographic_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the asset\'s primary location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|GBR|DEU|FRA|JPN|AUS|CHN|IND|BRA — promote to reference product]']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount covered by the insurance policy.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of any insurance covering the asset.']
  insurance_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the insurer providing coverage for the asset.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance activity was performed.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical site or room where the asset resides (e.g., "Data Center 3 – Rack 12").']
  maintenance_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost incurred for the most recent maintenance activity.']
  maintenance_schedule varchar [note: 'Regular maintenance cadence for the asset.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that produced the asset.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model identifier assigned by the manufacturer.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying amount of the asset (cost minus accumulated depreciation).']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Business department that owns the asset.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the purchase order that funded the acquisition.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the asset record.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated economic life of the asset expressed in years.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer\'s warranty on the asset expires.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset master record for capitalized assets owned by the payments fintech enterprise including data center hardware, HSM devices, POS terminal infrastructure, and software licenses. Captures asset number, asset description, asset category, acquisition date, acquisition cost, accumulated depreciation, net book value, depreciation method, useful life, salvage value, location, custodian, and disposal status. Managed in Oracle Financials Fixed Assets (FA) module.'
}

Table ledger.depreciation_schedule {
  depreciation_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the depreciation schedule record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center associated with the depreciation expense.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Depreciation expense is recorded in a currency; linking supports consolidated reporting.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fixed asset to which this depreciation schedule applies.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'General Ledger account used to post the depreciation expense.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Journal entry identifier that records the depreciation transaction.']
  accumulated_depreciation_to_date decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total accumulated depreciation up to the end of this period.']
  beginning_net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net book value of the asset at the beginning of the period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the depreciation schedule record was created.']
  depreciation_basis varchar [note: 'Basis used for depreciation calculation (e.g., cost, revaluation, fair value).']
  depreciation_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text comment or note regarding the depreciation calculation.']
  depreciation_expense_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Depreciation expense recognized for the period.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate depreciation for the period.']
  depreciation_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Depreciation rate applied for the period (e.g., 0.2000 for 20%).']
  depreciation_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of the depreciation period within the asset\'s depreciation schedule.']
  depreciation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the depreciation record.']
  ending_net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net book value of the asset at the end of the period.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period within the fiscal year (e.g., FY, Q1).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for the depreciation period.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the depreciation amount is an estimate (true) or based on actual usage (false).']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the depreciation period.']
  period_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the depreciation period (e.g., \'FY2023 Q1\').']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the depreciation period.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the depreciation expense was posted to the ledger.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the depreciation entry complies with relevant accounting standards (e.g., IFRS, GAAP).']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated salvage value of the asset at the end of its useful life.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the depreciation schedule record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the depreciation schedule record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of the asset in years.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number for the depreciation schedule record for concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Depreciation schedule record capturing the periodic depreciation calculation for each fixed asset. Captures asset reference, depreciation period, depreciation method applied, beginning net book value, depreciation expense amount, accumulated depreciation to date, ending net book value, GL account, cost center, and journal entry reference. Supports asset lifecycle accounting and financial statement accuracy for capitalized payments infrastructure.'
}

Table ledger.payable {
  payable_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the payable (invoice) record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center that will bear the expense of the payable.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Payable invoices are settled in a specific currency; regulatory tax reporting requires a normalized currency link.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Payments of invoices can be funded directly from a digital wallet; tracking the source wallet is essential for cash‑flow and audit trails.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'GL account to which the payable is posted for accounting.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor (supplier) to whom the payable is owed.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the internal approval workflow for the payable.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the payable.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payable was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payable record was first created in the system.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early‑payment or promotional discount applied to the invoice.']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the discount program or agreement.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be made to avoid late fees.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'FX rate applied when converting foreign‑currency invoice to functional currency.']
  exchange_rate_date date [note: 'Date on which the exchange rate was sourced.']
  expense_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the expense (e.g., Marketing, Operations).']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Invoice amount expressed in the company\'s functional currency.']
  invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount billed on the invoice before taxes, discounts, or adjustments.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was issued by the vendor.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice number assigned by the vendor or generated by the system.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'True if the payable has been consolidated with other invoices for payment.']
  is_early_payment_discount_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an early‑payment discount was applied to this payable.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'True if the payable is part of a recurring billing schedule.']
  is_tax_exempt boolean [note: 'True if the invoice is exempt from tax under applicable regulations.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Invoice amount after tax and discount adjustments (payable amount).']
  original_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Invoice amount in the vendor\'s original currency.']
  payable_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or memo entered on the invoice.']
  payable_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payable.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the payable was settled.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Means used to settle the payable (e.g., ACH, Wire Transfer).']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'External reference such as check number, ACH trace number, or transaction ID.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment processing state of the payable.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms (e.g., Net 30, Net 45) governing the payable.']
  scheme_invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by card scheme (Visa/Mastercard) for fee invoices.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax charged on the invoice.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction or code applicable to the invoice.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payable record.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the vendor as recorded in the master vendor catalog.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable invoice record representing amounts owed by the payments fintech enterprise to vendors, card schemes, and service providers. Captures supplier reference, invoice number, invoice date, due date, payment terms, invoice amount, currency, tax amount, GL account distribution, cost center, approval status, payment status, and scheme invoice reference (for Visa/Mastercard scheme fee invoices). Managed in Oracle Financials AP module.'
}

Table ledger.receivable {
  receivable_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the receivable record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.cost_center']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Receivable amounts are recorded in a currency; linking enables proper foreign‑exchange gain/loss calculation.']
  digital_wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to wallet.digital_wallet. Business justification: Incoming payments are often settled into a specific digital wallet; linking receivables to that wallet enables accurate revenue recognition and reconciliation.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the partner associated with the invoice, if applicable.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.gl_account']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant billed.']
  aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Aging bucket based on days past due.']
  audit_trail_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if audit trail is enabled for this invoice.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current collection status of the invoice.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was created.']
  currency_exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if invoice currency differs from functional currency.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is under dispute.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due.']
  exchange_rate_date date [note: 'Date of the exchange rate used.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number from partner or third‑party system.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., month) of the invoice.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year of the invoice.']
  interchange_income decimal(18,2) [note: 'Interchange fee income component of the invoice.']
  invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount billed before taxes and fees.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was issued.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Unique invoice identifier assigned by the enterprise.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the invoice.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates if the invoice is part of a consolidated statement.']
  mdr_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the merchant based on MDR.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Invoice amount after tax and fees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding the invoice.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which payment was made.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Primary payment method used for settlement.']
  payment_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the first payment received against the invoice.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms associated with the invoice.']
  platform_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee for platform services associated with the invoice.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice was posted to the ledger.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center associated with the invoice.']
  receivable_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of invoice purpose or line items.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating inclusion in regulatory reporting.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Business segment code for reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the invoice originated (e.g., ERP, AR module).']
  sox_control_classification varchar [note: 'SOX control classification for the invoice.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax applied to the invoice.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applied to the invoice.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the invoice is tax‑exempt.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was last updated.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable invoice record representing amounts owed to the payments fintech enterprise by merchants, partners, and customers. Captures customer/merchant reference, invoice number, invoice date, due date, payment terms, invoice amount, currency, tax amount, GL account distribution, collection status, aging bucket, and dispute flag. Includes MDR billing, platform fee invoices, and interchange income receivables. Managed in Oracle Financials AR module.'
}

Table ledger.budget {
  budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the budget record.']
  accounting_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.accounting_period. Business justification: Budgets are defined for a fiscal period; linking to accounting_period enables period‑driven budgeting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center associated with the budget.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Budgets are prepared in a specific currency; compliance reports require a currency reference.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the budget.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GL account to which the budget amount is allocated.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (company) owning the budget.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the budget.']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Basis used to allocate the budget across cost centers or accounts.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned monetary amount for the budget line.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the budget was formally approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the budget.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the budget purpose.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget record.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget as original, revised, or forecast.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget record was first created.']
  currency_exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied when converting the budget amount to the reporting currency.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the budget expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the budget becomes effective.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Numeric period within the fiscal year (e.g., month number).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the budget applies.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to generate forecast budgets.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the budget contains confidential information.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent budget review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional comments or explanations about the budget.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the budget is reviewed.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of how many times the budget has been revised.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the budget record.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who last updated the budget record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the budget record.']
  variance_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate variance between budget and actuals.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the budget record for tracking revisions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who created the budget record.']

  Note: 'Annual and periodic budget record capturing the financial plan for each GL account, cost center, and legal entity. Captures budget version (original, revised, forecast), budget period, GL account, cost center, legal entity, budget amount, currency, budget owner, approval status, and variance-to-actuals. Supports financial planning and analysis (FP&A) for the payments fintech enterprise including TPV-based revenue budgeting and technology infrastructure cost planning.'
}

Table ledger.sox_control {
  sox_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SOX internal control record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: SOX controls apply to specific GL accounts; replace textual scope with FK to gl_account for enforceable linkage.']
  control_assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the control was last assessed for design effectiveness.']
  control_assessment_result varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent design effectiveness assessment.']
  control_audit_trail_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an immutable audit trail is captured for this control.']
  control_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the control.']
  control_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes about the control.']
  control_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was created.']
  control_data_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the control record.']
  control_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., URL or document ID) to the formal control documentation.']
  control_effectiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Rating of how effective the control is perceived to be.']
  control_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the control is performed.']
  control_last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the last modification.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the internal control.']
  control_objective varchar [note: 'Statement of the business objective the control is designed to achieve.']
  control_owner_department varchar [note: 'Department that owns the control.']
  control_performer varchar [note: 'Person or system that executes the control.']
  control_regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory frameworks applicable to the control (e.g., SOX, PCI DSS, GDPR).']
  control_remediation_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed action to remediate a deficiency.']
  control_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the control.']
  control_testing_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to test the control (e.g., walkthrough, sampling).']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the control (preventive, detective, or directive).']
  control_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']
  deficiency_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of any identified deficiency.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the control becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the control expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  evidence_requirements varchar [note: 'Documentation or artifacts required to demonstrate control execution.']
  last_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent control test.']
  process_owner varchar [note: 'Individual or team responsible for the process the control covers.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completing remediation.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation activities.']
  sox_control_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent control test.']
  testing_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the control is tested for effectiveness.']

  Note: 'SOX internal control record defining each financial reporting control required under Sarbanes-Oxley Act compliance. Captures control ID, control name, control objective, control type (preventive/detective), frequency (daily, monthly, quarterly), GL account scope, process owner, control performer, evidence requirements, testing frequency, last test date, test result, deficiency classification (significant deficiency, material weakness), and remediation status. Critical for payments fintech regulatory compliance.'
}

Table ledger.sox_control_test {
  sox_control_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SOX control test execution record.']
  sox_control_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.sox_control. Business justification: SOX control tests are executed against a specific SOX control; linking provides traceability.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Immutable log of changes and actions related to the test record.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework(s) applicable to the control test.']
  control_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the control (e.g., financial reporting, IT general controls).']
  control_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the control is performed.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the control under test.']
  control_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team responsible for the control.']
  control_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier or code of the internal control being tested.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test record was initially created in the system.']
  deficiency_details varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any control deficiencies identified during testing.']
  evidence_documentation varchar [note: 'Reference or link to supporting documentation and evidence collected for the test.']
  exceptions_identified varchar [note: 'Count of exceptions or deviations discovered in the test sample.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the control is deemed critical to financial reporting.']
  management_response varchar [note: 'Management\'s response to identified deficiencies, including corrective action plans.']
  remediation_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions performed to address identified deficiencies.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which remediation actions must be completed.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the control based on the test outcome.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of items, transactions, or records sampled during the test.']
  test_conclusion varchar [note: 'Overall result of the test indicating whether the control is effective or ineffective.']
  test_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the control test was performed.']
  test_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time in minutes spent executing the test.']
  test_location varchar [note: 'Physical or logical location where the test was performed.']
  test_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to test the control (e.g., inquiry, observation, inspection, reperformance).']
  test_period_end date [note: 'End date of the financial period covered by the test.']
  test_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the financial period covered by the test.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the test record.']
  test_time timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the test execution started.']
  tester_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who executed the control test.']
  tester_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the test was performed by internal audit staff or an external auditor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the test record.']

  Note: 'SOX control test execution record capturing each instance of a SOX control being tested by internal or external auditors. Captures control reference, test date, test period, tester identity (internal audit, external auditor), test methodology (inquiry, observation, inspection, reperformance), sample size, exceptions identified, test conclusion (effective/ineffective), deficiency details, and management response. Provides the audit evidence trail required for SOX 404 certification.'
}

Table ledger.fx_revaluation {
  fx_revaluation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the FX revaluation record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: FX revaluation entries must reference the functional currency entity for auditability.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the GL account whose balance is being revalued.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the GL journal entry generated for the revaluation.']
  accounting_period varchar [note: 'Period identifier (year and month) to which the revaluation belongs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revaluation record was first created in the system.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Numeric period within the fiscal year (e.g., 1‑12).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year of the revaluation.']
  functional_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the entity\'s functional reporting currency.']
  is_manual_adjustment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the revaluation was entered manually (true) or generated automatically (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or comments about the revaluation.']
  original_balance decimal(20,4) [note: 'Monetary amount of the GL account balance before revaluation, expressed in the original currency.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code of the currency in which the original balance is recorded.']
  revaluation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the revaluation event for tracking and reconciliation.']
  revaluation_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert the original balance to the functional currency at the revaluation date.']
  revaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the revaluation record.']
  revaluation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the FX revaluation was performed.']
  revalued_balance decimal(20,4) [note: 'Balance amount after applying the FX revaluation rate, expressed in the functional currency.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin that produced the revaluation record.']
  unrealized_gain_loss_amount decimal(20,4) [note: 'Difference between revalued balance and original balance, representing unrealized foreign‑exchange gain or loss.']
  unrealized_gain_loss_type varchar [note: 'Indicator whether the FX impact is a gain or a loss.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the revaluation record.']

  Note: 'Foreign currency revaluation record capturing the period-end revaluation of monetary GL account balances denominated in non-functional currencies. Captures revaluation date, accounting period, GL account, original currency, functional currency, original balance, revaluation rate, revalued balance, unrealized gain/loss amount, and GL journal entry reference. Essential for payments fintech enterprises operating across 200+ countries with multi-currency settlement and FX exposure.'
}

Table ledger.consolidation_entry {
  consolidation_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consolidation journal entry record.']
  audit_trail_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the detailed audit trail for this entry.']
  consolidated_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'General Ledger account impacted by the consolidation entry.']
  consolidation_group_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.consolidation_group']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to ledger.cost_center']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Consolidation entries aggregate amounts across entities; a unified currency reference is required for elimination.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity to which the consolidation entry applies.']
  adjustment_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code that identifies the business reason for the adjustment.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Category of adjustment applied to the entry.']
  consolidation_entry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context for the consolidation entry.']
  consolidation_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the consolidation entry.']
  consolidation_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period for which the consolidation entry is prepared, expressed as year and quarter (e.g., 2023-Q4).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consolidation entry record was first created.']
  elimination_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount eliminated or adjusted in the consolidation entry, expressed in functional currency.']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date the consolidation entry was generated in the business process.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Unique business number assigned to the consolidation entry.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Category of the consolidation entry indicating its purpose.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied when translating foreign currency amounts.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1‑4) of the entry.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year of the entry.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry relates to intercompany elimination.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'True if the entry represents an adjustment rather than a primary transaction.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks.']
  period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period identifier in YYYY‑MM format.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the consolidation entry was posted to the ledger.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Project identifier related to the entry, if applicable.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Reporting segment code associated with the entry.']
  source_legal_entity_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of source legal entity identifiers participating in the consolidation.']
  sox_control_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Sarbanes‑Oxley control governing this entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consolidation entry record.']

  Note: 'Consolidation journal entry record capturing elimination and adjustment entries required for consolidated financial statement preparation. Captures consolidation group, consolidation period, entry type (intercompany elimination, minority interest, goodwill amortization, currency translation adjustment), source legal entities, elimination amount, currency, and consolidated GL account. Supports the global consolidation process for a payments fintech enterprise with subsidiaries across multiple jurisdictions.'
}

Table ledger.tax_provision {
  tax_provision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tax provision record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit predefined roles; treated as OTHER.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Tax provision calculations depend on the reporting currency for accurate tax expense recognition.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the journal entry that records the tax provision.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity to which the tax provision applies.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tax provision was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the tax provision.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to calculate the tax provision.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tax provision record was created.']
  current_tax_expense decimal(20,2) [note: 'Current tax expense recognized in the period.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated the tax provision (e.g., ERP‑Oracle).']
  deferred_tax_asset decimal(20,2) [note: 'Deferred tax asset amount arising from temporary differences.']
  deferred_tax_liability decimal(20,2) [note: 'Deferred tax liability amount arising from temporary differences.']
  effective_tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Effective tax rate applied to pre‑tax income (percentage).']
  exchange_rate_to_reporting_currency decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate used to convert amounts to the reporting currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Period code within the fiscal year (e.g., Q1, Q2, H1).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year associated with the tax provision.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the tax provision.']
  pre_tax_income decimal(20,2) [note: 'Income before tax for the legal entity and period.']
  tax_account_code varchar [note: 'GL account code used for the tax provision posting.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction (e.g., country or region) for the provision.']
  tax_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the tax reporting period covered by this provision.']
  tax_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the tax reporting period covered by this provision.']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the tax provision record.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Classification of tax (e.g., income, sales, property).']
  uncertain_tax_position_reserve decimal(20,2) [note: 'Reserve for uncertain tax positions per ASC 740 / IAS 12.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tax provision record.']

  Note: 'Corporate tax provision record capturing the current and deferred tax calculations for each legal entity per accounting period. Captures legal entity, tax jurisdiction, tax period, pre-tax income, current tax expense, deferred tax asset, deferred tax liability, effective tax rate, uncertain tax position reserve, and tax provision journal entry reference. Supports income tax accounting under ASC 740 / IAS 12 for payments fintech entities operating across multiple tax jurisdictions.'
}

Table ledger.financial_statement {
  financial_statement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the financial statement record. _canonical_skip_reason: role inferred as OTHER, no minimum categories required.']

  equity decimal(20,2) [note: 'Equity (shareholder\'s equity) reported.']

  filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for regulatory filing (e.g., SEC accession number).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1-4) of the statement.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year associated with the statement.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates if the statement is consolidated across multiple entities.']
  net_income decimal(20,2) [note: 'Net income (profit) reported.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes or comments about the statement.']
  preparation_status varchar [note: 'Current preparation status of the statement.']
  reporting_entity_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the reporting entity.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the reporting period covered by the statement.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period covered by the statement.']
  reporting_standard varchar [note: 'Accounting standard used for the statement.']
  restatement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the statement is a restatement of a prior filing.']
  revenue decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total revenue reported (for income statement).']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Review status after internal audit.']
  statement_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial statement (e.g., income statement, balance sheet, cash flow, statement of equity).']
  total_assets decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total assets reported in the statement.']
  total_liabilities decimal(20,2) [note: 'Total liabilities reported in the statement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the statement record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the statement (incremented on revisions).']

  Note: 'Published financial statement record representing a formal financial report (income statement, balance sheet, cash flow statement, statement of equity) for a legal entity or consolidated group for a reporting period. Captures statement type, reporting entity, reporting period, reporting standard (GAAP/IFRS), currency, preparation status, review status, external audit sign-off, filing reference (SEC, regulatory), and restatement flag. The authoritative record of financial reporting outputs.'
}

Table ledger.allocation_rule {
  allocation_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the allocation rule.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Allocation rules are defined per GL account; FK enables direct association.']
  allocation_basis_metric varchar [note: 'Metric that drives the allocation (e.g., headcount, TPV).']
  allocation_fixed_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount allocated when allocation_method is fixed_amount.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate the allocation (e.g., fixed percentage, headcount based).']
  allocation_metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the allocation_basis_metric (e.g., "employees", "transactions").']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage value used when allocation_method is fixed_percentage.']
  allocation_rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the rule in external systems.']
  allocation_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the rule\'s purpose and logic.']
  allocation_rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the allocation rule.']
  allocation_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  allocation_rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of entity to which the rule applies (e.g., cost center, legal entity).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the rule.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the rule.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rule record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for monetary values in the rule.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the rule becomes effective.']
  effective_time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone used for interpreting effective_from and effective_until dates.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the rule expires (null for open‑ended).']
  is_system_defined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is defined by the platform (true) or by a business user (false).']
  last_applied_by varchar [note: 'User or process that last applied the rule.']
  last_applied_date date [note: 'Date when the rule was last executed in a cost allocation run.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or audit notes.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority determining rule evaluation order (lower = higher priority).']
  rule_condition varchar [note: 'Logical expression defining when the rule applies (e.g., "transaction_type = \'FX\' AND amount > 1000").']
  rule_owner varchar [note: 'Business owner responsible for the rule.']
  rule_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of applicability for the rule.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the rule.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the rule for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Expense and cost allocation rule configuration defining how shared costs are distributed across cost centers, legal entities, or business units. Captures rule name, allocation type (fixed percentage, statistical basis, headcount, TPV-weighted), source account range, target account range, allocation basis metric, effective date range, approval status, and last applied date. Supports management accounting and transfer pricing governance for the payments fintech enterprise.'
}

  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the action was performed.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audited record has been logically deleted.']
  is_sensitive boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the changed data contains sensitive information.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction applicable to the event.']
  new_values varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing the values after the change.']
  operation_context varchar [note: 'Additional context describing the operation (e.g., batch, real-time).']
  previous_values varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing the values before the change.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the audit record is scheduled for archival or deletion per data retention policy.']
  source_application varchar [note: 'Name of the application that generated the event.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or component within the application that generated the event.']
  success_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operation completed successfully.']
  user_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the user who performed the action.']
  user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who performed the action.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_trail. Referenced by audit_trail_id.'
}

  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the vendor.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the primary contract with the vendor.']

  is_tax_exempt boolean [note: 'True if the vendor is exempt from tax withholding.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the vendor record.']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'Value‑Added Tax registration number for the vendor.']
