  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Internal business code used to reference the vendor.']

  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the vendor organization.']
  vendor_status_detail varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text detail describing the vendor\'s current status.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Category of vendor (e.g., service, product, software, hardware, consulting).']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the vendor.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for vendor. Referenced by vendor_id.'
}

Table ledger.revenue_line {
  revenue_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for revenue_line']
  card_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or service that generated the revenue.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue line records reference a cost center; using a FK removes the redundant code column and enforces referential integrity.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'FK to reference.currency']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue line amounts belong to a GL account; a properly named FK (gl_account_id) replaces the ambiguous ledger_account_id.']
  parent_revenue_line_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on revenue_line (parent_revenue_line_id)']
  revenue_recognition_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent revenue transaction header to which this line belongs.']
  amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Pre-tax, pre-discount monetary amount for the line.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue line record was first created in the system.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Discounts or rebates subtracted from the gross amount.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date when the revenue line becomes effective (used for deferred revenue).']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date when the revenue line ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the revenue header.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the revenue line (e.g., sale, refund, fee).']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final amount after tax and discount adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or comments about the revenue line.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units or amount of service that the revenue line represents.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which the revenue is recognized for accounting purposes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream operational system that originated the revenue line.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the revenue line.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax component applied to the gross amount.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., transaction, hour, GB).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the revenue line record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for revenue_line. Referenced by revenue_line_id.'
}

Table ledger.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contract']
  contract_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Party tasked with day‑to‑day management of the contract.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Party responsible for the contract on the organization side.']
  master_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on contract (master_contract_id)']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Counter for the number of amendments applied to the contract.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the contract.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract received final approval.']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'True if the contract renews automatically at term end.']
  billing_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are generated under the contract.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission percentage earned on contract revenue.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the contract record.']
  contract_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of contracts for reporting.']
  contract_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the contract purpose and scope.']
  contract_document_hash varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 hash of the contract document for integrity verification.']
  contract_expiration_notice_sent boolean [note: 'True if an expiration reminder has been sent to the counter‑party.']
  contract_last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was last reviewed for compliance or renewal.']
  contract_line_of_business varchar [note: 'Business line or product area the contract belongs to.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the contract.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract number used in business communications and legal documents.']
  contract_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the contract based on credit and compliance factors.']
  contract_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that created the contract record.']
  contract_status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current status, e.g., pending approval, under negotiation.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the agreement.']
  contract_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored contract document.']
  contract_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the contract at inception.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contract value.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base contract price.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or ends; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes legally binding.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'True if the contract is currently active.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'True if the contract has been archived and is read‑only.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the legal jurisdiction governing the contract.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed for the contract.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contract meets all applicable payments‑fintech regulations.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is eligible for renewal.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the renewal period in months.']
  signing_date date [note: 'Date the contract was signed by all parties.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the contract was terminated, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for contract termination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for contract revisions.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for contract. Referenced by contract_id.'
}

Table ledger.performance_obligation {
  performance_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for performance_obligation']
  parent_performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on performance_obligation (parent_performance_obligation_id)']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Quantity actually delivered or achieved to date.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the obligation is billed to the counter‑party.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance obligation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for total_amount.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the scope, deliverables, and conditions of the performance obligation.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment for the obligation is due.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the performance obligation becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the performance obligation expires or is scheduled to end; null for open‑ended obligations.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current state of obligation fulfillment against the performance metric.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for target_quantity and actual_quantity.']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the performance obligation for easy identification.']
  obligation_number varchar [note: 'External contract number or code assigned to the performance obligation.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the performance obligation indicating the nature of the promised deliverable.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of payment terms (e.g., Net 30, due on receipt).']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Description of any penalties for non‑fulfillment or late performance.']
  performance_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to measure fulfillment of the obligation.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the obligation is eligible for automatic renewal.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the renewal period in months if renewal_flag is true.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue for this obligation per IFRS 15 / ASC 606.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the performance obligation.']
  target_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned quantity or volume to be delivered as defined by the performance metric.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the performance obligation was terminated, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for early termination of the performance obligation.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with the performance obligation as defined in the contract.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the performance obligation record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for performance_obligation. Referenced by performance_obligation_id.'
}

Table ledger.audit_session {

  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit session ended.']

  ip_address varchar [note: 'Source IP address from which the audit session was initiated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments captured for the audit session.']
  object_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific business object involved in the action.']
  object_type varchar [note: 'Type of business object that was accessed or modified.']

  user_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who performed the audited actions.']
  user_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the user associated with the audit session.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_session. Referenced by session_id.'
}

Table reference.country {
  country_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the country record.']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key linking to the country‑specific holiday calendar.']
  timezone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.timezone. Business justification: Linking country to timezone for consistent timestamp handling; time_zone string becomes redundant.']
  capital_city varchar [note: 'Official capital city of the country.']
  country_name varchar [note: 'Official short name of the sovereign nation or territory.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the country record was first inserted.']
  dialing_code varchar [note: 'Country calling code used for telephone numbers.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the country record is considered active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the country record is no longer active; null if still active.']
  fatf_high_risk_flag boolean [note: 'True if the country is identified by FATF as high‑risk for money‑laundering or terrorism financing.']
  is_amex_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether American Express transactions are supported in the country.']
  is_eu_member boolean [note: 'True if the country is a member of the European Union.']
  is_g20_member boolean [note: 'True if the country participates in the Group of Twenty.']
  is_g7_member boolean [note: 'True if the country is part of the Group of Seven economies.']
  is_mastercard_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Mastercard transactions are supported in the country.']
  is_rtp_supported boolean [note: 'True if real‑time payment infrastructure is available in the country.']
  is_swift_supported boolean [note: 'True if the country participates in the SWIFT global payments network.']
  is_un_member boolean [note: 'True if the country is a member of the United Nations.']
  is_unionpay_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether UnionPay transactions are supported in the country.']
  is_visa_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Visa card transactions are supported in the country.']
  iso_alpha2_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter country code defined by ISO 3166‑1.']
  iso_alpha3_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code defined by ISO 3166‑1.']
  iso_currency_code varchar [note: 'Primary ISO 4217 currency code used for transactions in the country.']
  iso_numeric_code varchar [note: 'Three‑digit numeric country code defined by ISO 3166‑1.']
  language_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 639‑1 language codes spoken officially in the country.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the country\'s centroid.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the country\'s centroid.']
  ofac_sanctions_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the country appears on the OFAC sanctions list.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current usage status of the country record within the platform.']
  psd2_applicability boolean [note: 'True if the country falls under the EU PSD2 regulatory regime.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region grouping (e.g., Africa, Americas).']
  sub_region varchar [note: 'More granular sub‑region classification (e.g., Western Europe, Southern Asia).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the country record.']

  Note: 'Global country reference master defining all sovereign nations and territories recognized by ISO 3166-1. Stores alpha-2 code, alpha-3 code, numeric code, country name, region, sub-region, dialing code, OFAC sanctions flag, FATF high-risk jurisdiction flag, PSD2 applicability, and operational status. Used across all domains for geographic classification, regulatory jurisdiction mapping, and cross-border payment routing.'
}

Table reference.currency {
  currency_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the currency record.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter country code of the primary issuing country for the currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the currency record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter alphabetic code representing the currency per ISO 4217.']
  currency_description varchar [note: 'Additional free-text description or notes about the currency.']
  currency_name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the currency.']
  currency_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the currency as fiat, digital (e.g., cryptocurrency), or commodity-backed.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the currency became valid for use.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the currency was retired or became invalid; null if still active.']
  exchange_rate_source varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source used for exchange rate data for this currency.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the currency is currently active for transactions.']
  is_crypto boolean [note: 'True if the currency is a cryptocurrency.']
  is_fiat boolean [note: 'True if the currency is a traditional fiat currency.']
  is_historic boolean [note: 'True if the currency is no longer in active circulation.']
  iso_3166_numeric_country_code varchar [note: 'Numeric country code associated with the currency\'s primary country.']
  minor_unit varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places used by the currency (e.g., 2 for USD).']
  numeric_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit numeric code for the currency per ISO 4217.']
  rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rounding rule applied for the currency (e.g., round half up).']
  symbol varchar [note: 'Common symbol used to represent the currency (e.g., $).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the currency record.']

  Note: 'ISO 4217 currency reference master covering all active and historic currencies used in payment processing, FX conversion, and settlement. Stores currency code, numeric code, currency name, minor unit (decimal places), currency symbol, active flag, and associated country. Supports DCC, cross-border payment routing, and multi-currency settlement calculations.'
}

Table reference.mcc {
  mcc_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated surrogate key uniquely identifying each MCC record.']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Linking MCC to transaction type for transaction categorization; transaction_type string becomes redundant.']
  average_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average monetary value per transaction for this MCC (currency‑agnostic).']
  average_transaction_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average number of transactions per month for merchants in this category (historical baseline).']
  card_scheme_applicability varchar [note: 'List of payment card schemes for which the MCC is valid (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Discover).']
  category_group varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of MCCs (e.g., Retail, Services, Travel).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the MCC with applicable regulations (e.g., PCI DSS, AML).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the MCC record was first created in the reference catalog.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the MCC definition becomes active for processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the MCC definition is retired or superseded (null if still active).']
  high_risk_flag boolean [note: 'True if the merchant category is considered high risk for fraud or compliance monitoring.']
  industry_sector varchar [note: 'Broad industry sector associated with the merchant category (e.g., Food & Beverage, Transportation).']
  interchange_eligibility varchar [note: 'Indicates whether transactions using this MCC qualify for standard interchange rates.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the MCC record was last reviewed for accuracy and compliance.']
  match_list_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the MCC is included in the industry MATCH list for heightened monitoring.']
  mcc_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code that classifies the merchant\'s primary business activity as defined by ISO 18245.']
  mcc_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the merchant category represented by the MCC.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any supplemental information or remarks about the MCC.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO‑3 country code indicating the primary regulatory jurisdiction for the MCC.']
  restricted_category_flag boolean [note: 'True if the MCC falls under a regulatory restricted category (e.g., gambling, adult services).']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating (0‑100) derived from fraud and compliance analytics for the MCC.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the MCC record (e.g., Merchant Management System).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the MCC record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the MCC record to support change tracking.']

  Note: 'Merchant Category Code (MCC) reference catalog as defined by ISO 18245 and adopted by Visa, Mastercard, Amex, and Discover. Stores the 4-digit MCC code, merchant category description, card scheme applicability, interchange program eligibility, high-risk flag, restricted category flag, and MATCH list association indicator. Authoritative lookup used by merchant onboarding, interchange qualification, and fraud scoring.'
}

Table reference.bin_range {
  bin_range_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the BIN range record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a reference lookup table for BIN/IIN ranges.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: reference_bin_range references a card scheme; linking to reference_card_scheme via reference_card_scheme_id removes the duplicated string and enforces referential integrity.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Linking bin range to country for standardized country reference; country_code becomes redundant.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Linking bin range to currency for standardized currency reference; currency_code becomes redundant.']
  mcc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.mcc. Business justification: Linking bin range to MCC for merchant categorization; merchant_category_code becomes redundant.']
  bin_end varchar [note: 'Ending numeric prefix of the BIN/IIN range (inclusive).']
  bin_length varchar [note: 'Length of the BIN prefix (6, 7, or 8 digits).']
  bin_range_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the BIN range purpose or product line.']
  bin_range_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BIN range.']
  bin_start varchar [note: 'Starting numeric prefix of the BIN/IIN range (6 to 8 digits).']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'Specific brand or product line of the card (e.g., Visa Signature).']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Category of card product associated with the BIN.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status with relevant regulations (PCI DSS, PSD2, etc.).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BIN range record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the BIN range became effective for issuance.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the BIN range is no longer valid for new issuances.']
  interchange_fee_category varchar [note: 'Interchange fee category associated with the BIN range.']
  is_3ds_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if the card supports 3‑D Secure authentication.']
  is_commercial boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the card is a commercial (business) card.']
  is_contactless boolean [note: 'Indicates if the card supports contactless (NFC) transactions.']
  is_dcc_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if Dynamic Currency Conversion is supported for this BIN range.']
  is_mcc_specific boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the BIN range is restricted to specific MCCs.']
  is_prepaid boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the card is a prepaid product.']
  is_sca_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates if transactions with this BIN range are exempt from Strong Customer Authentication.']
  is_tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates if tokens can be generated for cards in this BIN range.']
  is_virtual boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the card is a virtual (non‑physical) card.']
  issuing_bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the issuing financial institution.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that performed the last update.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed transaction amount per transaction for this BIN range.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum allowed transaction amount per transaction for this BIN range.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction applicable to the BIN range (e.g., US, EU).']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Risk score assigned to the BIN range for fraud assessment (0‑100).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the BIN range data.']
  token_service_provider varchar [note: 'Identifier of the token service provider (TSP) for this BIN range.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'Default currency for transactions using this BIN range.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BIN range record.']

  Note: 'Bank Identification Number (BIN) / Issuer Identification Number (IIN) range registry defining the mapping of 6-to-8 digit BIN prefixes to issuing institutions, card schemes, card products, and funding types. Stores BIN range start/end, IIN, issuing bank name, card scheme, card type (credit/debit/prepaid), card brand, country of issuance, 3DS capability flag, contactless flag, and commercial card indicator. Critical for transaction routing, interchange qualification, and fraud risk assessment.'
}

Table reference.authorization_response_code {
  authorization_response_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each authorization response code record.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Linking authorization response code to card scheme for scheme‑specific handling; scheme string becomes redundant.']
  authorization_response_code_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the response: approved, declined, referral (e.g., need for additional authentication), or error.']
  authorization_response_code_code varchar [note: 'Standardized two- to four-character code returned by the issuing bank or scheme (e.g., "00", "05", "51").']
  authorization_response_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the code record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first inserted into the reference table.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the response code becomes valid for processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the response code is no longer valid (null if still active).']
  is_retriable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction may be automatically retried (true) or must be halted (false).']
  is_soft_decline boolean [note: 'True when the decline is considered soft (e.g., insufficient funds) and may be resolved without cardholder action.']
  message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description associated with the response code (e.g., "Approved", "Do not honour").']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text field for additional remarks, change‑log entries, or governance comments.']
  scheme_override_message varchar [note: 'Optional scheme‑specific description that overrides the generic message for this code.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating system or standard (e.g., "ISO8583", "VisaCore").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Standardized authorization response code reference table defining all ISO 8583 and card scheme response codes returned by issuing banks during transaction authorization. Stores response code, response message, response category (approved, declined, referral, error), retriable flag, soft decline indicator, and scheme-specific override descriptions for Visa, Mastercard, Amex, and Discover. Used by the authorization engine for decline management and retry logic.'
}

Table reference.decline_code {
  decline_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each decline code record.']
  card_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.reference_card_scheme. Business justification: Linking decline code to card scheme for decline categorization; scheme string becomes redundant.']
  cardholder_message varchar [note: 'Message displayed to the cardholder when decline occurs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the decline code record was created in the system.']
  decline_code_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of decline reason (e.g., do-not-honor, insufficient-funds, fraud-suspected). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: do_not_honor|insufficient_funds|lost_card|stolen_card|fraud_suspected|expired_card|invalid_cvv|invalid_pin|restricted_card|generic_decline — promote to reference product]']
  decline_code_code varchar [note: 'Standardized decline code used in transaction processing.']
  decline_code_description varchar [note: 'Human readable description of the decline reason.']
  decline_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the decline code.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the decline code becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the decline code is retired or superseded.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates if this decline code is the default for its category.']
  merchant_message varchar [note: 'Message provided to the merchant for the decline reason.']
  retry_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction can be retried after this decline.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the decline code (e.g., Payment Gateway, Authorization Engine).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the decline code record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version of the decline code definition for change management.']

  Note: 'Decline code reference catalog mapping internal and scheme-level decline codes to standardized decline reason categories. Stores decline code, decline reason description, decline category (do-not-honor, insufficient-funds, lost-card, stolen-card, fraud-suspected, expired-card, etc.), retry eligibility flag, cardholder-facing message, and merchant-facing message. Distinct from authorization response codes — these are the business-level decline classifications used for reporting, retry orchestration, and cardholder communication.'
}

Table reference.timezone {
  timezone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the timezone record. _canonical_skip_reason: Inferred role REFERENCE_LOOKUP; no minimum category enforcement required.']
  abbreviation varchar [note: 'Common abbreviation for the time zone, e.g., \'EST\'.']
  associated_country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the time zone is applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the time zone record was created in the system.']
  dst_observed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the time zone observes daylight saving time.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date until which the time zone definition is effective; null if currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the time zone definition is effective.']
  iana_name varchar [note: 'Standard IANA time zone identifier, e.g., \'America/New_York\'.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates if the time zone record is currently active for use.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the time zone entry.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system that supplied the time zone data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the time zone record.']
  utc_offset_dst varchar [note: 'UTC offset during daylight saving time period, expressed as ±HH:MM.']
  utc_offset_standard varchar [note: 'UTC offset during standard (non-DST) period, expressed as ±HH:MM.']

  Note: 'Timezone reference master covering all IANA timezone identifiers used for timestamp normalization, settlement cutoff scheduling, and regulatory reporting. Stores IANA timezone ID, UTC offset (standard), UTC offset (DST), DST observance flag, timezone abbreviation, associated country codes, and effective date range. Ensures consistent timestamp handling across multi-region payment processing and settlement batch scheduling.'
}

Table reference.holiday_calendar {
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each holiday calendar record.']
  payment_rail_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.payment_rail. Business justification: Linking holiday calendar to payment rail for rail‑specific holiday schedules; payment_rail string becomes redundant.']
  applicable_scheme varchar [note: 'Card scheme(s) for which the holiday is observed.']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the holiday calendar (e.g., "US National Holidays").']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'Category of holiday (national, banking, scheme, ACH, RTGS, or other).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code to which the holiday applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the holiday record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date from which the holiday definition is valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date after which the holiday definition is no longer valid (null if indefinite).']
  holiday_calendar_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text description providing additional context about the holiday.']
  holiday_calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the holiday record.']
  holiday_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the holiday occurs.']
  holiday_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the holiday (e.g., "Independence Day").']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'True if the holiday recurs annually on the same date.']
  is_settlement_holiday boolean [note: 'True if the holiday prevents settlement processing for the applicable rail.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier used to interpret the holiday date for regions spanning multiple time zones.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the holiday record.']

  Note: 'Business holiday calendar master defining non-processing days for each country, payment rail, and card scheme. Stores calendar name, country code, calendar type (national holiday, banking holiday, scheme holiday, ACH holiday, RTGS holiday), holiday date, holiday name, and applicable payment rails. Used by settlement batch scheduling, RTGS/RTP cutoff management, and SLA calculation engines to determine valid processing days.'
}

Table reference.card_scheme {
  card_scheme_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each card scheme record. _canonical_skip_reason: Inferred role REFERENCE_LOOKUP; no minimum categories required.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Adds a foreign key from reference_card_scheme to regulatory_jurisdiction, replacing the string column and providing a normalized relationship.']
  card_scheme_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the scheme, including business purpose and key characteristics.']
  card_scheme_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the card scheme.']
  card_scheme_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the scheme within the platform.']
  card_scheme_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scheme based on network structure.']
  chargeback_time_limit_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days a cardholder can initiate a chargeback under this scheme.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the scheme with relevant standards.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for scheme support and communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for scheme support.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scheme record was first created.']

  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the scheme record expires or is superseded; null if indefinite.']
  headquarters_country varchar [note: 'Country where the scheme\'s corporate headquarters is located.']

  iso_8583_message_type_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO 8583 MTI values supported by the scheme.']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Standard short code identifying the payment network (e.g., VISA, MC, AMEX).']
  supported_card_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of card product types the scheme supports (e.g., credit, debit, prepaid, commercial).']
  supported_transaction_types varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of transaction categories (e.g., purchase, cash‑advance, refund, recurring).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scheme record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official public website of the card scheme.']

  Note: 'Card scheme (payment network brand) reference master defining all payment networks supported by the platform — Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, UnionPay, JCB, Diners Club, and domestic schemes. Stores scheme code, scheme name, scheme type (four-party, three-party, domestic), headquarters country, supported card types, supported transaction types, chargeback time limits, and scheme contact details. SSOT for card scheme identity referenced across transaction, interchange, dispute, and network domains.'
}

Stores rail code, rail name, rail type (card, ACH, wire, RTP, RTGS, crypto), settlement finality type (gross, net, deferred), supported currencies, geographic coverage, message standard (ISO 8583, ISO 20022, NACHA), and STP capability flag.'

  is_gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'True if the General Data Protection Regulation applies to the jurisdiction.']
  is_psd2_applicable boolean [note: 'True if the EU Payment Services Directive 2 applies to the jurisdiction.']

  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the jurisdiction\'s regulatory information was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or comments related to the jurisdiction entry.']
  primary_regulator varchar [note: 'Name of the principal regulatory authority overseeing the jurisdiction (e.g., FCA, ECB).']
  privacy_law varchar [note: 'Primary privacy legislation applicable (e.g., GDPR, CCPA).']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Geographic region or sub‑region the jurisdiction belongs to (e.g., Europe, Asia‑Pacific).']
  regulator_type varchar [note: 'Category of the regulator: supervisory agency, self‑regulatory organization, or government body.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the jurisdiction record.']

  Note: 'Regulatory jurisdiction reference master mapping geographic regions and countries to their applicable regulatory frameworks, governing bodies, and compliance requirements. Stores jurisdiction code, jurisdiction name, jurisdiction type (national, supranational, state/provincial), primary regulator, applicable regulations (PSD2, GDPR, CCPA, BSA, Regulation E, Regulation II), AML/CTF regime, data residency requirements, and cross-border data transfer restrictions. }

Table reference.transaction_type {
  transaction_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each transaction type record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a reference lookup, no mandatory minimum categories required.']
  applicable_payment_rails varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of payment rails that can process this transaction type.']
  card_scheme varchar [note: 'Payment card network(s) that support this transaction type.']
  chargeback_timeframe_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after settlement during which a chargeback may be initiated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the transaction type record was first created.']
  cross_border_fee_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a cross‑border fee is applied, waived, or not applicable.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code used for the transaction type when fixed.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the transaction type is valid for processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the transaction type is no longer valid; null if open‑ended.']
  financial_impact varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction type results in a debit, credit, or neutral effect on the account balance.']
  is_chargeback_possible boolean [note: 'True if a chargeback can be filed against this transaction type.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the transaction type is used for cross‑border payments.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'True if this type is used as the default when no explicit type is supplied.']
  is_external boolean [note: 'True if the transaction type is defined by an external payment scheme rather than internal policy.']
  is_fraud_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction type is subject to heightened fraud monitoring.']
  is_recurring_allowed boolean [note: 'True if the transaction type can be used in recurring payment schedules.']
  is_settlement_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction type must go through settlement processing.']
  is_tokenizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction type can be tokenized.']
  iso_20022_message_type varchar [note: 'Corresponding ISO 20022 message identifier (e.g., pain.001).']
  iso_8583_processing_code varchar [note: 'Six‑digit processing code defined by ISO 8583 for routing and handling.']
  max_amount_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit for transactions of this type.']
  min_amount_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower monetary threshold for transactions of this type.']
  processing_fee_indicator varchar [note: 'Who bears the processing fee for this transaction type.']
  recurring_interval varchar [note: 'Default interval for recurring instances of this transaction type.']
  requires_3ds boolean [note: 'True if the transaction type mandates 3‑Domain Secure authentication.']
  requires_authentication boolean [note: 'True if the transaction type requires real‑time authentication (e.g., 3‑DS).']
  requires_sca boolean [note: 'True if Strong Customer Authentication is required per PSD2.']
  reversal_eligibility_reason varchar [note: 'Business rule description why a type is or isn’t reversible.']
  reversal_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the transaction type can be reversed (e.g., refund, void).']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Typical settlement timing for this transaction type.']
  settlement_treatment varchar [note: 'Defines how the transaction is settled: immediate, deferred, or netted.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method of token generation for tokenizable transaction types.']
  transaction_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category aligning with ISO 20022 (e.g., purchase, refund, cash‑advance).']
  transaction_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Flat fee charged per transaction when fee type is fixed.']
  transaction_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of transaction amount charged as fee when fee type is percentage.']
  transaction_fee_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate any fee associated with the transaction type.']
  transaction_type_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the transaction type (e.g., PUR for Purchase).']
  transaction_type_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the transaction type, its purpose and typical usage.']
  transaction_type_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the transaction type (e.g., Purchase, Refund).']
  transaction_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transaction type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Transaction type reference catalog defining all standardized payment transaction types processed on the platform — purchase, refund, authorization, pre-authorization, capture, void, reversal, balance inquiry, cash advance, quasi-cash, P2P transfer, A2A transfer, BNPL disbursement, and more. Stores transaction type code, transaction type name, ISO 8583 processing code, financial impact (debit/credit/neutral), reversal eligibility, settlement treatment, and applicable payment rails.'
}

  subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing finer detail (e.g., duplicate_processing, counterfeit_card).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the reason code entry.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version of the reason code entry (e.g., v1.0, v2.3).']

  Note: 'Comprehensive chargeback and dispute reason code reference catalog covering all card scheme reason code taxonomies — Visa Dispute Resolution (VDR), Mastercard Dispute Resolution (MDR), Amex, and Discover. Stores scheme-specific reason code, reason code description, dispute category (fraud, authorization, processing error, consumer dispute), response time limit (days), documentation requirements, and effective/expiry dates. Distinct from dispute.reason_code which is the operational instance — this is the authoritative reference definition owned by the reference domain.'
}

Table reference.fx_rate {
  fx_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each FX rate record.']
  base_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Linking FX rate to base currency for accurate currency conversion; base_currency_code becomes redundant.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate record was first created in the system.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rate becomes effective for transaction conversion.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,9) [note: 'Numeric exchange rate representing how many units of the quote currency equal one unit of the base currency.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the rate expires or is superseded by a newer rate.']
  fx_rate_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the rate, such as market conditions or special handling.']
  fx_rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate record.']
  is_intraday boolean [note: 'True if the rate is an intraday (real‑time) quote; false for end‑of‑day rates.']
  market varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region where the rate is primarily used (e.g., "EMEA", "APAC").']
  precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places the rate is rounded to.']
  provider_identifier varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier of the external or internal provider supplying the rate.']
  quote_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the quote currency in the pair.']
  rate_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the rate based on market segment.']
  rate_pair varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier of the currency pair, e.g., "USD_EUR".']
  rate_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the rate data – European Central Bank (ECB), Reuters, or internal pricing engine.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate: spot, mid, buy, sell, or dynamic currency conversion (DCC).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system or feed that supplied the rate record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rate record.']

  Note: 'Foreign exchange rate reference feed capturing daily and intraday FX rates for all currency pairs used in cross-border payment processing and DCC. Stores base currency, quote currency, exchange rate, rate type (spot, mid, buy, sell, DCC), rate source (ECB, Reuters, internal), effective timestamp, expiry timestamp, and rate provider identifier. Serves as the authoritative FX rate reference for transaction conversion, settlement FX, and DCC pricing across the platform.'
}

}

Table reference.iban_structure {
  iban_structure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the IBAN structure record. _canonical_skip_reason: Inferred role REFERENCE_LOOKUP; no minimum category enforcement required.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Linking IBAN structure to country for validation rules; country_code becomes redundant.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Linking IBAN structure to currency for validation rules; currency_code becomes redundant.']
  account_number_length varchar [note: 'Length of the account number segment in characters.']
  account_number_position varchar [note: 'Location of the account number within the BBAN.']
  bank_identifier_length varchar [note: 'Length of the bank identifier segment in characters.']
  bank_identifier_position varchar [note: 'Location of the bank identifier within the BBAN (e.g., start, middle, end).']
  bban_length varchar [note: 'Number of characters in the BBAN portion of the IBAN.']
  bban_pattern varchar [note: 'Pattern describing the structure of the BBAN portion of the IBAN (e.g., 4!a6!n2!a).']
  bban_regex varchar [note: 'Regular expression used to validate the BBAN portion of the IBAN.']
  branch_identifier_length varchar [note: 'Length of the branch identifier segment in characters.']
  branch_identifier_position varchar [note: 'Location of the branch identifier within the BBAN.']
  check_digit_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to calculate the two check digits of the IBAN, typically MOD‑97‑10.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the IBAN structure record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the IBAN format definition is valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the IBAN format definition is no longer valid (null if still active).']
  example_iban varchar [note: 'A valid example IBAN for the country, used for testing and documentation.']
  iban_length varchar [note: 'Total number of characters in a valid IBAN for the country.']
  iban_regex varchar [note: 'Regular expression used by validation engines to verify the full IBAN format.']
  iban_structure_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the IBAN format and any special notes.']
  iban_structure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IBAN definition.']
  last_validated_date date [note: 'Date when the IBAN format definition was last validated against live data.']
  national_check_digit_length varchar [note: 'Length of the national check digit segment in characters.']
  national_check_digit_position varchar [note: 'Location of any national check digit within the BBAN.']
  sepa_member_flag boolean [note: 'True if the country participates in the Single Euro Payments Area (SEPA).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system or standard that supplied the IBAN definition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the IBAN structure record.']
  validation_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the validation rule set applied to this IBAN structure.']

  Note: 'IBAN (International Bank Account Number) structure reference defining the format, length, and validation rules for IBANs by country. Stores country code, IBAN length, BBAN format pattern, check digit algorithm, example IBAN, SEPA membership flag, and effective date. Used by payment validation engines to verify IBAN format correctness before cross-border payment submission, reducing SWIFT rejection rates.'
}

Table reference.nacha_return_code {
  nacha_return_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each NACHA return code record.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Linking NACHA return code to regulatory jurisdiction for compliance tracking; regulatory_reference string becomes redundant.']
  applicable_sec_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of Standard Entry Class (SEC) codes to which the return code applies (e.g., PPD, CCD, WEB, TEL). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PPD|CCD|WEB|TEL|CTX|BCH|RCK|POP|POS|ARC|BOC|CIE|CIP|CNR|DNE|EPN|IAT|MTE|PPD|TEL|WEB|XCK] ']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return code record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the return code definition becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the return code definition is no longer valid (null if still active).']
  is_noc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the return code also serves as a Notification of Change (NOC) code.']
  nacha_return_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the return code record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or business comments about the return code.']
  reinitiation_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the originating institution may re‑initiate the ACH entry after this return; otherwise false.']
  return_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the return reason (administrative, account, authorization, or network).']
  return_code varchar [note: 'Standard four‑character NACHA return code (e.g., R01, R02).']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the return reason associated with the return code.']
  return_time_limit_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of banking days allowed for the return to be processed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the return code definition (e.g., ACH Processor, Core Banking).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the return code record.']

  Note: 'NACHA ACH return and notification of change (NOC) code reference catalog defining all standardized return reason codes for ACH transactions. Stores return code, return reason description, return category (administrative, account, authorization, network), NOC flag, re-initiation eligibility, time limit for return (banking days), and applicable SEC codes (PPD, CCD, WEB, TEL). Used by ACH processing and settlement reconciliation modules.'
}

Table reference.emv_tag {
  emv_tag_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each EMV tag record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a reference lookup catalog of EMV data elements, thus no minimum role categories required.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Linking EMV tag to regulatory jurisdiction for security standards mapping; regulatory_jurisdiction string becomes redundant.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance verification status against EMVCo and PCI DSS requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tag record was first created in the reference catalog.']
  data_format varchar [note: 'Format of the tag value as defined by EMVCo.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the tag definition is considered active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the tag definition is no longer active (null if indefinite).']
  emv_tag_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tag definition.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tag is deprecated and should no longer be used.']
  iso_7816_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the ISO 7816 standard clause that defines the tag.']
  kernel_applicability varchar [note: 'Kernel types that support the tag (contact, contactless, NFC, or both).']
  length_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of bytes for the tag value.']
  length_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of bytes for the tag value.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes for additional context, such as implementation tips or historical changes.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the tag data: card, terminal, or issuer.']
  tag_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the data element represented by the tag.']
  tag_hex varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal code of the EMV tag as defined by EMVCo (e.g., \'9F02\').']
  tag_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the EMV tag (e.g., \'Amount, Authorised (Numeric)\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tag record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the tag definition for change management.']

  Note: 'EMV tag reference catalog defining all standardized EMV data elements used in chip card transaction processing per EMVCo specifications. Stores EMV tag identifier (hex), tag name, tag description, data format, length (min/max), source (card, terminal, issuer), kernel applicability (contact, contactless, NFC), and ISO 7816 reference. Used by the authorization engine and terminal management system for EMV transaction parsing and cryptogram validation.'
}

  kyc_document_type_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the document type for KYC processes.']
  kyc_document_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the document type, its purpose and typical usage.']
  max_file_size_mb varchar [note: 'Maximum permissible file size for the document upload, expressed in megabytes.']

  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that originated the document type definition.']
  supporting_documents_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional supporting documents must accompany this document type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document type record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the document type definition, incremented on changes to attributes or rules.']

  Note: 'KYC (Know Your Customer) and KYB (Know Your Business) document type reference catalog defining all accepted identity and business verification document types. Stores document type code, document name, document category (government ID, proof of address, business registration, beneficial ownership, financial statement), accepted jurisdictions, expiry tracking required flag, and regulatory basis (BSA, AML, PSD2 SCA). Used by merchant onboarding, cardholder KYC, and compliance verification workflows.'
}

  control_reference_standard varchar [note: 'External standard(s) that the control aligns with.']

  control_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the control definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the control became effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the control is no longer effective (null if indefinite).']
  nist_csf_category varchar [note: 'Corresponding NIST Cybersecurity Framework category.']
  nist_csf_subcategory varchar [note: 'Specific NIST CSF subcategory mapping.']
  pci_version varchar [note: 'Version of the PCI DSS standard that defines the control.']
  requirement_description varchar [note: 'Full text of the PCI DSS requirement.']
  requirement_number varchar [note: 'Number of the PCI DSS requirement to which the control maps.']
  saq_type varchar [note: 'Applicable SAQ type for the control. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAQ A|SAQ A-EP|SAQ B|SAQ B-IP|SAQ C|SAQ C-VT|SAQ D — promote to reference product]']
  testing_procedure varchar [note: 'Step‑by‑step method for testing compliance with the control.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']

  Note: 'PCI DSS control reference catalog defining all PCI DSS requirements, sub-requirements, and testing procedures applicable to the payments platform. Stores PCI DSS version, requirement number, requirement description, control category (network security, access control, encryption, monitoring, policy), applicability scope (SAQ type, merchant level, service provider), and NIST CSF mapping. Used by compliance management, audit, and security assessment workflows.'

Used by real-time sanctions screening in transaction processing and merchant onboarding.'
}

Table reference.settlement_cycle {
  settlement_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each settlement cycle configuration.']

  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Linking settlement cycle to currency for currency‑specific settlement processing; currency_code string becomes redundant.']
  payment_rail_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.payment_rail. Business justification: Linking settlement cycle to payment rail for settlement timing; payment_rail string becomes redundant.']
  applicable_country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country codes where the cycle is valid.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement cycle record was first created in the system.']
  cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Local time of day when the cut‑off for the settlement batch occurs.']
  cutoff_timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the cut‑off time (e.g., "America/New_York").']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the settlement cycle within the reference domain.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the settlement cycle (e.g., "T+1 US Card Settlement").']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the settlement cycle becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the settlement cycle expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  funding_lag_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between settlement execution and merchant funding.']
  is_default_cycle boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cycle is the default for its payment rail and card scheme.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement cycle configuration was last reviewed for compliance or operational relevance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes for operational or compliance teams.']
  processing_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard processing fee expressed as a percentage of the settled amount for this cycle.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory domain (e.g., PCI DSS, PSD2, AML) governing the settlement cycle.']
  settlement_cycle_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context or business rules.']
  settlement_cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement cycle configuration.']
  settlement_frequency varchar [note: 'How often settlements are executed for the cycle (e.g., real‑time, daily, T+1).']
  settlement_window_end varchar [note: 'End time (HH:mm) of the daily settlement processing window.']
  settlement_window_start varchar [note: 'Start time (HH:mm) of the daily settlement processing window.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement cycle record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the settlement cycle definition for change management.']

  Note: 'Settlement cycle reference configuration defining the settlement timing, cutoff schedules, and funding windows for each payment rail, card scheme, and geographic region. Stores cycle code, cycle name, payment rail, card scheme, settlement frequency (daily, T+1, T+2, T+3, real-time), cutoff time, cutoff timezone, funding lag (days), applicable country codes, and effective date range. Used by the settlement engine to determine batch generation schedules and merchant funding timelines.'
}
