Table reference.qr_code {
  qr_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each QR code record.']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant associated with a static QR code.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount encoded in a dynamic QR code for a specific transaction.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel or ecosystem through which the QR code is intended to be used.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the QR code (e.g., PCI DSS, PSD2).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the amount.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the QR code becomes invalid.']
  generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the QR code was created in the system.']
  is_test_code boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the QR code is generated for testing purposes.']
  last_scanned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent scan of the QR code.']
  payload varchar [note: 'Encoded data that the QR code carries (e.g., payment reference, UPI VPA, merchant ID).']
  qr_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the QR code.']
  qr_code_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the QR code is static (merchant), dynamic (transaction), or peer‑to‑peer.']
  qr_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the QR code format or specification used.']
  scan_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the QR code has been scanned.']
  scheme varchar [note: 'Name of the wallet or payment scheme that generated the QR code (e.g., UPI, proprietary).']
  usage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of allowed scans before the QR code is automatically deactivated.']

  Note: 'QR code payment artifact record — static or dynamic QR codes generated for merchant acceptance or P2P payment initiation. Captures QR code ID, QR type (static merchant, dynamic transaction, P2P), QR payload (encoded reference), wallet scheme, merchant reference, amount (for dynamic), currency, expiry timestamp, scan count, status (active, expired, used), and generation timestamp. Supports QR-based payment flows including UPI and proprietary schemes.'
}

Table reference.wallet_scheme {
  wallet_scheme_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each digital wallet scheme record.']
  settlement_currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Scheme settlement currency aligns with reference.currency to enforce settlement rules and fee calculations.']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Scheme supports specific transaction types; linking to reference.transaction_type enables validation and fee logic.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the scheme\'s compliance certification process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scheme record was first created.']
  cross_border_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scheme can be used for cross‑border transactions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code representing the primary currency used for fees and limits.']
  dcc_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Dynamic Currency Conversion is available for the scheme.']
  dispute_resolution_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how disputes are settled for this scheme.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the scheme becomes effective for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the scheme ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of ISO‑3 country codes where the scheme is available.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the scheme was first made available to customers.']
  interchange_fee_model varchar [note: 'Model used to calculate interchange fees for the scheme.']
  interchange_fee_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Base interchange fee rate expressed as a percentage (e.g., 0.1500 = 0.15%).']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the scheme\'s parameters.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount allowed per transaction under this scheme.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary amount required per transaction under this scheme.']
  pci_dss_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the scheme meets PCI DSS requirements.']
  psd2_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the scheme complies with PSD2 strong customer authentication rules.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk rating (0‑100) assigned to the scheme based on security and compliance factors.']
  scheme_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or document reference for the legal agreement governing the scheme.']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used internally to reference the scheme.']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the wallet scheme (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay).']
  scheme_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scheme: OEM wallet, proprietary, UPI, or QR based.']
  supported_nfc_kernels varchar [note: 'List of NFC kernel versions supported by the scheme (e.g., NXP, STMicro).']
  supported_provisioning_methods varchar [note: 'Provisioning channels supported (online, offline, in‑app, API).']
  supported_token_requestor_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of token requestor identifiers authorized for this scheme.']
  tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scheme supports tokenization of payment credentials.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scheme record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the scheme definition.']
  wallet_scheme_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the scheme, its purpose and key characteristics.']
  wallet_scheme_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scheme within the platform.']

  Note: 'Reference master for supported digital wallet schemes and their operational parameters — Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay, proprietary wallet brands, and UPI. Captures scheme ID, scheme name, scheme type (OEM wallet, proprietary, UPI, QR), supported token requestor IDs, supported NFC kernels, supported provisioning methods, scheme certification status, scheme agreement reference, and go-live date. Governs which wallet schemes are active on the platform.'
}

Table reference.issuer_wallet_config {
  issuer_wallet_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each issuer wallet configuration record.']
  ecosystem_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the issuing financial institution that owns the BIN range.']
  issuer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the issuing financial institution that owns the BIN range.']
  wallet_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital wallet scheme (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay) for which this configuration applies.']
  allowed_card_schemes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of card scheme codes (e.g., VISA, MASTERCARD) that may be tokenized.']
  allowed_countries varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of three‑letter country codes where token usage is permitted.']
  compliance_pci_dss_version varchar [note: 'Version of the PCI DSS standard that this configuration complies with.']
  compliance_psd2_version varchar [note: 'Version of the PSD2 regulatory framework applicable to the configuration.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the configuration used by operations and reporting.']
  config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the configuration record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the configuration ceases to be effective; null for open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the configuration becomes effective for the issuer.']
  green_path_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score percentage below which transactions follow the green (low‑risk) path.']
  is_cross_border_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cross‑border transactions are allowed for tokens issued under this configuration.']
  is_dynamic_currency_conversion_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates support for DCC in tokenized transactions.']
  is_tokenization_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tokenization is enabled for this issuer under the configuration.']
  issuer_wallet_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the configuration purpose and scope.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the last update.']
  max_tokens_per_fpan varchar [note: 'Maximum number of active tokens that may be issued for a single primary account number.']
  otp_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the OTP for step‑up authentication.']
  provisioning_rule_set varchar [note: 'JSON or structured text describing the step‑by‑step provisioning flow for tokens.']
  red_path_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score percentage above which transactions follow the red (high‑risk) path.']
  risk_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum risk score allowed for automatic token issuance without manual review.']
  step_up_auth_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional authentication is required during token provisioning.']
  step_up_trigger_condition varchar [note: 'Condition that triggers step‑up authentication.']
  token_assurance_level varchar [note: 'Assurance classification of tokens issued under this configuration.']
  token_expiry_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after issuance that a token automatically expires if not used.']
  token_expiry_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing token lifetime: rolling (renew on use), fixed (static date), or session‑based.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the configuration.']
  yellow_path_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk score percentage range for the yellow (moderate‑risk) path.']

  Note: 'Issuer-specific configuration governing how a card issuer participates in the wallet tokenization platform — provisioning rules, step-up authentication requirements, token assurance levels, and green/yellow/red path thresholds. Captures config ID, issuer BIN range, wallet scheme ID, provisioning path rules, step-up trigger conditions, OTP delivery method, maximum tokens per FPAN, token expiry policy, and last updated timestamp. Enables per-issuer customization of the tokenization flow.'
}

Table reference.scheme_message {
  scheme_message_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the scheme message record.']
  scheme_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment scheme (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, EMVCo) associated with the message.']
  correlation_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used to correlate this message with related processing flows or transactions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scheme message record was first created in the data lake.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the message was received from the scheme (inbound) or sent to the scheme (outbound).']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Error identifier returned when processing fails, aligned with scheme-specific error taxonomy.']
  message_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the scheme message was generated or received.']
  message_type varchar [note: 'Category of the scheme communication, such as provisioning response, token status update, lifecycle notification, or cryptogram validation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional context, comments, or audit remarks related to the message.']
  payload_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash of the raw message payload for integrity verification.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the message within the wallet platform.']
  raw_message_uri varchar [note: 'Pointer (URI) to the encrypted raw message payload stored in secure storage.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of retry attempts performed for this message after a failure.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system that produced the scheme message (e.g., Digital Wallet Platform).']
  status_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the outcome or state of the message processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scheme message record.']

  Note: 'Network scheme messaging record for wallet-related scheme communications — EMVCo token lifecycle notifications, Visa VTS and Mastercard MDES API messages, provisioning responses, and token status updates received from or sent to card schemes. Captures message ID, scheme ID, message type (provisioning response, token status update, lifecycle notification, cryptogram validation), direction (inbound, outbound), message timestamp, processing status, retry count, and raw message reference (pointer to encrypted store). Supports scheme integration audit trail.'
}

Table reference.iso_message_type {
  iso_message_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each ISO message type record.']
  response_iso_message_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on iso_message_type (response_iso_message_type_id)']
  transaction_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.transaction_type. Business justification: Linking ISO message type to transaction type for message routing; transaction_type string becomes redundant.']
  applicable_payment_rail varchar [note: 'Payment rail(s) that can carry this message type (e.g., card, ACH, SWIFT, SEPA, RTP, P2P).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the message type meets all applicable regulatory and standards requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the message type record was first created in the catalog.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the message initiates a request, provides a response, or is a standalone notification.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the message type becomes valid for processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the message type must no longer be used (null if indefinite).']
  example_message varchar [note: 'A representative example of a fully populated message instance in XML/JSON format.']
  fee_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether fees are always applied, never applied, or applied conditionally for this message type.']
  field_count varchar [note: 'Total count of data elements defined for this message type.']
  is_batch boolean [note: 'True if the message type is typically transmitted in batch files rather than real‑time.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'True if the message type supports or is commonly used for cross‑border transactions.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'True if the message type is retired or superseded by a newer definition.']
  is_fee_applicable boolean [note: 'True if the message type may incur processing fees or interchange charges.']
  is_fraud_sensitive boolean [note: 'True if transactions using this message type are subject to heightened fraud monitoring.']
  is_real_time boolean [note: 'True if the message type is used in real‑time processing flows.']
  iso_message_type_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the message purpose, structure, and usage.']
  iso_message_type_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the message type within the organization.']
  message_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the message (e.g., financial, status, reporting). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: financial|status|reporting|reconciliation|settlement|risk|compliance — promote to reference product]']
  message_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the ISO message type.']
  message_standard varchar [note: 'The ISO family to which the message belongs: ISO 8583 (card‑based) or ISO 20022 (financial messaging).']
  message_type_code varchar [note: 'Standard code that uniquely identifies the message type within ISO 20022 or ISO 8583 specifications.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes for internal commentary, version history, or special handling instructions.']
  processing_code varchar [note: 'ISO 8583 processing code that defines the transaction processing logic (e.g., "000000" for purchase).']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Code of the jurisdiction whose regulations apply to the use of this message type (e.g., "US", "EU").']
  requires_authentication boolean [note: 'True if processing this message type mandates a prior authentication step.']
  schema_url varchar [note: 'Link to the official schema definition (XSD, XSD, or JSON Schema) for the message type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the message type record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the message definition (e.g., "2021-09", "v2.1").']

  Note: 'ISO 20022 and ISO 8583 message type reference catalog defining all standard financial message types (pacs.008, pacs.002, pain.001, camt.053, etc.) used in payment processing, clearing, and settlement messaging.'
}

Table reference.issuer {
  issuer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for issuer']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Add country reference to issuer to link issuer to its jurisdictional country, replacing the free‑text country_code.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Add currency reference to issuer to link issuer to its base currency, replacing the free‑text currency_code.']
  parent_issuer_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on issuer (parent_issuer_id)']
  abbreviation varchar [note: 'Standard abbreviation or ticker symbol for the issuer.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the issuer\'s primary business address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the issuer\'s primary business address (optional).']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the issuer\'s primary business location.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification based on market reach.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the issuer with regulatory requirements.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for issuer communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the issuer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the issuer record was first created.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned by rating agencies.']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate daily transaction amount limit for the issuer.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the issuer\'s business and services.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the issuer became effective for processing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the issuer ceased to be effective (null if still active).']
  is_preferred boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this issuer is preferred for routing decisions.']
  is_test_issuer boolean [note: 'True if the issuer is used for testing environments only.']
  is_virtual boolean [note: 'True if the issuer represents a virtual or tokenized entity.']
  issuer_code varchar [note: 'ISO 9362 Business Identifier Code uniquely identifying the issuer.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum single transaction amount the issuer permits.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the issuing institution.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes or remarks about the issuer.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the issuer\'s primary address.']
  processing_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transaction processing (e.g., real‑time, batch).']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration identifier assigned by the jurisdiction of incorporation.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority governing the issuer (e.g., FCA, OCC).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Internal risk assessment rating for the issuer.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for settlement of transactions with this issuer.']
  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of settlement processing (e.g., T+1, T+2).']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method used for settlement (e.g., ACH, SWIFT).']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used name for the issuer.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the issuer\'s primary address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the issuer.']
  supported_card_schemes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of card schemes the issuer supports (e.g., Visa,MasterCard,Amex). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Visa|MasterCard|Amex|Discover|JCB|UnionPay — promote to reference product]']
  supported_payment_methods varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of payment methods (e.g., credit,debit,prepaid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit|debit|prepaid|digital_wallet|contactless — promote to reference product]']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identification number for the issuer (e.g., EIN, VAT).']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the issuer\'s headquarters (e.g., America/New_York).']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the issuing organization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the issuer record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the issuer.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for issuer. Referenced by issuer_id.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the employee.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Employee payroll expenses are allocated to cost centers; cost_center_id on employee enables accurate expense tracking.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Required for tax residency and AML compliance reporting; payroll must know employee\'s country of tax residence.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Ensures payroll currency aligns with master currency list for exchange rate calculations and reporting.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Employees belong to a legal entity for tax and compliance reporting; legal_entity_id creates the required link.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee\'s direct manager.']
  work_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.work_schedule. Business justification: Employees are assigned a work schedule; many employees can share the same schedule, so employee gets a foreign key to work_schedule.']
  workforce_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.workforce_location. Business justification: Replace free‑text work_location on employee with a foreign key to the authoritative workforce_location table.']
  address_full varchar [note: 'Full mailing address of the employee\'s primary residence.']
  background_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of pre-employment background screening.']
  bonus_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates if employee is eligible for performance bonus.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the employee.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the department to which the employee belongs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when employment became effective (may differ from hire date for retroactive hires).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary corporate email address.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of emergency contact person.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of emergency contact.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current employment lifecycle status.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment contract.']
  equity_grant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if employee has equity compensation.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the employee.']
  fte_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'FTE proportion (e.g., 1.00 for full-time).']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee was hired.']
  hourly_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly compensation rate for hourly employees.']
  job_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or band associated with the position for compensation.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official title of the employee\'s position.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the employee.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name as on official documents.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name of the employee, if any.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued personal identifier (e.g., SSN, SIN).']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if employee can accrue overtime.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary corporate phone number.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'Preferred name or nickname used by the employee.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employee record.']
  remote_status varchar [note: 'Indicates if employee works on-site, remotely, or hybrid.']
  reports_to_position varchar [note: 'Title of the position the employee reports to.']
  salary_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual base salary in USD.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax identifier for payroll and reporting.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employment ended, if applicable.']
  visa_status varchar [note: 'Immigration status for employment eligibility.']
  work_permit_number varchar [note: 'Number of work permit or visa, if applicable.']
  work_shift varchar [note: 'Scheduled shift pattern.']

  Note: 'Core SSOT master record for every employee of the Payments Fintech enterprise. Captures full identity, employment classification, hire date, employment type (full-time, part-time, contractor, intern), legal entity assignment, cost center, work location, employment status (active, on-leave, terminated), job title, grade band, and regulatory identifiers (national ID, tax ID). This is the authoritative golden record for workforce identity across all HR, compliance, and operational systems.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the position record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation of salaries uses the cost center linked to each position; cost_center_id enables accurate budgeting.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ledger.legal_entity. Business justification: Positions must be tied to a legal entity for payroll tax and reporting; legal_entity_id creates that association.']
  reporting_manager_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager\'s position to which this position reports.']
  reports_to_position_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on position (reports_to_position_id)']
  budgeted_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of headcount budgeted for this position (typically 1).']
  compensation_band varchar [note: 'Compensation band or range category for the position.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for salary amounts.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department name or code where the position resides.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the position is no longer effective (end of budget period), nullable if open-ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the position becomes effective (start of budget period).']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of a full-time workload allocated to the position (e.g., 1.0 for full-time).']
  grade_level varchar [note: 'Organizational grade or level assigned to the position.']
  is_remote_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the position can be performed remotely.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the job grouping similar roles.']
  job_function varchar [note: 'Specific functional area of the job.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Geographic location code (e.g., office site) for the position.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Human readable code for the position used in HR systems.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the position responsibilities and scope.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the position within the workforce planning lifecycle.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Employment type for the position.']
  recruitment_source varchar [note: 'Source channel through which the position was originally opened (e.g., internal, external, referral).']
  required_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum education level required (e.g., bachelor\'s, master\'s).']
  required_experience_years decimal(3,1) [note: 'Number of years of experience required for the position.']
  salary_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum salary for the position\'s compensation band, in local currency.']
  salary_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum salary for the position\'s compensation band, in local currency.']
  skill_set varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of key skills required for the position.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official job title for the position.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the position record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the position record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the position record.']

  Note: 'Authorized headcount position master defining each approved role slot within the organizational structure. Captures position code, job family, job function, grade level, FTE allocation, budgeted headcount, position status (open, filled, frozen, eliminated), reporting line, and the legal entity and cost center to which the position is budgeted. Distinct from the employee record — a position can be vacant or filled by multiple employees over time.'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each organizational unit.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.country. Business justification: Headcount and budgeting reports aggregate by country; linking to reference country provides standardized codes.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Budget allocations use reference currency for consistency across entities.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity that owns the unit.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on org_unit (parent_org_unit_id)']
  parent_unit_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediate parent unit in the hierarchy.']
  unit_head_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who heads the unit.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the unit\'s physical address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the unit\'s physical address (optional).']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest audit.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated to the unit, expressed in the unit\'s functional currency.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the unit\'s address.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the unit with regulatory requirements.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier used for budgeting and expense allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the organizational unit record was first created in the system.']
  data_retention_policy varchar [note: 'Policy description governing how long data associated with the unit is retained.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the unit ceased operation (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the unit became operational.']
  headcount varchar [note: 'Current number of full‑time equivalent employees assigned to the unit.']
  is_cost_allocation_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit participates in cost‑allocation calculations.']
  is_financial_reporting_unit boolean [note: 'True if the unit is a reporting entity for financial statements.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or financial audit for the unit.']
  org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used in internal systems to reference the unit.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the unit\'s purpose and scope.']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the unit (e.g., "Consumer Payments Division").']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the unit.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the unit\'s address.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the unit\'s administrative contact.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the unit\'s administrative contact.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 country code representing the primary region of the unit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|AUS|CHN|IND|BRA — promote to reference product]']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numerical risk rating assigned to the unit based on internal risk models.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the unit\'s primary location (e.g., "America/New_York").']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the unit within the enterprise hierarchy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the organizational unit record.']

  Note: 'Organizational unit master representing the hierarchical structure of the enterprise — divisions, departments, teams, and cost centers. Captures unit name, unit type (division, department, team, squad), parent unit reference for hierarchy traversal, effective date range, owning legal entity, geographic region, and unit head employee reference. Supports org chart rendering, headcount reporting, and cost allocation across the payments fintech enterprise.'
}

Table workforce.job_profile {
  job_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the job profile record.']
  parent_job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on job_profile (parent_job_profile_id)']
  reports_to_job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent job profile in the reporting hierarchy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the job profile record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the pay grade (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the department to which the role belongs.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the job profile becomes active for new hires.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the job profile is retired; null if open‑ended.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Classification under the Fair Labor Standards Act indicating exempt or non‑exempt status.']
  is_manager_role boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the role includes managerial responsibilities.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the job profile, used in HR and payroll systems.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of responsibilities, duties, and expectations for the role.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of related roles (e.g., Risk Management, Engineering).']
  job_function varchar [note: 'Specific functional area within the family (e.g., AML Monitoring, Data Engineering).']
  job_profile_level varchar [note: 'Organizational seniority level (e.g., Analyst, Manager, Director).']
  job_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job profile definition.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the role (e.g., Senior Fraud Analyst).']
  location_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the role (global, regional, local).']
  pay_grade_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the salary range for the role, expressed in the local currency.']
  pay_grade_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the salary range for the role, expressed in the local currency.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Employment arrangement for the role.']
  regulatory_license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role must hold a regulatory license (e.g., AML compliance officer).']
  regulatory_license_type varchar [note: 'Name of the specific regulatory license required, if any (e.g., MSB License).']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of professional certifications required (e.g., CISA, CAMS).']
  required_competencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of core competencies needed for the role (e.g., analytical thinking, communication).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the job profile record.']

  Note: 'Job profile catalog defining standardized role definitions used across the enterprise. Captures job code, job title, job family, job function, FLSA classification (exempt/non-exempt), pay grade range, required competencies, required certifications, and whether the role requires regulatory licensing (e.g., MSB compliance officer, AML analyst). Serves as the template from which positions and employee assignments are derived.'
}

  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment relationship (e.g., permanent, fixed‑term, zero‑hours, contractor).']
  contract_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the contract for change tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Classification level indicating the sensitivity of the contract data.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract terminates or expires; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes legally binding.']
  employee_classification varchar [note: 'Classification such as exempt, non‑exempt, contractor, etc.']
  employee_grade varchar [note: 'Organizational grade or level assigned to the employee.']
  full_time_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the contract is for a full‑time position; false otherwise.']
  governing_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Country or legal jurisdiction whose labor laws govern the contract.']
  non_compete_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a non‑compete clause is included.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the employee or employer must give to terminate the contract.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the employee is eligible for overtime compensation.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'How often salary payments are made to the employee.']
  payroll_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the payroll group to which the employee belongs.']
  probation_period_days varchar [note: 'Length of the initial probationary period measured in days.']
  remote_work_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is authorized to work remotely.']
  salary_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base salary or compensation amount agreed in the contract.']
  salary_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the salary amount.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is exempt from certain tax withholdings.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract was terminated.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for contract termination, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  work_location varchar [note: 'Primary physical location or office where the employee performs duties.']

  Note: 'Employment contract master record capturing the formal terms of engagement between the enterprise and an employee or contractor. Stores contract type (permanent, fixed-term, zero-hours, contractor SOW), start date, end date, notice period, probation period, governing jurisdiction, applicable collective bargaining agreement, and contract status. Critical for workforce compliance in the 200+ country footprint of the payments fintech enterprise.'
}

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compensation plan record was first created.']

  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the compensation plan expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended.']
  equity_grant_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates eligibility for equity‑based compensation.']
  equity_grant_type varchar [note: 'Type of equity award provided under the plan.']
  equity_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of equity units (e.g., shares, RSUs) granted.']
  grade_step varchar [note: 'Specific step within a pay grade, often reflecting seniority or performance.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the compensation plan is applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments related to the plan.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Regular interval at which base compensation is paid.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Organizational pay grade associated with the plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the compensation plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the compensation plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plan indicating the primary compensation model.']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether compensation is subject to tax withholding.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compensation plan record.']
  variable_pay_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for variable compensation such as bonuses or commissions.']
  vesting_schedule varchar [note: 'Textual description of the vesting timeline and conditions for equity awards.']

  Note: 'Compensation plan master defining the pay structure assigned to an employee or position. Captures base salary, pay frequency (monthly, bi-weekly, weekly), currency, pay grade, step within grade, variable pay eligibility, bonus target percentage, equity grant eligibility, and effective date range. Supports payroll processing, compensation benchmarking, and regulatory pay equity reporting across all jurisdictions.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_record {
  payroll_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the payroll record.']
  currency_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.currency. Business justification: Payroll reporting uses reference currency for audit trails and multi‑currency handling.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee receiving the payroll.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to reference.regulatory_jurisdiction. Business justification: Tax withholding and regulatory filing require linking payroll to master jurisdiction data.']
  reversal_payroll_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_record (reversal_payroll_record_id)']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Employee\'s bank account number for direct deposit.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Routing number identifying the employee\'s financial institution.']
  bonus_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional bonus compensation paid in the period.']
  commission_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Commission earnings paid to the employee for the period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll record was first created in the system.']
  gross_pay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total earnings before any deductions for the pay period.']
  health_insurance_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employer‑paid health insurance contribution for the pay period.']
  net_pay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final amount paid to the employee after deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for any additional comments or remarks about the payroll record.']
  other_employer_contributions_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of any additional employer contributions (e.g., bonuses, allowances).']
  overtime_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary compensation for overtime hours.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours worked in the pay period.']
  pay_cycle varchar [note: 'Regular frequency at which the employee is paid.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the payroll period.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the payroll period.']
  pay_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payroll run was executed.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to disburse the payroll to the employee.']
  payment_status_date date [note: 'Date on which the payroll payment was successfully posted.']
  payroll_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the payroll run, unique per employee and pay period.']
  payroll_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll record.']
  payroll_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll component (e.g., salary, hourly, bonus).']
  pension_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employer‑paid pension contribution for the pay period.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided when a payroll record is reversed or voided.']
  social_security_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Employer‑paid social security contribution for the pay period.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is exempt from tax withholding for this period.']
  tax_withholding_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax withheld from the employee\'s earnings for the period.']
  total_deductions_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount of all deductions applied to the gross pay.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payroll record.']

  Note: 'Payroll processing record capturing each pay run result for an employee. Stores pay period start and end dates, gross pay, net pay, total deductions, tax withholding amounts by jurisdiction, employer contributions (pension, social security, health), pay run status (calculated, approved, paid, reversed), payment method (direct deposit, check, wire), and bank account reference. The authoritative transactional record for each payroll disbursement event.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_deduction {
  payroll_deduction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each payroll deduction line.']
  adjusted_payroll_deduction_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_deduction (adjusted_payroll_deduction_id)']
  benefit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the benefit plan or legal order governing the deduction.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom the deduction applies.']
  payroll_record_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll processing batch (header) that contains this deduction.']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll processing batch (header) that contains this deduction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the deduction line was initially recorded.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the deduction amount.']
  deduction_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount deducted from gross pay, expressed in the payroll currency.']
  deduction_basis varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the deduction is a fixed amount (flat) or a percentage of gross pay.']
  deduction_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the deduction in payroll configuration.']
  deduction_description varchar [note: 'Full description of the deduction for reporting and employee communication.']
  deduction_quantity varchar [note: 'Numeric count that drives the deduction amount when applicable.']
  deduction_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage rate used to compute the deduction when basis = percentage.']
  deduction_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the deduction (e.g., federal tax, pension contribution, health insurance premium).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the deduction starts to apply.']
  employer_match_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount contributed by the employer for matching benefit plans.']
  end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the deduction ceases; null for ongoing deductions.']
  is_post_tax boolean [note: 'True if the deduction is applied after tax calculation.']
  is_pre_tax boolean [note: 'True if the deduction reduces taxable income (pre‑tax).']
  is_voluntary boolean [note: 'True for employee‑initiated, voluntary deductions (e.g., 401(k) elective).']
  legal_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the legal order (e.g., garnishment, child support) that mandates the deduction.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the deduction line in the payroll run.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or explanations for the deduction line.']
  payroll_deduction_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the deduction (e.g., active, reversed due to correction).']
  tax_withheld_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the deduction that represents tax withheld.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the deduction line.']

  Note: 'Individual deduction line item within a payroll record capturing each pre-tax and post-tax deduction applied to an employee\'s gross pay. Stores deduction type (federal tax, state tax, pension contribution, health insurance premium, garnishment, union dues, FSA/HSA contribution), deduction amount, deduction basis (flat, percentage), and the governing benefit plan or legal order reference. Enables granular payroll audit and regulatory tax reporting.'
}

Table workforce.benefit_enrollment {

  contribution_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for contribution amounts (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  coverage_end_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s benefit coverage ends (may differ from termination_date).']
  coverage_start_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s benefit coverage actually begins (may differ from effective_date).']

  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the enrollment record.']

  employee_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount the employee contributes toward the benefit each pay period.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount the employer contributes toward the benefit each pay period.']

  enrollment_method varchar [note: 'Channel used to submit the enrollment.']

  enrollment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the enrollment request.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment record.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'True if this record represents the employee\'s current active enrollment for the plan.']
  last_updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the enrollment record.']
  payroll_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency with which contributions are deducted from payroll.']
  payroll_deduction_code varchar [note: 'Code used in payroll system to map the contribution to the correct deduction line.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Internal code that uniquely identifies the benefit plan within the benefits catalog.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the benefit plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the benefit plan (e.g., medical, dental, vision, life insurance, disability, pension/401k, FSA, HSA, EAP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical|dental|vision|life|disability|pension|fsa|hsa|eap — promote to reference product]']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the benefit plan, useful for tracking plan changes over time.']
  post_tax_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contribution is taken from post‑tax earnings.']
  pre_tax_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contribution is taken from pre‑tax earnings.']
  taxability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee contribution is tax‑deductible (true) or not (false).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the benefit enrollment ends or is scheduled to end (null if open‑ended).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the enrollment record.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'True if the employee has waived participation in the benefit.']

  Note: 'Employee benefit enrollment record capturing active enrollment in employer-sponsored benefit plans. Stores benefit plan type (medical, dental, vision, life insurance, disability, pension/401k, FSA, HSA, EAP), plan name, coverage tier (employee only, employee + spouse, family), enrollment effective date, termination date, employee contribution amount, employer contribution amount, and enrollment status. Supports benefits administration and ACA/regulatory compliance reporting.'
}

Table workforce.leave_request {
  leave_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the leave request record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or HR representative who approved or rejected the request.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or HR representative who approved or rejected the request.']
  original_leave_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on leave_request (original_leave_request_id)']
  primary_leave_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who submitted the leave request.']
  accrual_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the specific accrual rule set governing this leave type.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was approved, rejected, or otherwise acted upon.']
  approved_days varchar [note: 'Number of days approved by the manager or HR after review.']
  attachment_flag boolean [note: 'True if supporting documentation (e.g., medical certificate) is attached to the request.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the leave request record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day of the requested leave period.']
  entitlement_balance_at_approval varchar [note: 'Accrued leave balance remaining after the request has been approved.']
  entitlement_balance_at_request varchar [note: 'Accrued leave balance available to the employee when the request was submitted.']
  extended_until_date date [note: 'New end date when a leave request is extended.']
  is_accrual boolean [note: 'True if the leave consumes accrued entitlement; false if it is granted without accrual (e.g., discretionary).']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'True if the leave request is marked confidential (e.g., medical or legal leave).']
  is_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the employee meets eligibility criteria for the requested leave type at the time of request.']
  is_extended boolean [note: 'True if the leave period was extended beyond the originally requested dates.']
  is_paid_leave boolean [note: 'True if the leave is paid according to company policy; false for unpaid leave.']
  is_partial_day boolean [note: 'True if the request includes a partial (half‑day) leave.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country or region code indicating the legal jurisdiction governing the leave entitlement.']
  leave_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the leave request.']
  leave_type varchar [note: 'Category of leave requested (e.g., annual, sick, parental, FMLA, sabbatical, bereavement, jury duty, unpaid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: annual|sick|parental|fmla|sabbatical|bereavement|jury_duty|unpaid — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments entered by the employee or approver.']
  partial_day_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the partial day is taken in the morning or afternoon.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation provided when a leave request is rejected.']
  remaining_balance_days varchar [note: 'Employee\'s leave balance remaining after this request is accounted for.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the leave request, often used in HR workflows and reporting.']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the leave request submission.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the employee submitted the leave request.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar day of the requested leave period.']
  total_requested_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days (including partial days) the employee initially requested.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the leave request record.']

  Note: 'Employee leave request and entitlement record capturing all absence management events. Stores leave type (annual leave, sick leave, parental leave, FMLA, sabbatical, bereavement, jury duty, unpaid leave), requested start date, end date, total days requested, approved days, leave status (pending, approved, rejected, cancelled, in-progress), approver reference, and jurisdiction-specific leave entitlement balance. Supports statutory leave compliance across all operating jurisdictions.'
}

Table workforce.leave_balance {
  leave_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each leave balance record.']
  carryover_leave_balance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on leave_balance (carryover_leave_balance_id)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom the leave balance applies.']
  accrual_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate leave accruals.']
  accrual_rate_per_month decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard accrual rate of leave days per month.']
  accrued_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total leave days accrued during the policy year.']
  adjusted_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Manual adjustments to leave balance (additions or deductions).']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to audit trail record for changes to this leave balance.']
  balance_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for leave balances.']
  carried_forward_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Leave days carried forward from previous policy year.']
  carryover_limit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum number of days that can be carried over per policy.']
  closing_balance decimal(5,2) [note: 'Leave balance remaining at end of policy year.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the leave balance record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when this leave balance period becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when this leave balance period ends (nullable if open-ended).']
  forfeited_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Leave days forfeited at end of policy year per policy.']
  is_eligible_for_carryover boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is eligible to carry over unused leave to next year.']
  is_paid_leave boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the leave type is paid.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the leave payout is taxable.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the jurisdiction governing leave policy.']
  last_accrual_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent accrual event for this balance.']
  last_taken_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent leave taken event.']
  leave_balance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the leave balance record.']
  leave_type varchar [note: 'Category of leave entitlement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding adjustments or special conditions.']
  opening_balance decimal(5,2) [note: 'Leave balance at the start of the policy year.']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the leave policy is applicable.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the leave balance data.']
  statutory_minimum decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum leave days required by law for the employee\'s jurisdiction.']
  taken_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Leave days taken (used) during the policy year.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the leave balance record.']

  Note: 'Employee leave balance master tracking accrued, used, and remaining leave entitlements by leave type and policy year. Captures leave type, policy year, opening balance (days), accrued days, taken days, adjusted days, carried-forward days, forfeited days, and closing balance. Supports leave liability reporting for financial provisioning and ensures compliance with statutory minimum leave entitlements across 200+ jurisdictions.'
}

Table workforce.performance_review {
  performance_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for performance review record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit predefined role categories, treated as OTHER.']
  primary_performance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee being reviewed.']
  prior_performance_review_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on performance_review (prior_performance_review_id)']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who performed the review.']
  calibration_group varchar [note: 'Identifier of the calibration group to which this review belongs.']
  calibration_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated score from the calibration process.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Status of the calibration process for this review.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments provided by the reviewer.']
  compensation_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review resulted in a compensation change (e.g., raise, bonus).']
  compensation_change_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of compensation change resulting from the review.']
  compensation_change_type varchar [note: 'Type of compensation adjustment (raise, bonus, or none).']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the review record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance review record was created.']
  development_areas varchar [note: 'Areas for development or improvement identified for the employee.']
  employee_department varchar [note: 'Department to which the employee belongs during the review period.']
  employee_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location or office of the employee during the review.']
  employee_position varchar [note: 'Job title or position of the employee at the time of review.']
  goals_achieved varchar [note: 'Summary of goals the employee achieved during the review period.']
  goals_set varchar [note: 'Performance goals established for the employee at the start of the review period.']
  is_finalized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review has been finalized and locked.']
  overall_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric overall rating assigned to the employee for the review period.']
  performance_improvement_plan_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a performance improvement plan was initiated as a result of the review.']
  performance_review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the performance review.']
  promotion_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion, if any, becomes effective.']
  promotion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for promotion based on this review.']
  rating_explanation varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation supporting the overall rating.']
  rating_scale varchar [note: 'Scale used for the overall performance rating (e.g., 1-5, 1-10, custom).']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the performance review was finalized.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'The appraisal cycle type (e.g., annual, mid-year, probation, performance improvement plan).']
  review_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored performance review document.']
  review_period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the performance review period.']
  review_period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the performance review period.']
  review_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the review record to support revisions.']
  strengths varchar [note: 'Key strengths identified for the employee during the review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the performance review record.']

  Note: 'Employee performance review record capturing formal appraisal cycles. Stores review cycle (annual, mid-year, probation, pip), review period start and end dates, overall performance rating, rating scale used, reviewer employee reference, calibration status, review completion date, and whether the review triggered a compensation action or performance improvement plan. Supports talent management, promotion decisions, and regulatory documentation for employment actions.'
}

Table workforce.goal {
  goal_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the performance goal.']
  alignment_objective_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the company‑wide strategic objective that the goal supports.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the team to which the goal is assigned, if applicable.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who owns or is responsible for the goal.']
  parent_goal_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on goal (parent_goal_id)']
  team_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the team to which the goal is assigned, if applicable.']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantitative value captured during or after the review period representing realized performance.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the owner or manager regarding the goal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goal record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned completion or expiration date for the goal.']
  goal_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the goal indicating whether it is an individual, team, or company‑wide OKR.']
  goal_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the purpose, scope, and expected outcome of the goal.']
  goal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the goal.']
  is_calibrated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the goal\'s weight and target have been calibrated across the organization.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the goal is required for the employee\'s role or compensation eligibility.']
  last_calibrated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent calibration activity for the goal.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the target and actual values (e.g., count, percentage, USD, hours, points).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the goal.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the goal becomes effective for the employee or team.']
  target_metric varchar [note: 'Name of the key performance indicator or metric that the goal is measured against.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantitative value that defines the goal\'s intended achievement level.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the goal as defined by the employee or manager.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the goal record.']
  visibility varchar [note: 'Scope of audience that can view the goal within the organization.']
  weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Portion of the overall performance rating that this goal contributes, expressed as a percentage.']

  Note: 'Individual and team goal record linked to a performance review cycle. Captures goal title, goal description, goal category (individual, team, company OKR), target metric, measurement unit, target value, actual value, goal weight (percentage of overall rating), goal status (draft, active, completed, cancelled), and alignment to company strategic objective. Supports OKR frameworks and performance calibration in the payments fintech enterprise.'
}

Table workforce.headcount {
  headcount_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each headcount snapshot record. _canonical_skip_reason: Snapshot entity representing headcount per org unit and date, not a persistent master or transaction.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (company) under which the workforce is employed.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the organizational unit (e.g., department, division) to which the headcount applies.']
  prior_period_headcount_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on headcount (prior_period_headcount_id)']
  attrition_count varchar [note: 'Number of employees who left the organization (voluntary or involuntary) during the reporting period.']
  authorized_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of FTE positions authorized for the org unit for the reporting period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the headcount snapshot record was first created in the data lake.']
  filled_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of FTEs actually filled (employees working) in the org unit for the reporting period.']
  headcount_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the snapshot record (active = current, inactive = archived).']
  headcount_type varchar [note: 'Classification of headcount: permanent employees, contractors, interns, or temporary staff.']
  new_hire_count varchar [note: 'Number of new employees hired into the org unit during the reporting period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the reporting period for which headcount changes are measured.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period for which headcount changes are measured.']
  reporting_date date [note: 'The calendar date for which the headcount snapshot is reported.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the headcount data (e.g., Workday, SAP SuccessFactors).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the headcount snapshot record.']
  vacant_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of authorized FTE positions that remain unfilled (vacant) for the reporting period.']

  Note: 'Point-in-time headcount snapshot record capturing the authorized and actual headcount position for each org unit at a given reporting date. Stores reporting date, org unit, legal entity, headcount type (permanent, contractor, intern), authorized FTE count, filled FTE count, vacant FTE count, attrition count for the period, and new hire count for the period. Supports workforce planning, budget variance analysis, and regulatory headcount disclosures.'
}

  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the upcoming performance or role review.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the role assignment record.']
  work_location_address varchar [note: 'Street address of the work location; used for compliance and travel expense calculations.']
  work_location_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the physical or virtual location where the employee performs the assignment.']

  Note: 'Employee role and position assignment record capturing the active and historical mapping of employees to positions, org units, and job profiles. Stores assignment type (primary, acting, secondment, interim), effective start date, effective end date, position reference, org unit reference, job profile reference, FTE percentage, work location, and assignment status. Enables point-in-time org chart reconstruction and supports SOX segregation-of-duties controls for payments fintech.'
}

Table workforce.employment_event {
  employment_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each immutable employment lifecycle event.']
  corrected_employment_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on employment_event (corrected_employment_event_id)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who initiated or approved the employment event.']
  hr_business_partner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR business partner responsible for the event.']
  primary_employment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee whose employment record is affected.']
  tertiary_employment_hr_business_partner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HR business partner responsible for the event.']
  approval_chain varchar [note: 'Ordered, delimiter‑separated list of approver identifiers that reviewed the event.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks associated with the event.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification for the event record as defined by corporate policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employment event record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the employment event becomes legally effective for the employee.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the employment event.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the employment event was recorded in the source system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the employment lifecycle change (e.g., hire, promotion, termination, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hire|rehire|promotion|demotion|transfer|compensation_change|leave_of_absence|return_from_leave|termination|retirement|contract_renewal — promote to reference product]']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event is considered critical for regulatory or compliance reporting.']
  new_state_snapshot varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded snapshot of the employee\'s relevant attributes after the event.']
  previous_state_snapshot varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded snapshot of the employee\'s relevant attributes before the event.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the employment event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that originated the employment event (e.g., HRIS, payroll).']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or service that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the employment event record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or service that created the employment event record.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of all significant employment lifecycle events for an employee. Captures event type (hire, rehire, promotion, demotion, lateral transfer, compensation change, leave of absence, return from leave, termination, retirement, contract renewal), event effective date, previous state snapshot, new state snapshot, initiating manager reference, HR business partner reference, and approval chain. Provides the authoritative employment history for compliance, litigation support, and regulatory reporting.'
}

Critical for payments fintech regulatory compliance — tracks mandatory AML, BSA, and PCI training certifications.'
}

  training_program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the training program.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the enrollment record.']
  virtual_meeting_link varchar [note: 'URL for virtual training session (if applicable).']

  Note: 'Employee training enrollment and completion record for mandatory and elective learning programs. Captures training program name, training type (mandatory regulatory, compliance, technical, leadership, onboarding), delivery method (e-learning, instructor-led, virtual, on-the-job), enrollment date, completion date, pass/fail status, assessment score, CPE credits earned, and whether the training satisfies a regulatory compliance requirement (AML awareness, PCI DSS, GDPR, BSA). Distinct from compliance.training_completion which tracks enterprise-wide compliance training — this record is the HR system of record for all workforce learning.'
}

  termination_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the termination takes effect, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for termination when action_type is termination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the disciplinary action record.']

  Note: 'Employee disciplinary action record capturing formal HR disciplinary proceedings. Stores action type (verbal warning, written warning, final written warning, suspension, demotion, termination for cause), incident description, investigation reference, action date, effective date, duration (for suspensions), appeal status, appeal outcome, and the HR business partner and legal counsel involved. Supports employment law compliance, litigation defense, and regulatory fitness-and-propriety assessments for licensed roles.'
}

Table workforce.work_schedule {
  work_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work schedule record.']
